raw_text: ABRAHAM, HENRY CHARLES, F.L.S.—B. 1887; ed. Taunton's Schl., Southampton and Southampton Univ. Coll.; surrv. probr., F.M.S., Jan., 1911; surrv., grade II, topo. br., Apr., 1912; 2nd lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.), Sept., 1915; lieut., July, 1917; asst. supt., topo. br., Jan., 1919; rel. comm., Mar., 1920; ag. supt., topo. br., June, 1927; ag. supt., surveys, Penang Feb., 1928; Empire, conf., survey offrs., Southampton and London, July, 1928; sen. asst. supdt., topo. br., Jan., 1929; Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. Medal, Nov., 1929; senr. asst. supt., topo. br., May to Dec., 1930; do., survey dept., F.M.S. and S.S., Feb., 1932; supt., rev. surveys, F.M.S., 1933.

ABRAHAM, JOHN CONRAD, M.A. (Oron.), M.B.E. (1918).—B.1889; ed. Keswick and Wadham Coll.; Oxford; astt. res., Nyasaland, 1911; O.C. Details, Fort Johnston; attd. 1st K.A.R., 1914; sp. serv., carrier transport, N.F.F., 1915-16; men in desps., three times; admin. offr., 1st gde., 1923; ag. astt. ch. sec., Nov., 1926; N. Nyasa native reserves commn., 1928; sp. duty, Tanganjika, Nov., 1929; astt. sec., native affrs., Nyasaland, Jan., 1931; astt. ch. sec., July, 1931; chinm. native languages bd., 1931; supt., census, 1931; ag. ch. sec., May-Nov., 1932; gov.'s dep., July, 1932; senr. prov. commn., Nov. 1933.

ABRAHAMS, SIDNEY SOLOMON, B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1885; ed. Bedford Modern Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Middle Temp., June, 1909; Midland circuit; town mag., Zanzibar, June, 1915; crown counsel, Kenya, Nov., 1915, but did not proceed; seconded for serv. with Mesopotamia civil admnstr., Jan., 1920; advoc.gen. and regist. of companies, Baghdad, Apr., 1920; pres., ct. of first instance, ct. of sessions and ct. of criminal appl., Basrah, Jan., 1921; atty-gen., Zanzibar, June, 1922; atty.-gen., Uganda, Jan., 1925; atty.-gen., Gold Coast, 1928; ch. just., Uganda, Feb., 1933; joint edr., Zanzibar law repts., 1868-1918; sole edr., Zanzibar law repts., 1918-22; 2nd cls. Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1924.

ABRAMSON, MAJOR ALBERT, C.B.E. (1927), O.B.E.—B. 1876; war serv. with E.F.F.; O.B.E., 1918; twice ment. in despatches; mily. gov., Hebron, 1918; chmn., land comm., and dir., land dept., 1920; ch. Br. rep., Transjordan, 1921; dist. gov., southern dist., 1922; dist. comm., northern dist., 1925; comm., lands, 1927.

ACHESON, A. B.—B. 1895; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1913); on mil. service, Rifle Brigade and Machine Gun Corps, 1914-19; served in France, Belgium and Germany; ment. in despatches, Croix-de-Guerre; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. cl., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; sec. to comte., on the development of scientific training and research 19th Nov., 1920; sec. to comte., on trade and taxation for Br. W. Africa, Oct. to Dec., 1921; priv. sec. to permanent under-seer., 3rd Oct., 1923; ag. prin., Jan., 1926.

ADAMS, CHARLES EDWARD, D.Sc. (N.Z.), A.I.A. (Lond.), F.R.A.S.—B. 1870; ed. Christchurch Boys' High Sch., Wellington Coll., Otago Boys' High Sch., and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; ent. N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1900; ch. trigonometrical computer and dir. tidal survey some years; gov't. astronomer and seismologist since 1911; Martin Kellogg fellow in astronomy, Univ. of California, 1915, and assoc. in astronomy, Yale Univ., 1916.
ADAMS, C. H.—Asst. dist. comm., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; dist. comm., Mar., 1917.

ADAMS, ERNEST, O.B.E. (1924), F.C.I.S. (Eng.), F.A.A. (Australia).—B. 1884; ed. Royal Orphanage, Wolverhampton; company sec., Sydney, N.S.W., 1908-1914; office of agt. gen. for N.S.W., London, 1915; liquidator of enemy firms, E.A. Prot., 1915; custodian of enemy property, occupied territory of German E. Africa, 1916 and Tanganyika territory, 1919; compt. of cust., and custodian of enemy property, Tanganyika, Jan., 1923; chmn., Tanganyika trade and infrn. local advisory committee.
ADAMS, F.V., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.).—B. 1904; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1928.

ADAMS, G. H., A.S.A.—B. 1891; C.A. office, Nov., 1911; temp. acctnt., Nyasaland, July, 1915; cashier, 1915; asst. comm., income tax, 1921; senr. asst. treas., Apr., 1925; ag. dep. treas., in 1921, 1925 and 1927; dep. treas., Oct., 1929; ag. treas., May to Dec., 1930; dep. treas., Palestine, 1933.

ADAMS, JAMES WILMOT, F.R.C.S. (Eng.), R.A., M.B., B.C. (Cantab.).—B. 1884; ed. Tonbridge schl. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; med. offr., S.S., Feb., 1913; surg., Tan Tock Seng's hosp. and gen. hosp., 1915 to June, 1917; surg., Penang, Aug., 1926; ag. ch. med. offr., Penang, June, 1929 to Nov., 1929.

ADAMS, THEODORE SAMUEL.—B. 1885; ed. King's Sch., Canterbury, and All Soul's Coll., Oxford, B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1905; asst. dist. offr., Klang, 1911; dist. offr., K Langat, 1912; dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, 1914; audlt., lands, Kelantan, 1918; ag. 1st asst. sec., 1919; ag. 1st asst. sec. to gov't., 1920; ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, 1920; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1925; dist. offr., Larut and regir. titles, Perak, Nov., 1925; offr., cls. IB, chmn., sany. bd., K. Lumpur, June, 1928; mag., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1928; ag. under sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; ag. Br. res., Selangor, Mar., 1932.

ADAMS, THOMAS, F.S.I.—B. 1871; ed. Corophine and Edinburgh; pres., Young Scots' Soc., 1900; sec., Garden City Assoc., 1901-1904; sec. and man., First Garden City Co., 1903-1906; town planning adviser to Marquis of Salisbury, Earl of Lytton, Sir Richard Paget, Bt., and other landowners, 1906-1909; town planning inspr., L.G.B. of England and Wales, 1909-1914; examr. in civic design, Univ. of Liverpool, 1912-1914; rep. of L.G.B. on arterial roads; Confere. of Greater London, 1913-1914; prep'd. offr. repts. on housing and town planning investigtns. in U.S., Germany, Sweden, and other countries and on first town planning schemes in Britain, 1908-1918; organised cheap cottage exhibts., Letchworth, 1905, and Wolverhampton, 1908; housing and town planning adviser to Canadian Gov't. since 1914; hon. mem. Instn.of Mun. and Cnty. Engrs. (England); mem. of American Soc. of Mun. Improvements; pres. Town Planning Inst. of Britain, 1913-1914; and mem. of coun., 1914-1919; mem. bd. of govt. American City Planning Inst., 1915-1918; mem. Town Planning Inst. of Canada, 1919.

ADAMS, MAJOR WILFRID CARNE, M.C.—B. 1894; supt., constab., N. Borneo, Dec., 1919; adjt., Apr., 1922; ag. offr. commdg., constab. and off. mem., leg. coun., May, 1927; commdt. and off. mem., legis. coun., Apr., 1931.

ADAMS, WILFRID GEORGE, B.A.—B. 1885; ed. Shrewsbury and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. comm., Uganda, Dec., 1910; dist. comm., Apr., 1919; dep. prov. comm., Oct., 1928; prov. comm., 2nd grade, Sept., 1929; 1st grade, Oct., 1930.

ADAMSON, SIR JOHN ERNEST, KT, B.A.C.H. (1924), C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1867; ed. in London: Litt.D., M.A., London Univ.; principal, Normal Coll., Pretoria, 1902; dir. of educn., Transvaal, 1905; M.L.C., Transvaal; mem. of coun. of Cape University; dir. of educn., Transvaal Prov.; vice-chmn., Univ. of S. Africa; master, Rhodes Univ. Coll., Oct., 1925-31; vice-pres., educul. sec., Br. Assn. (centenary meeting).

ADDERLEY, CHARLES J. H., B.A., B.A.I., A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.Inst.C.E.I.—B. 1884; Chn. nesev. govt. rly., 1908-16; war serv., R.G.A., France, 1916-19; road surv., Nigeria, 1920; exec. engnr., 1923; senr. exec. engnr., 1926; ag. asst. dir., in 1927-30; D.P.W. in 1928-29.

ADDIS, WILLIAM.—B. 1901; ed. Rugby and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (hons.), 1923; asst. dist. comm., Zanzibar, Feb., 1924; ag. asst. sec., clk. of couns., edr., offl. gazette, Mar., 1929 to June, 1930; ag. rec. mag. and dist. regar., Pemba in 1931 and 1932.

ADDISON, D'ARCY WENTWORTH, C.M.G. (1928), M.V.O., I.S.O.—Ent. Tasmanian pub. serv., 1st Dec., 1887; clk. of exec. coun., 1905; sec. to premier since 1902; ch. electoral offr., 1914; mag., 1918; admstr. of charitable grants, 1914-19; sec. for neglected children's dep., 1914-19; state organis., visit of H.R.H. Prince... of Wales, 1920 and the Duke and Duchess of York, 1927; under sec. for Tasmania, 1928; agt.-gen. in England for Tasmania, 1930.
ADDO, J. A., B.A.—B. 1903; 4th cls. teacher, Gold Coast, 1922; 2nd divn. teacher, 1923; cadet inspr., schls., 1927; inspr., schls., 1928.
ADELAIDE, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. ARTHUR NUTTER THOMAS, D.D., Cantab.—B. 1869; rector of Guisborough, Yorks., 1901-1906; bishop of Adelaide since 1906.

AGAR, ARTHUR KIRWAN.—B. 1877; ed. at Brighton Coll., called to the bar, Grays Inn, May, 1919; temp. lieut. R.A.S.C., Oct., 1919; agt.-capt. R.A.S.C., Aug., 1916; staff capt., W.O., Feb.-Dec., 1919; dist. mag., dep. judge, ct. of summary juris., coroner, regis. of sup. ct., escheator gen., provost marshal, regis. gen., regis. of titles and legal adviser to gov't. of Montserrat, 1st Oct., 1920; dist. comsnr., Belize, Br. Hond., Nov., 1923; agt.-atty-gen., Dec., 1923 to Apr., 1925; res. mag., Jamaica, Oct., 1925.

AHEARN, CHRISTOPHER DOMINIC.—B. 1886; ed. Christian Schls., Cork, and Trinity Coll., Dublin, scholar 1907; senr. moderator, B.A., 1909; cadet, S.S., 1910; India, studying Tamil, 1911; asst. supt., Indian immigrants, Selangor, 1912; 1st asst. contrlr., lab., Penang, 1913; dep. contrlr., lab., Klang, 1914; dep. regis., sup. ct., Penang, 1916; dist. offr., Balik Pulau, 1916; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, S'pore, 1917; asst. regis., impts. and expts. (war dept.), 1918; offr., cls. iv., Jan., 1919; asst. advr. to gov't. of Johore, Batu Pahat, 1919-21; 2nd mag., Singapore, Mar., 1923; dep. contr. lab., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1923; dep. treas., S.S. (cls. Ia.), 1928; contr. lab., Malaya, 1930; leader S.S. deleg., Simla shipping confce., July, 1931; chmn., S.S. retrenchment comtee., Feb., 1932.

AINSGLIE, JAMES ROBERT, B.Sc. (Oxon.), Diploma of Forestry (Oxford), F.L.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. at Graeme Walker's (Aberdeen); Aberdeen Univ. and Oxford; probationary asst. cons., forests, Imp. Indian Forest Serv. (Oxford), Aug., 1907; asst. cons., forests, Ceylon, Nov., 1910; on active serv., 1914-17 (Egypt and France); attd. to W.O., timber supplies dept. (XI (Forest) Divnl. staff), Aug., 1917; dep. cons., forests, Ceylon, May, 1919; senr. cons., forests, Nigeria, Feb., 1922; dir., forests, Nigeria, May, 1929; author of "The Physiography of Southern Nigeria and its effect on the Forest Flora of the Country" and "Timber Resources of Nigeria."

ITCHISON, JAMES, B.Sc. (Glas.).—B. 1880; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Pahang, Feb., 1911; exec. engnr., Feb., 1920; sr. exec. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., June, 1927; seconded for serv. as senr. exec. engnr., Johore, Sept., 1929.

AITKEN, JOHN, B.A., LL.B. (1906).—B. 1885; ed. Giggleswick Coll. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1909; asst. dist. offr., S. Nigeria, 1913; station mag., Nigeria, 1915; crown counsel, Gold Coast, 3rd Nov., 1920; agt. solr.-gen., Dec., 1920 to Apr., 1921, July to Sept. and Oct. to Nov., 1921 and June to July, 1922; agt. puisne judge, Sept. to Oct., 1921, and from July to Sept., 1922; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1925; atty.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1929; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1933.
AITKEN, JOHN BANKS.—B. 1903; ed. Stewart's Coll., Edin. and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1925; asst. conserv., forests, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1926; asst. conserv., forests, Br. Guiana, Nov., 1928.
AITKEN, W. J., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (Liv.).—B. 1899; mily. serv., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1926.

AKBAR, MAAS TRAJON.—K.C., B.A., LL.B. Cantab., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1880; ag. crown coun., Ceylon, Aug., 1907; crown coun., June, 1909; ag. 2nd addnl. dist. judge, Colombo, and as solr.-gen. on several occasions; solr.-gen., Oct., 1924; ag. puisne just., Nov. to Dec., 1925; agt.-atty.-gen., Dec., 1927; puisne judge, sup. ct., Apr., 1929.

ALABASTER, CHALONEER GREENVILLE, O.B.E. (1918), K.C. (1922).—B. 1880; ed. Tonbridge; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1904; ag. atty.-gen., Hong Kong, 1911, 1912, 1928, 1930; confirmed in appt., 1931; unoff. mem., leg. coun., 1919, 1924, 1929; edr. of "Laws of Hong Kong 1844-1912."
ALBURY, JOSEPH JOHNSON.—B. 1881; Out Island comsnr. (4th div.), Bahamas, 1910; 4th grade comsnr., 1912; 3rd grade, Jan. 1918; 2nd grade, 1926; 1st grade, Mar., 1931.

ALBURY, WILTON GRANT, M.B.E. (1931).—B. 1877; public school teacher, Bahamas, 1896; resigned, 1906; re-apptd., 1909; head-master, boys central school, 1914; inspr. of schools, 1915; ag. S. and C. mag., June to July and from July, 1926.

ALCOCK, WILLIAM D. R.—B. 1892; ed. King Edward's schl. and Birmingham Univ.; B.A., 1913 (1st class, two distinctions); cadet, Fiji, and Western Pacific civ. serv., 1915-20; asst. polit. offr., 2nd grade, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; ag. dist. offr., 1927; seconded as lab. offr., 1929; dist. offr., July, 1931.
ALEXANDER, JOHN EDWARD—Asst. land ranger, E.A.P., Oct. 1912; lands offr., Nyasa-land, July, 1922.

ALLAN, ALBERT, A.M.Inst.I.—B. 1893; war serv., 1914-19; station supt. and traffic inspr., F.M.S. rly., Feb., 1920; asst. sec., G.M.R. office in 1921, 1923, 1925 and 1929; asst. sec., bd. in 1924, 1927 and 1930; divnl. supt. (W.), K. Lumper, Aug., 1932.

ALLAN, PETER.—B. 1886; ed. George Heriot's Schl., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ., M.B., Ch.B. (Hons.), 1910; D.P.H., 1912; M.D., 1920; R.A.M.C., France, 1917-18; capt., R.A.M.C.; med. inspr., Union of S. Africa health dept., 1920; major, S.A.M.C., 1921; med. supt., Nelsoport sanatorium, 1923; seconded to tuberculosis research comtee., 1927-30; reassumed M.S., Nelsoport sanatorium, 1930.

ALLARD, JULES.—B. 1859; ed. Nicolet Coll., Quebec; lawyer; elec. to legis. assem., Quebec, 1897, 1900, 1904; apptd. to legis. coun. and min. of pub. wks., Quebec, 1906; min. of agric., 1907; min. of lands and forests, 1909-1917; re-elec. to legis. assem. at bye-election 1910 and g.e., 1912; re-called to legis. coun., 1916; prothonotary of the superior ct., Montreal, 1919.

ALLDER, WM. HOWELL.—B. 1873; 5th clk., C.S.O., Barbados, 1892; 4th do., 1898; 3rd do., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., P.O., 1904; ch. clk., do., 1908; ch. clk., audit off., 1913; acted on several occasions as clk. of the exec. coun. and comtee., as acctnt. in P.O., and as auditor-gen.; sec. to Barbados quarantine coman., 1903, to West Indian quarantine confce., 1904, and to confce. on trade relations between West Indies and Canada, 1910; sec., Barbados civ. serv. comsn., 1920.
ALLEN, ARTHUR AUGUSTUS.—B. 1890; ed. Christ Hospital; examr., Exchequer and Audit

ALLEN, Col. Sir Stephen Shepherd, K.B.E (1933), C.M.G. (1919), D.S.O. (1917), with bar (1918).—Volunteer serv., Cambridge Univ., 1904 and in N.Z., 1911-15; left N.Z. with 6th reinforcements N.Z. Exped. Force, with rank of major, 1915; lieut.-col., Jan., 1917; in commd., 2nd batt., Auckland Regt., Dec., 1916 to June, 1919; comdr., Auckland Infy. Brig. (T.F.), Feb., 1920 to May, 1925; col., Feb., 1920; in commd., Hauraki Regt., Sept., 1926 to Apr., 1928; R. of O., Apr., 1928; admstr., W. Samoa, 1928-31; four times ment. in despatches; hon. A.D.C. to gov. genl., N.Z., Jan., 1925 to May, 1928.
ALLEN, W. R., B.A.—B. 1895; inspr., schla., Gold Coast, Jan., 1929.

ALLEYNE, Herbert Massy.—B. 1899; ed. Blundell's and R.M.C., Sandhurst; comd., Connaught Rangers, Dec., 1918; transfd., King's Own Royal Regt., 1922; ret., 1929; admstr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929; dist. dist. offr., Aug., 1931.

ALLEYNE, James Douglas, M.D. (Tufts Univ., U.S.A.)—Ag. pol. med. offr., dist. A. Barbados and visiting physician, leper asylum, 1917; asst. port health offr., 1919; ag. sup. lunatic asylum, 1919; port health offr., 1921; ex officio mem., quarantine board.

ALLIES, E. J.—B. 1891; apptd. asst. cl. Scottish Educn. Dept., 1910; apptd. after compet. exam. cl. of the 2nd divn. of the civ. serv., to National Insurance Audit Dept., 1913; on mily. serv., 1917-19; assigned to the C.O. Aug., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.
ALLISON, J. S.—B. 1879; ed. Glenalmond and Edinburgh Univ.; M.A. (Edin); L.L.B. (Cape); Cape law dep., 1903; Union dep. of native affrs., 1918; under sec. for native affrs., 1929.

ALLISON, William Francis Dudley, A.M. Mech.E., M.I.Loco.E.M.Inst.T.—B. 1889; ed. Glasgow High Schl. and R. Tech. Coll., Glasgow N.B.R. Co. and L. and S.W.R., 1906; inspr. engr., crown agts., 1908; asst. loco. supt., Nigerian rly., 1911; seconded as loco. supt., m.rlys., Togoland, 1915; asst. loco. supt., Uganda rly., 1918; dist. loco. supt., Tanganyika rly., 1922; ch. mech. engr., July, 1930.

ALLNUTT, Richard Buswell, B.Sc. (Agric.)—B. 1907; ed Malvern Coll. and S. Eastern Agric. Coll., Wye (B.Sc. (agri.) (Lond.) and dipl., agr., 1928; crop. observn. offr., Norfolk agric. stat., 1928-30; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930.
ALLSOP, Herbert Henry.—B. 1885; German East Africa campaign, 1914-16; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1916; 1st grade admistr. offr., Aug., 1922.

ALUWIHARE, Richard.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., Oct., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to Jaffna kach., Oct., 1920; office asst. to dir. of agr., Feb., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Dandagama, Oct., 1923; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1923; ditto, Panadure, Nov., 1924; 2nd landing surr. cust., 1926; comdr., requests, Kandy, Nov., 1929; asst. sttlm. offr., Oct., 1929; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1931.
ALVAPPILLAI, Kovindapillai.—B. 1908; cadet., Ceylon civ. serv., 1929; attd., Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1929; attd., Matale kach., Sept., 1929; attd. office asst., Puttalam kach., Oct., 1930.

AMBLER, Capt. Goodman, M.C., V.D., F.R.G.S.—B. 1885; ed. Steyning Grammar Schl., Brighton Grammar Schl. and St. Mary's... Chelsea; asst. mast., Victoria Inst., F.M.S., Jan., 1910; war serv., Gallipoli, 1914-19; rejoined Victoria Inst., 1919; sec., Selangor cadets with rank of Mar., 1924; Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. Med., Mar., 1924; headmast., Outram schl., Apr., 1926; lieut., S.V.C., May, 1926; Aug., 1930; transf'd to S.S.V.F., R. of O.; normal instr., S'pore, Dec., 1931.

BLER, LLOYD EDWARD.—B. 1894; clk., dept., Fiji, June, 1909; 4th cls. clk., dept., Aug., 1913; served with 1st Batt., R.C., France, 1916-17; Indian Army Res., Oct., 1917; Baluchistan, 1917-19; cls. clk., col., sec.'s office, Nov., 1916; lands dept., Fiji, Sept., 1919; ag. 2nd clk. and sec., mining bd., Oct., 1919-Oct., ag. sub-instr. constab. (during Indian Mut.), Feb., 1920; ag. 1st cls. clk. and clk., exec. couns., Jan. to Apr., 1921; regy. clk., Western Pacific high commr., May, 1921; ag. ch. Sept., 1922 to Feb., 1923; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1923; ag. ch. clk. and acctn., on various occasions, 1923-31; ag. sec., Western Pacific high commr., June to Aug., 1931; ch. clk. and acct., 1932.

BERASINGHE, SAMSON FELIX, B.A., B.M.B.—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., July, 1925; attd., Kalutara kach., Aug., 1926; office asst. to gov't. agt., Lagamuwa, Sept., 1928; ditto, Negombo, Dec., 1928; office asst. to gov't. agt., Uva, June, 1929; office asst. to gov't. agt., N.W. Prov., June, 1929; pol. mag., Kandy, Nov., 1932.

BERMERY, LIEUT.-COL. L.C.M.S.—B. 1873; late Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; late fellow of All Souls, Oxford; Ouseley Scholar in Comp. Inst. in Turkish; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; on editorial staff of The Times, 1909-1909; served during the war in Flanders, Salonika, and the Near East; on the staff of the Imperial War Coun. at Versailles, and as asst. sec. to the War Cabinet and Impl. Cabinet to Jan., 1919; under-sec. of state for the colonies, 14th July, 1919; acted as sec. of state during Lord Milner's absence in Egypt, Nov., 1919 to Feb., 1920; First Lord of the Adm'y., Oct., 1920; S. of S. for the Colonies, 7th Nov., 1924; also S. of S. for Dominion affrs., 17th July, 1925 to June, 1926; capt. 14th 1st B'h'm Batt., Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1914; lieut.-col. on general staff, 1916; M.P. (U) for S. Birmingham, May, 1911; to Sparkbrook div., Dec., 1918; edited "The Economic History of the South African War"; author of "Problem of the Army," 1903; "Fundamental Fallacies of Free Trade," 1906; "The Great Question" (with J. M. Robertson, P.E.), 1909; "Union and Strength," 1912.

BERMYER, WILLIAM BANKES, C.B.E. (1920).—B. 1883; ed. Christ Hosp.; ent. cust. and excise officer, 1901; asst. sec. (temp.) war trade dept., 1916; estab. offr., ministry of transport, 1919; finance offr. (with rank of prin.) overseas settlement dept., D.O., 1922; rep. of the U.K. govt. to the Commonwealth of Australia under the Empire Settlement Act, 1925; prin., O.S.D., 1929; temp. adm., D.O., 1932.

BERNDSON, ADMIRAL SIR DAVID MURRAY, C.B. (1930), C.M.G. (1918), M.V.O. (1910).—B. 1874; ed. Stubbington and H.M.S. "Britannia"; served with Brass river expedit. and M'Wele expedit., 1905; Ashanti, 1906; Great War, 1914-19, including operns. in Rufiji river, 1915; served with Grand Fleet, 1918; capt., sup't., Pembroke dockyard, and senr. naval offr., Milford Haven, 1920-22; rear-adm., 1922; naval A.D.C. to the King, 1921-22; senr. naval offr., Yangtze river, 1923-26; C. in C., China station, April, 1925; Africa station, 1927-29; vice-adm'l., 1927; high commr., S. Africa, June-Dec., 1928; adm'l., 1931; adm'y. rep., L. of N. perm't. advisory comm'r., 1929-31; gov't. and comdr-in-ch., Newfoundland, 1932.
ANDERSON, E. L. B.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Oct., 1919; pvte. sec. to ag. gov., 1925.

ANDERSON, MAXWELL HENRY.—C.B.E. (mil.) (1919); O.B.E. (1918); K.C., Aug., 1919; capt., R.N. (ret.); ent. Royal Navy, 1893; ret. at own request, 1912; recd. thanks of Adm'y. for series of magnetic observations carried out in the Pacific Ocean; inst., R.N. Coll., Portsmouth, 1908-10; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1909; younger brother of Trinity House, 1910; rejoined Navy on outbreak of war; promoted capt., Apr., 1919; gov't. mem., central (unemployed) body for London, 1913 to 1919; atty-gen., Gibraltar, May, 1919; ag. chief just., and col. sec. on several occasions; ch. just., Fiji and judi. commr., W. Pacific, 1929; ag. high commr., W. Pacific, June to Aug., 1930.
ANDERSON, THOMAS JOHN, M.A., B.Sc., (Hons. Edin.) F.Z.S., F.S.E.—Entomologist, agric. dept., Kenya, May, 1908.

ANDERSON, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1896; ed. Kilmarnock Acad. and Tech. Coll.; active serv. (France) 1914-19; dep. storekeeper, Hongkong, Feb., 1926; ag. supt., accts. and stores, P.W.D., 1926; sup't., accts. and stores, Jan., 1928.
ANDREWES, LANCELOT RUGGLES.—Ed. Fort Street, Sydney; war serv., 1915-18; admitted sol'r., sup. ct., London, 1924; asst. crown sol'r., Hong Kong, 1928; J.P., 1931; ag. dep. regist., 1932.

ANDREWS, NORMAN FREDERICK STEWART.—B. 1902; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and Oxford; schol., Brasenose Coll., 1919; Bridgeman Prize, 1923, 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1924, B.A., 1924; M.A., 1928; cadet, adminstr., Uganda, 1926; asst. dist. offr., 1927; temp. serving in C.O., 1927-28; seconded to secretariat, Uganda, 1929; sec., cotton enquiry commr., 1929.

ANNECKE, D. H. SIEGFRIED.—B. 1895; ed. Grey Univ. Coll. Bloemfontein; B.A. (lit. and phil.), 1914; Univ. C.T., M.A. (physiology), 1918; Guy's Hosp., Lond., 1919-23, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1921; all house appts. Guy's, 1921; M.B., B.S., Univ. Durham, 1924; D.P.H. and B. Hy., Univ. Durham, 1925; research schl. and asst. dept. protozoology, Lond. Schl. Hyg. Trop. Med., 1925-28; D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), 1927; joint educ. grant, govt. S.A. and Health Section L. of N. for study of anti-malarial methods in Italy, 1927; senr. govt. pathologist, Union health dept., Durban, 1928; med. inspr. (malaria), 1931.

ANNETTS, HERBERT HENRY.—B. 1888; Ed. Magdalen Coll. schl., Oxford, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1910; asst. master, preparatory schl., Repton, 1910-1911; served in Oxfordshire educa. comtee's office, and inspr. of evening schl., Oxfordshire county council, 1911-1912; apptd. junr. supt., educa. dept., Northern Nigeria, Sept., 1912; senr. supt., Jan., 1919; dep. dir., Jan., 1920; ag. dir., 1920 and various dates to 1928-29; asst. dir., July, 1929.

ANSDELL, GEORGE ARTHUR REES WETTENHALL.—B. 1894; ed. Liscard High Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; war serv., Egypt, 1916; Turkey in Asia, 1919; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1921; passed lower Swahili, 1922; admin. offr., 2nd gde., 1924; passed law, 1925; ag. dist. offr., 1926; seconded as lab. offr., 1928; A.D.C. and P.S. to gov., Apr. to Nov., 1927 and Dec., 1928 to Jan., 1929.

ANTHONY, PHILIP ARNOLD, C.M.G. (1918), M.I.C.E.—B. 1873; ed. at Mill Hill Schl.; served in engineering dept., G.W. Ely., 1894-1910; gen. man. and chief engrn., F.M.S. Ryia., 1910; services lent to ministry of munitions, May, 1915 to Nov., 1916, resumed duty in F.M.S., Feb., 1917; services lent temporarily to govt. of South Aust.; Apr., 1917; resumed duty in F.M.S., Jan., 1919; ret., Dec., 1924; reported on Palestine Rlys., 1925; ag. ch. engrn. (design), C.A., Nov., 1925 to Dec., 1926.

ANTHONY, WILLIAM THOMAS.—B. 1874; clk., mag's office, Kokstad, S. Africa, 1894; elk., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1894; essg. off., 1899; 1st cls. clk., Cape Town, 1903; prin. clk., Port Elizabeth, 1915; ch. clk., Durban, 1926; head office, Pretoria, 1926; collr., cust. and shipping mast., Port Elizabeth, 1930; Cape Town, 1930.

ANTROBUS, MAURICE E. B.A.—B. 1895; ed. Winchester, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; math. exhibt., Trinity Coll., Dec., 1913; 2nd lieut., 6th King's Royal Rifle Corps, Aug., 1914; lieut., June, 1915; capt., Jan., 1917; served in Flanders, Salonica and France, 1915-19; 2nd cls. math. tripes, pt. I., 1919; 2nd cls. hist. tripes, pt. II., 1920; B.A., 1920; temp. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Aug., 1920; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, ast. prin., 7th Feb., 1921; ast. pvtc. sec. to S. of S., 2nd Nov., 1925; seconded as pvtc. sec. to gov., Ceylon, July, 1927; prin., D.O., 23rd July, 1930.

ANTROBUS, SIR REGINALD L., K.C.M.G. (1911), C.B. (1898).—B. 1853; ed. at Winchester and at New Coll., Oxford; scholar of New Coll., 1872; 1st class in classical moda, 1874; 2nd class in final classical school, 1876; B.A., 1876; apptd., after an open compet. exam., to be a clk. in the C.O., May, 1877; ast. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, Dec., 1880 to Dec., 1882; priv. sec. to the Earl of Derby, Dec., 1882 to June, 1885; to Col. the Right Hon. F. A. Stanley, M.P. (afterwards Earl of Derby), June, 1885 to Feb., 1886; and to Earl Granville, Feb. to Aug., 1886; administ. the govt. of St. Helena, Nov., 1889 to June, 1890; priv. sec. to Mr. Meade, Feb., 1892, and to Mr. S. Buxton, M.P., Aug., 1892; sec. clk., Jan., 1894; prin. clk., 1896; ast. under-sec. of state, Oct., 1898; crown agent for the cols., 1909; ret., 1918.

APPLEGATE, F. W.—B. 1887; ed. Bath Coll. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1911; ast. dist. comsmr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1915; junr. ast. col. sec., May to Sept., 1915; travelling comsmr., Nsawam, Oct., 1915, to May, 1916, and from Apr., 1917; ag. dist. comsmr., Kwahu, 1919; dep. prov. comsmr., 1929; prov. comsmr., 1932.

APTHORP, MARMADUKE GWYN, B.A.—B. 1875; ed. Malvern and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; elk., atty-gen.'s office, Cape Town, Sept., 1900; elk., survr.-gen.'s office, Feb., Apr., 1909; ag. prin. elk., P.W.D., 1910; prin. elk., native affrs. dept., Jun., 1911; active serv. with E. African exp., 1912-17; ag. ch. elk. and ag. undersec., fur. min., 1917; mag. Lady Frere, May, 1918; June, 1923; ch. elk., ch. mag.'s offic. and sec. and treas., Transkeian Terr., Pondoland gen. couns., Dec., 1925; commr., Ciakai, Oct., 1928.

ARCHER, SIR GEOFFREY FRANK (1920), C.M.G. (1915).—Ass. collr. to Prot., Dec., 1902; attached secret. 1902 to Mar., 1905; dist. commr., Ag. spee. serv., Northern Frontier, East Afr. offr.-in-charge, May to Dec., 1911; subbert Peak Grant by the R.G.S., 1913; from E. Africa connecting Major Gwyn's triangulation with the triangulation of K. transf'd to Somaliland Prot., June, 1912; govt., July to Oct., 1912, and from Apr., Oct., 1913; dep. commr., Mar., 1913; and commr-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., 1914; assumed govt., May, 1914; operations against the Dervishes at Berriq, Feb., 1915 (African General Serv. and clasp); member of British Mission occasion of coronation of the Emperor of Abyssinia, Feb., 1917 (Star of Ethiopia, 1st govt., Somaliland Prot., Nov., 1919; operations against the Mullah, 1920; near complete destruction of Dervish power in land; govt. and commr.-in-chief, Uganda Sept., 1922; govt., Sudan, 1924-26.

ARCHER, NORMAN E.—B. 1892; ed. Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth; midshipman Mediterranean and Home Fleets, 1912; lieut. (six 1st cls. certif.), Sept., 1913; June, 1913; H.M.S. Warrior in Mediterranean and Grand Fleets, 1912-15; qualified as course, as torpedo lieut., 1915; served w/ Navy in Black Sea, &c., 1918-19 (Order Stanislas, 3rd cls.); passed exam. as interpreter, 1918; battell. diva. admv. Lowestoft in Adriatic, minelaying w/ Grand Fleet, and H.M.S. Malaya, 1919; under reconstruction scheme ast. prin. lab., Sept., 1920; transf'd. to C.O., Dist. pvtc. sec. to perm. under sec. for Doc., Sept., 1927; seconded as sec. to Br. mission to Australia and visited Austria and Canada, 1928-29; pvtc. sec. to perm. sec. for Dom. affrs., June, 1929; prnt. 4th Sept., 1929; seconded as sec. offr. commr. in Canada for H.M.G. in U.K., Apr., 1932.

ARCHIBALD, EDGAR S., B.A., R.S. 1885; recd. prmr. educn. at Yarmouth pub. and Yarmouth acad.; grad. Acadia Univ., also grad. from the Nova Scotia hort. sch., same year, and from Nova Scotia agric. 1906; recd. deg. of Bach. of Scien. Agr. the Ontario agric. coll., 1908; inst. of agr. experimt. at the Nova Scotia agric. coll., prof. of agric. and farm supt. of the same, 1910; entd. the serv. of the Fed. Dept. of June, 1912, as Dominion animal man at the Cent. Expmtl. Farm, Oran dir. of Kpmtl. Farms, 1918-1919; dir., 1919.
ARDAGH, OSMOND CHARLES.—B. 1890 Epson Coll. and Merton Coll., Oxford (Oxon), 1922; cadet, Nyassaland Prot., 1925; dist. offr., 1926; sec. to secretariat, Jun., ag. asst. ch. sec., June-Oct., 1933.
ARDRON, Harold Graham.—B. 1892; mily. serv., 1916-21; educn. dept., Gold Coast, 1922; transf. polit. serv. as ass't dist. commr., 1925; ast. col. sec., 1930.

ARGYLE, Sir Stanley Seymour, K.B.E. (1930), M.B., B.S., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1887; ed. Trinity Coll., Melbourne Univ.; lieut.-col., A.A.M.C., A.I.F. in European war; M.L.A., Victoria since 1920; ch. sec. in four ministries, 1923-29; premier and treas., Victoria, May, 1932.

ARMSTRONG, Arthur Leopold.—B. 1888; cadet, Fiji, Apr., 1915; ast. sec. for native affrs., Jan., 1920; resigned, Oct., 1920; dist. commr., and prov. commr., Dec., 1921; under sec. for native affrs., Oct., 1922; ast. sec., do., Jan., 1927; ag. sec., do., on various occasions, 1927-33; ag. col. sec. (conjunct), June-Aug., 1930; nom. mem., leg. coun. and mem. exec. coun. for various periods, 1929-32; 1st ast. col. sec., Mar., 1932; ag. col. sec., May-June, 1933; gov'ts. dep., June, 1933.
ARMSTRONG, George James.—B. 1901; elk., Basutoland, grade II., 1920; grade I., 1927; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1929.
ARMSTRONG, J. S., M.C., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.)—B. 1891; mily. serv., 1915-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925.

ARNOLD, Sir Algernon Edward.—B. 1871; ed. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; sec., West India Comtee, since 1898 and of the Impl. Coll. of Trop. Agr. since 1921; pres. of W. Indian Club; mem. of comtee. on Col. blue-books and blue-book repts., 1917; mem., W. Indian shipping comtee., 1919; hon. sec., Trop. Agr. Coll. comtee., 1920; hon. sec., W. Indian Contingent comtee., 1915-21; mem., W. Indian currency comtee., 1923.

ASHER, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Joseph John, K.C.B. (1924), K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C.V.O. (1917), C.B. (1915), and many foreign decorations.—B. 1867; served with Nile expedit., 1897-9; in command of Kordofan expedit., Soudan, 1910; A.G., Egyptian Army, 1907-16; served in European War, 1914-19; base commr., 1914-16; lieut.-gen., 1919; gen. offr. commdg. Br. troops, France and Flanders, 1919-20; gov't. of Bermuda, 4th May, 1922; assumed gov't., 9th Aug., 1922; ret., 1927.
ASTON, Arthur Vincent.—B. 1896; ed. King's Schl., Chester, and Queen's Schl., Oxford open maths. schol.); R.F.A., 1915-18 (M.C.),

ATHILL, SAMUEL LAUGHLAND, JUNE.—B.1885; ent. civ. serv., Leeward Is., Sept., 1901; clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Aug., 1906; sec. to legal affairs inquiry commnr., Montserrat, 1911; ag. registrar, Montserrat circuit, Feb.-Mar., 1915; deputy coroner, dists. "A" and "B," Antigua, 6th Oct., 1917; mag., dist. B., Antigua, 1st July, 1924; city comsnr., St. Johns, Antigua, Nov., 1925.

ATHLONE, FIRST EARL OF (created 1917), ALEXANDER CAMBRIDGE, K.G. (1928), G.C.B. (1911), G.C.V.O. (1904), C.M.G. (1917), D.S.O. (1900).—Served in Matabele campaign, 1896-7; S. African war, 1899-1900; European war, 1914-18; maj., 2nd Life Gds., 1911-19; brevet-col., 1919; G.S.O., 1st grade, 1915; ret. with hon. rank of brig.-gen., 1919; personal A.D.C. to the King from 1919; gov. gen. and commodr.-in-chief, Union of S. Africa, 1923-30; Grand Cordon of Order of Leopold of Belgium, Croix de Guerre (Belgium); G.O. Leg. Hon. and Croix de Guerre (France); Knt. of Justice of St. John of Jerusalem.

ATKINSON, VIVIAN BUCHANAN—B. 1886; N. Staffs. Rly. Co., 1907; A.M.I.C.E., 1916; asst. engrnr., Uganda Rly., Jan., 1912; dist. engrnr., Tanganyika rly., Oct., 1920; asst. ch. engrnr., Kenya and Uganda rly. and harbrs., 1930.
ATTERBURY, H. W.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 14th Oct., 1921.
ATTERBURY, WILLIAM ALBERT.—Brit. post office, 1910-19; war serv., 1916-19; sub-tel. engrnr., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; tel. engrnr., 1926.

AUBER, EGERTON LANCELOT.—B. 1889; ed. Wesleyan High Schl., Freetown, Sierra Leone; supernumy. clk., cust. (indoor), Gambia, Feb., 1908; 3rd clk., treasry., Nov., 1908; 2nd clk., Nov., 1911; regraded 4th grade clk., 1915; 3rd grade clk., July, 1916; 2nd grade clk., Dec., 1918; ch. clk., treasry., Oct., 1920; seconded for duty as marine acctnt., Feb., 1927 to July, 1929.

AUCHINLECK, GILBERT GRAHAME.—B.Sc. (McGill), F.C.S., B. 1884; ed. West Indies and McGill Univ., Canada; 1st cls., hon. in chemistry and botany, McGill Univ.; govt. laboratory, Leeward Is., 1902-04; McGill Univ., 1904-08; science and 2nd mast., St. Kitts-Nevia gram. schl., 1908-09; supt. of agric., Grenada, 1909-14; asst. dir. of agric. and chemist, Mauritius, 1914; ag. dir. of agric., and regnr., C.C. societies, Dec., 1916 to May, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, Jan.-May, 1917; reported on Is. of Rodrigues to the Mauritius govt., 1919; Mauritius del. to Imp. Entom. Confce, in London, 1920; divnl. agric. offr., Ceylon, 1920; seconded to Seychelles to report on the possibilities of agr. development, 1921-22; dep. dir., agric., G. Coast, Aug., 1925; sent to F.M.S., Java and Sumatra to rept. on palm oil industry, Apr. 1926 to Jan., 1927; del. to W. African agric. confce., Nigeria, Mar., 1927; author of papers on land sttlm. and various trop. agric. subjects; dir. agric., Dec., 1928.

AUCHINLECK, MAURICE M.—B. 1888; treas. and cust., Antigua, June, 1906; col. sec.'s office, Antigua, 1912; clk. to comsnr. and educnl. dist. offr., Montserrat, 1915; ag. postmr. and harbmr., Antigua, 1919-20; ch. clk., treas. and cust., Antigua, 1921; ag. treas., Dominica, in 1925; treas. and comptlr., cust., Antigua, and Fedl. treas., Leeward Is., local chmn., income tax commnr., Antigua; mem., exec. and leg., couns., Antigua, and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Leeward Is., 1926-30; served on hurricane relief comm., 1928; J.P. and visiting just. of prison, mem., city comsnr. and road bd., Antigua, 1930; senr. asst. recr. gen., Gambia, 1931; recr. gen., 1933.

AUCKLAND, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. ALFRED WALTER AVERILL, D.D.—Ed. St. John's Coll., Oxford (2nd cls. Th. sch.), 1887; M.A., 1890; Elv Coll., 1888; deacon, 1888; priest, 1889; Lon.; formerly curate of St. George's, Hanover Square, 1888-91; Holy Trinity, Dalston, Middlesex, 1891-94; vicar of St. Michael's, Camberwell, N. Zealand, 1894-1909; chaplain of both Christchurch, 1895-1909; hon. can. of Christchurch Cathedral, 1902-1909; archdeacon of Akaroa, 1909; bishop of Waipu, 1910-1913; bishop of Auckland, 1913; primate and archbishop of N.Z., 1913.

AUDEN, FRANCIS THOMAS.—B. 1869; ed. Shrewsbury; M.D., Edinburgh Univ., 1906; M.B., 1891; civil surg., S. African war, 1900-01 (medal and clasp); dist. comsnr. and gov. med. offr., Turks and Caicos Islands, Dec., 1914; med. offr., Jamaica, 1917; Kenya, 1918; Bahamas, 1924; Cayman Is., 1925; St. Vincent, 1926; Seychelles, 1927.
AUDETTE, HON. LOUIS ARTHUR, B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1856; apptd. regnr. of exchequer of Canada, 1887; asst. judge, exchequer ct., 1912; puisne judge, 1919.
AUSTEN, HAROLD CHOLMLEY MANSFIELD.—B. 1878; tempy. harb'r. engrnr., Mauritius, 1/2 gen. man., rlys. and harbr. engrnr., May, 1931.
AUSTIN, T. A., L.R.C.P. and S.I.L.K. (Dub.), D.T.M. and H. (Liverpool).—B. 1856; ed. Royal Coll. of Surgeons, Dublin; East Afr. med. serv. (Zanzibar), May, 1924.

AVICE, TRISTAN, O.B.E. (1923).—Ed. Rev. Coll., Mauritius; matric., Lond. Univ., 1888; ed. civ. serv. Mauritius, 1893; 6th cls. clk., 1894; 2nd ditto, Apr., 1899; 4th ditto, Nov., 1899; 2nd dir., 1903; ch. clk., 1905; asst. regnr.-gen., 1912; regnr.-gen., recr. of registrn. dues, comrn., regnr., gages, regnr. of companies, 1920; nom. mem. coun. of govt., May, 1926.

AYLESWORTH, HON. ST ALLEN BRISTOW K.C.M.G. (1911), K.C., B.A. (1874), M.A. (1875).—B. 1854; educ. at Newburgh high school and Toronto univ.; Prince of Wales prize-winner; bencher of law soc. of Upper Canada; called to bar, Q.C. (Ontario); one of H.M.'s commrs. for settlm. of Alaska boundary, 1903; elected to the H. of C. for North Ykt., 1904; postmr.-gen. of Canada, 1905; min. of just., 1906; British ag. internat. fisheries arbitn. at the Hague, 1910; resigned portfolio and retired, 1911; mem., privy coun. of Canada; called to the Senate, 1923.

AYRE, CLAUDE FREDERICK CHARLES—B. 1881; asst. mast., Raffles Inst., Singapore, Oct., 1907; lent to F.M.S. govt., inspr., schls., Jan., 1912; grade II, Jan., 1913; ar. mast., Ras-Ist., Oct., 1915; grade I, and prin. High Sch., Malacca; pol. mag., Aug., 1919; census of Mar., 1921; prin., Anderson Schl., Ipoh, May, 1922; sr. normal instr., Nov., 1924.

AYTON, A. L.—B. 1878; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser. and assigned to science and art dept., Mar., 1899; to C.O. J., 1899; clk. in charge of accnts., Western Pct., high comm., Dec., 1913; ag. sec., W.P.H.C., 2nd Mar., 1915; returned to C.O. as staff clk. in agr. dept., 29th Jan., 1920; ag. astt. prin., 16th Aug., 1920; sec. to "Discovery" comm., 1925; prin., 1st June, 1929.

BER, EDWARD ALFRED.—B. 1888; copyist, hy dept., B. Honduras, 1905; apprentice, 1906; passed exam. in theory of land surveying; 2nd cls. olk., Aug., 1910; ag. 1st cls., 1911; 1st cls. olk., col. sec.'s dept., Nov., 1914; olk. of coun., 1914-15; ag. inspr. of schls., olk. to bd. of educn., Nov., 1915; senr. olk., sec.'s dept., Feb., 1917; ag. ch. olk., etc., various periods, 1917-21; ag. astl. olk. sec., 1921; ag. prov. sec. to Sir Eyre Hutson, Aug., 1922; ch. olk., Apr., 1921; astl. olk., June, 1923.

CKWELL, HENRY FLEMING, B.A. Cantab.—884; ed. at Charterhouse (jun. and sen. shipships), and at King's Coll., Camb.; asst. mast., Portsmouth gram. schl., 1908; ast. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1908; dist. offr., Jan., 1914; dist. offr., 2nd cls., 1917; res., July, 1925.

DDELE, SIR FRANK MORRISH, K.B.E.—Kt. BACH, C.S.G.M. (1925).—M.A., Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 21st wrangler, Math. s., 1896; cadet F.M.S., 1897; called to the Inner Temple, Jan., 1913; recd. thanks wt. in connection with Ulu Selangor riots, 1902; revenue auditor, Pahang, Jan., 1913; ag. offl. assigne, Singapore, Apr., 1917; i. currency commr., May, 1917; dist. Kuantan, June, 1908; supt. govt. monopolies, S. Stilts., Sept., 1909; P.M.G., S. ita., Feb., 1916; food controller, Oct., 1920; sec., S. Stilts., July, 1921; ag. col. sec., Stilts., Apr.-Sept., 1922; May-Oct., 1923 and Sept.-Nov., 1924; ch. sec., Nigeria, Nov., 1924; O.A.G., Nigeria, in 1925, 1927 and 1928-29; dep. for various periods in 1925, 1926 and ret., 1930.
DER, H.E., B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.), star-at-law, dist. offr., Kenya, 1925; sec. audtire advisory comtes., 1932-33; ag. olk., l., 1933.

EZA, JOSHUA ISADORE, M.B., B.Ch., F.R.C.S. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Oxon.).—L.S.T.M.—B. 1890; jr. res. surgn., Bar., gen. hosp., Oct., 1913; med. offr., W. a., May, 1914; war serv., 1914-16; med. and ag. bacteriologist, Trinidad, 1919-22; health offr., Penang, June, 1923; ag. ch. offr., Malacca, Feb., 1924; health offr., S'pore, Mar., 1924; sr. health offr., Kedah, 1929.

GOT, EDWARD.—B. 1892; pol. probnr., S., Nov., 1912; astl. comanr., pol., Oct.; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, Dec., 1918; pol. depot., June, 1920; 1st astl. comanr., gor., June, 1922; astl. comanr., pol., Ipoh, 1925; offr. i/o detectives, Perak, Mar.; astl. comanr., pol., fed. hqrs., Dec., 1929; pol. offr., Pahang, Jan., 1930; ch. pol. offr., Apr., 1930; dep. comanr., pol., F.M.S., 1930; comanr., pol., Trengganu, Nov., 1930.
ILWARD, A.N.—Ast. dist.comanr., Kenya, 1920; junr. sec., native affrs. dept., 1926; sec. to ag. gov., 1927.

IN, ARTHUR HERBERT.—Cik., Sept., 1895; r. deeds, April, 1912; 1st gr. examir., deeds, 1919; ast. regtr., deeds, King Williamstown, 1919; ast. regtr., deeds, Natal, Jan., 1926; regtr., deeds, Cape Town, Oct., 1928; regtr., Natal, Jan., 1932.

IN, JAMES M.C., M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1892; edin. Univ., European mast., S.S. Raffles, S'pore, Jan., 1921; head mast., Geylang English Schl., Jan., 1923; ag. inspr., schls., 1923-28; ag. supt., educn., Kedah, Apr., 1930; senr. educn. offr. (B), Jan., 1932.

BAIN, NORMAN KERR, B.A. Cantab.—B. 1883; cadet, S. S., Jan., 1907; offr. of cls. V., Mar., 1911; mag., Ipoh, Aug., 1921; ch. astl. dist. offr., Kinta, Aug., 1922; pol. mag., Penang, Sept., 1924; offr. 2nd mag., Penang, Sept., 1924; offr. regar., sup. ct., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1925; offr. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, Nov., 1925; cls. IB; dist. offr., Lower Perak, Apr., 1929; mem., Perak state coun., Mar., 1932; ag. res. coun., Malacca, Mar., 1933.

BAKER, ALAN CUSTANCE, M.C., B.A., Oxon.—B. 1886; cadet, S. S., Dec., 1908; attched. to land office, Malacca, Jan., 1909; officiating astl. supt., monopolies, Penang, June, 1919; officiating astl. assigne and dep. regar., sup. ct., Penang, Aug., 1919; capt., Penang volunteers, Nov., 1919; offr., cls. III, Oct., 1920; collr., land rev. and astl. treas., Malacca, Mar., 1923; comanr., trade and cust., Johore, June, 1927; cls. IB; ag. Br. advr., Kedah, Apr., 1932; do., Kelantan, Apr., 1933.

BAKER, ERNEST LAIDLAW.—B. 1880; ed. Australia; olk., col. sec.'s office, Fiji, 1901; shorthand writer, legis. coun., 1906; sec., Albert Park Bd. (conjoint apptnt.), 1908; 1st cls., col. sec.'s office, and clk. to legis. coun., 1909; clk., exec. coun. (conjoint apptnt.), 1910; ag. 2nd astl. col. sec., 1913; dist. comanr., Colo East, 1914; ditto, Ra, 1917; ditto, Savu Savu, 1918; ag. astl. col. sec., 1920; dist. comanr., Lautoka, 1920; ditto, Macuata, 1923; ditto, Rewa, 1928.

BAKER, FRANCOIS HORACE.—B. 1887; ed. Stratford-on-Avon Schl.; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1910; practised in England until 1913 and in Hong Kong, Jan., 1914; on mily. serv., 1914-19; comanr., R. Artillery, 1915; staff capt., 1919; mily. mag., Nablus dist., Apr., 1919; mily. mag., Jerusalem dist., May, 1919; pres., Jerusalem dist. ct., Aug., 1919; also pres., Beerseba dist. ct.

BAKER, HERBERT ARTHUR, D.S.C., D.I.C., F.G.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1885; ed. Univ. Coll. and London Day Training Coll., Univ. of London, 1906-08; B.S. hon.; B. of E. teacher's certif., 1908; M.S., London, 1918; D.S.C., London, 1920; dipl. of membership, Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Technology (D.I.C.), 1920; R.F.C., France, 1915-16; warrant offr., R.N., 1916-19; govt. geologist, Falkland Is., 1920-22; headmaster, Grenada boys secondary schl., 1924.

BAKER, JOHN ERNEST.—B. 1895; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; junr. draughtsman, off. supdt. civ. eng., H.M. dykd., Roysth, 1914-15; R.F.C., France, Mesopotamia, Persia, 1916-20; studied Scale-Hayne Agr. Coll., 1920-24; agrl. offr., Zanzibar, 1925; del., Imp. Agrl. Res. Confece, 1927.

BAKER, ROBERT M. Inst. C.R., M.I. Struct. R., F.P.W.I.—B. 1874; F.M.S. Rly., 1899; Athens water supply, 1906; Kowloon-Canton rly., Hong Kong, 1906; port engrnr., 1926; man. and ch. engrnr., Kowloon-Canton rly., 1927.
BALDWIN, JOSEPH MASON, M.A., D.S.C.—B. 1878; ag. govt. astronomer, Victoria, 1915; govt. astronomer, July, 1919.
BALDWIN, THOMAS HAYWARD, B.A.—B. 1896; ed., Stamford Schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, Oct., 1921.
BALE, EDGAR GEORGE.—B. 1891; ed. King Edward's Schl.; Imp. cust. and excise serv., 1912-21; astl. to comanr. of cust., Kenya and
UGANDA, MAR., 1921; dep. comsrr., cust., April, 1923; ag. comsrr., cust., for various periods, 1924-29.
BALFE, R. S.—B. 1892; cler.-asst., Basutoland, 1912; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1915; war serv., 1915-19; inspr., 1927; dep. asst. comsrr., 1930.

BAFOUR, DOUGLAS HASTINGS.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1911; attd. to Jaffna Kach., Feb., 1911; ag. pol. mag., P. Pedro, May, 1911; extra office asst. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., Nov., 1911; attd. to Trincomalee Kach., May, 1912; ag. office asst. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., July, 1912; pol. mag., Matara, July, 1913; asst. comsrr., excise, Jaffna, 1917; dist. judge, Kegalla, July, 1923; ag. asst. gov't. agt., Mannar, Apr., 1928; dist. judge, Negombo, June, 1925; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, July, 1927; asst. judge, Anuradhapura, Oct., 1929; ag. dist. judge, Jaffna, Sept., 1930.
BAFOUR, STEPHEN FRANCIS.—B., 1905; ed. King's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., 1930; 1st clk., mag., Kowloon, 1932; asst. head, sany. dept., Feb., 1933.
BAFOUR, F. E.—Dep. chief acctnt., G.P.O., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919; ch. acctnt., G.P.O., Kenya, Oct., 1919; ch. acctnt., Kenya-Uganda-Tanganyika post. and tel. service, 1933.

BAMFORD, LIEUT.-COLONEL H.W.M., C.B.E., (1933), O.B.E. (1918); M.C. (1916).—B. 1882; Cape Mounted Riflemen, 1900-1906; S. African War, 1900-1902; Transvaal Mounted Rifles, Zulu Rebellion, 1906; Natal Police, 1907-1912; dep. sheriff, N. Zululand, 1913-1914; adjt., Natal Light Horse, S.A. Rebellion and G.S.W., Africa, 1914-1915; adjt. 2nd S. African Infantry, Senussi Campaign, W.F.F. Egypt (wounded Hazalin Jan. 23rd, 1916); to France with S. African bde., 9th Scottish Div. (wounded Delville Wood, July 15th, 1916); bde. major, 90th infy. bde., France, 1917; comdg. 2nd S. African infy., 1917-1918 (very severely wounded Beaurevoir, Oct. 8th, 1918); service in France includes Somme, Arras, Messines, Ypres, defence of Hazebrouk, Le Cateau; three times mentioned in despatches; O.B.E., M.C., Croix de Guerre avec palme; comsrr. of police and inspr. of prisons, Malta, July, 1919; commdt., constab., N. Territories, G. Coast, July, 1922; inspr.-gen., pol. G. Coast, Aug., 1924.
BANCOFT, EDWARD NATHANIEL, M.C., M.Am.Soc.C.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; lieut., R.E., 1916; France, 1917-18; survey engnr., Nigerian rly., July, 1927; survr., gen., Jamaica, May, 1929.

BANCROFT, OSWALD LAWRENCE.—B. 1888; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1909; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1910; admitted bar, Barbados, 1910; barrister-at-law and solr., sup. ct., Br. Columbia, 1911-24; pol. mag., dist. A, Barbados, 1925; dep. regisr., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1926; crown. coun., N. Rhodesia, 1928; pol. mag. and coroner, Barbados, 1931.

BANNERMAN, CHARLES EDWARD WOOLHOUSE, O.B.E. (1924).—B. 1884; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, Nov., 1913; admitted to Gold Coast bar as solr. and advoc., Jan., 1914; practised before sup. ct., G. Coast up to 31st July, 1919; pol. mag., sup. ct. 1st Aug., 1919; senr. pol. mag. since 1920; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1925-1931.

BARBADOS, BISHOP OF (founded 1824). RIGHT REV. DAVID WILLIAMS BENTLEY.—Ed. Durham Univ.; B.A. (1910), M.A. (1913), D.D. (1919); asst. curate, St. John's, Barrow-in-Furness, 1906-10; do., St. James-the-Less, Rednal, 1910-14; vicar, St. Mary's, Platton, 1914; warden, St. Peter's Coll., Jamaica and suffr. chaplain to bishop, 1917; canon mission; asst. bishop of Jamaica, 1919; bishop of Barrow-in-Furness, 1927.
BARGERY, REV. GEORGE PERRY.—B. 1893; jnr. supt., educ. dept., Nigeria, Aug., 1919; supt., 1920.
BARKER, H. P., D.S.C. (1917).—B. 1898; master mariner, 1919; asst. mariner, mercantile marine; ch. instr., Dublin Sch. Navig. and Seamanship, 1918; port of Iganayi, Oct., 1919; marine supt., 1931.

BARLAS, WILLIAM.—B. 1888; ed. Pietermaritzburg High Sch. and Thomson's Acad., 1906; travelling teacher, Falkland Is., Feb., 1906; gov't schoolmr., June, 1913; asst. teacher, Pietermaritzburg schl., July, 1914; mag., &c., S. Sheland, 1914; jnrn. clk., C.S.O., Apl., 1915; mag., New Is., Mar., 1916; mag., &c., S. tern, Apl., 1918; dep. mag., S. Georgia, Jan., 1919; ag. mag., S. Georgia, on various occasions, 1919; ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., and elk. of comm., S. Georgia, 1922-Feb., 1923; ag. col. postmr. and loc. June, 1926-Feb., 1927; mag., S. Georgia, June, 1928.

BARLEY, JACK CHARLES.—B. 1887; ed. Cambridge Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; 2nd els. hon.; cadet, W. Pacific civ. ser., 1911; attd., res. comsrr.'s off., Apl., 1912; dist. off., Gizo, Nov., 1912; ag. treas. collr. cust., Apl., 1913; passed cadet, 1914; dep. comsrr., July, 1914; dist. off., 1915; ag. res. comsrr., Apl., 1917; dep. comsrr., Fiji, Mar., 1924; judl. comsrr. (temp. provisional), Feb., 1928; 1st dist. off., 1928; chmn., lab. comsrr., 1928; res. comsrr., Gilbert and Elllice Is., Coll., 1934.

BARNARD, SIR FRANK STILLMAN, K.C.V.O. (1918).—B. 1856; attd. ser. of B. Columbia Electric Co. in 1879; subsequently became gen. pres. of the company; promoter, and for many years of the B. Columbia Electric Co.; identified with many other important newts. of H. of C., Canada, 1879-1896; mayor of Victoria, B.C., 1896 and 1897; retired from active business life in 1906; heir of B. Columbia, 1914-1919.

BARNES, ARTHUR CHAPMAN, B.Sc. (Eng.), F.I.O., A.M.I.Ch.E.—B. 1891; ed. Peterborough, Munic. Coll. of Tech., Victoria Univ., Manchester (schol.); census survey dept., K.A.P., Oct., 1914; asst. agr., Apr., 1916; chem. offr., Kenya, 1919; ag. chem. research, May to Aug., 1920; ag. chem. Nigeria, Apr., 1923; asst. dir., agr., Zanzibar, June, 1927; ag. dir., agr., Jan.-Sept., 1928; prov. offl. mem., leg. coun., dir., agr., Fiji, 1929; offl. mem., leg. coun.; chmn., comsrr. committee; chmn., livestock record assn.; dir., 1930; dir., agr. and island chemist, 1933.
BARNES, HAROLD CHARLES VERNON.—B. 1905; ed. Uppingham Schl.; sec., Kenya, July, 1928.

BARNETT, ERNEST ARTHUR.—B. 1890; pay and grtrmr., 1900; sub-insp. constab., 1906; attached to R.L.C. for 1908; inspr., Fiji constab., 1911; higher rank, 1914; asst. adjutant to local forces, 1914; in Fiji Def. Forces, 1914; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab. and sheriff, June to Dec., 1916; J.P. for Fiji, 1916; press censor, 1917; ag. inspr.-gen.

BARROW, JOHN.—B. 1903; ed. Rugby and Caius Coll., Cambridge (B.A., 1925, M.A., 1929) abroad and Univ. Coll., Lond.; cadet, Hong Kong, Jan., 1927; 3rd astt. to sec. for Chinese affrs., Mar., 1929; 4th astt. col. sec., June, 1929; pvtc. sec. to gov., Nov., 1929 and to O.A.G., Feb., 1930; 2nd astt. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1930; pvtc. sec. to O.A.G., 1932; 1st clk. magistracy and mag., 1932; astt. col. sec., 1933; astt. supt., impts. and expts., May, 1933.
BARRY, CHARLES EDWARD, K.C.—B. 1878; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; admitted Inner Temple, 1905; admitted advoc., Pretoria, 1906; judge, sup. ct., 1926.

BARRY, EDWARD.—B. 1896; ed. Mount St. Mary's; served in Army, Apr., 1915-Oct., 1927; expedy. forces, European War, Aug., 1915-Nov., 1918; 3rd K.A.R., Nov., 1922 to Oct., 1927; commr., 3rd gde., Somaliland, Mar., 1928.

BARRY, HON. JEREMIAH HAYES, K.C.—B. 1858; ed. at public schls. of Saint Mary's and Fredericton; mem. of Senate of Univ. of New Brunswick; admitted to the bar, 1882; K.C., 1898; judge, sup. ct., New Brunswick, 1909; King's Bench division, 1913; ch. just., King's Bench divn., 1924.

BARRY, RICHARD JOHN.—B. 1876; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; clk. C.S.O., Cape, 1895; C.S. Law, 1899; clk., mag. dept., 1901; astt. mag., various places; relief staff, 1904; clk., master's office, Cape (insolv. brch.), 1906; Natal, 1912; Transvaal, 1912; master, sup. ct., Natal, 1915; ditto, Cape, 1923.

BARTH, SIR JACOB WILLIAM, KT. BACH.—(1922), C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Wadham Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1896; M.A., 1898; barrister-at-law, Mid. Tem., 1900; regis. and prin. regis. of documents, E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1902; Crown advoc., Oct., 1902; apptd. to serve on land comm., Oct., 1904; judge, high ct., E. Africa Prot., April, 1906; atty.-gen., E.A.P., Feb., 1914; ag. chf. sec., Aug., 1918; ch. just., Kenya, Aug., 1920; ret. 1934.

BARTLEY, THOMAS DOUGLAS MURRAY.—B.A. Trin. Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1925; war serv., Aug., 1914-Jan., 1920; admistr. offr. (cadet) Tanganyika Territory, May 1920; astt. sec., May, 1922; ag. senr. astt. sec., Sep. to Nov., 1923; astt. admstr. gen. and astt. regis. of companies, June, 1924 and Nov., 1925 to Jan., 1926; res. mag., Oct., 1925; ag. admstr.-gen., ag. regis.-gen., births, deaths and marriages, ag. regis., companies, ag. liquidator German savings bank, ag. regis., trade marks, Jan. to Aug., 1926.

BARTLEY, WM., M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1885; B.A., Dublin; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1908; passed cadet, July, 1910; offr. of cls. V., Jan., 1912; passed in Javanese, Feb., 1912; dist. offr., Dindings, May, 1912; astt. regis., impts. and expts., S.S., Dec., 1915; sec., food control comtec., June, 1917; mem., shipping comtec., Apr., 1918; sec., food contrlr's. comtec., Dec., 1918; agent, food controller, Singapore, June, 1919; services recog. by S. of S., Aug., 1919; sec., profiteering comm., 1920; supernmy. offr., cls. III., Nov., 1920; sec. to advising comtec. on allowances, in addition, Dec., 1920; astt. adviser, Kelantan, Apr., 1922; sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1926; cls. I.A.; under-secr., S.S., 1930; ag. col. sec., S.S., May to June and Aug. to Sept., 1930; pres., mun. commrs., Singapore, Mar., 1931; supernmy. offr., cls. I.A., S.S., 1932.

BARTOLO, PROF. SIR AUGUSTO, KT. BACH.—(1929), LL.D., B.Lit., F.R.H.S.—B. 1883; matric., Malta Univ., 1901; B.Lit., 1904; called to bar, 1907; edr. for many years of "Daily Malta Chronicle"; prof., hist. of legislation and examr. in law and lit., Malta Univ.; chmn., Malta branch, Empire Parly. Assocn.; Malta Univ. del. at congress of Univ. of Empire in Gt. Britain, 1921; el. for 2nd divn. in leg. assem., Oct., 1921; re-el., June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for educn. and migrn., Aug., 1927; author of several works on Maltese hist. and Imp. affrs.

BARTON, A.E.V., O.B.E. (1933).—Imp. cust. serv., 1912; cust. asst., Kenya, Sept., 1919; collr. of cust., Apr., 1923; comptlr., cust., B. Guiana; collr., gen., Jamaica, 1927; collr., cust., Trinidad, June, 1929.

BARTON, C.J.J.T., O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1891; ed. Denstone and Downing Coll., Cambridge; history and law tripeses; B.A., 1913; M.A., 1920; asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Aug., 1914; ag. dist. commr., 1917, 1919, 1921; asst. for native affrs., 1923; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1924-26; ag. asst. col. sec., 1927; ag. prin. asst. col. sec. and clk. to exec. coun. on various occasions, 1928-33; senr. asst. col. sec., 1933; author various papers on Kenya tribes and local history.

BARTON, CECIL MOLYNEUX, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1883; ed. Shrewsbury and Trinity Coll., Dublin (hons., classics, law); barrister-at-law, King's Inns, Dublin, 1906; asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., 1913; res. mag., 1914-21; ag. judge, sup. ct., Apr., 1921 to Oct., 1922; legal adviser, Gambia, Dec., 1922; commr. to enquire into double inoculation of cattle, 1918; chmn. of comm. to enquire into inoculation of cattle for rinderpest by vetv. dept., 1919; ag. judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1923-28; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; circuit judge, Gold Coast, 1929.

BARTON, GEORGE SAMUEL HORACE, B.S.A., D.Sc.A.—B. 1883; ed. Vankleek Hill High Sch., Ontario Agrl. Coll., Toronto Univ. (B.S.A.), Laval Univ. (D.Sc.A.); asst., animal husbandry, 1907; lecturer, 1910; prof., 1911; dean, faculty of agr., Macdonald Coll., McGill Univ., 1925; comdr., Agricole Merite, Quebec govt., 1929; dep. min., agr., Canada, 1932.

BARTON, MAJOR P.F., V.D.—Asst. treas., G. Coast, Mar., 1907; attd. col. sec.'s office, Oct.-Nov., 1907 and Sept.-Nov., 1910; senr. asst. treas., Nov., 1908; ch. asst. treas., Apr., 1917; ag. treas. and currency offr., July-Oct., 1919 and Dec., 1920 to Aug., 1921; dep. treas., Jan., 1920; commanded G. Coast Volunteers, 1914 and during War; ment. in deaps., Aug., 1917; col. treas. and currency offr., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1922; treas., Dominica, 1932; treas. and collr., cust., St. Helena, Mar., 1933.
BARTON, T., M.A.—B. 1904; qualified teacher Scottish educn. dept.; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Oct., 1928.

BARWELL, HON. SIR HENRY NEWMAN, K.C.M.G. (1922), LL.B. (Adelaide Univ.), Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1877; ed. St. Peter's Coll., Adelaide; mem., H. of A., S. Australia, Stanley dist., 1915-25; atty.-gen., 1917 and 1918-20; premier and atty.-gen., 1920-24; fedl. Senator, 1925; agt. gen. for S. Australia, 1928.

BASSETT, JOHN HAROLD, Barrister-at-law, Middle Temple.—B. 1896; ed. Wallacey and Glasgow Univ.; war serv., 1914-19; priv. sec., dep. contrlr. (organisation), Min. of Lab., 1919-20; cadet, F.M.S., May, 1921; offr., cls. V, Oct., 1923; offr., cls. IV, Oct., 1927; offr., cls. III, Oct., 1932; dep. pub. prov., Spore, Apr., 1933.

BASSETT, RALPH HENRY.—B. 1896; on milly. serv., Sept., 1914; cmdt., Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1920; attd. to Ratnapura Koch., Jan., 1921; pol. mag., Jaffna, May, 1922; asst. sttlmt. of July, 1925; pol. mag., Colombo, June, 1930.
BATEMAN, G.S., L.D.S., R.S.C., Engr. Serv. with Forces in E. Africa, 1915 and 1916; dental surg., Uganda, May, 1912.

BATES, ISAAC GRAINGER.—B. 1886; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1909; equity bar, 1909-13; mag. and legal adviser, Br. Solomon Is. Prs. with jurisdiction as a judi. commr. for the Western Pacific, Nov., 1913; ag. res. commr. in addn. during 1919; seconded for serv. as ch. pol. mag. Feb., May, 1920 to Dec., 1921; senr. mag., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1922; ag. judge, high ct., various periods, 1924-33; ag. solr.-gen., Jul. to Oct., 1927.

BATHO, CHARLES PHILIP ARTHUR, M.I.E. (1920).—B. 1883; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey and Victoria Coll., Jersey; control and acc. office, Cape Colony, 1900; sec., tender bd., 1901; transf. Transvaal prisons dept., July, 1902; pr. clk., July, 1910; pr. clk., Union prisons dept., Nov., 1912; ch. clk., Nov., 1917; asst. dir., pres., Apr., 1926; is also commr. of oaths for Transvaal gaol and outstations and warden, Baviaanspoort farm col. and inebriate reformatory, S. Afr., 1930.

BATTERBEE, SIR HARRY F., K.C.M.G. (1922), K.C.V.O. (1927), C.M.G. (1918), C.V.O. (1913), B. 1880; ed. at Grammar Sch., Faversham and at Oxford; classical scholar, Hertford Coll., 1899-1904; 1st cls. class. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. math. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1904; apptd. after compet. exam. 2nd cls. clk., exchequer and audit dept., Jan., 1906; 2nd cls. clk., May, 1905; agt. 1st cls. clk., 4th June, 1916; cls. clk., 6th July, 1917; sec. to W. African lands commr., 1912; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewontin (now Lord) Harcourt, 9th Dec., 1912; priv. sec. to Lord Islington, 12th Oct., 1914; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, 31st May, 1915; priv. sec. to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916 to 13th Jan., 1919; priv. 1st Apr., 1920; agt. asst. sec., 1st Mar., 1923; polit. sec. to Adm. commdg. ap. serv. squadron during Empire cruise, 1923-24; asst. sec., 12th Dec., 1924; polit. sec. to H.R.H. the Duke of York during his visit to Australia and N.Z., 1927; asst. under sec. state for dom. affrs. 25th Jan., 1930; dep. sec. Imp. Conf., 1930; regier., Order of St. Michael and St. George since 1930.

BATTERSHILL, WILLIAM DENIS.—B. 1890; on milly. serv., Aug., 1914-19; cadet Ceylon, 1920; attd. to Colombo cust., May, 1921; attd. to Badulla Koch., Jan., 1921; pol. mag., Panay, Oct., 1922; dep. fiscal, Colombo, June, 1922; addnl. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1925; 4th asst. col. sec., Mar., 1925; 3rd asst. col. sec., May, 1927; 2nd asst. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun. May, 1928; asst. sec., Jamaica, 1928; agt. col. sec. on various occasions, 1929-32.

BATTISCOMBE, CHRISTOPHER FRANCIS, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1890; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Haileybury Coll.; school, Lincoln, Oxford; B.A., (hons.) 1914; asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., 1914; served with R. Berks Regt., Grenadier Guards during European War; rec. in col. serv. cancelled on acct. of wounds, 1914; re-enl. col. serv. as headmr., Machakos mts. schl. and inspr. of schls., Ukambani prov., E. Afr., 1921; tutor to Prince Sevaid Abdulla, son of the sultan of Zanzibar, 1922; sec. to schl. dominions office and colonial office list.

VERSTOCK, WILLIAM CHARLES BEST, or, K.R.R.C.—Ed. Wimborne Grammar and Milton Abbas Grammar Schh., Bland İştevewe, Pacific Phosphate Co. (Br. phosphate Coman.), 1905; served, Great War’s Own Dorset Yeomanry, Dorset Regt. and R.C., Aug., 1914 - July, 1919; min. of iations, 1919; dist. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., Jan., 1930; dep. comanvr., W. P. high., 1920.

VIN, HON. Sir THOMAS RAINSFORD, M.G. (1933), K.C.B., Ll.B.—B. 1874; Auckland, N.Z. and at Sydney Univ.; called bar, 1897; prof. of law, Univ. of Tasmania, 1898; to prime min. C. of A., 1901-4; A., N.S.W., from 1917; premier and coi.ner, N.S.W., 1927-30.

XTTER, JOHN BABINGTON MACAULEY, K.C., L.—Elected to New Brunswick Legislature, 1886; atty-gen., 1914; leader of provincial union, 1920; min. of cust. in Dom. gov’t., 1911; e. min., New Brunswick, 1925; judge, sup. N.B., 1931.

YES, HAROLD NEWTON, M.I.R.S.E.—B.; signal inspir., Burma, May, 1907-June, rly. and signal engr., Australia, Apr., 1909—Sept., 1913; signal and tel. inspir., constrn., F.M.S. Rlys., Sept., 1913; ast. signal r., F.M.S. Rlys., 1917; district signal engnr., II, Jan., 1922; grade I, Jan., 1926; dist. signal engnr., Tampin, Apr., 1926; dist. signal tel. engnr., Gemas, Aug., 1928.

YERS, HERBERT LAUNCELOT—B. 1886; ed. vich Coll.; clk., O.A.’s office, 1905; clk., serv., Nyasaland, 1909; P.W.D., 1910; Island Field Force, 1914; asst. finan. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1916-17; ast. treas., zibar, 1921; caf. treas., in 1921, 1922, 1923, and 1926; dep. treas., Kenya, Mar., 1924; treas., Trinidad and chmn., agrl. bank, June, 1924; treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1933.

AYNIS, EDWARD WILLIAM, C.B. (1928), E. (1920).—B. 1880; clk., G.P.O., Antigua, 1889; clk. to comanr., Virgin Islds., Jan., 1891; priv. sec. and clk. to adminatr., Dominica, 1899; transfd. to Impl. civ. ser. and apptd. ofcl. clk., National Health Insurance Comm. (Ireland), June, 1912; 1st cls. clk., May, 1913; sec. to sec. ministry of shipping, Jan., 1919; sec., National Health Insurance Joint tee., Oct., 1919; priv. sec. to partly, sec. ministry of shipping, Apr., 1920 to Mar., 1921; g. clk., Min. of Health, Oct., 1920; sec., “covery” comtes., C.O., July, 1923; col. sec., ward Is., Oct., 1925; admtd. govt. of gua on numerous occasions; ag. gov. Leeward Apr. to Sept., 1930 and May-Oct., 1932.

AYNES, THOMAS EDWIN PREWOOL, O.B.E. (2)—B. 1884; ed. St. Kitts Grammar and Queen’s Royal Coll., Trinidad; cal ast., col. sec’s, off., Trinidad, 1903; 6th C.S.O., 1903; 5th klk., C.S.O., 1906; 1st klk., office, Antigua, 1907; 3rd klk., C.S.O., 1911; clk. to admintr., Dominica, 1912; 2nd klk., C.O., clk. of exec. and legis. couns., Antigua, supt. of govt. printing office, 1912; clk. to str., and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., antica, 1919; offr. in ch. (later, warden), N. dist., 1924; temp. mem., leg. coun., Dominica, Feb., 1926; O.A.G., Montserrat, in Mar., 1926 and Apr., 1928; O.A.G., Virgin Is., Aug. to Nov., 1926; offr. mem., leg. coun., Dominica in 1927 and 1928; ag. ast. col. sec., Leeward Is., Apr.-Sept., 1927; treas., Dominica, June, 1928; offr. mem., exec. coun., Dominica, June, 1928; O.A.G., Montserrat, June-Sept., 1929; O.A.G., Dominica on three occasions in 1929, 1930 and 1931; comm’r., Montserrat, 1932.

BAZELL, C.—B. 1888; ed. Christ’s Hosp. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar 1904-08); B.A. 1908 (2nd hon. mods. 2nd lit. hum.); ast. mast., Raffles Inst., Singapore, 1914-17 and 1922; ag. headmast., Malay Coll., Kuala Kangsar, 1923.

BEAL, REAR-ADM. ALISTAIR FRANCIS, C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1919), R.N.—B. 1875, ed. H.M.S. “Britannia,” Dartmouth; comdr., H.M.S. “Princeps Royal,” 1912-15; capt., 1915; served on Adm’ty. war staff, 1915-18; in command H.M.S. “Weymouth,” 1918-19 and 1920-21; dep. dir., operations divn., Adm’ty. naval staff, 1921-23; commodore commdng. N.Z. squadron and naval adviser to N.Z. Gov’t, 1923-26; offr., Legion of Honour, 1919; Italian Silver Med. for mil’y. valour, 1918.

BEARDMORE, ERNEST.—B. 1882; ed. Manchester and Cape Town; B.A. (1907); LL.B. (1911); clk., Cape civil serv., 1900; prof. ast. and ch. clk., solr.-gen., Cape, 1920; solr.-gen., Cape, 1931; crown procs., Witwatersrand, 1933.

BEATLEY, FRANK EDWARD MANEMUS.—B. 1882; ed. Lucton Grammar Schh.; traffic supt., Mauritius govt. rly., 1914; dep. traffic man., Sierra Leone rly., Jan., 1925; traffic man., 1930; gen. man. and traffic man., 1933.

BRATTIE, LEUT.-COL. ALEXANDER ELDER, C.M.G. (1933), C.B.E. (1920), M.C. (1915), ret. pay (res. of offrs.)—Ent’d. Army (Argyll and Sutherland Hi’rsh.), 1906; transtp. on promotion to the Queen’s (now The Queen’s Royal) Regt., 1908; served in India and India; seconded for serv. with W.A.F.F., 1911; ast. adjtnt, Southern Nigeria Regt., 1912; ag. regt. adjtnt, S.N. Regt., 1913; adjtnt, 4th Bn. Nigeria Regt., 1914; capt., 1914; Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914; staff offr. to various field columns, 1914-15; ment. in des., 1914; M.C., 1915; severely wounded, 1915; ast. staff offr., W.A.F.F., Col. Office, 1915; staff offr. (gen. staff offr.), W.A.F.F., 1916; temp. major, 1916; offg. staff offr., K.A.R., 1915; promoted major by brevet, 1918; temp. lieut. col., 1918; C.B.E., 1920; accompanied inspr.-gen., W.A.F.F., on tour of inspection, Nigeria, Gold Coast, Sierra Leone and Gambia, 1920; C.O. rep. on various inter-deptl. comtess., 1915-21; recd. thanks of S. of S. for services under the C.O.; ast. sec., Gibraltar, Sept., 1922; recd. instr. of schls., Oct., 1922; clk. of exec. coun., Oct., 1922; sec., bd. of educ’n. (ex officio), 1922; ag. D.P.W. on two occasions; col. sec., Falkland Is., 8th Oct., 1929; ag. gov. from May, 1936 to Apr., 17, 1927; ch. astt. col. sec., Cyprus, 8th Aug., 1927; ag. col. sec., on various occasions, 1927-29; col. sec., Gibraltar J., 1930.
BEATTIE, JAMES, O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1891; European War, 1914-18; Somaliland Camel Corps., 1918-20; M.C., 1920; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, 1920; dist. comanr., 1922; comdt., pol., Oct., 1928.

BEATTIE, SIR JOHN CARRUTHERS, K.T. (1920), C.R.S.E. and S.A., D.Sc., LL.D. (Ed. and Wits.).—B. 1866; ed. Moray House, Edinburgh Univ.; Vans Dunlop schl. in Physics, 1851 Exhibn. Schl., Univs. of Munich, Vienna, Berlin and Glasgow; prof. of physics, S. African Coll., 1897; medallist S. African Assocn. for Advancement of Science, 1910; crossed Africa from Cape Town to Cairo with Prof. J. T. Morrison on magnetic survey; chmn., survey comm., 1921; pres., S. African Assocn. for Advancement of Science, 1928; prin., S. African Coll., 1917-18; prin. and vice-chano., Univ. of Cape Town since 1918; prin. publns.: rept. of a Magnetic Survey of S. Africa, Cambridge, 1909, and (in conjunction with Prof. J. T. Morrison) "On Magnetic Work in Southern and Central Africa," Washington, 1913.

BEATTY, SIR KENNETH JAMES, KT. BACH. (1926).—B. 1878; ed. Univ. High Schl. and Melbourne Univ.; qualified Victorian bar, 1900; Transvaal law certif. (including Roman Dutch law), 1904; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1908; served in S. African war as lieut., V.M.I., wounded in action (Queen's med. and 4 clasps); apptd. pub. pros., Transvaal, Nov., 1902; addtnl. asst. res. mag., Mar., 1903; asst. res. mag., Dec., 1905; attd. Natal Carabiners, Natal rebellion, 1906; recd. thanks of Natal govt., med. and clasp; pol. mag., master of sup. ct. and regisr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Sept., 1908; has acted as ch. just., circuit judge and atty.-gen., Sierra Leone; on mily. serv., 1915-18; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1921; ch. just., Bermuda, 1924; ch. just., Bahamas, 1927; dep. gov. in 1928; ch. just., Gibraltar, 1931.

BEAUBRUN, IGNATIUS CECIL, B. 1889; treas. clk., St. Lucia, 1906; asst. audit clk., 1907; rev. offr., St. Vincent, 1912; ag. audit clk., 1910 and 1912; rev. offr., Grenada, 1915; 3rd treasy. clk., 1916; ch. clk., treasy. and commr. of income tax, 1922; asst. treasy., 1929; col. treasy., collr. of cust., aspt. of inld. rev., ch. of excise, Grenada, June, 1930; ag. col. sec. and regisr.-gen., June, 1932.

BEAUCHAMP, 7th Earl (creat. 1815), VISC. ELMLEY (1815), BARON BEAUCHAMP (1806, U.K.); K.G.; K.C.M.G. (1899); WILLIAM LYON: B. 1872; succeeded his father, 1891.—Ed. Eton and Chr. Ch., Oxford; D.L. Worcestershire; Mayor of Worcester, 1895-6; mem. London schl. bd., 1897-9; gov. N.S.W., 1899 to 1900; capt. of hon. corps of Gentlemen-at-Arms, 1906; first commr. of works, 1911-14; lord-pres. of the coun., 1910-15.

BEAUCHAMP, ARTHUR KENNETH.—B. 1888; ed. Worcester Cath., King's Schl. and Christ Church, Oxford, B.A., 3rd cls. math. hons., 1910; headmtr., gram. schl. and sub-inspr., schl., Dominica, Sept., 1912; ag. inspr., schl., Leeward Is., July-Oct., 1916; inspr., schl., St. Lucia, Jan., 1921; aspt., educn., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1926.

BEAUCHESNE, ARTHUR, M.A., K.C., LL.D., Litt.D., F.R.S.C.—B.1876; ed. St. Joseph's (class. course), Memramcook, N.B.; grad. as B.A., M.A. and Litt.D. valedictorian, 1895; studied law, Laval, Montreal; admitted to bar, Quebec, 1904; K.C., 1914; legal adviser, justice dept., 1913-16; clk. asst., House of Commons, Canada, and commr. to admstr. oath of allegiance to M. of P.; clk., House of Commons, 1925; author of "Beauchesne's Parliamentary Rules and Forms," "Ecrivains d'Autrefois," and of several lectures and pamphlets.

BECK, WALD EGERTON HERBERT.—B. 1894; ed. Weymouth Coll., Dorset; pol. prolbnt., F.M.S., Oct., 1914; 1st. asst. commr. pol., Selangor, Feb., 1923; aspt., convict estabt., Taiping and imp., prisons, F.M.S., Sept., 1923; offr. l/c, pol. dist., Kuantan, Aug., 1927; comdt. pol., depot, K. Lumpur, Mar., 1929; aspt., pol. S.S., Oct., 1931; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, Feb., 1932.

BECKETT, HAROLD.—B. 1891; ed. at Monmouth Grammar Schl. and Oxford; exhibitioner at Wadham Coll., 1910-1914; also Symons exhibitioner, 1912-1914; 1st cls. Class. Moda., 1915; 3rd cls. Lit. Hum., 1914; B.A., 1914; appd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 3rd Nov., 1914; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Milner, 14th Jan. to 16th Mar., 1919; ag. 1st cls. clk., 17th July, 1919; priv. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir G. Fiddes), 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; mem., W. Indies currency commte., 1923; sent x Mauritius as aspt. commr. to enquire into acc. rept. to S. of S. on questions of currency and exchange, Apr., 1925; returned to C.O., Oct., 1925; ag. ast. sec., 28th Sept., 1931.

BECKETT, HERBERT JAMES RUST, A.C.A.—B. 1885; treasy., N. Borneo, Feb., 1911; dist. treasy., Apl., 1911; mag., 1st cls., Apl., 1914; seconded for mil. serv., 1916-19; dist. offr., Aug., 1919; asst. commr., out. and excise, Mar., 1921; asst. commr., excise, Feb., 1926; auditor, Mar., 1929; finance contr., Dec., 1930.

BEDINGTON, GUY CYRIL, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.C.E., F.S.I., M.R.S.I., Chartered C. Engr., and Chartered Surv.—B. 1889; ast. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., July, 1913; exc. engr., Sept., 1923; ag. sr. exec. engr., Mar., 1927; attd. hydraulic br., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1929; senr. exec. engr., P.W.D., S.S. and F.M.S., 1934.

BEECHING, HERBERT.—Seconded from Basutoland service; apptd. ag. sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. pol., Dec., 1920; ag. ast. res. mag., 1921; ag. res. mag., 1923; passed Sri grade Seowana exam., 1926; sub. inspr., Bechuanaland prot., July, 1927; hon. rankinspr., Nov., 1927.
BEGBIE, RALPH STOCKMAN, M.D., D.P.H., D.T.M. and H. (Edin.).—Health offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1930; ag. malariologist, 1931; ast. bacteriologist, 1932.

BELAND, HON. HENRI SEVERIN.—B. 1899; ed. Three Rivers (Que.) Coll. (B.A.), Laval Univ. (M.D.); physician; mayor of St. Joseph, 1916; el. to leg. ass., Quebec, 1897 and 1900; el. to H.C. bye-el., 1902; re-el., 1904, 1908, 1911, 1914 and 1921; postmr.-gen. in Sir Wilfrid Laurier govt., Aug. 19th until resignation of govt., Oct., 1911; served with the Belgian forces in the early part of the war, and made prisoner of war; m. of soldiers' civ. recl. and health in Kitas admin., Dec., 1921; called to senate, 1925.

BELCHER, SIR CHARLES FREDERIC, K.T., B.A. (1931), O.B.E. (1923), M.B.E. (1919), M.A., LL.B.—B. 1876; ed. at Geelong Gratt. Schl. and Trin. Coll., Melbourne (final scholarship in classics and comp. philology, Shakespeare scholarship); admitted to practise, Victoria, Oct., 1902; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, June, 1909 (cert. of honour); conveyancer, Uganda Prot., July, 1914; custodian of enemy property, Uganda, Apr., 1915; mag., Uganda, June, 1915; asst. judge, Zanzibar, June, 1920; judge of H.B.M. ct. of appeal for E. Africa, Aug., 1920 and June, 1925; atty. gen., Nyassaland, Sept., 1920; req. exec. and legis. couns.; ag. judge, high ct., May, 1921 to Jan., 1922; judge, high ct., May, 1924; asst. just., sup. ct., Cyprus, May, 1927; ch. jus., Trinidad, 1930.

BELGRAVE, WILLIAM NORMAN CUMMING, B.A. (Cantab.), Camb. Univ. Dip. of Ag.—B. 1891; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; ast. mycologist, ag. dept., F.M.S., Oct., 1914; plant physiologist, Jan., 1920; B. 1891; ch., divn. of soils and plant physiology, Feb., 1927; deleg. to internat. soc. of soil science, Konigsberg, 1929; deleg., 2nd imp. mycological confere., London, 1929; asst. to dir., in addn., June, 1930; in ch., divn. of agr. chem., in addn., Sept., 1930; ch. research offr., Sept., 1931.

BELL, HON. LIEUT.-COL. EDWARD, O.B.E. (1924), M.B.E. (1918), V.D.—Served in R.I.C., Dec., 1885 to Aug., 1896; attached 2nd Batt., Durham Light Infantry, 1896; Hythe certif. of musketry and instr.'s certif., Maxim machine gun; drill instr. to local force, St. Kitts-Nevis, 12th Aug., 1896; ag. inspr., L. Ildsa. police and adjut., St. Kitts-Nevis defence force, 1898; inspr., L. Ildsa. police, Sept., 1898; and capt. and adjut. defence force; ag. inspr.-gen., L. Ildsa. police, and ag. inspecting offr. of defence forces of colony, Apr. to Nov., 1903; ag. inspr.-gen., L. Ildsa. police, 1st Apr., 1905, subsequently confirmed in commd. of force as chief inspr.; comdt. local forces, with local rank of lieut.-col.; extra A.D.C. to gov. Sir Bickham Escott, 1912; mem. exec. coun., Leeward Is., 1916; police medal, 1915; recd. thanks of H.M.'s Govt., for services rendered during riot and disturbances in Antigua in March, 1918; is a J.P.

BELL, EDWARD PETER STUBBS.—B. 1902; 4th clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, 1920; snr. elk., 1927; clk. to atty. gen., 1927; ag. priv. sec. to offr. adm. govt., Leeward Is., April-Sept., 1924 and June-Oct., 1927; elk., ex. and leg. couns., Antigua, 1927 prte. sec. to gov., Leeward Is., June, 1929 to Apr., 1930; passed bar final exam., 1931; seconded for duty in connec. with relief measures, Antigua, June-Nov., 1931; mag. dist. F and afterwards dist. E. Dominica, Nov., 1931.

BELL, Rt. Hon. Sir Francis Henry Dillon, P.C. (1926), G.C.M.G. (1923), K.C.M.G. (1915), K.C.—B. 1851; B.A.; Camb.; barrister, Middle Temple, 1874; entd. New Zealand parl., 1893; leader of legis. coun., 1912-26 and 1927-28; min. of internal affairs, 1912 and 1920; min. of immigr. in National ministry, 1915; also leader of legis. coun.; atty.-gen., 1918-26; min. of educn., min. of public health and min. of immigr., 1919-20; commnr. of state forests, 1919-22; min. of marine, 1921-22; min. of just., Jan. to June, 1923-26; min. of external affrs., 1923; prime min., 14th-30th May, 1925; mem. of exec. coun. without portfolio, 1926-28; min. of marine, 1928; ret. from office, 1928.

BELL, GEORGE FREDERICK.—Cape civ. ser., 8th Feb., 1902; regis. of native lab., Transvaal, 1910; pub. pros., native affrs. ct., Johannesburg, 1913; S. African Expedy. Force, Dec., 1915; polit. offr. Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1916; admstr. offr., 1st grade, 1923.

BELL, MAJOR G. H., M.C., Officer Legion d'Honneur.—B. 1885; entered army, 1906; India, 1907-9; Nigeria, W.A.F.F., 1911; asst. dir. of surveys, Nigeria, 1912-14; served in France, 1914-16; Palestine and Syria, 1917-18; dep. ch. polit. offr., Egyptian Expedy. Force, 1919; G.S.O., Intelligence, E.E.F., 1920; dir. topographical branch, Gold Coast Survey, 1920.

BELL, Sir Henry Hesketh Joupon, G.C.M.G. (1925), K.C.M.G. (1908), C.M.G. (1903).—B. 1865; ed. in Paris; 3rd elk. gov.-in-chief's office, Barbados, May, 1882; transferred to treas. dept., Grenada, 1883; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Jan., 1890; sen. ast. treas., G. Coast, Aug., 1891; ag. dist. commnr. and sheriff, Accra, 1892; ag. col. treas., 1893; rec-gen., Bahamas, Dec., 1894; represented Harbour Isld. in House of Assam., 1896-6; ag. col. sec., July to Oct., 1897, Apr. to Nov., 1898; admstr. of Dominica, Aug., 1899; ag. gov., Leeward Is., Aug., 1904, and from June, 1905; commnr. and commr.-in-chief, Uganda Prot., Jan., 1906; gov., ditto, Oct., 1907; gov., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1909; gov. Leeward Is., 1912; gov., Mauritius, Jan., 1916; assumed govt., May, 1916; author of "Geography of the Gold Coast," "Obeah," etc.
BELL, P. S., M.B., B.S., M.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1897; mily. serv., 1915-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1926.

BELLANTI, J. J., LL.D., Malta Univ., 1910.—B. 1888; ed. St. Ignatius' Coll., Malta; matric. (3rd place in homs. list), Malta Univ., 1902; clk., civil serv., Malta, 1907; mem. of coun. of govt., 1911; ast. crown advocate, Gozo, 1914; assessor of taxes, Malta treasy., Apr., 1923.

BELLETH, BENJAMIN, I.S.O. (1926).—B. 1875; ag. office asst., Kalutara Kach., Ceylon, July, 1918; apptd. to cls. V. of civ. serv., July, 1919; extra office asst. to the col. sec., Aug., 1920; off. ast. to col. sec., Apr., 1925; cls. IV., June, 1927; ast. col. sec., Sept., 1929; ast. contr., estab. lishments, July, 1931.
BEMINSTER, FREDERICK ARBON.—B. 1885; tech. instr., N. Provs., Nigeria, 1914; supt., arts and crafts schls., 1920; inspr., 1930.

BENHAM, JOHN ELLIOTT.—B. 1886; major, 3rd E. Lancs. Regt.; war service, German and Portuguese E. Africa, Mar., 1917 to Dec., 1918; ast. dist. commnr., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1914; dist. commnr., Aug., 1922; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1925; J.P., 1926; ag. senr. ast. col. sec. and elk., exec. coun. on many occasions; ag. col. sec. in Mar. and June, 1928 and Sept. and Oct., 1930; gov't. dep. on two occasions.

BENJAMIN, JOHN.—B. 1888; ed. Christ's Hosp., Lond. and Horsham; Br. P.O., Oct., 1903-Dec., 1913; postal elk. and telegraphist, Sept., 1913; ast. ch. acctnt., G.P.O., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1918; 1st cls. patmstr., Kenya, Apr., 1920; dep. ch. acctnt., June, 1924; ast. P.M.G., July, 1931; sr. acctnt., S.S. and F.M.S., Aug., 1932.

BENNETT, HON. SIR JOHN ROBERT, K.B.E. (1916).—M.H.A., St. John's West, Newfild., 1904; M.E.C., 1913; col. sec., 1913; mem., defence comte., 1915; deputy chief censor, telegraphs and cables, 1914; press censor, 1915; min. of militia, 1917.

BENNETT, Rt. Hon. RICHARD BEDFORD, P.C. (1930), P.C. (Can.), LL.B., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. pub. and high schls., N.B., Dalhousie Univ., N.S.; hon. degree LL.D., Dalhousie Univ., Queen's Univ. and Univ. of Alberta; admitted to bar of N.B., 1903; mem. legis. assem., Northwest Territories, 1898-1905; legis. assem. of Alta., 1909; rep.
