Calgary in H. of C., Canada, 1911-17; min. of just. and atty.-gen., 1921; min. of finance in Meighen adminstr., 1928; leader of conserv. party, Oct., 1927; prime min. of Canada after g.e., June, 1930.

BENNETT, SYDNEY CHRISTMAS, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.Inst. Mun. and Cty. Engrne.—Ed. Sherborne Schl.; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., E.A.F., 1914-15; pioneer, E. A. Pioneers, 1915-16; 2nd lieut., E.A. Rly. Corps., 1916-17; lt., 1917-18; ment. in depts., "1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory meds.; exec. engrnr., Kenya, 1921.

BENNETTS, ALBERT THOMAS.—B. 1890; ed. Camborne Schl. of Mines, 1908-11; medallist of the Royal Cornwall Polytechnic Soc'y., 1910; 1st cls. hons. and silver medallist, City and Guilds of London Inst., 1911; 1st cls., diploma, Camborne Schol. of Mines, 1911; mines dept., F.M.S., 1912-26; ast. ward., mines, Perak, Nov., 1929; senr. inspr., mines, Jan., 1931.

BENSON, GEORGE ALLEN RALSTON.—B. 1879; 6th cls. offr., cust., Br. Guiana, 1901; 5th cls., 1904; 4th cls., 1911; 3rd cls., 1915; 2nd cls., 1924; 1st cls., 1925; ast. survr., cust., 1926; survr., cust., Br. Guiana, 1927.

BENSON, SIR J. HAWTREY, KT. BACH (1912), A.B., M.D., F.R.C.P. Irel.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; 1st hon. maths, graduated, 1864; consulting physician to Royal City of Dublin Hosp., and to the Monkstown Hosp. Fellow of Acad. of Med., Ireland; late censor and examr., Royal Coll. of Phys., Ireland, and late external examr. in med., Dub. Univ.; is now med. advsr. for Ireland to C.O.; author of numerous papers and articles on technical med. subjects; ret., 1926.

BENTINCK, LILLIE JOSEPHINE, M.B., B.Ch., certif. L.S.T.M.—Lady med. offr., Batu Gajah, May, 1921; Ipoh, July, 1921; K. Pilah, July, 1923; Taiping, Apr., 1925; K. Kangsar, June, 1925; lady med. offr., infant welfare centre, Taiping, Aug., 1925; Seremban, July, 1926; K. Lumpur, Sept., 1927; Ipoh, Nov., 1927.

BENTINCK, MAJOR WALTER GUY, BARON, C.M.G. (1912), D.S.O. (1902).—Ed. at Marlborough Coll., Jens Univ., Germany, and Royal Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; joined Rifle Brig., 1885; capt., 1894; major, 1902; ret., 1905; intelligence dept., W.O., 1899; served throughout S. African war, 1899-1902; (ment. in desps., Queen's medal with six clasps, King's medal with two clasps); dist. comanr., Vereeniging, 1900; res. mag., Wakkerstroom dist., Transvaal, 1901-1907; Transvaal mem. of Natal-Transvaal boundary comanr., 1902; sec. in office of high comanr. for S. Africa, 1907; ag. Imperial sec., Aug., 1907, to Mar., 1908; ast. Imperial sec., Apr., 1908; chmn. Swaziland water comanr., 1909.
BENZIES, DOUGLAS, M.A., B.Sc.—B. 1899; ed. Heriot's Schol., Edinburgh; Edin. Univ., 1919-25; M.A., B.Sc.; France, June-Dec., 1918; demob., Jan., 1919; ast. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

BERENDSEN, CARL AUGUST, LL.M.—B. 1890; ed. Australian publ. schol., Gore Dist. High Schol. (N.Z.) and Victoria Univ. Coll.; entd. N.Z. educ. dept., 1906; lab. dept., 1916; chf. clk., 1917; dep. regiar., industrial unions, 1919; Imperial affrs. offr., prime min.'s dept., 1926; sec., external affrs., 1928.

BEEFELEY, HENRY SEGRAVE.—B. 1879; served in S. African War with Kitchener's Horse (med. and five clasps), 1900-1901; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1904; polit. offr., N Nigeria, 29th April, 1905; stattn. mag., Nigeria, Jan., 1914; pol. mag., July, 1921; ag. puisne judge, Apr. to May, 1923 and on various occasions since; puisne judge, Nov., 1928; ag. ch. just., July to Nov., 1930.

BERLYN, BERNARD HENRY ALFRED FORBES.—B. 1886; ed. St. Paul's Schol. and privately; Army chaplain (C. of E.), 1914; 2nd lieut., 4th Batt., Royal Irish Rifles, 1915; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; mem. of S.E. circuit; pol. mag., sup. ct., Gold Coast, 1923; ag. circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, 1925; 2nd puisne judge, sup. ct., Leeward Is., 1927; served on W. Indian ct. of appl., 1928; ag. ch. just. 1928 and 1933; 1st puisne judge, 1931; mem., W. Indian ct. of appl., 1928 and 1933.

BERNE, CAPT. JAMES LEO, O.B.K. (1918).—B. 1885; ed. Dulwich Coll.; 2nd Lieut., Royal Irish Regt., 1904; capt., 1910; served with Sierra Leone Batt. W.A.F.F., 1907-1912; served with Franco-Liberian bndry. comanr., 1908 (reco'd. thanks Franco gov't.); served with Anglo-French bndry. comanr., 1911; capt., R. of O.; ast. dist. comanr., Somaliland Prot., Sept., 1912; dist. comanr., March, 1914; served with Admiralty war staff, intell. div., Apr. to Aug., 1915; accompanied mission to Abyssinia on the occasion of coronation of Empress Zauditu, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia, 1917; sen. ast. sec. to gov. of Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1921; ag. ast. ch. sec., 1922 to May, 1923, Dec., 1925 to March, 1924; ag. prov. comanr., Apr., 1926 to May, 1931 and Feb., 1932; dep. prov. comanr., Feb., 1931; mem. leg. coun., 1931.

BERTEAU, F. C., I.S.O. (1914).—Ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, 1865-72; ent. Newfoundland coll. serv. as olk. H.M. Customs, 1878; collr. of cust., Labrador, 1881; clk. in col. sec.'s off., 1889; ch. clk., 1890; J.P. for col., 1893; mission to Ottawa to inquire into Canadian fin. system, with view of applying same to Newfild., May, 1898; audr.-gen. Newfild., July, 1898; ag. cashier, Newfoundland sav. bank, 1911.

BERTRAM, SIR ANTON, KT. BACH (1916), K.C. (1913).—Ed. at City of London schol., anc. Camb.; scholar of Caius Coll., 1887; Fellow 1891; 1st cls. class. trip, part I., 1890; ditta part II., 1891; Powis medal for Latin vers., chancellor's medal for classics, 1891; B.A., 1890; M.A., 1892; president, Union Soc., 1891. Tandred law studentship, Lincoln's Inn, 1892; called, 1893; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1902; elected to H. of Assem., June, 1903; ag. col. sec., May to Sept., 1903; admstr. govt., Sept. to Oct., 1904; ag. ch. justice, Sep.-Nov., 1906; chrmn. of comanr. on the Out Is., 1906; puisne judge, Cypra, 1906; atty.-gen., Ceylon, 19th May, 1911; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. ch. justice, Apr., 1918; ch. justice, July, 1918; services lent to govt. of Palestine, Dec., 1920 to May, 1921; ret., 1923; joint author, "Handbook to Women's Compensation Acts, 1897 and 1900."

BESSBOROUGH, 9TH EARL OF (creat. 1779), H. Hon. VERE BRABAZON PONSONBY, P.C. (G.C.M.G. (1931), C.M.G. (1919), B.A. (Cantab.), B. 1880; 2nd lieut., Bucks. I.Y., 1905-8; temp. capt., Suffolk yeo. and adjutant, 1915; served Great War, 1914-18; L.C.C., 1907-10; M.I., Cheltenham, 1910 and Dover, 1913-20; J.P., C. Kilkenny; gov.-gen. of the Dominion of Canada, 1931.

BEST, HON. SIR ROBERT WALLACE, K.C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1856; vice-pres. of the exec. com. Australian Commonwealth; leader of the Dem. govt. in the Senate, Feb., 1907; min. for trade and customs, C. of A., 1909; now retd.

BEST, SIR THOMAS ALEXANDER VAN, K.C.M.G. (1932), K.B.E. (1926), C.M.G. (1916), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1870; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; Taylorian Univ. scholar; gold meda list of French Ministry of pub. instruction; sup. B. Cent. Afric. admintr., 1896; judicial offr., Mar., 1898; ag. ch. judicial offr., Nov., 1899, to 1900; ag. vice-consul, Chinde, Aug., 1902, to Apr., 1903; ag. dep. comanr., Apr. to Dec., 1903; June, 1905 to Jan., 1906; dist. recs., 1st cls., Apr., 1906; col. sec., Falkland Is., Oct., 1909; ag. recs., Dec., 1909, to Dec., 1910; attended Impl. East Confce, 1911; col. sec., Leeward Is., Jan., 1913; ag. gov., May to Sept., 1914; Apr. to Aug., 1914. and from Jan., 1916, to Mar., 1919; reed. thanks of H.M.'s Govt. for services during disturbances in Antigua in Mar., 1918; col. sec., Trinidad, Aug., 1919; ag. gov., June-Aug., 1920, June, 1921 to Jan., 1922, June to Oct., 1923 and from Aug., 1924; lieut.-gov., Malta, 1925; O.A.G. for various periods in 1926, 1927, 1928 and 1929; gov., Windward Is., Sept., 1930.
BETANCOURT, NELSON.—B. 1887; sworn survr.; 2nd asst. inspr. of mines, Trinidad, 1st April, 1909; 1st inspr., mines, 17th Apr., 1921; ag. inspr., mines, May to Sept., 1927.

BETHEL, CHARLES PREVOT, I.S.O. (1929).—B. 1891; ag. 4th elk., post office, Bahamas, Apr. and Nov., 1909; cadet, Out islands, Feb., 1910; 3rd elk., C.S.O., and clk. to pub. bd. of wks., New Providence, Sept., 1912; 2nd elk., C.S.O., Feb., 1914; ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., May to Aug., 1919; ch. clk., C.S.O., Aug., 1919; enlisted for active serv., 1916, 2nd lieut., Mar., 1919; served in France; twice wounded; discharged from Army, Apr., 1919 resumed duties, May, 1919; ag. elk. in charge, col. sec.'s office, Aug. to Sept., 1923; ag. col. sec. and temp. mem., exec. coun., on various occasions, 1923-30 and 1926-30; ag. A.D.C. to admstr., Sept. to Nov., 1925; asst. col. sec., July, 1928; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1929-33; chmn., water and sewerage bd.

BETHELL, DONALD LESLIE.—B. 1885; ed. King Edward VI. Schl., Stourbridge; Canadian Exp. Force, 1914-19; asst. treas., Tanganyika Terr., Sept., 1919; senr. asst. treas., Somaliland, Apr., 1926; ag. treas., Somaliland in 1926, 1927 and 1928; treas., Apr., 1929; treas., Gibraltar, 1931.

BETHELL, WILLIS H.—B. 1875; pub. schl. teacher, Bahamas, 1897; customs offr., Mar., 1901; ag. chief clk., audit dept., June, 1912; chief clk., audit dept., Apr., 1913; ag. auditor of pub. accts. for various periods between 1916 and 1923; audr., Dec., 1925; audr., pub. accts., 1st Dec., 1925.

BETTINGTON, DIGBY ROWLAND ALBEMARLE.—Ed. at United Serv. Coll., Westward Ho!; served with Natal Mounted Pol., 1896-1901; with 2nd Kitchener's Fighting Scouts, 1901-1902 (dangerously wounded, ment. in despatches, Queen's med. and 5 clasps); inspr., mil. pol., Cyprus, 1903; inspr., pol., Sierra Leone, 1906; ag. comnstr., pol. and sheriff on several occasions; dep.-comnstr. of pol. and dep.-supt., prisons, Gold Coast, 1910; base comdt., Togoland Field Force, 1914; "1914-15" Star, War and Victory med.; title changed to dep. inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, 1915; inspr.-gen. pol. and prisons, Gold Coast, 1917; inspr.-gen. pol., Gold Coast on separation of depts. of pol. and prisons, 1920; King's Police med., 1920; ret., 1924; commdt., pol., Bahamas, 1924; resigned, 1926; chief of pol. Bermuda, 1927.

BEVIR, ANTHONY, M.A.—B. 1895; scholar of Eton, 1909, and of Hertford Coll., Oxford, 1914; M.A., 1923; served with 7th King's Liverpool Regt., July, 1915 to Apr., 1919; twice ment. in despatches; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 9th Feb., 1921; accompanied Mr. Ormsby-Gore on his visit to the West African Colonies, Jan.-May, 1926; pvt.-sec. to Mr. Ormsby-Gore, Feb., 1926; prin., 1st Apr., 1929.
BEWES, GEORGE PRICE HOPK.—B. 1892; ed., Clifton Coll. and Royal Naval Coll., Osborne; asst. dist. comnstr., Gold Coast, 25th Aug., 1920.
BEYERS, LT.-COL. LEONARD.—B. 1894; perm. staff, Union defence forces, 1914-32; dir., prisons, Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1932.

BHARUCHA, NAVROJI MANEKJI.—B. 1890; B.A., Cantab. cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Jan., 1914; office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Jan., 1914; attd. Kurunegala Kach., June, 1914; extra office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., in addn. to his own duties, Aug., 1914; ag. pol. mag., Kurunegala, Apr., 1916; office asst., govt. agt., R.Prov., Jan., 1917; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Nov., 1917; Balapitiya, Nov., 1918; dist. judge, Tangalla, Mar., 1922; ag. dist. judge, Chilaw, May, 1922; asst attmnt. offr., May, 1925; dist. judge, Kalutara, Jan., 1927; dist. judge, Kalutara, Dec., 1932.
BICKLE, W. H.—B. 1885; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th Mar., 1906; cler. offr. higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., 4th Sept., 1929.

BICKMORE, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.A.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1911; attached to Colombo kach., Dec., 1911; col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1912; Galle kach., Nov., 1912; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Feb., 1913; offr. asst. to govt. agt., W. Prov., Dec., 1913; pol. mag., Jaffna and Kayta, June, 1914; office asst. to govt. Cent. Prov., Nov., 1914; pol. mag., Panadure, Aug., 1915; seconded for serv. as ag. finan. asst. to chmn., mun. coun., Colombo, Mar., 1916; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Jan., 1917; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1917; on mil. duty, 1918; ag. dist. judge, Kegalla, June, 1919; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Nov., 1919; ditto, Matare, May, 1921; asst. col. treas., May, 1922; ag. col. treas., Sept. to Oct., 1926 and Jan. to June, 1930; dep. finan. sec., July, 1931; offr. cls. I, grade II, 1932.

BIDEN, CLAUDE ERIC DE BEER.—B. 1887; ed. Radley Coll.; asst. audr., S. Nigeria, 1911; on active serv., attd. Nigerian Overseas Contg., German E. Africa, and W. African Serv. Brig., 1917-19; senr. asst. audr., Nigeria, 1926; dep. audr., Gold Coast, 1930; mem., rly. retrenchment comte., 1931; ag. audr., May, 1932; audr., Palestine and Trans-Jordan, May, 1933.

BIENEMAN, G. A. J., B.A.—B. 1889; ed. Dean Close, Cheltenham and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; junr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, July, 1913; head of training coll., Oct., 1921; ch. inspr., educn., N. Prov., Nigeria, 1929; ag. asst. dir., educn., N. Prov., 1931 and 1932.

BIGELOW, HENRY VEDDER, K.C., B.A., LL.B., M.A.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schls., Truro, N.S., and Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1894; LL.B., 1896; M.A., 1897; admitted to bar of N.S. 1896; practised law at Truro, N.S., and Regina, Sask.; K.C., Sask., 1913; judge, King's Bench, Sask., Feb., 1918.

BIGG, WILFRED JOSEPH.—B. 1897; boy clk., G.P.O., 22nd Nov., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to C.O., 1st Dec., 1914; on mil. ser. from 31st Aug., 1914, to 8th May, 1919; cler. offr. higher grade, 1922; asst. prin., 1st Aug., 1925; prte. sec. to perm. under sec., D.O. (Sir Ed. Harding), 3rd Feb., 1930; ag. prin., 22nd June, 1931.

BIRCH, HUGH MASSEY, M.I.C.E.—Ed. privately and King's Coll., Ldn.; trooper, M.M. Pol., 1897; exec. engrn., P.W.D., E.A.P., 1907; local capt., E.A. Forces, 1916-18; exec. engrn., P.W.D., Kenya, 1918; B.S.A. Coy.'s, Br. War and Victory med.
BIRCH, W. C.—Asst. analyst, E.A.P., July, 1914; senr. chemical offr., Apr., 1923.

BIRD, EDGAR CLARENCE FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. Xaverian Coll.; asst. ch., cust., Zanzibar Prot., Oct., 1914; ag. ch., cust., Jan. to Aug., 1915, May, 1916 to Feb., 1917, and Dec., 1917; marshal, H.B.M. Court for Zanzibar (in Prize), Jan. to Aug., 1916 and from Dec., 1917; imigrn. offr., Sept., 1915; dep. compt., cust., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1920; ag. compt., cust., on various occasions, 1921-29; dep. compt., cust., Nigeria, 1930; compt., 1932.

BIRD, REGINALD.—B. 1893; ed. Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1919; war serv., 1914-19; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, June, 1920; ast. dist. judge, S'pore, Aug., 1922; ast. sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1924; dist. offr., K. Pilah, July, 1925; ast. advr., Batu Pahat, Nov., 1929; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1929; ag. stamp and prob. offr., S'pore, Aug., 1933.

BIRKINSHAW, FRANK.—B. 1887; ast. agrl. supt. and so. mast., gram. schl., St. Vincent, Aug., 1912; agrl. instr., Mauritius, Jan., 1916; inspr., co-op. credit socs. in addn., Oct., 1915; agrl. supt., Sept., 1919; ast. agrl. inspr., Perak, F.M.S., June, 1920; agrl. field offr., Perak North, Dec., 1924; ditto P. Wellesley and Penang in addn., Apr., 1926; sr. agrl. field offr., Perak, Sept., 1931.

BIRKITT, FRANK.—B. 1885; Imp. postal serv. (tels.) 1898-1902, mails, 1903-07; admtrv. (cler. and acctg.) 1908; staff record offr., 1909; postmr., Nyassaland Prot. (various offices) 1912; (active serv.), 1914-15, N.V.R., Nthn. Rhodn. Forces), ag. ast. P.M.G. various occasions; lst cl. clk., 1920; ch. acct. and skpr., 1923; P.M.G., Br. Guiana, 1927.

BIRSE, ARTHUR LOUIS.—B. 1893; cadet, S.S., Apr., 1921; collr., income tax, Singapore, Sept., 1921; treas., engg. cmte., Br. Malaya, Br. Emp. Exhibn., in addn., Jan., 1923; recvr., Alasagoff Concession, Kubub, Mar., 1926; offr., cl. III, June, 1930; ag. ast. adviser, Kemaman, Trengganu, July, 1930; ag. oomsnr., land and mines, Trengganu, Mar., 1932.

BIRT, DUDLEY THOMAS.—B. 1881; ed. Uppingham and Univ. of Durham; M.B., B.S., Durham, 1905; certif. L.S.T.M.; house surg., and supt., Grey Hosp., Pietermaritzburg, Natal, 1904; med. offr., Nigeria, West African med. staff, July, 1913; senr. med. offr., Mar. 1927, and transfd. to Sierra Leone; senr. med. offr., Gambia, July, 1929; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ast. dir., med. services, Nigeria, 1934.

BIRTWISTLE, WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Schl., Blackburn; ast. naturalist (fisheries), min. of agr. and fisheries; directed research in dept. of oceanography, univ., Liverpool; fisheries economist, S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1924; offr.-in-ch., fisheries dept., Apr., 1928.

BISCOE, CECIL JULIAN TYNDALK, M.C., B.A., F.R.G.S.—B. 1895; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond, Trent Coll., Derbyshire, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; war serv., R.A., 1914-18 (France and Palestine); supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; ag. dir., educn., May-Nov., 1932.

BISCOE, VINCENT FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. at Cheltenham Coll. and Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A., June, 1908; 2nd lieut. London Rifle Brig. (Territorial Force), June, 1909; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1910; ast. res., N. Nigeria, Aug., 1911; 2nd cls. dist. offr., Dec., 1919; ag. station mag., Dec., 1924.
BISHOP, CECIL WILLIAM EDWIN, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.Inst.W.E.—B. 1899; ed. Reigate Grammar Schl. and King's Coll., Lond. Univ.; war serv., 1918-19; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1922.

BISHOP, W. W.—B. 1878; ed. Glasgow and Glasgow Univ.; ast. engrnr., Cape prov., 1903; dist. engrnr., Baro-Kano Rly., 1916; engnr., 2nd grade, open lines, Nigeria, 1916; dist. engrnr., 1st grade, 1919; d.c.
BITCHENER, HON. JOHN.—B. 1866; N.Z. parl., 1919; jun. whip in refer. 1927; sen. whip, 1928; jun. whip in party, 1931; min. of pub. wks., 1933.

BLACK, ARCHIBALD MAULE.—Ed. & Coll., Cape Town; ast. civ. serv., Cap- 1898; addnl. ast. regstr., sup. ct., Cap- 1903; ast. regstr., 1904; regstr.; and taxsup. ct., Grahamstown, 1921; regismast., sup. ct., Cape Town and high adprov. of Capa' of Good Hope, 1928; service, 1914-19; ment. in despa.; awrd. 1921; major, Union R. of O. (art.).

BLACK, CAPT. THE HON. GEO.—B. 1875; at pub. schls., N.B.; el., mem. of Ymca three times, 1906-11; oomsnr., Yukon 1912-16; capt., C.E.F., 1916-19; el. E. Canada, g.e.i., 1921—re-el., 1925, 1926; el. speaker of H. of C., 1930.
BLACK, JOHN ALAN.—B. 1891; cad. 1914; offr., cls. V., 1917; offr., cls. offr., cls. III, Nov., 1926; ast. prot. S'pore, Dec., 1926; ag. prot., China, Nov., 1929; do., Dec., 1930; offr., cls.

BLACK, JOHN GRAHAM.—B. 1896; cad. May, 1920; pol. mag., ast. dist. judg., regir., sup. ct., Malacca, 1920; passed 1921; coroner, Malacca, Mar., 1923; mem. rev., May, 1923; offr., cls. V., May, 1923; sec. to O.A.G., S.S., May, 1925; ast. Tapah, June, 1926; ditto, Kroh, Feb. 1927; ast. advr., Trengganu, 1927; res., Labuan, Feb., 1930; offr., May, 1932.

BLACK, KENNETH MAJOR, F.R.C.S. B. 1879; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Guy's Thomas's hosps.; med. offr., gen. hosp., Apr., 1921; surg., native hosps., Ag. ophthalmologist, gen. hosp., in addn. Sec. prot., surgery, med. coll., S'pore, Ag. ag. surg., S'pore, Aug., 1923; Fr. June, 1928 and Apr., 1931.
BLACK, THOMAS.—B. 1894; supl. ass. corresp., port developmt. dept., Hess. 1925; acctnt., treasy., 1926.

BLACK, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1872; and high schls., Ont.; grad. in agr., Univ.; editor "Farmers' Advocate," 1902-04; dep. min., agr., Manitoba, 1904-6; Manitoba Agrl. Coll., 1906-15; sec. econ. development comm. of Canada, 1915-16; of agr., Canada, 1916-18; chmn., sociebd., 1918-20; dep. min., immigrn. and ization, 1921; man., colonisation dept., Nat. National Rly., London, 1923; ch. econ. dept., C.N. rly., Montreal, 1924.

BLACKABY, ERNEST JAMES, M.R.C.S. L.R.C.P.(Lond.)—B. 1902; ed. St. Barth. Hosp., London; house surg., R. hts. Sunderland, Feb., 1924; res. surg. c. infirmary, Ashton-under-Lyne, 1924; hos. London Lock Hosp., 1925; ast. med. Tonbridge Infirmary, 1926; dist. med. Cyprus, July, 1928; ag. surg. speclty. to Oct., 1929 and July, 1930; mem. R.A.M.S., Nyassaland, 1931.

BLACKALL, HENRY WILLIAM BUTLER. L.L.B. (1st place).—B. 1889; ed. Steventh- COLL., Dublin; Victoria prizeman, jurisce and internat. law; lst of cls. hon., history; John Brooke scholar, King's Inns; zo Irish Bar, 1912; crown counsel, E.A.P., 913; M.L.C., Apr., 1920; crown counsel, May, 1923; ag. solr.-gen., on various ns, 1923-31; mem. comm. on riots, S.E., 1930; atty.-gen., Cyprus, Jan., 1932.

CKMORE, HARRY, O.B.E. (1933). V.D.— St. Bartholomew's Sch., Wednesbury; art Sch., Walsall; St. John's Coll., and Victoria Univ., Leeds; senior ast., bartholomew's Sch., Wednesbury, 1900; h and music mast.; pupil teacher, and Higher Grade Sch., Walsall, 1904; mast., Riccall Sch., York, 1905; normal B. Guiana, July, 1906; headmast., y schl., G. Coast, 1912; inspr. of schls., seconded to G. Coast Regt., 1917; rtnd. to ty, 1918; ag. dir. educn., Mar., 1924; spr., schls., Sierra Leone, May, 1924; r. educn., Aug., 1924; dir., 1931.
CKWELL, BERNARD FLETCHER.—Ed. Woodford Acad., N.S.W.; R. Australavy, 1924-30 (paymr.-lieut.); ret., 1930; clk., New Hebrides, Sept., 1931.

CKWELL, CAPT. FRANCES.—B. 1885; P.O., 1901-13; ast. supt., posts and tels., Nov., 1913; on milly. serv., 1914-21; al ast. to dir., posts and tels., F.M.S., 21; supt., posts and tels., S.S. and F.M.S., 929; contrlr., ditto, Jan., 1929; contrlr., and Dindings, Nov., 1929; ag. contrlr., and tels., S'pore., Nov., 1930; contr., do.
CKWOOD, A. MC.A., M.B., Ch. B. (B.)—B. 1885; lieut., R.A.M.C., May, capt., 1918; med. offr., Tanganyika ty, Apr., 1919; senr. med. offr., Jan.

CKWOOD, JOHN ALEXANDER.—B. 1874; llar Academy, and at Heriot-Watt Coll., urgh; articled to Scottish solr., 1892; Impl. ye. (now I.S.A.R.), 1901; entd. Crown office, 1902; ast. regiar. of inscribed 914; head of insurance and checking dept., ast. sec. to W. African Currency Bd., 14; dep. head, stores dept., 14th Nov., 1921; hipping dept., July, 1927; dep. head, gen. dept., May, 1933; head, stores and appts. Nov., 1933.

BLAKELY, DONALD QUENTIN.—B. 1875; elk., atty.-gen.'s off., B. Honduras, Apr., 1898; ag. regiar.-gen., May, 1906, to Mar., 1907; 1st cls. clk., regiar.-gen.'s office, Apr., 1907; ag. regiar. gen., Oct., 1908 to May, 1909; commr. of sup., B. Hond., Mar., 1908; ag. regiar.-gen., Oct., 1908, to May, 1909, and Aug., 1912.

BLAND, CHARLES HEBER.—B. 1886; ed. pub. and high schl., Pembroke, Ont., and Queen's Univ., Kingston, Ont.; B.A. (1907); ent. Canadian civ. serv. at first compet. exam., 1909; served with Can. Field Art., 1917-19; ast. sec., civ. serv. comrn., 1919; ch. examr., 1921; civ. ser. comrn.,

BLANDFORD, HON. SYDNEY D.—B. 1868; M.H.A., Newfoundland; elected for Bonavista, 1904, 1908 and 1909; M.E.C. and min. of agric. and mines, 1909; M.L.C. and min. of agric. and mines, 1914; sheriff of Newfoundland, 1917.

BLANDY, CAPT. R. D.—B. 1891; ed. Victoria Col., Jersey; ent. Indian pol., 1911; Indian Army, R. of O., Apr., 1916; active serv., N.W. Frontier and Mesopotamia; returned Indian pol., Jan., 1919; dist. supt., pol., 1920-24; ch. of international pol., Tangier, July, 1928; ast. to res. commr., New Hebrides, Sept., 1929; ag. res. commr., New Hebrides, Aug., 1930 to Oct., 1931.

BLANKENBERG, SIR R. A., K.B.E. (1920), O.B.E. (1918).—Rhodesian service, 1905 to 1902; conf. clk. to lieut.-gov., Transvaal, 1902; ag. priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., Aug., 1902; ch. clk. to agt.-gen. in London, 1st June, 1907; ch. clk. to high commr. for Union of South Africa, London, 1910; ast. sec. to high commr., 1912; sec. to high commr., 1919; ag. high commr., 1920; ret., 31st Mar., 1926.

BLAXTER, KENNETH WILLIAM.—B. 1896; ed. Malvern and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.); milly. serv., France and Italy, 1915-19; ast. prin., miny. of transport, 1920; sec., royal comrn. on compensation for suffering and damage by enemy action, 1923; ast. prin., C.O., 1924; prte. sec. to perm. under-sec. of state for the cols., 10th Dec., 1928; prte. sec. to parly. under sec. of state for cols., 17th Sept., 1929; ag. prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

BLEASDELL, ERNEST HAMILTON.—B. 1887; ed. Keble Coll. Oxford; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1912; ag. dist. commr., Dec., 1912 to Feb., 1913, June to Dec., 1913, July, 1914 to June, 1915, Nov., 1915 to 1917, and from July, 1917; dist. commr., 19th Aug., 1918.
BLEDISLOE, 1ST BARON (or. 1918) RT. HON.

SIR CHARLES BATHURST, P.C. (1926); K.B.E. (1917).—B. 1867; ed. Sherborne, Eton, Univ. Coll., Oxford and Royal Agr. Coll., Cirencester (gold medallist and late chmn. of govt.); chancery barrister and conveyancer, 1894-1910; M.P. South Wilts Divn., 1910-18; mem. of coun. of Duchy of Lancaster; parly. sec. to miny. of food, 1916-17; chmn., R. comrn. on sugar supply, and dir., sugar distribution, 1917-19; parly. sec. to miny. of agrt., 1924-28; chmn., R. comrn., land drainage, England and Wales, 1927; gov. and commdr.-in-chef, New Zealand, 1930.

BLONDIN, Lieut. Col. the Hon. Pierre Kidouard.—B. 1874; ed. Seminaire de Nicolet and Laval Univ., Montreal; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1908; re-el., g.e., 1911; el. dep. speaker, 1911; min. of inland rev., 1914; sec. of state, 1916; P.M.G., Canada, 1917; overseas, 1917-18; apptd. to senate, 1918; re-appd. P.M.G., 1920; speaker of senate, 1930.

BLOOD, Hilary Rudolph Robert, C.M.G., (1834), M.A. (Glas.)—B. 1893; on mil. serv., Oct., 1914-1919; temp. employed at C.O., Mar.-Dec., 1919, Aug., 1924 to May, 1925 and Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1930; cadet, Ceylon, Jan., 1920; attd. to Columbo Kach., Feb., 1920; Anuradhapura Kach., Mar., 1920; ag. office ast., Anuradhapura Kach., Nov., 1920; attd., Kandy Kach., Apr., 1921; office astk., gov't. agt., Cent. Prov., June, 1921; 4th ast. col. sec., Apr., 1922; 3rd ast. col. sec., June, 1925; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1926; ast., gov't. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., Colombo, Mar., 1930; col. sec. and regis. gen., Grenada, 1930; O.A.G., Windward Is., June to Aug., 1930; admstd. govt., Grenada, on several occasions.

BLOXHAM, Capt. Hugh Fitzherbert.—B. 1893; ed. Bradfield Coll.; 4th batt., Wiltshire Regt., 1914-15; 102nd K.E.O. Grenadiers, I.A., 1915-24; served Mesopotamia, 1915-19 and Afghan War, 1919-20; twice ment. in desps.; ast. supt., pol., Hong Kong, Feb., 1924; ast. supt., prisons, Apr., 1925; ag. supt., prisons, Apr., 1925; ag. ast. supt., pol., Mar., 1926; ag. supt., prisons, Mar., 1929; dep. dir., prisons, Gold Coast, Oct., 1930; prison supt., S.S., 1932.

BLUMER, Rev. Reginald Charles, M.A., B.Sc., Dipl. Ed.—B. 1889; ed. Sydney High Schol. and Sydney Univ.; Caird schol. in chem.; B.A., 1910, Ist cls. hona., Latin, 2nd cls. hona., maths.; B.Sc., 1910; M.A., 1912; dipl. ed., 1921; deacon, 1912; priest, 1913; curate, St. John's, Parramatta, 1912-17; curate, Holy Trinity, Dulwich Hill, 1918; ast. mast., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1921-25; vice-prin., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

BLUNT, Denzil Latton, M.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1891; ed. Shrewsbury and King's Coll., Camb.; served under court of wards, Madras, 1913-14; served H.M. Forces, 1915-19; lieut., R.A.S.C.; senr. agrl. offr., Kenya, 1926; ast. to dir., agr., Kenya, 1930; dir., agr., Cyprus, Aug., 1935.

BLYTHE, Wilfred Lawson.—B. 1896; ed. Birkenhead Inst. and Liverpool Univ.; war serv., 1916-18; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; offr., cls. V., Apr., 1924; priv. sec. to ch. sec., F.M.S., Feb., 1925; offr., cl. IV., Apr., 1928; offr., cls. III., Apr., 1933; ag. prot., Chinese, Penang, Sept., 1933.
BOAST, Henry Walter—B. 1887; clk. Swaziland, 1909; regis. and mast., sup. ct. and sheriff, 1928; dep. ast. commr., 1930.

BODENSTEIN, Helgard Dewald Johannes, LL.D. (Leyden), barrister-at-law, Middle Temple.—Clk. mag.'s offices, Krugersdorp, Lydenburg, Pietersburg, 1909; Piet Retief, 1910; sec. judge pres., J. de Villiers, 1910-12; ch. clk. and professional ast. to atty-gen., Bloemfontein, Apr., 1912; prof., Roman Dutch Law, Univ. of Amsterdam, Aug., 1912; ast. ed., "Die Burger," 1920; prof., Roman Dutch Law, Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1922-1927; sec. to prime min. and for external affrs., 1927.

BODILLY, Commdr. Ralph Burland, R.N.—B. 1884; ed. Bradfield Coll. and H.M.S. "Britannia," 1899; midshipman, China station, 1901; sub. lieut. Atlantic Fleet, 1905; lieut., Home Fleet, Jan., 1907; invalided, 1909; called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1913; on war serv., Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1919; lieut. commdr., Jan., 1915; promoted commdr. for war services, Nov., 1918; res. mag., Jamaica, Feb., 1925; ag. jurist, Kingston on various occasions, 1927-31; acted in appellate ct. on various occasions, 1929-33; pros., marine and water cts.; ch. mag., Palest., Oct., 1932; author of "Inter-action between Vessels."

BODKIN, Gilbert Edwin, F.E.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1886; ed. at Repton Schol., Derbyshire, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1908; Camb. Univ., diploma of agric., 1910; govt. econoz. biologist, dept. of science and agric., Br. Guiana, 1911; Carnegie scholar in economic entomology, and as such toured the prin. agril. stations of the United States, 1912-1913; rep. Br. Guiana at W. Indian Agril. Conference, 1913; ast. dir. of sci. agr., and dep. chmn., bd. of agr., Br. Guiana, 1919; editor, jnl. of the bd. of agr., 1918; p.t. editor, Br. Guiana Handbook, 1921; govt. entomologist, Palestine, June, 1922; offr., locust campaigns, 1928-30; press., Intern. Locust Bureau, Damascus, 1930-31; dir., agr. prin., coll. of agr. and regis., co-op. credit soc., Mauritius, 1932; author of numerous papers on the insects of Br. Guiana and Palestine, scientific reptos. to govt., etc.

BOILEAU, Lewis Harold.—B. 1888; ed. Monkton Combe Schol., Bath and Garrick Chambers (Schoones); ast. dist. offr., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; ag. priu. ast. sec., Apr., 1923; ag. ast. ast. sec., S. Prov., in 1925, 1926 and 1927; admstd. offr., cls. I., Aug., 1930.

BOK, Dr. Willem Eduard, Junr.—Priv. sec. to prime min., Transvaal, June, 1907; sec. & prime min. and clk. to exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, May, 1910; law advsr., Feb., 1916; for just., Aug., 1918; judge, high ct., S.W. Afr., Feb., 1930; judge, sup. ct., S. Africa, Griqualand West local divn., 1933.

BOLAND, Frederick Malcolm, LL.B.—Called to bar, Middle Temple, 1913; mag. Court of St. Patrick, Trinidad, Mar., 1926; mag. eastern dist., Jan., 1927; ast. mag., St. George West, 1933; mag., Victoria, 1934.

BOLTON, Arthur, B.A., Dipl.—B. 1897; Rugby and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (clam. ext.), B.A. (hons. chem.), 1920; dipl. eugen., 1921; lab. ast., H.M. facty., Sandycroft, 1921; research, Queen's Coll., Oxford, 1921; served in France, 1917-18; wounded; lieut., O.T.C., 1922; ast. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.
BOLUS, Percy Reginald, M.B. (Lond.)—Ast. col. surgeon, Falkland Is., 1904.

BONAVIA, Walter Joseph.—B. 1899; on mil. serv., 1914-1916; clk., secretaries, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1917; ast. dist. offr. (cadet), Oct., 1920; admstd. offr., 2nd grade, Oct., 1923; ag. ditto, 1st grade, Feb. to Nov., 1923 to Aug., 1925; ag. dist. offr., in Nov., 1928, 1929-31; dist. offr., Oct., 1931.
BOND, Major B. W., M.C.—Ast. dist. commr., Kenya, May, 1920.

BOND, Ralph Norman, B.A. (Cantab.)—1900; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; ast. Puttalam kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, Hambantota kach., Oct., 1924; office ast., Badulla Is., Jan., 1926; pol. mag., Gampola, Jan., 1930; govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Oct., 1931; do., Kandy, June, 1933.
BONE, Major Chandos Eric, M.C., F.S. L.S. (Vict.)—B. 1889; survr., 1st grade, surveys beh., Perak, Oct., 1913; lieut., R.F.A.

BORG, ANTHONY J., M.D., Malta.—B. 1889; medical offr., Fiji, 1916; D.M.O., and M.O.H., Ra., 1917; ag. R.M.O., med. supt., pub. lunatic asylum and visiting med. offr., Suva gaol, 1919; D.M.O., Nadroga, 1920; D.M.O. and M.O.H., Nadi, 1921; ditto, Levuka, 1923; ditto, Labasa, 1924; D.M.O. and dist. commr., Ra (joint), 1929.

BOSHOFF, STEPHANUS PETRUS ERASMUS, M.A., D.Litt.—B. 1891; ed. Paarl Boys' High Schl., Grey Univ. Coll. and Univ. of Amsterdam; teacher, 1912; prof. of Afrikaans and comparative philology, Potchefstroom Univ. Coll., 1915; ditto, Univ. of Cape Town, 1930; dir., educ., Transvaal Prov., 1932.

BOSKER, WALTER LOMSDALE, A.M.I.C.E.— B. 1884; asst. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Mar., 1913; asst. engr., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1913; asst. engnr., irrigtn., May, 1915; ag. exec. engr., Kuala Langat, Oct., 1916; ditto, Seremban, Apr., 1921; ditto, Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1921; ditto, head office, Selangor, Oct., 1921; exec. engr., wks. and bldgs., Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1924; ag. senr. exec. engr., Seremban, Apr., 1927; senr. exec. engnr., F.M.S., July, 1928; ditto, Klang, Jan., 1929; ag. state engr., Trengganu, Jan., 1930.
BOSTOCK, J. K.—B. 1892; ed. Repton; L. & N.W. Ely., Crewe, 1909; on mily. serv., 1914-19; divn. supt., Nigerian rly., 1928.

BOSTOCK, MAJOR H. E., O.B.E. (Mil.), R. of O.R.E.), M.I.C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Marlborough and Univ. Coll., London; asst. engr., Gold Coast harbr. and Sierra Leone harbr., 1909; Br. Army, France, R.E. (ment. in des.), 1915-19; res. engr., Lagos harbr. wks., 1921; port engr., harbr. dept., Lagos, 1923.

BOSTOCK, WILFRED CRAMER.—B. 1879; ed. St. Lawrence Coll., Ramagate; served, S. African War, Imp. Yeomanry; asst. engr., P.W.D., N. Nigeria, Sepk., 1908; transfd., Baro-Kano rly., Jan., 1909; res. engr., constr., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1914 to Dec., 1915; asst. ch. engr., Nigerian rly., Jan., 1918; gen. man., Sierra Leone rly., Nov., 1919; ch. engr., Nigerian rly., Jan., 1922; dep. gen. man., Feb., 1924.

BOSTON, HUGH MERRICK.—B. 1893; ed. Wallasey H.E. Schi., New Brighton, Liverpool Univ., M.B., Ch.B. (Liv.), 1925; served with 2nd W. Lancs. (39th County Palatine) Field Ambulance (T.F.), 1914-19; med. offr., Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Apr., 1928.

BOTHA, CHRISTIAN LAURENS.—B. 1870; ed. at Grey Coll., Bloemfontein and at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1893; LL.D., Amsterdam and K.C., 1919; puisne judge, Orange Free State divn. of sup. ct., S. Africa,

BOTHA, COLIN GRAHAM, V.D., M.A.—B. 1883; ent. atty.-gen.'s dept., Cape Colony, 1901; serv. in offices of civ. commn., Cape, and mast. of sup. ct.; in charge, archives of Cape Col., 1912; ch. archivist, Union of S. Africa, 1919; accompanied Sir Reginald Blankenburg as pvt. sec. to first assembly of League of Nations, Geneva; hon. or corresponding mem. of several hist., lit. and genealogical socs. of Gt. Britain and Holland; contributed articles to various jnl., mags. and newspapers; author of "Place Names in the Cape District," 1917; "A Brief Guide to the Documents in the Cape Archives, 1652-1806." 1918; "The French Refugees at the Cape," 1919; rept. of tour to various archives in Europe, Canada and U.S.A., 1921; "Social Life in the Cape Colony in the 18th Century," 1927; "Place Names in the Cape Province," 1927; "Public Archives of South Africa, 1652-1910," 1928.
BOTTOMLEY, JOHN HUBERT, A.M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1899; ed. The Salt Schls., Shipley, Bradford Schls. of Art, and Bradford Tech. Coll.; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924; lieut., H.K.V.D.C.

BOTTOMLEY, SIR WILLIAM CRICK, K.C.M.G. (1930), C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1921), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1898; ed. Owens Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb., entrance maj. schlr., 1896; schlr., 1898; B.A. and 3rd wrangler, 1900; ent. C.O. after compet. exam., as 2nd cl. olk., 22nd Oct., 1901; sec. to col. surv. comttee., 1905; sec. to inter-deptl. pensions comttee., 1907; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Crewe, 16th Apr., 1908; priv. sec. to Sir F. Hopwood, 1st Feb., 1909; priv. sec. to Col. Seely, 1st Jan., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Lucas, 24th Mar., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Emmott, 26th Oct., 1911; 1st cls. olk., 1913; principal olk., 8th Dec., 1917; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; mem., E. African Currency Bd., 1920; asst. under sec. of state, 1927.

BOUCHER, Cyril Haslewood.—Headcrs. offr., armed nat. constab., R.N. Guinea, Apr., 1905; asst. res. mag., central div., Jan., 1906; mag. for native affairs, Jan., 1906; J.P., Jan., 1906; visiting justice, Port Morebey gaol, Mar., 1906.
BOULDERSON, G. H. C.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912; 1st grade admstv. offr., Jan., 1924.
BOUR, EDOUARD FRANÇOIS.—M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.R.C.P. Lond.; L.S.A. Lond.; govt. med. offr., Plaines Wilhelms dist., Mauritius, 1907; medical officer in charge H.M. troops, Mauritius, 1915-19.

BOURDILLON, SIR BERNARD HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1934), K.B.E. (1931), C.M.G. (1924).—Ed. Tonbridge and St. John's Coll., Oxford; asst. mag. United Provs., India, Nov., 1908; under-sec. to govt., Mar., 1913; regar., high ct. Allahabad, May, 1915; apptd. to I.A.R.C., Sept., 1917; jt. mag. (seconded), Mar., 1918; judl. asst., Persian Gulf, June, 1918; settlement offr., Baghdad, Mar., 1919; polit. offr., Baquba, Oct., 1920; polit. sec. to high commr., Jan., 1921; dep. sec. to high commr., Dec., 1921; sec. to high commr., Jan., 1922; col. sec., Ceylon, Nov., 1929; O.A.G., Apl. to Sept., 1930; ch. sec., July, 1931; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Uganda, 1932.

BOURNE, FREDERICK GEORGE.—B. 1884; joined S.S. pol., Oct., 1905; inspr., pol., in addn., June, 1911; passed ap. law exam., June, 1913; ch. ct. inspr., July, 1913; coroner, Sept., 1920; J.P., Feb., 1922.

BOURNE, JOHN DUNBAR MACDOWALL.—B. 1886; examiner, E. and A. dept., Feb., 1906; asst. auditor, N. Nigeria, Aug., 1909; asst. audr., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1920; audr., Somaliland Prot., July, 1921; audr., B. Guiana, Oct., 1925; asst. audr., Nigeria, July, 1927; audr., Leone, 1930.

BOVELL, SIR HENRY ALLEYN, K.T. (1902).—B. 1854; Gilchrist scholar (London), Jan., 1873; LL.B. (Lond.), Jan., 1877; wid. honours in jurisprudence and Roman law; called to the bar, Linc. Inn, Nov., 1876; ser.-gen. escheator-gen. of Barbados, and J.P., 1882; M.L.C., July, 1883; mem. of exec. revise laws of Barbados, Mar., 1886; chanc. the diocese, 1884; atty.-gen., Oct., 1886; gen., Br. Guiana, 1896; ag. legal ass't., 1900 and 1901; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1912.

BOVELL-JONES, LIONEL CLIFTON.—1897; ed. Christ's Hosp.; enlisted 1915; in France, Feb., 1916-Aug., 1918; despatched sub-inspr., pol., British Guiana, July, 1919; inspr., 1924; offr.-in-oh., punitive exp., Venezuelan bndry., 1926; passed coll. exam., 1927; county inspr., 1931.

BOWEN, F. C. P.—B. 1896; exec. dept., B. Honduras, 1912, also depts. of land and regar.-gen.; 3rd cls. olk., atty.-gen., 1915; 2nd cls. olk., ditto, 1918; clk. atty.-gen., 1919; sec'y., riot inquiry comm., 1920; regar.-gen. and cust. enemy ppty., and L.C.O., 1920; sec'y., yellow fever inquire, 1921; ag. regar.-gen., 1921, 1923; asst. regar.-gen., regar., lands titles, sup't, vexatious marriages and deaths, 1927; J.P.; stamp dir., pub. offr., guarantee fund; comm'r., tax, 1927; sec. in arbitrn. between pr. U.S.A. and Guatemala, 1930.

BOWER, SIR GRAHAM JOHN, K.C.M.G., O.M.G. (1886).—B. 1848; ret. comdr. entered navy, 1861, and served on Mediter. E. coast of Africa, E. Indian, Home, and Australian stations, until 1890, when he was appointed to Sir Horace Robinson, gov. Cape of Hope, and high commr. for S. Africa; sec. to the high commr., 1884-97; colonel, Mauritius, 1898; admnstr. govt. on occasions, 1900-09; ret. 1910.

BOWERLEY, WALTER, C.B.E. (1831).—St. John's Coll., Camb. (class. tripus, 1892); clk., audt. branch of R. and A. Dep., 1901; asst. audr., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1904; asst. treas., Oct., 1902; ag. local audr., S. Nigeria, 1906-7; ag. local audr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1907, to Mar., 1908; local audr., S. Nigeria, Apr., 1908; local audr., G. Coast, Nov., 1908; changed to audr., 1910.

BOWMAN, HUMPHREY ERNEST, C.B.E. (1879).—B. 1879; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford; mod. hist. 1902); B.A., 1902; M.A., ent. Egyptian min'y. of educn., 1903; second Sudan govt. (dept. of educn.), 1911-13; Egyptian students in U.K., 1913-14; sec. Br. Army, 1914-20; B.E.F., France, staff capt., H.Q., N. Command, 1916-18; Mesopotamia, 1918-20; ret. with major, 1920; seconded from M.E.F. to educn., Mesopotamia, 1918-20; seconded to Palestine, 1920-23; dir. of educn., Palestine, 1920; mem., advisory coun. since 1920; comm. on crafts and industries, 1920; translation comttee., 1923; chmn. on translation of geographical names, 1923.
BOWRING, ARCHIBALD GEORGE.—E. High School, Nottingham, and Nottingham Univ.; capt., Sherwood Foresters, 1914-19;

BOWRING, SIR CHARLES CALVERT, K.C.M.G. (1925), K.B.E. (1919), C.M.G. (1908).—Ed. at Clifton Coll.; col. audit branch of exchequer and audit dept., 1890; Hong Kong, Dec., 1892; loc. aud., B.C. Africa, Sept., 1895; loc. aud., East Africa Prot. and Uganda rly., June, 1899; treas., E.A.P., Oct., 1901; mem., exec. and leg. couns., E.A.P., 1907; ch. sec. to govt., E.A.P., Apr., 1911; ag. govt., Feb. to Oct., 1912, Aug. to Oct., 1913, Apr., 1917 to Jan., 1919, and from Nov., 1919; gov., Nyasaland, 1923-1924.
BOWRING, HON. SIR EDGAR RENNIE, KT. BACH. (1915).—B. 1858; mem. legis. coun., Newfoundland, 1897; high commr. for Newfoundland in London, 1919.

BOWRING, WALTER ANDREW, C.B.E. (1928).—Ed. at Eton; clk., col. audit branch, 1st Dec., 1894; served in Cyprus, 1896; asst. auditor, E.A.P. and Uganda Rly., 13th Feb., 1899; ag. local auditor, Uganda Prot., 1899; ag. local auditor, Somaliland Prot., 1900, and in 1902; local auditor, Uganda Prot., 31st May, 1902; treas., Cyprus, 11th Nov., 1909; mem. exec. and legis. couns., Cyprus, 1909; chief refugee commr., Cyprus, 1915 (during war); passed exam. in Turkish, Feb., 1919; treas., Gibraltar, 1924; admstr., Dominica, July, 1931; mem., fedl. exec. coun., Leeward Is., July, 1931.

BOWYER, ERIC BLACKLOCK.—B. 1902; ed. Whitehill Schol. and Glasgow Univ.; open scholarship, 1920; B.Sc. (with distinction), 1923; G.A. Clarke school, 1923; M.A. (1st cls. hons. in maths. and nat. philosophy), 1924; asst. prin. after compet. exam., 8th Nov., 1926; joint sec., col. survey comtce., 1927; seconded for serv. with colonial development comtce., 1st Oct., 1930; ptev. sec. to parly., under-sec. of State for Dominion affrs. (Mr. Lunn), 22nd June, 1931; do. (Mr. Malcolm MacDonald), 3rd Sept., 1931.

BOYCE, E.A., B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1898; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Uganda Prot., 1925; asst. engrnr. in charge Mbale dist., 1926; ditto, N. Prov., 1927; dep. D.P.W., Bahamas, Aug., 1928; ag. D.P.W. in 1930 and 1932.

BOYD, EDMUND B.—B. 1894; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schol. and Univ. of Aberdeen; M.A. 1916; 1st cls. hons. classics; on military service, 1916; with 60th siege batt., R.G.A., France and Belgium, Nov., 1917, to June, 1919; 2nd lieut., Oct., 1917; lieut., Apr., 1919; temporary clk. in C.O., Sept., 1919; apptd. by civ. ser. commrs., under reconstrn. scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. under-sec., May to Sept., 1921; sec. inter-deptl. comtce., income tax in cols., 1922; priv. sec. to parly., under-sec. (Mr. Ed. Wood), 4th Apr., 1922; (Mr. Ormsby Gore), 31st Oct., 1922; sec., Imp. Inst. comtce. of enquiry, 1923; priv. sec. to Lord Arnold, 26th Jan., 1924; ag. prin., 12th Dec., 1924; prin., 2nd Nov., 1925; sec., C.O. confce., 1927; sec., col. development advisory comtce., 1929; ptev. sec. to S. of S. for theCols. (Lord Passfield), 1st Apr., 1930; ptev. sec. to Mr. J.H. Thomas as S. of S. for Dom. Affrs. and for theCols., 26th Aug., 1931; do., to S. of S. for theCols. (Sir P. Cunliffe-Lister), 9th Nov., 1931.

BOYD, PETER FERGUSON.—B. 1893; telegraphist, Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1910 to Dec., 1911; postal asst., N. Rhodesia, June, 1912; ag. ch. asst., Nov., 1916 to Apr., 1917; acctnt., post office, Apr., 1920; ag. sec., Jan., 1925 to Apr., 1926; ag. dep. P.M.G., Jan.-Sept., 1927; ag. P.M.G., Sept.-Dec., 1927; col. postmr. and supk., tels., Fiji, Jan., 1928; P.M.G., 1930.

BOYD, ROBERT.—B. 1890; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; schol. (1912), B.A. (1913); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; on war serv., 1915-19; asst. contrlr. of labr., Nov., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; asst. dist. offr., Mar., 1923; registr., co-op. societies, May, 1923; asst. offr. in charge, co-op. societies, S.S., Nov., 1924; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; asst. dir., co-opern., F.M.S., and S.S., Dec., 1927; on sp. deputation to India, Nov., 1928; ag. dir., co-opern., F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1930; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930.

BOYD, WILLIAM RYDER.—B. 1887; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast and Dublin Univ.; B.A., Dublin; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1911; passed cadet, June, 1914; asst. dist. offr., May, 1915; dist. offr., July, 1920; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1923; ag. asst. treas. and collr., land rev., Malacca, May, 1928; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1928; 1st asst. sec. (B.), S.S., Mar., 1929; sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1930; ag. Br. res., Selangor in addn., Apr.-May, 1931; registr.-gen., statistics, S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1932.

BOYD-SHANNON, GODFREY ECCLESTON.—B. 1907; ed. Wellington and Cambridge; schol., St. John's Coll., 1926; 1st cls. class. tripes, pt. I., 1928, pt. II., 1929; Browne med., 1928; B.A., 1929; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O. 27th Oct., 1930.

BOYDELL, HON. THOMAS.—Served apprenticeship as engrnr. at R. & W. Hawthorns & Leslie, St. Peters, nr. Newcastle-on-Tyne; came to S. Africa, 1903; worked as a fitter in the Natal harbr. wks. and also in the S.A. rly. workshops, Durban; stood unsuccessfully as off. cand. for S. African Labour Party for the Greyville divn. of Durban at the first Union parly. elections, 1910; contested by-elect. for same const., 1912; re-elec., 1915-20, 21 and 24; dep. leader of the S. African Labour Party in parly.; chmn., parly. labour caucus; national organiser of party; min. of posts and tels. and pub. wks. in Hertzog cabinet, June, 1924; min. of lab., 1925-29; nominated senator, Sept., 1929.
BOYES, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1886; ed. Prince Albert Coll., Auckland; joined N.Z. pensions dept., 1903; registr., pensions, 1910; asst. commr., 1919; commr., 1929.

BOYLE, SIR ALEXANDER GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1921), C.M.G. (1908), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1872; ed. Charterhouse, Clifton Coll. and R.I.E.C., Cooper's Hill; priv. sec. to senior puisne judge, S. Stlitma., 1893; resig., 1895; asst. treas., Uganda Prot., Sept., 1896; ag. sec. to H.M. Comnstr., Uganda Prot., Feb., 1896, to Apr., 1898; ag. treas., Mar., 1899, to Feb., 1900; dep. treas., Apr., 1900; ag. sec. to admstn., May, 1901 to Jan., 1902; apptd. collr. with seniority of 18th Sept., 1895, 1st Apr., 1902; asst. sec., Apr., 1902; ag. sub-comnstr., E. Prov., 1st Nov., 1903; sub-comnstr., Jan., 1906; ag. comnstr., Apr. to Oct., 1907; ag. ch. sec., Apr., 1909; admstg. govt., Aug., 1909; col. sec., S. Nigeria, 1910; ret., 1920.

BOYSE, V.H., O.B.E. (1933), M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1883; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th July, 1902; on military serv. from 26th July, 1917 to 5th Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; asst. acctnt., June, 1922; sent on sp. mission to Iraq to advise on accounting methods, Nov.,

BRADBURN, COL. WILLIAM ERNEST HOMATTO.—B. 1884; served, Imp. Yeomanry, 1901; S. African Constab., Jan., 1902 to Oct., 1907; 8th King's Royal Hussars, Sept., 1914 to Apr., 1917; 1st King Edward's Horse, King's Oversea Dominions Regt. (adjutant), Apr., 1917 to July, 1919; local commdt., Cyprus mil. pol., Oct., 1919; mem., prison bd., Mar., 1921; passed exams. in mod. Greek, 1921 and 1923; gov., Athalassa juvenile prison, Dec., 1922 to June, 1924 in addn.; ag. ch. commdt., pol., July to Oct., 1923 and June to Oct., 1926; twice ag. A.D.C. to high commnr., 1924; dep. ch. commdt., pol., Aug., 1924; ag. dist. commnr., Aug., 1924; hon. A.D.C. to high commnr., Oct., 1924; nom. off., mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1926; inspr.-gen., pol. and commdt., local forces, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1927; ag. inspr., prisons in addn., May, 1927 to Nov., 1927; nom. off., mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1928 to June, 1930; off., mem., exec. coun., May to Dec., 1929; King's pol. med., 1932.

BRADFORD, Sir JOHN ROSE, Bart. (1931), K.C.M.G. (1911); M.D., D.Sc., F.R.C.P., F.R.S.—B. 1863; ed. Univ. Coll. Schi., Coll. and Hosp. George Henry Lewes student and Grocer research schlr.; late mem. of Senate, Univ. of London; physician to Univ. Coll. Hosp.; Holme lecturer on clinical medicine to Univ. Coll. hosp. med. schl.; sec. of the Royal Society; sen. med. adviser to C.O., 1912; resigned, 1924.
BRADLEY, CHARLES HUGH GRANVILLE.—B. 1901; ed. Wellington Coll.; clk., col. audit dept., June, 1925; asst. audr., Hongkong, Sept., 1925.

BRADLEY, JOHN THOMAS, O.B.E. (1933), L.R.C.P.E., L.R.C.S.E., L.F.P. and S.G.; M.D. (Brux.)—B. 1870; ed. at Queen's Coll., Belfast, and Lond. Schi. Trop. Med. (1901); undergrad. in arts, Royal Univ., Ireland; prizeman in med., midwifery, and diseases of women and children, Queen's Coll., Belfast, 1897-8; gold medallist in practical midwifery, Clifton st. Mater. Hosp.; 1st prizeman in med. and surg. diseases of children, Children's Hosp., Queen st., Belfast; late asst. med. offr., Glamorgan Co. Asylum, 1901; asst. med. offr., Seychelles, Aug., 1901; J.P. and chmn. of loc. bd. of health, 1st and 2nd divs., South Mahe; med. supt., lunatic asylum, Seychelles, Mar., 1906; ch. med. offr., 1924.

BRADLEY, KENNETH GRANVILLE, B.A.—B. 1904; ed. Wellington Coll., Berkshire, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; prob., N. Rhodesia, 1926; asst. native commnr., Jan., 1928; dist. offr., grade III, Apr., 1929; seconded as ag. asst. prim., C.O., Nov. 1928 to May, 1929; asst. sec., N. Rhodesia, June, 1930.

BRADNEY, GEORGE PRESTON.—B. 1877; ed. at Oratory Schi., Edgbaston, and Trinity Hall, Camb.; B.A., Cantab.; hon. in hist., 1899; col. audit off., June, 1901; asst. audr., B.C. Africa, Feb., 1902; asst. auditor, East Africa Prot., Sept., 1904; auditor, Fiji, and Western Pacific Protectorates, 1909; dir. of external audit, S. Sttlmtns, and F.M.S., Oct., 1914; ag. aud.-gen., S. Sttlmtns, in addition, Jan. to Feb., 1916; audr.-gen., F.M.S., May, 1919; pres., comm. to inquire into finan. position, Singapore municipality, Sept., 1921; mem., comm. to inquire into organization and expend. of govt. depts., F.M.S., etc., Feb., 1922; ag. finan. ad. and treas., F.M.S., Mar., 1929; auditor, S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1932.

BRADSTREET, FREDERICK GEORGE—B. 1884; ent. C.A.'s office, June, 1902; asst. head checking and insurance dept., May, 1919; gen. dept., Jan., 1924; sec., Zambezi Edga. inter deptl. comtes., 1928-31; dep. head, finance dept., Mar., 1931; dep. head, gen. dept. and dep. ch. elk., 1933.

BRADY, Lieut.-Col. JOHN BANKS, D.S.O.—Head mast., Grey Coll. Schi., Dec., 1901; mem. of schls., O.R.C., Feb., 1904; inspr. of schls. Rhodesia, Aug., 1909; on active serv., 1914-18; commanded 4th and 18th batta. K.R.R.C.; mem. in desps.; croix de guerre with palm leaves; dir. of educn., S. Rhodesia, 1920-21.

BRAGG, ALGERNON.—B. 1890; col. audit dept., Apr., 1915; asst. audr., Nigeria, May, 1915; mily. audit, E. African Expedy. Force, 1916-19; asst. audr., Uganda, 9th May, 1919; senr. asst. audr., Uganda, Sept., 1924; asst. audr., Uganda, for various periods, 1926-30; prin. asst. audr., Nov., 1928; ag. audr., May to Nov., 1929; dep. audr., Kenya, 1930.
BRAILSFORD, H. E.—Asst. dist. comtes., E.A.P., Jan., 1917.

BRANCH, Sir CHARLES ERNEST ST. J. BRANCH (1924), B.A., B.C.L., Durham Univ. Called to the bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1892; chief mag., St. Vincent, 1895 to Jan., 1896; asst. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islds., May, 1896; ag. atty.-gen., July to Nov., 1899; mem. lega. coun. and exec. coun., Dominica; mem. genra. legis. coun., Leeward Is., Dec., 1899; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 2 Sept., 1902; chancellor of the Diocese of Antigua, 1903-1909; pres., Antigua defence reserve force, 1903; ag. col. sec., 1903; apptd. to combined offices of col. sec. and atty.-gen., Leeward Islds., Jan., 1903; deleg. agric. confere., Jamaica, 1907; Barbados, 1908; Canadian reciprocity confere., Barbados, 1908; cotton confere., Liverpool, 1908; commr. to prepare a new and revised edition of laws of Leeward Islds., Feb., 1909; admtd. gov't. of Antigua at various occasions in the years 1902 to 1924; ag. gov., Leeward Islds., Nov.-Dec., 1907, and Apr. to Oct., 1909; atty.-gen., Jamaica, Oct., 1915; deleg. to Canada on bananas tax question, 1915; deleg. to Indent. Emigrn. Confere., Trinidad, 1916; ag. ch. just., Jamaica, Apr., 1919; rep. of Jamaica at Canada-W. Indies Trade Confere., May-June, 1920; puisne judge, sup. ct., Penang, Apr., 1921; judl. comtes., F.M.S., Jan., 1922; ch. just., Jamaica, 1923; ch. just., Ceylon, 3rd Jan., 1925; ret., 1926.

BRANCH, EDMUND RALPH.—M.R., Ch. Edin.; dist. med. offr., Dominica, 1902; dist. med. Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, 1902; med. super. Cunningham hosp., and surgeon of the govt., St. Kitts, Feb., 1903; med. offr., dist. 2, St. Kitts, Nov., 1916; served with R.A.M.C. during the war.

BRANCH, STANLEY, M.B.—Ch. Edin., 1898; D.T.M., Liverpool, 1907; D.M., St. Lucia, 1899; Uganda, 1900; St. Vincent, 1902; house surg., St. Lucia, 1904; D.M.O., St. Lucia, 1912; seconded as dir. under Internat. Bd. campaign for eradication of hook-worm disease, St. Lucia, 1914-24; ch. med. and med. offr., St. Vincent, 1925.
BRANDON, KENNETH REVIE.—B. 1888; asst. of cts., Jamaica, 1919; ag. regar., sup. ct., Apr. to Dec., 1921; ag. res. mag., Oct. and Nov., 1921; and Nov., 1924 to Aug., 1925; res. mag., Sept., 1925.
NISON, VICTOR CECIL, M.C., A.R.C.S., B.S., A.I.C.—B. 1896; 1st ast. analyst, pt., Hong Kong, 1924; ag. analyst, 1929-30; analyst, Sept., 1932.
SNETT, NORMAN VINCENT, M.A., Dipl. Landant.—B. 1894; ed. Giggleswick School, Cambridge; R.F.A., 1914-18 (esp.); ast. cons., forests, Kenya Col., enr. A.O.F., Kenya, 1928; cons., forests, 1929.

SSEY-EDWARDS, H.—Veterinary offr., Feb., 1910; ag. dir. of agric., 1919; st. offr., Kenya, Apr., 1920; ag. dep. ch., 1924; dep. ch. vet. offr., 1924; ag. ch., 1927; ch. vety. offr., 1929; dep. dir., imal industry, 1931.

WN, ALAN C.—B. 1878; ed. "Queen's gymn. schl.", Walsall, and St. Mark's Chelsea (1st ed. Queen's scholarship); certif. (Ed. of Duke, England); matric. Univ., 1898; ast. mast., diocesean schl., ong., 1901-1909; jun. ast. mast., Queen's Long Kong, 1910; lecturer, Tech. Inst., Kong, 1910-1912; headmaster, Wansai hl., 1913; headmaster, Yaumati schl., 1915; seconded ser., 1917; 2nd lieut., 1917; 1st lieut., rved in France, 1917 to 1919; demobilised, ep. regir., Hong Kong Univ., 1919; ter., Wansai schl., 1919; headmaster, schl., 1919; ast. mast., cls. II., 1920; Victoria Br. schl., 1920; ast. mast., 1921; headmr., Wansai schl., 1921; ditto, schl., 1921; ast. mast., Queen's Coll. and schl., 1922; headmr., Wansai schl., admir., Yaumati schl., 1924-28; dir., tech. sch.; inspr., English schls., 1933.
LEY, ALEXANDER JOHN, B.A.—B. 1907; sch. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; ast. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Apr., 1930; passed sod. Greek, May, 1931; ag. ch. ast., 1932.

NE, CHARLES VALENTINE.—B. 1877; ton Combe schl., and Pembroke Coll., ye (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., ast. to gov't. agt., N.C. Prov., Apr., ast. collr. cust., Trincomalee, May, it. gov't. agt., Mullaitivu, Feb., 1906; Negombo, Jan., 1909; Badulla, Feb., it. land settlment, offr., Oct., 1909; agt., Colombo dist., June, 1912; ditto, Uva, July, 1912; ditto, Colombo dist., 2; offr., Cls. II., Jan., 1913; ag. dist. ftna, June, 1914; ast. gov't. agt., Kalu., 1914; ast. gov't. agt., Colombo, Jan., gov't. agt., Kalutara, May, 1916; gov't. Prov., Feb., 1920; ag. contr., rev., 1927; addnl. ditto, Aug., 1927; agt., Feb., 1929 and Apr. to Sept., 1930; ands., July, 1931.

HAY, SIDNEY, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. Ripon Coll. and St. John's Coll., e; tech. instr., tech. inst., P.W.D. K., Apr., 1911; ast. engrnr., Oct., 1914; Sept., 1918; exec. engrnr., Apr., 1920; nr., i/c, tech. schl., K. Lumpur, Dec., sr. exec. engrnr., hydraulics, Selangor, ); exec. engrnr., Seremban, Mar., 1932.

BRARLEY, L. J.—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to ld. chamberlain's office, Feb., 1901; transferred to C.O., 18th Feb., 1901; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918; staff offr., 7th Apr., 1933.

REBBNER, GEORGE GUSTAV RADLOFF, M.A. (Edin.), LL.B. (Lond.), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple).—B. 1883; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; prte. sec. to Gen. Botha, 1916-19, and attended Paris Peace Conf., 1919; prte. sec. to Gen. Smuts, 1919-21; ast. crown pros., Johannesburg, 1922; 1st prof. ast. to atty.-gen., Pretoria, 1924-27; law adviser to crown, Jan., 1927.

BREEN, MICHAEL JAMES.—B. 1884; ed. at Clongowes Wood Coll. and Univ. Coll., Dublin; B.A., math., science (honours) in Royal Univ. of Ireland, 1906; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1907; passed cadet, 1911; ag. 2nd ast. regist. gen., 1911; ag. ast. P.M.G., 1911; ag. supt., impts. and expts., 1913; ast. P.M.G., 1913; ast. dist. offr., Jan., 1914; ag. ast. col. sec., 1914 to 1915; on mil. serv., 1916-19; ag. ast. P.M.G., 1920; ag. P.M.G., 1920 to 1921; ast. treas., 1921; P.M.G., 1924; ag. col. treas., 1929 and 1933; chmn., retrenchment comtee., July, 1930.

BRRTN, REGINALD WALTER.—B. 1883; trooper, S.A.C., Boer War, 1900-02 (Queen's Med. 4 clasps); ast. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, Jan., 1911; 2nd lieut., M.M.G.S., 1915; served in France, 1915-17 (M.C.); Mesopotamia, 1919-20 (Iraq med.); resumed duty as ast. conserv., forests, 1920; dep. conserv., forests, Dec., 1930; ag. conserv., forests, 1930-32.
BRETT, E. A.—B. 1880; apptd. to emigrants' information office, 2nd July, 1906; overseas settlment office, 1919.

BRETT, Francis William, C.M.G. (1934)—B. 1885; Cape Mounted Rifles, Nov., 1903-05; Royal Warwickshire Regt., Jan., 1906-08; ast. dist. commr., R.A.F., Sept., 1908; priv. sec. to gov', Oct., 1912-15; and sec. to high commr., Zanzibar, Jan., 1914-15; seconded to Royal Flying Corps, 1915-16; dist. commr. (seconded), Dec., 1915; dist. polit. offr., German E. Africa, provisional admin., Nov., 1916; adminstr. offr., 1st grade, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; prov. commr., Jan., 1926.
BREWIS, HELEN ELIZABETH STUART.—B. 1904; ed. Sherborne Schl. for Girls, Dorset, Froebel Educln. Inst. Training Coll. for Teachers, Roehampton; mistress, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Sept., 1927.

BRICE-SMITH, HUGH MIDDLETON, M.A.—B. 1884; ed. Pocklington and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 1909; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1917; ag. res. in ch., Niger Prov., 1927; cls. I, grade I, admstive. serv., 1929.

BRIDGE, BLACKER FRANK, B.A. (Dublin), Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1930.—B. 1891; ed. St. Columba's Coll. and Dublin Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; ast. contr. of lab., June, 1918; ast. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Feb., 1922; dist. offr., Bentong, Apr., 1922; dep. contr., lab., Mar., 1924; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1926; dep. pub. pros., Negri Sembilan and Pahang, June, 1929; mag., Ipoh, Aug., 1930; offr., cls. II., 1931; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, Nov., 1932.

BRIDGES, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Tom, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.B. (1918), D.S.O. (1904), F.R.G.S.—B. 1872; Grenadier Guards; served S. African War, 1899-1901; Somaliland, 1902-4; European War, 1914-15; mil. attache at the Hague, Brussels, Copenhagen and Christiania, 1910-14; lieut.-col., 4th Hussars, 1914-16; brev., col., 1916; maj.-gen., temp. lieut.-col., 1917; col., Dragoon Gds., 1920; commodr., Br. mly. mission to Smyrna, 1920; gov., S. Australia, 1922-1927.

BRIDGES, WILLIAM FRANCIS NOEL, D.S.O.—B. 1890; ed. Melbourne C. of E. Gram. Schl. and Trin. Coll., Melbourne Univ.; suvr.-on-agreement, F.M.S., Sept., 1912; served A.I.F. Gallipoli and France, 1915-18; ast. supt., surveys, F.M.S., Jan., 1919; supt., surveys, F.M.S., Oct., 1927; add. suvr.-gen.'s office, Jan., 1928; ag. ast. survr. gen., F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1929 and Aug., 1930 to Feb., 1931; ast. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Dec., 1931.
BRIERCLIFFE, RUPERT O.B.E., M.B., Ch.B., B.Sc., D.P.H. (Mano.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.).—B. 1889; dep. dir., health, Palestine, 1920; dir., med. and san. services, Ceylon, July, 1930.
BRIERLEY, FRANK NORRIS.—B. 1887; ent. Trinidad civ. serv., June, 1906; warden, county of Coronii, Mar., 1933.

BRINK, MAJOR-GEN., JACOB JACOB KRISTEN, D.T.D., D.S.O.—Joined civ. serv., S. African Repub., 1896; served Mpufu (native) campaign, 1898; Zoutpansberg and Anglo-Boer War, 1899-1920; elected veldt cornet, S. African Repub. forces, 1901; capt., permanent force (staff), Union defence forces, 1912; promoted major, and served as dist. staff offr., No. 6 and later as dist. staff offr., No. 7 mil. dist., 1914; brig.-major, 1st mounted brig., German S.W.A. campaign, 1915; temp. lieut.-col., Mar., 1915; dist. staff offr., No. 6 mil. dist., with rank of temp. lieut.-col., whilst so emp., Aug., 1915; brev. lieut.-col., Sept., 1915; brig.-major, 2nd mounted brig., E. Africa, Mar., 1916; gen. staff offr. with rank of col. headqrs., 3rd E. African divn., Apr., 1916; resumed duty as dist. staff offr., No. 6 mil. dist., Dec., 1916; transf. to defence headqrs., and assumed duty as ast. adj.-gen., defence headqrs., Sept., 1917; ag. ch. of gen. staff and adj.-gen., with temp. rank of col., Nov., 1919; lieut.-col., 1st grade staff offr., Nov., 1919; brig.-gen., and ch. of gen. staff, Sept., 1920; combined post of ch. of gen. staff and sec. for defence, 1st Oct., 1922; maj.-gen., Nov., 1929; sec. for defence and G.O.C., Union Defence Forces, May, 1933; 1914-15 Star, Br. War Med., Victory Med., D.S.O., (1917), French Croix de Guerre; men. in despe., for serv. in G.S.W.A. campaign (1918), medal, Anglo-Boer oorlog.

BRINK, COLONEL CAROLUS, D.T.D., D.S.O.—B.1879; served Jameson Raid, S. African Republic, 1896; Mpufu campaign, Zoutpansberg, S. African Republic, 1898; Anglo-Boer War, Heidelberg commando, staff General Spruyt, 1899-1901; German S.W. Africa campaign, as brig.-major, 2nd Mounted Brig., Northern Front, 1914-15; German E. African campaign as G.S.O.I., 2nd E. African divn., 1916-17; Europe, Western Front, 1917-18; qualified, staff course, Bloemfontein, 1914; qualified, sen. staff course, Cambridge, 1919; qtrmr.-gen. Union Defence Forces; ment. in despe.; Croix de Guerre (French); med., Anglo-Boer War.

BRINK, DANIEL.—Ck., C.C., Kimberley, 1895; ck., deeds, Vryburg, 1897; ck., C.C. Kimberley, 1898; ck., deeds, Vryburg, 1899; ck., res. mag., Simonstown, 1899; ck., deeds, Cape Town, 1900; ditto, Vryburg, 1902; ditto, Kimberley, 1906; ch. ck., C.C. and R.M., Piquetberg, 1908; ch. ck., deeds, Vryburg, 1908; 1st grade Union ck., deeds, Vryburg, 1912; examr. (ast. regar.), Vryburg, 1913; ast. regar., deeds, Windhoek, 1921; ditto, Pietermaritzburg, 1922; ditto, Pretoria, 1925; ditto, Cape Town, 1927; regar., deeds, Pietermaritzburg, Oct., 1928; ditto, Cape Town, Jan., 1932.

BRISTOW, CHARLES FRANCIS.—B. 1882; 2nd Middlesex, R.G.A., 1899-1901; S. African constab., 1902-07; served in S. African war (Queen's medal and clasps); Moulmein Artillery, 1908-10; ast. inspr. of police, R.A.P., Jan., 1911; passed pol. promotion exam., Aug., 1911; passed Kiswahili exam., lower standard; inspr. of police, Mar., 1913; ast. supt. of police, 1st Apr., 1914; during war period 1914, attached to Moulmein Rifles as lieut.; on active service, R.A.P., 1915-17, awarded "1914-15" Star, War and Victory med.; resumed civil duties as ast. supt. of police, 1918; ag. qtrmr., pol. and praca dept., Oct., 1920 to Mar., 1921; imigr. offr., Apr., 1921 to June, 1922; supt. of pol., 1st Jan., 1923; headgr. supt., Jan., 1926.
BRITTEN, EDWIN HAROLD.—B. 1886; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Midie Temple, 1909; admitted bar, Mauritius, 1909; advoc., sup. ct., Natal, 1911; judge, native high ct. of Natal, 1930.

BROCKLEBANK, S. H., M.B.E.—B. 1877; ed. Calday Schol.; served Boer War, 1899-1902; ast. supt., repatriation dept., 1902; acct. gen., dept., 1904; inland rev. dept., 1905; defense dept., 1914; survr., inland rev., 1921; recr., rev., Krugersdorp, 1928; ditto, Bloemfontein, 1929; Durban, Feb., 1930.

BROCKMAN, SIR EDWARD LAWSON, K.C.M.G. (1913), C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1865; cadet, Straits Stltmts., 1886; passed in Malay, 1888; 3rd mag. Penang, June, 1890; dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1892; 2nd ast. col. sec., June, 1896; ast. ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, June, 1896; collr. land rev., Malacca, 1898; commr., court of requests, Singapore, Aug., 1902; ast. col. sec. and clk. of coun., Sept., 1903; ag. res. gen., May, 1907; confirmed as fed. sec., continuing to act also as res. gen., July, 1907 to Feb., 1908; ag. res., Perak, Apr., 1908; res., Pahang, Nov., 1908; res., Negri Sembilan, 1st Jan., 1919; col. sec., S. Stltmts., 1910; chief sec., F.M.S., 1911; ret., Sept., 1920; apptd. agt., Malay State Infrn. Agency, Nov., 1920.

BROMLEY, Rear-Adml. ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1876; ed. Farnborough Schol.; H.M.S. Britannia; joined navy, 1892; lieut., 1898; commdr., 1908; capt., 1915; served Russo-Jap. War, 1914-18; H.M.S. Courageous, 1916-19; d. capt. to Vice-Adml. Napier commdr. light cruiser force; ret. list, 1922; rear adml., ret., 1928; a gentlemen usher to the King since 1927; priv. sec. (ceremonial and reception) to the S. of S. (Lord Passfield), 1931.
BROOK, THOMAS.—B. 1873; ed. Lawrence dist. high schl., N.Z.; various positions N.Z. and survey dept., 1919-1925; valuer-gen., 1926.

BROOKES, VICTOR S.—B. 1888; passed sen. Cambridge local exam., 1906; apptd. coppr. col. sec.'s office, Antigua, 1906; ag. clk. to regar. gen. of births, etc., Jan. to Dec., 1908; cashier St. John's savings bank, Sept., 1908; accnt. ditto, June, 1909; ag. acctnt., treasur. dept., Jan. to Sept., 1909; clk. to commr. and exec. ccna. Virgin Is., Apl., 1911; rev. offr., St. Kitts, Nov., 1914; acctnt., treasur., St. Kitts; ag. audit clk. St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918.

BROOKS, ARCHIBALD JOSEPH, F.L.S., F.C.S., F.R.H.S.—B. 1881; entd. Royal Gardens, Kew, 1902; offr. in charge, agric. schl., Dominica, 1903; awarded R.H.S. teacher's certificate, 1904; ag. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, 2nd June to Dec., 1904, and July to Dec., 1908; mem. of coun. of agric. soc'y., Dominica, 1905; deleg. to agric. confce., Trinidad, 1906; ast. sec., govt. permanent exhibn. comttee, 1906; mem. of Dominica reserve force, 1904-11; winner of Lady Sweet-Escott challenge shield; ast. agric. supt., St. Lucia, 1911; offr. in charge, Reunion Estate, 1913; land offr., 1914; agricultural supt., Dec., 1914; dir. of agr., Gambia, 1922; del. to Imp. Agric. Research Confce., London, 1927; del. to confce. of Empire Meteorologists, London, 1929; del. to Imp. Mycological Confce., Lond., 1929; author of "The West Indian Lime," and other works.

BROOKS, MAJOR LAWRENCE ABBOTT WALLACE, F.R.G.S., etc.—Ed., Woolwich and abroad; ent. Royal Arty., 1900; served, S. African War, 1901-2 (Queen's medal with clasp, ment. in desps.); Royal Canadian Field Arty., 1902-5; special serv., Russo-Japanese War, 1905-6; served, Natal Native Rebellion, 1906-7 (medal with clasp); road-mapping, Natal and Zululand, 1908; special serv. in Canada, 1909-10; Chinese Revolution, defence of Shameen, Canton, 1911; in Far East and Australia, 1911-14; served, Togoland, 1914; France and Flanders, 1915; anti-aircraft home defence, 1916; Egypt, and Palestine, 1917-18 ("1914-15" Star, "General Serv." and "Victory" medls., ment. in desps.); Woolwich, 1919; Australia and New Zealand, 1919-20; ast. travelling comtner., Gambia, Jan., 1921; ast. travelling comtner., S. Bank Prov., Nov., 1924; travelling comtner., July, 1926; senr. ast. col. sec., Apr., 1932; ast. col. sec., May-Sept., 1932.

BROOKS, R. L.—B. 1900; ed. Charterhouse; Artists Rifles; commsd., Guards' Machine Gun Regt., 1920; passed inter. exam., Surv'r's Inst.; dipl., forestry, Edinburgh, 1922; ast. conserv., forests, Uganda, 1922; seconded to adminstr., 1924; dep. conserv., forests, Trinidad, 1925.

BROWN, CHARLES CUTHBERT.—B. 1888; ed. Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; schlr., 1909; Bell schlr., 1908; 1st cls. class. tripes, 1910; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; ast. list. offr., Brusas, 1912-13; ast. sec. to res., Perak, 1913-15; 3rd ast. sec., Fed. secretariat and priv. sec. to oh. sec., 1915-19; ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1919; dist. offr., Nov., 1922; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1928; offr., cls. IB, Apr., 1931; Br. advr., Trengganu, June, 1933.

BROWN, FRANK LESLIE, M.C.—B. 1896; ed. Wilson Gram. Sch.; served in France, 1916-19; lieut., M.C., bar (ment.in desps.); prob., N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1919; ast. native comtner., Feb., 1922; native comtner., Oct., 1926; dist. offr., grade III, Apr., 1929; do., grade II, Apr., 1931; sec. as ast. sec., Aug., 1931.

BROWN, GAVIN ROBERT LOCKHART.—B. 1882; gov't travelling schl. master, Falkland Is., Dec., 1914; ast. gov't schl. mast., Jan., 1915; ast. local audtr., Apr., 1915; 2nd olk., treas. and cust., Feb., 1916; ch. clk., treas. and cust. and ship'g mast., Mar., 1917; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Oct., 1920; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1928 to Feb., 1931; harbrmr., Sept., 1929; ast. col. sec., May, 1929 to Feb., 1930; mag., registr., sup. ct., col. postmr. and harbrmr., Feb., 1931; pvt. sec. to gov., June, 1931.

BROWN, GEORGE PRITCHARD.—B. 1885; served with E. Africa pol. service batt., 1914-17; capt., Aug., 1915; Turkana expedit., 1915 (A.G.S. med.); ast. supt. of pol., Kenya, Apr., 1916; supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1923; staff offr. to comntr., pol., 1926; ag. dep. comntr., pol., July to Oct., 1931; order (serving brother) of St. John of Jerusalem; ast. comntr., pol., 1932.

BROWN, GERALD HORACE.—B. 1878; ed. Ottawa pub. schls. and Coll. Inst.; journalist and parly. correspondent; ast. dep. min. of labour, Canada, 1909-17; sec., reconstruction and development comtne.; re-apptd. ast. dep. min. of labour, 1920; Canadian del., League of Nations, Internat. Lab. Confce., Geneva, 1921.

BROWN, HARRY WHIDDEN, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1868; ed. at Acadia Coll., and Dalhousie Univ.; called to Nova Scotia bar, 1893; ent. civ. serv., Can., 1896, as priv. sec. to min. of militia and defence; dir. of contracts, militia dept., 1904; comntr., war purchasing comntr., 1919, returning to militia dept., on the dissolution of that comntr., 1920; ast. dep. min. of militia, 1920.

BROWN, HON. JAMES T., K.C., B.A.—B. 1871; ed. pub. and high schls., Huntingdon, Quebec, and McGill Univ.; called to bar, 1896; K.C., 1907; elec. to Sask. legislature, 1904; judge, sup. ct. of Sask., 1910; ch. just., ct. of King's Bench, 1918.

BROWN, JAMES VICTOR RAEBURN.—B.1897; ed. Falkirk High Sch. and Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin.; B.Sc. (Agric.) Edin., F.S.I.; Arg. and Suth. Hldrs., attd. R.F.C., Egypt, France, Italy, 1916-19; E.C.G. Corp.; post-grad. stud., Camb., 1925; prov. supdt.agr., Sierra Leone, 1926; agr.offr., Tanganyika, 1930; seconded, Zanzibar, 1931.

BROWN, JOHN DUNCAN.—B. 1884; ed. Clare Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1906; seconded ast. supt. of police, Colombo, Mar., 1907; ast. supt. of police, Kandy, Aug., 1907; W. Prov., Feb., 1908; Kalutara, Feb., 1909; off. ast. to gov't. agt., N.C. Prov., June, 1910; pol. mag., Karunegala, Jan., 1911; seconded for service under excise comntr., May, 1912; ast. comntr. of excise, S. divn., Jan., 1913; ditto, N.W. divn., Jan., 1914; ast. gov't. agt., Mannar, Dec., 1914; on military duty, 1917; ast. gov't. agt., Matara, Sept., 1919; dist. judge, Negombo, July, 1924; ast. gov't. agt., Kalutara, June, 1925; ditto, Kegalla, Jan., 1926; gov't. agt., Jaffna, Jan., 1928; do., Galle, Sept., 1931; offr., cls. I, grade I, 1932.

BROWN, KENNETH VINCENT.—B. 1890; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1915; mag., eastern dist., Trinidad, Dec., 1924; ast. mag., county of St. George West, 1926; mag., county of Victoria, June, 1930; do., St. George West, 1932.
BROWN, PHILIP FENTON, B.A.—B. 1903; ed. Rugby and Trinity Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1924; 2nd cls. honos. philos. and econ.; ast. mast., Achinota, Gold Coast, 1925.

BROWN, RALPH PHELPS.—B. 1877; ed. pub. and high sch., Nova Scotia and Ont.; 2nd cls. clk., militia dept., Can., 1904; 1st cls. clk., 1907; ast. acctnt., 1910; ast. acctnt. and paym. gen., 1917; ch. acctnt., 1918; gen. audr., 1920.
BROWN, ROBERT ALFRED.—B. 1888; draughtsman, Kano and Kaduna waterworks, Nigeria, 1914; ast. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1915; senr. exec. engr., 1927.

BROWN, ROBERT ALLAN.—B. 1897; ent. P.O. savings bank, Dec., 1912; trans. to gen. bd. of control for Scotland, Feb., 1914; apptd. ast. clk. after compet. exam., Dec., 1914; junr. clk., 26th Apr., 1915; on mily. serv., July, 1915 to Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf'd. to C.O., 27th Apr., 1922; seconded as acctnt., treasy., Cyprus, 1928 to 1930; treasy. acctnt., Gambia, Aug., 1930; asst. reor. gen., 1933.

BROWN, THOMAS ALEXANDER.—B. 1900; ed. Marlborough Coll. and Wellington Cadet Coll., S. India; 2nd lieut., 16th Cav., India Army, Feb., 1920; 1st lieut., Feb., 1921; active serv. Dazinstan Field Force, Feb., 1920-May, 1922; lieut., regular army R. of O., June, 1922; B.A. (Oriel Coll.), Oxford; barrister-at-law; 2nd cls. hona., jurisprudence; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1926; crown coun., Gold Coast, July, 1933.
BROWN, WILLIAM TWEDDLE STODDART, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1903; ed. St. Bees Sch., Edinburgh Univ. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; asst. commr., forestry, Gold Coast, 1926.
BROWNE, GEORGE GILHAM.—B. 1898; asst. survey dept., S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1924; acct., P.W.D., S.S., Apr., 1932.

BROWNE, GEORGE SIMONAL, C.M.G. (1931), M.A.—Ed. Malvern Coll. and Hartford Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law; B. 1880; asst. res., N. Nigeria, July, 1906; 3rd cls. res., June, 1915; dist. offr., Jan., 1914; 1st cls. disto., July, 1914; res., Mar., 1920; staff grad., 1920; secy., N.Provs., 1921-25; sec., native affrs., 1929; ag. lieut. gov., N.P., May, 1930; ch. commr., N.P., 1933.

BROWNE, PERCY WEST.—B. 1868; ent. civ. serv., Barbados as clk. to harbr., June, 1882; ch. clk., Bridgetown petty debt ct., Oct., 1901; clk., asst. ct. of appl. in addn., 1913; visiting just., Glendairy prison, 1916; acctnt., G.P.O., 1918; ch. clk., treasy., 1919; ch. offr., cust., 1922; has acted from time to time as pol. mag., govr., Glendairy prison, col. treas. and compt., cust.; audr.-gen., July, 1925.

BROWNE, ROLAND PAUL LAMBERT.—B. 1888; ed. at Marlborough Coll.; served as "élève consulaire" at British Consulate, Danzig, June, 1908 to July, 1909; asst. to res. commr., New Hebrides, Feb., 1912; commr., Cyprus, Jan., 1924.

BROWNE, VERNON DENNISON.—B. 1892; secretariat clk., N.E.R. admstr., May, 1909; mag. clk., N. Rhodesia (Fort Jameson), Aug., 1911; treasy. clk., Mar., 1913; asst. acct., Mar., 1914; served in R.A.F., 1918-19; senr. asst. treas., N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1930; ag. dep. treas. on various occasions, 1927-31.
BROWNELL, JOHN ALBERT.—B. 1890; asst. acct., Nigerian marine, 1914; senr. acct., 1922; ch. acct., 1926.
BRUCE, ROBERT RANDOLPH, B.Sc., C.E., F.R.S.C.—B. 1863; ed. Glasgow Univ.; lieut.-gov., Br. Columbia, 24th Feb., 1926.

BRUCE, CAPT. THE RT. HON. STANLEY MELBOURNE, C.H. (1927), P.C. (1923), M.C. (1915).—Member of the H. of R. for Flinders since 1918; served in European War, 1915-17; capt., R. Fusiliers, Dec., 1914; twice wounded and invalided from the Army, Apr., 1917; Croix-de-Guerre, 1916; resigned chairmanship of firm of Paterson, Laing and Bruce, on appt. as Federal treas., Hughes ministry, Dec., 1921; prime min., Commonwealth of Australia, 1923-29; hon. min. and res. min. in London, Lyons ministry, 1932.

BRUCE, THOMAS DUNDAS HOPE, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1885; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; final hona. schl. of jurisprudence, 1906; admitted solr., sup. ct. of England, 1911; crown solr., Fiji, Nov., 1913; ag. atty.-gen., Jan. to May, 1915; ag. chief pol. mag., ag. regis. of sup. ct., ag. regis. of titles, ag. curator of intestate and vacant estates, ag. public trustee, May to July, 1916; ag. atty.-gen., Dec., 1916; tempy. M.L.C., Aug., 1917; on active serv., Dec., 1917 to May, 1919; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1919 to May, 1920; dep. commr., Western Pacific, May, 1920; ag. res. commr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., May, 1920 to May, 1921; ag. atty.-gen., Fiji, May to Dec., 1921 and Mar. to Apr., 1922; appd. a judi. commr. for the W. Pacific, Dec., 1921; seconded for sp. serv. with the W. Pacific High Commr. to preside over certain murder trials in the Solomon Is., Dec., 1921 to Mar., 1922; res. mag., Jamaica, July, 1922; called to bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1925; solr.-gen., Kenya, Apr., 1927; ag. atty.-gen. in 1932 and 1933; King's proctor, Dec., 1928.
BRUCE, THOS. ROBERT.—B. 1862; 2nd clk. col. sec.'s off., St. Helena, Jan., 1898; postmaster, St. Helena, Apr., 1898.
BRUMACK, DOUGLAS O.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1919; 1st grade admstr. offr., Kenya, Dec., 1930.
BRUNNEN, JOHN EWART.—B. 1893; admr. and dockyard schls.; H.M. Navy, 1914; admr., 1915; engnr., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. asst.elec.engnr., 1922; ag. sen.asst.elec.engnr., 1924.
BRUNT, ABRAHAM DE VILLIERS.—B. 1879; Cape div. serv., 1897; Tvl. civ. serv., 1901; commr., Robben Is., 1915; ch. clk., health dept., 1919; under sec., health dept., Union of S. Africa, 1932.

BRYAN, SIR HERBERT, K.B.E. (1925), D.S.O. (1917), C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1865; 2nd lieut. Linc. regt., June, 1892; lieut., Jan., 1894; capt., Manchester regt., July, 1899; brev major, Nov., 1900; served in W. Africa, 1897-8; employed in Lagos Hinterland; also in operations on the Niger, including expedn. to Bassema (ment. in desp., medal with two clasps); served in N. Nigeria, 1900, slightly wounded (ment. in desp., clasp); served in operations in Ashanti, 1900; on the staff, D.A.A.G. (ment. twice in desp., medal); chief staff offr., Gambia expdn., 1901 (ment. in desp., medal with clasp); staff offr., W.A.F.F., Oct., 1901; employed at C.O., May 1902, to Nov., 1903; ag. col. sec. G. Coast, Nov., 1903; col. sec., G. Coast, Feb., 1904; ag. gov., on various occasions, 1904-09 and 1911-12; col. sec. and rev. commr., Jamaica, Aug., 1914; served in France, Flanders and Salonika, 1915-18; Constantinople, 1918-19; brev. lieut.-col., bvt. col., D.S.O.; ag. gov., Jamaica, on various occasions, 1919, 1922, 1924-25; ret., 1925.
BRYANT, G.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apr., 1918; served with R.N.V.R., Sept., 1918, to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 24th Aug., 1927.

BRYANT, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1887; ed Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (math. scholar); R.A., 1909; M.A., 1916; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1919; passed final exam. in Malay, Dec., 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Mar., 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, June, 1913; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, S.S., Apr., 1915; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Mar., 1917; sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Aug., 1918; offr., cls. IV, 1st Jan., 1919; sec. tin mining commn., in addition, Aug., 1919; and for sp. duty, Federal secretariat, Kuala Lumpur, July, 1922; ag. asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1924 and Apr., 1927; temp. asst. prin., C.O. May, 1926; ag. dist. offr., Raub, Mar., 1927; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1927; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1927; offr., cls. I., B. Jura, 1930; pres., munic. commn., Penang, Oct., 1930; ag. res. coun., Penang in addn., July, 1932.

BRYSON, HUGH PATTERSON, M.C.—B. 1888; ed. Royal Sch., Armagh; war serv., 1916-19; 2nd lieut., Tank Corps, 1917; France, 1918-19; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attached to dist. office, Kinta, B. Gajah; offr., cls. V, 4th ass't sec. to govt., F.M.S., Apr., 1924; offr., cls. V, att'mt. offr., Trengganu, Apr., 1928; ag. sec. to res., N. Sembilan, May, 1931; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933.

BUCHANAN, JOHN CMOIL RANKIN, M.D. (Edin.), M.R.C.P. (Ed.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.)—B. 1896; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; mily. serv., 1915-19; ment. in desps., 1916; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1926; med. offr., Somaliland, June, 1928; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1931.

BUCHANAN-SMITH, CAPT. WALTER, C.M.G. (1930), M.C. (1918)—B. 1879; ed. Repton Schol.; Br. N. Borneo civ. serv., 1903; asst. dist. commrs., S. Nigeria, 1909; ag. commrs., lands, 1912, 1914 and 1916; att'd., Nigeria Regt. in Cameroons 1914-15 and in E. Africa 1916-18; ag. commrs., lands, 1916; 1st cls. dist. offr., 1918; res., 1921; ag. prin., asst. sec., S. Prova, 1921, 1923 and 1926; ag. sec., S. Prova, 1923 and 1925; staff grade, 1926; ag. lieut.-gov., S. Prova, 1928-29 and 1930; lieut.-gov., S. Prova, 1930; O.A.G., Nigeria, Sept.-Nov., 1930.
