BUCKLAND, LANCASTER LIDDLE ROBBIE, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1897; ed. Dundee Schol., Univ. of Cape Town, S.A.; Artists Rifles, 1918; asst. engr., irrign. dept., S.A., 1919-23; asst. engr., Tanganyika Territory, 1928; exec. engr., 1929.

BUCKLE, DOROTHY M., O.B.E. (1951)—B. 1883; ed. Howard Gardens Munic. Secondary Schol., Cardiff and Whitleands Training Coll., Chelsea; 1st cls. B. of E. teachers' certif.; asst. prin., Raffles girls schol., Singapore, 1908; sg. prin., 1909; prin., 1911.

BUHAGIAR, HON. FRANCESCO, LL.D. (1901).—Ed., Malta Lyceum; matric., Malta Univ., 1894; called to bar, 1902; el. to rep., 4th dist., first Maltese parl., Oct., 1921; min. of just., July, 1922; judge, Sept., 1924.

BULKLEY, GEORGE VICARY OWEN, A.M.I. Meeh. E.—B.1892; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey and Owen's Coll., Manchester; 1st cls., rly. operating, Lond. Schol. of Economics; port man., Kenya and Uganda rly. and harbors., 1926; gen. man., Nigerian rly., 1933; author "Mechanical Appliances for handling Railway Traffic" (1922), "Railway and Seaport Freight Movement" (1926).

BULL, HAROLD ROBERT, M.A., Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1888; ed. Framlingham and Queen's Coll., Camb.; open entrance scholar, Queen's Coll., 1907; foundation scholar, 1908; 1st cls. hona., Nat. Sci. Tripos, Part 1, 1909; 3rd cls. Maths. Tripos, Part 1, 1910; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1911; attached to Chinese protectorate, Dec., 1911; passed cadet; offr. collr. stamp duties, Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, Apr., 1924; dep. cont., lab., Dec., 1924; sheriff, Singapore, Mar., 1925; 2nd mag., do., Oct., 1925; asst. offr. assignee, do., Jan., 1928; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1929; ag. dist. judge, Singapore, Apr., 1930.
BULL, OSWYN BOYS, M.A.—B. 1882; dir., Leroholi tech. schol., Basutoland, 1931.
BULLOCK, G.—Asst. traffic manager, Uganda Railway, Apr., 1919; senr. asst. aspt., transportation dept., Mar., 1927.

BULISTRODE, B. P. E.—B. 1887; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1913; asst. priv. sec. to govt., Leeward Is., 1907-1909; chief clk., govt. office, and clk. of couns., St. Lucia, Oct., 1914; ag. inspr. of schools, St. Lucia, Jan. to May, 1915; priv. sec. to admstr., Feb., 1916 to Apr., 1917; seconded from govt. office for sp. duties, Feb., 1917; priv. sec. to ag. admstr., Apr., 1917; trans. to Gambia as 2nd asst. recvr.-gen., Oct., 1918; offr.-in-charge, secretariat on several occasions between 1920 and 1926; ag. clk., leg. coun., Oct., 1920; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1921; clk., exec. and leg. couns., on many occasions; ag. inspr., schls., Apr. to Sept., 1926; asst. col. sec., Gold Coast, 1931.

BUNBURY, CREOL MOLESWORTH.—Ed. privately; employed at Crown Bridge Works, W. Bromwich, 1895-1898; Great Central, Great Northern and City Rlyws., 1898-1900; served in S. African War, 1900-1902 (medal with 4 clasps); Central S. African Rlyws., 1902-1904; asst. engnr., Uganda Rlyw., 16th Jan., 1906; maintenance engnr., Jan., 1926; ag. ch. engnr., 1925.

BUNNY, JOSEPH, F.L.S.—B. 1885; ed. Reading Schol. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; asst. conserv., forests, S. Nigeria, 1912; conserv., 1918; senr. conserv., 1922; attended Imp. forestry conf., in Australia and N.Z. as del. for Nigeria and Sierra Leone, 1928; ag. dir., forests, Nigeria in 1929.
BUNTING, BENJAMIN, N.D.A., F.L.S.—B. 1889; asst. agriculturist, F.M.S., Mar., 1914; ag. do., Dec., 1914; agriculturist, Mar., 1920; on sp. duty in Ceylon, Jan.-Feb., 1930.
BUNTING, ROBERT HUGH, F.L.S.—Asst. dir. of agriculture, Sierra Leone, 1913-15; asst. dir. of agric. and govt. mycologist, Gold Coast, 30th Jan., 1915; ag. dir. of agric. on several occasions.
BURDETT, HENRY LEATHAM, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; war serv., Nov., 1914 to Aug., 1919; asst. engr., Basutoland, 1921; seconded to Tanganyika as road inspr., 1929.
BURGE, G. H. K.—B. 1890; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Islands, Jan., 1916.
BURGESS, W. C.—S. African civ. serv.; offr.-in-charge, finger print bureau, Kenya, Oct. 1919.
BURKE-GAFFNEY, H. J. O'D., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.)—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925; asst. bacteriologist, 1929.

BURLINGHAM, DONALD.—B. 1892; ed. Norwich schol.; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1911; attached to Punjab pol., 1912-13; passed in Urdu, 1913; J.P., 1914; asst. supt. of pol., New Territories, 1914-1917; passed in Cantonese, 1918; 2nd lieut., Indian Army res. of offrs., 1917; on active serv., German E. Africa, 1917; attached army headqu., gen. staff branch, Simla, 1918-1919; asst. supt. of pol., Hong Kong, 1920; aide-de-camp to gov., 1921; passed in Punjabi, 1923; ag. dep. supt., pol., Apr., 1925; dep. supt., pol., 1926; divn. supt., pol., 1930.
BURNER, ERIC ANDERSON.—Ed. at Felsted Schol.; comand., R.F.A., France, Belgium and Germany, 1915-18; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1920; dist. commr., 1924.

BURNETT, BERTRAM DAVIDSON.—B. 1891; ed. Port Elphinstone Pub. Schol. and Inverurie Acad.; war serv., 6th Batt. Gordon Highlanders, 1914-19; foreman of wks., 2nd grade, P.W.D., Gold Coast, Feb., 1920; headmast., junr. trade schol., Apr., 1924.

BURNS, A.C., C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1887; apptd. to treasy. and cust. dept., St. Kitts, Leeward Is., Mar., 1905; clk. to mag., Dec., 1909; ag. mag., Anguilla, 1910; dep. coroner and J.P., St. Kitts, 1911; ag. clk. and priv. sec. to admstr., Dominica, 1912; supervisor of cust., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1912; asst. sec., Nigeria, June, 1914; col. sec., Bahamas, Feb., 1924; el. mem., H. of A., 1925-28; del. to W. Indian confce., London, 1926; admstr. govt. on various occasions, 1924-28; dep. ch. sec., Nigeria, Jan., 1929; acting ch. sec., Feb. to July 1929; gov.'s dep. for various periods in 1929 and 1930; on active serv. with W. African Regt., 1914-15; adjutant, Nigeria Land Contingent, 1915; served with Egba Exped., 1918; joint compiler, Index to Laws of Leeward Is., 1910; compiler, Nigeria Handbooks, 1917-23; author of "A History of Nigeria," 1929.

BURNS, DAVID GRAHAM, B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Kent House and St. Bees Schi., Cumberland; mily. serv., 1916-19; regular army res. of offrs., 1923; B.A., Queen's Coll., Cambridge; dist. agril. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924.

BURNS, R. E., LL.B., London (1908)—2nd elk., regis.'s off., St. Kitts, June, 1898; ag. elk., town commrs., Basseterre, June to Dec., 1899; elk., P.O., Oct., 1901; extra rev. offr., treas., Dec., 1899; ag. elk., treas., May, 1902, to Mar., 1903; ag. gov't. elk., clk., ex. and legis. couns., elk., bd. of health, Apr., 1903, to Mar., 1904, June, 1904; ag. elk., treas., St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1904; tariff elk., treas., St. K.N., Oct., 1904; ag. audit elk., May, 1905, to Feb., 1906, and Oct., 1906, to May, 1907; 2nd cls. supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Aug., 1907; jun. ast. treas., July, 1908; ast. treas., Mar., 1912; ast. censor (temporary) G. Coast, Aug., 1915; ag. senr. ast. auditor, Apr., to May, 1917; ag. treas., May-July, 1920; dep. treas., Aug., 1922.
BURNS, WILLIAM ALOYSIUS, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1900; ed. St. Aloysius Coll. and Vety. Coll., Glasgow; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1923.

BURNSIDE, ROBERT EDWARD WARRICK, B.A.—B. 1900; ed. Christ's Hosp. and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; served R.A.F., 1918; headmast., N. Rhodesia, 1927; inspr. schls., Apr., 1931; ag. dir., European educn., 1932.

BURPEE, LAWRENCE JOHNSTON, F.R.G.S., F.R.S.C.—B. 1873; priv. sec. to three successive mins. of justice in Dom. govt., and for several years libr. of Ottawa pub. lib.; sec., Canadian section, International Joint Comm., 1st Jan., 1913; mem. Champlain Soc., Can. Inst., Ontario Hist. Soc., Société Archéologique de France, Hist. Soc. of the Mississippi Valley, Bibliographical Soc. of America; author and editor of numerous works relating to Canadian life and history.
BURR, PERCY BRARD FRANK, M.R.San.—B. 1886; ch. san'y. inspr., Selangor, Apr., 1912; do., Perak, Apr., 1915; joined Artists Rifles O.T.C., Oct., 1918; ch. san'y. inspr., Perak, Nov., 1919.

BURRILL, HON. MARTIN.—B. 1858; ed. St. John's Coll., Hurstpierpoint; fruit farmer in Niagara Peninsula fourteen years; moved to B. Columbia, 1900; apptd. mem. hd. of horticulture, B. Columbia; editor, Grand Forks Gazette: mayor, Grand Forks, 1903; fruit commnr. and lecturer in England for B.C. Govt., 1907-08; elected to H. of C., Canada, for Yale-Cariboo, 1908; re-elected, 1911 and 1917; mem. of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of agric. in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; sec. of state and min. of mines, Oct., 1917; joint libr. of parlmt., July, 1920.

BURROWES, BERTRAM, LL.B. (Hons.) Univ. Coll., London.—B. 1883; called to bar Gray's Inn, 1920; treas., Jamaica, 1903; juddl. dept., 1915; elk., cts., 1921; judge, grand ct., Cayman Is., 1924; supernumy. res. mag., Jamaica, 1925; ag. res. mag., 1925; res. mag., 1926.

BURROWES, ROMULUS, S.—B. 1906; ed. Queen's Coll.; cler. astl., col. secs' office, Br. Guiana, 1924; 6th cl. offr., cust., 1925; 5th cl. elk., col. secs' office, 1926; 4th cl. offr., 1927; astl. elk., leg. coun., 1929; 3rd cl. elk., C.S.O. 1933.

BURROWS, WILLIAM.—B. 1883; ent. "Britannia," 1898; ret. from Navy with rank of commdr., Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Gilbert and Ellise Is., Jan., 1920; seconded Fiji, Feb., 1922; dist. comanr., Fiji, Jan., 1923.

BURT, NORMAN FRANK.—B. 1899; ed. Reg. by Schl.; R. Mily. Academy, Woolwich and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; staff survr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1922; cadet, Tanganyika, Sep., 1925; astl. dist. offr., Sept., 1926.
BURT, ROBERT HILLHOUSE.—B. 1896; app't after compet. exam. elk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to C.O., 15th Mar., 1916; cler. offr., higher grade, 30th Apr., 1925.
BURTON, GERALD JOHN LLOYD, M.C., B.—Plant breeder, agrl. dept., Kenya, Jan., 1921.

BURTON, WILLIAM, Barrister-at-law, Grace Inn.—B. 1884; B.A., Cantab., 1st cls. div. III, clas. trip., 1906; 2nd cls., pt. II hist. trip., 1907; cadet, F.M.S., 1907; astl. dist. offr., K. Pilah, cls. V, cont. as mag., K. Banhar and harbrmr., T. Anson, and astl. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, 1911; astl. dist. offr., B. Padang, 1911; mag., Ipoh, 1913; astl. regist., sup. ct., Ipoh, 1914; mag., Seremban, 1916; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1918; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1919; regist., sup., K. Lumpur, 1920; offl. assignee in addition, Mar., 1921; regist., sup. ct., July, 1921, cont. of assignee in addition; offl. assignee, F.M.S., in admn., Mar. 1927; dep. legal advr., F.M.S., Apr., 1927; puisne judge, S.S., Apr., 1929.

BURTON, WILLIAM ELLIOT, M.D., C.M., McGill Univ., 1910; ag. temporary res. surg., Barbados general hosp., 1910; ag. med. offr., St. James' parish, Barbados; ag. visiting physician, leper asylum; ag. surg. to police, dist. II, Barbados, 1911; mag. and med. offr., Anguilla, Leeward Is., 1912; inspr. public health, St. Kitts-Nevis, Aug., 1918.

BUSH, BERTRAM OLIVER, Assoc.M.Inst.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engr.—B. 1890; ed. Hoe Gram. Schl. and Plymouth Tech. Schl.; astl. engr., Perak, F.M.S., Jan., 1920; ditto, K. Kangsar, Feb., 1921; ditto, K. Pilah, Dec., 1924; ditto K. Pilah and Jelebu, Oct., 1925; exec. engr. D.P.W.'s office, Jan., 1929; sr. drainage and irrigr. engr., Perak, Jan., 1932.

BUSHE, HENRY GRATTAN, C.B.(1832), C.M. (1927)—B. 1886; called to the bar, 1909; Western circuit, 1910-11; S. Eastern circuit, 1911; Hera and Essex sessions, 1916; sec. to royal comm. on the contracts between the War Office and Ss. John Jackson, Ltd., 1916-17; ag. legal assr., C.O., Nov., 1917; legal astl., 1st Jan., 1918; legal adviser to D.O. and C.O., lat. Apr., 1931.

BUSTARD, FRANK HARBOURT.—B. 1886; joined Northumberland Fusiliers, May, 1915; seconded to K.A.R., Aug., 1913; commissary in field, May, 1916; coy. commdr., Apr., 1917; in desps., 1918; Somaliland campaign, 1920; capt., reg. army R. of O.; temp. astl. supr., Tanganyika pol., May, 1925; cadet, Zanzibar, Oct., 1925; supt., pol., May, 1926.

BUSWELL, HENRY HOPE.—B.D. (Serampore Calcutta); L.C.P. (hons. English and French Fellow of L'Institut Francoise du Royaume Uni.; astl. chaplain, Quatre Bornes and Mahe Mauritius, 1908; civ. chaplain, Beau Rassin and Rose Hill, Mauritius, 1909; civ. chaplain Seychelles, 1919; sub. dean, St. Paul's Cathedr.

BUTCHER, JAMES WILLIAM.—B. 1889; ed. N.Z. state schls. and Waitaki Boys' High Schl.; ent. regiar. gen.'s dept., 1906; statistical branch, 1912; ch. compiler, census and statistics office, 1915; asst. govt. statistician, 1929; govt. statistician, 1932.
BUTLER, CHARLES OWEN.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., 1914-19; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. commnr., 1924; asst. col. sec., 1928; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., Apr.-Sept., 1932.

BUTLER, CHARLES RICE.—B. 1885; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin, and at the Sorbonne, Paris; B.A.; supt., educn. dept., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; attched. Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; ch. inspr., S. Provs., 1930; ag. asst. dir., educn., S. Provs., 1932.
BUTLER, ERNEST CREIL.—B. 1900; clk., Basutoland, 1920; sub.-insp., B.M. pol., 1927; war serv., Apr., 1917-Mar., 1919.

BUTLER, G. G., M.B.E., B.A., M.D., B.C., Cantab. (hons. nat. sci. tripes), M.R.C.S.(Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), certif., L.S.T.M. (with distinc.),—Apptd., W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, Apl., 1911; seconded for sp. investigation under yellow fever comm., May, 1913 to June, 1914; served Cameroons Expdy. Force, Jan., 1916 to Apl., 1916; temp.-capt. (gen. list) Jan. to Apl., 1916; seconded for serv. with R.A.M.C., 1916-19; dir. of laboratory, Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1920; ag. D.P.M.O., Mar., 1924; dir., med. research inst., Gold Coast, May, 1928.

BUTLER, HON. RICHARD LATTON, M.P. (S. Australia)—M.P. for Wooroora 1915-18 and since the elections of 1921; leader, parly. liberal party and of opposition, Dec., 1925; premier, treas. and min. of rly., 1927-30; now leader of opposition.

BUTTERFIELD, HARRY ROBERT FREDERICK.—B. 1896; ed. Christ Church, Westminster; 2nd lieut., Midx. Regt., Mar., 1915; served Great War, 1914-19; 6th K.A.R., Tanganyika Territory, 1922 to 1928; admin. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1927; asst. dist. offr., May, 1929; capt., 6th K.A.R. R. of O., July, 1930; ag. res. mag. and dist. regiar., June, 1931-Sept., 1932.

BUTTERS, HENRY ROBERT.—B. 1898; ed. Glasgow High schl. and Glasgow Univ., 1st bursar, 1916; cadet, Hong Kong, Jan., 1922; ag. dep. regiar., sup. ct., 1923; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1924; dist. offr., North, 1925; ag. dep. regiar. and 2nd pol. mag. in addn., 1925; 3rd asst. col. sec., 1926; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk. of couns., June, 1928; ch. asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1929; 2nd pol. mag., 1930; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., 1933; pol. mag., Kowloon, Sept., 1930; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1932.

BUTTERS, SIR JOHN HENRY, KT., BACH., M.I.E.E., M.Am.I.C.E., etc.—B. 1882; ed. Taunton Schl. and Univ. Coll., Southampton; ch. engrnr. and gen. man., govt. hydro elec. dept., Hobart, Tasmania.; chmn., Tasmanian state comte., of the C'wealth advisory coun. of science and industry; pres., Tasmanian Inst. of Engrns.; ch. commnr., fedl. cap. comm., Canberra, Nov., 1924; ret.

BUXTON, EARL (cr. 1914) OF NEWTIMBER, Rt. HON. SYDNEY CHARLES BUXTON, P.C., G.C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1853; ed. at Clifton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; was mem. of the London schl. bd. from 1876 to 1882; hon. sec. to Mr. Tuke's Irish emigr. fund, 1882-4; author of the "Handbook to Political Questions," the "Political Manual," "Finance and Politics," "An Historical Study, 1783-1883," &c., and editor of the imp. parlmt. series; M.P. for Peterborough from June, 1883, to Nov., 1885, when he was unsuccessful; contested Croydon unsuccessfully in Jan., 1886; elected July, 1886, and re-elected in 1892, 1896, and 1900, for Poplar (Tower Hamlets); parly. under-sea. of state for the colo., 17th Aug., 1892, to 28th June, 1895; postmr.-gen., Dec., 1906; pres. of B. of T., 1910; gov.-gen., Union of S. Africa, and high commnr. for S. Africa, 14th May, 1914; assumed govt., 8th Sept., 1914; relinquished office, 19th Nov., 1920.
BUXTON, C. E. V.—Asst. dist. commnr. N. Africa Prot., Oct., 1919.

BYATT, SIR HORACE ARCHER, G.C.M.G.(1930). K.C.M.G.(1918), C.M.G.(1912).—Class. exhibtn., Lincoln Coll., Oxford, 1894; B.A. (honours), 1898; asst. collr., B.C.A. Prot., Nov., 1899; Africa general serv. medal, 1899-1900; asst. polit. offr., Somaliland, Aug., 1905; asst. dist. offr., Nov., 1905; sec. to adminstr., Somaliland, Aug., 1906; admtrd. govt., June to Dec., 1910; commnr. and comdr.-in-chief, 1911; col. sec., Gibraltar, May, 1914; lieut.-gov. and chief sec., Malta, 1914; admtrd., German East Africa provisional adminstr., (Tanganyika Territory), 23rd Sept., 1916; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Tanganyika Territory, 25th Sept., 1920; gov., Trinidad, 16th Sept., 1924; assumed govt., 22nd Nov., 1924; ret., 1929.

BYNG, 1ST BARON OF VIMY, AND OF THORPE-LISMORE (Cr. 1919); GEN. JULIAN HEDWORTH GEORGE BYNG, G.C.B. (1919), K.C.B. (1916), K.C.M.G. (1915), C.B. (1906), M.V.O. (1902).—B. 1862; joined 10th Royal Hussars, 1883; maj., 1898; col., 1901; served Soudan expdn., 1884 (med. with clasp, Khedive's star); S. Africa, 1899-1902 (desp., Queen's med., 6 clasps, King's med.); commanded 10th Royal Hussars, 1902-4; cav. schl., Nethersavon, 1904-5; maj.-gen., 1909; commanded 2nd cav. brig., 1905-7; 1st cav. brig., 1907-9; E. Anglian divn., 1910-12; G.O.C., Egypt, 1912-14; lieut.-gen., Dardanelles, 1915; commanded 3rd Cav. Divn., 1914-15; Cav. Corps, 1915-16; 9th Army Corps, Feb., 1916; 17th Army Corps, Apr., 1916; gen., 1917; Canadian Corps, 3rd Army, 1917-19; thanked by Parliament for distinguished services, and granted £30,000; numerous foreign decorations; gov.-gen. of Canada, 1921-26.

BYRNE, BRIG.-GEN. SIR JOSEPH A., G.C.M.G. (1934), K.C.M.G. (1928), K.B.E. (1918), C.B. Military (1916).—B. 1874; joined R. Inniskilling Fusiliers, 1893; served in S. African War, 1899-1902; wounded); served with central judicial (claims) comm., Pretoria, 1903-05; major, 1914; bt. lieut.-col., 1915; brig.-gen., 1916; asst. adjutant-gen., W.O., 1916; dep. adjutant-gen., Ireland, 1916 (C.B. and desp.); inspr.-gen., R.I.C., 1916; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1922; gov., Seychelles, 21st July, 1922; assumed govt., 26th Sept., 1922; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Sierra Leone, 1927; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Kenya and high commnr. for transport, 1931.

BYRON, FREDERICK AUGUSTUS WILLIAM.—B. 1900; served in R.E. and Royal Corps Signals, 1918-1924; awarded M.S.M. and ment. in desp. for serv. on N.W. Frontier, India; wireless offr., Gold Coast, 1925; electrician in charge, elec. services, Falkland Is., 1930; broadcast offr., Sierra Leone, 1933.

CADE, REGINALD COURTNEY.—B. 1891; ed. Academie de Caen and in Germany; joined army, Aug., 1914; served in France, June, 1915 to Sept., 1916 and Jan., 1917 to Mar., 1918; lieut., gen. list, H. Qrs., Home Force, till Mar., 1919, invalided out of army; junr. examr., clearing office, (enemy debts.) Sept., 1920; senr. examr., June, 1921 to Dec., 1929; temp. adm'tive offr., C.O., Feb., 1930.

CADMAN, Sir John, K.C.M.G. (1918), C.M.G. (1916), M.Sc., M.I.M.E., F.G.S.—B. 1877; ed. High Sch., Newcastle-under-Lyme, and Durham Univ. Coll., Sa.; Staff. C.C. scholar, 1896; B.Sc. (Dunelm.), hona., 1899; M.Sc., 1902; pres., Union soc., 1898; certif. colliery man., 1900; asst. gen. man., Silverdale coal and iron works, Staffs., 1900; asst. agt., Walter Scott, Ltd., Collieries, Durham, 1901; H.M. insp. of mines (H.O.), East Scotland, 1902; ditto, Staffordshire, 1903; govt. mining engrnr., Trinidad and Tobago, 1904; ret., 1922; author of prize papers on "Iron Ore Deposit" before Inst. of Mining Engrs.

CAHAN, Hon. Charles Hazlitt, B.A., LL.B., L.L.D., K.C.—B. 1861; ed. Yarmouth Seminary and Dalhousie Univ.; leader of conserv. party in Nova Scotia legis., 1890-94; 1st hl. to H. of C., Canada at g.e., 1925; re-el., 1926 and 1930; sec. of state in Bennett cabinet, 1930.
CAIN, Henry Oliver.—B. 1892; Europ. War, 1914-18; 21st Lancers 1910; Indian Sig. Serv., 1916-23; qmr. agt., Somaliland pol. force, 1923; pay and qmr., 1928.

CAINE, Sydney, B.Sc. (Econ.)—B. 1902; ed. Harrow County Schl. and London Schl. of Economics; B.Sc. (Econ.), with 1st cls. hona., 1922; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. inspr. of taxes (Inland Rev.), 1923; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., Nov., 1926; ag. prin., 8th Nov., 1931; visited W. Indies and B. Guiana as sec., W. Indian sugar comm., 1929-30; sec., managing comtees, Imp. Inst. of Entomology, Oct., 1931.

CARNS, Thomas Crawford, B.Sc.—B. 1900; ed. Edinburgh Acad.; B.Sc. Edinburgh Univ., 1924; entomologist, Empire Cotton Growing Corp.; seconded to divn. of entomology, Union of S. Africa; cotton investigator, Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1928; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1930.

CALDECOTT, Andrew, C.M.G. (1932), C.B.E. (1926).—B. 1884; ed. Uppingham (schol. and exh.), and Oxford (schol.), 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1907; B.A., Exeter Coll., 1907; cadet, F.M.S., 1907; dist. offr., Jelebu, 1911; dep. contr., labr., 1913; 2nd asst. sec., Fed. Soc., 1916; seconded for sp. duty in connec. with Br. Empire Exhib. while on leave in England, Jan., 1923; town planning adm'tr. and state valuer, Ipoh, Mar., 1926; chmn. housing and P.W.D. enquiry comtees, Dec., 1926; dep. contr., lab., Malaya but con- tinues ag. under sec., S.S., July, 1927; offr. cls. I.A. sec., postal affrs., S.S., Dec., 1929; ag. Br. res., N. Sembilan, Dec., 1929; ag. Br. res., Perak, Oct., 1930; ag. Br. res., Selangor, May, 1931; ag. ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., July, 1931; supernm. staff offr. of rank of ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1932; col., sec., S.S., Dec., 1933; published, "History of Jelebu," 1912.

CALDER, James.—B. 1898; ed. Glasgow Univ.; M.A.; war serv., 1916-19; cadet, S.S., Apr., 1921; attd., treas'y, S'pore, June, 1921; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; asst. supt., govt. monops., S'pore and ast. contr., rubber in addn., Aug., 1926; ditto, Penang, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Dec., 1928; asst. dir., co-opern., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1929; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933.

CALDER, Hon. James Alexander, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1868; ed. Ingersoll pub. schls., pub. and high schls., Winnipeg; Manitoba Coll.; hon. grad. in science, Manitoba Univ., 1888 (silver medallist); called to the bar, North-West Terri- tories, 1908; prin. of Moosejaw high schl., 1891-94; inspr. of schls., N.W.T., 1894-1900; dep. comsnr. of educn., N.W.T., 1901-06; elec. to Sask. ass. at first g.e., 1905; prov. treas. and comsnr. of educn. in Scott min., Sept., 1905; unsucc. cand. for Milestone div. at g.e., 1906; elec. for Saltcoats at bye-elec., Dec., 1906; min. of immigr. and colonization in federal union govt., Oct., 1917; elec. to H. of C., Canada, for Moosejaw, Dec., 1917; pres., Canadian privy coun., July, 1920; called to senate, 22nd Sess., 1921.

CALDER, John A., M.A.—B. 1889; ed. at Heriot Acad., Dundee and Edin. Univ.; Van Dunlop scholarship in history, 1910; M.A. with 1st cls. hona. in history, 1911; 1st cls. hona. in philosophy, 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. dkt., C.O., Nov., 1912; ag. 1st cls. clk., 8th Dec., 1917; on military service from 16th Sept. to 10th Dec., 1918; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; visited S. Africa, N. and S. Rhodesia, Nyasaland, Tanganyika, Zanzibar, Uganda and Kenya as sec., E. Africa Parly. Comsn., Aug., 1924; asst. sec., 27th June, 1933.

CALDERWOOD, Wilfred Henry.—Passed Cape civ. serv. law exam.; clk., control and audt. office, Cape Colony, Apr., 1893; asst. examr. of accts., Oct., 1893; 2nd cls. examr. of accts., Apr., 1898; 1st cls. ditto, June, 1903; inspr., Jan., 1906; offr. in ch., rev. audit, control and audt. office, Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; also acctm. from Dec., 1916; ch. inspr., rev. audit and acctng. offr., Oct., 1919; ch. inspr., expenditure audit, Oct., 1927; asst. contr. and audr.-gen., Apr., 1933.

CALDICOTT, Kenneth Child, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., chtd. civ. engrnr.—B. 1887; ed. Malvern Coll. (sch.) and Edin. Univ.: Messrs. Rendel & Robertson, Westminster, 1909; p.m. asst. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., 1911; asst. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., 1912; dist. engrnr., grade III., 1915; ag. offr., asst. gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., 1918; dist. engrnr., grade II., 1919; dist. engrnr., grade I., 1921; ag. sec., gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., 1925; sen. dist. engrnr., 1926; senr. dist. engrnr., Apr., 1927; ditto, Ipoh dist., Mar., 1928; ditto, Kuala Lumpur, Oct., 1930; prin. asst. to engrnr., wks. and wks., Oct., 1930; divnl. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., Aug., 1932.
CALDWELL, Capt. K. F. T.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1918; asst. game warden, Oct., 1922; ag. game warden, Jan., Nov., 1923 and in 1926; senr. asst. game warden, 1st Jan., 1925.

CALDWELL, Reginald Percival.—B. 1894; held various appts., Fiji, 1910-17; served with 1st Batt., K.R.R., France, 1917-19; 1st cls. clk., treas. and sec., returned sailors and soldiers' last settlimt. scheme and returned sailors and soldiers' advances bd. of control, 1922; asst. comsnr. for Fiji, Br. Emp. Exhib., 1924; col. audit dept., Dec., 1924; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925; ag. senr. asst. audr., June, 1925; senr. audr., Uganda, July, 1930; ag. senr. asst. audr., Aug., 1930.

CALDWELL, Robert, M.A., (Glasgow)—B. 1894; ed. Irvine Royal Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; served Scottish Rifles and K.A.R., 1915-16; temp. asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, 1919-20; senr. educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; asst. dir. educn., Nyasaland, 1927; ag. dir., educn., Aug., 1928 to Feb., 1930; dir. native educn., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1931; M.L.C., 1933.

LTHROP, LANCMLOT HENRY CALTHROP.—46; ed. Blundell's; pol. probationer, Hong Nov., 1924; seconded as junr. dist. offr. aiwei, 1928; astt. supt., pol., 1928; J.P.; passed in Mandarin, 1928; attd., Punjab 1930-31.
LWELL, H. G., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Belf.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.) (Gold Med., see of Children, Belfast).—B. 1901; med., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1931.
MACHO, FABIAN JOSEPH.—B. 1896; called Middle Temple, 1933; mag., elect. dad., Nov., 1932.

MACHO, MAURICE VIVIAN.—B. 1885; ed. Edmund's Coll., Herts.; called to bar, Middle Ia., 1906; ag. atty.-gen., Dec., 1920; K.C.; judge, West Indian ct. of appeal for hear certain appeals, 1924; solr.-gen., Trinidad, ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1928 to Apr., 1929; sec., 1930, to Jan., 1931; ag. judge, sup. ct., June, 1929 and Jan. to June, 1931; gen., Jamaica, 1931.
MBRIDGE, RALPH.—M.A. Hartford Coll., B.; 1878; 3rd astt. mast., Queen's Royal Trinidad, Sept., 1901; 1st astt. mast., 1918; 2nd mast., July, 1920; prin., Queen's Coll., Sept., 1927.

MERON, SIR DONALD CHARLES, C.G.M.G., K.C.M.G. (1926), K.B.E. (1922), C.M.G. (1872); ed. at Rathmines School, Dublin; Br. Guiana civ. ser., 1890; 5th class clerk, 1891; 4th ditto, 1896; 3rd class and clerk, 1895; 2nd class, 1899; priv. to ag. gov., 1896, 1897, 1898 and 1901; ast. gov. sec. and clk. of councils, 1900-1901, pal olk., secretariat, 1901; priv. sec. to gov. Newfoundland (with permission of S. of S.) on leave, 1902; astt. col. sec., Mauritius, 1904; ag. col. sec. in 1904-5-6-7; temp. td. to S. Nigeria as astt. sec., Jan., 1908; pol astt. sec., 1911; sec. to S. Nigeria liquor inquiry comte., 1909; ag. prov. commr., 10, 1911 and 1912; ag. col. sec., 27th Feb., 1912; Aug. and 5th Sept. to 3rd Oct., 1912; y gov. in 1921-23; cent. sec., Nigeria, 1st 1914; ch. sec. to gov., 1st Jan., 1921; ag. n. 1921, 1923 and 1924; gov. and comdr-in-Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1924; ditto, ia., 1931.

MERON, SIR EDWARD J., K.C.M.G., C.M.G. (1905).—Ed. at Shrewsbury Clifton Coll. and Merton Coll., Oxon; sec. to Sir C. C. Lee, gov., Bahamas, 1882; and as gov. of the Leeward Is., Jan., astt. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, Nov., J.P. for the Sttlm., and visiting justice, own Gaol; ag. col. sec. and treas., Dec., 1885, ly., 1886; pres., Virgin Is., Feb., 1887; mem. Is. legis. coun., 1887, and exec. coun., commr., Turks Is., Mar., 1898; ag. judge it., May to Dec., 1893, Jan. to June, 1895, Apr. to July, 1896; admstr., St. Lucia, Apr., 1909; ag. Windward Is., June to Oct., 1909, July to 1911, and in 1912; representative of St., and chmn. of W. Indian delegates at policy conf. at Ottawa, Mar., 1912; gov. via., Feb., 1914; assumed gov't., Apr., 1914; 1920.
MERON, KATHERINE ROSS, M.B.E. (1926)—ing sister, Basutoland, 1911; ag. matron, nursing sister, Nyassaland, 1919; matron,

CAMERON, MAJOR SIR MAURICE ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1914); C.M.G. (1900), late R.E.—B. 1856; first coman. in Roy. Engrs., Ang., 1874; dept. col. engnr., S. Sttlm., Dec., 1883, to Apr., 1892; during which he acted about two years and a half as col. engnr. and surr.-gen., and was a mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; apptd. one of the crown agts. for the colonies, 1895-1920.
CAMPBELL, ALBERT BERNARD.—B. 1883; astt. store keeper, Baro Kano Ry., 1909; seconded, Nigerian Regt., 1917; astt. ch. storekeeper, 1920; ch. storekeeper, Nigeria, 1928.
CAMPBELL, COLIN BRUCE, B.A., M.C.—B. 1890; ed. Leatherhead Schl. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; supr., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, May, 1921.

CAMPBELL, GENERAL SIR DAVID, K.C.B. (1919), C.B. (1916)—B. 1869; ed. Clifton; joined 9th Lancers 1889; served Boer War, 1899-1902 (deps. bt. maj., Queen's med. 7 clasps, King's med. 2 clasps); served European War, 1914-1918; first in command 9th Lancers then of a cavalry brig. 21st Divn., 1916-19 (deps. six times, bt. col., C.B., promoted major-gen., etc.); G.O.C., Baluchistan dist., 1920-24; mily. sec. to S. of S. for War, 1926-27; G.O.C. in ch., Aldershot Commd., 1927-31; gov. and comdr-in-ch., Malta, 1931.

CAMPBELL, COL. GEORGE FREDERICK COLIN, C.M.G. (1919), V.D. (1908).—B. 1858; ed. Nelson Coll., New Zealand; entd. New Zealand civ. serv., 1874; dep. commr. of taxes, 1894-1904; dep. supt., advances to settlers' office, 1896-7; dep. valuer-gen., 1897-1904; valuer-gen., 1904-9; gen. man., state fire insurance office, 1909-10; commr. of taxes, 1910-2; sec. to treasy., recvr. gen., pay-master-gen. and supt. of state advances office, 1913; contr. and audr.-gen., 1922.

CAMPBELL, JAMES EDWARD FRANCIS, M.A.—B. 1879; ed. Christ Church, Oxford; double 1st cls. hon.; called to bar, Middle Temple; Order of the Nile; served throughout great war; maj. R. Artillery, 1916-18; ent. Palestine admin., Mar., 1919; astt. dist. gov., Jerusalem-Jaffa dist., 1920; dist. commr., Jerusalem dist., 1932.
CAMPBELL, J.M., M.B., Ch.B. (Ed.), D.P.H. (Camb.), D.T.M. (Liv.) (Alan Milne med.).—B. 1897; med. offr., Kenya, Nov., 1923; senr. health offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1930.

CAMPBELL, LACHLAN BAIN, M.C. (1918), A.M.I.C.E. (Lond.).—B. 1882; ed. Auckland Grammar Schl. and Canterbury Univ. Coll., N.Z.; 30 years' serv. in N.Z. pub. wks. dept.; commr., borough of Napier (during restoration period following earthquake), 1931-33; sec., marine, 1933.

CAMPBELL, S. McNEILL.—B. 1893; ed. Hamilton Acad., and Glasgow Univ.; M.A. (1st cls. hons. Classics) 1915; G.U.O.T.C., Sept., 1914; on military service, Jan., 1916 to Mar., 1919, with Cameronians (Scottish Rifles) 52nd Div. and R.A.F., Egypt and Palestine; lieut., July, 1917; capt., R.A.F., Nov., 1918; temporary clk., in C.O., Apr., 1919; apptd. by civ. serv. commrs., 2nd cl. clk., O.O., Jan., 1920; astt. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to permit under sec. (Sir J. Masterton Smith), Apr., 1922 to Nov., 1923; ag. prin., 3rd Oct., 1923; prin., 1st Oct., 1926; sec. W.-Indies Closer Union Coman., Nov., 1932-Mar., 1933.

CAMPBELL, WILLIAM KENNETH HUNTER.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall and Wadham Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; off. astt. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Sept., 1910; ditto, Galle, Sept., 1911; ag. pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1912; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, June, 1914; asst. govt. agt., Colomba and Negombo, Mar., 1917; ditto, Trincomalee, Nov., 1918; ditto Chilaw and Puttalam, Aug., 1920; on sp. duty in connection with co-op. credit societies Dec., 1924; joint regis., co-op. societies, May, 1926.

CAMPIGLI, Lieut.-Col. GEORGE MURRAY (R. of O.), M.C., M.Inst.T.—B. 1890; ed. pvtly.; Vict. state rly., 1906-14; served in Australian Forces in Gallipoli and Palestine, 1914-18; ment. in desps.; capt. (staff capt.), May, 1917; dep. asst. dir., rly. traffic, 1917; asst. dir., ditto, 1919; offr.-in-ch., Palestine milly. rly., 1919; transfd. to civ. govt. as asst. gen. man. Palestine rlys., 1920; lieut.-col., R. of O., July, 1921; ag. gen. man, in 1922, 1924, 1926-1927-8; mem., advisory bd. to Palestine govt. for commerce and industry, Mar., 1928.

CAMPION, Lieut.-Col. Sir WILLIAM ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1924), D.S.O. (1917), T.D. (1914).—B. 1870; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford; lieut.-col., 4th Batt., R. Sussex Regt., 1915; col., 1924; M.P., Lewes divn., Sussex, 1910-24; gov., W. Australia, 1924-31.

CAMSELL, CHARLES, B.A., LL.D., F.R.S.C., F.G.S.A.—B. 1876; ed., St. John's Coll., Winnipeg; grad., Man. Univ., 1894; post grad., geol., of Queen's Univ., Kingston, Harvard, and Massachusetts Inst. of Tech., Boston; geol., Algoma Cent. Rly., 1901; geol., Canadian Northern Rly., 1902-03; geol., geological surv. of Can., 1904; geol., investigations and explns. in Br. Columbia and N. Can., 1904-20; in charge of B.C. and Yukon br. of the geol. surv., 1918; dep. min., federal dept. of mines, 1920; apptd., Dom. fuel bd., 1921.

CAPETOWN, ARCHBISHOP OF THE MOST REVEREND FRANCIS ROBINSON PRELFS, D.D., Ed. Kable Coll. Oxford; B.A., 1886; M.A., 1889; D.D., 1916; bishop of Grahamstown, 1916; archbishop of Capetown and metropolitan of the church of the province of S. Africa, 1931.

CARDINALL, ALLAN WOLSEY.—B. 1887; ed. Winchester Coll. and Heidelberg; Lond. Matric., 1914; asst. dist. commr., Ashanti, G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; dist. commr., G. Coast, 1st Jan., 1921; ag. commr., S. Prov., N.T. Apr. to Aug., 1928; ag. commr., N. Prov., N.T., Jan. to June, 1929; ret., 1932; commr., Cayman Is., 1934.

CAREY, CECIL WILLIAM VICTOR.—B. 1887; B.A., LL.B., Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to bar, Dublin, 1910; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, Oct., 1915; mag., 1918; admstr.-gen., custodian of enemy property, offr. recr. in bankruptcy, 1919; crown coun., Nigeria, 1921; ag. solr.-gen. in 1923-30; puisne judge, 1930.
CARLTON, ERIC JAMES WATTS.—B. 1908; ed. Bedford Schl. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; passed in sch. of agr., B.A., 1930; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1931.
CARMICHAEL, FRANK BERNARD—B. 1883; loco. supt., Trinidad govt. rly., June, 1928.

CARMICHAEL, Lieut.-Col. Sir JAMES FORREST HALKETT, K.T. BACH (1929), C.M.G. (1920), C.B.E. (1918), Chevalier of the Legion of Honour, 1919; M.I.C.E. Royal Engineers.—B. 1868; ed. Clifton Coll., and Royal Milly Acad., Woolwich; joined R. Enginrs., 1887; served in India, Aden, Somaliland and Burma, 1889-90; served Chin Hill Expdn., 1893; operations on N.W. Frontier, India with Tirah Expdy. Force, 1897-98 (med. with 2 clasps); W.O., 1900-03; ordnance survey, 1903 to 1904; joined staff of Crown Agents for Colonies as head of engrg. dept., 1904; asst. dir. of raw materials dept., Miny. of Munitions, 1916-17; ch. engr., 1919; fourth Crown Agent and engrn. in ch., 1921-32.
CARNCROSS, Hon. Sir WALTER CHARLES FREDERICK, K.T. BACH (1922).—B. 1855; ed. privately; mem., N.Z. House of Reps., 1890-1902; M.L.C. since 1903; chmn., comtee. of L.C., 1910-18; speaker, 1918.

CARNELLEY, STEPHEN H., F.R.G.S.—Ed. Rugby and Exeter Coll., Oxford; solr., sup. ct. England; mem. of bar, F.M.S.; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 1910; ag. dist. commr., 1912; asst. col. sec., 1913; res. mag., E. Africa Prot., 1914; seconded as capt., E. African Forces, 1915-1918; res. commr., Kenya, 1921; senr. res. mag., Jan., 1923.
CARR, EDWARD THOMAS WHITMORE, B.A., T.C.D.—B. 1883; sub-insp. of constab., Trinidad, Oct., 1907; insp., July, 1916.

CARR, JAMES BENJAMIN.—B. 1889; ed. Bedford Mod. Schl. and Culham Training Coll. (Oxford Diocesean); European ast. mast., Victoria Inst., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1911; with H.M. Forces, Oct., 1914 to Feb., 1920; Raffles Inst., S'pore, Jan. 1921; headmast., T. Kurau govt. eng. schl., June, 1926; asst. inspnr., schls., S'pore, in addn., Sept. to Nov., 1926; headmast., K. Pilah govt. eng. schl., Dec., 1928; headmast., Rangoon Rd. schl., S'pore, Dec., 1930.
CARR-BIRKBECK, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1905; ed. Bradfield and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; B.A. 1928; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, May, 1928; asst. dist. offr., May, 1930.

CARRIE, WILLIAM JAMES, M.A., B.Sc. (1914).—B. 1891; ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; cadet, civ. ser., Hong Kong, 1914; passed in Cantonese, 1917; on military service, 1918 to 1919; ast. col. treas., 1920; J.P., 1922; 2nd ast. col. sec., 1922; sec., Sui On parish comm., 1922; sec., stocks and shares comm., 1925; ast. supt., impts. and expts. dept., 1928; head, sany. dept., Nov., 1927; ast. col. sec. and clk. of coun., Oct., 1928; head, sany. dept., 1929; sec., salaries comm., 1928 and 1929 in addn.; supt., census, 1930-31; senr. ast. col. treas., Nov., 1931; ag. P.M.G., 1933.
CARRROW, Lieut.-Commdr. JOHN HISTON R.N. (Ret.), D.S.C.—B. 1890; ed. Clifton Coll.; admtrve. offr., cls IV, Nigeria, June, 1919; ditto, cls I, Dec., 1932.
CARRUTHERS, ROBERT.—B. 1886; ed. Annan Academy; pub. wks. dept., Nigeria, 1914-20; instr. in arts and crafts, N. Prova., Nigeria, Sept., 1920.
CARTER, EDWARD STANLEY.—B. 1888; ed. Carpenter's Co. Tech. Inst.; war serv., 1914-18; ast. engrn., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1920; succed. Kowloon-Canton Rly., 1926-27 and in 1928.
CARTER, HUGH CLAY, M.C., M.E.I. (Canada).—B. 1884; ed. St. Albans Schl.; ast. engrn., Br. Honduras, Jan., 1921; ag. dir., pub. wks., Apr., to Dec., 1923; D.P.W., Br. Honduras, May, 1924.

CARTER, REGINALD HENRY.—2nd cls. ast. acctnt., army accounts dept., War Office, July, 1907; col. audit dept., May, 1911; ast. audt. to Uganda Prot., July, 1911; ag. audt., in 1914-1920 and 1921; senr. ast. audt., Apr., 1924; audt., Fiji, Sept., 1924; audt., N. Rhodesia, May, 1928; audt., Uganda, 1930.

CARTER, Sir WILLIAM MORRIS, K.T. BACH (1919), C.B.E. (1918), B.A., B.C.L.—Ed. at King's Schl., Canterbury, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; open exhibtn. in mod. hist., Jan., 1892; clas. mods. (honours), 1894; 2nd cls. hona. juris, 1896; certif. of honour, bar exam., 1899; awarded spe... prize in English constitutional law and legal hist., by coun. of legal educ., 1899; called to the bar, Lino.'s Inn, Nov., 1899; reglar. and prin. regisr. of documents, E. Africa Prot., Jan., 1902; ag. town mag., Mombasa, Apr., 1902; mag., 1st Oct., 1902; judge of H.M. high ct. of Uganda and H.B.M. ct. of appeal for E. Africa, Nov., 1903; ag. prin. judge and legal adviser, Apl. to Sept., 1904; ag. prin. judge, Mar. to Oct., 1907, Aug. to Dec., 1909, and Aug., 1911, to Jan., 1912; apptd. comsnr. to enquire into native land tenure, 1906; mem. of comtess., to consider land legislation, 1906; and to draft land legislation, 1909; president, native land settm. comtess., 1911; chief just., Uganda Prot., July, 1912; joint compiler of first edition of laws of Uganda Prot., 1909; ch. just., Tanganyika Territory, 12th Oct., 1920; ret., 1924; chmn., cotton enquiry comn., Uganda, 1924.

CARTMEL-ROBINSON, HAROLD FRANCIS, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1889; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Merton Coll., Oxford; probtnr., N. Rhodesia, Sept., 1912; asst. native comnrr., 1914; war serv., 1917-19; native comnrr., 1918; asst. mag., 1926; ag. prov. comnrr. for short periods in 1928, 1931, 1932 and 1933.
CASEY, NORMAN BRANDRETH.—B. 1882; ent. Fiji civ. serv., 1908; gaoler, 1917; ag. supt., 1918; ag. inspr.-gen., prisons, 1920; supt., prisons, 1923.
CASHIN, HON. SIR MICHAEL PATRICK, K.B.E. (1918); B. 1866; M.H.A. Newfoundland; mem. for Ferryland since 1893; mem. exec. coun. and min. of finance and customs, 1909; min. of finance, Jan., 1918.

CASOLANI, HENRY, I.S.O. (1928), M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1869; entd. Malta civ. serv., 1887; elk., 1895; translator, M.H.A.'s super. ct., 1899; transf. to lieut. gov.'s office, 1913; sent on spec. mission to the Regency of Tunis, 1917 and again in 1918, in connection with food supply of Malta; principal clk. lieut.-gov.'s office, 1919; sec. to Malta deleg. to the French govt. in connection with Maltese labour in France, 1919; sec. and exec. offr., emigrn. comtess., 1919; read. the "Palmes d'officier d'Academie," of the French Republic, 1920; supt., emigrn., Malta, 1922; sent on mission to London to confer with Commonwealth and other authorities on emigrn. questions, May, 1922; visited France with refnc. to Maltese lab., 1922; author numerous off. repts. on emigrn., wages, etc.
CASTONGUAY, JULIUS.—B. 1882; asst. in office of clk. of crown in chancery for Canada, 1908; asst. ch. electoral offr., 1920; ch. electoral offr., 1927.
CATCHPOLE, CAPT. E. K.—B. 1889; Rhodian pol., 1910-15; K.A.R., 1915-20; asst. supk., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1920; supt., 1928.

CATOR, GEOFFREY EDMUND, B.A.—B. 1884; ed. King's schl., Bruton and Selwyn Col., Camb. (Scholar); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1907; passed cadet, June, 1909; offr. cls. V, Mar., 1911; ag. readt., Labuan, in addn., Feb., 1917; offr. cls. IV., Jan., 1918; offr. cls. III., Nov., 1919; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1924; mem. stand. advry. comtess. on use opium, Malaya, Feb., 1926; temy. mem., legis. council, S.S., May, 1927; offr. cls. IB, dist. offr., Klang, Jan., 1928; ag. hr. readt., Perak, in addn., June-July, 1928; offr. cls. I.A., June, 1930; supt., govt. monopolies, S.S., 1930; M.L.C.; Br. rea., Selangor, July, 1932; ag. Br. res., Perak, July, 1933.

CATT, JOHN EDWARD, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1885; ed. Shoreham Gram. Schl. and Brighton Tech. Coll.; ch. engng. lectr., Seafield Park Coll., Hants, 1906; engrnr.-in-ch., S. Nigeria, 1911; lieut., R.N.V.R., 1918; asst. elect. engrnr., Kuala Lumpur, May, 1919; ex. elect. engrnr., F.M.S., May, 1925; elect. inspr., Selangor and Palang, June, 1925; ag. dep. ch. elect. engrnr. and inspr., F.M.S., Sept., 1926; ch. elec. engrnr. and inspr., F.M.S., Apr., 1932.
CATTANACH, A.—B. 1880; on milly. serv., Feb., 1915 to Feb., 1918; asst. cashier and cashier. Disposal and Liquidation Comn., 12th May, 1919 to 22nd July, 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 24th July, 1922.

CAVENAUGH, HERBERT JAMES LAWRENCE.—Ed. Ardingly Coll. and King's Coll., London; attd., R.I.C., Dublin for training, May, 1908; sub-inspr., Trinidad constab., Dec., 1908; serv. with H.M. Forces, Sept., 1915, June, 1919; capt.; G.S. and Victory Meds., Order of the Nile (4th cl.); ag. staff offr., local forces, Aug. and Sept., 1919; ag. inspr., Trinidad constab., Sept. to May, 1920; supt. of pol., Br. Honduras, 1st June, 1920; ag. dist. comnrr., Belize, Mar.-May, 1921; ag. supt., prisons in addition to substantive appt., Sept., 1924 to Mar., 1926; dep. dir., prisons, Gold Coast, 1927; dir., ditto, 1930.

CAVENDISH, RICHARD CHARLES ALKAXANDER.—B. 1883; served with B.S.A. pol., S. Rhodesia, 1906-11; asst. comnrr., pol., S. Nigeria, 1912; King's pol. med., 1926; senr. comnrr., pol., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1927; asst. inspr.-gen., pol., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1930; dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Nigeria, 1931; comnrr., pol., Kenya, 1931.

CENTLIVRES, ALBERT VAN DE SANDET, B.A. (Cape and Oxford), K.C.—Rhodes Schl., 1907; S. African Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1910; advoc., Cape prov. divn., sup. ct., S. Africa, 111; advoc., high ct., S. Rhodesia, 1913; partly draftsman, part. of Union of S. Africa, 1920; ag. judge, high ct., S. West Africa, 1922; ditto, Cape prov. divn., 1932-33.

CHALMERS, 1ST BARON, OF NORTHAM, (or. 1919); RIGHT HON. SIR ROBERT CHALMERS, P.C. (Ireland) 1916; G.C.B. (1916); K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1900)—B. 1858; ed. City of London schl. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; asst. sec. to the Treas., 1903; chmn. Ed. of In. Rev., 1907; permitt. sec. of Treas., 1911; gov. of Ceylon, July, 1913; assumed govt., Oct., 1913; joint sec. to the Treas., 1916; also under-sec. for Ireland, May to Sept., 1916; author of "History of Currency in the British Colonies," "A Translation of the Jataka from Pali," etc.

CHAMBERLAIN, GEORGE DIGBY.—B. 1898; B.A., St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; war serv., 1917-19; lieut., reg. R. of O.; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1925; seconded to C.O., as asst. prin., Oct., 1930.
CHAMBERLAIN, KENNETH SEYMOUR JAMES.—B. 1901; treas., Kenya, 1924-33; dep. treas., Fiji, Apr., 1933; ag. treas. and comnrr., inland rev., June, 1933.

CHAMBERS, FREDRICK JAMES—B. 1899; lt.-cmdmr., R.N. (retd.); ed. R.N. Coll., Osborne and Dartmouth; European War, 1914-18; pvt. to H.M. min., Addis Ababa, 1927-28; comnrr., III. grade, Somaliland, July, 1928.

CHAMBERS, WILFRID METCALFE, M.D. (Brux.) L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.)—B. 1882; med. offr., Hants. County Asylum, 1910-13; med. offr., grade III, F.M.S., Apr., 1913; med. offr., European hosp., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1923; surgn., Tan Tock Seng hosp., ag. prof., clin. surgery in addn., July., 1923; C.O. deleg., Br. Social Hygiene Cong., Wembley, Oct., 1926; ch. med. offr., Social Hygiene, Jan., 1927; ag. ch. med. offr., Singapore, in addn. Sept., 1927; ch. med. offr., Penang, Sept., 1927; S.S. deleg. 4th Imp. Social Hygiene Cong., London, July., 1929; ag. ch. med. offr., S'pore, Sept., 1931.
CHAMPION, A. M.—Aast. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Aug., 1909; dist. comsrr., Oct., 1918; 2nd grade prov. comsrr., 1931.

CHAMPION, REGINALD STUART, O.B.E. (1934),—B. 1895; ed. Sutton Valence; commands, E. Surrey Regt. (S.R.), 1913; served France, Egypt and Palestine, 1914-19; O.E.T.A., Palestine, 1917-19; dist. offr., Hebron, Palestine civ. adminstr., July, 1920; do., Beerseba, 1924; do., Jerusalem 1927; seconded to Aden as 2nd ast. res. and prot. sec., 1928; ag. 1st ast. res., July to Oct., 1929; dist. mag., Aden, 1932; ag. res. and commdr.-in-oh. (ch. comsrr.), Aden, Apr.-Oct., 1933.

CHANCELLOR, MAJOR SIR JOHN ROBERT, G.C.V.O. (1924), G.C.M.G. (1922), K.C.M.G. (1913), C.M.G. (1909), D.S.O.—Major R.E.; served with expdn. to Dongola, 1896, with force at Suakin (medal and Egyptian medal); in operations on N.W. frontier of India, 1897-8, with Tirah expdn. force, action of Dargai (ment. in desps.); sec. to col. def. comittee, Oct., 1906; ast. sec. to Impl. comittee on naval and mil. defence, 1909; gov., Mauritius, July, 1911; gov., Trinidad and Tobago, Dec., 1915; gov., S. Rhodesia, Sept., 1923; high comsrr. and comdrr.-in-oh., Palestine, and high comsrr. for Trans-Jordan, 1928-31.
CHANDLER, MAJOR HERBERT WILLIAM RALPH, M.C.—B. 1894; customs dept., Gold Coast, July, 1920; sr. collr., cust., June, 1928; inspr., cust., Sept., 1933.
CHANDLER, STANLEY FREDERICK.—B. 1898; entd. C.O., Nov., 1913; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Apr., 1916; on mil. serv., Sept., 1915 to Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

CHANDLER, SIR WILLIAM KELLMAN, K.C.M.G. (1927), KT. BACH. (1915), C.M.G. (1902), B.A., LL.D.—B. 1857; St. John's Coll., Camb. (1875-79); law tripes, 1879-80; called to the bar, Inner Tem., June, 1879; ag. escheator-gen. of Barbados, 1880-81; J.P., 1880; apptd. comsrr. of probates, Dec., 1880; ag. pol. mag. of Bridgetown, Aug. to Nov., 1880; ag. solr.-gen., Nov., 1880, to Mar., 1881; ag. judge of ast. ct. of appeal, Mar., 1881, to June, 1883; master-in-chancery, May, 1882; judge of ast. ct. of appeal, June, 1883; mem. of house of assem., Mar., 1883; represented parish of St. Peter until Nov., 1884; M.L.C., 1884; represented Barbados in negotiations at Washington in connection with McKinley Tariff Act, 1891; ag. ch. just., Nov., 1901, to Apr., 1902; ag. col. sec., July, 1902, to Feb., 1903, and Apr. to Oct., 1903.

CHANTER, ALFRED BERNARD, M.Inst.T.—B. 1884; ast. traffic supdt. and ag. dist. traffic supdt., S. Indian rly., 1907-17; dist. traffic supdt., E. African Mily. rly., German E. Africa (Occupied Territory), Aug., 1917-19; ag. traffic man., Tanganyika rly., in 1920, 1921 and 1923; traffic man., 20th June, 1923.

CHAPLIN, SIR FRANCIS DRUMMOND PERCY, G.B.E. (1923), K.C.M.G. (1917).—B. 1866; ed. Harrow and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A.; barrister, Lincoln's Inn, 1891; pres. of chamber of mines, Transvaal, 1906; M.H.A., Transvaal, 1907-10; M.L.A. for Germiston, Union of South Afr.; admstr. of Southern Rhodesia, 24th Dec., 1914 and at N. Rhodesia, March, 1921; resigned but app'ts., Sept., 1923.
CHAPMAN, HON. SIR FREDERICK REVANS KT. BACH. (1923).—B. 1849; judge of sup. c. New Zealand, 1903-21; pres. of ct. of arbtr., 1903-7; compiler of statutes, 1921-26.

CHARLEWOOD, LIEUT.-COMDR. CLINTON JAMES, D.S.C. (1914), R.N.R. (Ret.)—B. 1898; ed. Newcastle Prep. Sch., Newcastle-upon-Tyne; and on board H.M.S. "Worcester" from May, 1903 to July, 1905; midshipman in R.N.R. 4th Aug., 1905; employed by British India Steam Navigation Co., Ltd., July 1909 to Aug., 1914; comd. small craft taking part in blockade and subsequent capture of coast of German E. Afr., being selected by Rear-AdmL E. Charlton to surrondr. of Darossalam on 4th Sept., 1914; attached to Zanzibar govt. to superint. docks and repairs to Zanzibar govt. steamers "Cun." and "Khaliifa," July, 1917 to Apr., 1918; appt. to H.M.S. "Orooma" for escort duties in N. Atlantic, Apr. to Nov., 1918; ast. port ed., Zanzibar, Apr., 1919; port. offr., 5th Dec., 1922; lieut. comdr., R.N.R. (ret. list), 1st Apr., 1925.

CHARTER, ARTHUR ERNEST, O.B.E. (1927)—B. 1882; Transvaal land stimlt. bd., 1901; tax dept., 1902; priv. sec. to comsrr. of lands, 1902; ag. en. elk. and irrigtn. dept., 1908; ast. olk., admstr.'s off., June, 1910; prov. nat., Natal, Feb., 1928.

CHARTER, JOHN RICHARD NOEL.—B. 1894; forest ranger, grade I, F.M.S., Apr., 1906; ast. conserv., Jan., 1913; sub.-licent., R.N.V.K., Mar., 1916; demob., Jan., 1919; state forest offr., Batu Gajah, June, 1919; ast. conserv. forests, Nov., 1920; state forest offr., Pahang East, 1923 and 1925; dep. conserv., forests, Apr., 1929; hony. game warden, N. Sembilan, addn., July, 1923; conserv., forests, Jan., 1930; state forest offr., Pahang West, Nov., 1931.

CHASE, WILLIAM HENRY, O.B.E. (1929)—B. 1880; ed. Blundells; graduated Lond. F.R.C.V.S., 1907; govt. vet. surg., Cap. Colony, 1902; ch. vet. offr., Bechuanaaland Prov., 1905; hon. sub-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol.; J.P., 1907.

CHASEN, FREDERIC NUTTER.—B. 1890; trooper, Norfolk Yeomanry (K.O.R.R.), Apr., 1915; 2nd lieut., Suffolk Regt., Mar., 1919; curator, Raffles Museum, S'pore, Jan., 1924; curator, Mar., 1924; dir. Raffles Museum & Library, S'pore, Mar., 1932.

CHAYTOR, C. J.—B. 1889; ed. King's Worcester; served in Royal Navy, 1906-12 as paymr.; ast. res., N. Nigeria; attd. as btr. Cameroons Expdy. Force, Aug., 1914; 1st May, 1916; seconded to Admty., Aug., 1917; served as paymr. lieut.-commdr., H.M.S. Liverpool, Adriatic, Mediterranean and Black Sea, demob., May, 1919; 1st cls. interpreter, French and higher standard Hausa, 1919; lower standard Arabic, 1924; temp. attd. to C.O., 1927.

CHAYTOR, MAJ.-GEN. SIR EDWARD WAUGH CLERVAUX, K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C.V.O., C.K.P., A.D.C. TO THE KING.—B. 1868; jnd. Marlborough Hussar Vol. (N.Z.), 1886; lieut.-col., Oct., 1888; maj.-col., Mar., 1893; maj. in N.Z. Militia, Mar., 1896; lieut.-col., Jan., 1902; temp. col., Jan., 1903; transf. to N.Z. Staff Corps, Jan., 1911; prov. col., June, 1913; maj.-gen., Apr., 1917; com. N.Z. mil. forces, Dec., 1919; ret., Mar., 1920; served in S. African War, 1900-1902 (seriously wounded); mentd. in desps.; (Queen's...

SMAN, HAROLD AMBROSE ROBINSON. ed. Perse Schol.; European mast., Sehl., Sept., 1907; instruc. Penang ses in addn., Aug., 1908; snr. instruc., 1909; headmast., Northam rd. schl.; inspr. schls., Penang, Jan., 1923; n., Johore, Mar., 1928.

JOHN NAPIER THEIGER, G.C.M.G. M.G.—B. 1868; ed. at Winchester and Joll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. law), 1891; Fellow of All Souls, 1892; called to ter Nem., 1893; mem. of London schl.; mem. of L.C.C., 1904; gov. of Queens- Aug., 1905; gov. of N.S. Wales, 16th 9, to Mar., 1913; ag. gov.-gen. of Dec., 1909, to Jan., 1910; chancellor of of St. M. and St. G., 1914-1916; India, 1916; first lord of the adm'y., 1924.

ETON, REGINALD LESLIE, M.R.C.S., Lond.—B. 1901; casualty house surg., se surg., 1927, and house physician, hosp., Birmingham; dist. med. offr., ec. 1928; ag. dir., health, Aug., 1933; specialist, June-Sept., 1933.
ON, N., B.A. (Hons.), B.M., B.Ch. T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1900; med. anyika Territory, Mar., 1929.
EOBERT SYDNEY ALGERNON, Dip. Agr.—B. 1908; field offr., genetic dept., research station, Trinidad, 1931; spl. agrl. tserrat, Mar., 1931.

OLM, JOSEPH ANDREW.—B. 1868; b. schls., St. Andrews, Univ. of St. Xavier, Antigonish (B.A. and M.A.), ousie Law Schl., Halifax (LL.B.); to the bar, 1886; mayor of Halifax, and 1911; pres. of Union of Canadian cities, 1910; K.C., 1907, puisne judge, Nova Scotia, Feb., 1916; ch. just., 1.

TIAN, JOHN HAROLD SMITH, M.C.—ed Alderman Newton's schl., Leicester; ter compet. exam., elk., G.P.O., 1904; 4th Aug., 1914 to 8th June, 1919; lesp., Feb., 1915; commanded cavalry als., 1919; loaned to C.O., 17th Jan., 1921; r. offr. and assigned to C.O., 1st Apr., cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.

TIANI, HENRY PEROT, F.R.G.S.—B. Cranleigh Schol.; 6th cls. elk., cust., ia., 1897; 5th cls. elk., treasy., 1903; 3rd and govt. survr., lands and mines dept., 1 cls. offr. and govt. survr., 1908; drawing office supt., 1924; sec., lands and mines dept., 1927; sec. and acctnt., dept. of lands and mines, 1929; ag. commr., lands and mines, 1933.

CHRISTIE, JAMES, LL.M. (N.Z.).—B. 1880; ed. State schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; schl.-teacher, 1897; N.Z. treas. dept., 1901; crown law office, 1904; law drafting office, 1907; astt. law draftsman, 1912; ag. law draftsman, 1916; law draftsman, 1918.

CHRISTISON, FRED HAMILTON, M.B.E. (mily.) (1918).—Ast. treas., G. Coast, 1901-03; temp. commr., army pay dept., 1915; K.A.R. pay dept., July, 1916; ag. ch. paymr., K.A.R., 1917; ment. in desps., 1917; astt. treas., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1919; senr. astt. treas., May, 1930; ag. dep. treas., Oct., 1932-Apr., 1933.

CHRISTOFFELSZ, ARTHUR ERIC.—B. 1890; B.A., LL.B., Cantab.; barrister-at-law; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1915; attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Mar., 1915; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, in addition to his own duties, July, 1915; attached to Kegalle Kachcheri, Apr., 1916; addtl. commr. of requests and pol. mag., Kegalle, in addn. to own duties, Apr., 1916; ag. extra officio astt. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1916; ag. pol. mag., Jaffna, 1917; office astt. to govt. agt., Uva, Mar., 1919; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Oct., 1920; ditto, Kurunegala, Sept., 1921; astt. attlit. offr., Oct., 1926; ag. pol. mag., Panadura, Oct. to Nov., 1926; ag. dist. judge, Matare, Mar., 1927; astt. attlit. effr., Dec., 1927; astt. govt. agt., Kegalle, June, 1931.
CHRISTOFFELSZ, BASIL MORRIS.—B. 1886; cls. V, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1931; office astt., Kalutara kach., Feb., 1931.

CHURCHILL, WILLIAM FOSTER NORTON.—B. 1898; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb. (exhib.); B.A. (1921); 2nd lieut., R.F.A., Aug., 1917; served in France, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; astt. dist. office, Kajang, May, 1921; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; astt. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, Apr., 1924; astt. sec. to res., Selangor, Apr., 1926; priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; chmn. and sec., sany. bd., Larut, Aug., 1932; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933.

CHURCHILL, RIGHT HON. WINSTON LEONARD SPENCER, P.C. (1907).—M.P. for Oldham, 1900-6; for N.W. Manchester, 1906-8; for Dundee, 1908; late lieut. 4th Queen's Own Hussars; ed. Harrow, Sandhurst; entd. army, 1896; served with Spanish forces in Cuba, 1896 (1st cls. Spanish Order of Military Merit); served, attached 31st Punjab Infantry, with Malakand field force, 1897; present at operations in Bejaur, including actions of 16th and 30th Sept. (desps., medal with clasp); served as orderly offr. to Sir W. Lockhart with Tirah exped. force, 1898 (clasp); served, attached 21st Lancers, with Nile exped. force, 1898; present at battle of Khartoum (medal with clasp); contested Oldham (C.) 1899; served as lieut. South Africa Light Horse; acted as correspond., "Morning Post," South Africa, 1899-1900; taken prisoner, action 16th Nov., but escaped 12th Dec.; present at actions of Aston Homes, Venters Spruit, Huscar Hill, Cingolo, Monte Cristo, and at battles of Spion Kop, Vaal Krantz, and Pieters; also operations round De wetshoep, April, 1900; passage of Sand River, 15th May; engagements of Johannesburg and Diamond Hill, and capture of Pretoria (medal with six clasps); partly, under-sec. of state for the Colonies, Dec., 1905; visited Uganda and E. Africa, 1907; pres. of Bd. of T., 1908; Home sec., 1910-11; first lord of the Admiralty, 1911; chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, 1915; min. of munitions, 1917; sec. of state, War Office and Air Ministry, Jan., 1919; S. of S. for the Colonies, 14th Feb., 1921 to 26th Oct., 1922; chancellor of the exchequer, 7th Nov., 1924; author of "The Story of the Malakand Field Force"; "The River War"; "Savrola"; "London to Ladysmith, via Pretoria"; "Ian Hamilton's March"; "Life of Lord Randolph Churchill."

CHURMS, W. H.—B. 1890; apptd., after exam., asst. library attendant, C.O., 6th Apr., 1911; on military service from 5th Aug., 1914, to 19th Jan., 1919; paper room clk. in library, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Apr., 1921.

CLARENCE, EARL OF (or. 1776) Rt. Hon. GEORGE HERBERT HYDE VILLIERS, P.C. (1931), G.C.M.G. (1930), J.P., D.L.—B. 1877; ed. Eton; prev., lord-in-waiting to H.M.; hon. lieut.-col. (Hertford Reg.) A.D.C. to Lord Lieut. of Ireland (Lord Dudley); chanc., Primrose League, 1919-21; capt., Gentlemen-at-Arms, 1922-23 and 1924-25; dep. speaker, House of Lords; partly. under-sec. of state for dominion affrs., and chmn., overseas settlement cte., 9th July, 1929; chmn. B.B.C., 1927; gov. gen., Union of S. Africa, 1931.

CLARK, ADRIAN JOHN, LL.B. (Lond.), O.B.E. (Mily.)—B. 1889; King's schol. and Good-enough medallist (Fr. and German), Westminster; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1910; junr. coun. to treasy., 1917; served in Great War, France, Mar., 1915 to May, 1919; capt., 23rd London Regt.; D.A.A.G., Third Army and Lieut.-Col., R.A.F., (S.O., 1st grade); brevet-major and twice ment. in despatches; mag., Victoria, Trinidad, July, 1924; ag. puisne judge, Sept. to Dec., 1924; P.O.S., Trinidad, Jan., 1927; puisne judge, Jamaica, May, 1927; ag. ch. just., July-Nov., 1932.

CLARK, GEORGE HARVEY.—B. 1873; ed. Brixton gram. schl., London, and Potadam schl., Jamaica; clk., col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, Mar., 1890 to July, 1891 (resigned); admitted solr., sup. ct., Jamaica, Sept., 1897; clk. of cts., St. Mary, Jamaica, Nov., 1903; super. res. mag., Dec., 1903 to May, 1904; ag. res. mag., St. Mary, Aug. to Nov., 1904; ag. res. mag., Clarendon, Aug. to Sept., 1905; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, Aug., 1905; ag. res. mag., St. Mary, June, 1906, July, 1906 to Aug., 1907, and from Nov., 1907; ag. res. mag., St. Thomas, in 1909; ditto, Clarendon, in 1910; ag. asst. res. mag., Kingston, in 1910; ag. res. mag., St. Mary, in 1911 and 1912; ditto, Portland, for 6 wks. in 1912; res. mag., St. Mary, Feb., 1913.

CLARK, JOHN BUCHANAN.—B. 1897; ed., George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh; S.A. Coll. Schls. and S.A. Coll. (now Univ. of Cape Town); Balliol Coll., Oxford; M.A. (Cape Town), B.A. (Hons.), Oxford; Rhodes schol., 1917; 2nd lt. and lt., R.A.S.C., France, Aug., 1917 to Feb., 1919; lect. in hist., Univ. of Cape Town, 1921; lect., classics and hist., Univ. of Witwatersrand, 1924; inspr., schls., June, 1925; ag. dir., European educn., June, 1926-June, 1927; dir., European educn., N. Rhodesia, June, 1927; mem., European educn. comsn., N. Rhodesia, 1929; temp. M.L.C. in 1930 and 1931.

CLARK, J. McK., Ch.B. (Aberdeen), D.T.M. (Liverpool), Certif., Liverpool Schl. of Trop. Med.—Apptd. to W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, 1914; served in Cameroons Expdy. Force, 1915-16; temp. lieut. (gen. list), 1915-16; temp. med. offr., E. Africa, 1916; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 19th Apr., 1918; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1924.

CLARK, WILLIAM CLIFFORD, M.A.—B. 1889; ed., Williamstown High Schl., Queen's Univ. (Kingston) and Harvard Univ. (Ricardo prize scholarship, Harvard Univ., 1915); Henry L. memorial fellow and tutor, divn. of hist., gov't and economics, Harvard Univ., 1914-15; successively lecturer, ast. prof., assoc. prof. and prof. economics, Queen's Univ., 1915-23; advr., dept. of lab., Ottawa, 1918-19; prof., commerce, Queen's Univ., 1931-32; finan. advr., Inp. econ. Comm. Confere., Ottawa, 1932; dep. min. finan. and sec. treasy., bd., Ottawa, Nov., 1932; advr., World Monetary and Economic Conf., 1933; author of various papers in economics and finance and collaborator in Warren's "Representative American Industries."

CLARK, SIR WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.S.I. (1915), K.C.M.G. (1930), C.S.I. (1911), C.M.G. (1903)—B. 1876; Ed. Eton and Trinity Col., Cambridge; clk. Bd. of T., 1899; sec. to mission to Shanghai to negotiate comm., treaty with China, 1901; ag. 2nd sec., diplomatic serv., 1902; sec. to royal commission on supply of food, etc., in war, 1903-5; pvt. sec. at Bd. of T. to Rt. H.R.H. D. Lloyd George, 1906, and to Rt. Hon. W. Churchill, 1908; and to Rt. Hon. D. Lloyd George as chancellor of the exchequer, 1916; mem. for commerce and industry of the com., the Viceroy of India, 1910-16; comptroller comm'l. intell. dept. Bd. of T., 1916; com. gen. dept. of overseas trade, Sept., 1917; com. comsnr. for Great Britain in Canada, Sept., 1917.

CLARKE, ARTHUR GRENfell, B.A.—L.C.E., ed. Mountjoy Sch., Dublin and Trinity Col., Dublin; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Dec., 1920; passed cadet, Jan., 1932; asst. to sec., Carriacou, affr., Feb., 1932.
CLARKE, F. H.—Ast. game warden, K.A.P., 1919; ag. game warden, Kenya, 1932.

CLARKE, HON. SIR FRANCIS GRENfell K.B.E., M.L.C.—B. 1879; ed. Scotch Col., Melbourne, Melbourne Univ. and Exeter Col., Oxford; M.L.C. for N. Prov. (Vic.); mun. lands and water supply, Nov., 1917 to Oct., 1920; min. of pub. wks. and water supply, Oct., 1920 to July, 1923; pres., leg. comsn., Victoria, Jan., 1923.

CLARKE, HON. SIR FREDERICK JAMES K.C.M.G. (1911), C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. Christ Church Mid. Schl., Barbados, and Carus Col., Cambridge; B.A., 1879; M.H.A., Barra, 1887; pres., Barbados agric. soc'y., 1905; rec. poor law board, M.E.C., 1906; J.P.; Barbados vol. force, 1906; speaker, H. of Barbados, since 1898.
CLARKE, GROPPET T. S.—B. 1879; ed. P.M.O., Basutoland, 1913; clk., treas., 1914; asst. to asst. comsnr., 1919; sub-inspr., Basuto Mounted Police, 1921; war serv., 1916-19.
CLARKE, JOSEPH SHERWOOD, B.A. (Hons.), B.A.—B. 1903; ed. Rossall Schl. and Harrow Univ.; mupt., educn., Nigeria, Apr., 1920; headmstr., Grammar Schl., St. Vincent, 1931.
CLARKE, ROBERT WILLIAM, F.I.C., dist. food and drugs, agric. chem., bioi., etc., and bacteriology.—Analyst to san. comsnr., Gibraltar, Oct., 1913 to Jan., 1921; govt. anal., Cyprus, Jan., 1921.

CLARKE, THOMAS LISLE EVANS, M.C., McGill (1911).—B. 1885; ed. at the Law Schl. and Harrison Coll., Barbados, and McGill Univ., Montreal; junr. res. surgeon, gen. hosp., Barbados, Aug., 1911; med. offr., V.C., May, 1912; ag. comsnr. and mag., May, 1912; mem. of quarantine bd., health offr., exec. exec. coun., and J.P., Virgin Is., July, 1912; comsnr., Virgin Is., Apr., 1913 to Jan., 1914; comsnr., mag. and dep. judge, summary jur. et al., Virgin Is., from Dec. 1914 to May, 1917; and from July, 1917 to Oct., 1918; med. offr., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1918; provisional mem. fed. leg. and exec. couns., Leeward Islands, Mar., 1918; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918.

CLARKK, W.—B.1895; ed. Christian Brothers' Coll., S. Africa; war serv., Rebellion and German S.W. campaign, 1914-15; S. Africa Overseas Contg., 1916-18; temp. lieut., 1918; aspt., cust., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ag. secr., aspt., cust., Feb.-May, 1930.

CLARKSON, CHARLES FRANCIS.—B. 1881; ed. Boys' Model Schl. and Boys' High Schl., Durban; admitted atty., Natal prov. divn., Feb., 1905; offr., Durban Light Infy., and adjt.; mem., prov. coun., 1915; mem., exec. coun., 1926; senator, 1930; min., posts and tels. and pub. wks., Mar., 1933.

CLARKSON, FRANK CECIL, O.B.E. (1933), M.B.E. (1930)—B. 1882; P.W.D., Sudan Govt., 1905-07; ch. asst., S. Nigeria surv., 1911-12; European War, Gallipoli, Egypt and Palestine, 1914-19; asst. aspt., pub. wks., and cotton inspr., Nevis, Jan., 1921; ag. commnr., mag., etc., Virgin Is., Apr., 1922; col. engr. and survr.-gen., Dominica, Jan., 1923; mem., leg. coun.; commnr., mag., etc., Virgin Is., Nov., 1926.

CLAUSON, GERARD LESLIE MAKING, C.M.G. (1933), O.B.E. (Mily.), 1919—B. 1891; scholar of Eton, 1903, and C.C.C., Oxford, 1910; Boden Sanskrit Scholar, 1911; 1st cls. Class, Hon. Mods., 1912; Hall-Houghton Syriac prizeman, 1913; 2nd cls. Greats, 1914; B.A., 1919; James Mew Arabic Scholar, 1920; apptd. after compet. exam. 2nd cls. elk., B. of Inland Rev., Sept., 1914, but released for mil. service; 2nd lieut., 7th Som. L.I., 21st Aug., 1914; transf'd. to gen. list, 19th April, 1915; served in War Office, Gallipoli, Egypt and Mesopotamia; capt., Gen. Staff, 26th Dec., 1915 to 12th Apr., 1916, and 23rd Apr., 1918, to 28th Feb., 1919; mentioned in despatches Mesopotamia, 1917, Egypt, 1918; French Croix de Guerre, with Palms, 1917; 2nd cls. elk. in C.O., 1st Mar., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; on sp. serv., Lausanne, for Turkish peace treaty, June to July, 1923; accredited rep. to Permanent Mandates Coms., at various sessions from June, 1926 onwards; student at Imp. Defence Coll., Jan.-Dec., 1927; sec., C.O. currency comte., 1930; visited S.S. and Hong-Kong as mem. and sec. of Hong-Kong currency comte., Feb. to June, 1931; deptl. adviser to U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Conf., Ottawa, July to Aug., 1932.

CLAXTON, H. W., A.C.A.—Ed. Weymouth Coll., 1909-16; chartered acctnt. (Eng.), 1924 (hons. inter.); asst. treas., Somaliland, 1924; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; senr. asst. treas., Somaliland, 1929; treas. and ch. of cust., 1933.

CLAY, GROFFREY FLETCHER, M.C., B.Sc.-Agr. (Edin.), N.D.A., N.D.D. (Hons.), Lanc. Dip. in Agric., B.1895; capt., 5th Batt. Duke of Wellington's Regt., 1914-19; agr. offr., Uganda, 1924; geneticist, Amani, Tanganyika, 1927-28; senr. agr. offr., Uganda, 1928; dep. dir., agr., 1931; agr. dir., agr., 1931.

CLELAND, JOHN STOCKWIN, M.B.E., F.R.I.B.A., M.I.A.—Draughtman, P.W.D., Transvaal, Mar., 1909; asst. archt., P.W.D., Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; archt., Sept., 1920; sec. for pub. wks., June, 1932.

CLEMENTI, SIR CECIL, G.C.M.G. (1931), K.C.M.G. (1926), C.M.G. (1916)—Ed. St. Paul's schl. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford (demy, 1894; B.A., 1898; M.A., 1901), hon. mention Hertford schl., 1895; 1st cls. mods., 1896; hon. mention Ireland and Craven schl., 1896; Boden Sanskrit scholar, 1897; prox. acc. Gaisford (Greek Prose), 1897; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1898; prox. acc. Chancellor's Latin essay, 1899; cadet, Hong Kong, 1899; passed cadet, 1900; ag. asst. col. sec., and ag. clk. of councils, Dec., 1900, to Oct., 1901; asst. regi.-gen., Aug., 1901; mem. bd. of examrs. in Chinese, Apr., 1902; seconded for spec. serv. under govt. of India, May, 1902; J.P., June, 1902; ag. asst. col. sec., and elk. ef councils, Sept., 1902, to June, 1903; sec. interpretation bd., Dec., 1902; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si, April to June, 1903; mem. of land ct., New Territories, Dec., 1903; ag. asst. land offr. and pol. mag., New Territories, May, 1905, to Sept., 1906; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Oct., 1907; attended Internat. Opium Conf. at Shanghai, Feb., 1909; priv. sec. to H.E. the Admstr., Apr., 1910; ag. col. sec., and mem. exec. and legis. couns., on various occasions, 1911-12; col. sec., B. Guiana, 1913; admstn. govt., 8th June, 1916 to 14th Apr., 1917, Apr. to Oct., 1919 and June to Oct., 1921; col. sec., Ceylon, Nov., 1922; admstn. govt., Nov., 1922 to Feb., 1923 and Apr. to Oct., 1925; gov., Hong Kong, 1st Nov. 1925; Knt. of Grace of St. John of Jerusalem, 1926; hon. I.L.D., Hong Kong Univ., 1926; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Straits Settlements, and high commnr., Malaya States, 1929.

CLEMENTI, LOCKHART.—B. 1881; served in Cape Mounted Rifles; sub-inspr., Beaufortland Mounted Pol., 1905; inspr., 1913; served in European War; staff offr., 1926; dep. asst. commnr., 1929; asst. commnr., 1930.
CLEMENTS, JOHN BURTON, B.Sc. Forestry (Edin.)—B. 1891; for. offr., Nyasaland, Sept., 1920; ag. ch. for. offr., Oct. to Dec., 1920 and May to Dec., 1923; ch. for. offr., Dec., 1923.
CLEMMYE, A. V., M.A., M.B., B.Ch. (Oxf.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.M.S.A.—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1928.

CLENDINNING, J., B.Sc. (Lond.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. Queen's Univ., Belfast; asst. dir., surveys, Gold Coast, Oct., 1919; dep. dir., cadastral branch, Jan., 1920; dir., do., Dec., 1920; dep. survr.-gen., Apr., 1924; ag. survr.-gen. on various occasions, 1921-26; survr.-gen., Oct., 1926.

CLIFFE, CEDRIC ARTHUR LEWIS.—B. 1902; schol. of Eton, 1915 and of Balliol Coll., Oxford, 1921; Craven schol., 1922; 1st cls. hon., mods., 1923; Ireland schol., 1923; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1925; apptd. to C.O. after compet. exam., 20th Oct., 1925; sec., managing comte., Imp. Bureau of Mycology, 1926; seconded as pvt. sec. to gov., Nigeria, 1927; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec., C.O. (Sir Samuel Wilson), 3rd Feb., 1930; temp. ag. prin., 1st Oct., 1931; asst. pvt. sec. to S. of S. for the cols. (Sir Philip Cunliffe-Lister), 9th Nov., 1931.; ag. prin., 4th Aug., 1933.

CLIFFORD, CAPT. HON. SIR BEDE EDMUND HUGH, K.C.M.G. (1933), C.B. (1931), C.M.G. (1924), M.V.O. (1920).—B. 1890; served, European War; pvt. sec. Lord Novar, gov.-gen. Commonwealth Australia, 1918-20; sec., H.R.H. Prince Arthur of Connaught (gov.-gen. and commdr.-in-ch., Union of S. Africa) 1921-23; sec. Earl of Athlone, gov.-gen. and commander-in-ch., Union of S. Africa, 1924; Imp. sec., S. African high commnr., Apr., 1924; rep. of H.M. Govt. of Gt. Britain in the Union of S. Africa, 1928; gov. and commdr.-in-ch. of the Bahamas, 1931.

CLIFFORD, Sir Hugh Charles, G.C.M.G. (1921), G.B.E. (1926), K.C.M.G. (1909); C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1866; joined Perak service, 1883; passed in Malay, Feb., 1885; collr. land rev., Kuala Kangsar, Mar., 1885; on special service to Pahang, 1887 (recd. thanks of S. of S.); ag. govt. agt. there, 1887-8; supt., Ulu Pahang, 1899; ag. Br. rea. Pahang, 1890 to 1891, and from Feb., 1893; took a leading part in suppression of Pahang rebellion, 1892; sec. to govt., Selangor, Dec., 1894, but continued to act as rea. of Pahang; visited Coco-Keeling Is., as special commnr., June, 1894; led armed expd. into native states of Trénggânn and Kelantan to effect capture of fugitive rebel leaders, Mar.-June, 1895 (recd. thanks of S. of S.); res., Pahang, July, 1896; gov. of North Borneo and Labuan, 1899 to 1901; Br. rea. Pahang, 1901; ag. col. sec., Trinidad and Tobago, Sept., 1903, confirmed Sept., 1904; sent on deputation to B. Guiana in Nov., 1905, to enquire into municipal institutions; admstd. govt., Mar. to Aug., 1904, and from Apr. to Oct., 1906; col. sec., Ceylon, May, 1907; admstd. govt., on various occasions, 1907-12; gov., G. Coast, Dec., 1912; gov., Nigeria, July, 1919; gov. of Ceylon, Nov., 1925; gov. S. Stltms. and high commr., Malay States, June, 1927; ret., 1929; concluded with M. Noufliard, the lieut.-gov. of Dahomey, in Aug., 1914 an agreement for the temporary partition between the British and French Govts., and for the provisional administration of Togoland; admstd. Br. sphere of occupation, Togoland, concurrently with the G. Coast, from Aug., 1914, to Aug., 1919; chmn., comte. on W. African trade and taxation, Oct.-Nov., 1921 (received thanks of S. of S.); is part author, with Sir Frank Swettenham, of a Malayan dictionary, and author of more than a dozen books, mostly on Malayan subjects, and of a translation of the penal code into Malay; also of a history of the G. Coast regt. in the E. African campaign.

CLIFT, Hon. J. Augustus, C.B.E. (1918), K.C.—Called to the bar, 1884; M.H.A. Newfoundland, 1889; speaker of assembly, 1891; clk. of assembly, 1893-97; elected to assembly, representing Twillingate dist., 1900; re-elected, 1904; min. of agric. and mines and mem. of cabinet, 1904; re-elected, Twillingate dist., 1908 and 1913; min. of agric. and mines and mem. of cabinet, 1918.

CLINKARD, George William, M. (Com.) (N.Z.).—B. 1833; ed. Auckland Grammar Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; N.Z. govt. serv., 1909-14 in inspection of machinery and land and income tax depts.; census and statistics office, 1917; industries and commerce dept., 1920; sec. to Samoan admstrn. and dep. admstr., 1929; sec. for industries and commerce and gen. man., tourist and health reports, 1930.
CLIPSHAM, Philip Richard.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 31st Sept., 1920; seconded as regis. on staff of high commr., in Canada for H.M.'s govt. in Gt. Britian, 1932.
CLIVE, Capt. J. H.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, May., 1920.
CLOKTE, Montrose Claverhouse.—B. 1880; master's office, Cape, 1899; Pretoria, 1902; asst. regtr., high ct., Jo'burg., 1903; asst. regtr., Cape prov. divn., 1921; regtr., appellate divn., 1923.

CLUBB, Hon. William Reid—B. 1884; served on Morris munic. coun., 1916-20; mem. leg. assembly for Morris, Manitoba, 1920-22; re-elec., July, 1922; min. of public works, Manitoba, 8th Aug., 1922; re-elec. for Morris by accl., 26th Aug., 1922.

CLUTTERBUCK, P.A., M.C.—B. 1897; schol. of Malvern Coll. and of Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, 1916; Coldstream Guards, 1916-19; caps and adjt., 3rd batt., Oct., 1917; M.C. and med. in depts., 1918; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., G.P.O., 1st Sept., 1919; transf. to C.O., 13th Feb., 1922; sec., managing comte., Imperial Bureau of Mycology, 1923-25; addnl. pvt. sec. to parly. under sec. (Lord Arnott), June-Oct., 1924; sec., Milner-Lovat agrl. centre, 1924-25; sec., Donoughmore commn. on Ceylon constitution and visited Ceylon, 1927-28; priv. sec. to perm. under sec. for Doma. affr., 23rd Aug., 1928; prin., Doma. Office, 14th Nov., 1929; U.K. delegation to L. of N. assmte., 1929, 1930 and 1931, and to reparations confer., Lausanne, 1932.

CLUVER, Eustace Henry, M.A., M.D., B.Ch., D.P.H.—B. 1894; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S. Africa, Hartford Coll., Oxford, and King's Coll. Hosp., London; Rhodes scholarship for Stellenbosch; Burrey Yeo. schol., King's Coll.; senr. demy, Magdalen Coll., Oxford; int. sci., final hon., nat. science (physiology); served with 1st S. African field ambulance in France; major, S.A.M.C., Active Citizen Force; lecturer in geology, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, 1934; prof. of physiology, Univ. of Witwatersrand-Johannesburg, 1919-26; asst. health offr., Univ. of S. Africa, 1926; assoc. edr., Journal of Industrial Hygiene (Americas); published many papers in tech. juls. on subject of physiology and hygiene.

CLUVER, Frederick William Paul, B.A. (Cape), M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Cape Town), Fellow of the Royal Institute of Public Health; major, S.A.M.C. (res. of offrs.).—Ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Edinburgh Univ. and Univ. of Cape Town; res. med. offr., gen. hosp., Pretoria; and med. offr., to S.A. Air Force (major S.A.M.C.), 1924; O.C., mily. hosp., Wynberg, 1926; O.C. mily. hosp., Roberts Heights, 1927; asst. health offr., Union health dept., Pretoria, 1932; de. Durban, 1933.

COAKER, Hon. Sir Wm. F., K.B.E. (1923)—President, fisherman's protective union, Newfoundland; M.H.A. for Bonavista, 1913; M.H.A. Twillingate, 1914; mem. of tonnage comte., 1917; M.E.C., 1917; min. without portfolio, 1918.

COALES, Francis George, M.I.C.E., M.I.S.E.—B. 1889; ed. Elstow Schl., Bedford; draftsmen, P.W.D., Perak, 1914; asst. engrnr., 1917; ag. exec. engrnr., grade II., 1919; ag. exec. engrnr. and asst. to D.P.W.D., exec. engrnr., 1923; ag. asst. D.P.W.D., 1925; exec. engrnr. in ch., deptl. tech. schl., 1925-27; Lieut., M.S.V.R., 1926; exec. engrnr., Larut; Lieut., M.S.V.R., 1929; ag. senr. exec. engnr., K. Kangsar, 1929; senr. exec. engrnr., Jan., 1930.
COATES, John Spencer.—B. 1878; prim. m. survr., Ceylon, Sept., 1910; designation exchanged to govt. mineralogist and salt adviser.

COATES, Rt. Hon. Joseph Gordon, P.C. (1926), M.C.—B. 1878; ed. privately; mem. of New Zealand parl. since 1911; min. of juris., 1919-20; P.M.G., 1919-25; min. of pub. wel., 1920-26; min., native affr., 1921-1928; min., nat. exter. affr., 1928; min., mines, 1928; min., agr., 1928; ret. from office, 1928; joined coalition min., 1931; min., pub. wks., 1931-33; min., transp., since 1931; min., finance, 1933; min., star. duties, 1933; N.Z. rep. at Imp. Conf., 1935 as at Ottawa conf., 1932; served with N.Z. Knt. Force as capt. and major, 1916-1919; awarded M.C. with bar.

COATS, ROBERT HAMILTON, B.A., F.S.S.(hon.), F.R.C.S.—B. 1874; ed. Toronto Univ.; bankers scholarship in polit. economy, and Wyld prize, English; on staff of "Toronto Globe," 1897-1900; editor, Labour Gas., and ch. statistician, dept. of labour, 1901-12; mem., comm. on the cost of living, 1913-14; dom. statistician and contrlr. of census, 1914; author of "The Labour Movement in Canada" (Canada and its Provinces); joint author of life of James Douglas (Makers of Canada series); off. del. to Br. Empire Statistical Confnee, 1920; mem. of comm. on statistics under the League of Nations, 1920; mem., International Statistical Inst.

COBB, CHARLES ERIC, M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. (Liverpool).—B. 1895; med. offr., F.M.S., Apr., 1920; ag. sr. med. offr., July, 1927; med. offr., dist. hosp., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1929; ag. sr. med. offr., Pahang, July, 1929.
COBB, ENID MARGARET MARY, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. (Liverpool).—Med. offr., F.M.S., Apr., 1920; infant welfare centre, Taiping, Mar., 1928; gen. hosp., Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1929.

COBBE, HON. JOHN GEORGE.—Ed. national schls., church schl., Ireland, and Johnson's Academy, Dublin; el. to N.Z. Parlt., 1928; min. of marine and immigrn., 1928-30; min. of industries and commerce, 1928-29; min. of defence, 1929; min. of just., 1930; min. of marine, 1931.

COCHRANE, CAPT. BASIL WILLIAM DUNDONALD.—B. 1891; Cape Mounted Riflemen, 1908-16; served S.W. Africa campaign and Mesopotamia (R. of O.); "Dunsterforce" (Azerbaijan), North Persia, 1918-19; South Persia Rifles, Kerman Brig., capt. and adjt., 1st Cavalry, 1919-20; "1914-15" star, Br. War, Victory and I.G.S. medal (bar, S. Persia); constable, Kenya pol., 1921; asst. supt.d., pol., 1925.

COCHRANE, EDGAR, M.B., Ch.B. (Glasgow), (1925), D.P.H. (1931).—B. 1897; on war serv., 1915-19; ag. dist. surg., Jesselton, Br. N. Borneo, 1925-26; asst. govt. med. offr. of health and dep. port health offr., Br. Guiana, 1932.

COCKEY, C. E. E.—Ed. St. Edwards Schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; M.A.; served in France and Salonica; 3rd Devons, atchd. 2nd Glouc. Regt., May, 1915, to Jan., 1919; ag. capt.; mentd. in despatches; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, June, 1919.

COCKRAM, BEN.—B. 1903; ed. Victoria, Taunton's Schl. and Oxford; hist. schl., Queen's Coll., 1921-25; 1st cls., mod. hist., 1925; Goldsmith's sen. student, 1926; 1st cls., med. and mod. hist., London, 1923; fellow, Michigan, 1925-26; Ph.D., 1926; apptd. after compet. exam. asst. prin., Dominions Office, 4th Nov., 1926; pvt. sec. to the parl'y, under sec. for dom. affrs. (Mr. Lunn), 1st Oct., 1930; pvt. sec. to perm't. under sec. of state for Dominion affrs., 22nd June, 1931.

COCKS, HUGH CECIL, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; attd. Colombo kach., Jan., 1928; attd. secretariat, May, 1928; office astt., Nuwara Eliya kach., Jan., 1929; office astt. to govt. agt., Uva, May, 1930; asst. sttlm. offr., June, 1930.

COCKSEDGE, THOMAS ABRAHAM BRYAN, M.R.C.V.S.—B.E.F., France, Oct., 1914-Apr., 1919; Army of Occupation, Germany, Apr.-Sept., 1919; Egypt and Palestine, Sept., 1919-Feb., 1921; mentd. in despatches, 1916; vety. offr., Uganda, Feb., 1921; transf'd. Br. Somaliland, Feb., 1924; ch. vety. offr., Apr., 1927.

CODERRE, HON.
