LOUIS, K.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1885; ed. primary schl., St. Ours, Hyacinthe and Montreal Colleges and Legal Univ.; called to the bar, 1892; head of the firm Coderre and Coderre, advocates, Montreal; city attorney, St. Henri, 1896-1908; syndic of the Montreal bar, 1904-1909; unsuccessful cand. for Quebec legislature, 1908, and for H. of C. in same year; elec. to H. of C. for Hochelaga, Sept., 1911; sworn of the P.C. for Canada and sec. of state, Oct., 1912; also min. of mines, 1913; judge, super. ct., Quebec, 1915.

COE, THOMAS PEROWNE, M.C.—B. 1887; ed. Norwich Gram. Schl. and Basenose Coll., Oxford; (schol.) 2nd cls. class. moda., 1908; 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1910; B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; passed cadet, Dec., 1912; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1913; on war serv. with Norfolk Regt., 1915-18; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; demob., Feb., 1919; 2nd asst. sec., Fed. secretariat, Nov., 1919; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec. in add., Nov., 1919 to June, 1920; 2nd-lieut. M.S.V.R., Apr., 1921; ag. capt., Nov., 1921; capt., Aug., 1922; asst. army, offr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; major, M.S.V.R., Jan., 1923; dist. offr., July, 1924; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1927; asst. adviser, Kelantan, Sept., 1928; offr. cls. I B; asst. adviser, Muar, Johore, Feb., 1930; ag. sec. for postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., Mar., 1933.
COGHLAN, B. A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), D.T.M. (Liv.).—B. 1899; I.F.S., A.M.C., 1922-23; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1926.

COHEN, ANDREW BENJAMIN.—B. 1909; ed. Malvern and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls., 1st pts., class. tripes, 1st cls., 2nd pts., class. tripes; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., inland rev., Oct., 1932; transf'd. to C.O., 2nd Oct., 1933.

COHEN, EZCHIEL, M.B.E. (1924).—B. 1882; clk., Cape govt. rly., May, 1900; transf'd. to Imperial Mil. Rly., Nov., 1900; transf'd. to Bech. Prot. service and attached to office of high commr. for S. Africa, Aug., 1901; asst. imp. acctnt., Apr., 1918; prin. clk., Nov., 1923.

COLE, ARNOLD KIRTLAND.—B. 1884; sec. to bd. of educn., Bahamas, 1907; ag. recs. justice, Rum Cay, 1908; ag. inspr. and genl. supt. of schools, 1910, 1911, 1913; 3rd clk., treasry. dept., 1913; 1st clk., P.O., 1914; oh. clk. and asst. examr. offr., cust. dept., Aug., 1921; ag. contrlr., cust. in 1922, 1923, 1924 and 1928; asst. contrlr., and examining offr., Dec., 1923; ag. contrlr. asst., July to Aug., 1924 and Sept. to Nov., 1925; survr., shipping, Mar., 1925; ag. contr. cust. and regstr., shipping from Apr., 1926; ag. harbmr., Mar. to Apr., 1927; contr., cust., Sept., 1928; M.L.C., Dec., 1931.

COLEBATCH, HON. SIR HAL PATESHALL, KT. BACHE. (1927).—B. 1872; ed. Goolwa, S. Australia; M.L.C. for E. Prov., W. Australia, May, 1912; col. sec., min. for educn., min. of pub. health and leader of legis. coun., 1916-19; premier, W. Australia for short period, 1919; min. of educn. and pub. health, 17th May, 1919; agt.-gen. for W. Australia, 1923-27; mem., Australian constitution royal commn., 1927-29; C'wealth senator for W. Australia since 1929.

COLEMAN, CECIL GEORGE.—B. 1882; ed. Guildhall Schl., Bury St. Edmunds and St. Peter's Coll., Peterborough; B. of E. certif.; master, Victoria Inst. Kuala Lumpur, F.M.S., June, 1906; inspr. of schl., Selangor, June, 1920; ag. dittor. Perak, Jan., 1924; ag. prin., King Edward VII schl., Taiping, Mar., 1924; head master, Malacca high schl., June, 1924; inspr., schls., S'pore and Labuan, Dec., 1930.

COLEY, Capt. C. F. (Reg. Army, R. of O.)—B. 1897; ed. Oxford Univ. and Université de Paris; served with Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., Devon Regt., and R. Corps of Signals in European War, 1914-18; diplomatic serv., 1918; cadet, Nigerian adminste. serv., Mar., 1926; ag. asst. dist. offr., Apr., 1926.

COLLENS, ARCHIBALD EDGAR, F.I.C., F.C.S.—Lab. asst., and chem. demstr. to the prof. of chem., Queen's Royal and St. Mary's Colls., Trinidad, 1900-08; lect., agric. chem., govt. training schls., 1902-08; asst. analyst, govt. lab., Trinidad, 1908-16; offr. in charge, rubber tapping experiments, dept. of agr., 1911-17; scientific asst., bd. of agric., 1908-10; science master, Queen's Royal and St. Mary's Colls., Trinidad, 1906-08, 1910, and 1914; ag. prin. asst. anal., govt. lab., 1914-15; supt., field experiments, 1916-16; examr. in pharm. chem., to med. coun., Trinidad, 1908-17; examr. in agr., bd. of educn., 1908-17; govt. chem. and supt. of agr., Leeward Is.; olmtn., granary bd., Antigua; mem., food control prices bd., Antigua, 1917; mem., Barbuda bd., pub. lib. trustees; mem., Fed. exec. and leg. couns. and leg. coun., Antigua; gov. and on exec. coun., Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agr., 1921.
COLLETT, J. W., M.R.C.P., M.R.C.S.—Med. effr., S. Nigeria, 1901; author of "Text Book of Physiology and Hygiene."

COLLIER, C. W. H.—B. 1892; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; cler. asst., immigrn. dept., Br. Guiana, 1909; 5th cls. clk., 1911; 4th ditto, 1914; 3rd ditto, 1915; sec., excess profits comtee., 1916; sec., col. development (immigrn.) comtee., 1918; 2nd cls. offr., 1919; ag. senr. immigrn. agt., 1919; 1st cls. offr., 1920; ag. immigrn. agt., Berbice county, 1920-2; immigrn. agt., 1926; sec., colonization (500 family scheme) comtee., 1927; senr. immigrn. agt. and immigrn. agt., Demerara county, 1928; ag. immigrn. agt. gen., 1929; senr. clk., col. sec.'s office, 1930.

COLLIER, HON. PHILIP.—B. 1873; M.L.A. for Boulder, W. Australia, since 1905; min. for mines and rlys. in Scaddan govt., 1911-14, and of mines and water supply, 1914-16; leader, parly labour party, May, 1917; premier, W. Australia, Apr., 1924 to Apr., 1930.
COLLIER, REGINALD.—B. 1880; stock inspr.; Basutoland, 1910; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1915; inspr., 1924; Zulu Rebellion, 1906; Great War, 1917-19.

COLLINS, CHARLES HENRY, B.A., Lond.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1910; attd. Karunegala kach., Dec., 1910; office asst., Kandy Kachcheri, July, 1913; asst. land settm. offr., May, 1915; asst. land settm. offr., Sept., 1915; addtl. asst. govt. agt., ditto, Sept., 1916; office asst. to govt. agt., W. Prov., Oct., 1916; 2nd asst. col. sec., Oct., 1917; 3rd asst. col. sec., Sept., 1920; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1922; prin. asst. col. sec., Feb., 1924; addnl. asst. col. sec., Oct., 1925; dep. collr., cust., Dec., 1925; ag. dep. ch. sec., Dec., 1932; govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Feb., 1933.

COLLINS, JAMES RICHARD, C.B.E. (1923), C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1869; treasy. clk., Victoria, 1886; acctnt. to Commonwealth of Austr. treasy., 1901; sec. to treasy., 1916; finan. adviser to high commnr., London, and mem., London bd. of advice of Commonwealth Bank of Australia, 1926.

COLLINS, W. H., Ph. D., F.R.S.—1878; ed. Univ. of Toronto, 1904; Univ. Univ., 1907; Chicago Univ., 1909; Wisconsin, 1911; asst. geologist, govt. Can., 1906; jun. geol., 1912; geol., 1914; Royal Soc. of Can., 1918; dir. geol. Can., 1920; ex-off. mem., Can. Inst. mem., Geol. Soc. of America, 1920; numerous papers and memoirs on petro-geol. and petrology.

COLLISSON, PERCIVAL LORIMER (Mil.), 1919.—B. 1883; ed. Warwick King's Coll., London; examr., m. dept., 1901; col. and branch, Sep., examr. of accts., O.R.C., Jan., 1906; col. and branch, Jan., 1906; asst. aux. Nigeria, Sept., 1908; 2nd cls. clk., 1914; mil. audit., E. Africa Exped. Bd. (twice ment. in despatches); senr. asst. auditors L.A., 1919; ag. audr., R.A.F., 1920; aud. W. Pacific, 1922; audr., N. Rhodesia, 1924; audr., Uganda, 1927; audr., Kong, Aug., 1930; J.P., 1932.

COLLYMORE, ERNEST ALLAN, M.I.C. (1933)—B. 1883; ed. Harrison Coll. and Merton Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. clk., schl. of jurisprudence, 1914; barrister, Inner Temple, 1920; serv. in K. Lancs., 1914-19; capt., Oct., 1915; capt., Barham and O.C., cadet corps of the island, 1921; mag., Bridgetown and dist. A., June, 1925; wrecks, Aug., 1925; ag. judge, ass. c. in 1925 and 1926; stip. and circuit mag., and coroner for New Providence, July, 1927; atty.-gen. in 1927 and 1928; atty.-gen., June, 1928.

COMBE, SIR RALPH MOLYNEUX, K.C. (1920).—Ed. at Haileybury Coll. and Keble Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Tem., 1897; Crown advocate, E. Afr., 4th Aug., 1905; mem. of exec. and leg., 1907; atty.-gen., E.A.P., 1912; atty.-gen., 1913; ch. just., Nigeria, 1918; ret. M.A.

COMMISSIONG, TERENCE BERTRAND.—3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Grenada, Apr., 2nd clk., gov.'s office, June, 1915; 2nd sec.'s office, Dec., 1919; ag. clk., col. sec., clk. of couns., med. regis., and nat. printing sub-dept., Feb.-Sept., 1920; sec. active serv., Sept., 1915 to Oct., 1919; meritorious serv. med. in German E. campaign, June, 1916; apptd. J.P. in recog. of war serv., July, 1919; ch. col. sec.'s office, clk. to exec. coun., med. man., govt. print. dept., May, 1921; sec., Grenada, Jan., 1922.
CONNELL, W. K., M.B., Ch.B., F.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), 1893; mily. serv., R.A.M.C., 1916-20; Tanganyika Territory, July, 1924.

CONNOLLY, HON. SIR JAMES DANIEL BACH (1920).—B. 1870; ed. Warwick P. and Catholic Schl.; M.L.C., Western 1902; col. sec. and min. of com. and col. sec. and min. of pub. health, 1910; leg. coun., from 1906 to 1911; M.L.A. in 1914; agt. genl. in London for Western 1917-23; agt. gen. in London for Malta.

CONRADIE, DAVID GIDRON, R.A. (1870); ed. Prince Alfred Hamlet Schls., Stellenbosch and Trinity Coll.; sen. mod. and gold medal, LL.D., 1906; Cape Town and Bloemfontein, 1907-09; dept., O.F.S., 1909-15; atty., Reitz and fontein, 1915-33; mem., prov. and prw. I.P., Bethlehem and Lindley, 1926-33; W. Africa, 1933.

DIE, JOHANNES HENDRIK.—B. 1872; dorp, Robertson, pvt. study, later ch.; schmaster, Christiana, 1889; 90-95 and Grey Coll. Sch., 1896-1900; atty.-at-law, 1905; finan. sec., Dutch Ch., Cape Prov., 1905-12; sup't, ab. col., 1912-1920; organising sec'y, t Party, Cape Prov., 1921-29; mem., for Gordonia, 1924-29; admnstr., Cape t., 1929.
ANT CHARLES FREDERICK, M.R.C.S. R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1888; radiologist, pr., 1924.

LY, WILLIAM KUAN.—B. 1893; ed., Dundee; govt. travelling schl. master, 1913; 2nd clk., C.S.O., Mar., 1916; ag. iv. sec. to govt. of Falkland Is., June, r., 1917 and Sept., 1917 to Apr., 1920; t., G. Coast, Oct., 1920; senr. collr., s., 1928.

EDWARD ROBERT CECIL, M.C., B.A., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1886; ed. Univ. and Univ. Coll. Hosp., London; sn., R.A.M.C., 2nd Sept., 1914; med. Scottish Rifles, France; ag. major, and divnl. anti-malarial offr., 1919; F.M.S.R., Jan., 1922; health offr., Port Swettenham, Mar., 1927; ag. venereal diseases, F.M.S., June, 1928; tr., soo. hygiene, F.M.S., Sept., 1928.
ELEANOR MARGARET, B. 1891; chimota Coll., Gold Coast, May, 1928. S.V.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E.A.P.; dist., offr. Tanganyika Territory.

WILLIAM ERNEST, M.A., F.R.A.S., elade observatory, 1878; B.A. Adel.; ast. astronomer, Adel. obser., 1884; Univ., 1889; govt. astronomer, W.A., t. astronomer, New South Wales, prof. of astronomy at Sydney Univ.

ON, CLAUDE EDWARD, C.M.G. (1932), serve of offs.; barrister-at-law; educa. ng'scholar, 1891-97; served in regular -1910; capt., 1908; with Bedfordshire India, Gibraltar and Bermuda; station Aden, 1907-8; ag. brigade major, 1908; ir. of police, Gold Coast, Sept., 1911; C. to Mr. J. J. Thorburn, C.M.G., Coast, Feb. to May, 1912; comsnr. of Coast, Jan., 1915; ast. col. sec., July, ad to the bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1919; of prisons, Feb., 1920; title changed to sons, Nov., 1926; senr. ast. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Feb., 1926; col. sec., Sept., 1930; gov.'s dep. on various occasions, 1926-32; ag. gov., 1930, 1931 and 1933.

COOLS-LARTIGUE, THEODORE.—B. 1867; ed. at Mount Saint Mary's Coll., Derbyshire; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1889; admitted to the bar, St. Lucia, 1889; ditto, Leeward Is., 1894; ag. regisr., St. Lucia, 1891; ag. mag. in 1903-4-5; ag. regisr., St. Lucia, 1906-7; ag. mag., 1911; elective mem., Castries town bd., 1903-1912; regisr. and prov. marshal, Dominica, and also supplementary mag., Roseau, Dominica, Feb., 1912; offl. mem. leg. coun., Dominica, visiting just. of Dominica prison and J.P., 1912; govt. mem., Roseau town bd., 1914; valuation commr. for assessing of taxes, 1916; ag. crown atty. and mem. exec. coun., 1916-19; crown atty. and regisr., sup. ct., Oct., 1919; ret., 1929; re-apptd. ag. addnl. mag. dist. K, 1930.

COOMARASWAMY, CHINNAPPAH.—B. 1887; ed. Hindu Coll., Jaffna, and Royal Coll., Colombo; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. serv., April, 1910; extra office asst. to govt. agt., Batticaloa, Apr., 1910; pol. mag., Matara, May, 1913; pol. mag. and office asst. to govt. agt., Puttalam, June, 1913; pol. mag., Chilaw, Feb., 1914; pol. mag., Point Pedro, June, 1915; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Feb., 1918; ditto, Chilaw and Puttalam, Mar., 1920; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Chilaw and Puttalam, in addn., Aug., 1920; comsnr. of requests, Colombo, May, 1922; dep. comsnr., stamps, May, 1925; dist. judge, Kalutara, Apr., 1926; dep. comsnr., stamps, May, 1926; regisr., motor cars, Jan., 1928; regisr.-gen., Sept., 1931; dist. judge, Kurunegala, June, 1932.

COOMARASWAMY, VALUPPILLAI.—B. 1892; Cadet, local division, Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1913; attached to Puttalam Kadchcheri, Mar., 1913; Anuradhapura Kach., June, 1913; ag. addnl. office asst., Anuradhapura Kach., Nov., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Puttalam, Feb., 1914; extra office asst. to ast. govt. agent, Puttalam and Chilaw, in addition to his own duties, Mar., 1914; pol. mag., Negombo, July, 1917; ditto, Dandagamuwa, July, 1918; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Feb., 1919; pol. mag., Panadura, Oct., 1920; dist. judge, Kegalla, June, 1924; ast. govt. agt., Hambantota, July, 1927; ast. govt. agt., Puttalam and Chilaw, Jan., 1931; clk., state coun. and sec., bd. of ministers, July, 1932; ag. govt. agt., Batticaloa, June, 1933.

COOMBES, H. P.—B. 1894; elk., govt. sec.'s office, Nyasaland, May, 1917; senr. clk., Aug., 1923; ag. ast. sec. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Dec., 1926 to Mar., 1927; ag. senr. ast. sec. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Apr. to Dec., 1927; ag. office supt., secretariat and clk., exec. and leg. couns., in 1929 and 1930; office supt., secretariat, and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Apr., 1931.

COOPE, ARTHUR EGERTON.—B. 1888; ed. Fonthill Schl., St. Edward's Schl., and Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A. Oxon.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; passed cadet, June, 1913; offr., ols. V, Dec., 1914; ast. dist. offr., May, 1915; offr., ols. IV, Jan., 1919; ast. dist. offr., Apr., 1919; dist. offr., Mar., 1921; offr., ols. III, Nov., 1923; offr. ast. advr., lands, Kedah, May, 1924; town planning admr. and state valuer, Ipoh, Oct., 1928; offr., ols. II., Nov., 1928; ag. ast. dist. offr., Ipoh, in addn., Apr. to May, 1929; ag. mag., Seremban, Sept., 1929; ag. chmn., sany. bd. in addn., ast. adviser, Muar, Johore, Mar., 1930; mag., K. Lumpur, July, 1931; ag. legal advr. and judl. comsnr., Kelantan, Mar., 1933.

COOPER, BERTRAND, M.N.Z.I.S.—B. 1882; mounted infy., S. Africa, 1901-02; licensed survr., N.Z., 1906-07; survr., Pahang, Apr., 1921; asst. supt., rev. surveys, F.M.S., Jan., 1926; asst. supt. (field), Kinta, Aug., 1929; ag. sr. asst. supt., rev. surveys, Kinta, June, 1930 to Feb., 1931.

COOPER, EVANS GUSTAVUS.—B. 1875; asst. clk. of courts, Jamaica, 1893; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1912; clerk of cts., Hanover, Dec., 1912; J.P., 1913; ag. res. mag. on various occasions; supernm. res. mag., June, 1923; res. mag., June, 1924.

COOPER, COMDR. GUY EDWARD, R.N. (ret.).—B. 1891; ed. R.N.; Great War, ment. in desps.; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1923; legal adviser, Seychelles, Apr., 1928; ag. ch. just., May to Nov., 1928 and Nov., 1930 to May, 1932; pol. mag., Gold Coast, Nov., 1932.
COOPER, NELSON GEORGE, M.D., C.M. (Univ. of Manitoba)—B. 1874; health offr., S.S. Nov., 1920.

COOPER, WILLIAM GERALD GROVES, Ph.D. (Lond.), B.Sc. (Lond.), A.R.C.S., F.G.S., A.Inst. M.M.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Schl., Crediton, Devon, Battersea Polytechnic Inst. and Imp. Coll. of Sc. and Technology, 1911-14; res. engnr. on location and constrn. of Canadian Northern Ontario Rly., 1911-14; war serv., 1914-18; geologist, Gold Coast geol. survey, Nov., 1919.
COORAY, EDMUND JOSEPH, B.A., LL.B. (Lond.), Barrister-at-Law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1907; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1931; attd. Colombo kach., Nov., 1931.

COPP, HON. ARTHUR BLISS.—B. 1870; ed. pub. schls., prov. normal schl., Mt. Allison Univ., New Brunswick, Dalhousie Law Schl., Halifax, N.S., Harvard Law Schl. (LL.B. 1894); admitted to bar, N.B. 1895; mem., legis. ass., N.B., 1901-12; el. to H.C., bye-el., Feb., 1915; re-el., g.e., 1917 and 1921; sec. of state of Canada, 29th Dec., 1921; senator, Sept., 1925.

CORBETT, ALAN FREDERICK.—B. 1878; ed. K.H.S., Coventry, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1902; barrister-at-law (Inner Temple), 1903; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Colony, 1904; sup. ct. (T.P.D.) Union of S.A., 1919; clk., res. mag., Worcester, July, 1903; clk., mast. sup. ct., Cape, Jan., 1905; clk., res. mag., Beaufort West, Dec., 1905; clk., civil. commr., Kimberley, May, 1906; clk., inland rev. dept., Apr., 1911; ag. recr. of rev., Germiston, Oct., 1915; senr. clk., inland rev. dept., Nov., 1916; prin. clk., inland rev. dept., Jan., 1921; asst. commr. for inland rev., Apr., 1929; commr. for inland rev., Sept., 1929.

CORBETT, GEORGE HAMLIN, B.Sc., F.E.S.—B. 1887; ed. King Edwards Schl., Bath and Edin. Univ.; govt. entomologist, F.M.S., Apr., 1920; deleg. of F.M.S. and S.S. govs., at 2nd Imp. Entomological Conf., London, June, 1925; consltg. entomologist, inst., med. resch., in addn., June, 1930 and 1931.

CORBIN, EDWIN ARTHUR, M.I.E.R.—B. 1888; asst. elec. engnr., elec. dept., F.M.S., May, 1913; ag. inspr., in addn., Apr.-Dec., 1916; exec. engnr., Nov., 1922; mem., bd. of examiners, Sept., 1923; inspr., Apr., 1925; exec. elec. engnr., Perak, July, 1929; senr. dist. elec. engnr. and inspr., Perak, Jan., 1932.

CORDEAUX, MAJOR SIR HARRY EDWARD SPILLER, K.C.M.G. (1921), C.B. (1904), C.M.G. (1902).—Ed. at Cheltenham Coll., and St. John's Coll., Camb.; minor scholarship, 1889; class. trip, 1892; passed in Hindustani and Persian, 1896; Somali, 1899; B.A., 1892; entd. Indian Army, 1894; probationer, Bombay polit. dept., as 4th asst. polit. res., Aden, Apr., 1896; asst. res., Berbera, July, 1898; vice-consul, Berbera, Oct., 1898; consul, Nov., 1902; admtd. govt. of Somaliland Prot., in 1900, 1902, 1903 and 1904; served in operations against the Somali Mullah 1902-04, as polit. offr. (ment. in desps., medal and 3 clasps); dep. commr., Somaliland Prot., 1904; admtd. govt., June, 1905; commr. and commr-in-chief, 15th May, 1906; gov., Uganda, 1st Feb., 1910; gov., St. Helena, 22nd Nov., 1911; assumed govt., 21st Feb., 1912; gov., Bahamas, 1920.

CORDOAN, JEAN ALBERT.—B. 1887; elk., cust., Durban, 1904; Pretoria, 1911; 1st grade clk., 1916; senr. elk., 1919; prin. elk., 1921; asct., 1922; elk., cust. and excise, Pretoria, 1930; ch. insp., cust. and excise, 1933; dep. commr., 1933.

COREA, CARLTON VICTOR DEMETRIUS SENEWIRATNE.—B. 1903; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; attd., Matawa kach., Mar., 1927; ag. office asst., Koggala kach., Nov., 1928; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Mar., 1930; office asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1931; asst. govt. agt., Badulla, June, 1933.
CORNELL, H.K.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, Feb., 1921.

CORNEY, LEONARD GEORGE.—B.A. Cantab.; class. exhibtnr., St. John's Coll., Camb.; class. trip, 1908; examr., col. audit branch of E. and A. Dept., Feb., 1910; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1910; ag. audr., July, 1913, July to Dec., 1914, and Apl. to Oct., 1916; ag. asst. coll. sec., Dec., 1916 to Jan., 1917; seconded to G. Coast Regt., Apl., 1917 to Jan., 1918; senr. asst. audr., Jan., 1919; ag. audr., Jan. to Oct., 1919; dep. audr., Jan., 1920; asst. dir. cent. office (Col. Audit Dept.), Feb., 1930; dep. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1931.

CORNWALLIS, COL. SIR K., K.B.K. (1926), C.M.G. (1926), C.B.E., Mily. (1919), D.S.O. (1917).—Commander of the Order of King George I. of Greece, Order of Nahdhab (2nd cls.), Order of the Nile (3rd cls.), Order of Majidie (4th cls.), three times mentioned in desps.—Sudan civ. serv., 1906-14; Egyptian civ. serv., 1914; dir. Arab Bureau, Cairo, 1916-19; dep. ch. polit. offr., Egyptian Expedy. Force, 1918; asst. ch. polit. offr., ditto, 1919; attd. to Amir Faisal in London, 1919; attd. to P.O., 1920-21; accompanied Amir Faisal to Iraq and seconded to Iraq govt. for two years, June, 1921; adviser to min. of interior, Iraq, September, 1921.

CORRIE, OWEN CECIL KIRKPATRICK, M.C.—B. 1882; ed. Monckton Combe Schl. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (maths. exhibtr.); on active serv. in France, Belgium and Palestine with N. Somerset Yeomanry and R.F.A. (T.F.), 1915-19; M.C. and ment. in desps.; judl. offr., Nablus, O.E.T.A. (S.), Mar., 1919; vice-pres., ct. of appeal, O.K.T.A. (S.), Oct., 1919; vice-pres., ct. of appeal, Palestine, July, 1920; senr. Br. judl. sup. ct., Sept., 1924; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1922 to Jan., 1923 and from Oct., 1924 to Jan., 1925.

CORSER, LIEUT. E. E.—Ed. Shrewsbury; 2nd lieut., 4th Batt. King's Shropshire Light Infantry (T.), Aug., 1914; lieut., June, 1916; served in France, 1917-18; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 3rd Dec., 1919.
CORSON, J.F., M.B.E., M.D., Ch.B. (Manch.), D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Cantab.).—B. 1878; W.A.M.S. (Gold Coast), Jan., 1913; pathologist, Dec., 1920; Nigeria, May, 1923; Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

CORY, Sir George Edward.—B. 1862; ed. at St. John's Coll., Hurstpierpoint, Mechanics Inst., Woolwich Arsenal and King's Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (nat. sci. tripes), 1889; M.A., 1891; D. Litt., 1921; demonstr. of chem., Univ. of Cambridge, 1888; vice-prin., pub. schl., Grahamstown, 1891; lecturer in physics and chem., St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown, 1894; prof., chem., Rhodes Univ. Coll., Grahamstown, 1904; hon. archivist to Union Govt., 1926; author "Rise of South Africa."

COSGRAVE, Alexander Kirkpatrick, M.C.—B. 1885; ed. Shrewsbury and Trim. Coll., Dublin; B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., 1909, D.P.H., 1917; L.M., Rot. Hosp., 1920; grad. with dist., L.S.T.M., 1920; D.T.M. & H. Lon., 1920; col. med. serv., 1910; med. offr., Ulu Selangor, 1911; on active serv., 1914-19; B.E.F. with 34th div., 1916; D.A.D.M.S., 16th (Irish) div., 1917-18; demob. with hon. rank of maj.; med. offr., Klang, ag. H.O., Coast and in charge, quarantine camp, Port Swettenham, 1920; H.O., Negri Sembilan, 1921; sen. med. offr., Selangor, 1922; S.M.O., Selangor, Jan., 1926; offr. mem., advisory coun. to L. of N. eastern health bureau, Singapore, Jan., 1927; senr. health offr., Selangor and Pahang, and ag. ch. health offr., F.M.S. in addn., Feb. to Oct., 1927 and Nov., 1927 to Mar., 1928; ag. ch. health offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1928; major, M.S.V.R., Feb., 1930.

COSSER, S. C. A.—B. 1880; ed. Tenbridge; clk., treas., Transvaal, 8th May, 1901; asst. acctnt., treas., 1st Dec., 1905; asst. acctnt., treas., Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1910; provincial acctnt., Transvaal Prov., 21st Nov., 1910; served in European War, 1915-19 (Gen. Serv., Victory and Mil. Med.)

COSTAR, Norman Edgar.—B. 1909; ed. Battersea Grammar Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; hist. schol., 1928-32; ols. I divn., hist. tripes, pts. I and II; B.A., 1931; Kellen prize-man, 1931; apptd. after compet. exam., O.C., Apr., 1932.

COTCHING, Houghton Aldgate, A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1887; serv. in mech. dept., N.E. Ry. Coy., 1903-15; on mil. serv., 1915; seconded for duty with R.E. (inland waterways and docks), 1917; major, D.A.D.R.T. and sent to Chemin de Fer du Hejiaz as loco. supt., 1919; ch. mech. engr., Palestine rly., 1920.

COTTON, Maurice John, O.B.E.—B. 1890; ed. Bromsgrove Schl.; asst. land ranger, Kenya, 1913; asst. land offr., 1921; on mily. serv., 1914-18; office succ., survey dept., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; finan. asst., prov. adminstr., 1930.

COULSON, Norman.—B. 1891; ed. Carterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; (M.A., hone. in cls. and mod. hist.); 2nd lieut., 10th Res. Regt., Cav., Sept., 1914; attd. 8th (K.R.I.) Hussars, Sept., 1915; (France and army of occupation); cadet F.M.S., Apr., 1920; passed cadet, June, 1922; attd. to treas., Penang on sp. duty, Sept., 1922; dist. offr., Dindings, Apr., 1923; dist. offr., Raub, Feb., 1925; seconded for serv. in Kelantan, Aug., 1926; offr., cls. III., Feb., 1928, supt., lands, Kelantan, Apr., 1928; mag., Seremban, Mar., 1930; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, June, 1931; offr., cls. II, Feb., 1933.

COURT, Philip Francis Shirley, M.R.C.S (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1900; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong; med. offr. in ch., Kowloon and New Territories, Oct., 1930; ditto, Victoria Hosp., Mar., 1932; asst. med. offr., Victoria Hosp., May, 1932; med. offr. in ch., Kowloon Hosp., Mar., 1933.

COUTTS, James Magnus.—B. 1896; ed. Heriot's Schl., Schol. of Medicine and Univ., Edinburgh; L.D.S., R.C.S.E., 1924; served great war with Cameron Highlanders, 1915-19; M.M., 1916; dental surg., Falkland Is., Oct., 1925; ag. loc. audr., Apr., 1926-Apr., 1927; lieut., Falkland Is. Def. Force, 1927; capt., 1929; ag. O.C., May to Nov., 1928; 2nd in comm., 1927-31; dental offr., Straits Settlements, Aug., 1931.
COWAN, John Archibald, M.B., B.S. (Lond.).—B. 1890; bacteriologist, S. Sttlmnts., Apr., 1925; pathologist, Aug., 1926.

COWAP, John Chester, B.Sc. (Vict.), F.I.C.—B. 1884; chem. asst., govt. lab'y., London, Sept., 1904; asst. govt. analyst, Singapore, Apr., 1909; dep. govt. analyst, Penang, Oct., 1909; govt. analyst, S.S., June, 1924; ditto and asst. supt., govt. monopolies, Singapore, Jan., 1929.

COWELL, Hubert Russell, B.A., C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1877; ed. Malvern and Cambridge; scholar of Clare, 1896-9; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., sec.'s dept., inland revenue, Oct., 1901; 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 27th Mar., 1902; sec. to inter-deptmtl. comtee. on oil fields of W. Indies, 1904; sec. to Roy. Coman. on trade relations between Canada and W. Indies, 1909-1910; priv. sec. to Sir J. Anderson, G.C.M.G., 26th June, 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Emmott, 9th Nov., 1912; priv. sec. to Lord Ilington, 8th Aug., 1914; ag. 1st cls. clk., 12th Oct., 1914; 1st cls. clk., 10th Mar., 1916; ag. prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920.

COWGILL, John Vincent.—B. 1888; ed. Durham Schl. and All Souls Coll., Oxford (bible-clerk, 1907-11), 3rd cls. mods., 3rd cls. lit., hum., B.A., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; studied Chinese, 1912-14; on mily. serv., 1915-19; M.C., 1917; ment. in despa., 1919; dist. offr., Dec., 1919; asst. advr., Trengganu, Nov., 1923; town planning admstr. and state valuer, Ipoh, June, 1926; collr., land rev., K. Lumpur and regisr., titles, Selangor, Nov., 1927; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928; ag. dist. offr., Kinta and dist. supt., prisons, July, 1930; mem., Perak coun. of state, Jan., 1932; ag. sec. to res., Perak, Mar., 1932.

COWPER, Herbert Wellesley.—B. 1883; ed. at St. Paul's School, London, Hertford Coll.; Oxford, and in Germany, France and Italy, asst. res., N. Nigeria, Nov., 1910; ag. res., 1916; 2nd cls. dist. offr., Dec., 1918.

COX, Arthur Henry.—B. 1888; ed. Felsted and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1910; asst. dist. comanr., Uganda, Nov., 1911; A.D.C. to gov., 1918-19; 1st gr. admstrive. offr., Jan., 1922; dep. prov. comanr., Dec., 1928; ag. prov. comanr., July, 1929 and June, 1932; asst. ch. sec., Uganda, Mar., 1930; prov. comanr., Dec., 1932.

COX, Charles Edward Langton.—B. 1889; entd. civ. serv., B. Guiana, 1907; 2nd cls. clk., col. sec.'s office; called to the bar, 1918; stip. mag. for the colony; revising barrister, 1919; served in France as lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.); injured at Loos, Sept., 1916; invalided out of army, Nov., 1917; served under Admiralty as A.T.S.O., mining depot, Grangemouth, Mar. to Dec., 1918; extra A.D.C. to Sir W. Egerton, K.C.M.G., 1913-15; lieut. artillery corp., B. Guiana Militia, 1908; capt., 1919; pol. mag., St. Vincent, 1920-23; ag. attv.-gen. and ch. just., St. Vincent; crown atty. and regiar., sup. ct., Dominica, 1923-25; mem., exec. coun., income tax comanr., offr. recr., visiting just., prison, Dominica; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1925; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., July, Oct. and Nov., 1929, and June-Oct., 1932.

COX, Lieut.-Col., C. H. F., C.M.G. (1927), D.S.O. (1917).—Ed. Rugby and R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich; comand, R.H. and R.F.A., 6th Jan., 1900; served S. African War; served in India, 1905-06; seconded Sudan civ. serv., 1913-15; served in Great War, 1915-18; four times ment. in desps., D.S.O., 1917, bar, 1918; brevet lieut.-col., 1st Jan., 1919; asst. admistr. offr., Palestine, Apr., 1919; mil. gov., Nablus, Mar., 1920; ditto, Galilee, Aug., 1920; dist. gov., Samaria, July, 1922; retd. from Army with rank of lieut.-col., Jan., 1923; ch. Br. res. Trans-Jordan, Apr., 1924.

COX, Herbert Charles Fabie.—Br. Guiana pol., 1913-19; called to bar, 1915; on war serv., France, 1917-19; asst. to atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1919; ag. mag. on various occasions; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1925; M.L.C., 1926; ag. ch. just., May-Sept., 1926, Mar.-Oct., 1927 and from June to Sept., 1929; chmn., bd. of health, 1926-27; chmn., water and sewage bd., 1928-29; dep. gov., Mar., 1929; dep. admistr., June and July, 1929; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, Dec. 1929; solr.-gen., Nigeria, 1933.

COX, Major-Gen. Sir Percy Zachariah, G.C.M.G. (1922), G.C.I.E. (1917), K.C.S.I. (1915), K.C.M.G. (1920), K.C.I.E. (1911), C.S.I. (1909), C.I.E. (1902), F.R.G.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1864; ed. Harrow and Sandhurst; joined army 1884; served with 2nd batt. Cameronians till 1889; joined Indian Staff Corps, 1889; Indian polit. dep., 1890; vice-consul, Zaila, Somali Coast, 1893; Berbera, 1894-5; consul and polit. agt., Muscat, 1899-1904; consul-gen., Bushire, 1904; polit. res., Persian Gulf, 1909; sec., foreign dept. gov't., India, 1914; ch. polit. offr., Indian Expedy. Force "D," 1914; ment. in desps., 1916; ag. British min. to Persia, 1919-20; high commr., Mesopotamia, 1920, ret., 1923.

COX, Wilfred Bernard.—B. 1895; ed. Denstone Coll.; served in European War 1914-18 (ment. in desps.); col. audit dept., May, 1920; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1920; ag. senr. asst. audr. on various occasions since 1924; ag. dep. audr., Mar. to Aug., 1930; senr. asst. audr., Sept., 1931; audr., Fiji, June, 1933.

CRABB, Richard Bruce—B.1891; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; asst dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1915; served European War, East Surrey Regt., 1916-18; dist. commr., 1922; asst. col. sec., 1923; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1926; ag. clk., couns. in 1927 and 1928; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., Apr. to Sept., 1932; senr. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1932.
CRAGG, William Gilliat, D.S.O. (1918), Major, R. of O. Loyal Regt.—B. 1883; ed. Shrewsbury and R.M.C., Sandhurst; asst. dist. offr., N. Nigeria, 25th Aug., 1920.
CRAIG, Ernest Duncan, M.A., B.Sc., F.Z.S.—B. 1888; ed. Aberdeen Univ. and at Exeter Coll., Oxford; aspt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, Jan., 1920.
CRAIG, George, C.M.G. (1928), LL.D. (N.Z.)—B. 1873; ed. Otago Boys' High Schol. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. cust. dept., 1891; ch. elk., 1910; asst. comptr., 1913; comptr., 1923.

CRAIG, John.—B. 1884; ed. at Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., 1st cl. hona. Classics; univ. prize in Latin, 1906; asst. at Christ Church, Oxford; B.A., 2nd cl. hona., lit. hum., 1909; after examn., examr., E. and A. dept., Jan., 1909; asst. auditor, S. Nigeria, May, 1909; temporarily transf'd to N. Nigeria, Aug., 1909; joined col. audit dept., Jan., 1910; audr., Keduras, Nov., 1912; priv. sec. to gen. Collett, K.C.M.G., Nov., 1914; priv. to A.D.C. to gen. gov. R. Walter, C.M.G.; Hart-Bennett, C.M.G., and gov. Ewe-C.M.G., 1915-19; lieut., B. Hausa's Terr. Force; audr., Mauritius, Apr., 1920; asst. dept., 1st Apr., 1924; dep. audr., Imp. Territory, Dec., 1926; ag. audr., India, 1927; treas., Fiji, Apr., 1930.

CRAIG, Major John Conklin (1918), M.R.I.C.—Res. eng., P.W.D., Settlements, 1905-13; asst. gen. P.W.D., Vancouver, Br. Columbia, 1914; lieut., Can. Inf., 1914; seconded to 1st Can. Pioneer force, France, Oct., 1915; promoted major, 1st ch. engrnr. and 2nd in command, 9th Can. Troops, Dec., 1916; asst. dir. ordn., agt. G.H.Q., Franco, Sept., 1918; demob., Apr., 1919; R. of O., desps. 3 times; ag. ch., soundings and borings, Vancouver, 1919; asst. ch. engrnr., Gold Coast harb., ch. engrnr., 1924; D.P.W., Br. Guiana mem., exec. and leg. couns.

CRAIG, Rupert Beckmann, A.M.I.C.E., M.R.San.I.—B. 1897; apprentice, P.W.D., Guiana, 1913; served with H.M. Forestry dept. in desps.; ag. asst. engrnr. and ch. engrnr., Oct., 1921-July, 1922; asst. drags., P.W.D., Jan., 1924; seconded to carry Georgetown improvement schemes, Jan., 1925; seconded to harbour, bd., as exec., Berbice River training wks., Jan. to Oct., 1929; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., 1926; dist. engrnr., 1929.

CRAIG-McFEELY, J. J.—B. 1878; Catholic Univ. Schl. (Dublin), St. Xavier's (Liverpool) and Stonyhurst Univ. of Liverpool; barrister-at-law, Temple, Jan., 1923; on mil'y. serv. Aug. to Dec., 1919; 2nd lieut., Kent Crys. (T.F.), Aug., 1915; seconded for div. M.G.C., Nov., 1916 to Dec., 1919; (ag. capt.), B.K.F., Mar. to Oct., 1917; asst. offr. and mag., Nigeria (S. Prov.), asst. res. and mag., Nyasaland Prot., July to July, 1926; dep. land offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1926; dep. regir., July, 1928; ag. land offr., and regir., title, May, 1928 and May-Dec., 1931; ag. regir.-gen., May-Dec., 1931.

CRAIGIE-HALKETT, Montagu C. (1934).—Ag. priv. sec. to gov. Trinidad Isl., 1897; writer, col. sec.'s off., Apr., 1897; priv. admistr., Apr., 1897; clk., col. sec.'s off., 1897; treas., clk., 1899; J.P., Nov., postmr., man., savings bank, regir.-gen., regir. of sup. ct., and offl. admistr., temp. mem., leg. coun., 1911 and 1914 census, 1911 and 1921; local audr., regir.-gen. and mag., 1926; temp. mem., coun., 1923; treas. and colls., 1926; as col. postmr. on various occasions; as gov., Jan. and Dec., 1932 and May, 1933; col. sec., Feb. to Mar., 1932; ag. prov. gen. CRANSTON, T. D.—M.A. (Glasgow), master, training instit., for teachers, Accra Coast, Apr., 1912; asst. censor, in add., 1915 to Jan., 1916; ag. qtrmr., G.C. Volunteers, Nov., 1916; lieut., ditto, ag. prin., Accra Training Coll. on several occasions between 1917 and 1923; ag. prov. imp., Apr., 1926; asst. dir., educn., Nov., 1926; dir., educn., in 1930, 1932 and 1933.

CRARKY, WILLIAM RAYMOND DUNHAN, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1900; ed. Gresham's Sch., Holt and London Univ.; asst. engr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, Apr., 1926; asst. engr. in charge, P.W.D., Pemba, Aug., 1927; ag. dist. engr., Mar.-Sept., 1930 and June-Dec., 1931.

CRAWFORD, JOHN GERALD.—B. 1894; ed. King's Sch., Chester and Merton Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1920; lieut., Cheshire regt., T.F., Nov., 1915; capt., June, 1916; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; attd. to dist. office, K. Selangor, Apr., 1921; attd. to office of conserv., forests, Jan., 1922; passed cadet, Feb., 1923; supernumy. offr., cls. V, Feb., 1922; asst. dist. offr., B. Padang, Nov., 1923; ditto, Kroh, May, 1924; offr., cls. IV, Feb., 1926; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, June, 1927; attd. to co-op. socs. dept., Oct., 1931; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1931.

CREAN, B. A.—Barrister-at-law; dep. regar. of titles, Kenya, Oct., 1920; res. mag., Apr., 1921; puisane judge, sup. ct., Cyprus, Apr., 1930; ag. pres., dist. ct., June to Sept., 1930; ag. ch. just., Dec., 1930 and June-Oct., 1932.

CREASY, GERALD HALLEN.—B. 1897; ed. Rugby; schol., King's Coll., Camb., 1915; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Nov., 1916; lieut., May, 1918; capt. and adjutant, Nov., 1918; apptd. under reconstruction scheme asst. prin., C.O., 26th July, 1920; pvt. sec. to parl. under sec. for dominion affrs., 6th Aug., 1925; dep. sec., C.O. confee., 1927; prin. (agril.), 17th Sept., 1928.
CREED, HENRY.—B. 1871; apptd., after exam., 3rd class messenger in C.O., July, 1900; 2nd class, 1st Feb., 1904; King's mess., 30th Jan., 1911; office keeper, 1924; med., Order of Br. Empire, 1931.

CREMER, ARTHUR STANLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Durham sch.; col. audit dept., 23rd Jan., 1911; asst. audr., G. Coast, 1911-13; asst. audr., Nyasaland, 1913-16; asst. audr., Uganda, 1916-18; asst. audr., provisional admstn., G.E.A., 1918-19; senr. asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, 1st Oct., 1919; audr., Mauritius, July, 1924; audr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1933.

CREMER, JOHN AWDRY.—B. 1881; B.A. (Oxon.), 1903; clk., col. audit branch, E. and A.D., 1903; asst. auditor, Lagos, 1905; asst. auditor, Nyasaland, 1908; 2nd cls. clk., cent. office, 1913; auditor, Nyasaland, 1914; audr., Cyprus, Aug., 1926.

CRESSALL, PAUL EWART FRANCIS, M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Cranleigh Sch.; ent. civ. serv., B. Guiana, 1911; cler. asst., secretariat and subs. inspr., B. Guiana pol., 1912; ag. dist. inspr., 1914-15; 2nd lieut., B. Guiana Militia Infv., 1914; served in B.W.I. Regt. as capt., E. Africa and Palestine, 1915-19; dist. inspr., B. Guiana pol., 1920; J.P. and coroner, 1920; ag. county inspr., 1920; passed final law exam., 1921; capt., B. Guiana local forces, 1922; ag. staff offr., B. Guiana local forces, 1922; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1923; county inspr., pol., 1923; comanr., for oaths and revising barrister, 1924; stip. mag., Apr., 1924; ag. stip. mag., Georgetown, Aug., 1924 to Dec., 1924 and July to Dec., 1926; chmn., comm. of enquiry into Onderneeming indus., schl., Sept., 1925; ag. chmn., soldiers' pensions bd., 1926; chmn., ditto, Nov., 1926; senr. stip. mag., Nov., 1930; senr. Br. mag., Palestine, June, 1931; ag. pres., Nablus dist. ct., May, 1933; do., Jerusalem dist. ct., June, 1933; chmn., legal bd., June, 1933.

CRESWELL, COL. THE HON. FREDERIC HUGH PAGE, D.S.O.—B. 1866; ed. Bruce Castle, Derby Sch., and R. Sch. of Mines (grad. 1st cls.); arrived in S. Africa, May, 1893; mining in Rhodesia, 1893-94; man., Durban Roodepoort Deep Mine, 1894-99; served in Imp. Light Horse during Anglo-Boer war; man., Village Main Reef, 1902-03; supported agitation for expulsion of Chinese labour; elec. to first Union parlt., 1910, and led Labour Party; 2nd in comm., Rand Rifles, S. W. Africa, 1914; 2nd in comm., subsequently commdg., 8th S.A. Infy. Brig., German E. Africa campaign; mem. for Denver; min. of defence and labour, 1st and 2nd Hertzog miny., June, 1924 to Mar., 1933; M.P. for Bellville, May, 1933.

CRESWELL, REAR-ADMIRAL SIR WILLIAM ROOK, K.C.M.G. (1911), K.B.E. (1919), C.M.G. (1897).—Joined R.N., Dec., 1865; lieut., 1873, for service in action at Laroot River (wounded) engaged in suppression of slave trade on African coast, 1875-7; ret. from Navy, 1878; apptd. to S. Australian naval force, 1885; comdr., 1891; post. capt., 1896; naval comdt., 1893-1900; commanded H.M.C.S. "Protector" to China during Boxer outbreak, 1900; naval comdt. of Queensland, 1900-4; dir. of commonwealth naval forces, 1904; attended Imp. Conf. on naval and mil. defence, 1909; rear-admiral, 1911; retired, 1919.

CREWE, 1ST MARQUESS OF (cr. 1911); ROBERT OFFLEY ASHBURTON CREWE-MILNE, K.G., P.C., M.A., F.S.A.—B. 1858; Baron Houghton of Great Houghton in the county of York, 1863; Earl of Crewe (cr. 1895); Earl of Madeley (cr. 1911); ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; asst. priv. sec. to sec. for foreign affairs (Earl Granville), 1883-84; lord-in-waiting to Queen Victoria, 1886; lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-96; lord pres. of the coun., 1905-08; S. of S. for the Colonies, Apr., 1908; Lord Privy Seal (in conjunction with Secretaryship of State), 1908; S. of S. for India, 1910-15; accompanied H.M. King George V. on the occasion of the Royal visit to India, 1911; lord pres. of the coun., 1915.
CRICHLLOW, NATHANIEL.—M.B., Ch.B.; med. offr., Solomon Islands, Oct., 1914; ag. senr. med. offr. on several occasions; travelling med. offr., July, 1927.

CRITIEN, ATTILIO, O.B.E. (1927), M.B.E. (1919), M.A., M.D., Malta Univ., D.P.H., D.T.M. (Liverpool), 1906.—B. 1872; demonstr., schl. of anatomy, 1899 to 1901; civil surgeon attchd. R.A.M.C., 1901 to 1904; med. offr. of hth., Malta, 1906 to 1917; chf. govt. med. offr. and aspt. of pub. hth., with seat in exec. coun. and coun. of govt., 1917-21.

CROCKER, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1890; served in R.A.S.C. (M.T.) in Mesopotamia, Persia and India; asst. govt. printer, Gold Coast, 1921; ag. govt. printer, 1925, 1927, 1928, 1930 and 1932; aspt., govt. press, 1929.

CROFTON, RICHARD HAYES.—B. 1880; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. class. tripes, 1901; fellow, Royal Economic Soc.; assoc., Town Planning Inst.; clk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., July, 1901; clk., audit dept., Hong Kong, Aug., 1903; ag. local auditor, Feb. to Dec., 1904; ch. clk., col. sec., off., Mar., 1905; ag. asst. col. sec., and clk. of coun., for periods in 1907, 1911, 1912, 1913; asst. chief sec., Zanzibar, Feb., 1914; ag. ch. sec. for various periods, 1914-18, 1922-24 and 1926-28; ag. sec., admtn., Tanganyika Terr., Dec., 1919 to Mar., 1920; ag. treas., Zanzibar, for various periods, 1920-22; ch. sec., Jan., 1928; ag. Br. res., in 1928, 1929, 1930 and 1931; mem., educn. coman., 1920; chmn., coman. on agr., 1923; chmn., museum comitee.; publications "Statistics of the Zanzibar Prot." (annual), "Adventures in Administration."

CRONEEN, BRIAN.—Ed. Dulwich Coll.; served B.S.A. pol. and Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1911; 2nd grade elk., 1921; ag. sub-instr., 1922; ag. ast. res. mag., 1926; passed 2nd grade Seeawana exam., 1923; ag. res. mag., 1927; 1st grade elk., Apr., 1928; sub-instr., Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1929.

CROSBIE, HON. SIR JOHN C., K.B.E. (1919).—M.H.A., Bay de Verde dist., Newfoundland, 1908; M.E.C., 1909; mem. defence comte., 1915; chmn., tonnage comte., 1917; min. of militia, 1917; min. of shipping, 1918.

CROSBIE, ROBERT EDWARD HAROLD, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1886; ed. Cheltenham and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (schol.); B.A. (class. tripus, hon. cl. II), 1908; Egyptian civil serv., 1910-19; seconded for mily. serv. with E.R.F., 1915; apptd. to O.E.T.A. (S.), 1918; dep. mily. gov., Brisban, 1919; dep. mily. gov., Tiberias, 1920; dep. dist. gov., Jenin, 1920; ast. gov. (continuing as ast. dist. comanr.), S. dist., 1922; passed advanced Arabic exam., 1924; dist. comanr., Southern dist., 1931.

CROSS, GRAHAM.—C.I.C., native ct., Johannesburg, May, 1902; ast. res. mag. and ast. mag., native ct., Witwatersrand dist., Aug., 1908; mag., Nov., 1915; under sec. for lands, Sept., 1923; sec. for lands, Aug., 1932.

CROSS, MATTHEW ARNOLD.—B. 1881; elk., deeds, Transvaal, Mar., 1902; examr., deeds, July, 1904; 2nd grade examr., deeds, Pretoria, Oct., 1919; 1st gr. ditto, Nov., 1919; ch. examr., July, 1922; ast. regis., deeds, Bloemfontein, Nov., 1927; ditto, Pretoria, Oct., 1928; regis., deeds, Bloemfontein, Apr., 1933.

CROSSE-CROSSE, WILLIAM WICKHAM REDDEN.—B. 1899; ed. Blundells schl.; 2nd lient., Welsh Guards, 1918; lt., 1920; served with 1st and 2nd K.A.R., in Tanganyika Territory, 1922-24; R. of O., (Welsh Guards), 1924; cadet, Uganda Prot., 1924; ast. dist. offr., 1926; ag. lab. comanr., 1929; temp. ast. prin., C.O., 1930.

CRUICKSHANK, ARTHUR HOWARD, M.I.MAR.—B. 1884; jnr. mech. engr. and river surv., P.W.D., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. mech. engr., 1921; transf. as engrnr., transport offr. to motor transport dept., Aug., 1923; ag. ch. mech. transport offr., 1924; ag. ch. transport offr. for periods 1926, 1928, 1929 and 1930.

CRUICKSHANK, JOHN MERRILL, M.D., C.M., McGill Univ., Canada, 1925, Licentiate Med. Coun. of Canada, 1925.—Med. dir., Alexander sanatorium (mental diseases) San Francisco, 1927-28; med. offr., Bahamas, 1928; res. surg., 1929; ag. dir., med. services in 1930; ch. med. offr. and res. surg., 1931.

CRUTCHLEY, ERNEST TRISTRAM, C.M.G. (1932).—C.B.E. (civ.), 1926, O.B.E. (R.), 1918—B. 1878; lient.-col., R.E., T.F. Res.; ast. survr., G.P.O., 1908; dep. dir., Army Postal serv. (T.F.), 1914-19; staff offr., grade I, miny. of transport, 1919; ag. prin., ch. sec.'s office, Dublin, 1921; Irish office and mem., Irish grants comte., 1922; C.O., 1923; ag. finance offr., overseas settlement dept., 1925; Br. govt. rep. for migr. in the C'wealth. of Australia, 1928; rep. in the C'wealth. of Australia of H.M's. Govt. in the U.K., 1931.

CUBITT, MAJOR-GEN. SIR THOMAS K.O.B. (1931), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1908); (1903)—B. 1871; ed. Haileybury and Ll Woolwich; ent. army, 1891; capt., 1891; W. Africa, 1893 (med. and clasp) staff ch. Africa (despa., brevet-major, med. vid., 1901; W. Africa, (despa., clasp), 1922; sokoto campaign, 1903 (despa., clasp); European war in commd., troops, Sana 1914-15; in commd., divn., France, vid. of major-gen., 1918; G.O.C. Presid. Assam dist., 1920-24; 53rd Welsh Div. Shrewsbury, 1927; G.O.C., 2nd Div. gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Bermuda, 1931.

CUFF, CYRIL CHARLES HERBERT, M.L. (Durham), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Edin.).—B. 1888; ed. Gov. Durham; lient., R.N.V.R., 1914; pass. of Trade exam. for master, 1916; med. R.A.M.C., 1918; house surg. and instr. St. Lucia, 1921-23; resigned; re-emt. and apptd. to Seychelles as ast. med. ast. res. surg. and visiting med., outlying nat. ag. ch. med. offr., June to Nov., 1929; med. offr., Cyprus, Apr., 1929; surg. med. Dec., 1929; ag. dir. health on various in 1928 and 1932-33.
CULLEN, JAMES REYNOLDS, M.A. (civ.) B. 1901; ed. Tonbridge and Balliol Coll. in Hertford school, 1919; 1st ast. med. 2nd lient. hum., 1923; dir., educ., Cyprus, 1930.

CULLEN, HON. SIR WM. PORTER, K.C. (1912), KT. BACHE, (1911), K.C., M.A. Barrister-at-law; M.L.A., New South July, 1891; M.L.C., Aug., 1895; fellow of Sydney Univ. since 1896; vice-chancellor resigned from legis. coun. and apptd. as of N.S.W., Jan., 1910; lient. gov., N.S.W., 1910; ret., 1925; chancellor of S. Univ. from Sept., 1914.
CUMARASWAMY, SURRAMANIAN.—B. 1894; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1899; Badulla kach, Feb., 1929; attd. Bateman Jan., 1930; office ast. to ast. gov. till Mar., 1930.

CUNLIFFE-LISTER, RT. HON. SIR G.B.E. (1929), K.B.E. (1920), P.C. (1927), M.P. (Hendon), —B.1884; ed. Winchester Univ. Coll., Oxford; called to bar, 1891; in army, 1914-17; joint sec. of Min. of Serv., 1917-18; chmn., perm. lient. com. War Cabinet war priorities comte., 1918; select comte. on ast. expend. and select on high prices and profits, 1919; ast. B. of T., 1920-21; sec., D.O.T., 1921-22; Imp. Economic Confere., 1923; pres. R. 1923-23, 1924-29 and Aug.-Nov., 1931; for the Cols., 9th Nov., 1931.

CURLEWIS, JOHN STEPHEN, B.A. (Cape).—Cape civ. serv., 1883-1888; sup. ct., Cape Colony, 12th Oct., 1887; high ct. (late S.A.R.), May, 1888; ast. Imp. gov't. in asth. with late S.A.R. Asiatic question, 1896; ag. judge (late S.A.R.), Apr., 1899 to Jan., 1901; crim. ct. for Witwatersrand, Nov., Jan., 1900; puisne judge, sup. ct., Natal, Sept., 1903; puisne judge, ast. Africa (Transvaal Prov. Div.), ag. judge pres., July, 1924; judge of supreme O.A.G., Union of S. Africa during Lord Clarendon, 1933.
CURMI, CAPT. HENRY C.—B. 1900 Malta Lyceum, Univ. and priv. tutor.

CUTTERIDGE, JOSEPH OLIVER, M.B.E., (1932), A.C.F., F.R.G.S.—B. 1887; ed. Borough Road Coll., Isleworth, London, 1906-08; teacher's certif. (with 4 distinctions) Bd. of Educn., Lond.; A.C.T. cert., Bd. of Educn., Lond.; staff-capt., H.M. Forces; inspr. Q.M.G. services; maths. and drawing master, Junr. Tech. Schol., Liverpool, 1919; prin., govt. training coll., Trinidad, Jan., 1921; mem., Inter.-Col. Educn. Conf., 1921; mem., bd. indust. training; supt., tech. classes, 1922; ag. senr. inspr. schls., Feb., 1923; senr. inspr. schls., Trinidad, 1st July, 1923; ag. dir., educn., July, 1925 to Mar., 1926; asst. dir., educn. and senr. inspr., schls., June, 1926; asst. dir., educn. and ch. inspr., schla., Jan., 1928; ag. dir., educn., May to Nov., 1929; dir., educn., 1934; author "West Indian Readers" etc.

CUZEN, ALLAN LEEKIE.—Served in R.E., 1901-2; S. African War (medal and five clasps); Cape police, 1902-6; Bech. Prot. police, 1906-14; passed Cape civ. serv. lower law exam., 1914; 2nd clk. to asst. comsrr., N. Dist., Bech. Prot., 1914; ag. asst. res. mag., ditto, 1915; passed 2nd grade Seawana exam., 1916; 1st grade (B.), ditto, 1927; ag. res. mag., 1917; ag. ass't. res. mag., 1919; res. mag., 1921.

DABNER, CHARLES J.—B. 1876; on mily. serv., France and Flanders, Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1919; clk., Army pay office, R. Engnrs., Mar. to Oct., 1919; clk., Army pay office, command paymr., Overseas Bases, Oct., 1919 to July, 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 16th July, 1922.

DAKERS, COLIN HUGH, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Manch. Gram. Schl.; war serv., 1915-19; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; offr., cls. V., Nov., 1923; inspr., liquor shops, F.M.S., Sept., 1925; 2nd asst. dir., educn. (Chinese), S.S., Apr., 1926; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1927; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1932; asst. dir., educn., Chinese schla., F.M.S., Dec., 1932.

DALLARD, BERKELEY LIONEL.—B. 1889; ed. Rangiora High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. govt. serv., 1907; stamp duties dept., 1907-13; audit office, 1913-18; advisory acctnt., bd. of trade, 1919-23; inspr., pub. serv. comsrr.'s office, 1924-26; contr.-gen., prisons, 1926; also asst. pub. serv. comsrr., 1929; under sec., justice and regist.-gen., births, deaths, marriages, 1933.

DALLIN, THOMAS.—Late lieut. 3rd Batt. Essex Regt.; served in S. African War (medal and two clasps); hon. 2nd lieut. in the army, Oct., 1902; joined col. audit dept., Feb., 1904; examr., exchequer and audit dept., July, 1906; asst. audr., S. Nigeria, Oct., 1906 to Feb., 1910; reverted to ex. and audit dept., Feb., 1910; asst. audr., Hong Kong, July, 1910; ag. audr. in 1911, 1912, 1913 and 1914; asst. cable censor, Aug., Oct., 1914; on mil. serv., Mar., 1915 to July, 1916; ag. audr., Aug., 1916 to Dec., 1917; J.P., 1918; attd. to finan. branch, W.O., Sept., 1918 to Mar., 1919; ag. audr. on various occasions in 1920, 1922 and 1924-30.
DALTON, ALFRED, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1892; G.S. and W.R., Ireland, 1909; asst. traffic supt., Tanganyika rly., Nov., 1920; traffic supt., 1930.

DALTON, LLWELYN CHISHOLM.—B. 1879; ed. Marlborough and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1900 (Hist. Tripos); M.A., 1905; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1901; legal asst., land setttn. bd., O.R.C., Oct., 1901; asst. res. mag. and J.P., June, 1902 to Nov., 1910; advoc., sup. ot., 1904; reglar., B. Guiana, Nov., 1910; judge advoc., local forces, 1914; ag. puisne judge, Nov., 1912 to Feb., 1913; Nov., 1914 to Jan., 1915; July, 1916, to June, 1917; July to Sept., 1918; Mar. to June, 1919; ag. solr.-gen., May to Aug., 1914; Sept., 1914 to Jan., 1915; ag. atty.-gen., Aug.-Sept., 1914; Feb.-Apr., 1915; ag. chief just., July-Sept., 1917; June-Oct., 1919; puisne judge, B. Guiana, June, 1919; puisne judge, Gold Coast, Feb., 1923; puisne judge, Ceylon, Mar., 1925; compiled and edited "Statutory Rules and Orders (B. Guiana)" to 1914, 2 vols.; editor, B. Guiana Law Reports, 9 vols.; an ass't. editor "Burge on Colonial and Foreign Laws," 2nd edit., vol. iv.; compiled Digest of Case Law of British Guiana, 1910-1920; author of "Civil Law of British Guiana."

D'ANDRADE, WILLIAM ALBERT.—B. 1884; cler. asst., surg. genl.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1903; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1903; 5th ditto, 1906; 4th ditto, 1913; 3rd ditto, 1921; 2nd ditto, 1923; ch. clk., cust., 1925; compt'r., cust., 1927.

DANDRIA, HON. REV. ENRICO, B.L. Can., D.D., Ph.D.—B. 1892; ed. St. Paul's Inst., Valletta; matric. Malta Univ., 1906; B.L. Can., D.D., 1913; awarded govt. prize as first student of academical course; Ph.D., Gregorian Univ., Rome, 1915; teacher of philosophy and Eng. lit. and prefect of studies in Archepiscopal Seminary, Malta, 1916-19; elec. for 2nd divn. in Maltese parlmt., Oct., 1921; min. for pub. instr., July, 1923; resigned, June, 1927; re-ele. Aug., 1927.

DANEEL, JOSIAS, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.)—B. 1884; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Edin. Univ.; admitted F.R.C.S., Edin., 1922; med. supt., Rietfontein hosp., Union of S. Africa, 1927.

DANIEL, FRANCIS DE FOREST.—B. 1886; ed. Marlborough Coll.; admitted solr., 1910; asst. acctnt., Lagos rly., July, 1912; transfd., cust. dept., May, 1913; asst. dist. offr., N. Provs., Nigeria, July, 1914; res., Mar., 1930.

DANIELS, REGINALD YOUNG, M.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1894; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to Galle kach., Oct., 1920; ag. office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Matara, Sept., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Galle, Jan., 1922; ag. office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Mar., 1922; ditto to govt. agt., N. Prov., Nov., 1922; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Dec., 1925; pol. mag., Kalutara, Dec., 1926; pol. mag., Kandy, Apr., 1928; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Aug., 1930; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Sept., 1930; do. Ratnapura, Oct., 1931; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Mar., 1933.
DANNATT, ROBERT MALCOLM, M.B., B.S. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.)—B. 1881; med. offr., F.M.S., Oct., 1925; surgn., F.M.S., Mar., 1926.

DARLING, JOHN SINGLETON.—B. 1898; ed. Marlborough Coll.; served in Indian Army, 1917-22; war serv., Palestine, 1918; Waziristan, 1921; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929; asst. dist. offr., Aug., 1931.

DARRELL, ARTHUR LLEWELYN.—B. 1883; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; asst. clk. to regis., St. Vincent, 1900; 3rd clsk. treaswy., 1901; 2nd clsk. to gov., Windward Is. (Grenada), 1909; pte. sec. and A.D.C. to gov., Windward Is., 1913; treas. ast., 3rd grade, Nigeria, 1914; treas. ast., 2nd grade, 1919; senr. ast. treas., 1921; ag. treas. in 1928.
DAVENPORT, CECIL THORNHILL, M.A.—B. 1892; ed. Malvern and Oriel Coll., Oxford; ast. dist. comsnr., Kenya, Jan., 1920.

DAVIDSON, EDWARD ERNEST.—B. 1878; extra clk., C.S.O., Ceylon, July, 1898; impr. of post offices, Jan., 1900; office ast. to dir. of public instruction, Jan., 1906; w. sche., Apr., 1911, and May, 1912; ast. V. of civ. serv., Jan., 1913; ag. ast. educn., Apr., 1920; addnl. ast. dir. Oct., 1920; office ast. to dir. educ., Oct., 1920.

DAVIDSON, GERALD LABOR.—B. 1898; on mily. serv., Sept., 1917; ast. civ. serv., Nov., 1921; ast. colmn., Dec., 1921; attd., Badulla kach., office ast., Ratnapura kach., Oct., Kurunegala kach., Oct., 1926; pol. mag., Sept., 1927; ast. dir. statistics, Jan., commr., lands, Oct., 1931.

DAVIDSON, WALKER WHITFIELD M.A. (Cantab.), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Y., B. 1886; ast. engrn., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1914; on mily. serv., 1916-19; ast. Mentakab, 1919; exec. engrn., Jr. senr. exec. engrn., P.W.D., S.S., 1934.

DAVIES, D. R.—B. 1889; ed. Lancaster and Worcester Coll., Oxon.; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1915; junr. ast. May-July, 1915; ast. dist. commr., Apr., 1915 to May, 1916; ast. dist. commr., Sep., 1916 to Sep., 1917.

DAVIES, DAVID, A.R. San., I.R.C., dept., Hong Kong, 1914; second, serv., 1917-20; storekeeper, san., Ist clk. and ast. sec., san., bd., 1923-27; ch. clk., senr. cler. and col. secretariat, 1928; J.P., 1930.
DAVIES, EBENZER DYER.—Clk., Natal, 1920; senr. clk., 1913; priz. sec., educn. dept., Natal, 1922; ch. clk., dept., Natal, June, 1932.

DAVIES, EDWARD HAROLD.—B. 1885; serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1914; to Kandy kach., Nov., 1920; agt., Matara, Jan., 1922; ditto Panadura; office ast. to govt. agt., Prov. of Ceylon, ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Feb., 1924; ast. statistics, Mar., 1924; dep. fiscal, addn., May, 1924; off. ast., Kan., Oct., 1925; ast. dir. statistics, Feb., dir. ditto, Aug., 1927; ast. govt. agt., May, 1928; ast. land commr., July, Colombo port commr., Nov., 1931.

DAVIES, EDWARD JOHN.—B. 1885; doverry Coll. and Univ. of Wales, (Wales); served with Artists' Rifles, Yorks. Regt., 1916-18; France, 1917; bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1922; com. Kenya, May, 1927; legal mem. of law, Dec., 1931-July, 1932; crown corn., Feb., 1933; ag. aslr.-genl., May, 1933.

DAVIES, GEORGE ALBERT DARWIN.—ed. Godolphin Gram. Schl.; ast. Fd. dan., 1905-08; ast. ch. clk. & ast. P.W.D. Coast, 1914; ag. ch. clk. & ast. Ns., 1916; ag. ch. ast., Mar. to Sept., ast. treas., Apr., 1918; ast. treas., ag. dep. treas., in 1929, 1932 and 1933.

DAVIES, GEORGE FREDERIC M.I.E.E.—B. 1886; Imp. serv., 1908; serv. (R.E.) 1914-19; seconded to Ken. ch. tel. engrn., Tanganyika, 1919; do. Nigeria, 1925; ast. engrn. in ch., in-ch., poets and tels. dept., 1933.
DAVIES, H. N., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.),—B. 1897; mily. serv., 1916; offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jaffa charge tuberculosis scheme, Kilimanjaro.
DAVIES, HENRY NOEL, O.B.E., I.R.C.T.—B. 1881; ast. audr., S. India.

IES, RICHARD MORGAN.—B. 1892; on rv., Dec., 1914; cadet, Ceylon, Sept., 1920; Puttalam Kaschcheri, Oct., 1920; ditto, Kach., June, 1921; Colombo Port Oct., 1921; ag. offic. to asst. govt. egalla, Jan., 1922; pol. mag., Matale, 23; dep. rubber contr., May, 1924; asst. t., Batticaloa, Sept., 1925; ditto, Trinco- June, 1926; asst. attm. offr., Dec., 1926; vt. agt., Kandy, Aug., 1927; dist. judge, hapura, Nov., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Many., 1930; pol. mag., Matale, Nov., 1931; cal, Colombo, Mar., 1932; asst. attm. tly., 1932.

IES, RUPERT MEREDITH, B.Sc., B.Sc. —B. 1899; ed. Bedford Schl.; mil. serv., cot. & R.E.s, 1917-19; Aberdeen Univ., Cambridge Univ., 1924, post grad.; agt., Nigeria, 1928'; dist. agr. offr., yka Territory, Aug., 1930.
IES, THOMAS WALTON.—B. 1907; ed. Ige and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. st., 1929; apptd. after compet. exam. astt. miny. of Transport, 1930; transf. to C.O.,
IES, TUDOR THOMAS.—B. 1887; astt. rinter, Nyassaland Prot., 1916; ag. govt. and compt. stationery, May to July, 1917, 919 to Mar., 1920 and Apr. to Dec., 1923; inter., Dec., 1923.

IES, WILLIAM ALFORD NOEL.—B. 1883; mouth Gram. Schl.; admitted solr. of England, Apr., 1906; private practice zland, 1906-1907; private practice in ica, 1907-1908; astt. dist. comsnr., Sierra Dec., 1908; passed lower standard Mendi June, 1911; ag. dist. comsnr., Dec., 1911, ie, 1912; ag. pol. mag., June to Dec., acted as crown prosecutor on several ns; ag. dist. comsnr., 1913-14; master and sup. ct., S. Leone, July, 1914; curator estate states, Oct., 1914; regir., prize 1914; 2nd lieut., general list, Mar., 1916; ned in desps., 1916; attached S. Leone W.A.F.F., May, 1916; served in S. Leone, transf. to G. Coast Regt., 1917; served E. Africa, 1918; staff capt. with Col. column, Apr., to June, 1918; staff mr. 2nd W. African Service Brig., Oct., demob., Dec., 1918; resumed duties as sup. ct., Sierra Leone, May, 1919; sup. ct., S.S., Nov., 1920.

IS, SIR CHARLES THOMAS, G.C.M.G. K.C.M.G.(1923), C.M.G.(1915).—B. 1873; rist's Coll., Brecon, and Oxford; classical Balliol Coll., 1892-6; 1st class classical 1894; Jenkyns exhibnr., 1896; 1st class m., 1896; apptd., after compet. exam. cl., acct-gen.'s dept., admiralty, Oct. 1896; transf. to sec.'s dept., inland far., 1897; 2nd cl. cl., col. office, Apr. 1897; went to Gibraltar as sec. to Rt. Sir C. R. Thompson on a mission to inquire currency question, May and June, 1898; cif. cable comtece., 1900; priv. sec. to Sir manney, June, 1902; 1st cls. cl., 1st Apr., principal cl., 4th June, 1916; senr. sec. to Imperial Conference; astt. sec., 1st 920; astt. under-sec. of state, 1st Dec., 1921; under-sec. of state for dominion affrs., 9th 1925; ret., 1930.

DAVIS, NORMAN STEPHEN.—Second divn. cl., B. of T. and A.G.D., G.P.O., London; transf. as acctnt., G.P.O., Sierra Leone, Sept., 1913; attd. as lieut., W. Africa Regt., 1917; astt. P.M.G. and acctnt., 1915; ag. P.M.G. on four occasions, 1915-1923; P.M.G., Apr., 1925.
DAVIS, RICHARD PRITCHARD, M.I.M. & C.E., M.T.P.I., M.R.S.I.—B. 1888; town planning supt., Selangor, F.M.S., Dec., 1928; town planner, Selangor, July, 1932.

DAVIS, SIR STEUART SPENCER, KT. BACH. (1930), C.M.G.(1919)—B. 1875; rev.offr., St.Kitts, 1893; 1st clk., treasy. and cust., 1898; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis def. force, 1898; ag. adjutant, 1899; att. col. audit branch, E. and A.dept., July, 1901; acctnt., treasy., G. Coast, 1901; astt. treas., 1902; oh. astt. treas., Nov., 1908; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1915; treas., civil adminstr., German Africa (occupied territory), Nov., 1916; treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ag. sec. to admstr., June to Aug., 1919; dep. admstr., Mar.-Apr., 1920; treas., Palestine, May, 1922; chmn., economy comtece., 1922; ag. ch. sec. on various occasions, 1923-26; chmn., currency comtece., 1924; del. for Palestine at Constantinople in connec. with Ottoman pub. debt, Oct. to Nov., 1924; chmn., comsn. on finances of Orthodox Patriarchate of Jerusalem, Dec., 1925.; chmn., standing comtece. for commerce and industry from 1928; O.A.G. Palestine and ag. high. comsnr., Trans-Jordan, July-Sept., 1930; gov. and commdr.in-ch., St. Helena,
DAVIS, THOMAS ARTHUR WARREN.—B. 1899; ed. Haverfordwest and Keble Coll., Oxford; Indian army, 1917-22; astt. conserv., forests, Br. Guiana, 1925; ag. conserv., forests, July-Dec.,
DAVIS, WILLIAM.—B. 1881; G.P.O. Glasgow, 1894; supt., grade II, Singapore, Nov., 1918; supt., grade I, G.P.O., Singapore, Nov., 1920; supt., Jan., 1921; ag. acct., Mar.-Dec., 1926.

DAVSON, SIR CHARLES SIMON, KT. BACH. (1917), B.A., LL.D., K.C.—Ed. at Westminster and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Tem., Jan., 1881; admitted to bar of Br. Guiana, 1882; acted as solr-gen. on many occasions; stip. mag., 1888; solr-gen., 1898; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1898, to Oct., 1899; again, Oct., 1900, to Apr., 1901, and on several other occasions; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1905; ch. just., Fiji, and judicial comsnr., W. Pacific, 1914; ret., 1922.

DAVSON, SIR EDWARD, 1st Baronet (er.1927), KT. Bach (1919)—Ed. Eton; pres., associated West Indian Chambers of Commerce, 1916-26; chmn., Imp. Cocoa Confce., 1924; mem., Imp. Economic Comtece., 1925; chmn., federation of Chambers of Commerce of Br. Empire, 1925-28; chmn., W. Indies Confce., 1929; advr. on col. trade to S. of S. for the Cols., Ottawa Confce., 1932 and World Economic Confce., London, 1933; rep. of cols. on Imp.Communications Advisory Comtece, and Imp. Economic Comtece.; a trustee of Imp. Inst., and gov., Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agric.

DAWE, ARTHUR JAMES, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1891; ed. Berkhamsted Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; scholar, 1910-1914; B.A.; war service, 1914-18; staff, 1917; lieut., R.N.V.R. (R.N.D.); temp. appt. in C.O., Jan., 1918; apptd., under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., June, 1919; astt.prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to Mr. Amery, 13th June, 1920; priv. sec. to Mr. E. F. L. Wood, 1921; prin., 1st Dec., 1921; dep. sec. to Imp. Economic Confce., 1923; visited Sierra Leone as sec. to coman. of enquiry into affs. of Freetown municipality, 1926; accompany U.S. of S. on visit to Palestine and Cyprus, 1930; sec. to Malta royal comm., 1931; sent on mission to Malta, 1933-34.

DAWE, MORLEY THOMAS O.B.E. (1925), F.L.S.—B. 1880; asst. botanist, forestry and scientific dept., Uganda, 1902; head of dept., 1903; dir. agric., Mozambique, 1910; agric. advr. to Colombia, 1915; commr. by C.O. to visit and report on agric. conditions and needs of Gambia, 1920; consultg. agric. expert to Fomento Geral de Angola, 1921; commr., lands and forests, S. Leone, 1922; commr. repg. S. Leone at British Empire exhibn., 1924; commr. repg. S. Leone at Canadian Nat. exhibn., Toronto, 1926; dir. agr., Cyprus, 1928; mem., leg., May, 1928 to Nov., 1931; headed Cyprus trade mission, June-July, 1930; rep. gov't., 9th Internat. horticultural cong., London, 1930, confere. on fruit production, London, 1930 and 5th Internat. botanical cong., 1930; dir., agr. and forests, Palestine, 1933.

DAWSON, CHRISTOPHER WILLIAM, Barristerat-Law (Gray's Inn).—B. 1896; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (class. schol.); 2nd lieut., 5th Batt., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; served India (N.W. Frontier), Mesopotamia; demobilised with rank of capt., 1919; cadet, S.S., Mar., 1920; 3rd mag., S'pore, July, 1920; 2nd mag., S'pore, Oct., 1922; 2nd asst. advr., B. Pahat, Nov., 1923; 1st mag. and regiar., sup. ct., Johore, Mar., 1924; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Feb., 1925; called to bar (Gray's Inn), June, 1929; dist. judge, Malacca, Sept., 1929; 2nd asst. sec., S.S., Jan., 1930; 1st asst. sec., S.S., Mar., 1932; as legal advr., July, 1933.
DAWSON, ERNEST EDWARD.—B. 1886; B.A. (engg.) Cambridge with 2nd cl. honours, A.M.I.C.E.; Canadian N. rly., 1910; asst. engr., Tanganyika rly., Jan., 1921; dist. engr., Nov., 1928.
DAY, SIR ALBERT CRICII, KT. BACH (1932), C.M.G. (1925), C.B.E. (1919).—Asst. priv. sec. to gov. of New Zealand, 1910-1912; offl. sec. to govgen. since 1913.

DAY, EDWARD VICTOR GRACE.—B. 1896; ed. Christ's Coll., Christchurch and Timaru High Schl., New Zealand; cadet, S.S., Sept., 1921; attd., col. sec. office, Nov., 1921; asst. sec., Malaya Borneo Exhibn., 1921; A.D.C. to O.A.G., June, 1922; sec., many bds., A. Star in addn., Feb., 1923; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; offr. cls. IV, Apr., 1928; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933.

DEAKIN, CHARLES SIDNEY, B.A.—B. 1897; ed. at Orme Schi., Newcastle-on-Tyne, Christ's Hosp. and Queen's Coll., Camb., schol., Hughes Eng. essay prize, 1st cl. mech. tripods, engnr., 1923; research grant, bridge oscillations, 1923-24; junr. engrg. dept., science and indust. research, bridge stress comm., 1924-25; John Wimbolt prize, engnr. research, 1925; R.A.S.C. (M.F.), 1916; Iraq, 1917; attd., polit. dept. as Q.M.S., Native Militia and hd.clk., Zubair, 1919; demob., 1920; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

DEAN, H. E.—S. African constab., 1902-07; mast., fencing dept., E.A.P., May, 1908; transport offr., Nairobi, 1908-11; office supt., P.W.D., B.E.A., 1911-16; acctnt., pub. wks. dept., 1916- 21; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, 1921-25; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1925; treas., Dominica, 1932.

DEANE, SIR GEORGE CAMPBELL, KT. BACH (1930).—B. 1873; ed. at St. John's Coll., Oxford; M.A.; Oxon (honours in jurisprudence); called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1898; practised at the bar, B. Guiana, 1898-1903; ditto, Trinidad, 1903-1910; stip. mag., Arima, Trinidad, Sept., 1910; as S.J. Port of Spain, Trinidad, May to Dec., 1911, and May to Oct., 1912; stip. mag., Port of Spain, twice apptd. commr. under order, 1919; as ag. puisme judge, on various occasions, 1919; twice apptd. commr. under order, 1919; as adjutant, Trinidad Light Horse; 2nd puisme Judge Apr., 1920; ch. just., Leeward Is., Jan., 1923; puisme judge, S. Settlements, and commr. F.M.S., Nov., 1924; ch. just., S. Carib. Coast, 1929; pres., W.A. ct. of appl., 1930.

DEANE, LIEUT.-COL. ROBERT, O.B.E. (1879); B. 1879; S.A. War, 1900-02; S.A. const. 1901; Transvaal pol., 1908; S.A. Perm. Staff, (Staff), 1912; German S.W.A. (staff capt. a brig. machine gun offr.), 1914; W.F.F. (brig. machine gun offr.), lst S.A. Inf. Bnch., 1915; France, 1916; ch. of pol., St. Lucia, 1917; dep. inspr. gen. pol., Mauritius, 1922; inspr. gen. pol. and supt., prisons, 1926; M.L.C., 1926.

DE BOER, HENRY SPERLINGER, M.R.C.S., Eng., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.P.S. (Cantab.), D.T.M. and H. (Lond.).—B. 1888; privately and at London Hosp.; served R.A.M.C., 1915-20 (Gallipoli, Sinai, Palestine) and Syria, ment. in despatches; med. offr., K.R. May, 1920; senr. health offr., S. African reserv., Jan., 1926; dep. dir., san. services, S. Rhodesia, Mar., 1931; do., med. services, 12th dep. dir., med. services, Uganda, 1933.

DE CARTERET, VALENTINE EDWARD ROSS, B. 1883; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, May, 1915; commr., Jan., 1922; col. pol. offr., co- R.I.C., 1915, and senr. pol. offr., course at No. Scotland Yard, 1927; senr. commr., pol., 1929.
DE CHAIR, VICE-ADMIRAL, SIR JAMES

RAWSON STRATFORD, K.C.B. (1916), K.C.M. (1933), M.V.O. (1908).—B. 1864; mil. of "Alexandra," at bombardment of Alexandria, 1882; landed with naval brig., naval attaché courts of the Maritime Powers, 1902; and to S. America, 1903-4; A.D.C. to the King, 1912; naval sec. to 1st lord of the admry., 1912-14; sec. of training service, 1914; comdg. tenth crs. squadron, 1914-16; naval adviser to F.O., 1916; vice-adml., comdg. battle squadron, 1917-18; recadml., 1917; adml., comdg. coast guard reserve, 1919; gov., New S. Wales, Oct., 1927; ret., 1930.

DECK, S. F.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Aug., 1907; dist. commr., May, 1913; ed., charge, Northern Frontier Dist., 1914-15; inspr. of labour, Dec., 1920; supt., nat. res. Kenya and Uganda Rly., Oct., 1925; as commr., 1928; 1st grade prov. commr., 1931.

DECOMARMOND, HENRI MAXIME—B. 1876; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, M. Temple, June, 1922; ag. dist. and sup. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Aug., 1925; ag. subst. procureur and advoc. gen., on var. occasions, 1925-28; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Jan., 1927; 2nd subst. procureur and advoc. gen., Jan., 1928; ag. procureur and advoc. gen., Dec., 1932; ag. procureur and advoc. gen., May, 1933; mem., com. comm. and coun. of govt.

DE FREITAS, SIR ANTHONY, KT. B.C. (1927), O.B.E. (1918), B.A. (Clare Coll., Cambridge), B. 1869; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn; chief just., St. Vincent, May-Oct., 1904; ag. gen., St. Lucia, July, 1906; atty.-gen., St. Lucian, 1907; chief just., St. Vincent, May, chief just., St. Lucia, Sept., 1919; ag. atty.-gen., St. Vincent, 1920, 1915, and Mar., 1917, 1918; ag. col. sec., St. Lucia, on seven occas. ag. admnstr., St. Lucia, on 15 occasions, including July, 1914, to May, 1915; puisne judge, Jamaica, Aug., 1921; ag. ch. just., Jamaica, July, 1922 to Sept., 1923 and Jan., 1925 to Feb., 1926; ch. just., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1927.

DE FREITAS, JULIAN MIGNON, O.B.E.—B., 1889; ed. St. Augustine Coll., Ramsgate, Haileybury Coll., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; B.A., LL.B., 1912; called to the bar, 1921; asst. mag., St. George West, Trinidad, 1924; pol. mag. and inspr., schls., Gambia, Sept., 1926; relieving pres., dist. ct., Palestine, 1928; pres., do., 1930.

DE GLANVILLE, R. J. A. P. G.—B., 1886; called to the bar, Bahamas, 1914; notary pub., 1914; priv. sec. to H.E. Wm. Hart Bennett, Esq., C.M.G., admnstr. of Bahamas, 1909; also to H.E. Sir W. Grey-Wilson, K.C.M.G., gov., 1912; ag. coroner for New Providence, 1911 and 1913; confirmed, 1914; 2nd elk., H. of A., 1912; chief elk., 1913; ag. regar., sup. ct. from 1913; ag. stip. and circuit mag. and coroner on various occasions, 1921-31; ag. atty.-gen., advoc. gen. and admv. advoc., contr., local clearing office and temp. mem., exec. coun. on various occasions in 1923, 1924 and 1925; regar.-gen., Apr., 1923; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1922-32.

DE HAVILLAND, THOMAS LITTLETON, C.M.G., D.S.O.—B., 1874; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey and Naval Sch., London; Bechuanaland campaign, 1897; Anglo-Boer war, 1899-1902; France and Belgium, 1916-19; sergeant-at-arms, H. of A., Union of S. Africa.

DE HEVENINGHAM, N. W. P.—Ed. King's Coll., London and Worcester Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law (Inner Temple); ent. home civ. serv. (open compet.), 1901; serv.-d H.O.L.G. Bd. and miny. of munitions; practised at bar, 1918-24; ch. mag. and legal adviser, Br. Solomon Is.; mem., advisory coun. and judi. commr. for W. Pacific, 1924; dep. regar., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1928; ag. regar., May-Dec., 1931; res., mag., 1932.
DE JAGER, SAMUEL JACOBUS, K.C., B.A., LL.B., Camb.—B., 1879; called to the bar, Mid-Tem., 1902; admitted advocate, O.R.C., 1903; atty.-gen., O.F.S., 1911; ditto, Cape Prov., 1929.

DE KOCK, GILLES VAN DE WALL.—B., 1889; ed. Stellenbosch, Pretoria, Capetown and London; M.R.C.V.S., Lond. (1913); vety. research offr., Onderstepoort, 1914; prof. of anatomy, Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1920; wr. vety. research offr., Onderstepoort, 1920; sub-dir., vety. services, Onderstepoort, 1927; D.Sc., Witwatersrand Univ., 1928; dep. dir., divn. of vety. services and animal husbandry, 1931.

DE KOCK, MICHIEL HENDRIK, B.A. (Cape), M.A. and Ph. D. (Harvard).—B., 1898; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Harvard Univ., U.S.A.; sen. lecturer, economics, Univ. of Cape Town, 1923-24; mem., bd. of trade and industries, Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1924-29; mem., diamond control advisory comte., 1930; S. African Reserve Bank, 1931; dep. gov., S. African Res. Bank, 1932; author of "Finance Of the Union of South Africa," and "Government ownership in South Africa," and "Economic History of South Africa."

DE LA HARPE, PETER HENRY.—B., 1877; apptd. to cls. V., Ceylon civ. serv., June, 1923; extra off., asst., Galle Kach., June, 1923; ditto to col. sec., Aug., 1928; office asst. to col. sec., Sept., 1929.

DENCH, L. J. E.—Served Br. post office, 1896; trustful, to post and telegraph dept., East Africa and Uganda Prota., 1907; postmr., 1910; astt. postmr. gen., 1921; island postmr., Cyprus, 1922; title altered to P.M.G., July, 1927.

DENHAM, SIR EDWARD BRANDIS, K.C.M.G. (1931), K.B.E. (1927), C.M.G. (1922)—B., 1876; ed. at Malvern and Merton Coll., Oxford (exhibn.), B.A., 1899; cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1899; astt. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1902; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., May, 1904, Mar., 1905; astt. govt. agt. N. Prov. for pearl fishery work, Mar., 1905; 2nd astt. col. sec. and sec., agric. bd., May, 1905; priv. sec. to Sir A. Ashmore, O.A.G., Sect., 1905; organising sec., agric. bd., Dec., 1905; landing survr., cust., Colombo, June, 1906; dist. judge, Negombo, Sept., 1908; 2nd astt. col. sec., Jan., 1909; and priv. sec. to Sir H. Clifford, O.A.G., June, 1909; seconded as supt. of census, Aug., 1910; priv. sec. to Sir H. Clifford, O.A.G., June, 1911; attached to C.O., 1st Jan. to 13th Oct., 1913; prin. astt. col. sec., Ceylon, 25th Apr., 1914; chief censor, in addition to his own duties, Aug., 1914; capt., administrative section, town guard, Colombo, 1915; ag. govt. agt., E. Prov., Sept., 1915; offr. of cls. I., grade II. on app't. as dir. of educn., Aug., 1916; dir., food production, Mar.-Dec., 1920; col. sec., Mauritius, 14th Dec., 1920; admstd. govt. in 1921, 1922 and 1923; col. sec., Kenya Col. and Prot., Aug., 1923; admstd. govt., Feb. to Oct., 1925 and from Jan. to Aug., 1927; govt., Gambia, 1928; govt. and commodr.-in-ch., Br. Guiana, 1930; joint author of "Rubber in the East."

DENMAN, LORD.—3rd Baron (cr. 1834), Rt. Hon. Sir Thomas Denman, P.C. (1907); G.C.M.G. (1911); K.C.V.O. (1909); b., 1874; educ. at Sandhurst; lieut., reserve of offrs.; late lieut., Royal Scots, and major, Middlesex I.Y.; served in S. Africa war (wounded, Queen's medal, 3 clasps); lord-in-waiting in ordinary, 1905-7; capt., Loin. corps of gentlemen-at-arms, 1907-11; J.P., Sussex; govt. gen. and commodr.-in-chief, Commonwealth of Australia, 1911-1914.

DENNIS, OWEN GREGORY.—B., 1894; ent. Army, King's Own Scottish Borderers, 1915; war. serv. in Egypt and France, 1915-18; seconded serv., W.A.F.F., 1919; astt. commr., jol., Nigeria, 1922; resgd. Army commr., 1923; invalided from col. serv., Nov., 1923; re-apptd. as local commd.t., pol. and astt. commr., Cyprus, Mar., 1928; commr. and local commd.t., pol., Dec., 1929.

DENNISTOUN, ROBERT MAXWELL, C.B.E. (1918). B.A.—B., 1864; served in European War, 1914-18; capt., Fort Garry Horse; major, 45th batt.; lieut.-col., 53rd batt.; col. on H.Q. staff; dep. judge advoc. gen., Overseas Forces of Canada, 1917-19; ment. in desp., 1918; colonial officers' long serv. med.; K.C., Ont., 1908; Msn., 1910; bencher, Law Soc., Upper Can., 1906-07; bencher, Law Soc., Man., 1912-18; judge, ct. of appeal, Man., 1918; publications—"Notes on Milly. Law," "Notes on Dist. Courts Martials."
DENNY, WILLIAM JOSEPH, M.C.—M.P., S. Australia, since 1900; atty.-gen. and min. for Northern Territory, 1910-12; capt., A.I.F., European War, 1915-19; atty.-gen., 1924-27 and vice Apr., 1930.

DE NOBRIGA, John.—B., 1884; 3rd elk., P.W.D., Trinidad, Apr., 1901; astt. elk., water and sewerage dept., Aug., 1903; storekeeper and elk., engr.'s office, Apr., 1903; elk., mines dept., Sept., 1908; ch. elk., Ditto, Dec., 1910; 2nd elk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1921; ch. elk. and elk., leg. coun., Feb., 1925; 2nd astt. col. sec., Jan., 1930; ag. 1st astt. col. sec., Feb., 1931.

DE NORMAN, ALBERT WILFRED NOEL.—B., 1891; ed. United Services Coll. and Chateau du Rosey, Switzerland; Canadian Expedy. Force, 1914; lieut., Royal Fusiliers, 1915; West African Forces, 1916-20; survr., Nigeria survey, 1921; natl. survr.-gen., 1930; ag. survr.-gen., Apr.-Oct., 1933.

DE PASS, HAROLD S.—B. 1881; sub-inspr. of constab., Trinidad, July, 1904; inspr., Apr., 1912; has acted as senr. inspr. and detective inspr. on many occasions; ag. deputy inspr. gen. of constab. and supt. of fire brigade, 1918; senr. inspr., constab., June, 1920.

DE PINTO, CYRIL ERNEST.—B. 1892; cadet, local division, Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1914; attached to Galle Kachcheri, Sept., 1914; ag. office ast., Matara Kachcheri, July, 1915; ditto, Kalutara Kachcheri, Nov., 1915; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Apr., 1916; attached to Kandy Kachcheri, May, 1916; office ast., Batticaloa Kach., Nov., 1917; pol. mag., Avissawella, Nov., 1919; office ast. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, July, 1921; pol. mag., Jaffna, Oct., 1921; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, Aug., 1923; pol. mag., Matara, Apr., 1925; ditto, Galle, Mar., 1927; dist. judge, Kegalle, June, 1933.
DE PUITRON, PIERRE.—B. 1886; ed. Eton and Univ. Coll., Oxford; asst. res., Nigeria, 1909; dist. offr., 1918; res., 1926; res., Bornu, 1928.

DE ROME, F. J., B.Sc. (Lond.), M.B.E.—B. 1886; B.Sc. Lond. Univ., 1908; Jena Univ. (Germany), 1908-1909; asst. mast., Queen's Coll. Hong Kong, 1910; asst. postal censor, 1914-19; asst. mast., cl. II., 1919; asst. mast., cl. I., 1920; ag. headmr., Ellis Kadoorie schl., 1923; ag. headmr., Wantai schl., 1923; headmr., Ellis Kadoorie schl., 1925; headmr., Queen's Coll., Mar., 1930.
DECRIMAN, FRANCIS PHILIP LOUIS.—B. 1901; ed. Ratcliffe Coll.; col. audit dept., Oct., 1924; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925.
DE SANTOS, EBROL LIONEL.—B. 1890; ent. Trinidad civ. serv., Apr., 1912; asst. treas., Jan., 1929; treas. and chmn., agrl. bank, 1933.

DE SILVA, JOSEPH MARTIN.—B. 1880; apptd. to cl. V., Ceylon civ. serv., Apr., 1926; extra off. ast., Ratnapura kach., Apr., 1926; ditto, Galle kach., Oct., 1929; ag. office ast., Colombo kach., Feb., 1932.

DE SILVA, LUCIAN MACULL DOMINIC, B.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn.—B. 1893; commr., requests, Colombo, 1925; dep. solr.-gen., Ceylon, Apr., 1929; ag. atty.-gen., in 1930 and 1931; solr.-gen., 1932.

DE SILVA, WALWIN ARNOLD, B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1905; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; attd., K-galle kach., Jan. and Sept., 1928; attd., Jaffna kach., June, 1928; attd., Kegalle kach., Sept., 1928; attd., Matara kach., Nov., 1928; office ast., Galle kach., Oct., 1932.

DE SOYZA, GUNASENA, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1926; attd., Matara kach., Sept., 1926; attd. to Jaffna kach., June, 1927; office ast., Kegalle kach., Mar., 1928; 2nd landing survr., cust., Nov., 1928; office ast., Badulla kach., Oct., 1931; asst. govt. act., Badulla, Aug., 1932; do., Mullaitivu, June, 1933.

DESPICHT, STANLEY MONK.—B. 1890; ed. Boston Gram. Schl.; passed lower stand. exam. in Mendi; war service, E. Africa, 1917-18; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1915; ag. asst. col. sec., 1919; dist. commr., July, 1923.
DESROCHERS, FELIX.—B. 1886; ed. St. Hyacinthe Seminary, Quebec; B.A. (1905); LL.B. (1908); called to bar, 1908; ch. librn., ibid., Montreal, 1930; gen. librn., libry. of parl., Canada, 1933.

DEVAUX, JUSTIN LOUIS.—B. 1884; student Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1903; called to bar, Jan., 1906 (cert. of hon.); revised laws of St. Lucia, 1916; ag. mag., 1st dist., St. Lucia, Sept., 1917; to Sept., 1918; ast. legal adviser and pol. mag., Seychelles, Mar., 1919; ag. ch. just., Seychelles, Nov., 1919; crown pros., legal adviser, ew., Seychelles, June, 1920; ag. ch. just., in 1921, 1922 and 1923; ch. just., Apr., 1924; govt., dept., July-Aug., 1923; O.A.G. in 1925, 1927 and 1928; res. mag., Jamaica, 1929; solr.-gen., Trinidad, 1931.

DEVRE, ROBERT STEPHEN, J.P., B.A., LL.B. barrister.—B. 1872; ed. Charterhouse and Trin. Coll., Cambridge (B.A., LL.B.); called to bar, 1898; serv. in Paget's Horse, S. African vol. (Queen's med.); dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1905; leg. advsr. and ag. ch. just., Seychelles, 1906-08; recd. comm. in 5th Batt. R. Sasse. Rgt., Feb., 1915; mil. censor, B.E.F., Aug., 1915; Br. judge, Anglo-French condominium, N. Hebrides, 1922; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Feb., 1926; ch. just., Grenada, 1931.
