DE VERTEUIL, ARTHUR.—B. 1878; ent. Trinidad civ. serv., July, 1899; warden, com. of Coron, July, 1929; do., eastern coast, Mar., 1933.
DE VERTEUIL, ERIC J., M.D., B.S. (Harv.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., D.P.H. (Lond.), B. 1881; ast. med. inspr. of health, Tr., Oct., 1930.
DE VERTEUIL, JOSEPH, F.C.S.—B. 1874; ast. govt. analyst, Trinidad, Dec., 1899; field experiments, dept. of agr., Apr., 1910; agrl. chem., dept. of agr., Sept., 1920; agrl. inst., 1931.
DE VERTEUIL, LEON EUGENE.—B. 1878; ast. draughtsman, P.W.D., Trinidad Jan., 1900; 2nd grade dist. offr., Oct., 1903; 1st grade dist. offr., Feb., 1911.

DE VERTEUIL, ST. YVES DANIEL, M.R.E. (1932).—B. 1875; clk. of works, waterworks, Trinidad, Dec., 1900; head overseer, A-Z., Jan., 1903; dist. engnr., 2nd grade, Aug., 1910; dist. engnr., 1st grade, Sept., 1916; warden counties Nariva-Mayaro, Dec., 1925; warden, George, prot. of immigrts. and dir., lab. exchange, Mar., 1933.

DE VILLIERS, ISAAC PIERRE, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. S. African Coll.; atty. and commr. served S.W. Africa, in 1st Eastern Rifles, 1915; France and Palestine in R.F.A., 1918; dep. commr. (headqrs.) S.A. pol., Feb., 1920; commr., S. African pol., Dec., 1928.

DE VILLIERS, SIR JEREMY ETHERIDGE, M.A., LL.D., Kt.Bach. (1923).—B. 1875; ed. African Coll. and Cambridge; B.A., 1894; L.L.B., 1897; LL.D., 1902; fellow of St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1901; judge of water etc. from 1919; judge pres., O.F.S., 1929; judge of ass., 1933.

DEVINE, A. SIDNEY.—B. 1892; ed. F. Coll., Oxon.; enlisted Middlesex Regt., Aug., 1914; 2nd lieut., R. Warwickshire Regt., 1915; lieut., 1917; served in France and India; wounded; served with disposal bld. in India, 1919-20; disposal and liquidation commn., 1923; cler. offr., C.O., 19th Mar., 1923.
DEVONSHIRE, 9TH DUKE OF (created 1856).
VICTOR CHRISTIAN WILLIAM CAVENTH, K.C.V.O., 1916; P.C.; G.C.M.G.; G.C.V.O.; T.T.I.

CAVENDISH, 1605; EARL OF DEVONSHIRE, MARQUESS OF HARTINGTON, 1694; EARL MANTON, 1831; BARON CAVENDISH (U.K.), B. 1868; ed. Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; (L.U.) Derbyshire W., 1891-1903; treas. I. Household, 1900-1903; financial sec. to I., 1903-1906; lord-lieut. of Derbyshire; int. territorial forces, Derby; chancellor of Univ.; civil lord of the Admiralty, 1916; n. of Canada, 1916-21; S. of S. for the Oct., 1922 to Jan., 1924.

WAAL, HON DANIEL, D.S.O., K.C.—3; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Hall, Cambridge; B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.); admitted, advoc., Cape and Transvaal, practised Pretoria; German S.W. Africa, 1915; K.C., 1919; ag. judge, Griquast Local Divn., Oct., 1919; judge, sup. P.D., Aug., 1920; judge pres., T.P.D., 1920.

WAAL, HON. JAN HENDRIK HOFMAYR, B. 1871; ed. Normal and S.A. Colls., own; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1897; Cape bar, 1897; mem., Union H. of A. uetberg since 1915; whip to Nationalist 1915-24; chmn. of comites. and dep. 1924-29; speaker, 1929-33; el. pres. nse Taal Vereeniging, 1910; formerly Regs Kommissie and chmn., head comitee, list Party, Cape; author of "Afrikander," and of various works of fiction in ns.; ed. "De Goede Hoop," 1903-14.
AR, H. J.—B. 1878; alk., repatriation R.C., 1902; alk., Br. refugee aid, 1903; assy., O.F.S., 1904; clk., treasy., Union icy., 1910; acctnt., 1916; ch. acctnt., 1928.

AR, JOHN.—B. 1883; ed. Otago Univ., land and survey dept., New Zealand, Fvr., F.M.S., 1906; asst. supt., surveys, 1911; supt. of surveys, Kedah, July, ipk., surveys, F.M.S., Oct., 1924; asst. n., F.M.S. and S.S., Oct., 1927; dep. n., F.M.S. and S.S., Dec., 1931.
VET, JOHANNES HENDRIK, B.A.—B. Univ. of Pretoria; educ. dept., 1911-18; b. serv. from 1918; sg. under sec., dept. r., 1931; under sec., do., 1932.
ET, THE HON. N. J., K.C.—Min. of ion of S. Africa, 1913-24; puisne judge, l prov. divn., 1932.
NS, K. J., B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Christ's schol. and exhib.; open classical scholar, Col., Oxford, 1925; final honour schol., language and lit., 1929; imptr., schls., st. 1929.

NSON, ARTHUR HAROLD.—B. 1892; ed. we Schi.; pol. probationer, S.S., Nov., st. supt., pol., Jly., 1916; adjt., S.S., 1920; supt., pol., Jan., 1928; King's Jan., 1928; ag. dir., erim. intell. S.S. in ag. dir., pol. intell. bureau, Dec., pol. offr., Malacoa, Dec., 1929; dir., ll., S.S., Jan., 1932.

NSON, BENJAMIN.—B. 1880; ed. ck and Caius Coll., Camb.; barrister-in-law's Inn, 1904; dist. commnr., G. 9-16; pol. mag., Aoraa, 1916-17; pres., Cyprus, 1918; puisne judge, 1926; senr. lge., 1927; ag. ch. just. six times; ge, Kenya, 1930.
SON, JOHN ROBERT, M.C.—Ed. Liver- solr., 1920; 2nd lieut., The King's Own ster Regt.), 1914; lieut., July, 1915; , 1916; France, 1915-18 (M.C. and dist. commnr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1921.

DICKSON, THE HON. RICHARD SEBASTIAN WILLCOX, D.S.O. (1916).—B. 1897; ed. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1922; priv. sec. to Sir Hugh Clifford, 1922-23; seconded as priv. sec. to Sir H. J. Stanley, gov. of N. Rhodesia, 1925-26; asst. ch. sec., N. Rhodesia, 1926; ag. prin. asst. ch. sec. on various occasions, 1929-32; ag. ch. sec., 1933.
DICKSON, T. AINSWORTH, C.M.G. (1930), M.C. (1916).—B. 1881; cust. asst., E.A.P., 1909; asst. dist. commnr., 1911; dist. commnr., Kenya, Jan., 1919; res. commnr., Swaziland, 1928.

DIEMONT, J. F. W.—B. 1884; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; asst., secretarial branch, posts and tels., O.R.C., 1903; clk., prov. sec.'s office, O.F.S., 1910; admvte. inspr., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1922-29; prin. elk., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1929.

DILLON, ALBERT BARROW, I.S.O. (1932).—B. 1867; ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad; elk., toinspr. of schls., B. Hond., 1893; ag. clk. to audr. and in treasy. and cust., 1893; clk. to col. engrnr., 1896; ag.-insp. of schls., 1895-8; 2nd elk., treasy. and cust., 1897; insp. of schls., June, 1898; asst. bot. station, Sept. to Dec., 1904; ag. ch. elk., C.S.O., and clk. of couns., June, 1905 to Jan., 1906; lieut., B. Hond. Territorial Force, Apr., 1918; ag. audr., May to Aug., 1918 and from Oct., 1919 to Oct., 1920.

DIMES, CAPT. DOUGLAS HENRY, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1886; ed. Kingsbridge Gram. Schl. and Schol. of Agr., S. Devon; R. Vety. Coll., London; lieut., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1913; war serv. in France and Belgium, 1914-19; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1923.

DINGLE, PERCIVAL ALFRED, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.S.M., certif. T.M.H., L.S.T.M.—B. 1881; dist. surg., N. Borneo, Dec., 1911; dep. health offr., July, 1915; civil res. surg., No. 1 London genl. base hosp., Jan., 1917 to May, 1918; P.M.O. and offl. mem., leg. coun., Apr., 1918; deleg. to 5th cong., Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Sept., 1923; deleg. to L. of N. Health Confco, S'pore, Feb., 1925; deleg. to meeting of advisory coun., eastern bureau, L. of N. health organisation, S'pore, Jan., 1926.
DISCOMBE, A. H.—B.1891; ed. Berkhamsted Schol.; asst. loco. suptd., Nigerian rly., 1914; dist. loco. suptd., 1920; asst. supt. of line (loco.), 1926; divnl. supt., 1929.

DISCOMBE, JOHN.—Apptd., after compet. examn., 3rd class elk., sup. et., Gibraltar, 1892; 2nd class elk., 1899; asst. regist. and dep. elk. of arraigns, 1900; called to the bar, Gray's Inn; regist. and elk. of arraigns, admstr.-gen., ex officio J.P., and mem. of cemetery comtee., 1911; marshal, ex officio, 1913; regist. and marshal, prize court, 1914; custodian of enemy property, 1916; during the war rendered legal assistance to the atty.-gen., in addition to other duties, July, 1916, to Feb., 1919; regist. under Deeds of Arrangement Ordee., 1917; regist. of business names, 1918; contrlr. local clearing office for enemy debts, 1920; ag. pol. mag. and coroner on several occasions.

DIVE, Major HUBERT ROY, M.C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), D.O.M.S. (Lond.), graduate Lond. Schol. of Trop. Med., certif. Royal Lond. Ophthalmic Hosp. (Moorfields).—B. 1887; great war, Gallipoli; Egypt, Palestine and France, 1915-19; 1st med. offr./c war pensioners., St. Barth. Hosp., Lond., 1919-20; student and house surgn. L.S.T.M., and Albert Dock Hosp. Feb.-Aug., 1920; med. offr., F.M.S., Dec., 1920; sr. med. offr., Pahang, Nov., 1927; assisting in office of prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Aug., 1930; ag. senr. med. offr., Selangor, Sept., 1931.
DIX, C. C., C.M.G., D.S.O., R.N., (ret.)—Ed. R.N. Schl., Eltham and H.M.S. "Britannia"; R.N., 1898-1919; harmbr., Barbados, 1926; harmbr., King-ton, Jamaica, 1927.
DIXEY, FRANK, O.B.E. (1929), D.Sc., F.G.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1892; govt. geologist, Sierra Leone, Aug., 1918 to Dec. 1921; dir., geological surveys, Nyasaland, Dec., 1921.

DIXON, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1932), O.B.E. (1924), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1888; ed. at Clifton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 1st cl. mods., 1909; 1st cl. Lit. Hum., 1911; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., O.C., 11th Oct., 1911; priv. sec. to Sir G. Fiddes, 8th July, 1917; 1st cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec. D.O., 4th Sept., 1929.

DIXON, CAPT. HAROLD PARSON.—B. 1888; ed. Dover Coll. and Merchant Taylors Sch.; mil. serv., 1914-20; served in Salonika, Caucasus and Anatolia; mentd. in despatches; capt., 5th Durham L.I., T.A. (Res.), 1921; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1921.
DIXON, OWEN, K.C., M.A., LL.B. (Victoria).—B. 1886; ed. Melbourne Univ.; called to bar, 1910; ag. just., sup. ct., Victoria, July—Dec., 1926; a just. of high ct., C'wealth of Australia, 1929.
DIXON, CAPT. OSWALD, M.C., M.R.C.V.S.—Veterinary offr., Kenya, Dec., 1912; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1920; asst. ch. vety. offr., 1932.

DOBBS, CECIL COOPER, C.M.G. (1931), O.B.E. (1928).—Ed. St. Columba's Coll., Rathfarnham, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A. Classics (honours); asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 25th Oct., 1906; dist. commnr., 1910; senr. commnr. (2nd cls.), Jan., 1925; ditto, 1st cls., Sept., 1927; ag. ch. native commnr., Nov., 1928.

DOBBS, SIR HENRY ROBERT CONWAY, G.B.E. (1929), K.C.S.I. (1921), K.C.M.G. (1925), K.C.I.E. (1921), C.S.I. (1916), C.I.E. (1903), F.R.G.S.—B. 1871; ed. Winchester Coll. (schl.) and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (schl.); ent. I.C.S., 1892; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov. N.W.P., 1896-99; polit. serv., Mysore and Baluchistan, 1899-1901; investigated navigation of Euphrates, from latitude of Aleppo to Bagdad, 1902-3; consul for Seistan and Kain, 1903; Br. commnr., Russo-Afghan boundary, 1903-4; sec. to Kabul mission, 1904; famine commnr., Rajputana, 1905; dep. sec., foreign dept., 1906; on sp. duty in connec. with visit of Amir of Afghanistan to India, 1906; rev. and judg. commnr., Baluchistan, 1909, 1911 and 1917; judg. commnr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Jan., 1914; res. and cons. gen., Turkish Arabia, Oct., 1914; polit. offr., with Mesopotamian Force supervising civ. adminstr. of occupied territories, Jan., 1915 to Aug., 1916; agt. to gov-gen. and ch. commnr., Baluchistan, Dec., 1917; ch. Br. rep., Indo-Afghan confere., Mussoorie, 1920; head of Br. mission to Kabul, Dec., 1920 to Nov., 1921; high commnr., Iraq, 1923-29.
DODDS, E. W.—B. 1881; clk., treasy., Natal, 1899; clk., treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., treasy., 1920; acctnt., treasy., 1928; acctnt., dept. of posts and tele., Dec., 1931.

DOOLIS, JAMES HUGH HAMILTON.—B. 1880; ed. at private schls. and Eastbourne Coll.; enlisted in I.Y. for serv. in S. Africa, Jan., 1901; reed. commn. as lieut. in I.Y., Oct., 1901; resigned commn. and granted hon. rank of lieut. in the Army, Feb., 1902 (Queen's med., five clasps); lieut., S.A.C., Feb., 1902; res. J.P., Transvaal, Mar., 1905; sub.-inspr., S.A.C., July, 1906; retrenched from S.A.C., July, 1907; lieut., Bedfordshire I.Y., Nov., 1907; asst. dist. offr., Somaliland Prot., Sept., 1908; dist. offr., Somaliland Prot., Apr., 1910; represented H.M.'s commr. of Somaliland on a mission to Abyssinia, July-Aug., 1910; seconded for service in Abyssinia, to be vice-consul, Harrar.

DOHERTY, JAMES, M.B.E. (1933).—B. 1889; Imp. cust., 1907; storekeeper and baggage exam., cust., E. Africa Prot., 1912; preventive offr., cust., E.A.P., 1914; inspr., cust. preventive serv., E.A.P., 1918; asst. govt. coast agt., Kenya and Uganda, 1919; ag. coast agt., Kenya and Uganda, Aug. to Dec., 1920 and Sept. to Dec., 1922; govt. coast agt., Kenya and Uganda, 1923; naval agt. and dist. treas., Mombasa, Jan., 1933; naval art., E. African Vol. Art'y., 1914-18; "1914-15" Star, War and Victory Medals.
DOOLE, CAPT. EDWARD, R.E. (R. of O.). A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1886; asst. mech. engrn., P.W.D., Gold Coast, Nov., 1921; transfd. as engrn. transport offr. to motor transport dept., Aug., 1922.

DONALDSON, ARTHUR WILLIAM HUNTER, O.B.E., M.B., B.Ch. (Oxon.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), certif. (with distinc.) L.S.T.M., D.P.H. (Lond.).—B. 1881; ed. St. Thomas Hosp., London; joined R.A.M.C., Jan., 1915; R.F.F. in France as surg. specialist, 18th Casualty Clearn. Station, 1916-19; ag. major, R.A.M.C., mentd. in despatches; served in Somaliland in operations against the Dervishes in 1920; med. offr., Somaliland, Nov., 1920; med. offr. in ch., June, 1933.

DONOVAN, THOMAS.—B. 1885; ed. Christian Brothers' Coll., Cork; Mediterranean Exped. Force, Apr., 1915-Jan., 1916; B.E.F., Apr., 1916-June, 1919; Q.M.S., Somaliland pol., Oct., 1919 to Mar., 1923; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, Apr., 1923.

DONOVAN, WILLIAM, M.Sc. (N.Z.), F.I.C.—B. 1879; ed. Thames (N.Z.) High Schl. and Schl. of Mines, Otago Univ. and Auckland Univ. Coll.; joined N.Z. govt. serv., 1905; asst. dom. analyst, 1919; dom. analyst, 1930.

DOORLY, A. N., B.A. (Oxon.), Barrister at Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1887; asst. dist. commnr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1913; res. mag., Kenya, 1919; senr. mag., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1923; ag. judge, high ct., 1923, 1924 and 1925; puisne judge, Zanzibar, 1925; ag. ch. just. in 1926, 1928 and 1929; atty.-gen., 1929.
DOORLY, REV. CHARLES STOKESLEY, B.A. Selwyn Coll. Camb.—B. 1882; 4th asst. mast., Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, July, 1911; 2nd asst. mast., July, 1914; 2nd asst. mast., July, 1920.

DOORLY, CHARLES W., C.B.E. (1930).—B. 1875; 3rd cls. clk., imigrn. dept., Jamaica, 1892; 2nd cls. clk., 1894; Hindustani interpreter, 1902; inspr. of imigrs., 1903; protector of imigrs., 1909; govt. imigrn. agt., Madras, 1913; asst. col. sec., Jamaica, 1922; admstr., St. Lucia, Feb., 1928; ag. gov., Windward Is. in 1930, 1931 and 1933.

DORAN, CHARLES FREDERICK GARFIELD, B.A.—Ed. Royal Belfast Academical Inst. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd Lieut., R.A.F., 1919; served in France, Nov., 1916 till wounded; 1917; called to Irish Bar (king's Inn) 1913; crown counsel, Kenya, Dec., 1923; ag. solr.-gen. and mem., leg. coun., Mar.-June, 1930.

DORION, Hon. Charles Edward.—B. 1860; ed. Quebec Seminary; Laval Univ. (L.L.L., 1884); admitted to bar of Prov. of Queb., 1884; practised law in Queb. City until 1911; LL.D., Laval Univ., 1894; prof. of laws, Laval Univ., 1898; and since, prof. of civ. code; judge, superior ct., Prov. of Queb., 1911; judge, ct. of king's bench, 1921.

DOUGHTY, Arthur George, C.M.G. (1905). M.A., Litt.D.—B. 1860; priv. sec. to min. of pub. wks., Quebec, Canada, 1896; librarian, Quebec, 1900; Dominion archivist, Canada, 1904; mem. of comsn. on War Records and Trophies, Dec., 1918; joint del. to internat. congress of the history of America at Rio-de Janeiro, Sept., 1922.

DOUGHTY, Leslie Roy, B.Sc. (Agric.), N.D.A.—B. 1902; ed. Derby Munie. Secondary Schl., Midland Agril. Coll. and Schl. of Agr., Cambridge; geneticist, East African agril. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1929.
DOUGLAS, Kenneth Justyn.—B. 1880; ed. Sherborne and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1903; clk., col. audit dept., Feb., 1906; asst. audr., S. Nigeria, Oct., 1907; dep. audr., Nigeria, Jan., 1919.

DOUGLAS-JONES, C. D., C.M.G. (1920).—Ed. Harrow; priv. sec. to res. comsnr., S. Rhodesia, Aug., 1898; lieut., S. Rhodesia volra., July, 1908; capt., May, 1910; musketry instr.'s certif., Hythe, 1908; sec. to res. comsnr., S. Rhodesia, Apr., 1911; ag. res. comsnr., S. and N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1918; res. comsnr., July, 1918 to Sept., 1923; res. comsnr., N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1923; col. sec., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1924; admstd. govt., Sept., 1924 to April, 1925 and from Apr. to July, 1926; col. sec., Br. Guiana, 1926; O.A.G. on various occasions, 1927-28.

DOVEY, John Edward, M.B., Ch.B., D.T.M. & H. (Edin.).—Med. offr., Hong Kong; do., Kowloon, 1927-28; med. offr. in ch., subord. staff and families, med. offr. in ch. venereal disease clinic and asst., Victoria Hosp., 1929; M.O. in ch., Victoria Hosp., May-Sept., 1932.

DOWBIGGIN, Sir Herbert Layard Kt., Bach., C.M.G. (1926).—B. 1880; inspr. of pol., Ceylon, Jan., 1901; asst. supt. of pol., Apr., 1901; aspt. of pol., Feb., 1905; inspr. gen. of pol., Nov., 1913; on ap. duty, Cyprus, Nov., 1926; resumed duties as inspr.-gen., pol., Ceylon, Nov., 1926.

DOWLAND, Charles Edwin James.—B. 1880; ed. Macandrew Road Schl., normal schl. and Otago Boys' High Schl., Dunedin, N.Z.; ent. N.Z. land and income tax dept., 1897; dep. comsnr., taxes, 1928; comsnr., taxes, 1932.

DOWNIE, H. F., O.B.E. (1934), B.A. (1912), M.A. (1914).—B. 1889; ed. at Christ's Hospital and Oxford; Lodge exhibur. (class.) Univ. Coll., 1908-1912; 1st class moda., 1910; 2nd class Lit. Hum., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Dec., 1912; on mil. serv. from 6th Aug. to 19th Oct., 1914, and from 10th June, 1915, to 7th Apr., 1918; ag. 1st cls. clk., 3rd Jan., 1919; prim., 1st Apr., 1920; visited Sudan, Uganda, Kenya, Tanganyika, Nyassaland, N. and S. Rhodesia and S. Africa as sec., East Africa comsnr. on closer union, Dec., 1927-May, 1928.

DOWNING, Capt. Geoffrey George David, J.P.—Ed. Corrig Schl., Ireland, Royal Irish Univ., and Royal Coll. of Surgeons, Ireland; served in Cape Mounted Rifles, 1883; Cape pol. (Griqua-land West), 1888; Mashonaland exped. ('ioneers), 1890 (med. and clasp); Dominica defence res., 1904-1914; heut., Nov., 1914; ag. sub-inspr., Leeward Is. pol., O.C., Montserrat defence force, O.C., local forces, Montserrat, Dec., 1916; ag. sub-inspr., Leeward Is. pol., and offr. instr., local forces, Antigua, July, 1917; O.C., Marine Batty., Antigua, Oct., 1917; O.C., flashlight service, Antigua-Montserrat, Jan., 1919; capt., Dominica defence force, 1919; O.C., Dominica defence force, Nov., 1919; manager, Barbuda and receiver of wrecks, Mar., 1920; mag. dist. L. (Barbuda), July, 1921; ag. warden, N. dist., Dominica and offl. mem. legis. coun., Mar. to Sept., 1927; ag. warden, Nevis, and mem., legis. coun., St. Kitts-Nevis, Mar. to Apr., 1928; warden, Barbuda, 1929.

DOWSE, William Robert, A.I.R.E.—B. 1891; Br. P.O. engrn. dept., G. Britain, Jan., 1912; Dub. Univ. ex-graduates, O.T.C., Aug., 1914; comand. R.E., Jan., 1915; Gallipoli and France, May, 1916; asst. teleg. engrn., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., June, 1919; demob. reg. army R.of.O., June, 1919; engrn., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., Oct., 1926; senr. engrn., ditto, May, 1928; senr. engrn., P. and T. Selangor and Pahang, Mar., 1932; asst. engrn.-in-ch., posts and tels., Malaya, 1933.
DOWSETT, Francis Dudley.—B. 1908; ed. Bristol Grammar Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1931.

DOYLE, Thomas Francis.—B. 1903; ed. St. Patrick's Acad., Dungannon; offr. in imp. cust. and excise serv., Aug., 1912; inspr., cust., Palestine, July, 1926; ag. dep. dir., cust., excise and trade, periods in 1929 and 1930; dep. collr., cust. and excise comsnr., Mauritius, Feb., 1932; ag. collr., cust. and harbr., June, 1932.

DRAKE, Harold William.—B. 1889; ed. City of London Schl.; entd. Col. Audit Dept., July, 1904; seconded to W.A.F.F., Oct., 1914 (Nigeria and Cameroons); paymr., Overseas Contingent, W.A.F.F. (G.E.A.); Col. Audit Dept., Aug., 1918; asst. audr., Nyassaland, Mar., 1919; senr. asst. audr., Dec., 1921; ag. audr., Apr. to Oct., 1922 and Apr. to Oct., 1925; audr., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1927; asst. dir., col. audit dept., Dec., 1931; audr., Nigeria, 1933.

DRAYTON, Bernard Morgan.—B. 1886; service in post office, pub. wks. and treasy. dept., Grenada, 1902-14; treasy. asst., 3rd grade, Nigeria, 1914; asst. treas., civ. adminstr., German E. Africa (Occupied Territory), 1917; asst. treas., Nigeria, 1920; senr. asst. treas., Nigeria, 1928.

DRAYTON, Hon. Sir Henry L., Kt. Bach. (1915), K.C.—B. 1869; ed. in England and Canada; called to the bar, Ontario, 1891; counsel, railway comte., Ontario legislature, 1902; counsel for City of Toronto, 1910; Ontario govt. comsnr. on Toronto Power Coman., 1911; ch. comsnr., bd. of railway comsnrs., Canada, 1912; resig. to assume portfolio of min. of finance in Union Govt., Aug., 1919; elec. by acclamation for Kingston, Oct., 1919; re-ele. for York West, g.e., Dec., 1921; resig. portfolio on defeat of admstr., Dec., 1921; chmn., bd. of liquor comsnrs., Ontario, 1928.

DRAYTON, Robert Harry, LL.B. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1892; solr. (Eng.); lieut., Devonshire Regt. and Machine Gun Corps, 1914-1918; serv. in France, Jan., 1916 to Sept., 1917; miny. of reconstrn. and Lord Chancellor's dept., 1919; treas. solr.'s office, 1919-20; asst. legal sec., govt. of Palestine (continuing as asst. atty.-gen.), Nov., 1920; ag. legal sec., Aug., 1921 to Oct., 1921; mem. of advisory and exec. couns., Apr. to July, 1923 and July to Sept., 1924; title changed to solr-gen., 1925; rep. Palestine at internat. arbitrn. on partition of Ottoman pub. debt, Geneva, 1925; mem., finan. comm., orthodox patriarchate of Jerusalem, 1924-28; one of counsel rep. govt. in parly. comm. re disturbances of 1929; ag. atty.-gen. on several occasions, 1922-31; legal draftsmen, Jan., 1932.

DRAYTON, SYDNEY RACKER.—B. 1884; engrnr.-operator, Trinidad, Apr., 1906; do., Penang, S.S., May, 1915; do., S'pore, June, 1923; do., Penang, Dec., 1927; senr. engrnr., wireless, S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1928.
DRAYTON, VIVIAN FORSTER.—B. 1883; ent. civ. serv., Grenada, 1900; ch. clk., govt. office, Aug., 1918; commr., Carriacou, Jan., 1932.

DRENNAN, JOHN MATTHEW, D.C.M. (1918).—B. 1891; ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin.; appd., after compet. exam., elk., 2nd div., civ. serv. and assigned to B. of T., 1910; appd., after compet. exam., clk., 3rd div., Crown Agents' office, Feb., 1912; on mil. serv., 1914-19 (France, Salonica, Palestine); asst. head, appts. dept., 1919; stores dept., 1922; shipping dept., 1930; dep. head, gen. dept., Jan., 1933; shipping dept., May, 1933.
DREW, DESMOND, B.A., M.B., Ch. (T.C.D.).—Med. off., Bechuanaland Prot., 1921.

DREW, HENRY WILLIAM.—ed. Diocesean Coll., Rondesbosch; clk., deeds office, Cape Town, 1893; asst. mag., King William's Town, Aug., 1907; Woodstock, Apr., 1908; Rietfontein dist., Gordonia, Aug., 1908; Williston, Nov., 1912; civ. commr., and mag., Uniondale, Mar., 1915; ag. crown pros., Dec., 1919; chmn., native affrs., deptl., coman., Dec., 1920; chmn., debt settlement bd., Aug., 1921; chmn., Rehoboth bastard gebiet bdry. coman., Feb., 1922; native commr. for territory, 1923; mem., admtr.'s advisory coun. as sp. rep. on native affrs., Aug., 1923; senr. grade mag., Newcastle, Natal, Mar., 1926; Rustenburg, Transvaal, Sept., 1928; sp. "C" grade mag., King William's Town, Nov., 1929; Port Elizabeth, June, 1931; sp. "A" grade mag., Cape Town, Oct., 1932.

DREW, RINGROSE CHARLES WILLINGTON, B.A., R.A.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. Monkton Combe Schol. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Dec., 1912; exec. engrnr., Dec., 1921; ag. state engrnr., Kelantan, Apr., 1928; senr. exec. engrnr., Apr., 1930; state engrnr., June, 1931.
DRIBBERG, ALLAN, K.C.—B. 1873; ag. puisme just., Ceylon, Jan., 1927; puisme just., Sept., 1927.

DROWER, E. M., C.B.E. (1923).—B. 1880; admitted solr., 1904; practised, consular ct., Cairo, 1904-6; Sudan bar, 1906-14; served European War, 1914-18; pres. of first instance, Hillah, 1918; Mosul, 1918-19; Basrah, 1919; asst. judicial adviser, Iraq, 1921; tempy. judicial adviser, 1921; govt. counsellor, 1921; adviser to miny. of just., 1922.

DRUMMOND, DAVID B.—B. 1898; ed. King Edward's Schol., Birmingham and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; M.A., 1925; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, July, 1923; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1925; ag. dist. commr., on five occasions, 1924-30; ptcr. sec. to gov. and ag. gov. on five occasions, 1924-29; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., Aug.-Sept. 1930; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., May-Oct., 1933; "Sierra Leone Studies".
DRYBURGH, NORMAN CHARLES.—B. 1890; printer, E. Africa Prot., 1912-17; govt. pr., Tanganyika Territory, 1917.

DRYBURGH, ALEXANDER MITCHELL (Edin.).—B. 1895; ed. George Hope Edin. and Edin. Univ.; cadet, F.M.S.; govt. rice agt., Tapah, Mar., 1920; int. offr., B. Padang, Sept., 1930; attd. Fed. Nov., 1920; supernum. offr., cln. V., 1924- IV., 1926; dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, 1930; offr., Raub, 1928; sec., req., Negri Sembilan, 1930; offr., cln. III., 1931; dist. offr., Selangor, June, 1933.

DU BOULAY, GEORGE CORNELL (1930).—B. 1883; appd. after compet. asst. audit elk., St. Lea., Apr., 1899; int. govt. office, Aug., 1899; 4th elk.; trans. customs, Mar., 1903; ag. 2nd elk., trans. Mar. to Nov., 1903; chief elk., post office, Jan., ag. harbr. master, St. Lucia, Sept., 1928; sec. and elk. to gov. of Seychelles, elk. acct. and asst. of printing, Nov., 1912; ag. admin., 1916; ag. treas. and mem. exec. and leg. ser., Mar. to May, 1919; asst. sec., col. ser., Gibraltar, Dec., 1919; senr. asst. col. sec., Leane, Jan., 1922; ag. col. sec. and govt. on various occasions, 1924-25; ch. asst. on Gold Coast, Jan., 1926; ag. col. sec., on various occasions; gov.'s dep. on various occasions; gov., Apr. to Sept., 1930 and Dec., 1931.

DU CANE, GEN. SIR JOHN, G.C.B., K.C.B. (1916), C.B. (1910).—B. 1865; R.A., 1884; capt., 1893; major, 1900; col., 1902; 1908; major-gen., 1915; gen., 1919; gen., 1926; D.A.A.G., Staff 1905-07; G.S.O., 1st grade, hqrs. of 1908-10; C.R.A., 3rd divn., 1911-12; S.O. Home Forces, 1913-14; served, S. Africa; despa., brevet lieu.-col., Queen's med.; s.a. King's med.; 2 clasps); European war, brig. III. Corps, 1914; maj.-gen., R.A., G.H.Q. sp. appl., min. of munitiona, 1916; comm. XV. Corps.; Br. rep. with Marshal Foch (7 time ment. in despa.); master-gen., arm. 1920-23; G.O.C.-in-ch., Western command, 24; G.O.C.-in-ch., Br. Army of Rhine, 1927; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Malta, 1927; ret., 1931.

DUCKWORTH, EDWARD HARLAND.—ed. Cheltenham Coll., City and Guilds Coll., Finsbury, and R. Coll. of Science, Lond.; A. R. C. Sc.; war serv. with Kleo. Engars., R.E. (T), 1915-18; France, 18; inspr., educn., S. Prov., Nigeria, Oct. author of "The Laboratory Worker" various articles in the "School Science Educ."

DUFF, DAVID, M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. Dubl.), D.P.H. (Irel.).—B. 1863; ed. P. Kanniskillen and Trin. Coll., Dubl.; classics; B.A. (nat. sci.) with 1st elk., bec. gold med.; med. offr., W.A.M.S. and per. Gold Coast, June, 1910; senr. med. offr., asst. dir., med. serv., Jan., 1927; called Gray's Inn, 1927; mem. bd. of exam. in languages, Apr., 1928; dep. dir., med. Aug., 1931; dir., med. and sanv. serv., Feb., 1932.

DUFF, SIR H. L., K.B.K. (1918); C. (1915).—B. 1872; appd. by F.O., asst. to Africa, 1897; resign. from serv., 1903; Apr., 1904; res., 1906; seconded, 1909-10; mem. of Inner Temple, Jan., 1909; int. const. law, 1909; ag. dep. gov., Jan., and Apr. to July, 1911; 1st grade req., Jul.

DUFF, Rt. Hon. Sir Lyman Poore, G.C.M.G. (34), P.C. (1919), B.A., LL.B. Toronto Univ.—1865; apptd. judge, sup. ct., British Columbia, 1874; judge, sup. ct. of Canada, 1906; cent. appeal judge, Mil. Serv. Act, 1917; dep. gov. of Ont., 1926; ch. just., Canada, 1933.

DUFF, STEUART MURRAY, B.A.—B. 1906; atty., Ceylon civ. serv., 1928; attd., Kandy kach., Jan., 1929; attd. Anuradhapura kach., Nov., 1930; office astt., Nuwara Ellya kach., May, 1930; pol. mag., Gampola, Oct., 1931.

DUFFY, HON. SIR FRANK GAVAN, K.C.M.G. (29), K.C., M.A., LL.B.—B. 1852; ed. at Doncaster Coll. and Melbourne Univ.; called to the bar, 1874; judge of the high ct. of Australia, Feb., 1913; ch. just., high ct., wealth of Australia, 1931.

DUGUID, JOHN.—B. 1892; served with 51st Highland Dvn., 1914-16; Rosyth and Aultbea, 1917-18; supt., post office, S.S., 1918; supt., post office, Singapore, 1921; supt., P. & T., Malacca, 1924; senr. acctnt., Singapore, 1929; contr., P. & T., F.M.S., Mar., 1930; ag. senr. acctnt., M.S., July, 1931.

DUKE, EDGAR MORTIMER, LL.B.—B. 1896; Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; Br. Guiana open schol. in maths., 1911; Andrews' entrance schol. in science, 1913 and Joseph Hume schol. in jurisprudence, 1914, Univ. Coll., London; Campbell Foster Prizeman of Middle Temple; sp. citizen in crim. law and procedure, 1916; LL.B. Lond., 1916; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1917; admitted to practise Br. Guiana, 1917 and Trinidad and Tobago, 1921; ag. regar., deeds, p.c., etc., Br. Guiana, in 1929, 1930 and 1932; dep. regar., deeds, sup. ct., etc., Jan., 1930; pol. mag. and mem., dist. licensing bds., Demerara and Essequibo, Sept., 1931; regar., deeds, 1933; author, "Law of Immovable Property in British Guiana."

DUMBRELL, HENRY JAMES EDWARD, B.A.—B.1885; ast mast., Heaton Lodge Sch., nr. Suddeanfield; mast. St. Bedes Coll., Estoort, Natal; headmtr., Ompurulo Native Training Coll.; aspr., schls., Natal, 1921; inspr., educn., Swaziland Natl. Prot. and Swaziland, 1928.

DUNCAN, CLAUDE WOODRUFF, C.B.E. (1930).—C-Ck., govt. sec.'s office, B. Guiana, May, 1890, to Feb., 1901; sub-inspr., B. Guiana police, 1st Mar., 1901; passed school of musketry, Hythe, 1901; passed school of instruction, Chelsea Barracks, 1905; dist. inspr. and J.P., May, 1907; ag. country inspr., Jan., 1908 to June, 1909; passed in Hindi, 1909; in command of special expeditions to Venezuela frontier, Wena-ru, Oct. to Dec., 1910, and May to June, 1911; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Mauritius, Sept., 1912; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol. and supt. of prisons, Nov. 1914 to June, 1915; comnr. of police, Malta, July, 1916; also inspr. of prisons, Oct., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1919; inspr.-gen. of police and director of prisons, Southern Provinces, Nigeria, June, 1919; relinquished appt. dir. prisons, S. Provs., Nov., 1921; inspr.-gen., N. Provs., in addn. to sub. stantive post, Oct., 1929.

DUNCAN, HAROLD HANDASYDE.—B.1885; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; hons. schl. med. hist.; M.A.; qualified in compet. exam. for appt as junr. olk., 1st cls., F.O. (placed 4th in 1908 and 5th in 1909); conf. sec. to London comte., Imp. Ottoman Bank for work relating to Turkish govt. finance and indu. enterprise in Near East from Dec., 1909; on sp. mission to Constantinople, 1912; served in army, Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1919; France and Italy; liaison offr. (intelligence A.) between Br. G.H.Q., Italy and Br. Mily. Mission, Comando Supremo from Aug., 1918; capt., Norfolk Yeomanry; called to bar, Inner Temple, May, 1919; S. Eastern circuit; temp. legal ast., Miny. of Health, 26th Apr., 1927; est., 15th Mar., 1928; 2nd ast. legal adviser, D.O. and C.O., 31st Mar., 1930; ast. legal adviser, do., 1st Apr., 1931; ag. legal adviser, Dec., 1931 to Mar., 1932 and Feb., 1933 to July, 1933.

DUNCAN, PATRICK, C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1870; ed. at Oxford, schol. Ball Coll.; 1st cls. class. mod., 1891; 1st cls. final class. school, 1893; Craven scholarship, 1890; olk., after compet. exam., incl. rev., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir A. Milner; prim. olk., 1898; treas., Transvaal, 1901; col. sec., 1st Dec., 1903; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; mem. I.C.C.; ag. lieut.-gov., Oct., 1906; M.L.A., for Yeoville; min. of interior, pub. health and educn., 1921-24.

DUNCAN, WILLIAM McFARLANE.—B. 1896; ed. Craigie Pub. Schol. and Acad.; certifs. in building constr., mechanics, mathematics, etc.; Caledonian rly., Perth, 1911; ast. engr., Tanganyika rly., Feb., 1921.

DUNCOMBE, F. A. C.—B. 1880; 4th olk., P.O., Bahamas, 1903; 2nd olk., C.S.O., 1904; olk., record office, 1909; comnr., 4th div., 1912; comnr., grade III., June, 1920; olk., grade III., regar.'s. office, July, 1927; ag. ast. regar.-gen., 1928 and from June, 1929.

DUNCOMBE, WALTER KEILALL.—Bahamas treasy. and cust., 1896-1900; Out Island comnr., 1900-6; transf. Lagos Col. as supervisor, cust., Jan., 1906; collr., cust., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1909; senr. collr. cust., Nigeria, Jan., 1920; dep. contr. cust., Dec., 1922; contr., Dec., 1929; mem. ex. and leg. couns.

DUNDAS, HON. C. C. F., C.M.G. (1934), O.B.E. (1923).—Ast. dist. comnr., E.A.P., Sept., 1908; dist. comnr., Aug., 1914; dist. polit. offr. (with hon. rank of major), German E. Africa, provisional admtn., Jan., 1916; senr. comnr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; ag. ast. ch. sec., June, 1924; ag. sec. for native affrs., 1926; col. sec. Bahamas, Apr., 1929; admtr., May to Nov., 1929, June to Aug., 1930 and from June, 1931; dep. gov., in 1931 and 1932; O.A.G., Aug. to Sept., 1932.

DUNEDIN, 3RD BISHOP OF (founded 1864), Rt. REV. ISAAC RICHARDS, M.A., D.D.—B. 1859; consec., 1920; ed. Wesleyan Coll., Taunton and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1882, M.A., 1885, D.D., 1921; deacon, 1882; priest, 1883; curate of St. Paul's, Truro, 1882-6; vicar, St. Mark's Remuera, N.Z., 1886-95; warden, Selwyn Coll., Dunedin, 1896-1900; vicar, Tuspeka, 1900-16; vicar, St. John's, Invercargill, 1916-20; canon of St. Paul's, Dunedin, 1896-1916; archdeacon of Queenstown, 1908-16; archdeacon of Invercargill, 1916-20.
DUNN, C. de S.—Served in South Africa, 1901-1902 (medal and five clasps); S.A.C., 1903-1906; camel constab., Somaliland, 1912.
DUNN, J. S., B.Sc.—B. 1902; ed. Secondary Schl., Blyth, Northumberland and Univ. of Reading; B.Sc. (Hons.), London and Reading; inspr., schla., Gold Coast Sept., 1926.

DUNSTAN, Sir WYNNDHAM ROWLAND, K.C.M.G. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—M.A. (Oxon.); LL.D. (Aberdeen); F.R.S.; ed. Bedford schl.; lec. and demonstr. of chem. Oxford, 1884-86; prof. of chem., Pharm. Soc., 1886-96; lec. on chem., St. Thomas' Hosp., 1892-1900; sec., chem. soc., 1903-5; vice-pres., 1903-6; pres. section of chem. and agric. science, Brit. Assoc., 1906; mem. of coun., Royal Soc., 1904-7; mem. of advisory comttee. on trop. agric., C.O., 1908, vice-pres., Internat. assoc. of trop. agric., 1907; pres., 1910; pres., Internat. Congress trop. agric., London, 1914; mem. comm. intell. comttee. Bd. of T., 1900-6; dir., scientific and tech. dept., Impl. Inst., 1896-1903; dir., Impl. Inst., from 1903 to 1923; reported on agric. resources of Cyprus for C.O. in 1904, and of Asia Minor in 1907 (reports presented to parlmt.); visited Ceylon at request of colonial govt., 1910, and again in 1913; visited Newfoundland at request of colonial govt., 1914; arranged and supervised govt. surveys in Ceylon, N. and S. Nigeria and Nyasaland (reports presented to parlmt.); author of numerous scientific papers in the Phil. Trans. and Proceedings of Royal Soc., chem. soc., etc., and of tech. reports relating to the utilisation of the resources of the colonies, including "Cotton Cultivation in the Brit. Empire and in Egypt," 1904, "Brit. Cotton Cultivation," 1908; edited "Tech. Reports and Scientific Papers," Impl. Inst., 1903; "Selected Reports," Impl. Inst., 1909 (presented to Parlmt.); "Reports on work of Impl. Inst., 1906-14" (presented to Parlmt.); "Report on present position of Cotton Cultivation," 1910; editor, Impl. Inst. Handbooks on Commercial Resources of Tropics; author of article, "Rubber," in last edition "Ency. Brit."

DUPESSIS, STEPHANUS HENDRIK.—B. 1885; mast., high ct., Windhoek (S.W.A.), Feb., 1920; asst. mast., sup. ct., Bloemfontein (O.F.S.), July, 1926; ditto, Capetown (C.P.), Dec., 1926; mast., sup. ct., Bloemfontein (O.F.S.), Oct., 1931.

DUPRE, Hon. MAURICE, K.C., LL.L., M.P.—B. 1888; ed. at Levis Coll.; called to bar, 1911; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1930; solr.-gen. in Bennett admin., Aug., 1930; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office, Aug., 1930.

DURANLEAU, Hon. ALFRED, P.C. (Can.), LL.L., K.C., M.P.—B. 1871; ed. at Ste. Marie de Monnier; Laval Univ.; mem., Laurier divn., Montreal, 1923-27; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1930; minister of marine in Bennett cabinet, Aug., 1930; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office, Aug., 1930.

DUSEK, ORMONDE THEODORE, B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and Univ. of London; B.A., London, 1909; mem. of convocation; senr. ast. mast., Alleyne's Grammar Schl., Uttoxeter, 1909-11; senr. ast. mast., High Schl., Malacca, 1912-14; prin., Malay Training Coll., Malacca, 1914-22; prin., Sultan Idris Training Coll., F.M.S., 1922; in admn., ast. dir. of educn. (Malay), and examr. in Malay, Mar., 1924.

DU TOIT, PETRUS JOHANN, Ph.D. (Zurich), Dr. Med. Vet. (Berlin), B.A. (Capo), F.R.S. (S. Africa). Senr. vetv. offr., vetv. research divn., Vryburg, Dec., 1919; sub-dir., vetv. educn. and research, Pretoria, July, 1920; dep. dir., vetv. educn. and research, Pretoria, July, 1921; dir., vetv. services, Pretoria, Apr., 1927; dean of faculty and prof., trop. diseases and protozoology, Univ. of S. Africa, 1927; visited Nigeria at request of C.O. to rept. on stock problems, 1927; del. to Imp. Agril. Research Conf., London, 1927; chmn., 6th Pan-Africa Vetv. Conf., Pretoria, 1929; dir., vetv. services and animal industry, 1929.
DU TOIT, STEPHANUS FRANCOIS, LL.B.—B. 1897; ed. Victoria Coll. Stellenbosch and Univ. of C.T.; translator, Union senate, 1929; gent. usher of black rod, 1928; elk. asst., 1930.

DUTTON, CHARLES LEONARD O'BRIEN.—B. 1877; ed. Ellesmere Coll. and Bradford Gram. Schl.; elk., Impl. mil. rlys., S. Africa, Dec., 1900; chief elk., fincl. bd. of rly. control, Transvaal and O.R.C., Jan., 1902; chief elk. to hut commr. for S. Africa, Nov., 1904; acted as impl. sec., Aug., 1913 to Feb., 1914, Nov.-Dec., 1917; ag. imp. sec., May-Nov., 1921; govt. sec., Bechuana land Prot., Nov., 1923.

DUTTON, ERIC ALDHelm TOLOUGH, O.R.E. (1930), M.A.—Ed., Hurstpierpoint, and Keir Coll., Oxford; served in Gallipoli as Maj. W Yorks Regt. (ment. in desp.); cler. asst. to asst. commr., Quathing, Basutoland, 1918-19; priv. sec. to gov. of Uganda, 1920-22; priv. sec. to gov. of Kenya and sec., high commr., Zanzibar, 1922-25; priv. sec. to gov., Kenya and high commr., transport, 1925; prin. asst. ch. sec., N Rhodesia, Nov., 1930; ag. ch. sec., Aug., 1931 and Apr. to Nov., 1932; gov't. dep., Apr.-May and July, 1932.

DUTTON, ERNEST GODFREY.—B. 1886; clk., Impl. mil. rlys., Apr., 1902; elk., high commr. for S. Africa, Apr., 1903 to May, 1916; Swazil. service, Apr., 1907; elk. to asst. commr., Masu, Basutoland, June, 1916; ch. elk., master of ct. ad regiar., July, 1919; ag. govt. sec., June to Dec., 1923 and Feb. to Oct., 1924; asst. commr., 1924.
DUTTON, STEPHEN BERNARD.—B. 1881; served in stores dept., C.S.A.R., 1902-3; served in Zululand rebellion, 1906; comptroller of stores, Basutoland, Oct., 1909.

DYE, W. H., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.D.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), cert., Lord S.H. and T.M. (Distinc.).—B. 1889; R.A.M.C., 1914-25; surg. specialist, 1918-20; N. Forces memorial meda. for research, 1924 and 1927; seconded, Nyasaland Aug., 1921-Apr., 1925; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

DYER-MELVILLE, SYDNEY WHITMORE, M.I.E.E., S.A.—B. 1885; ed. City of London schl.; ast. dir., elec. and wireless dept., Zambesi, Dec., 1911; ag. dir., Apr. to Nov., 1913, Oct., 1915 to Apr., 1916 and from Dec., 1919 to Feb., 1925; immigr. offr., Sept. to Dec., 1917; ag. director. Jan. to Dec., 1919; ag. dir., rly. elec. and wireless tel. depts. for various periods, 1925-32.

DYKE, HAMILTON WILLIAM, M.B., Ch.B., Glasgow, 1905.—B. 1881; ed. South Afric. Coll., Cape Town and Glasgow Univ.; house phys., Glasgow Royal Infirmary, house surg., Glasgow Western Infirmary, Glasgow Orthopaedic Inst. and Glasgow Royal Hosp. for sick children; med. offr., Maseru, Basutoland, 1913; temp. capt., R.A.M.C., 1915-19; served in France, 1915-19; S.M.O., S.A. Native Lab. Ctt., Rouen area, 1919; surg. spec., govt. hos., Jerusalem and govt. of Palestine, 1927; med. offr., Bechuana land Prot., 1929; auth. of "An Outbreak of Scurvy among South African Natives in Rouen," Lancet, 1917; "A case of Madura Foot in Basutoland," S. African Med. Jnl. KENNETH H., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.)— med. offr., Basutoland, 1924.

SIR JOHN EGAN, K.C.M.G. C.M.G. (1908).—M. Inst. C.E.; served in rly., India, 1890; recd. thanks of gov't., Khojak tunnel, 1892, Godavari 1900; services lent to C.O., 1st Sept., D.P.W., Northern Nigeria; in charge of Kano rly. from 1907; dir. of rlys. and Nigeria, 1912; ret. 1919.

SHIBLEY, C.B.E. (1928). O.B.E. (1922). 883; served throughout S. African war, 1899- 2nd cls. clk., gov't. office, Transvaal and July, 1902; 1st cls. clk., July, 1904; d. to Basutoland service and att. to of high commr. for S. Africa, July, 1908; clk., Dec., 1911, and ast. Impl. acctnt. 1915; chief clk., Apr., 1919; mem. of to enquire into establishments, etc., in land Prot., Basutoland and Swaziland, 2; ag. ast. Imp. sec., May-Nov., 1921 and Sept., 1922; ast. Imp. sec. 1923; ag. Imp. n 1925, 1927, 1929 and 1931-2; title changed ntive. sec. to high commr., 1933.

LIONEL, K.C.B. (1916); C.B. ); C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1866; ed. at Marl., s. of Gottingen and Paris, and Merton Coll., rd; ast. sec. to royal comsn. on Paris m., 1898-1900; ag. 2nd sec. of embassy, priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1902priv. sec. to lord pres. of the coun. (Earl rew), 1907; priv. sec. to S. of S. for the ies (Earl of Crewe), Apr., 1908; priv. sec. to f S. for the colonies (Mr. Lewis Harcourt), 1910; sec. to H.M. Office of Works,

ASTER, B. H. M., M.B.E., B.A. (Hons.) —B. 1893; ed. Christ's Coll., Finchley, R.N. Dn., 1917; Scot's Guards (lieut.); Ampleforth Coll., O.T.C., 1919-23; lwr., St. Mary's Coll., St. Lucia, 1923; dir., cn. and headmtr., Grenada, 1928; ag. col. sec. admnr., Grenada, Dec., 1930-Aug., 1931; local adviser, educn. comtee., 1931; dir., educn., Jamaica, Apr., 1932.

BERTHIE JAMES, F.I.C., F.C.S., O.B.R. (Milv. Div., 1919).—B.1883; ed. Dorchester Gram. Sohl., Finsbury Tech. Coll. (City and Guilds of London Inst.) and Birkbeck Inst.; ast. research chem., Impl. Inst., 1904-06; govt. chem., F.M.S., 1906-10; govt. agrl. chem., F.M.S. and S.S., 1910; del. to Internat. Rubber Congress and Exhibn., Batavia, 1914; recvd. thanks of the S. of S. and the Govt., F.M.S., for re-searches on rubber, 1918; 2nd lieut., gen. list, adjutant, Civ. Guard, Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1916; lieut., M.V.I.; O.C., M.V.I., Selangor, 1917; major, M.V.I., Jan., 1923; del. of F.M.S. and S.S. at 2nd Pan- Pacific Service Congress, Australia, July to Oct., 1923; Br. Empire Exhibn., Apr., 1924; O.C., Selangor Volunteer Corps, Jan.-Dec., 1926; mem., co-opern. bd., F.M.S., Aug., 1926; seconded for serv., Rubber Research Inst., Nov., 1926; del., rubber confce. and 4th Pacific serv. cong., Java, May 1929; mem., agrl. advy. comtee., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1930; dir., rubber research inst., Nov., 1930; author of "The Preparation and Vulcanisation of Plantation Rubber," "The Cultivation and Preparation of Camphor in F.M.S." contribs. on rubber researches to the Society of Chemical Industry, numerous articles on tropical agr., industries, etc., to the Agrl. Bulletin, F.M.S. and other journals.

EAVES, LIBUT.-COLONEL FREDERICK, D.S.O. T.A.(Res.), Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1881; admitted soll. (Eng.), 1908; 2nd-lieut., 5th King's Own Royal Regt.(T.F.),1908; served in France,1916-19; commnd. 6th King's Own at battles of "Second Ypres" and "Loos," 1915; 1/5th Loyal N. Lancs. at "Messines," 1917 and "Cambrai," 1918; 18th Cameronians at Escuat, 1918; 8th Scottish Rifles in Rhine Army and Ireland 1919; C.M.O.N., Ireland, 1921-22; D.S.O., 1916; ment. in desps. three times; ast. land offr., Hong Kong, 1922; called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1923, J.P., Hong Kong, 1923; ag. land offr., 1924 and 1927-32; ag. land offr., regisr. marriages (in adrn. tempy.), 1st pol. mag., regisr., trade marks and patents, off. rer., 1928.

EBDEN, WILLIAM SYDENHAM.—B. 1887; ed. Clifton and Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; class. tripes, 1909; cadet, S.S., 1911; 2nd lieut., Jan., 1915; lieut., Salonika Front; wounded and p. of w., Bulgaria; dist. offr., F.M.S., 1920; dep. contr., rubber expts., mag., Jahore, and recr., Alisagoff concession, in admn., July, 1924; regisr., sup. ct., Jahore and 1st mag., Jahore Bharu, Dec., 1925; ast. adviser, Segamat, Jan., 1926; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928; ast. treas., Penang, Iec., 1928; dist. offr., Upper Perak, Nov., 1930; offr., ubs. I.B., Apl., 1931; comsnr., lands, S.S., Mar., 1932.

ECCLES, WILLIAM HUNTER.—Ed. St. Paul's Sch., London; clk.; educn. office, Trinidad, 1st Apr., 1907; clk. to commrs. of currency, June, 1908; customs, Jan., 1911; temp. sub-inspr.of.pol., Nov., 1914 to Feb., 1915; on mily. serv., lieut. afterwards capt., 1915-19; returned to cust., Trinidad, July, 1919; on sp. mil. duty to B. Guiana and Trinidad, Aug. to Oct.; asst. recvr.-gen. Gambia, Oct., 1919; relinquished comm., with rank of capt., Sept., 1921; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Mr. G. Gwyn, Sept., 1921 and to Mr. C. R. M. Workman, Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; ag. clk., exec. coun., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; offr.-in-charge, posts and tels. dept., Feb. to July, 1924; offr.-in-charge, cust. on various occasions between 1923 and 1928; asst. comptr., cust., Sierra Leone, 1928; ag. comptr., cust., on various occasions, 1929-32; comptr., cust., 1932.
ECKBO, NILS BONNIVIK (M. F. Yale).—Offr. in ch., timber seasoning investigations, Pretoria, 1919; apptd. to perm. estab. as timber investigations offr., 1924; ch., Forest Products Inst., 1932.

EDGINTON, EDWIN.—B. 1886; sorting clk. and telegraphist, mp. serv., 1908; resigned, Jan., 1912; re-apptd., Dec., 1912; dist. surrv., posts and telegs., G. Coast, Oct., 1913; senr. dist. surrv., Feb., 1917; served as offr. in ch., posts and telegs., Br. Zone, Togoland, Nov., 1916 to Aug., 1917; postmsr., Cyprus, Feb., 1920; P.M.G., Trinidad, 1922; divnl. surrv., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, 1927; contr., ditto, 1930.

EDMONDS, B. D.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., 7th Aug., 1914 to 5th Dec., 1919; awarded Mily. Med.; temp. clk., C.O., 2nd Feb., 1920; apptd. after compet.exam., cler.offr., C.O., 5th Nov., 1921; clerical offr., higher grade, 3rd Feb., 1928.

EDMONDS, EDWARD REGINALD.—B. 1901; matric., London Univ., June, 1921; entered C.O., 1917; apptd. after exam., asst. clk., 23rd Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; clk., P.W.D., Uganda, 1926; higher grade cler. offr., C.O., 31st July, 1929.

EDMUNDS, HERBERT GLYN.—B.1900; ed. Swansea and Univ. Coll., London; L.M.S.S.A. (Lond.) 1930; med. offr., Falkland Is., June, 1931; lieut., Falkland Is. Def. Force, July, 1931; ag. prin. med. offr., Mar. to Oct., 1932.
EDMUNDSON, K., M.B., Ch.B. (Liv.).—B. 1903; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1928.

EDWARDS, MAJOR WILLIAM ARNOLD DONALDSON, M.C., F.S.I., F.R.G.S.—B.1882; ed. privately; served Boer War, srgt., 1st V.B., Queen's Own R.W. Kent Regt., (Queen's med., 3 bars); pupil, munic. eng. off., Sutton, Surrey, 1903-05; P.A.S.I., 1904; asst. supdt., royal survey dept., Siam, Dec., 1905; surrv., grade I, trig. br., survey dept., F.M.S., April, 1911; mil. ser., Sept., 1914; comm. temp. lieut., R.A., Nov., 1914; apptd. A.D.C., Nov., 1914; temp. capt., Aug., 1915; ag. G.S.O. III intell., 2nd. Army, Jan., 1916; regtl. duty, Mar., 1916; wounded, July, 1916; ag. major and comdr., 383 bat. 179 army brigade, R.F.A., May, 1917; M.C. (immediate award), Nov., 1917; wounded, July, 1918; asst. supdt., trig. br., survey dept., F.M.S. & S.S., Jan., 1919; demob. rank, major, June, 1919; topo. reconn., Eastern Pahang, 1920-23; capt., M.S.V.R., July, 1922; transf. topo. br., Jan., 1924; ag. supdt., Mar.-Dec., 1924; C.A.F.L.S. med., June, 1926; ag. sen. supdt., Nov., 1928; sen. supdt., topo. survey, F.M.S., Dec., 1928; major, M.S.V.R., Mar., 1929.

EDWARDS, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR ALFRED HAMILTON MACKENZIE, C.B. Served in 1st Dragoon Guards, Jan., 1883, to Apr., 1892; adjutant, Behar Light Horse Volunteers, India, Apr., 1892 to Apr., 1897; 1st and 5th Dragoon Guards and Imperial Light Horse, Apr., 1897 to Oct., 1900; Hazara (N.-W. Frontier, India, 1898; asst. prov. marshal (ment. in despatch medal and clasp); commanded Imperial Light Horse, S. African war, Nov., 1899 to June, 1900; asst. commr., Potchefstroom and Krugersdorp dists., June to Oct., 1900; commanded "A" division, S.A.C., Oct., 1900, to end of war (ment. in despatch); Queen's medal, 4 clasps, King's medal, 2 clasps; substantive lt.-col. in the army, dated 29th Nov., 1900; commdt., Transvaal volunteers; left for service, 1906; A.A.G., Northern com., India, 1906-7; chief constable, metrop. police, 1907-12; commdt.-gen., Rhodesian forces, 1912; ret. 1st Aug., 1923.

EDWARDS, CAPT. DAVID, M.A (1912). LL.B (Aberdeen), 1914.—Temp. offr., R. Arty., 1914-1920; served at home, N.W. Frontier of India, German E. Africa and Mesopotamia; Scot. solr., 1921; asst. dist. commr., Kenya, 1921; ag. regiar. sup. ct., Kenya, 1921; regiar. sup. ct., Kenya, 1924; called to Scottish bar, 1924; res. mag., Kenya, 1928.
EDWARDS, ERNEST JAMES.—B. 1886; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Nov., 1912; head mast., Wansai Eng. schl., 1928.

EDWARDS, WILLIAM MAXWELL.—Engineer diagrams, surrv.-gen.'s. office, Transvaal, Jan., 1903; professional asst., surrv.-gen.'s. office, Transvaal, Apr., 1912; surrv.-gen., O.F.S., 1922; do., Transvaal, Feb., 1929.

EDWARDS, WILLIAM STUART.—B. 1886; called to the bar, Ontario, Sept., 1909; loco offr., dept. of justice, Canada, Nov., 1910; sec. of dept. of justice, 1913; asst. dep. min. of justice, 1914; dep. min. of justice, 1924.

EGAN, W. J.—Ed. Montreal; Canadian gov't trade commr., Manchester, 1911 and later transv. to S. Africa; in ch., Wembley Exhibn., for dept. of trade and commerce, Canada, 1922-3; instr. Canadian gov't trade commrs. offices throughout the world, 1923; dep. min. of immigr. and colonization, 1923.

EGERTON, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G. (1906 C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1858; ed. Tonbridge Schl. cadet, S. Stiltns., Oct., 1880; asst. to the Chinese prot., Jan., 1881; mag., Singapore, Jan., 1881; passed final exam., Malay Gov., 1881; collr. of land rev., Penang and P.W., Jan., 1882; 2nd mag. of pol., Penang, May, 1883; also offr. assignee under the Bankruptcy Ordinance (Sept., 1882); a J.P. and coroner for S. Stiltns., and visiting justice for Penang; offr. in char., Surge Ujong, July, 1893; offr. assignee and regiar. deeds, Singapore, June, 1896; inspr. of prov. S. Stiltns., May, 1897; dep.-pres. of mun. commrs., Singapore, Mar., 1897; 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1897; 1st mag., Singapore, Oct., 1898; continued to act as res. coun.; res. Neg. Sect., Aug., 1902; high commr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1903; and gov't., Lagos, July, 1904; gov't. and comdr.-in-chief of the amalgamated colony; prof. of S. Nigeria, 1st Mar., 1906; represented African Colonies and Prots. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; gov't., B. Guinea, Mar., 1912; ret., Mar., 1917.

EGG, RICHARD TYRRELL.—B. 1886; called to bar, Inner Temple, June, 1913; admitted to practice before sup. ct., Br. Guiana, 1913; ag. mag., Oct., 1919; stip. mag., 2nd grade, 1920; ditto, 1st grade, 1924; ag. police judge, S.E. to Dec., 1925, May, 1926 to Feb., 1927 and Apr. to Nov., 1928; pol. mag., Gold Coast, May, 1928.

ELDER, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, O.B.E. (1925).—B.1881; ed. Reading and Edinburgh; graduated M.R.C.V.S., 1902; F.R.C.V.S., 1914; served in S. African war as civ. vet. surg. with army vet. serv., 1902-3; vet. offr., Swaziland admin., May 1903; prin. vet. offr., 1919; agr. offr., 1928.

ELIAS, DAVID HENRY, M.C.—B. 1882; G.W.R., Eng., May, 1899-May, 1902; S. African Rlys., May, 1902-Sept., 1914; S. African Mounted Rifles, German S.W. Africa, S. African Infy. Brig., France, M.C., Sept., 1914-July, 1919; S. African Rlys., July, 1919; supt. line, Kenya and Uganda Rly., Sept., 1922; ditto, Nigerian Rly., Sept., 1925; dep. gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., Sept., 1931; ag. gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., Dec., 1932.

ELKINS, MAJOR WALTER HENRY, O.B.E. (Mily.).—B. 1883; ed. Cirencester College Sch.; S. African and Rhodesia Rlys.; Mesopotamia Mil. Rlys.; reorganisation rlys. Trans. Caucasus; dep. dir., rly. transport, S. Russia, 1919; dep. traffic man., F.M.S. Rly., 1928; ag. traffic man., Dec. 1928 to Aug. 1929; divil. supt., F.M.S., Rh.s., Gemas, Sept., 1932.
ELLENBERGER, VIVIAN FREDERIC.—Clk. to res. comsnr., Bech. Prot., 1915; passed 2nd grade Seewana exam., 1919; sub-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1920; 1st grade clk., Apr., 1928.

ELLIS, JOHN AUGUSTUS GILBERT.—B. 1881; Lieut. reserve of officers; ed. privately and at Edin. Univ.; enlisted in C.I.V. (M.I.) for S. African war, Jan., 1900; 2nd lieut. R. Scots Fus., Aug., 1900; dangerously wounded at battle of Frederikstad, Oct. 1900; 1st lieut., May, 1903; served with 8th and 4th regts. of M.I., S. Africa, 1903-4; intell. dept., S. Africa, 1904-1906; rejoined 2nd R. Scots Fus., Salisbury Plain, June, 1906; M.I. certif.; musk. certif., Hythe, 1907; pub. health certif., Edin. Univ., 1908; asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., 14th Apr., 1909; dist. comsnr., Oct., 1917.

ELLIOTT, COLIN FRASER (Fraser), K.C., B.A., Sc., C.E.—Solt., income tax br., dept. of finance, Canada, July, 1919; asst. comsnr., income tax dept. of national rev., May, 1926; comsnr., income tax July, 1932.

ELLIOTT, FRANK FIELD.—Tempy. asst. regisry. survr., O.F.S., Mar., 1903; asst. regisry. survr., May, 1903; chief draughtsman and comptlr., Apr., 1912; add. prof. asst., Apr., 1914; prof. asst., Sept., 1917; survr.-gen., O.F.S., Nov., 1930; do., Cape Prov., Dec., 1931.
ELLIOTT, PHILIP JOHN.—B. 1892; entd. crown agents' office, Dec., 1911; asst. head, dept., July, 1927; dep. head, stores dept., Nov., 1933.

ELLIS, CAPT. FREDERICK ROBBINS, M.C., Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1924.—B. 1891; ed. Blundell's Sch., Tiverton; 2nd lieut., 4th batt., Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy.: M.C.; served in Mesopotamia with 2nd Dorset Regt., 1915-16; prisoner of war on capitulation of Kut-el-Amarah, 1916; escaped from custody, 1918; W.O. staff, 1919; Siberia, 1919; passed solrs. final exam.; junr. adminis. offr., S. Leone, 1920; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Jan. to Apr., 1921; J.P., 1921; ag. asst. col. sec., 1921; ag. comsnr. pol., 1921; ag. dist. comsnr., headqrs. dist., 1921-23; dist. comsnr., Aug., 1928.

ELLIS, JOHN MEDICOTT, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1895; ed., Haileybury and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schl.); served, Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1914-19; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, June, 1919; passed exam. in mod. Greek, March, 1920 and Turkish, July, 1921; ch. asst. sec., July, 1922; ag. ch. sec., on various occasions, 1923-25; ag. col. sec., Falkland Is., Sept., 1926 to Aug., 1927; col. sec., Aug., 1927; ag. gov., Apr. to June, 1927, June, 1928 to Jan., 1929 and Feb. to June, 1931; local audr. from May, 1930 to Feb., 1931; registr. gen. and mag. from July, 1930 to Jan., 1931.
ELLIS, HON. WM. J.—M.H.A., Ferryland, Newfoundland, 1904; mayor of St. John's, 1912; M.L.C., 1917; M.E.C., 1917; minister without portfolio, 1918.
ELLISON, F. O'BRIEN, M.D., B.S., B.A.O.—B. 1879; prof. of physiology and registr., Ceylon Med. Coll., 22nd Oct., 1923.
ELPHINSTONE, SIR H. G., BART.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, June, 1921.

ELPHINSTONE, SIR LANCELOT HENRY, Kt. Bach. (1931), K.C., M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1879; ed. Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Lincoln's inn, 1904; atty.-gen., B. Honduras, 1913; solr.-gen., Trinidad, 1919; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1921; puisne judge, designate S. Sltlnta, 1924; ag. solr. gen., Ceylon, June, 1924; atty.-gen., Oct., 1924; ch. just., F.M.S., Apr., 1929; ret., 1932.

EMBURY, BRIG.-GEN. HON. JOHN FLETCHER LEOPOLD, C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1916), K.C.—B. 1875; ed. Toronto Univ. and Osgoode Hall, Toronto; served European War, 28th Can. Inf., 13th Inf. Bde., 2nd Inf. Bde., Can. Contingent; Br. G.H.Q., France (Canadian sec.), 1914-19; ment. in despatches three times, C.B., C.M.G.; judge of King's Bench, Saskatchewan, 1918.
EMBURY, W. E.—B. 1898; on mil. serv., Oct., 1916 to Dec., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., Dec., 1919; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., 8th Nov., 1921.
EMERSON, HON. CHARLES H., K.C.—B. 1864; M.H.A., Newfoundland; elected for Burgeo and La Poile, 1900, Fortune Bay, 1908 and 1909; mem. exec. coun. without portfolio, 1909; registr., supreme ct., 1917.

EMMENS, JOHN HOWARD.—B. 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 11th Mar., 1901; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918; deptl. asst., 26th Jan., 1922; promoted ag. asst. prin. under clause 51, 4th Sept., 1929.

EMPSON, CHARLES.—B. 1898; ed. Harrow and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; R.F.A. (S.R.); joined Iraq civil admin., Aug., 1930; addnl. sec. under sec. to high commr., Sept., 1921; under sec. (consular), July, 1922; H.M.'s consul, Bagdad, Dec., 1924; consular sec., July, 1927.

ENGLAND, WILLIAM WENTWORTH.—Entd. govt. ser., Antigua, Feb., 1907; clk. to registr.-gen. of births and deaths; ag. treasy. offr., Montserrat, Jan. to Dec., 1908; ag. clk. to comsnr., Virgin Is., Mar. to Aug., 1909.
ENGLISH, JOSEPH SANDYS, B.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), L.M. (Rotunda Hosp., Dub.).—B. 1890; prof. of midwifery and gynaecology, King Edward VII. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, Jan., 1922.

ENGLISH, PATRICK THOMAS.—B. 1902; 5th clk., treasy., Montserrat, 1921; clk. to mag. and regtr., 1924; ag. offr. in ch., prison, 1924; 3rd clk., treasy., 1925; ag. regtr., sup. cl., 1925; jnr. clk. col. sec.'s office, Antigua, 1927; ag. clk. of couns., 1928 and 1930; ag. clk. to admstr. and clk. of couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1930; ag. asst. col. sec., Leeward Is., and clk., fed. couns., 1930; clk. and cust. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., 1931; ag. acct., 1931-33; ag. sec. to govt., June-Aug., 1933.
ENGLISH, ROBERT.—B. 1888; asst. printer, Tanganyika Territory, 1919; senr. dist. do., 1920; govt. printer, Sierra Leone, 1930; do., Nigeria, 1932.

EPHRAIM, HERBERT COLEMAN, K.C., LL.B. (Manitoba).—B. 1890; ed. public and high schls., Arnprior and Carleton Place, Ont., and Univ. of Manitoba; barrister-at-law, Manitoba, 1922; K.C. (Dom.), 1929; sec. treas., Canadian Bar Assoc., 1919-33; dean, Manitoba Law Schl., 1929-33; bencher, Law Soc. of Manitoba, 1928-33; under-sec. of state and dep. regiar-gen., Canada, 1933.

ERAUT, WALTER, M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1874; ed. Aske's Haberdashers' Company's Schl. and Imperial Coll. of Science; engrnr. asst., Crown Agents, Mar., 1901; asst. head, engrnr. contracts dept., 1913; ch. engrnr., contracts, 1922.

ERNST, NOEL EDWARD.—B. 1891; cadet, local division, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1912; attd., Ratnapura kach., May, 1912; Trincomalee kach., Mar., 1913; office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., July, 1913; pol. mag., Jaffna and Kayta, Nov., 1914; pol. mag., Avisawella, June, 1915; asst. comsrr., excise, Nov., 1916; pol. mag., Gampola, Mar., 1918; office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1918; pol. mag., Galle, Oct., 1924; dep. comsrr., stamps, Feb., 1928; comsrr., do., July, 1931; dist. judge, Ratnapura, 1933.

ESPEUT, CLAUDE VIVIAN ARMIT., M.Inst. C.E.—B. 1876; ed. St. Paul's Schl., Lond. and Crystal Palace Schl. of Engnr.; served, P.W.D., Jamaica; asst. engrnr., Lagos, govt. rly., 1900-01; dist. engrnr., Gold Coast Govt. rly., 1901; exec. engrnr., P.W.D. E. Africa Prot., June, 1905; ag. asst. D.P.W., 1908 and 1909; D.P.W., Uganda Prot., 1911; Uganda Volunteers, 1914-19; hon. lt.-col., 1916; miny of munitions while on leave, 1917; D.P.W., Jamaica, Aug., 1921; mem., leg. coun., D.P.W., F.M.S., Mar., 1925; mem., leg. coun.; chm. electrical bd. and others; mem., rly. bd., immigr. comm. and others; ret. July 1928; re-employed as eng. in ch., colonial development fund wks., St. Lucia, Oct., 1930.

ESPITALIER-NOEL, JOSEPH GEORGES.—B. 1894; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, May, 1916; ag. dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, 1919; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Sept., 1921; addul. subst. procureur et advoc. gen., Dec., 1922; ag. subst. procureur et advoc. gen. on various occasions, 1924-29; ag. procureur et advoc. gen., Mar., 1929; ag. mast. and regiar. supt. ot., Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1932 and Sept., 1932; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct. in 1932 and 1933.

EVANS, ARTHUR ANTHONY, O.B.E., M.C., M.I.E.E., Lieut., Col., R. Corps of Signals (Res.).—Ed. Brodribb Acad., Carmarthen and Univ. of Wales, Cardiff; served in S. African War (Queen's med., 4 clasps); engrnr. branch, G.P.O., 1898-1914; R.E., signals, Gallipoli, Egypt; asst. dir., Army signals and ch. signal olfr., Palestine and Syria, 1914-20; four ment. in despa., Order of White Eagle, 5th cls. with swords, O.B.E., M.C., Br. and Allies war med., "1914-15" Star; ch. engrnr., Palestine posts and tels., 1920.

EVANS, BENJAMIN DAVIES, F.R.A.S.—B. 1887; computer, Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1902; junior asst., Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1909; lst asst., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1912; on military service, 1917 to 1919; ag. ch. asst. for various periods in 1915, 1916, 1920, 1921, 1925, 1926 and 1929; asst. dir., 1932.

EVANS, EDWARD WALTER, B.A. (Oxon.), C.M.G. (1931).—B. 1890; ed. Marlborough and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; 1st class mok., 1st class lit. hum.; asst. dist. comm., Zanzibar Prot., Feb., 1915; second for mil. ser., June, 1917; temp. com. K.A.R.; ag. 1st asst. sec., Zanzibar, 1919-20; asst. sec., Uganda, Jan., 1921; senr. asst. sec., June, 1923; ag. asst. ch. sec., July, 1924; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, Apr., 1925; asst. col. sec., Jan. to Mar., 1927; col. sec. Ex-Honduras, June, 1927; admstd. govt. on two occasions; col. sec., Bermuda, 1929; col. sec. Mauritius, Apr., 1932; admstd. govt., Aug., 1932 to Apr., 1933.

EVANS, FRANK DUDLEY, M.Inst., C.E.—B. 1883; asst. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S. 1907; main adv. bd., 1911; exec. engrnr., grade III, 1913; asst. regt. srgt. maj., M.S.V.R., 1917; 2nd heus. co., 1917; engrnr., malaria adv. bd., 1919; ment. in despa., 1919; lieut., M.S.V.R., 1919; asst. malaria engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1921; awarded Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. med., 1921; ag. D.P.W., 1924; ag. state engrnr., Negri Sembilan, 1924; ag. capt., M.S.V.R., 1924; visited and malaria wks., Palestine and Italy, 1926; D.D.P.W., Nigeria, 1926; ag. D.P.W., and M.L.C., Nigeria in 1926 and 1928; malarial comm., health sect., League of Nations, Geneva, 1928.

EVANS, SIR FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (Sir), K.C.V.O. (1912), C.M.G. (1881), C.V.O. (1881), C.B.E. (1918); Comdr. of Order of Isabella la católica (Spanish), 1912; Knight Comdr., ditto, 1918; 1849; entered the civil ser., adm. Somerset house, Dec., 1867; transf. to sec.'s dept. at Whitehall, Jan., 1872; asst. col. and treas., S. Leone, May, 1878; priv. sec. to S. Rowe, K.C.M.G., Feb., 1873, to Feb., 1881; elk. of legis. coun., Sept., 1879; ag. col. dep., 1873 to Feb., 1880; ch. sec. and ch. of staff to Sir S. Rowe during threatened hostil. with Ashanti, 1881; asst. col. sec., G. Coast, inc. ag. collr., cust. and treas., Oct., 1882, to Jan., 1883; ag. col. sec., Aug. to Dec., 1883 and other occasions; elk. legis. coun., Feb., 1883 to Apr., 1884; dep. gov., Lagos, July to Aug., 1883 and May, 1885 to Jan., 1886; dep. gov., G. Coast, Sept., 1883 and Apr., 1885; col. sec., Lagos, Jan., 1886; ag. adminstr., July, 1886; sec., G. Coast, 1887; Leeward Isl., 1888; Jamaica, 1895; ag. 1st cl. elk., C.O., Feb., 1896; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1901; ag. gov. on seven occasions, ret., 1914; ag. col. sec., Gibraltar, in 1915 and 1917.
EVANS, H. G.—Asst. dist. commntr., K.A.F., Apr., 1913; 1st grade admstive, offr., 1925.

EVANS, IVOR HUGH NORMAN, M.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1886; asst. curator and ethnographical sec., F.M.S. museums, Apr., 1912; ag. dir. museum, F.M.S., Jan., 1924; game warden, Selangor, Apr., 1925-Oct., 1926; ethnographer, F.M.S. museum, Aug., 1926; game warden, Penang, Oct., 1926-Aug., 1927.

EVANS, HON. SIR JOHN WM., KT., KBE (1926), C.M.G. (1906)—B. 1855; M.L.A. for Kingborough, Tasmania, since 1896; warrant master warden of marine bd. of Hobart for many years; premier and min. of educn., Tasmania, July, 1904 to Oct., 1905; premier and treas., Oct., 1905, to May, 1906; premier and ch. sec., May, 1906 to June, 1909; speaker.

EVANS, LEONARD WINNE, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., B.Sc., certif. L.S.T.M. (with distinc.), B. 1891; med. offr., gen. hosp., Sitkang, Jan., 1922; med. and health offr., P.W. north and central, June, 1923; ch. med. offr., Kelantan, Jan., 1929.

TANS, SAMUEL, Hon. LL.D., Witwatersrand.—B. 1859; pvt. sec. to finan. adviser, Transvaal govt., 1883; ch. contr., Egyptian coast def. serv., 1886; inspr.-gen., Imp. Ottoman leg., Constantinople, 1889; man., Turkish To. Regie, 1892; during Anglo-Boer War served as adj. of Lord Roberts from Kroonstad to Johannesburg, and on staff of mil. gov. of Johannesburg; comnstr.; medal, 3 bars; pub. debt comnstr., n. of S. Africa.
TANS, SAMUEL ALBAN, M.R.C.V.S.—B.; ed. Mountainjoy Schi. and R. Vety. Coll., in; war serv., R.A.F., cadet, 1918; vety. Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1921; asst. pathologist, May, 1926.

ELYN, EDWARD A.—2nd elk. to regisr., St., 1899; govt. off., Sandy Point, Apr., 1903; off., Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, June, 1905; regisr.'s off., May, 1906; regisr. of births and marrs., St. George's parish; ag. ch. clk., regisr.'s Antigua, Dec., 1910, to July, 1911; ag. r. and provost marshal for several periods, 1913; regisr. and provost marshal and regisr.'s, St. Kitts-Nevis, Dec., 1922; prov. off., Leg., coun., Oct., 1923.
ELYN, EDWARD A. (jr.)—B. 1904; ed. St. Grammar Schi.; supernumy. rev. offr., 1922; elk., post office, 1927; dep. harbour. and treas., 1928; govt. offr., Eastern Dist., Jan., 1930.

ELYN, MERRITT.—2nd elk. to pres., St., Dec., 1878; ch. clk., treas., Nevis, 1881; reas., Nevis, July, 1882-1883; rev. offr. in Treas., Nevis treas., 1883; prot. of imigrants, 1883; land and house tax comnstr., 1892; ag. Nevis, 1904-1958-6 and 1912; water comnstr., 1901; chmn., quarantine bd., Nevis, 1911.

WART, F. K.—B. 1902; ed. Botelar Schi., Exeter, and Rossall Schi., Williams Mem., 1902; schol., Exeter Coll., Oxford, 1920; pres., S.C., 1923; B.A., 1923 (chem. pt. 1); B.S., 1923; 2nd chem., pt. 2, 1924; mem., Middle Temple, 1925; asst. mast., King's Coll., Hongkong, 1926; M.A., 1927.

UBERS, GEORG VON WOLFING.—B. 1887; Soy's High Schi., Beaufort West, Victoria Schi., Stellenbosch, Univ. of Amsterdam and London's Coll., London; B.A. (Cape); M.A., D. (London); prin., Graaf Reinet Coll. and Hoët skool, 1919-26; mem., Cape provin. educn. comm., 1926-27; inspr., schls., 1927; under sec., Aug., 1927; author, various works on S. African hist. and travel.

RE-SMITH, CAPT. ST. JOHN ROBERT, M.C.—B. 1888; ed. St. Edmunds Coll., Ware; served R.A.S.C., France and Germany, 1915-19; ey., 1919-20, ment. in desps.; asst. dist. nr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. comnstr., 1925; sec., native affrs., 1931.

ECHIEL, PERCY HUBERT, C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1875; B.A. Bombay, 1888 (at. 14); B.A., on, 1894; major sohrl., Trin. Coll., Camb., 4th wrangler and B.A., Camb., 1897; apptd. compet. exam., 2nd cl. elk., C.O., 11th Oct.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1903; to crown agts. for the colonies, May, 1905; of Fair Wages Advisory Comte., 1909-1920; Colonial Economic Development Comte., 1920; sec. and mem., E. African Currency bd., (resigned secretariatry 1921); crown agt., colo. coll., Dec., 1920; dir. of colonial scholars, rep. of C.O. on exec. coun. of Br. Empire bd., 1921-26; chmn., Palestine currency bd., 1921; mem., Hong Kong currency comn., 1931.

FAHEY, FRANCIS JOSEPH.—B. 1878; ed. St. Michael's Coll., Toronto, Canada; printer; arrived S. Africa, 1898; mem., prov. coun., Natal, 1920-23; prov. exec., 1924; joined bd. of trade and industries, Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1924; re-apptd., Apr., 1930; chmn., Mar., 1932.
FAIR, ARTHUR, LL.B. (N.Z.), K.C.—B. 1886; ed. state schls., Nelson Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; crown sohrl., 1921; prin. law offr., 1923; solr.-gen., 1926.

FAIR, LIEUT.-COL. J. G., D.S.O.—B. 1864; entd. Army (21st Hussars—now Lancers) 1886; capt., 1892; major, 1900; staff offr. to Gen. Gatacre during Athara campaign, 1898 (desps., Khedive's medal and clasp); with 21st Lancers at Omdurman (Queen's medal, clasp, 4th cls., Modjidiyah); adjut., cavalry depot, Canterbury, 1899-1900; S. African war, 1901-2 (desps., D.S.O., Queen's medal, 5 clasps); commanded div. S.A.C., Eastern Transvaal, 1901-4; ditto, O.R.C., 1904-8; nom. mem. of I.C.C., 1906; res. comnstr. and comdt.-gen., Southern Rhodesia, 1908-1911; dep. asst. dir. of remounts since 1911.

FAIRBAIRN, SIR GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1928).—B.1856; ed. Geelong Grammar Schi. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; M.L.A. for Toorak, Victoria, Oct., 1903 to Sept., 1906; M.H.R. for Fawkner, 1906-13; Commonwealth Senator for Victoria, May, 1917; agt.-gen. for Victoria, 1924-27.
FAIRBAIRN, H., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1898; milly. serv., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925.

FAIRBURN, HAROLD.—B. 1884; pol. prob., F.M.S., Oct., 1904; asst. comnstr., pol., F.M.S., June, 1908; regisr., crimin., F.M.S. and S.S., Jan., 1913, Oct., 1913 and Jly., 1915; asst. comnstr., pol., ch. pol. offr., Pahang, Aug., 1917; dep. comnstr., pol., F.M.S., Jan., 1921; in charge, intell. branch, F.M.S., Apr., 1921 and Feb., 1923; inspr.-gen., pol., S.S., Jly., 1925.

FAIRCOUGH, F.R.—B. 1886; apptd., after compet. exam., elk. of the 2nd div. of the civil serv. and assigned to C.O., Aug., 1914; on military service from 13th Dec., 1915 to 9th March, 1919; priv. sec. to Sir James Stevenson, 12th Aug., 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 7th July, 1923; staff offr., 15th July, 1933.

FAIRLEY, WILLIAM, B.A., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1911; ed. St. Paul's Schi. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.), B.A., (mech. science tripes), 1913; 6th Bn. E. Surrey Regt., 1914; 1st K.A.R., 1917-19; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Kenya, Dec., 1919; ag. exec. engnr., Mar. to Dec., 1920; dist. engnr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, Feb., 1922; ag. asst. D.P.W., Mar. to Oct., 1923 and 14th Mar., 1924; exec. engnr., P.W.D., Kenya, Aug., 1925.
FAIRWEATHER, C.—Apptd. govt. printer, Gold Coast, 19th Oct., 1907.

FAIRWEATHER, EUGENE ERNEST, M.A., LL.B.—B. 1884; ed. at King's and Acadia Unv. and Dalhousie Law Schi., N.S.; asst. sohrl., dept. of rly. and canals, 1912; sohrl., 1914; counsel, 1919; asst. gen. counsel, Can. nat. rly., 1922.

FAIRWEATHER, WILLIAM GEMMEL, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1886; ed. at Univ. of St. Andrews (B.Sc.), A.M.I.C.E.; asst. surv., N.W. Rhodesia, Sept., 1910; ch. asst. surv. and examiner of diagrams, Nov., 1917; ch. surv. on various occasions, 1916 to 1920; ch. surv., 1920; dir. surveys, Oct., 1924; mem., leg. coun., 1929-32, 1933.

FALCONER, JOHN.—B. 1898; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll. and Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., Aberdeen (1st cls. hona.); cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1922; asst. collr., land rev., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1924; dep. regar., titles, Selangor, June, 1924; second asst. adviser, Muar, Feb., 1926; offr., cls. IV., Apr., 1928; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Aug., 1930; do., Raub, July, 1931; offr., cls. III., Apr., 1933.

FALK, EDWARD MORRIS, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Bradford Grammar Schol., on the Continent and at Victoria Univ.; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, 1907; West African G.S. Medal, 1909; dist. comsner., 2nd grade, 1913; interned as prisoner of war in Germany, 1914; escaped and retld. to England, 1916; resumed duty, Nigeria, 1915; res., 1923; staff grade, 1929; ag. admstr. of col., 1931.

FALLOWFIELD, J. B., M.I.C.E., etc.—Asst. engnr., P. W. D., Gold Coast, 1915; exec. engnr., grade II, 1919; dep. prov. engnr., 1921; exec. engnr., 1924; res. constrn. engnr., Cape Coast water works, 1925; transf., Nigeria, P.W.D., as senr. exec. engnr., 1928.

FALLS, NORMAN.—B. 1881; sec. to ch. just., S.S., May, 1905; attlt. offr., N. Semilan, June, 1907; supervisor, cust., Feb., 1912; supt. cust., F.M.S., Oct., 1923; senr. supt., trade and cust., F.M.S., Jan., 1932.

FARFAN, JOSE MANUEL.—B. 1876; 6th clk., secretariat, Trinidad, Apr., 1895; 5th clk., Aug., 1897; 4th clk., Feb., 1902; 3rd clk. and sec. to cen. road., Feb., 1906; 2nd and conf. clk., secretariat, Aug., 1915; 2nd asst. col. sec. and supt. of govt. printing, Apr., 1920; ag. 1st asst. col. sec. on several occasions since 1920; 1st asst. col. sec., Jan., 1930; ag. dep. col. sec., in 1931 and 1932.

FARMER, FRANK.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Dec., 1914; asst. clk., Apr., 1917; on mily. serv., Mar., 1917 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted, under order in council of 1910, clk. of the 2nd div., Feb., 1920; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Dec., 1924; cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.
FARQUHAR, EDWARD ALLAN.—Comsnr., S. Aust. harbours bd., 1914; chmn., 1924.

FARQUHAR, GEORGE NEIL, M.C.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Dec., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Mar., 1920; attd. to secretariat, Apr., 1920; ditto, Puttalam Kach., June, 1921; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Mar., 1922; ditto to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., 1925; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1926; sec. land comsnr., Aug., 1928; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1930; clk. to leg. coun., Mar., 1930; clk. to state coun., July, 1931; contr., establishments, July, 1932.

FARQUHARSON, ROBERT ALEXANDER, M.A. (Oxon), M.Sc., F.G.S., A.O.S.M.—B. 1883; ed. Boys' High Schol., Otage Univ., N.Z. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; senr. schol., N.Z. Univ., 1905; dipl. in mining engnr., 1905; 1st cls. hona., geology and 2nd cls. physical science, N.Z., 1906; Rhodes schol., N.Z., 1906; B.A., Oxford, hona. in geology and mineralogy, 1908; M.A., 1912; govt. petrologist, mines dept., W. Australia, 1911-22; lent at request of C.O. to Somaliland govt. to furnish rept. on geological and mineral resources of Prot., 1923; govt. geologist, Somaliland, 1923-26; recd. thanks of S. of N. for gr. map of Somaliland; dir., agr. and geologist, Somaliland, 1926; geol. advr., Br. Somaliland-Ethiopia bndry. comsnr., 1932-33; in ch., water boring scheme under Col. Development Act, 1932-33.
FARR, FRANK JAMES, M.B., Ch.B., L.D.S., D.M.R. & E.—B. 1900; radiologist, med. dept., Hong Kong, 1928.

FALKELL, CAIT, WILFRID JEROME, M.C., M.A., R. of O.—B. 1882; ed. Ushaw; Hymers; Jesus Coll., Cambridge; fellow of Jesus Coll., 1906; student, Br. Sch. of Archaeology, Athens, 1906; Munich Univ., 1907; on staff of Rugby Schol. and Haileybury Coll., 1911-19; served in European war, 1914-19; civ. educn. offr., Iraq, Sept., 1919; officiating dir. and adviser, miny. of educn., Iraq, 1921; senr. educn. offr., Palestine, Apr., 1923; asst. dir., educn., Apr., 1927; dep. dir., Oct., 1929; ag. dir., June to Aug., 1927 and July, 1928 to Jan., 1929; Br. mag. for riot cases, Sept. to Oct., 1929.

FARRELL, WILLIAM JOSEPH HENRY.—Ent. post office, C. of G.H., 1889; tel. dept., S. African Repub., 1895-1902; priv. sec. to min. of just., Union of South Africa, 1910; priv. sec. to min. of posts and teles., 1912; priv. sec. to min. of lands, 1913; dept. of lands, 1916; asst. sec. to prime min. and asst. clk. of exec. coun., 1924; under sec. to prime min., under sec. for external affrs., and clk. exec. coun., 1927.

FAULKNER, ODIN TOM, C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1890; ed. St. Albans and Caius Coll., Cambridge (Schol.), B.A. (1st cls., nat. sci. tripus, 1911); dipl., agr., 1912; mycologist and azil. expert to Rubber Growers Assocn., Malaya, 1912-14; dep. dir., agr. to govt. of Punjab 1914-21; dir., agr., Nigeria, 1921.
FAULKNER, S.N., O.B.E. (1918).—Asst. auditor E.A.P., Jan., 1909; senr. asst. auditor, Apr., 1915; dep. ch. acctnt., Uganda Rly., 1917.
FAWCETT, J. F. ST. A.—Depuy regar., high clk., E.A.P., 1910 regar., high court, Feb., 1915; mast. and registr., sup. ct., Sierra Leone, July, 1921; ch. regar. and sheriff, Gold Coast, May, 1924.
FAZAN, S. H., O.B.E. (1930).—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; dist. comsnr., Kenya, Feb., 1920.

FEARON, FRANK ST. CLAIR.—Ed. Charterhouse (schol.) and Exeter Coll., Oxford exhibt., R.A. (litt. hum.), 1902; clk., law dept., Cape Colony, 1903; asst. regar., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1908; 1st asst. regar. and taxing offr., 1923; regar. and taxing offr., sup. ct., Johannesburg, 1927; ditto, Grahamstown, 1930.

FEETHAM, RICHARD, C.M.G.—B. 1874; ed Marlborough and New Coll., Oxford; mem. Inner Temple; called to bar, 1899; deputy town clk., Johannesburg, 1902-3; town clk., Johannesburg, 1903-5; admitted advoc. Transvaal, 1903; legal adviser to high comsnr. for S. Africa, 1907-10 and 1912-13; K.C., 1919; mem., leg. coun., Transvaal, 1907-10; mem., H. of A., Union of S. Africa, 1915-23; lieut., 1st battn. Cape Corps., 1916-19; mem., Southborough comte., on Indian Reforms and chmn. functions comte., 1918-19; mem., India Office comte., on Govt. of India Bill, 1919; ag. pres., sup. ct. of Swaziland, 1922-23; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal Prov. Divn., Aug., 1923; judge pres., sup. ct., Natal prov. divn., May, 1930.

FEHILY, JOSEPH PATRICK, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. N.U.I., F.A.C.S., D.P.H., Queen's Univ., Belfast.—B. 1892; ed. Blackrock Coll. and Univ. Colls., Cork and Dublin; late temp. surg., R.N. and capt., R.A.M.C.; res. med. offr., Br. Hosp., Buenos Aires, 1919; Hong Kong govt. med. serv., 1924; health offr. of port and insp., immigrts., 1932.
FELIX-JONES, IVOR, B.A.—B. 1899; ed. Westminster and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; succ. educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, Mar., 1922.
FELTHAM, STANLEY CRATHERN, A.R.I.B.A., chtd. architect.—B. 1886; ed. Northrn Poly technic, London; architect, P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.
FENN, EGERTON DUDLEY.—B. 1893; clk., staff of gov.-gen., Union of S. Africa, 1912-18; sub-inspr., Swaziland pol., 1928; inspr., 1931.

FENTON, HON. JAMES EDWARD.—M.H.R., C'wealth of Australia since 1910; mem., standing comtee., of pub. wks., 1914-17 and of joint comtee., pub. acct., 1917-25; opposition whip, 1922-29; min. for trade and cust., 1929; Australian del. to Disarmament Conf., London, 1930; P.M.G., Lyons' Ministry, 1932; resigned, Oct., 1932.

FENTON, JAMES STEVENSON.—B. 1891 ed.; Dundee High Sch., and Edinburgh Univ., M.A. (Edin.); war service, Mesopotamia 1917-19; passed progress exam. in Medici; ast. dist. commnr., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1915; dist. commnr., Apr., 1923.
FERGUSON, DAVID GILBERT.—B. 1861; called to the bar, 1890; judge of sup. ct., New S. Wales, Mar., 1912.

FERGUSON, HON. GEORGE HOWARD, P.C. (Can.), 1931, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1870; ed. Kemptville, Ont., High Sch., Toronto Univ. and Osgoode Hall; called to bar, 1894; el. to Ont. legis., g.e., 1905; re-el., g.e., 1908, 1911, 1914, 1919 and 1923; leader of conservative party since 1920; premier of Ont., 1923; high commr. for Canada in London, Nov., 1930.

FERGUSSON, GEN. SIR CHARLES, BART., G.C.B. (1932); G.C.M.G. (1924); K.C.B. (1915); K.C.M.G. (1918); C.B. (1911); M.V.O. (1906); D.S.O. (1898), D.L., J.P.—B. 1865; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; joined Grenadier Guards, 1883; adjutant, 1890-94; joined Egyptian Army, 1895; Sudanese campaigns, 1896-98; severely wounded, Rosaries (med. with eight clasps, ment. five times in desps.); commanded 15th Sudanese, 1899 (2nd cls. Medjideh); garrison and dist., Omdurman, 1900; adjt.-gen., Egyptian Army, 1901-3; commanded 3rd Batt. Grenadier Guards, 1904-7; brig.-gen., gen. staff, Irish commd., 1907-8; inspr., infy., 1909-13; served European War, 1914-18; in commd., 5th divn. and subsequently of 2nd and 17th Army Corps; mily., gov., occupied German territory, 1918-19 (seven times ment. in desps.); gov. and commdr-in-ch., New Zealand, Oct., 1924; assumed govt., Dec., 1924; ret., 1930; chmn., W. Indian Closer Union Comm., 1932.

FERNANDO, SIR HILARY MARCUS, KT. BACH. (1923), M.D., B.Sc. (Lond.), Fellow of Univ. Coll. (London); J.P.—B. 1864; ed. at the Royal Coll., Colombo, and Univ. Coll., Lond.; Ceylon Govt. Univ. Scholar and Gilchrist Scholar for the East, 1882-83; 2nd medical entrance scholar, Univ.
