Coll., 1884; Atchison scholar, 1888, 1st cls. hons. and gold medal, physiology, medicine, and forensic medicine (Univ. of London); ast. col. surg. and registr., Ceylon med. coll., 1890; 1st physician, general hosp., and lecturer in medicine, Ceylon med. coll., 1898; reported on outbreak of malaria in Galle, 1894, and plague in Bombay, 1897, (rec. thanks of govt.); ret. from govt. serv., 1909; served on Colombo lake development, railway traffic, education, opium, prevention of tuberculosis, and several other commissions and comtees of enquiry (1909-1918); nominated mem. of legis. coun. of Ceylon as the first low-country Sinhalese representative, Jan., 1917; nom. unoffl. mem., leg. coun., 1921-1924; ditto, exec. coun. from mag., Puttalam, Apr., 1924; ast., Ratnapura kach., Sept., 1925; office ast., ditto, Mar., 1926; pol. mag., Panadura, Dec., 1926; office ast., Mannar kach., Oct., 1927; pol. mag., Matale, Jan., 1929; ast. stlitm. offr., Jan., 1930; pol. mag., Gampola, May, 1933.

FERNANDO, SELLAPERUMAGE SHELTON CLARENCE, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1907; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1931; ast., ch. sec.'s office, Dec., 1931; ast., Ratnapura kach., Sept., 1932; office ast., Matale kach., Mar., 1933.

FIDDES, SIR GEORGE VANDELEUR, G.C.M.G. (1917), K.C.M.G. (1912), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1905), B.A.—B. 1858; ed. at Dulwich Coll., and late scholar of Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. class. mods., 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., 25th Mar., 1881; priv. sec. to Earl of Onslow, Feb., 1887; and to Baron H. de Worms (afterwards Lord Pirbright), Feb., 1888 to Aug., 1892; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, 11th Mar., 1896; 1st cls. clk., 1896; imperial sec. and acentnt. to Sir A. Milner, high commr. for S. Africa, Sept., 1897; political sec. to Lord Roberts at Pretoria, June, 1900; sec. to the Transvaal administration, Dec., 1900; returned to C. O., 1902, as prin. clk.; accounting offr., 1907; ast., under-sec. of state, 20th June, 1909; chmn., West African currency board, 1912-1916; perm. under-sec. of state for the colonies; 10th Mar., 1916; sec. to the Order of St. Michael and St. George; ret., 1921.

FIDDAN, ALEXANDER.—B. 1875; ed. Univ. Coll., Cardiff, and Oxford; schlr. Pembroke Coll., 1893; 1st cls. classical honours, 1895; Goldsmith's co. exhib., 1895; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1897; B.A., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam, 2nd cls. clk. C.O., Oct. 12th, 1897; ast. priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, 1st Apr., 1905; ditto to Earl of Elgin, Dec., 1905; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1907; mem. of deptmtl. comtee. on W. African med. staff, 1909; principal clk., 1st Nov., 1917; ast. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; establt. offr. for C.O. and Dominions office, 16th July, 1925.

FIELD, EDGAR JOHN.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schls. and Christian Brothers' Coll., Adelaide; entr. survey dept., S. Australia, Jan., 1891; clk., office of commr. of crown lands, 1892; ch. clk. and acct., 1909; ch. immigr. offr., 1911; sec. for lands, chmn. of advances to settlers hd. and recr. of renta, 2nd Apr., 1917; chmn., irrign. comm., S. Aust., 1923; dir., lands, 1931.

FIELD, HENRY TREVOR CROMWELL.—B. 1891; ed. St. John's Sch., Leatherhead and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); on war serv., 1914-18; demob. (capt.) 1919; inspr. and schoolmast., S.P., Nigeria, 1922; supt., educn., 1926; prin., govt. coll., Ibadan, 1932.

FIELDGATE, ALAN FREDERIC EDMOND, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1889; ed. Worcester Coll., Oxford; ast., dist. commnr., Gold Coast Colony, Apr., 1915; ag. dist. commnr. on various occasions, 1918-21; dist. commnr., Aug., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Mar. to June, 1925; prov. commnr., 1934.

FIENNES, LIEUT.-COL. THE HON. SIR EUSTACE EDWARD TWISLETON-WYKHAM, BART.—B. 1864; Lieut.-Col., Oxford Imp. Yeo.; served Riel rebellion, 1885 (medal with clasp); Egypt, 1887 and 1888 (medal with clasp, bronze star); pioneer expedit. to Mashonaland, 1890; South Africa, 1900 (medal with three clasps; twice ment. in desps.); European war, 1914 (France, Belgium, Dardanelles and Home); M.P. (L.) Banbury div.

FINCH, FRANK GEORGE.—B. 1882; asst. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Mar., 1907; asst. state engr., Kedah, 1910-15; exec. engr., F.M.S., 1913; state engr., Pahang, 1923; state engr., Selangor, Mar., 1927; dep. dir., pub. wks., F.M.S., July, 1928; dir., advr., drainage and irrig., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1932.

FINDLAY, ALEXANDER JOHN.—B. 1886; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schl., Aberdeen Univ. and North of Scotland Coll. of Agr.; M.A., B.Sc. (Agric.), N.D.A., N.D.D.; supt., agr., Nigeria, 1912; senr. supt., 1922; asst. dir., 1927; dep. dir., 1930; dir., agr., Zanzibar, 1931; M.L.C., 1932.

FINDLAY, GEORGE HUGO, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1888; ed. Repton and Oriel Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1911; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1919; asst. sec., S. Prova., 1923; cls. I, grade I, admstive serv., 1928; ag. admstr. of colony, Lagos, 1930.
FINLAYSON, D. A.—B. 1892; home cust. and excise serv., 1912-24; war serv. in R.N., 1918; collr., cust., Sierra Leone, 1924; dep. dir., dept. of cust., excise and trade, Palestine, 1928.

FINLAYSON, GEORGE DANIEL—B. 1882; ed. Pictou Acad. and Dalhousie Univ.; B.A., 1907; apptd. to dept. of insurance, Canada, 1907; associate of Inst. of Actuaries of Great Britain, 1911; supt. of insurance, Canada, 1914; dep. min. of insurance, 1924.

FINN, BASIL ARTHUR.—B. 1888; ed. at Alleyne's Schl., Dulwich and Wadham Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1912, 2nd cls. mod. hist.; asst. dist. offr., N. Provinces, Nigeria, Apr., 1914; attached, C.O., Dec., 1915, to Mar., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., Gambia, May, 1919; ag. col. sec. for various periods, 1919 to 1930; asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Mar., 1931.
FIRR, TOM FREEMAN, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Crystal Palace Engnr. Schl.; 1st asst. survr., Nyasaland Prot., June, 1903; asst. dir. of pub. wks., 1909.

FIRTH, OWEN WATTS.—B. 1884; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; asst. dist. commr., Nigeria, 1911; polit. offr., Udi-Okiwigi Patrol, 1915; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1919; cls. I, grade I, admstive serv., 1927; ag. prin. asst. sec., 1928.
FISCHER, PERCY U., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1878; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontien, S.A. Coll., Cape Town, Trinity Hall, Camb.; judge, sup. ct., South Africa, 1929.

FISET, MAJOR-GEN. SIR EUGENE, KT. BACH. (1917), C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O., G.G.H.S., B.A., M.D.—B. 1874; staff, adjt. med. corps., Canada, 1902; dir.-gen., med. services, 1903; dep. min. militia and defence, vice-pres., the militia coun. since 1906; recd. D.S.O. for service in S. Africa; mentioned in despatches; ret., 1924; mem. for county of Rimouski.

FISHER, BERTIE ST. JULIAN.—B. 1885; Fiji serv., 1909; dist. commr., 1918; legal dept., 1920; ag. dep. regtr.-genl. and registr., sup. ct., 1921; ag. registr., sup. ct., curator of intestate and vacant estates, pub. trustee and offl. recr. in bankruptcy, 1923-27; registr.-gen. and registr., titles, 1929; registr., sup. ct., pub. trustee, offl. recr. in addn., Jan., 1931.

FISHER, JOHN CAMPBELL, O.R.E. (1918).—B. 1880; ed. at Oakham Schl. and Sid. Suss. Coll., Cambridge; clk., col. audit branch, exchm. audit dept., July, 1902; asst. audtr., Sept., 1903; detached for duty in N. Nga., Nov., 1903; asst. aud., N. Nigeria, 1906; local audr., July, 1909; examnr., col. branch, E. and A. dept., Feb., 1910; insp. loc. Moh., 1913; mem. of control board and bd. of commerce control office, Malta, 1917; clk., centrl. office, May, 1918; asst. dir., ext. Apr., 1928; deputy dir., Oct., 1929.
FISHER, HAROLD MAYSTOR, L.D.S. (Eng.).—B. 1893; mil. serv., 1914-19; surg., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; surg., 1930.

FISHER, REV. ROBERT, M.A.—B. 1876; Cambridge; asst. mast., educ. dept., East Coast, 1921; house mast., 1922; mast., Accra Coll., 1926; supt., educn., S. Prova., 1928; prin., govt. coll., Umuhia, 1928.

FISHER, SIR STANLEY, KT. R.A.C.—Ed. at Westminster and Brasenose Coll.; M.A., 1891; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple Lincoln's Inn (called 1890); appd. pres. ct. of Kyrena, Cyprus, May, 1902; puisne Cyprus, 1911; ch. just., Cyprus, 1919; mission to Cairo under F.O., 24th Jan. to Mar., 1924; ch. just., Trinidad, 1924; ch. just., Ceylon, 1926; ret., Aug., 1930; passed out modern Greek, Dec. 1903 and in Turkish joint commr. for compiling revised edition of late Laws of Cyprus (published 1907) for recd. thanks of legis. coun. and S. of S.; asst. commr. under reprint of statutes law, 1913; published, Feb., 1914; commr. under reprint of statutes law, 1922; edition printed 1924; compiler of "The Mukhtar's Handbook," 1911; published "Ottoman Land Law," 1915.
FISHER, V. M.—Medical offr., R.A.P.L., 1919; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1928; med. lab. instr., Feb., 1928.

FITCHETT, WILLIAM HERBERT.—Ch. dept., Cape Colony, Feb., 1896; clk. to Col. R.M., Wodehouse, Sept., 1898; Port Elizabeth, Oct., 1899; Colesteberg, Mar., 1900; Bree, Aug., 1901; acted as C.C. and R.M. Bruma, 1902; asst. regist., high ct., and chief dist. sheriff of the O.R.C., July, 1902; acted as dist. sheriff in 1903 and 1904; regar. and dist. O.F.S., Aug., 1921.

FITZGERALD, R. DESMOND, R.A. I., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dublin), D.M., M.H. (Certif. (hons.) L.S.T.M., M.C.—B. 1885; Trinity Coll., Dublin; M.O., Penang, July, 1912; prov. Wellseley, Dec., 1912; heal. of Malacca, Sept., 1914; med. offr., gen. hosp., Singapore, Sept., 1914; served in Europe war (ment. in desps., M.C.); med. offr., T.M. Hosp. Hosp. and surg., native hosp., Singapore, Dec., 1919; M.O., gen. hosp., Singapore, 1920; med. offr. in ch. officials, pol. surg., prison surg., Jan., 1921; prin. med. offr., June, 1923; offl. del., Far Eastern Ass. Med., Calcutta, Dec., 1927.
FITZGERALD, THOMAS, O. O.R.E.—F.M.S. postal service; dep. P.M.G., Kevington, 1920; P.M.G., Jan., 1922; P.M.G., Uganda-Tanganyika Territory postal service, 1933.

FITZGERALD, WILLIAM JAMES.—Ed. Rock Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; Med. Croix de Guerre (French); adminstr., Nigeria, 1919; crown coun., Nigeria, 1929; gen., N. Rhodesia, 1932; ag. atty.-gen., 1932; atty.-gen., July, 1933.

GIBBON, Henry Elliot.—B. 1889; ed., Stephen's Green Schl., Dublin and Trinity Publ.; B.A., B.A.I., Amec, M.Inst.C.E., M.T.M. & Cy. E., M.T.P.; inspr., possibilities, Egypt, 1913; war serv. with (ment. in desps); dir., irrig. and mech. min. of wafks, Egypt, 1919; Order of the chs cls.; munici. and town planning engrn., 1928; Pickering prize and gold medal, 1928; ed., Cy. E., 1931.
GIBBON, William Guerin.—B.A., Univ. of Ireland; B. 1885; rly. dept., Kong, 1913; 1st clk., col. secretariat, Kong, 1917; ag. ch. clk., col. secretariat, 1924; J.P., 1924; sec., P.W.D., 1927.

HENRY, William Cooper, A.M.I.C.E., 1888; ed., Dulwich Coll.; astt. engrn., South African rlys., 1908; lieut. R.E., 1915; capt., 1916; served with miny. of munitions and ordnance, 1918; in India on rly. work, 1920-27; engrn., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1927; ch. road N. Rhodesia, July, 1928; ag. D.P.W., 1930.
MAURICE, Alexander Lindsay—L.S., L.R.C.P. (1909); M.B., B.S. (Lond.), late out-patient offr., astt. house surg. and physician, Guy's Hosp.; med. offr. Somaliland, 1914.

PATRICK, Right Hon. Sir Charles, (1908); G.C.M.G. (1911); K.C.M.G. (1907).—B. 1858; med. offr., F.M.S., Quebec, 1858; Dufferin medallist, law schl., Laval Univ., 1876; one of the counsel for defence of Louis Riel at Regina in 1885; elected to Quebec legislature, 1890; to Dominion parl., again in 1900 and 1904; solr.-gen., Canada, 1898; mem. of Hague tribunal of arbitration, lieutenant-gov. of Quebec, 1918 to 1923.

PATRICK, James Patrick, L.R.C.P. (Ireland).—B. 1885; med. offr., F.M.S., 1921; ag. med. sept., Central Mental Hosp., Butten, May-Dec., 1929; med. offr., Seremban, 1931; do., L. Perak, Jan., 1932; do., Sept., 1932.

ZROY, Henry Somerset, B.A., LL.M., B. 1870 (schol. of Trinity Hall, Camb.); law tripod, part II (1st cls.), 1893; served 1896-1905; called to bar, 1910; served in India and New York, 1914-18; astt. to atty.-gen., Hong Kong, Apr., 1927; ag. atty.-gen., 1929; pub. pros. and astt. to atty.-gen., 1929; pub. serv. inspr., Pretoria, May, 1928; dir., prisons, Pretoria, Nov., 1929; mag., Pretoria, Sept., 1932.

FLECKER, Oriel Joyce, B.Sc.—Ed. Cheltenham Ladies' Coll.; assoc. of Cheltenham Ladies' Coll., 1908; gold med., Société Nationale des Professeurs de Français en Angleterre, 1909; B. Sc., Lond., 1912; Somerville Coll., Oxford, 1924-5; headmistress, girls' schl., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925; author of "A School Chemistry," 1923.

FLEMING, Edward Donovan.—B. 1898; ed., Bradford Gram. Schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Sept., 1917; Salonika, Dec., 1917-Feb., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921, attd., Chinese prot., K. Lumpur; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; astt. prot., China-e., Taiping, May, 1930 and supt., convict estabtn., in addn., Nov., 1930; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933.

FLEMING, Ernest.—B. 1891; 5th class clk., crown agts. office, Oct., 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd division of the civ. serv. and temporarily employed in the Bd. of Trade, Jan., 1912; assigned to C.O., April, 1912; on military service from 25th May, 1915 to 9th Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; 2nd astt. acctant, 22nd Oct., 1930.

FLEMING, Col. John Gibson, C.B.E. (1919), D.S.O. (1916), late R.E.—B. 1880; ed., Haileybury Coll. and R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich; joined R.E.'s 1898; served S. African War, 1901-2 (Queen's med. 5 clasps); European War, 1914-18 (desp. D.S.O., bt. lt.-col., C.B.E.); ret. with rank of col., 1924; apptd. fourth Crown Agent and engrn.in-ch., 1st June, 1932.
FLEMING, W. P.—B. 1887; apptd., after compet. exam., S. and T. clk., G.P.O.; lent to C.O., 9th May, 1921; cler. offr., C.O., 1st Sept., 1921; on mil. serv., 4th Apr., 1915 to 4th May, 1919.

FLETCHER, Sir Arthur George Murchison, K.C.M.G. (1930), Kt. Bach (1929), C.M.G. (1922), C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford (exhib.); cadet, Hong Kong, 1901; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si. May-June, 1903; ag. astt. regis.gen., 1903; J.P., 1904; ag. astt. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1905-8-9; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1907; dep. offr. recvr., 1909; astt. to atty.-gen., 1909; regis., trade marks and letters patent, 1910; ag. assessor of rates, 1911; ag. dep. regis., 1911; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1912; offr. recvr., 1912; ag. astt. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1912; ag. treasur., 1913; astt. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Aug., 1913; priv. sec. to O.A.G. and to gov., Aug.-Dec., 1913; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. and leg. couns. in 1916, 1917, 1918, 1919, 1920 and 1922; chmn., shipping control comm., 1917-19; ag. col. sec. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1923 and 1925; col. sec., Ceylon, Jan., 1926; O.A.G., May-Aug., 1927; gov., Fiji and high comsnr., W. Pacific, 1929.
FLETCHER, Reginald H.—Joined Jamaica civ. serv., post office dept. by compet. exam., 1890; 1st cls. clk., 1908; oh. clk., 1915; astt. postmtr., 1921; postmtr., Jamaica, 1925.

FLETT, Martin Teall.—B. 1911; ed. St. Paul's Schol. and St. John's Coll., Oxford (exhib., 1930, Cassel Schol., 1933); 1st cls., mod. hist., 1933; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. prin., D.O., 23rd Oct., 1933.

FLEURET, Arthur Isadore.—B. 1899; mesgr., C.S.O., Falkland Is., Dec., 1912; ag. jun. clk., Jan., 1914 to Sept., 1919; clk., treas. and cust., Oct., 1919; clk., C.S.O., Jan., 1924; ch. clk. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1928; offr.-in-ch., secretariat, Oct., 1928 to Jan., 1929 and Feb. to June, 1931; 2nd lieut., Falkland Is., Defence Force, Jan., 1931.

FLEURY, CAPT. ARTHUR MARLEY, O.B.E. (1932).—Second lieut., 3rd Royal Irish Fusiliers, May, 1900; capt., May, 1905; lieut., W.A.F.F., July, 1900; Ashanti campaign (medal), 1900; dist. comsrr., N. Territories, G. Coast, Jan., 1907; local comd., Cyprus mil. police, Feb., 1910; comsrr., Nov., 1919; ag. local comd., pol., Apr., 1930.

FLINN, MAJOR WILLIAM HENRY, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1895; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; Royal Irish Regt. and staff, 1914-19; admtn. offr., Nigeria, Sept., 1919; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Mar., 1921; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to H. Comsrr., 14th Apr., 1921; ag. elk., exec. coun., Feb.-Mar., 1922; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Apr., 1922; ag. ch. asst. sec., July, 1922; ag. comsrr., Sept., 1922; ag. local comd., police, Sept., 1922; ag. ch. asst. sec., June to July, 1923; ag. regiar., co-op. societies, June, 1923; regiar., co-op. societies, July, 1923; ag. regiar., co-op. credit societies, June, 1923 to Apr., 1924 and from Oct., 1924; ag. ch. asst. sec. for various periods, 1924-27; ag. col. sec. in 1926 and 1927; trade comsrr. for Cyprus in London, July, 1927.

FLOOD, J. E. W., C.M.G. (1933).—B. 1886; ed. at Portora Royal Schl., Enniskillen, and Trin. Coll., Dublin, B.A. (scholar univ. student); appd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. elk., C.O., 10th Oct., 1910; sec., edible nuts comte., 1916; 1st cls. elk., 15th Oct., 1918; on military service from 16th Apr., 1917 to 7th Dec., 1918; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., Feb., 1926; vice-chmn., col. advisory med. and sany. comte., 1924-26; mem. W. Afr. Currency Board, 1928.
FLYNN, J. B., B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Drogheda Grammar Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, 1928.

FOLKY, GERALD ROBERT EDWARD, O.R.F., B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Tipperary Gram. Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; schol. and senr. moderator in maths., Dublin Univ.; Royal Irish Constab., 1911-22; war serv. with R. Irish Regt. in France, Palestine and Salonica (rank major); twice ment. in desps. and awarded O.B.E. (mil.); asst. comd., Br. gendarmerie, Palestine, 1922; comd., Police Training Schl., 1926; dist. supt., pol., N. dist., 1927.

FOLLIT, HAROLD HARRY BAILEY.—B. 1883; ed. Bedford, Heidelberg and Cambridge; St. Bartholomew's Hosp.; European War, 1914-18; Egypt, Gallipoli and France; med. offr., Nyasaland, 1921; senr. health offr., Tanganyika, 1928; senr. health offr., Nyasaland, 1931; ag. D.M. and S.S., May, 1933.

FOLLOWS, CHARLES GEOFFREY SHIELD.—B. 1896; ed. Wellington; 2nd lieut., The King's (Liverpool) Regt., 1914; served in France, 1915-18, latterly as adjt.; on staff, 1918 and 1919, attached to H.Q., Southern dist., Ireland, and H.Q., Aldershot command; priv. sec. to gov. of Seychelles and elk. to couns., 1920; addt. C.O., May-Oct., 1925; asst. sec., Gibraltar, 1925; ch. asst. sec., 1927; ag. col. sec. on various occasions.

FOORD, FRANCOIS LAYTON, C.B.E. (1934).—B. 1874; served in Cape Mounted Rifles, S. African War, 1899-1902; lieut., 1st S. Staffs. regt.; sub-inspr., Basuto Land Mounted Pol., 1902; inspr., 1913; asst. comsrr., 1914; accompanied Basuto deputations to England in 1909 and 1919; mem., poor white comsrr., 1918; govt. sec., 1930; dep. res. comsrr. and govt. sec., 1931; ag. res. comsrr., Mar. to Oct., 1932.
FORAN, WILLIAM.—B. 1871; entered Canada, 1890; 2nd cls. elk., 1894; sec. exam. exam., 1896; 1st cls. elk., 1896; civ. ser. comsrr., 1908.

FORBES, Rt. HON. GEORGE WILLIAM (1930).—B. 1869; ed. Holy High School; church; mem., N.Z. parl. since 1906; sec. Liberal party, 1912-22; leader of opposition leader of National party, 1925-29; min. agr., 1928-30; prime min., min. of internal affrs., cust., and stamp duties, 1930; min., min. of rly. and min. of external affrs., colonial min., 1931; atty.-gen., 1931; N.Z., Imp. Confce., 1930 and Work in Confce, 1933.

FORBES, HARRY.—B. 1873; ent. ser., 1911; clk. of peace, Rowa, Fiji, 1914-1915; cashier, treas., 1918; ag. acct. of Ellise Col., June, 1921; ag. treas., postmr. and collr. of cust., June-Dec., 1921; Br. residency, New Hebrides, Aug., 1921; asst. dist. offr., Kinta, Feb., 1927; of. Nov., 1930; collr., land rev., Seremban, dist. offr., Klang, June, 1933.
FORBES, PAUL HENRY.—B. 1882; sec. (war staff), Aug.-Oct., 1914; B.R.F.; asst. ch. acct., Palestine rly., Aug., 1923; acct., Aug., 1923; ch. acct., F.M.S. rly., 1926.

FORRER, HENRY AUGUSTUS, M.A.—Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1888; S. S. Oct., 1909; passed cadet as supernum. offr. of cls. V., Mar., 1914; censor's office, May 1915; services in F.M.S., Jan., 1917; offr., cls. IV., Jul., 1921; 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, July, 1921; cls. III., Nov., 1921; 2nd mag. and overseer, Dec., 1923; dep. regiar., and asst. of Penang, Sept., 1924; offr. cls. II.; regiar., sup. pt., Penang, May, 1927; regiar., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1929; of. Apr., 1930; dist. judge and int. mag., Jan., 1933.

FOREST, ARTHUR ERIC.—B. 1882; audr., E. Africa Prot., July, 1915; service, Oct., 1915 to Dec., 1916; sec. charge, Somaliland, 1917; ag. senr. audr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1917 to Apr., 1919 to Jan., 1920; asst. audr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1920; senr. audr., Mar., 1922; senr. asst. treas., Nov., 1925; mem. and sec. Ken. living comsrr., 1926; ag. dep. treas., Jan., Nov., 1927 and Aug., 1929 to Apr., 1930; subst. post changed to prin. asst. treas., treas., Uganda, Apr., 1930; ag. treas., 1930 and May, 1932.

FOREST, ROBERT ANDREW JUN.—B. 1893; ed. Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., 1914; Hong Kong, Oct., 1919; passed final Cantonese, Jan., 1922; attd. chm., Jan., 1922; ag. 2nd pol. mag., May, 1923; head, sany. dept., Nov., 1925; ch. senr. Chinese affrs., 1928; ag. treas. and duty comsrr., 1931.

FORSTER, BARON (er. 1919).—HENRY WILLIAM, P.C. (1917). G.C.V.I., D.L.—B. 1866; ed. Eton and New C., M.P., Sevenoaks, Kent, 1892-1919; sec. of the treasy., 1902-06; finan. sec., 1906- 1915-19; mem. of army council; gov.-gen. and comdr. in chief, Commonwealth of Austr., 1920-26.
FORSYTH, L. E. W.—B. 1877; ag. comsnr., Mangrove Cay, Bahamas, 1922; Out Island comsnr., 4th grade, 1927; 2nd grade, July, 1927.
FOSBROOKE, HENRY ALBERT, B.A.—B. 1908; ed. Pocklington Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1931.
FOSDIKE, ALFRED ERNEST.—Served in Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1907-1917; contrlr. of stores, 1917.

FOSTER, LIONEL HUBERT LISLE.—B. 1889; ed. Bradford and Christ Ch. Oxford (B.A. 1912); min. of educn., Egypt, Jan., 1913; asst. res., Nyasaland, Jan., 1913; res., 1st grade, Apr., 1925; ag. asst. ch. sec., May-Nov., 1932; ag. prov. comsnr., Nov., 1933.

FOSTER, RICHARD SHEPHERD—B. 1894, apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil service; temp. employed at Nat. HI'th. Insurance Coman., Mar., 1913 to June, 1914; assigned to C.O., June, 1914; on military service from Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1919; col. audit dept., 1919; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; do., Sierra Leone, Feb., 1923; do., Gold Coast, Nov., 1931; ag. dep. audr., various occasions, 1931-33.

FOSTER, ROBERT SPENCE, M.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1891; ed. Magdalen Coll. Schl. and St. Edmund's Hall, Oxford; 2nd lieut., K.S.L.I., 1915; K.A.R., 1917-18; head mast., educn. dept., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; ag. dir., educn., Aug. to Oct., 1924; ag. dep. dir., educn., Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1930; Swahili examr.; dep. dir., educn., Uganda, 1930.

FOSTER, HON. WALTER EDWARD.—B. 1874; ed. St. John Pub. and Grammar Schls.; chosen leader of opposition liberal party, New Brunswick, 1916; premier, N.B., Apr., 1917; sec. of state of Canada, Sept., 1925; senator, Dec., 1928.

FOSTER, WILLIAM, M.H.E.—B. 1880; served in S. African War, 1900-02; Queen's Med., 3 clasps; King's Med., 2 clasps; Br. post office, 1897-1920; serv. in Gt. War, 1914-20; ment. in desps.; major, 1920; dep. postmtr.-gen., Palestine, July, 1920; ag. postmtr.-gen. on several occasions.

FOULGER, HARRY CECIL, M.A. (1928)—B. 1891; ed. Malvern Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; capt., R. Fusiliers; asst. res., Nyasaland Prot., Aug., 1914; attd., K.A.R., ag. pvt. sec. and A.D.C. to gov., 1919; res., 1st grade, Jan., 1924.

FOUND, WILLIAM AMBROSE, D.Sc.—B. 1873; ed. pub. schls. and Prince of Wales Coll., Prince Ed. Island; dept. marine and fisheries, Canada, 1898; supt., fisheries in combined dept., 1911; advr. to Br. plenipotentiaries when pelagic sealing treaty negotiated, 1911; mem., Canadian-American Fisheries Coman., 1918; dep. min., fisheries, 1928.

FOURIE, THE HON. A. P. J.—Formerly mem. Prov. coun. and subsequently mem. H. of A. for Somerset; admstr., Prov. of the Cape of Good Hope, Jan., 1926; min. of mines and industries, Aug., 1929; del., Imp. Conf., London, 1930 and del., Ottawa Econ. Conf., 1932; min., lab., commerce and industries, Apr., 1933.

FOURIE, LOUIS, M.B.E.—B. 1878; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Edin. Univ.; Berlin; M.B., Ch.B. (Hons) (Conan Doyle Prizeman); D.P.H., F.Z.S., F.R.A.I., Fell. Soc. M.O.H., M.R. San. Inst., mem., Amer. Anthropol. Soc., res. med. offr., R. Infirmary, 1905; city fever hosp., 1906; roy. hosp. sick. child., Edin., 1906-7; h.-surg., Paddington Green Child. hosp., Lond., 1907; act. serv., Boer Forces, 1899-1900; D.S. Taunga, 1913-15; capt., S.A.M.C., 1916-19; M.O. to adminstr., S.W.A., 1920-29; asst. health offr., Union Health Dep., 1930.
FOWLIS, HENRY GEORGE.—Entd. judl. dept., Gambia, 1890; govt. offr., 1893-1901; col. sec.'s offr., 1902-05; oh. clk. and acctnt., P.W.D., 1906.

FOXLEE, RICHARD WILLIAM, M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1885; Westminster Schl.; served in engnr. dept., G.N.R., 1903-09; G.C.R., 1909-15; P.L.A., 1915-21; dep. head, engnr. design dept., C.A., 1921; dep. ch. engnr. (civ.), 1928.

FRANCE, HENRY DUNLOP.—Rev. offr., Anguilla, 1896; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, 1899; tariff clk., treas., St. Kitts, 1901; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1902; supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Sept., 1904; 1st cls. supervisor of cust., Aug., 1909; ag. inspr., cust. preven. serv., Mar.-Oct., 1911; ag. inspr., maritime cust., Oct., 1916 to Jan., 1917; prov. collr. cust., Jan., 1921.

FRANCIS, LESTRAN ALEXANDER, O.B.E. (1928)—B. 1878; ed. St. Edmunds Schl., Canterbury, and St. Catherine's Coll., Camb.; B.A. (class. tripos), 1900; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., 1900; lieut., 1902; inspr. of immigrts., Mauritius, 1908; ag. auditor-gen., Jan. to June, 1908; prot. of immigrts. and poor law comsnr., 1912; temp. capt., R.G.A., Jan., 1915 to 1918; ag. reovr.-gen., Feb. to Nov., 1919, Dec., 1919 to Jan., 1922 and from Nov., 1922 to Aug., 1923; chmn., profiteering comtee., May, 1920; collr. of cust. and harmr., Aug., 1924; ag. col. sec., Aug., 1927 to May, 1928 and Dec.-Aug., 1930; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1929 to Dec., 1930.

FRANCIS, CLIFFORD CLAUDE, Barrister-at-Law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1889; ed. at King's Schl., Rochester, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; service in Fiji, 1908-12; cadet W. Pacific serv. (Br. Solomon Is. Prot.), June, 1912; passed cadet, May, 1914; dep. comsnr., high comsnr.'s ct., Dec., 1914; lieut., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; capt., Apr., 1916; served in France and Flanders, 1917-18; dist. offr., Gizo, Br. Solomon Is., Oct., 1919; ag. res. comsnr. of Prot., Oct., 1922 to Mar., 1923; rep. natives of Gizo dist. in claims before lands coman., 1923; judl. comsnr. for W. Pacific in New Hebrides and Br. judge, joint ct., New Hebrides condominium, Aug., 1925; pol. mag., Nigeria, 1930.

FRANCIS, CYRIL GERARD BROOKE.—Apptd. Fiji civ. serv., 1898; lieut., armed native constab., 1901; certif., musketry and machine gun, Hythe, 1906; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1907; inspr., Fiji constab., 1907; barrister and solr., sup. ct., Fiji, 1908; A.D.C. to ag. gov., Dec., 1908 to 1909; ag. inspr.-genl. constab., and prisons, 1909 to 1910; A.D.C. to ag. gov., Aug., 1910 to Feb., 1911; ag. comsnr. and stip. mag., 1911-12; ag. chief pol. mag., July to Aug., 1911; ag. inspr.-genl., constab., and prisons, 1912 to 1913; 2nd grade dist. comsnr., May, 1912; ag. chief pol. mag., Mar., 1913 to Dec., 1914; 2nd in comd. local forces with rank of major, 5th Aug., 1914; mentioned by Army Council for services in 1914 in connection with defence of Fiji; capt., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; cov. oomdr., K.A.R., E. Africa, 1916-19; 1st grade dist. comsrr., Fiji, Jan., 1916; regist. gen., reg. sup. ct., comsrr. stamps, and pub. trustee, Fiji, Jan., 1920; cust., enemy property and contr. L.C.O., Fiji and W. Pacific, Feb., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1920 to May, 1921; comsrr. for the revision of the laws of Fiji, 1920 (not completed); atty.-gen., B. Honduras, July 1921; ag. ch. just., July, 1921 to May, 1922 and from July to Nov., 1923; chmn., comsrr. to enquire into outbreak of yellow fever, 1921; chmn., retrenchment comtee, 1922; chmn., franchise comsrr., 1923; revd. and consol. laws, B. Hond., 1924; atty.-gen., Zanzibar, Nov., 1924; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.
FRANCIS, JOSIAH.—Served European war with A.I.F., 1916-19; M.H.R., C'wealth of Australia since 1922; hon. min., Lyons' ministry, 1932.
FRANCKLIN, B. J. F.—Asst. jun. staff survr., E. Africa Prot., 1914; asst. dist. comsrr., May, 1915; 2nd grade res., Nyasaland, Jan., 1925; 1st grade admstve.-offr., Apr., 1925.
FRANKS, JOHN WILLIAM.—B. 1879; prison comsrr., H.O., June, 1899; asst. supt., Victoria Gaol, Hong Kong, Nov., 1913; asst. supt. pol. res., Feb., 1919; supt., prisons, Dec., 1920.
FRASER, ALASTAIR GARDEN, B.A.—B. 1902; ed. Rugby and Queen's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1924; 3rd cls. hons., phil., polit., and econ.; asst. mnr., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

FRASER, REV. ALEXANDER GARDEN, C.B.E. (1930), M.A.—B. 1873; ed. Merchiston Castle, Edin., Edin. Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; deacon, 1913; priest, 1915; C.M.S., Uganda, 1900-03; prin., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1904; chaplain with 3rd divn., France, 1917-18; chaplain to the forces; prin., Prince of Wales Coll., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.
FRASER, H.—Ed. Grammar Schl., Inveraray; George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; B.Sc., 1925; asst. conservator of forests, Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

FRASER, HUGH—B. 1890; ed. Wellington and Exeter Coll., Oxford (schol.); cadet, F.M.S., 1913; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., Apr., 1914-Feb., 1918; ag. supervisor of cust., Port Dickson, May, 1915; ditto, Port Swettenham, Jan., 1916; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1916; 3rd astt. sec., Fed. sect., Aug., 1917, priv. sec. to ch. sec. in addn., Sept., 1917; dist. offr., Jelabu, Feb., 1918; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Sept., 1920; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; 3rd astt. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Dec., 1921; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1924; offr., class III., Nov., 1925; ag. astt. advr., Kedah, Mar., 1927; ag. temp. legal adviser, Kedah in addn., Apr. to Dec., 1927; ag. astt. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, June, 1929; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; dist. offr., Larut and regis., titles, Perak, Nov., 1930; offr., mag., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1932; ag. sec. to res., Perak, Mar., 1933.

FRASER, JOHN ALEXANDER, M.C., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1896; ed. Trinity Academy, Leith, and Edin. Univ.; on milly. serv., 1915-19; cadet, Hong Kong, 1919; attd. to sany. dept., 1921; 3rd astt. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1921; 2nd pol. mag., Apr., 1922; dist. offr. (North), June, 1922; passed in Cantonese, 1922; astt. head, sany. dept. and sec. to sany. bd., 1922; dist. offr., South, 1924; astt. supt., imps. and expts., 1925; dist. offr., North, Feb., 1926; dist. offr., North and South Sept., 1928; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1931; pol. mag., Kowloon, July, 1931; ag. astt. atty.-gen., Oct., 1932.

FRASER, SIR JOHN GORMAN (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1864; ass. civ. ser., Oct., 1887; ag. dist. judge, 1st Feb., 1894; astt. govt. agt., China, land settm. offr., Aug., 1901; ag. prov. Prov., 4th Jan., 1912; ag. controller in 1911, 1912, 1913, 1915 and 1916; W. Prov., July, 1914; M.L.C.; int. dist. ed. at Inverness; entd. N.Z. land and nat. dept., 1893; acct., pub. serv. superint., 1908; govt. statistician 1911-32; sec. of unemployment, 1930-33; admin. of and balloting, Expedy. Force Res., 1918; N.Z. rep. at British Empire Conf.; Statisticians, 1920; under sec., minister, 1932; mem., Internat. Statistical Inst.

FRASKR, WALTER MORTIMER.—B. 1883; els. clk., P.W.D., Jamaica, 1st Feb., 1912; and clk., lands dept., Oct., 1890; ag. rec. record office, Aug., 1900 to May, 1904; clk., treasy., Nov., 1910; acct., gen. bank, Nov., 1918; ch. clk. and sec., July, 1920; dep. treas., Apr., 1921; 1925; nom. mem., leg. coun.
FRASER, WINFORD BLACHE.—B. 1879; to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1892; mag., St. George, Trinidad, Jan., 1923; mag., Caroni, N.W. mag., St. George East, Dec., 1924.

FRAZER, HON. FRANCIS VERNON LL.B.—B. 1880; ed. Nelson Coll., One Coll., and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; barr. solr., stip. mag., 1911; chmn., N.Z. appeal bd., 1918; astt. pub. serv. exam. judge, ct. of arbit., 1921.
FREEMAN, GEOFFREY JAMES.—B. 1892; privately and in Clongoweswood Coll., he served in Royal Dublin Fusiliers and Munster Fusiliers in the Great War; col., cust., Gold Coast, 1922; sec., 1933.

FREESTON, LESLIE BRIAN, O.B.E. (1892); ed. at Willaston Schl., Nantwich and scholar New Coll., 1911-1919; 2nd cls. hist., 1913; B.A. (war), 1919; on mil. ser., 1914 to 8th Mar., 1919; temp. 2nd cls. cls. 10th Mar. to 15th July, 1919; recon. scheme, 2nd cls. cls. July, 1919; astt. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; to perm. under-secr., 18th June, 1920; Ceylon for temp. serv. as sec., malaria 1921; ast. prin., 6th Dec., 1922; pra. April, 1924; visited Bahamas and Br. India, 1927; for serv. with Col. Government Advisory Comtee., 1st Feb., 1930.

FRENCH, LEWIS, I.C.S. (Ret.).—Ed. MacTaylors' Schl. and St. John's Coll., Orford; as colonisation offr., Chenal Canal, Punjab; as dep. comsrr. and joint ch. sec., Punjab, 1896-1921; dir., development, 1931.

FRERE, SIR BARTLE HENRY TRUMBA BACH, (1918), LL.B.—Ed. at Charterhouse and Trin. Coll., Cam.; called to bar, Inn, 1887; pres., dist. court, Cyprus, 1897; to Gibraltar as pol. mag. and coroner, 1906; gen., 1911; ch. just., Apr., 1914; judg. ct., Aug., 1914; cable censor during War; ret., Aug., 1922.

FRERE, NOEL GRAVY.—B. 1885; ed. Wanganui Collegiate Schl., N.Z., and Cranleigh Schl.; dist. comsrr., S. Leone, July, 1909; dist. comsrr., Jan., 1915; ag. prov. comsrr., N. Prov., 1923, 1926 and 1928; prov. comsrr., Jan., 1931.

BERG, CAPT. GEOFFREY HERBERT, R.N. O.B.E. (Mil.)—B. 1881; served in R. 395-1926; China med., 1900; ment. in or services at Battle of Jutland when H.M.S. "Valiant"; awarded O.B.E.; or services afloat during the Great War; harbmr., Plymouth, from 1919; elec. a brother, Trinity House Corpnr., 1921; ist admr., 1922; master attendant, S. Apr., 1926.
JLES, NORMAN RALPH.—B. 1907; ed. Sir Manwood's Sch.; B.Sc., Wye (Lond. Univ.), T., Cantab and A.L.C.T.A., Trinidad; dist. fr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1930.

LER, HON. SIR GEORGE WARBERTON, G.(1919).—Elected to first H. of R., C. 1901; re-elected, 1903 and 1906; min. of affairs, C. of A., June, 1909 to Apr., 1910; c., N. S. Wales, Nov., 1916-20; premier, 5; agt.-gen., N.S.W., 1926-31.

TON, ADM. M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1897; lieut., (1915; capt., 1916; on active serv. with lst. d 2nd batt., Border Regt., Gallipoli, Sept., 1915; Egypt and Suez defences, Jan. to 1916; France, July to Sept., 1916 and from o Aug., 1917; wounded, 26th Sept., 1916; Dec., 1918 to Jan., 1919; demob., Jan., vety. offr., Gold Coast, 18th May, 1926.

RNESS, SIR ROBERT HOWARD, Kt. Bach.—B. 1880; ed. King William's Coll., Isle n.; solr. (hons.). 1902; practised at bar of Honduras, 1906-13; regisr.-gen., B. Honduras, served with B. W. I. Regt., 1915-19; to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 9th Jan., 1920; senr. mag., 1921; solr. gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1923; ch. Barbados, Oct., 1926.

RNESS-SMITH, CECIL, Barrister-at-Law, 1890; ed. Birkenhead Schl. and St. John's Camb.; B.A., 1912; LL.B., 1913; astt. comsrr., G. Coast, Apr., 1914; dist. mar., 1921; called to bar, Inner Temple, crown coun., Mar., 1925; ast. solr.-gen., Oct., 1927; ag. circuit judge, 1929; solr. Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1932.

RSE, MAJOR R. D., D.S.O.—B. 1887; ed. and Balliol Coll., Oxford, B.A.; astt. priv. to sec. of state (Mr. Lewis (now Lord Court), Dec., 1910 to Aug., 1914; on active serve in 1st King Edward's Horse, K.O.D.R., 5th Aug., 1914 to 26th Nov., 1918; served in France and Italy, Apl. 1915 to 26th Nov., 1918; promoted capt., 1914; major, 1916; wounded, 1917; D.S.O., July, 1918; Bar to D.S.O., gazetted, Mar., 1919; twice ment. in desps., astt. priv. sec. (apptmts.) to sec. of state (Lord Milner), May, 1919; Mr. Winston Churchill, Feb., 1921; Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; priv. sec. (apptmts.) to sec. of state, Mr. Thomas, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Nov., 1924; Lord Passfield, 8th June, 1929; visited Canada on C.O. business, 1922, and again in 1923 as rep. at Imp. Forestry Confce; mem., bd. of govt., Imp. Forestry Inst.; astt. sec., 1st Oct., 1930.

GALEA, ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1930)—B. 1872; ed. Malta Lyceum; ent. Malta civ. serv., 1891; 3rd cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1896; 2nd cls. clk., 1st July, 1901; astt. regisr. cts. of judicial pol., 17th Dec., 1913; regisr., 6th Jan., 1914; transfd., secretariat, 14th May, 1918; ch. clk., liet.-gov.'s office, Sept., 1920; oh. clk. to legislature, 26th Dec., 1921; compt., charitable institutions, 4th Apr., 1923; offi. sec. to head of ministry, 1st Mar., 1927.

GALEA, PROF. ROBERT V., L.S. & A.—B. 1882; matric. (with hona.), Malta Univ., 1900; graduated as land survr. and architect, 1903; lect. on engnr. and architecture, Malta Univ., 1906; prof., ditto, 1915; ag. rector of univ., 1923; el. for 1st divn. in leg. assem., June, 1924; re-el., Aug., 1927; min. for pub. health and charitable institutions, 16th Aug., 1927, also min. for treasy., 7th Aug., 1929.

GALLOWAY, WILLIAM CHARLES GOMBLETON.—B. 1892; Lond. Institution of City and Guilds bronze medalist, telephony, A.I.E.E.; Br. P.O., engng. dept., Jan., 1912; on active serv., R.E. (Signals), Nov., 1915; astt. teleg. engnr., P. and T. dept., F.M.S., June, 1920; engnr., Oct., 1926; ag. ar. engnr., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., May-July, 1928, and Feb-May, 1929; sr. engnr., Dec., 1930.

GALT, HON. ALEXANDER CABIM.—B. 1853; ed. Hellmuth Coll., Toronto Univ.; practised law in Toronto, 1876-1896; Roseland, B.C., 1896-1905; Winnipeg, 1906-1912; judge of the ct. of king's bench, Manitoba, 1912; contrib. to Can. Law Journal and Can. Law Times.

GALWAY, LT.-COL. SIR HENRY LIONEL, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.M.G. (1899), D.S.O. (1896).—B. 1859; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; entered army, May, 1878; promoted lieut., Mar., 1881; capt., Oct., 1887; brevet-maj., May, 1897; maj., Mar., 1899; and lt.-col., Apr., 1901; apptd. dep. comsrr. and vice-consul, Oil Rivers prot., Mar., 1891; dep. comsrr. and consul, Niger Coast prot., Jan., 1897; and div. comsrr. Niger Coast prot. (now South. Nigeria), Apr., 1899; ag. consul-gen. Niger Coast prot., Aug. to Nov., 1896; Jan., 1897; Feb., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ag. highcomsrr., South. Nigeria, Mar. to Dec., 1900; Brass expdn., 1895 (desps., medal with clasp, D.S.O.); Benin expdn., 1897 (desps., clasp, brevet majority); Aro expdn. as chief political officer, 1901-2 (desps., medal with clasp); gov., St. Helena, 1902; gov., Gambia, 30th Sept., 1911; gov., S. Australia, 27th Feb., 1914; assumed govt., 18th Apr., 1914, retired, 1920.

GALWAY, JOHN PATRICK FRANCIS.—Ed. St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Liverpool; clk., vety. pathological labv., Kabete, Aug., 1910; seconded, milv. serv., 1915; corp., E. African Vty. Corps, 1st June, 1916; lieut., ditto, 17th Oct., 1917; lieut., 3rd K.A.R.; "1914-15" Star, Gen. Serv. and Victory meda.; labour inspn. offr., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919; office supt., P.W.D., Dec., 1922.
GAMBLE, F. C.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; res. mag., July, 1923.

GAMBLE, JOSEPH SAMUEL.—Ag. sub-inspr. pol., B. Guiana, 1905; sub-inspr. pol., 1906; lythe musketry certif., 1908; dist. inspr. pol., 1912; county inspr., 1920; ag. dep. inspr.-gen. and supt., fire brigade for various periods in 1920, 1922, 1924 and 1926; inspr.-gen., pol., Fiji, and commdt., Fiji defence force, Oct., 1927; nom. mem., leg. coun., 1928.
GAME, FREDERICK HAROLD ROBERTS, A.C.G.I. (1921), A.M.I.C.E. (1925).—B. 1900; ed. Taunton Schl. and Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Technology, S. Kensington; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Uganda, Sept., 1925.

GAME, AIR VICE-MARSHALL SIR PHILIP WOOLCOCK, G.B.E. (1929), K.C.B. (1924), C.B. (1919), D.S.O. (1915).—B. 1876; ed. Charterhouse; ent. R.A., 1895; capt., 1901; adjt., 1902; major, 1912; air vice-marshal, 1922; G.S.O., 3rd and 2nd grade, W.O., 1910-14; served, S. Africa, 1901-02 (despa., Queen's Med. five clasps); European war, 1914-18; (despa. six times) dir. training and organization, Air Ministry, 1919-22; commanded R.A.F., India, 1922-23; air. mem. for personnel on leg. coun., 1923-28; ret. list, 1929; gov., New South Wales, 1930.

GAMMANS, LEONARD DAVID.—B. 1895; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1915; B.E.F., France, 1916; lieut. and capt., 1917; seconded for serv. as instr. in gunnery and equitation, R. Mily. Coll., Canada, May, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; passed cadet, Jan., 1922; ag. asst. dist. offr., K. Kubu, Apr., 1922; asst. supervar., cust., P. Dickson, Oct., 1922; ditto, P. Buntar, Mar., 1923; served at Malayan pavilion, Br. Empire Exhibn., Mar., 1925; offr., cls. IV., June, 1927; attd., co-op. socs. dept., F.M.S., Oct., 1927; offr., cls. III., June, 1932.

GANADO, ROBERTO F., LL.D.—B. 1875; ed. Malta Lyceum; matric., Malta Univ., 1891; LL.D., Malta, 1898; bar, Malta, 1899; advoc. for poor, 1905; mag., judl. pol., 1918; examr. laws, Malta Univ., 1919; chmn., licensing bd., 1920; visitor, notarial acts Nov., 1921; ag. crown advoc., June, 1922; ag. pub. pros. and treas. coun., July to Oct., 1922; chmn., bd. of prison visitors, 1924; ag. judge, supr. cts., June to Sept., 1924; mem., gen. coun. of Univ. of Malta, 1924; judge, supr. cts., Sept., 1925.

GARDINER, FREDERICK GEORGE.—B. 1874; ed. Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch, and Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A., Cape and Oxford; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1896; admitted to Cape bar, 1897; ag. puisne judge, Natal, 1907; atty-gen., Cape Province, 1910; puisne judge of sup. ct. of S. Africa (Cape Prov. Div.), 16th Oct., 1914; judge pres., Cape Prov. divn., 1926.

GARDNER, ALBERT EDWARD, M.M.—Enlisted, Apr., 1900; S. Africa, 1901-02; India, 1902-08; Europ. War, 1914-19; C.S.M., and R.Q.M.S., Somaliland Camel Corp., Oct. 1919-June 1929; mental, in despa., Somaliland operns., 1920; offr., Somaliland pol., July, 1929.
GARDNER, H. M.—Asst. conservator of forests, E.A.P., Mav., 1914; senr. asst. conservator, forests, Kenya, Dec., 1923; ag. conservator, 1925 and 1927; conservator, 1928.
GARDNER, JOHN HYMOP, B.Sc.—B. 1882; ed. Kelvinside Acad. and Univ., Glasgow; asst. engrn., Basutoland, 1909; govt. engrn., Swariland, 1919; served with R.E., Flanders, 1916-18.

GARDNER, JOSEPH HENRY.—B. 1882; active serv. with Imp. Yeomanry, S. African War; mil. serv., E. African Exped. Force attd. Rly. Corps, 1916-18; asst. loco. supt., Talensi-Rly., Mar., 1918; dist. loco. supt., 1919; loc. running supt., 1933.

GARLICK, GEORGE HERBERT, M.R.C. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.M.R.E. (Camb.), B. 1886; med. offr., Johore, 1917; Malaya r.c. serv., 1923; ag. prin. med. offr., Johore, 1927; physician and radiologist, Johore, 1927.

GARNER, JOSEPH JOHN SAVILLE.—B. 1876; ed. Highgate Schol. (senr. foundation schol., 1876) and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (mod. lang., schol. 1926-29); mod. and medi. languages, Pt. I, cl. 2, div. 1; ditto, Pt. II, cls. 1; B.A., 1930; asst. mod. lang. mast., Haileybury Coll., 1929-30; apptd. after compet. exam., asst pr. D.O., Oct., 1930.
GARNETT, CHRISTOPHER BRAINSHAW, M.A. (Agr.).—B. 1903; ed. Clayesmore Schol., Winchester and Peterhouse, Cambridge; B.A., 1924; M.A. (Cantab.), 1928; dist. agril. offr., Kangayi Territory, May, 1926.

GARNETT, W. J., B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1908; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Jan., 1924; seconded for service in Overseas Stills, Jan., 1919; promoted asst. prin. under clause 1, 1924; asst. to the rep. in the Commonwealth of Australia of H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., 1931.

GARRAN, SIR ROBERT RANDOLPH, K.C.M.G. (1920), KT. BACH. (1917), C.M.G. (1898-1907); barrister-at-law; sec. to drafting comm. of Australian Fed. Convention, 1897-98; to atty-gen.'s dept., C. of A., Jan., 1908; and parl. draftsman; solr.-gen., 1915; chmn., Canberra univ.; author of "The Coming Commonwealth," 1897, and joint auth. (with the Hon. Sir John Quick) of "An Annotated Constitution of the Austn. Commonwealth," 1901.

GARRAWAY, THOMAS WILLIAM SAVILE.—Ed. Denstone Coll., Staffs; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1906; admitted to Grenada bar, 1907; Vincent bar, 1909; mag., 2nd dist., St. Vinc., July, 1916; ag. atty-gen. on two occasions; ch. of pol. and supt., prisons, Oct. to Dec., 1919; chmn., educn. commn., 1919; mag., W. Dist., Grenada, Sept., 1920; mag. R. dist., June, 1921; ag. atty-gen., Nov., 1926 to Oct., 1927; mag. dist., Oct., 1927; ag. ch. just., on three occasions; ch. just. and mag., St. Lucia, Oct., 1930.

GARROOD, HARRY ST. CLAIR.—B. 1882; ed. St. Polycarp's Schol., Farnham; articled archt., Sudan civ. serv., 1906; 2nd lieut., gen. hist. capt., 1918; served with Hedjaz Expd. Force, Br. arm., Jeddah, 1919 with rank of major; 3rd cls. Order of Nahlia from King Hamid; ment. in despa.; demob., 1920; gen. head of Egypt to 1928; crown survr. and engnr., Germa, 1929.

GARSON, ALEXANDER DEVIS.—B. 1904; ed. Epsom Coll. and Magdalene Coll., Cambria; 2nd cls. hona., hist. tripods; M.A.; cadet, N.C.O., 1928; asst. dist. offr., 1931; seconded to C.O. asst. prin., Oct., 1933.

GARTON, HARRY.—B. 1889; served in R.F., France, Salonica, Egypt and Palestine, 1914 to Apr., 1919; clk. to court martials of R.A.F., May to Sept., 1919; Admin. Off., Oct., 1919 to Oct., 1922; served on Naval and Allied Coman. of Control in Germany, 1921 to Oct., 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 2nd cls., 1922.

GARVEY, FRANCIS WILLOUGHBY.—B. 1881; served in S. Africa, first in the ranks and afterwards as transport offr.; served Transvaal 1904-08; asst. comsnr., pol., S. Nigeria, comsnr., pol., 1918; read. thanks of govt. investigation and suppression of human sacrifice Agbara Juju at Awka, 1921; comsnr., pol., 1922; dir. prisons, S. Provs. and Colony, 1925 to Mar., 1925; dir., prisons, S. Provs. Colony, Mar., 1925.

SURVEY, RONALD HERBERT, B.A.—B. 1903; ment Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; Fiji and Western Pacific services, Nov., 1927; dist. offr., Mal., Apr., 1929; dist. Oct., 1930; asst. sec., W. Pacific high school, Aug., 1932.

RIVIN, THOMAS FORREST, K.C.—B. 1881; counsel, Ceylon, Mar., 1908; ag. solr.-gen., 1913; ag. addtl. dist. judge, Colombo, Feb., 1922; ag. atty.-gen., June 1922 to Dec., 1923; ag. puise just., sup. ct., Jan., 1923; ag. just., June, 1924; ag. ch. just., July to Dec., 1926.
SH, VINCENT GORDON.—Served in Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1911-15; sub-inspr., 1915; and 3rd grade Seawana exam., 1923; impr.

STER, BOSLEY ALAN REX, M.A., Dip. (Cantab.), D.I.C., F.R.M.S., F.E.S., F.Z.S. 1896; war serv., 1914-19; asst. entomologist, of agric., F.M.S., Nov., 1922; malaria offr., Nov., 1926; entomologist, Inst. Med., 1927; prof., biology, King Edward VII Inst. of Medicine, S'pore, Aug., 1930.

TFIELD, WILLIAM HUBERT, M.C.—B. ed. Streatham Gram.Schl. and St. George's London; apptd. to the 2nd divn. of the nav. serv., 1914; joined Queen's Westminster Regt., 1915; 2nd lieut., 1916; served in France, Belgium, with rank of lieut., 1919; cadet limbs., Nov., 1920; proceeded to China to Chinese, July, 1921; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1921; 2nd asst. prot., Chinese, S'pore, Nov., 1922; offr., cls. IV, Dec., 1926; offr. cls. III., 1931; ag. immigrn. offr., Aug., 1933.
UNT, A. E.—B. 1897; entd. C.O., Aug., 1915; apptd. asst. clk., Oct., 1915; on mil. ser. Dec., 1915 to Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Dec., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 28th Dec.,

UNTLETT, HUGH GAUNTLETT.—B. 1873; barrister-at-law; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1899; adm. int. civ. serv., Jamaica by compet. exam., 1919; and apptd. ast. clk. of cts., Mar., 1920; dep. ditto, Apr., 1903; ag. clk. of cts., on various occasions between June, 1907 and June, 1915; clk. of cts., Feb., 1915; ag. res. mag., Thomas, Aug., 1926 and from Sept., 1926; mag., 1929.

WERNER, SIDNEY.—Temp. ast. to irrign. dept., P.W.D., B. Guiana, 1st Aug., 1916 to Aug., 1918; ag. dist. engnr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1919; 1st cls. overseer, July, 1919; clk. of works, Cordia, 20th Aug., 1920.

WLDENHUYS, FRANS EDUARD.—B. 1889; B. 1910, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S.A.; studied in U.S.A. at Univs. of Cornell, Wisconsin, Illinois and California; B.Sc., Cornell Univ., 1913 and Ph.D., 1917; teacher of nature and agric. science, Grey Coll. Sch., 1918; "Die Landbau-weekblad," Bloemfontein, 1919; first ch., divn. of agric. economics marketing, dept. of agr., S.A., 1925; undersecretary agr., 1928; dir., forestry, 1931.

NEVE, FREDERIC PIERRE, K.C. (1929).— B. 1889; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; called to Middle Temple, Nov., 1909; ag. dist. and stip. mag. for varying periods; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, July, 1921; addnl. substitute procureur and advoc. gen., 1922; substitute procureur and advoc. gen., 1923; ag. ditto, Aug., 1924 to Feb., 1925 and Apr., 1926 to Jan., 1927; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1927; ag. col. sec., Apr.-Sept., 1928, Mar.-Dec., 1929 and Apr.-Sept., 1930; ag.ch. just., Mar., 1931.

GENT, GERARD EDWARD JAMES, D.S.O., M.C. —B. 1896; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and Trinity Coll., Oxford; served with Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., 1914-19; ast. prin., C.O., 1920; sec., rubber restriction comtee., 1922; act. prin., 1923; pvt. sec. to parly. under sec., 1924; prin., 1927; dep. sec., C.O. confce., 1927; sec., col. services comtee., 1929; seconded to India Office as a sec. of Indian Round Table Confce., 1930; accompd. Permt. Under-Sec. of State to Ceylon, Malaya and Hongkong, 1932.

GENTLE, GEORGE EDWIN PALMER, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1880; ed. Denstone Coll.; clk., gov.'s office, Grenada, 1898; warden, P.W.D., St. Vincent, 1901; road surr., Grenada, 1905; ast. supt., pub. wks., 1914, 1916, 1918, 1921, 1922; ast. supt., pub. wks., 1916; supt., pub. wks., 1922.

GEORGE, REGINALD, R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1887; ed. H.M. Schl. Ship "Conway"; seconded for serv., Lake Nyasa, 1915; commdr., marine transport, Sept., 1918; ag. marine supt., in 1922, 1925, 1928 and 1931; marine supt., Oct., 1932.
GEORGE, NANCY MARGARET FANNY.—B.1900; ed. St. Catherine's High Schl., Malta, Devonport Secondary Schl., Froebel Training Coll., Bedford; mistress, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Jan., 1928.

GERAHTY, CHARLES CYRIL, K.C. (1931).— B. 1888; ed. Trent Coll.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1909; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Res.) Batt. E. Lancs. Regt., 1910; lieut., 1911; capt., 1914; ast. res. and commr. prov. ots., N. Nigeria, 1911-14; war serv. in France, Belgium and W. Africa, 1914-19; restored to civil estabtn., N. Provs., Nigeria, Nov., 1918; legal ast., lands directorate, W.O., Sept., 1919 to Sept., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1920-26; ag. atty.-gen. and puise judge on various occasions; joint compiler, 2nd vol., stat. law of Cyprus, 1923; attygen., Dec., 1926; atty.-gen., Trinidad, 1929; puise judge, S.S. and judge, F.M.S., Apr., 1932.

GERMAN, RALPH LIONEL.—B. 1892; ed. Portsmouth Gram. Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (class. sch.); B.A. 1914; served with Hampshire Regt., 1915-19; Tank Corps, 1919-20; cadet, F.M.S., Feb., 1921; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., Perak, Feb., 1922; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. V., Oct., 1923; ast. dist. offr., Ipoh, Mar., 1924; offr., cls. IV., Oct., 1924; dist. offr., Pekan, June, 1926; offr., cls. III., Oct., 1929; supt., marine and cust., chandu, posts and tels., audr. and pub. pros., Kelantan, Jan., 1931; ag. ast. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Jan., 1933.
GERMAN, WALTER.—B. 1888; bldg. inspr., sany. bd., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1913; ag. ch. sany. inspr. in addn., Aug., 1919 to May, 1920.
GERRAND, JOHN KENNEDY.—B. 1899; ast. engnr., Basutoland, 1929.

GETHIN, PERCY EDWARD LOVELL, A.F.C. —B. 1884; ed. Radley Coll.; R. Geographical Soc'y. dipl. in surveying, 1908; explrn. in S. Africa, 1909; ast. supt., surveys, Ceylon, 1910; in charge, topographical surveys in the field, 1912; on mily. serv., 1914-20; major, R.F.C., 1916; ast. dir., surveys, Cyprus, 1922; passed exam. in mod. Greek, 1923; ag. reg.-gen., 1923; dir., surveys, Tanganyika Territory, 1926; tech. adviser, air bd., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; dir., civ. aviation, 1930.

GETLEY, ALFRED CURPHEY.—B. 1897; ed. Roborough Schi., Eastbourne; acctnt. branch, Royal Navy, 1915-22; paymr.-lieut., Jan., 1920; ret. at own request, 1922; seconded as pilot to R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1917-20; ent. col. audit dept., Apr., 1923; asst. audr., Uganda, June, 1923; ag. senr. asst. audr. on several occasions; asst. audr., Kenya, 1929; asst. audr., col. audit dept., 1933.
GIBB, JOHN.—B. 1881; supervisor of warehouses, govt. stores, Ceylon, Oct., 1914; col. storekeeper, Sept., 1916.

GIBBINGS, ALBERT VICTOR, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Mar., 1913; Kedah, Apr., 1913; ag. exec. engrnr., Kedah, Jan., 1917; exec. engrnr., Johore, Sept., 1925; ag. senr. exec. engrnr., F.M.S., Apr., 1927; senr. exec. engrnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1929; water wks. engrnr., Semban, June, 1930; senr. exec. engrnr., Klang, Apr., 1931; ag. state engrnr., Pahang, June, 1931; senr. exec. engrnr., Coast, Kuantan, Jan., 1932.
GIBBON, GEOFFREY MCKAY, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.)—B. 1896; ed. Elgin acad., Gordon Coll., Aberdeen and Univ. of Edin.; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, Oct., 1933.
GIBBON, JOHN FRANK, M.B., C.M. (Edin.)—B. 1889; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, Jan., 1899; dist. med. offr., Jan., 1916.
GIBBS, GEORGE HOWARD.—B. 1889; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1920.
GIBSON, CLARENCE HYN.—B. 1876; ag. dist. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., Jan., 1914; confirmed in apptntn., Jan., 1916; a dep. commnr. for the W. Pacific, with powers in Gilbert group, June, 1915.

GIBSON, H. D.—B. 1886; ed. Bedale and Lincoln Gram. Schls.; junr. traffic asst., Nigerian rly., 1910; asst. traffic supt., 1913; dist. ditto, 1920; traffic supt., 1922; divnl. supt., 1927; supt. of line, 1932.

GIBSON, LESLIE BERTRAM.—B. 1896; ed. King Henry VIII. Schi., Coventry; hon. LL.B. (War), London; flight sub-lieut., R.N.A.S., attds. Grand Fleet; lieut. R.A.F.; cadet, S.S., Mar., 1920; attd. atty-gen.'s office, S'pore, Apr., 1920; attd. food control office, Penang, May, 1920; dep. dir. food prodn., Apr., 1921; passed cadet, Mar., 1922; dep. regar. and asst. off. assignee, Penang, June, 1922; offr., cls. V., Mar., 1923; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1925; asst. regar., sup. ct., Ipoh, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1927; ag. dep. pub. pros., Penang, Dec., 1930; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1932; ag. legal advr., Kedah, Aug., 1932.

GIBSON, ROY ALEXANDER.—B. 1885; ed. Brandon High Schi. and Brandon Coll.; ent. fed. govt. serv. at Regina, 1908; transfd. to Ottawa, and apptd. ch. clk. to dep. min., 1908; asst. dep. min., June, 1919; oomsnr., Yukon and N.W. Territories, 1923.

GIBSON, WILLIAM SUMNER, C.M.G. (1834), R.A., Oxon., Barrister-at-Law (Lincoln's Inn);—B. 1876; ed. Marlborough and Keble Coll., Oxford (schol.), B.A. (1st clas. med. hist.), 1899; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1899; services lent to Johore govt., July-Nov., 1908; asst. man., monopolies dept., Penang, Oct., 1909; called to the bar, June, 1913; legal adviser, Kedah, Jan., 1916; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1916; judge of high. cd., Kedah (in addn.), July, 1916; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919; pres., comm. to inquire into organisation and expend. of govt. dep'ts., F.M.S., Feb., 1922; offr., cls. I.A., Dec., 1922; legal advr., F.M.S., Dec., 1922; pres., S.S. trade comm., Mar., 1933.

GIE, S. F. N., Ph.D.—B. 1886; ed. Van Coll., Stellenbosch and Amsterdam; lect. Univ.; teacher and inspr. schls., Cape Prov., hist. and chmn., senate, Stellenbosch; 1918; under-secr., educn., Union of S. Africa, 1926; sec., ditto, 1927; publications include new of S. Africa in Afrikaans in two vols.

GILBERT, HUMPHREY RALEIGH.—B. 1895; ent. R. Navy, 1912; served, Great War; ret., Jan., 1923; lieut. commodr. (ret'd), Feb.; admtn. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan.; asst. dist. offr., Jan., 1929; ag. dist. offr., N.

GILBERT, JOSEPH TROUSSELL, O.B.E., B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1888; ed. Bedford and Exm Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Lincolns Inn, 1914; asst. collr. (now asst. dist. commnr.), Zibar, Jan., 1912; ag. mag., Mar. to Jun., 1912; 2nd asst. sec., and priv. sec. to res., Jun.; off. examr. in the Swahili language, senr. ast. sec., Sept., 1924; asst. ch. sec., 1928; del., C.O. confcn., 1930; ag. ch. sec. various periods between 1924 and 1928; (for the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, cls. IV., 1928-III., 1930.

GILCHRIST, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1879; entd. H.M. Customs, B. Guiana, Dec.; copyist, regar.'s office, Oct., 1900; dist. ditto, Mar., 1902; 5th cls. clk., cust., 1904; commissary, Sept., 1905; clk. to stty. gr., 1906; stip. mag., J.T., coroner, and comm. oaths, 1910; chmn., Rose Hall village council of lands comm., 1911; called to the bar, Inn, 1914; ag. asst. govt. sec., Mar., 1914; censor (war) under defence scheme, 1914; censor (war) under defence scheme, 20; recd. thanks of S. of S.; practised at Edinbar, 1920-21; commr. to inquire into distur. H.M. penal stattn., B. Guiana, 1923; ag. puimse judge, in 1923 and 1925-6; ag. puimse judge, in 1923, 1924 and 1925-6; ag. puimse judge, in 1926 and 1927; puimse judge, Sept., 1927; puimse judge, Trinidad, 1931.
GILKS, J. L., C.M.G. (1933), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.)—ag. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1909; prin. med. offr., Feb., 1921; mem., exec. and leg. comm., med. and sany. services, 1926.
GILL, EDWIN—B. 1896; on naval serv., 1919; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., 12th July, 1926.
GILL, EDWIN LEONARD, D.Sc.—B. 1877; Manchester Museum, June, 1904; curat. cock Museum, Newcastle-on-Tyne, Jul., 1927; asst. R. Scottish Museum, July, 1927; African Museum, Cape Town, Jan., 1928.
GILL, HENRY SEWELL CUMBER, M.A.—1903; cadet, provincial adminstr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1927; asst. dist. offr., Aug.
GILL, OSCAR SAYLE.—B. 1880; engnr., elec. dept., Ceylon, Nov., 1927; engrnr. and man., elec. undertakings, June.
GILL, RONALD GODDARD.—B. 1886; Haberdashers' Askes Schi. (Hampstead); serv. overseas, 1917-19; commissioned, 1917; temp. clk., Miny. of Lab., 1919; after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.C.O. Mr.

GILLMAN, CLEMENT, F.G.S., M.S.A., F.R.G.S.—B. 1882; ed. Swiss Federal schl., Zurich; on rly. constr. and surve., Africa, 1906-14; served E.A.P. Force, b. capt., rly. corps.; exec. engrnr., E. Afric. rlys. (occupied territory), 1916-18; dist. ditto, 1918-19; dist. engrnr., Tanganyi 1919; senr. dist. engnr., Apr., 1925; ch. r., Apr., 1928.

LMOUR, ANDREW.—B. 1898; ed. Royal Schl., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; served with A. & S.H., B.E.F., 1916-17; M.A. hons., 1920; cadet, S.S., Apr., 1921; attch. to councillors' office, Penang, May, 1921; offr., astt. contrlr., lab., Penang and astt. prot., Kedah, Apr., 1924; astt. contrlr., lab., cem., Feb., 1926; collr., land rev., S'pore, 1927; astt. astt., govt. monops., S'pore, 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; res., Jan., June, 1928; dist. offr., Jasir, Sept., ditto, U. Kelantan, Dec., 1930; offr., cls., Apr., 1933.

MSON, FRANKLIN CHARLES.—B. 1890; B.A., cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; attd. to col. sec's office, Dec., 1914; attd. of naval intell. offr., in add. to his duties; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, in add. to his own duties; on mil. duty, 1918; astt. pol. astt. col. sec., July, 1919; office astt. to agt., N. C. Prov., Sept., 1919; addnl. astt., cem., Mar., 1920; 4th astt. ditto, Sept., 1920; astt. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1922; landing offr., cust., Feb., 1924; ag. dep. collr., cust., Mar., May, 1928; addnl. astt. dir. educn., Mar., sec. to min. educn., July, 1931; astt. agt., Trincomalee, Feb., 1932.

MEN, JAMES ALEXANDER, M.A.—Ed. Trinity, Dublin; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Dec., 1917; lieut., July, 1917; ag. capt., Nov., 1918; served in France and Palestine; demob., Jan., 1919; astt. dir. of edn., Iraq, Apr., 1920; ag. min. of educn., Apr., 1922.

MENDAY, VINCENT G., O.B.E. (1929), M.A., F.R.S., Diploma, Forestry, Edin. (hons. nat. sci.), F.G.S., Diploma, Forestry, dist. commr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. commr., N. Frontier, Jan., 1914 to June, 1919; offr. in charge, Somali disarmament, N. Frontier dist., Mar., 1919 (A.G.S. Med.); served as dist. commr., Br. Somaliland, Mar.-Dec., 1920; astt. dist. commr., Oct., 1922; ag. commr., Sept., 1922; dist. commr., 1926; mission in Somalia, 1927-28; ag. prov. commr., 1931 and from Mar., 1933.

LENDINGHAM, JAMES, B.Sc. (Lond.), I.C.E.—B. 1888; ed. Queen's Univ., Belfast; dir., surveys, Gold Coast, Oct., 1919; dep. cadastral branch, Jan., 1920; dir., cadastral sch., Dec., 1920; dep. survr.-gen., Apr., 1924; survr.-gen. on various occasions, 1921-26; survr.-gen., Oct., 1926.
ODDBOLT, S. B.—B. 1884; clk., treasy., Cape Town, Transvaal, 1905; Union of S. Africa, 1910; clk., treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1920; ch. grade II, 1924; ditto, grade I, 1928; commr., July, 1932.
ODDARD, HARRY FREDERICK.—B. 1902; cad. after compet. exam., cler. off., C.O., 4th Oct., 1920.

ODDARD, THOMAS NELSON, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1889; ed. Wadham Coll., Oxford; B.A.; priv. sec. to gov., Falkland Is. and clk. of leg. couns., Apr., 1914; lieut., Falkland Is. vol. force, Aug., 1914; capt., 1917; O.C.; postal censor, 1914; proper offr. of the n., prte., 1914; chief clk., col. sec's office, 1915; ag. commr. of currency, 1918; ag. audr., 1920; astt. col. sec., Sierra Leone; clk., exec. coun., 1921; A.D.C. to ag. gov., and to gov., 1922-23; census offr., 1921; astt. astt. col. sec. for various periods between 1922 and 1930; ag. col. sec. for various periods 1922-33; ch. astt. col. sec., Sept., 1930; gov.'s dep. on various occasions, 1930 and 1931; compiler, Sierra Leone, Handbook 1924.

GODDEN, HENRY JAMES, M.B.E. (1929)—B. 1877; telegraphist, Basutoland, 1902; postmr., 1907; clk. to astt. commr., 1917; ag. astt. commr., 1922; passed 1st grade Sesuto, 1922; ch. clk., mast. of ct. and regiar., 1924.

GODFREY, GEORGE CROSBIE.—B. 1880; ed. Waimataiata Schl., Timaru, N.Z.; N.Z. printing and stationery dept., 1897; priv. sec. to min. of pub. wks., 1901; astt. ch. clk., pub. wks. dept., 1908; ch. clk., 1914; astt. under-secr., 1920; sec. marine, 1923; sec. of lab. and commr. of employment, 1932.

GODFREY, SIR JOSEPH ED., KT. BACH. (1914).—B. 1856; M.B. and C.M., Edin., 1882; gov. med. offr., B. Guiana, Feb., 1883; med. inspr. of estates hospitals, May, 1898; deputy regiar. of births, deaths and marriages, 1898; deputy chmn., cent. bd. of health, 1902; astt. on sew. occasions as surg.-gen., regiar.-gen., and chmn. of cent. bd. of health; apptd. surg.-gen., regiar.-gen. of h. d. and m., chmn. cent. bd. of health, and offl. mem. of ct. of policy, and comb. ct., July, 1904; dep. chmn. of bd. of agric., 1905; chmn. of poor law commn., 1906; del. for B. Guiana and Bahamas to Internat. Leproay Confec., Bergen, 1909.

GODLEY, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR ALEXANDER JOHN, K.C.B., K.C.M.G.—B. 1867; lieut., Royal Dublin Fusiliers, 1886; adjutant, 1889; capt., 1896; major, 1897; colonel, 1906; apptd. comdt. New Zealand Forces, 1910, with local rank of major-general; comdg. New Zealand expeditionary force in Egypt and at Dardanelles, 1914-15; gazetted major-general, 1915; temp. lieut.-gen. in command of an Anzac army corps, France, 1916; lieut.-gen., 1918; gov. and comdr. in ch., Gibraltar, 1928; foreign decorations: Legion of Honour, Croix de Commandeur (1917); Order of the Crown, Grand Officier (Belgian) 1917; Order of the White Eagle (Serbian), 1917; Ordre de Guerre (Belgian), 1918.

GODSALL, WALTER DOUGLAS, B.A. (Oxon.).—Cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. N'Eliya kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, Badulla kach., Aug., 1924; ditto, Kegalla kach., Mar., 1926; office astt., Ratnapura kach., Dec., 1928; ditto, Galle kach., Mar., 1929; ag. astt. govt. agt., Kandy, Jan., 1930.

GODWIN-AUSTEN, ROBERT ANNESLEY, F.R.G.S.—B. 1885; ed. St. Lawrence Coll., Ramsgate; employed on rly. and land surveys, Canada, 1910-14; served with line and field survey units, France, 1915-19; ment. in despatches; rly. survey, Persia, 1920-21; astt. survr., N. Rhodesia, 1929; dir. roads, 1927; dep. dir., surveys, Tangaika, 1929; dir., land registn. and surveys, Cyprus, Sept., 1933.

GOLDBURG, JOSEPH.—B. 1886; entd. crown agents' office, 1902; astt. head, gen. dept., 1919; astt. head, finance dept., 1922; astt. head, shipping dept., 1925; astt. head, finance dept., 1930; dep. head, finance dept., May, 1933; acotnt., W. African Currency bd., 1912-33.

GOLLAN, SIR HENRY COWPER, KT. BACH. (1921), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1868; ed. at Charterhouse; M.A., Edin. (1887); called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Jan., 1891, N. circuit; priv. sec. to Col. Lugard, commr. and comdt., W.A.F.F., Sept. 1st, 1899; priv. sec. to H.C. of Nor. Nig., Jan.-Sept., 1900; ag. sec. to the admistr., June-Aug., 1900; atty.-gen. of Nor. Nig., Sept., 1900; ag. ch. just., Nov., 1900-Apr., 1901; ch. just., Nov., 1901; prepared criminal code for N. Nigeria, 1904; compiled proc. of N. Nigeria, 1905; ch. just., Bermuda, 1904; prepared Cts. Consolidating Act, Bermuda, Dec., 1906; chmn. of produce comm., 1905 and of aliens comm., 1906; chmn. of bd. of educ., Bermuda, July, 1907; atty.-gen., Trinidad, 1911; atty.-gen., Ceylon, 1918; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1924; chmn., salaries comm., 1928-29; ret., Aug., 1930.
GONTIER, GEORGE, C.A., L.I.A.—B. 1869; ed. Commissioner's Sch., Montreal and priv. tuition; audr.-gen., Dominion of Canada, Jan., 1924.

GOOCH, STANLEY JOHN WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., chtd. civ. engrn.—B. 1889; asst. engrn., P.W.D., F.M.S., June, 1912; exec. engrn., Johore, May, 1922; sr. exec. engrn., P.W.D., June, 1927; sr. exec. engrn., hydraulics, Selangor, Mar., 1929; ag. state engrn., Selangor, Jan., 1930.
GOOD, CRICL ALBERT.—B. 1892; called to bar, 1913; served in Army, lieut. and capt., 1915-20; pol. mag., Gold Coast, 1921.

GOODALL, EDWARD BASIL HERBERT, C.B.E. (1934), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1886; ed. Loughborough Grammar Sch., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; asst. nat. coms., N. Rhodesia, Mar., 1909; nat. coms., Sept., 1911; asst. mag., Nov., 1921; ag. dist. coms., Apr., 1928; dist. oomsh., Feb., 1929; prov. coms., Apr., 1929; ag. sec., nat. affrs., Sept.-Oct., 1931; mem., leg. coun.

GOODMAN, ARTHUR MITCHELL.—B. 1886; ed. New Coll., Oxon.; B.A. 1908; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1909; supernumerary mag., Ipoh, June, 1915; pres., rent assessment bd. for Penang, July, 1918; offr., cls. IV., F.M.S., Jan., 1919; supernum. offr., cls. III., Nov., 1921; offr., cls. II; ag. sec. for Chinese affrs., S.S., Nov., 1926; sec., ditto, Jan., 1927; offr., cls. IA, mem., exec. coun., S.S., July, 1931; res. conn., Penang, Apr., 1933.
GOODMAN, REGINALD ERNEST.—B. 1886; olk., Beaufortland, 1913; acmtg. olk., 1916; fin. sec., mast. and reglar., deeds, Bechuana land Prot., Oct., 1927.

GOODWIN, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR THOMAS HERBERT JOHN CHAPMAN, K.C.B. (1919), K.C.M.G. (1932), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O. (1898), hon. D.Sc. (Oxon.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.).—B. 1871; ed. Newton Coll., Devon and St. Mary's Hosp., London; comsdr., Army Med. Serv., 1893; staff surg., Mohmand Field Force, N.W. Frontier, 1897 (desps., med. and clasps); European War, 1914-18 (3 times ment. in desps., Mona Star, 1914, Allied and Victory meda.); A.D.M.S. with Mr. Balfour's mission to U.S.A., 1917; dir.-gen., Army Med. Serv., 1918-23; retired, 1923; comdr., Legion of Honour; comdr., Belgian Order of Leopold; comdr., Italian Order of the Crown; Belgian Croix de Guerre; American D.S.M., Knight of Grace, Order of St. John of Jerusalem; gov., Queensland, 1927-32.

GOONETILLEKE, OLIVER ERNEST, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1892; asst. audr., rly., Ceylon, Aug., 1921; asst. col. audr., Feb. to Dee., 1923; asst. col. audr., Feb., 1925; ag. col. audr., Feb. to Aug., 1927; audr.-gen., July, 1931.

GOPSIILL, WALTER LEONARD, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—Ed. Alleyne's Sch., Dulwich, King's Coll. and St. Thomas's Hosp.; R.F.C. (2nd lieut.), 1917; lieut., R.A.F., 1918-20; med. offr., Zanzibar, 1926.
GORDON, HON. SIR ALEXANDER, Kt. Bach. (1930).—Judge of sup. ct., New South Wales, 1910-28.

GORDON, CHARLES AUGUSTINE.—B. 1882; ed. Stonyhurst Coll., St. Edmund's Coll., Hert. and Edinburgh Univ. (faculty of law); writer to the signet (W.S.); war serv., France and Flanders, 1915-19 (The Royal Scots); asst. admr. m. Zanzibar, 1925; offr. admr. and jud. tr. Nigeria, 1929.
GORDON, SIR DAVID JOHN, Kt. (1925).—B. 1865; M.L.C. of S. Australia, 1913; pres., leg. coun. since July, 1932.

GORDON, GUY MONTGOMERY, B.A., T.C.D., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1885; mil. R.E. (S.R.), Gallipoli, Egypt, Palestine, France, 1915-20 (ment. in desps.); asst. dir., Iraq, 1920; dist. wks. offr., 1921; dir., Baghdad, Aug.-Sept., 1922; Basrah, Oct., 1921 to Jan., 1922; dist. Bahamas, 1923; asst. D.P.W., Trinida., 1928.

GORDON, HON. WESLEY ASHBY (Can.), K.C., M.P.—B. 1884; ed. at Osgoode Coll. and Osgoode Hall; 1st el. to H. Canada, g.e., July, 1930; min. of immigr. colonization in Bennett cabinets, Aug., 1931; elec. by accl. after assuming office, Aug., also min. of lab., Feb., 1932.

GORDON-HALL, WILLIAM ALBERT, B. 1894; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trinity Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; M.V.I., Mar., 1923; dist. offr., Kuala Lumpur, May, 1926; offr., cls. III., May, 1931; dist. Krian, May, 1932; ag. sec., res., Selangor.

GORING, GEORGE INGHAM, A.R.T.C. (gow), A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1890; ed. Alton Sch.; asst. mech. engrn., col. transport; asst. Br. Guiana; ag. ch. mech. engrn., transport, harbors dept., Nov., 1932-Aug., 1933; ch. engrn., Sept., 1933.
GORMLEY, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1894; Dover County Schl. and Reading Univ.; Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1927; asst. dist. May, 1929.

GORSUCH, LESLIE HAROLD.—B. 1895; Ipswich Schl. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (1920); war serv., May, 1916-Jan., lieut., Sept., 1917; cadet, F.M.S. Dec., attached to dist. office, Pekan; offr., cls. IV., 1923; 4th asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1923; asst. sec. to res., Perak, July, 1926; offr., cls. IV., dist. offr., Jelebu, Dec., 1927; offr. cls. IV., Dec., 1932; asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., 1933.

GOSDEN, MINNIE, M.B., B.S., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—War serv., V.A.D. nurse, 1914-17; chem. pathologist, Hale chem. lab., London hosp., 1925; past asst., 1926; asst. bacteriological dept., 1927; med. offr., Lingfield Epileptic Colony, bacteriologist, Cyprus, Feb., 1929.
GOSLING, WILLIAM RICHARD, B.A.—ed. Sutton Valence and Cambridge Univ.; in France, the Balkans and Turkey, 1914-18; demob. with rank of major, 1919; asst. coms., Gold Coast, May, 1920.

GOSTELOW, CECIL, F.I.A. (Lond.).—ed. Fort St. Model High Schl., Sydney; actuary, N.Z. nat. provident fund, 1921; actuary, N.Z. govt. life insurance dep., actuary, govt. life insurance and friendly dep., 1923; also sec., govt. life insurance, govt. actuary, 1926.
GOTT, JOHN BURY.—B. 1890; ed. Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; supr., educ. N. Provs., Nigeria, May, 1922.

GOURLAY, WILLIAM NEWLAND.—cadet, S.S., May, 1920; offr., cls. V., Feb., asst. dist. judge, Apr., 1925; offr., cls. IV., 1926; 3rd mag. Singapore, Jan., 1929; Gajah, Dec., 1929; offr., cls. III., Feb., 1931; dist. treas. and collr., land rev., Malacca, Nov., 1933; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1933.

ULD, Lieut.-Col. Hon. Sir Albert John, K.T., M.C., B.A. (1898).—B. 1847; solr., M.L.A., N. Rhodesia, 1881-98; M.L.C., 1899-1901; min. for mines in Reid ministry, 1894-8; returned to the senate of Commonwealth parlmt., 1901; min. of the senate, Feb., 1907, to June, 1910; retired from politics.

POWERS, Sir William Frederick, K.C.M.G. (1926), C.M.G. (1919), M.A., Barrister-at-law (Inner Temple).—B. 1875; ed. Rugby School, 1891; Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1898 (1st clas., hon. trip); entd. service of B.S.A. Co., Jan., 1900; asst. native commr., Matabeleland 1900; commr., 1902; 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1902; 2nd cls. res., 1906; 1st cls. res., 1910; chief sec., Sept., 1912; lieut.-gov., N. Nigeria, June, 1921; gov. of Uganda, 1925-32; crown agt. for the cols., 1st July, 1932.

AEME, Alexander William Sutherland, M.A., Mech.E.B. 1882; ed. Malvern, pupil, 1892-6; held various appts. on Eng. Scottish rly.; dep. loco. supt., F.M.S. rlys., 1915-24; ag. loco. supt., F.M.S. rlys., 1917, Apr., 1919, Mar., 1922, Dec., 1923; supt., F.M.S. rlys., Mar., 1924; ch. mech., F.M.S. rlys., Oct., 1932.

RAHAM, Capt. Bernard James.—B. 1882; educ. S. African war, 1899-1902; Cape Garrison Artillery, 1899-10; 2nd lieut., N. Rhodesia pol., June, 1917; temp. major and 2nd in commd., N. Rhodesia Pol. Service Batt., June, 1918; commd. in desps.; hon. capt., Aug., 1919; commd. town and dist. pol., C.I.D., Livingstone, emigr. offr., N. Rhodesia and supt., Livingstone prison, July, 1920; offr. commd. N. Rhodesia pol., supt., cent. prison and dist. commd., volunteers, Mongu, Barotse, Apr., 1923; ditto, Livingstone, Jan., 1923; ditto, Fort Mason, Dec., 1923; capt., N. Rhodesia pol., 1924; J.P. for N. Rhodesia; ag. commd., 1933.

RAHAM, Rt. Hon. George Perry, P.C., M.L.D.—B. 1859; ed. High schls., Iroquois and Morristown, Ontario; journalist; Morristown, Ont.; Ottawa Free Press, Brockville Recorder; elected to Ontario legislature for Brockville, 1898, 1902, 1905, 1908, and 1905; provcl. sec. of Ontario, 1904; leader of the opposition in the Ontario legislature, 1907; sworn of the privy coun. for Canada and min. of rlyws. and canals, 1907; elected to the H. of C. for Brockville, by acclamation, 1907; defeated at g.e., 1911; re-elec. for S. Renfrew, bye-elec., 1912; did not seek re-election at gen.-elec., 1917; re-ele., g.e., 1921; min. of militia and defence, and of naval service in King admntr., Dec., 1921; min. of rlyws. and canals, 1922; senator, Dec., 1926.

RAHAM, James Mackay.—B. 1879; ed. Oxford Gram. Schl. and S. African Coll.; entd. Transvaal civ. ser., 1905; asst. mag., Johannesburg, 1916; mag., Heidelberg, 1927; ditto, Kimberley, 1930; ditto, Durban, 1933.
RAHAM, J. W., M.C., M.A., M.D., Ch.B. (Cantab.), Gold Med. (S. S.), Thesis in Trop. Med.—1896; mily. serv., 1914-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1925.

RAHAM, Sir. Thomas Lyneoch, K.T., M.C. (1920), K.C.—B. 1860; ed. at St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown, and Clare Coll., Camb.; M.A., barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1885; advoc. of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope; M.L.C., 1898; atty.-gen., May to Oct., 1898; col. sec., 1900-1902; atty.-gen., 1902; ag. prime min., June to Aug., 1902; judge of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope, July, 1904; judge pres., Eastern Districts local div., 15th Apr., 1913; chrmn. pub. serv. inquiry comm., June, 1918.

GRANNUM, Sir Edward Allan, K.T., Bach. (1926). C.M.G., 1915.—Passed Barbados, W. I., civ. ser. exam., 1886; 3rd cls. regar.'s off., Jan. to Oct., 1885; 2nd cls., Oct., 1885, to Nov., 1887; cls. master-in-chancery off., Nov., 1887, to June, 1888; honours graduate, diploma, Ontario Com. Coll., Canada, affiliated institute chartered accountants, Canada, Dec., 1889; practised public accountant, Barbados, W.I., to Aug., 1897; asst. auditor, Sierra Leone and Gambia, Sept., 1897, to Jan., 1900; acted auditor May to Nov., 1898; asst. auditor, Gold Coast and Lagos, Jan. to Mar., 1900; auditor, March, 1900; introduced system double entry accounts Gold Coast P.O., July, 1901; prepared scheme for introduction of double entry system of accounts for Accra town council, Aug., 1901; local auditor, Cyprus, 1902; auditor-gen., Mauritius, Apr., 1909; reovr.-gen., Mauritius, 1912; ag. col. sec., on various occasions, 1919-20 and 1922-23; col. sec., Aug., 1923; admst. govt. on various occasions, 1924-30; ret., 1931.

GRANNUM, Reginald Clifton, C.M.G. (1925).—B. 1872; 2nd cls. to gov., Windward Is., Sept., 1891; ditto, St. Vincent, Apr., 1892; supvr. of cust., G. Coast Col., Oct., 1893; asst. local audr., G. Coast and Lagos, May, 1896; local audr., S. Leone, June, 1896; J.P., S. Leone, 1897; ch. asst. treas., G. Coast, Apr., 1903; ag. treas. and mem. ex. and leg. couns., mem. bd. of educ., July, 1903, to June, 1904, Dec., 1904, to Dec., 1905, June, 1906, to May, 1907; Oct., 1907, to Sept., 1908; admitted student of Gray's Inn, Aug., 1908; reovr.-gen., B. Guiana, Sept., 1908; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Dec., 1908; ag. govt. sec., July to Oct., 1913; deputy gov., Sept. to Oct., 1913; treas. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Kenya, Sept., 1922.

GRANT, Charles Cameron, M.B.E.—Ed. Aberdeen Univ. (King's student); M.A., ditto, 1899; 2nd asst., Fraserburgh high schl., Aberdeenshire, 1899-1901; 1st asst., Aberlour high schl., Banffshire, 1901-2; priv. coun. certif., Scotch educn. dept., 1901; teacher, refugee camp, Springfontein, O.R.C., Nov., 1902; priv. govt. schl., Winburg, Jan., 1903; corprince, elk., educn. dept., Sept., 1904; admstve. clk., 1905; junr. clk., educn. dept., 1912; ag. sec., educn. dept., 1913; German S.W. Africa, 1914-15; M.B.E. for services in E. Africa, 1916-17; asst. regar., Univ. of S. Africa, 1918; regar., Univ. of S. Africa, 1922.
GRANT, D. K. S.—Asst. conservator of forests, E.A.P., Dec., 1912; conservator of forests, Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920.

GRANT, Ezekiel Augustus.—Ent. British Honduras civ. serv. after compet. exam., as copyist to survey dept., Nov., 1899; 3rd cls. clk. (asst. keeper), King's warehouse, Feb., 1906; 2nd cls. clk., P.W.D., Apr., 1907; on active serv. with B.W.I. Regt. during war, Apr., 1916 to July, 1919; ag. 1st cls. clk., P.W.D., Aug., 1919 to Mar., 1920; acct., P.W.D., Apr., 1920; ag. clk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., Apr., 1921; ag. asst. inspr., schls., during 1921 to 1923; asst. inspr., schls., provisionally, Apr., 1923; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1928; dist. commsn., Orange Walk, Nov., 1928; ag. atty.-gen., Apr. to May, 1931; ag. dist. commsn., Belize and offl. adminstr. and offl. recr. from Apr., 1931.

GRANT, RICHARD WILLIAM, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Jones' Sch., Mon. and Royal Mil. Acad., Woolwich; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; attd., col. secs., office, Dec., 1920; offr., cls V, dist. offr., N. Tobal, Nov., 1923; offr., cls IV, Nov., 1927; dist. offr., Bentong, Dec., 1929; asst. advr., K. Tinggi, Mar., 1930; offr., cls III, Nov., 1932; asst. advr., Segamat, Oct., 1933.

GRANT, THOS. ST. JOHN.—B.A., Dublin Univ., 1896; elk. to C. C. and R. M., Komgha, Cape Colony, Dec., 1897; ditto, East London, June, 1898; ditto, Port Elizabeth, Jan., 1899; passed Cape civ. serv. law exam., Feb., 1899; elk. atty.-gen.'s off., Cape Town, July, 1899; elk. to R. M., Cape, Oct., 1899; regiar. to judge pres. of high ct., Griqualand West, Sept., 1901; transf. to O.R.C. civ. serv. as asst. res. mag., Winburg, May, 1902; ditto, Bloemfontein, July, 1904; ag. res. mag., Bloemfontein, Nov., 1905, to Mar., 1906; res. mag., Hoopstad, 1907; mag., Bethulie dist., 1913; Parys, Feb., 1919; Newcastle, Sept., 1928; Queenstown, Oct., 1929; Kingwilliams Town, Aug., 1933.

GRANTHAM, ALEXANDER WILLIAM GEORGE HERDER, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1899; cadet, Hong-Kong, Nov., 1922; attd., C.S.O., 1925; 2nd pol. mag., Sept., 1929; sec. retrenchment comte., July, 1930; asst. to P.M.G. in addn., July, 1930; dist. offr., South, in addn., Dec., 1930; extra asst. col. sec., and sec. to harbr. advisory comte., 1931; lat pol. mag. in addn., 1932; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk. of couns., June, 1933.
GRAVER, GEORGE STANLEY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1898; ed. Devonport High Sch.; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

GRAY, CHAS. EDGAR.—B. 1904; cler. asst., offr. recr.'s. office, Br. Guiana, 1919; 6th cls. offr., col. sec's. office, 1921; 5th cls. offr., 1923; 4th cls. offr., 1926; sec. to prisons comte., 1928; 2nd cls. clk., 1933; ag. prin. clk. and clk., exec. and leg. couns. in 1932 and 1933.

GRAY, H. W., O.B.E.—Office supt., lieut.-gov.'s off., E.A.P., May, 1908; elk. of the couns., Oct., 1908; asst. dist. consmr., 1911; dist. consmr., Kenya, Jan., 1920; ch. regiar. of natives, Jan., 1920.

GRAY, JOHN.—B. 1874; ed. Glasgow Univ. and F.C. Training Coll., Glasgow; held several educational appts. in Scotland and England; served in S. African war; supt., Brandfort refugee camp, 1902-3; prin., Hoopstad schl., O.F.S., 1903; 1st cls. clk., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1904; senr. clk., educn. dept., 1912; ag. sec., educn. dept., Nov., 1918; prin. clk., educn. dept., 1919; sec., educn. dept., 1921.

GRAY, JOHN, B.A., M.D., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), L.M., Rot. Hosp. (Dub.), certif.
