L.S.T.M.—B. 1882; med. offr., gen. hosp., Singapore, Apr., 1909; grade II, F.M.S., Apr., 1910; sr. surgn., class III, Oct., 1913; ch. med. offr., Malacca, but contd. former appt., Jan., 1919; ch. med. offr., Singapore, but contd. former appt., Nov., 1920; ch. med. offr., Singapore, Nov., 1922; ag. priv. civil med. offr., S.S. and tempy. M.L.C., for various periods, 1923-31; ag. state surgn., Kedah, Sept., 1931.
GRAY, JOHN MACFARLANE, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (I), L.M. (Rot.), D.P.H. (Univ. Dub.)—B. 1898; health offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1933.

GRAY, R.—B. 1880; elk. treasy., 1902; elk. rev. dept., 1904; dep. recr., rev., Lichtenburg, 1905; elk. rev. dept., Jo'burg, 1908; Pretoria, 1912; prin. clk., 1921; survr., 1928; acctnt., 1929; recr., rev., E. London, 1931; do., Pretoria, 1932; do., Durban, 1933.

GRAY, SIR REGINALD, KT. BACH. (1920 K.C. (1908).—B. 1851; called to the bar, Inner L.t., 1875; compiled, in 1884, Bermuda laws from 1690-1883; revising offr., Bermuda laws, 1894; compiled, in 1903, Bermuda laws from 1690; counsel to statute law consolidation comte., 1900; atty.-gen., Bermuda, 28th May, 1901; chief just. in Oct. and Nov., 1911; ex-officio mem. of exec. coun.; M.L.A., 1895-1897, 1904-1906 and from 1907; ret.

GRAY, THEODORE GRANT, M.B., B.C. Sc., B. 1884; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Sch., Aberdeen Univ.; asst. med. offr., 1911; med. supt., Nelson (N.Z.) mental hosp., 1920; med. supt., Auckland mental hosp., 1924; dep. med. gen. mental hosps., N.Z., 1925; inappr. gen. 1925.

GRAY, MAJOR WILLIAM BAIN.—B. 1872; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh; Univ. of Edinburgh; M.A., 1st cls. hon.; Gladstone Prizewinner and Ph.D., and New Coll., Oxford, B.Litt.; part-time Carnegie Research Fellow; lect., Tech. Coll., Brighton; examnr., Univ. of Edinburgh, Univ. and Cambridge joint bd. and Oxford local exam.; 2nd lieut., 6th Batt., the Royal Scots, Feb., 1915; mob., Aug., 1914; adjt., Jan.-Dec., 1915; capt., Aug., 1916; gen. staff, Alderhot comnd., Feb.-Aug., 1918-Apr., 1921; major, Army Biucl. Com., Nov., 1920; major, R.A.R.O., Mar., 1923; comm. educn., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1924; (app't. admin. dir., Nov., 1927); mem., educn. coman., Nov., 1924; chmn., Georgetown public free lib., May, 1926; mem., ct. of policy and educn., June, 1927; chmn. diamond and gold industries coman., Sept., 1927; mem. first exec. of leg. couns. under reformed constitution, Jan., 1928; ag. col. sec. and gov.'s dep. on various occasions.

GREAVES, AUBREY VERNON, M.B. (T.M.C.P. & S. (Ont.), D.T.M. (Liverpool).—B. 1876; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Univ. of Toronto; Med. med. in trop. med. Univ. of Liverpool; on active serv. as capt., Canadian A.M.C., 1916-19; govt. bacteriologist and asst. med. of health and med. offr. in charge V.D. clinic and infant welfare clinic in addn., 1926; sent to Miami, Florida, to investigate epidemic of smallpox, 1926; ag. ch. med. offr., Apr.-Dec., 1927; bacteriologist, Bahamas, 1928; asst. bacteriologist, Hong Kong, 1929; bacteriologist, 1931; lecturer forensic med. and toxicology, Univ. of Hong Kong.

GREAVES, SIR WILLIAM HERBERT, K.B.A. (1904), K.C.—B. 1857; B.A., Oxon; honours, juris., 1879; called to the bar, Tem., 1880; ag. mbr.-gen., Barbados, Aug.-Sept., to July, 1885, and July, 1886, to Jan., 1887; confirmed; J.P., Sept., 1885; exchequer-gen., Q.C. for Barbados, 1890; mem. exec. coman., acted as atty.-gen., July to Dec., 1887, and July to Dec., 1891; mem. of house of assem., act. of educn., 1888-1902; atty.-gen., Mar., 1902; just., Apr., 1902; ret.

GRECH, WINDHAM, LL.D. (Malta).—B. 1891; Called to the bar (Grays Inn), 1916; 2nd cls. R.F.C., 1916; capt., R.A.F., 1918; esp. Malta and Rome, 1917; attd., Italian Sup. Com., 1918-19 (Chevalier, Crown of Italy); unemployed list (R.A.F.), 1919; ass't. adviser, Seychelles, 1921; ag. legal adviser, crown prov., 14th Mar., 1921 to 25th Sept., 1922; and in 1924; ag. ch. just., Dec., 1923 to July, 1924, Mar.-Oct., 1925 and Apr. to July, 1927.
GREEN, CLAUDE.—B. 1879; ed. Temp. civ. serv., Mar., 1886; ch. clk. to gen. man. of hwys., Mar., 1897.
KEN, FREDERICK WALTER VICTOR.—B. ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; 6th cls. ist., 1914; asst. comary., 1917; sec., excise July, 1923; 3rd gde. comary., July, 1925; offr., dist. admtn., Jan., 1932.

KEN, GEORGE.—B. 1894; ed. Westminster chl.; on mil. serv., Oct., 1914 to Mar., 2nd lieut., The Buffs; wounded in France; clk., C.O., Oct., 1919 to Feb., 1920; miny., Sept., 1920 to Dec., 1920; C.O., Dec., June, 1922; apptd. cler. offr., O.C.O., 21st June, 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st June, 1928.
KEN, GEORGE C.—B. 1882; apptd., after exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. and assigned to C.O., 26th Aug., 1901; cler. higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.

KEN, GEORGE C., M.B.E. (1926)—B. 1897; asst. sec., sec. office, Br. Guiana, 1912; clk., 1913; 3rd cls. clk., 1918; sec. to for considering disposal of colonial lands, 2nd cls. clk., 1920; sec. to civ. serv., 1923; sec., Br. Guiana section, Wembley, prin. clk., 1924; sec., rly. coman., 1925; offr., partly delegation to Br. Guiana, sec., Br. Guiana delegation to W. Indian London, 1926; sec., civ. serv. coman., 7; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec. and clk. exec. and coun., various occasions, 1925-30; sec., iana constitution comsn., 1927; pvt. sec., in 1938; 2nd astt. col. sec., 1930; astt. l., 1931.

KEN, HAROLD.—B. 1887; ed. at Queen Gram. Schl., Hants.; entd. Royal botanic s., Kew, 1907; astt. supt., botanical and y dept., Hong Kong, 1911; seconded for rv., 1917-20; astt., botanical and forestry Hong Kong, 1920; J.P., 1922.

KEN, MAURICE JOHN, V.D.—B. 1878; ed. int pub. schl. and S.A. Coll.; astt. clk. of Cape H. of A., 1896; clk. of papers, 1897; clk., Cape leg. coun., 1904, and Union 1910; gent. ush., black rod, 1916; clk., 1926; clk. of senate, 1930; sworn trans. Dutch; sec. and shorthand writer various omana; astt. sec. speaker's confos. on future senate; war serv., Anglo-Boer, in W.P.M.R., war, in Ger. S.W. Afr. as nav. trnsp. in Harwich light cruiser, squad. and war secretariat; comdr., cdmg. Cape Town flys. and harbours, 13th July, 1908; Natal deleg. to S. African National Convention, Oct., 1908 to May, 1909; represented Natal at Impl. Confos. on defence, 1909; mem., rly. and harbours bd., Union of S. Africa, 1910 to 1916.

GREENE, MAURICE CHESTER.—B. 1881; ed. at Ratho's schl. and St. Stephen's Green schl., Dublin, and Dublin Univ.; B.A. 1905; student, King's Inns, Dublin, 1910-11; dist. offr., N. Nigeria, July, 1912; served with W.A.F.F., 1914-15; station mag., 1918; pol. mag., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Mar., 1928.

GREENFIELD, LIEUT.-COL. HERBERT, O.B.E., R. of O.—B. 1880; ed. privately; served in S. African War, 1901-02; joined R.A.V.C.; lieut., May, 1903; served in India, 1903-08; capt., May, 1908; attd. staff coll., 1910-12; sp. serv. and research work, N. and S. China, 1912-14; served in Great war, 1914-18 Tsingtau operns.; major, July, 1915; D.A.D.V.S., 10th divn., Gallipoli, Greater Macedonia, Serbia, Bulgaria, 1915-16; temp. lieut.-col., 1916; D.A.D.V.S., France and Belgium, 1917-18; ment. in desps.; A.D.V.S. (lieut.-col. temp.) Rhine Army, 1919; served in Waziristan operns., 1920 as D.A.D.V.S. and D.D.V.S. (temp. lieut.-col.) Wazir Force, 1920-21; ment. in desps.; D.A.D.V.S., India and Burma, 1921-24; exec. duty, Aldershot, 1925-28; vety. offr., Barbados, Oct., 1928.

GREENHALGH, N., B.Sc., Hons. (Vict.).—B. 1888; agric. and science master, Antigua gram. schl., 1910; lecturer in agric. science to students of training coll., Antigua; astt. inspr. of schl., Barbados, 1912; lecturer in educ. and master of method, training inst., Codrington Coll.; ag. chief inspr. of schl., Trinidad, June to Oct., 1915.

GREENHILL, MARCUS ARTHUR.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Sep., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. clk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on military service, May, 1917 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st June, 1929.

GREENIDGE, CHARLES WILTON WOOD.—B. 1889; eduo. Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Downing Coll., Camb.; B.A. (Nat. Sci. Tripos)1910; LL.B., 1911; called to bar, Mid. Term., 1912; 1st cls. hon. criminal law; admitted to bar, Barbados, 1915; mag., dist. D. St. Kitts, 1919; mag. dist. D. Barbados, 1923; judge, ot. of apl., Barbados, 1926, mag., Port of Spain, Trinidad, 1927; ag. solr.-gen., Oct., 1928 to June, 1929; ag. puisne judge in 1929; ag. atty.-gen., Oct. to Nov., 1929; ag. regiar., Oct.-Nov., 1930; ag. solr.-gen., Jan., 1931; ch. just., Br. Honduras, 1932.

GREENLAND, STANLEY ARTHUR.—Served with Natal Field Force, Rebellion, 1906; clk., govt., sec. dept., Papua, Mar., 1912; J.P., Sept., 1912; ag. astt. res. mag., Iona, May, 1913; ag. astt. res. mag., Kairuku, Mar., 1915; inspr., dept. native affairs, Mar., 1917; ag. curator, intestate estates, regist.-gen., etc., Apr., 1917; ag. ch. inspr., native affairs, 1918; ag. Papua gov't. agt., Sydney, N.S.W., Dec., 1919; travelling inspr., native affairs, July, 1922; J.P. for N.S.W., Oct., 1923; ag. mem., land bd., Papua, Nov., 1924; ch. inspr., native affairs, Dec., 1924.

GREENWAY, PEYOT JAMES, F.L.S.—B. 1897; ed. East Anglian Inst. of Agr., Chelmsford, R. Horticultural Soc., Wisley, Ripley, and R. Botanic Gardens, Kew; mily. serv., France and Italy, 1916-19; herbarium astt., R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1922-25; do., and ag. demonstr. in systematic botany, Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford, 1926-28; systematic botanist, E. African agrl. research sta., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

GREGOROWSKI, REGINALD ADDINGTON.—2nd clk., govt. sec.'s office, O.F.S., Dec., 1895; priv. sec. to state pres., Mar., 1896; landrost clk., Bethlehem, Dec., 1897; priv. sec. to state pres., July, 1898; landrost clk., Wepener, Jan., 1899; prin. govt. schl., Reddersburg, O.R.C., Oct., 1900; ast. clk., educ. dept., Mar., 1901; ch. clk. and regis., educ. dept., July, 1901; prin. clk., col. sec.'s off., June, 1909; ch. clk. to admstn., O.F.S. prov., Aug., 1910; prov. sec., O.F.S., Oct., 1924.

GREGORY, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.B.E. (1919).—Natal Mounted pol. (Zululand), 1896-1901; attached to Army Service Corps, 1901-1902; chief storekeeper, central stores, Pretoria, Transvaal repatriation department, 1902-1904; Transvaal civ. ser., 1906; chief storekeeper, P.W.D., East Africa Prot., Sept., 1906.

GREGORY, HON. FRANCIS BROOKE, LL.B.—B. 1862; ed. at pub. schls. and Harvard Univ.; admtd., an atty., New Brunswick, 1884, and called to the bar, 1885; called to the bar, B. Columbia, 1890; regis. of probate et., York Co., New Brunswick, 1885-1890; French cons. agt. at Victoria, B. Columbia, 1904-1909; commd. 6th regt. Canadian garr. artil. at Victoria, B. Columbia, 1898-1903; lieut.-col., reserve of offrs. (Canadian Militia); mem. of Diamond Jubilee contingent to England, 1897; on spec. ser. to Alaska in connection with Behring sea fisheries dispute, 1891-2; judge of sup. ct., B. Columbia, Nov., 1909.

GREGORY, HON. HENRY.—B. 1860; M.L.A., for N. Coolgardie, W. Australia, 1897; ditto for Menzies since 1901; min. for mines, 1901-2, and 1902-4; min. for mines and rly., Aug., 1908; ditto, 16th Sept., 1910, to Oct., 1911; mem. H. of R., C. of A., 1913.

GREIG, CLINTON HENRY, O.B.E. (1920).—B. 1875; entd. cust. dept., B. Guiana, 1895; sub. inspr., B. Guiana pol., Dec., 1900; 2nd lieut.(unatt.), B. Guiana militia, Dec., 1900; on sp. serv. to Trinidad (after riots), Apl. to July, 1903; inspr., pol., Trinidad, and comdt. Port of Spain fire brigade, Sept., 1903; capt., Trinidad local forces, May, 1905; sp. certif. (captain's), inf. training, schl. of instr., Chelsea Barracks, London, and Hythe certif. (musketry), D., 1905; ast. supt., pol., Gambia, Aug., 1910; comntr. pol., sheriff and inspr. of prisons, July, 1911; ag. ch. mag., Gambia, July to Oct., 1912; capt. comdgs. Gambia volr. artil., Sept., 1912; ag. rec-gen. in 1912 and 1914; ag. offr-in-charge of secretariat, and provvn. M.L.C., 1912; provsnl. M.E.C., Dec., 1913; on active serv. in Cameroons, June, 1915, to Mar., 1916; dist. offr., with judicial powers, Duala prov., Cameroons, July, 1915; apptd. hon. capt. in the Army (for services with Cameroons Exped. Force), as from Mar., 1916; temp. capt., W.A.F.F., Gambia, May, 1917; mem., African taxation comte., 1921; del. to confce. of W. African contrs. of cust., London, 1921.

GREIG, GORDON FASTLEY, A.R.S.M.—B. 1879; ed. Wellington Coll., and Royal Schl. of Mines, London; inspr. of mines, F.M.S., 1902; ag. ast. warden, Palang, 1907; ast. warden, Perak, 1910; ag. warden, Selangor, 1914; ag. warden, Perak, 1916 and 1920; ag. senr. warden of mines, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; warden of mines, Perak, Mar., 1921; senr. warden of mines, F.M.S., Feb., 1922.
GRELL, JESSIE MITCHINSON POTTER, M.P.—B.S. Cantab., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., entd. med. serv., Trinidad, June, 1908; dist. med. offr., Aug., 1912.
GRENFELL-HICKS, J. B.—B. 1891; Rhodesian pol., 1911-16; lieut., Rhodesian Nat. Regt., 1916-19; Rhodesian adminstr., 1919-22; ast. supt., Kenya pol., Nov., 1920; staff off., 1923; supt., 1926.

GRENIER, NORMAN ST. JOHN CHARLES.—B. 1879; clk., ser., Perak, 1894; ag. ast. aud., F.M.S., Apr., 1902; ast., audit dept., F.M.S., Jan., 1904; financl. ast., P.W.D., Perak, Jan., 1906; secy., san. bd., Selangor, Mar., 1911; regis., vehicle, Selangor, Mar., 1913; ast. audr., Selangor, Negri Semilan and Penang, 1919; ag. rev. audr., Perak, 1920 and 1921; state treas., Perak, 1920; ag. ast. aud., F.M.S., 1921; ast., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1924.

GRETTON, RONALD HENRY.—B. 1897; King George High Schl., Vancouver, Canada; Univ. of Br. Columbia, B.A.; ast. audr., Nigeria, 1929; transferred to Gambia, 1931; sec. and A.D.C., 1931 and 1933; ast. co., 1932; clk. of couns., 1933.

GRICE, NORMAN, B.A. (Cantab.).—F. cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; astd., Chinese sect., Perak, Feb., 1923; astd., cust. dept., Mar., 1925; astd., Chinese sect., Jan., 1925; ast. prot. sect., Selangor, Jan., 1925; ast. supt., gov't. pol., Penang, Feb., 1927; ast. contr. sect., S'pore, Mar., 1927; ast. dist. judge, S'pore, addn., Aug., 1927; cl. III, Oct., 1929; 2nd cl. S'pore, Sept., 1933.

GRIEK, Sir SELWYN MACGREGOR, K.T. (1934), C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1878; ed. Marlboro' Coll. (classical scholar) and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (classical scholar); 2nd clas. tripus, 1900; 1st class French and Eng. special, 1901; B.A. 1900; called to the bar, Nov., 1910; ast. rea., Northern Nigeria, 1910; part. in Hausa, 1907; 3rd class rea., Int. coll., in charge of Zaria prov., April, 1910; and in Zaria to May to Nov., 1911; sec., native affrs., Nigeria, Jan., 1921; col. sec., Trinidad, Aug., 1925; gov', Aug., 1922 to Mar., 1930, and from Jan., 1932.

GRIERSON, CHARLES HENRY ALEXANDER.—B. 1889; ed. privately and St. Paul's Sch.; served local forces and 2nd Batt. Loyal N. Lancs. Regt. in E. African campaign, Aug. 1914 to Feb., 1920; Turkana expdn., 1915; ast. polit. sec., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1916; admin. 1st grade, Apr., 1921; dep. prov. commr., Jan., 1926; prov. commr., Feb., 1930.

GRIFFIN, ARTHUR WILFRID MICHAEL SWART, M.C. (1919).—B. 1887; ed. Harrow, Trinity Coll., Cambridge; B.A. 1910; barrister-at-law, 1919; probationer, N.E. Rhodesia, 1919; ast. native commr., July, 1922; prov. commr., Apr. 1913; N.R. Rifles, Jan., 1923; commd. on N. Rhodesia pol., 1915-19; ast. gen., Nov., 1923; prov. commr., Apr., 1929.

GRIFFIN, SIR CHARLES JAMES, K.T. (1923), K.C., Ireland (1920), K.C., Great Britain (1914).—B. 1875; B.A. (hona.), Royal Irish Acad.; 1st scholar in modern literature exhibition; Chancellor's gold medallist; ed. the Irish Bar, June, 1898; went Consul circuit; apptd. Crown prosecutor, B.C.A. Protorate (now Nyasaland), July, 1901; ag. judicial offr. and H.B.M. vice-consul, Nov., 1901 to June, 1902; ag. judge of High Court, Feb., 1902, Oct., 1904 and Nov., 1905 to Feb., 1906; gen., Nov., 1905; judge of High Court, Feb., 1906. member of H.B.M. FFITH, Carl.—Asst. auditor, gold Coast,

GRIMBLE, Athub Francis, M.A., F.R.A.I.— B. 1888; ed. Magdalene Coll., Cambridge, France, Germany; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Is., Mar., 1914; passed cadet, Mar., 1915; dep. commr. for W. GRIMMER, Ward Chipman Hazen, K.C., LL.D.—B. 1858; admitted atty., 1880; barrister, 1881; K.C., 1902; elected to prov. legin., N. Brunswick, 1903; re-elected, 1908, 1912; surv.-gen., N. Brunsw., 1908; atty.-gen., 1911; puisane judge, appeal ct. of N. Brunsw., Jan., 1914.

GRINDLEY-FERRIS, Ivon K.C., B.A. (Cape). B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.).—B. 1876; ed. GRIST, Donald Honey, Camb. Univ. Dip. of Agric.—B.1891; ed. Huntingdon Grammar Schl., Camb. County Schl. and Camb. Univ. GRIST, Major H. G., R. of O., R. Fusiliers.— B. 1881; ed. Aldenhams Schl., Herts., 2nd lieut., 7th bn., R. Fusiliers, 1899; lieut., 1900; capt., 1903; R. of O., 1907; S. GROBLER, Hon. P. G. W., M.P.—Rep. Rustenburg elec. divn. in Union of S. Africa parlmt. since 1915; min. of lands, 30th June, 1924; del., Ottawa Econ. Conf., 1932; min. for native affrs., Mar., 1933.

GROOM, Hon. Sir Littleton Ernest, K.C.M.G. (1924), K.C., M.A., LL.M.—B. 1867; M.P. for Darling Downs, Queensland; elected to last hse. of rep., O. of Aust., 1901; re-elected, Dec., 1903; mem. of royal comm. on Iron Bonus, 1902, and Navigation Bills, 1904-5; several times Crown prosecutor, and twice ag. judge of dist. et al., Queensland; min. for home affairs, C. of Aust., July, 1905; atty.-gen., Oct., 1906; min. of external affairs, June, 1909, to Apr., 1910; min. of trade and customs, June, 1913-Sept., 1914; hon. min., Feb., 1917; vice-pres., exec. counc., Nov., 1917 to Mar., 1918; min. for works and railways, Mar., 1918; ag. atty.-gen. from Apr., 1918; atty.-gen., Dec., 1921; atty.-gen., Bruce ministry, 1923; speaker, house of rep., Jan., 1926-29; re-el., M.H.R., Dec., 1931.

GUILLEMARD, Sir Laurence Nunnis, G.C.M.G. (1927), K.C.B. (1910), K.C.M.G. (1923), C.B. (1906).—B. 1862; ed. Charterhouse and Trin. Coll. Camb.; entered Treasury, 1888; priv. sec. to Sir W. Harcourt and Sir M. Hicks-Beach, 1892-1902; deputy chmn., board of inland rev., 1902; chmn., bd. of customs and excise, 1903; gov., S. Stirlings, 7th Feb., 1920; ret., 1927.

GULLIBETT, Hon. Sir Henry Somke, K.C.M.G. (1933).—B. 1878; served Europe: War, 1915-18; dir., Australian War Museum and offl. historian, A.I.F. in Palestine; dir., Australian immigration bureau, Oct., 1920; M.H.R., O'Connell of Australia from 1925; min. for trade and custom, 1928-29; dep. leader of opposition, Nov., 1929; min. for trade and custom, Lyons' ministry, 1932.
GULLIVER, E. G.—Cust. asst., Kenya, July, 1916; collr., cust., Jan., 1926.

GULLOCH, William Sutherland.—B. 1883; 2nd lieut., Scottish Rifles, 1914; lieut., Scottish Rifles, 1916; lieut., Gold Coast Regt., 1918; capt., Gold Coast Regt., 1919; served France and Togoland; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, 1919; ag. commr., pol., Eastern prov., 1924-5; commr., pol., Cent. prov., 1926; ch. pol., Gibraltar, Nov., 1927.

GUMMER, Wallace Albert.—B. 1880; royal observatory, Greenwich, 1895; ditto, Cape of Good Hope, Jan., 1900; trig. survey, Transvaal and O.R.C., Feb., 1903; surv., rev. survey dept., Selangor, Nov., 1907; ditto, trig. br., Nov., 1914; supt., rev. surveys, Singapore, Aug., 1924; surv., Malacca, Mar., 1927; ditto, Kedah, Jly., 1929; supt., rev. surveys, Perak, 1932; senr. surv., 1933.

GUNARATNA, Walter Dandris, B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; addl., Galle kach., Jan., 1928; addl., Kandy kach., May, 1928; ag. pol. mag., Dumbaree, Dec., 1928 to Jan., 1929; extra office asst., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1929; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1930; pol. mag., Puttalam, Oct., 1931; office asst., Badulla kach., Aug., 1932.

GUNASEKARA, Septimus Theodosius, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.P.H. (Lond.), L.M.S. GUNAWARDANA, Don Charles Rajakaruna, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1924; addl., Matara kach., Jan., 1925; ditto, Jaffna kach., Apr., 1926; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, June, 1927; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Sept., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Ratnapura, Jan., 1930; do., Colombo, Jan., 1931; do., Batticaloa, Mar., 1931; office asst., Kandy kach., Dec., 1931.

GUNDY, John Arnold, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1900; ed. St. Bees Schol. and Faraday House; engr.-li.-ch.'s office, G.P.O., Lond., 1920: asst. engr., posts and tele., Nigeria, Mar., 1929; engr., Mar., 1929; divnl. engr., Apr., 1930.
GUNN, Hon. John.—B. 1885; M.L.A., Adelaide, 1916; leader, party. Labor for 1918; premier and treas., S. Australia, 1926; mem., migration and development comm., Australia, 1926.

GUPPY, Robert.—Imperial postal ser., Mar., 1892; ch. clk. and acctt., post dept., G. Coast, 1900; ag. dir. of telegraph June to 26th July, 1901; acctt., 1902; present system of accounting passed in Comm. brought into force 1st Sept., 1902.
GURNEY, H. L. G.—Asst. dist. nat. Kenya, June 1921; ag. sec., secretariat, 1922.
GURUSINHA, Kankanana Thanaka, N.T. Tripatha—B. 1877; apptd. cl. V. Ordn. serv., Mar., 1923; office asst., Kalawala mar., 1923; addnl. asst. commr., stamp, May, 1930.

GUTHRIE, Hon. Hugh, P.C. (Can.), C.B.E., V.D.—B. 1866; ed. Coll. Inat. Guelph and Ont. West, 1919; judge, eastern dista. local distr., Grahamstown, 1930.

HAARER, Alex Ernest, F.L.S.—Ed. Birkenhead Inst., Battersea Gram. Sch.; Royal Horticultural Coll. (dipl. hort. agrl. offr.), Tanganyika Territory, 1931; agt., 1930.
HADDRIEL, Cyril F.—B. 1902; agt., compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 29th April.
HALES, Everett Olive.—B. 1886; pub. trust office, 1894; offr. in charge of branch, 1914; inspr., 1919; local dep. trustee, 1920; asst. pub. trustee, 1922; N.Z. state advances office, 1931.

HALLIER, CAPT. FRANK COLLINGS, M.C., R. of C.—B. 1888; U.D.F. and Cape Corps, 1913-18; S. African Rebellion and German S.W. Africa, 1914-16; G.E.A., 1916-18; seconded as asst. pol. offr., civ. admin., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1918; admin. offr., 1st gde., Feb., 1922; dep. prov. commnr., 1933.

HALL-JONES, HON. SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. HALLOCK, ANANDA RAJAH.—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1927; attd. Puttalam kach., Dec., 1927; attd., Kalutara kach., Nov., 1928; office asst., ditto, Oct., 1929; ditto, Jaffna kach., Feb., 1931; pol. mag., Puttalam, July, 1932.

HAM, LAURENCE PETER VAN ZYL.—Ck., rly. and cent. tel. office, 1898; clk., res. mag., Hoopstad, June, 1902; A.R.M., Hoopstad, Aug., 1902; Jacobadal, Nov., 1903; Thaba 'Nchu, Feb., 1907; Winburg, Feb., 1910; D.A.R.M., Fouriesburg, Aug., 1911; Trompsburg, May, 1915; mag., Philippolis, Jan., 1918; Winburg, 1920; Wakkerstroom, May, 1920; ag. mag., Harrismith, Apr., 1925; inspr., pub. serv. comm., Feb., 1926; sec., ditto, Jan., 1928; sec., dept. of mines and industries, Sept., 1928.

HAMBLIN, R. A.—B. 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser. and temporarily employed in the science and art dept. from Mar. 21st to May 26th, 1899; assigned to C.O. July, 1899; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918; deptl. astt., 1st Apr., 1925.

HAMILTON, HON. ADAM.—B. 1880; ed. tech. schl., Dunedin, N.Z.; Knox Coll. and Otago Univ.; mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1919-22 and since 1925; P.M.G., min. of tels., min. of lab. since 1931; min., internal affrs., 1931-33; min., employment, 1933.

HAMILTON, ANDREW TODD.—B. 1879; astt. mast., educ. dept., Hong Kong, 1910; on mily. serv., Dec., 1917 to Feb., 1919; astt. mast., ols., II, Jan., 1920; astt. mast., 1921; cls., I, Mar., 1928; headmr., Ellis Kadodori schl. for Indians, Feb., 1929; senr. mast., 1933.
HAMILTON, ARCHIBALD JOHNSTON.—B. 1892; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; ent. service of govt. of Trinidad, Apr., 1911; mag., county of St. Patrick, Jan., 1927; do., St. George East, Jan., 1933.

HAMILTON, ERIC WILLIAM, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Blundell's; exhtr., Slid. Suss. P.M.G., 1931; ag. S.I.E., 1932.

HAMILTON, PERCIVAL JOHN ALEXANDER.—Col. audit dept., Aug., 1915; astt. audr., Nyassaland, Sept., 1915; audr., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1922; audr., Leeward Is., Dec., 1923; audr., Fiji, Br. Solomons Is. Prot., Gilbert & Ellice Is. Col., May, 1928; seconded to audit accounts of condominium govt., New Hebrides, Apr.-July, 1929; astt. audr., Hong Kong, 1932.

HAMILTON, Sir ROBERT WILLIAM, K.T., B.A.C.H. (1918).—Ed. at St. Paul's schl. and Trin. Hall, Camb.; class. scholar, B.A., 1889 (honours); M.A., 1892; student, Inner Temple, 1893; called to the bar, 1895; dist. commr., Lagos, 1895 to 1897; regir., E. Africa Prot., 1897; town mag., 1899; astt. judge and admistr.-gen., 1900; judge of high court of E. Africa, Aug., 1902; apptd. to serve on land comm., Oct., 1904; prin. judge, E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1906; chief justice, 1911; ret., 1920; partly under sec. of state for the cols., 3rd Sept., 1931; reassptd., 9th Nov., 1931; resigned, 1932.

HAMILYN, WILLIAM TEMPLE.—B. 1901; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1926; cadet, secretariat, Gambia, Jan., 1926; admstv. offr., 1928; ag. elk., exec. and leg. couns. and ag. inspr., schl. on various occasions; author of "A Short History of the Gambia."
HANCOCK, CYRIL GEORGE.—B. 1902; ed. Kingsland Grange, Shrewsbury, Bromsgrove Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; astt. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1925; dist. commr., Jan., 1931.

India, 1924; hon. life mem., Inst. of Engrs. dian.

English, Glasgow Univ., 1913; 1st place language, English hist., Glasgow Univ., 1914; astt. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, July, 1915; mast. of method, Hong Kong Univ. Feb., 1920; astt. prim., 1st Apr., 1920; sec. to Duke of Devonshire, 1st Mar., 1921; priv. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas, 1924 and to Mr. Amery, 26th Nov., 1925; priv. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas, 1924 and to Mr. Amery, 26th Nov., 1925; priv. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas, 1924 and to Mr. Amery, 26th Nov., 1925; rep. in Commonwealth of Australia of H.M.'s. govt. in U.K., 1931.

HANNAN, GERALD HUGH CRAWFORD.—Ed. Mercers' Schl., London; served in S.War, 1900-02 (2 meda, 5 clasps); 2nd astt. exec. and leg. couns., Transvaal, Oct., 1902; clk., olk., 1903; gentleman usher of his leg. couns., Transvaal, 1907; clk., astt. couns., Transvaal, 1910; elk. of prov. exec. comites, 1917.

SON, and St. John's, Camb.; foundtn. schlr., 1st class nat. sci. trip. part I., 1900; B.A., M.A., 1920; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. and cl. clk., Oct., 1901; sec. to comte., fire into organisation of Crown agents' office; astt. priv. sec. to Lord Crewe, 1st Feb., sec. to Royal comm. on Mauritius, 1909; priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, Nov., 1910; astt. clk., Sept., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from Apr., 1912; 1st cls. clk., 12th Oct., 1914; colonial blue-books comte., 1917; ag. prin. 1st Jan., 1920; astt. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; W., W. African currency bd., 1926-1928; dir., Palestine currency bd., 1926-1928; dir., audit, 1st Oct., 1928.

ARDING, SIR EDWARD JOHN, K.C.M.G. (3), C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1917), M.A. (1907). 1880; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1899); 1st cls. class, 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1903; B.A.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1912; after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., marine bd. of trade, 30th Nov., 1903; 2nd cls. C.O., 31st May, 1904; sec. to distressed naval seamen comte., 1909; astt. priv. sec. to Lewis Harcourt, 14th Oct., 1912; sec. to dominions Royal Comm., Dec., 1912; ag. 1st cls. 10th Apr., 1916; 1st cls. clk., 4th June, 1916; military service from 20th Nov., 1915, to 9th 1916; sec. to comte. for stimul. of ex-service within the Empire, 1917; ag. prin. clk., Apr., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; astt. sec., Dec., 1921; astt. under-sec. of state for dominions affairs, 30th July, 1925; perm't. under-sec. of state for dom. affrs., 24th Jan., 1930.

ARDING, SIDNEY WALTER.—B. 1895; R.S., (R.C.S.Ire.); house surg., Inc. Dent. Coll., Dublin, 1925-29; demonstr., 1929-31; house surg., Middlesex Hosp., Lond., Mar. to Aug., 1931; dental surg., Falkland Is., Nov., 1931; served in Great War, 1914-19; R. Dublin Artillery, Leinster Regt. and R.A.F.; lieut., Island Is. Defence Force, 1932.
ARDY, THOMAS JAMES.—B. 1900; ent. O.O., Mar., 1916; apptd. astt. clk. (afterwards clk.), 24th Apr., 1918; cler. offr., higher grade, Nov., 1928.

ARINGTON, GEN. SIR CHARLES, G.B.E. (2), K.C.B. (1919), C.B. (1917), D.S.O. (1900), 1872; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and R.M. Coll., Thrust; ent. 2nd Batt., King's Liverpool Regt., 1892; served on staff, S. Africa, 1899-1900 (desps. twice, D.S.O.); European War, 1914-18 (desps. bt. lieut.-col. and col. comdr. of Honour, maj.-gen.); instr., R.M. Coll., Thrust, gen. staff, W.O.; bde. maj., 6th Army Brig.; G.S.O.I., B.G.G.S., Canadian Corps; M.G.S.; 2nd Army; dep. ch., Imp. staff, 1918-20; G.O.C. Army of Black Sea, 1921; G.O.C. allied forces of occupation Turkey, 1921-23; Northern command, 1923-27; 1927; G.O.C. in C., Western command, 1927-31; G.O.C. in C. Aldershot command; A.D.C. gen. to King since 1930; gov. and gen. in-ch., Gibraltar, 1933.
ARKNESS, J., L.R.C.P. & L.R.C.S. & F.P.S. (Edn.& Glas.), L.D.S. (Ed.), D.T.M., H. (Liv.).—B. 1896; mily. serv., 1914-19; offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1927.

ARMER, FRANCIS EDWARD.—B. 1884; pol. offr., F.M.S., Oct., 1904; ag. astt. comte., Oct., 1907; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, Jly., 1908; astt. supt., pol., S.S., May, 1909; astt. pol., Singapore, Oct., 1913; astt. comte., N. Kedah, Oct., 1916; sr. astt. supt., Singap., Sept., 1923; ch. pol. offr., P.W., Feb., 1926; personal astt. to ch. pol. offr., Singapore, Jly., 1927; astt. supt., pol., P.W., Oct., 1930.

HARPER, JOHN DANIEL.—B. 1876; ed. Linlithgow and Trinity Old Stratford Schls., Edinburgh Univ., M.B., Ch.B., Edm., F.R.C.S., Edin.; war serv., 1914-17; med. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1917; surg. specialist, Apr., 1928.
HARKNESS, EDWARD BURNS, C.M.G. (1922). Entered treasury, New S. Wales, 1891; sec. to Premier's office, 1901-16; under sec., depts. of ch. sec. and public health, since 1916.
HARLEY, JOHN A.—M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), certif. Lond. S.T.M. (with distinction), July, 1909; apptd. to W.A.M.S., Gambia, Apr., 1910; ag. prot. med. offr., Jan. to May, 1911.

HARMAN, WM. HENRY.—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to science and art dept., 10th Apr., 1900; to bd. of agric., 6th June, 1901; transf'd. to C.O., 27th Jan., 1902; staff offr., 8th Apr., 1930.

HARNETTY, LIEUT. EDWARD.—B. 1887; ed. Brompton Oratory schl.; capt., Lincolnshire Yeomanry; lieut., Sierra Leone Batt., W.A.F.F., 1918-21; passed lower stand. exam. in Mendi and Mandingo; astt. dist. comnr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1922; passed higher standard exam. in Mende; dist. comnr., May, 1923.

HARNEY, EGBERT EWART.—B. 1898; clk. to regir.-gen., Antigua, 1916; 3rd audlt. clk., 1921; 2nd do., 1922; ch. clk., regir.'s office, 1927; comnr. for oaths, 1927; ag. regir. and prov.-marshal, Dominica, 1931.

HARPER, SIR CHARLES HENRY, K.B.E. (1930), C.M.G. (1921), O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. at Blundell's schl., Devon, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1899; called to bar, Inner Temple, May, 1909; cadet, G. Coast, Jan., 1900; attd. Ashanti field force, June to Sept., 1900; astt. dist. comnr., Sept., 1901; ag. astt. col. sec., Oct., 1904; astt. col. sec., Feb., 1906; ag. ch. astt. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Apr. to June, 1908; cantonment mag., Coomassie, Oct., 1908; prov. comnr., July, 1909; census offr., 1911; ag. ch. astt. and clk. of couns., May to June, 1912; ag. col. sec., June to Oct., 1912 and from May to Aug., 1914; attd. Togoland field force, Aug., 1914; sen. polit. offr., Togoland, Sept. to Dec., 1914; ch. astt. col. sec., Feb., 1915; ag. col. sec. in 1915, 1916 and 1917; ch. comnr., Ashanti, May, 1920; gov., St. Helena, Dec., 1924; assumed govt., Feb., 1925; ret., 1932.

HARPER, FRANCIS HENRY, M.B.E. (1920).—B. 1877; transferred from the service of the Royal Niger Co. as supplementary clk. to C.O., 1st Jan., 1900; oof. clk., Nov., 1912; deptl. astt., 1st Apr., 1922; sec., "Discovery" comte., 1st June, 1928.

HARRAGIN, WALTER.—B. 1890; barrister-at-law; clk. of the peace and sub-regir., San Fernando and Victoria, Trinidad, Mar., 1914; served with the army as temporary major; mag., Victoria, Sept., 1919; crown counsel, 1924; ag. solr.-gen., Apr. to Oct., 1924 and from Jan., 1925 to Aug., 1926; atty.-gen., Nyasaland, Apr., 1927; ag. judge in 1927, 1929, 1930 and 1932; atty.-gen., Kenya, Oct., 1933.

HARRIS, SIR CHARLES ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1917); C.B. (1904); C.M.G. (1900); C.V.O. (1917); M.V.O. (1911), B.A.—B. 1855; scholar, prizeman, and Porteus medallist of Christ's Coll., Camb.; Tancred student in com. law, Linc.'s Inn, 1877; 12th classic, 1878; apptd. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., after open competn., 1st July, 1879; sec. to W. Indies Finance Comms., Dec., 1882; asst. sec. Sugar Bounties Conf., 1889; priv. sec. to Mr. Buxton, M.P., Jan., 1894, to June, 1895; and to the Earl of Selborne, June, 1895; 1st class clk., 1896; specially engaged in work connected with Venezuelan bdry. arbitn., 1896-8; prim. clk., Nov., 1898; sent to Paris, 1899, in connection with the Venez. bdry. arbitn.; conducted British case in Brazilian-B. Guiana boundy. arbitn., 1901-4; mem. of comtce. to inquire into organisation of Crown agents' office, 1908; ch. clk., 1st Feb., 1909; represented C.O. on advisory comtce. of comm. intell., B. of T., 1900-17; mem. of managing comtce. of Imp. Inst., 1909-16; gov. and c. in c., Newfoundland, 1st Nov., 1917; assumed govt., 17th Dec., 1917; ret., 1922; author of numerous articles in Dict. of Pol. Econ. on economic and statistical subjects, and of lives of various colonial govts. and statesmen in Dict. of Nat. Biog.

HARRIS, DAVID JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1897; apptd. after compet. exam., boy clk., G.P.O., 1912; on mily. serv. (R.E. and R.A.F.), 8th Oct., 1915 to 27th Feb., 1919; tempy. employment clk., Miny. of Labour, 1st Oct., 1919 to 20th June, 1920; permanent ditto, 21st June, 1920 to 30th Sept., 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 1st Oct., 1922.

HARRIS, HENRY GLADSTONE BOLTON.—B. 1885; ed. Exeter Schl. and Cambourne Schls., mines; inspr. mines, F.M.S., 1910; ag. asst. warden, mines, Perak, 1914; ag. warden mines, Selangor, 1920; ag. warden mines, Perak, 1923; warden mines, Negri Sembilan, 1927; warden, mines, Selangor, 1929; do., Perak, 1933.

HARRIS, HERBERT JOHN.—B. 1884; entd. serv. of Imp. Post Office, Jan., 1900; transfd. to dept. of posts and tels., Cape of Good Hope, Nov., 1902; resig., Dec., 1907, and entd. S. Rhodesia tel. serv., Jan., 1908; seconded for serv. with African Trans-Continental Tel. Co., Apr., 1908; resig., Jan., 1910; prov. postmtr., S. Nigeria, Feb., 1910; asst. supt. posts and tels., F.M.S., June, 1913; seconded for mil. serv., Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., Indian army res. of offrs., unattached, Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., 91st Punjabis, July, 1918; lieut., Mar., 1919; released from army serv., Oct., 1919; resumed duty, Kuala Lumpur, July, 1920; relinquished coman., I.A.R.O., Sept., 1920; contr., posts and tels., F.M.S., Oct., 1926; contr. P. and T., S.S., Jan., 1929; do., S'pore., June, 1931; asst. sec. for postal affirs., S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1932; dir., posts, Malayan postal union, Jan., 1934.

HARRIS, REGINALD ROBERT.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Mary's Schl., Basingstoke; clk., govt. sec.'s office, Nyasaland Prot., June, 1913; editor, govt. gaz., since 1914; clk., 1st grade, Oct., 1920; ag. ch. clk., secretariat, Nov. 1920 to June, 1921; asst. treas., Apr., 1925; ag. senr. asst. treas., in 1930 and 1931.

HARRIS, WILLIAM VICTOR, M.Sc., A.I.C.T.A., F.E.S.—B. 1904; B.Sc. (Agr.), Armstrong Coll., Durham Univ., 1925; Cambridge, 1925-26; assoc. Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1927; asst. entomologist, dept. of agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1927.

HARRISON, ERNEST, M.S.A. (Ivwa), B.Sc. (Agric.), N.D.A. (Hons.).—Lecturer in agr., schl. of agr., Groothfontein, Cape, 1904; prim. schl. of agr., Cedara, Natal, 1913; dep. dir., agr., Kenya, 1921; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Nov., 1922 and Mar.-Oct., 1924; dir., agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1930.

HARRISON, CAPT. HORACE DERNFORD.—B. 1884; war serv., with D.L.I., 1914-19; France, 1915-18; twice ment. in desps.; headmast., govt. primary schls., Gold Coast, June, 1920; mast., training coll., Jan., 1921; inspr., schls., Apr., 1926; ag. asst. dir., educn. in 1932 and 1933.

HARRISON, NEWCOME HERBERT, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.D.S., R.C.S. (Eng.), L.S.T.M.—B. 1885; med. offr., Johore, Nov., 1920; med. offr., gen. hosp., Johore Bahru, Sept., 1923; med. offr., i/c, Trengganu, Sept., 1925; ch. med. offr., Trengganu, Jly., 1928.

HARRISON-JONES, CHARLES.—B. 1884; ed. Reading and St. John's Coll., Oxford B.A.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1907; pol. mag., Puttalam, Jan., 1909; asst. govt. agt., Colombo and Negombo dists., July, 1910; pol. mag., Matara, May, 1912; on military duty, Jan., 1915; dist. judge Tangalla, Dec., 1919; govt. agt., E. Prov., Jan., 1929; govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1932; grade I, cls. I, 1933.
HARROWER, JOHN GORDON, M.B., Ch.B. (Hons.), D.Sc. (Edin.), F.R.S.E.—B. 1890; prof. of anatomy, King Edward VII Med. Coll., Singapore, Mar., 1922.

HART, BRIG.-GEN. HERBERT, C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1915), Croix-de-Guerre-French (1919), V.D. (1925).—Served in S. African War, 1902; lieut., N. Z. volunteers, 1907; capt., in territorial force, 1911; major, 1912; major in N.Z. expd. force (main body), 1914; lieut.-col., 1915; col., 1916; temp. brig.-gen., 1917; in commd. territorial brig., 1920; R. of O., 1925; ret. list, 1930; admstr., Western Samoa, 1931; was A.D.C. to govt.-gen., N. Z., 1920-24; five times men in desps.

HART, H. W.—B. 1897; on mily. service in France and Flanders, Mar., 1915 to Jan., 1919; service in corps of mily. accnts., 1919-21; temp. clk., C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 9th July, 1926.

HART, LAWRENCE HERKWARD.—B. 1891; ed. Lancing Coll., pol. probtnr., F.M.S., Nov., 1912; asst. comanr., pol., June, 1918; ditto, Selangor, Oct., 1920; pol. offr., J. Bahru, Dec., 1922; 1st asst. comanr., pol., Perak, Dec., 1924; ch. pol. offr., Malacca, Apr., 1928; 1st asst. comanr., pol., Perak, Mar., 1929; asst. comanr., pol., F.M.S. Ryks., June, 1930.

HART, RUPERT LANCELOT LEON, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1885; ed. Reading Schl. and R. Veterinary Coll., London; govt. vety. surg., N. Rhodesia, 1910-14; vety. offr., Uganda, 1914; served with E. African Forces in Great War, 1914-17 and on Western Front, 1917-19; senr. vety. offr., Uganda, 1920; ag. D.D.V.S. for several periods ag. D.Y.S., June, 1932.

HART, WILLIAM HUME, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), grad., L.S.T.M.—B. 1876; ed. Sedbergh, Yorks and Dub. Univ. (Trin. Coll.) med. offr., grade III, F.M.S., May, 1912; grad II, Nov., 1913; seconded for mil. serv., 1914-15; temp. 2nd lieut., R.A.M.C., Feb., 1916; capt., Feb., 1917; discharged, Sept., 1919; ag. medl. aspdt. cent. mental hosp., Tanjong Lambutan, June, 1920; senr. med. offr., Pahang, but continues ag. senr. med. offr., Selangor, Aug., 1926; senr. med. offr., Perak, Nov., 1927; ag. prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Feb., 1930; ag. stat. med. offr., Perak, Jan., 1932.

HART-DAVIS, CHARLES HENRY, C.M.G. (1931).—B. 1874; ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford, B.A.; served in 13th L.Y. in South Africa, 1900 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); priv. sec. to Sir W. Hayne Smith, Cyprus, 1901; asst. insp. of schools, Cyprus, 1901 to 1906; passed exams. in Turkish and Greek, 1903; insp. of schools, Gold Coast, 1906; dis. comanr., 1906 to 1910; sec. to high comanr. for the W. Pacific, 1910; on mily. serv., 1915-19; ch. astt. sec., Cyprus, Apr., 1919; ag. ch. inspr. of schls., Aug. to Oct., 1919; ag. ch. sec., Sept. to Dec., 1919; ag. regisr.-gen., Dec. to May, 1920; ag. comsnr., July to Oct., 1920; ag. ch. sec., on various occasions, 1920-21 and 1923; comsnr., July, 1922; mem., leg. coun., 1924-31; ag. col. sec. for various periods, 1929-32; supt., census, 1921 and 1931.

HARTER, Jean Denis Edgard.—B. 1879; ed. R. Coll. of Mauritius; astt. mast., R. Coll., 1901; astt. mast., Victoria Schl., Seychelles, Aug., 1902; astt. mast., King's Coll., 1910; headmast., Victoria Schl., July, 1920; ag. dir., educn. and inspr. of schls., Seychelles, Jan., 1922; inspr. of schls., Seychelles, Jan., 1924; sec. to bd. of educn.; local sec., Cambridge local exam.; ag. pvtc. sec., clk. to gov. and clk. to couns., from Dec., 1924.

HARTLEY, Brian Joseph, N.D.A., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1907; ed. Loughborough Grammar Schl. and Midland Agrl. Coll.; C.O. agrl. course, Oxford and Trinidad, 1927-29; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1929.
HARNELL, G. F. W.—Chief mechanical engnr., Uganda rlyw., June, 1918, and from Apr., June, 1922; comsnr., July, 1922; ag. regisr.-gen., July-Sept., 1922.

HARTWELL, Charles Herbert, B.A. (Cambr.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1926; attd., Kandy kach., Jan., 1927; attd., secretariat, Oct., 1927; office astt., N. Eliya kach., May, 1928; astt. ch. sec., Aug., 1931; office astt., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1932.
HARVEY, Cedric, B.Sc. (Agr.), A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1902; ed. Woodford Coll., Essex, Transvaal Univ. Coll. Pretoria; Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., 1926; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1927.
HARVEY, Hon. Sir John Murggrave, Kt. Bach. (1933).—B. 1865; judge of the sup. ct., New S. Wales, 1913.
HARVEY, Reginald James.—B. 1901; ed. Charterhouse and Exeter Coll., Oxford, 1920-23; 4th cls., hon., mods. hist.; St. George Schl., Jerusalem, 1923-25; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

HATCHER, C. M.—B. 1873; apptd., after compet exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. serv., and assigned to W.O., 11th Sept., 1893; transf. to C.O., 18th Mar., 1896; minor staff offr., Feb., 1902; staff clk., first grade, July, 1913; deptl. astt., 26th Feb., 1923.

HATHORN, Alexanier Anthony Roy, B.A.—B.1882; ed. Hilton Coll., Lancing Coll. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1904; admitted advoc., Natal, 1904; K.C., 1922; judge, Natal prov. divn., sup. ct., S. Africa, 1931.

HAVENGA, Hon. Nicolaas Christiaan.—Atty-at-law; mem., prov. coun. for Fauresmith, O.F.S. and mem., exec. comte. for O.F.S., 1910-15; mem., Union H. of A. for Fauresmith from 1915; min. of finance, from June, 1924; del., Ottawa Econ. Confce., 1932; del., World Economic Confce., London, 1933.
HAWS, Richard Brunel, M.B., M.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. (Lond.).—B. 1893; med. offr., Kedah, June, 1923; prof. of medicine, King Edward VII Med. Coll., Singapore, Apr., 1927.

HAWKEN, H. E. A.—B. 1879; ed. pub. schl., Ottawa Coll. Inst.; ent. civ. serv. of Canada as jun. clk., dept. marine and fisheries, 1901; 2nd cls. clk., 1908; 1st cls. clk., 1910; ch. clk., 1917; astt. dep. min., Apr., 1920.
HAWKER, Charles Allan Seymour.—Served European War 1914-17 (wounded); M.H.R., C'wealth of Australia, 1929; min. for markets and repatriation, Lyons' ministry, 1931-2; resigned, Oct., 1932.

HAWKINS, Brian Charles Keith.—B. 1900; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.), hons., mod. hist., 1923; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1924; 2nd astt. collr., treas., June, 1927; astt. supt., impts. and expts., Nov., 1927; astt. head, sany. dept., Oct., 1930; dist. offr., South, May, 1932; 3rd astt. col. sec., May, 1933.

HAWKINS, Gerald.—B. 1891; B.A., London Univ.; on mily. serv., 1914-20 (capt.); cadet, S.S. Nov., 1920; attd. to land office, Penang, Dec., 1920; dep. collr. of land rev., Penang, Jan., 1921; offr., cls. V, May, 1919; cls. IV, May, 1923; sec., Pahang, Apr., 1926; dist. offr., Mar., 1928; cls. III, May, 1928; assessor, wild life comsn., Aug., 1930; astt. advr., Segamat, May, 1931; offr., cls. II, May, 1933.
HAY, Clifford H., C.M.G., C.B.E. (1928), M.V.O.—Under sec., and perm. head, premier's dept., New S. Wales since Mar., 1916.

HAY, Mortimer Cecil.—B. 1891; ed. The Schl., Bishop Stortford and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge (exhbr.); M.A., Cantab.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; passed cadet, July, 1916; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1920; dep. contrlr. of lab., Klang, Dec., 1920; dep. contrlr., lab., Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; astt. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Jan., 1930; cls. II, Nov., 1930; Br. advr., Perlia, Dec., 1930; ag. legal advr., Johore, Mar., 1933.

HAY, Capt. William Ross, M.B.E., A.M.I.E.E.—B.1883; ed. St. John's, Highbury and Northampton Poly.; chartered elec. engnr.; Br. post office, 1901-20; B.E.F., France, 1915-17; E.E.F., Egypt, Palestine and Syria, 1917-20 (staff capt.); M.B.E. (mily.), "1914-15" Star, etc.; engr., posts and tels., Palestine, 1920-30; ag. ch. engr., on several occasions; divnl. engr., posts and tels., Nigeria, Oct., 1930.

HAYCRAFT, Sir Thos. Wagstaffe, Kt. Bach. (1919).—B.A., St. John's Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1885; practised in S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; examr. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lond. chain. of arbit., 1897; pres. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1899; pol. mag., Gibraltar, 1911; puine judge, Mauritius, 1913; ch. just., Grenada, 1916; ch. just., Palestine, 1921; ret., Mar., 1927; author of "Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder."

HAYDEN, James J.—B. 1883; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Dublin; clasped in feudal and English law, Dublin Univ. (dual gold medallist); admitted solr., sup. ct. of judicature, Ireland, 1906; called to Irish bar, 1926; registr., high ct., Uganda, Jan., 1921; also addnl. dist. mag., 1st cls. admstr.-gen., rev. authority, registr. of companies, patents, designs, trade-marks, documents and business names, custodian of enemy property and contr. of local clearing house; mag., Uganda, Mar., 1927.

HAYDOCK-WILSON, Hugh.—B. 1897; ed. Bedford Modern Schl.; on war serv., Artists Rifles (London R.) and Manchester Regt., 1915-19; ent. civ. serv., Br. Guiana as 4th cls. clk., C.S.O., Apr., 1921; 3rd cls. clk., July, 1924; capt., B. G. Militia (Art.), 1922-25; 2nd cls. clk., July, 1928; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., Oct. to Nov., 1928; hon. A.D.C. to gov. Feb., 1929; sec., colonization comte., Jan., 1929 to Mar., 1930; ag. prin. clk., C.S.O., Nov., 1930 to Mar., 1931; astt. comsnr., pol., Nigeria, May, 1931.

HAYES-SADLER, CECIL WILLIAM, M.A. (Oxon)—B. 1892; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxon; served in France with R. Engnr., (Signals), 1914-18; French Croix de Guerre, 1917; asst. dist. commr., Kenya Col., 1920; ag. sen. asst. sec., 1926; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., 1933.

HAYNES, ALWYN SIDNEY, C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1878; ed. at Haileybury Coll.; cadet, F.M.S. Nov., 1901; attached to dist. office, Batang Padang, Jan., 1902; mag., Perak, 1902; passed cadet, Aug., 1903; 1st cls. mag., Negri Sembilan, 1906; asst. supt., Indian immigrants, Nov., 1908; examr., Tamil, Perak and Penang; mem., bd. of examrs. in Malay for Perak, Apr., 1915; dist. offr., 1916; sec., high commr., Apr., 1920; sec. for agr., and dir., food production, Feb., 1922; chmn., small holdings comttee. (rubber restrictions), Nov., 1923; sec. for agr., S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1927; ag. commr., lands, F.M.S. in addn., June to Aug., 1927; represented S.S., F.M.S. and R.R.I., Malaya, at Imp. Agr. Research Conf., London, 1927; mem., Col. Agr. Service comttee., 1927; dist. offr. Lower Perak, Feb., 1929; cls. IA, Apr., 1930; contr., lab., Malaya, Apr., 1930; Br. adviser, Kelantan, Nov., 1931; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1933.

HAZEN, HON. SIR JOHN DOUGLAS, K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C., LL.D.—B. 1860; ed. at Collegiate Schl., Fredericton, and Univ. of New Brunswick; called to the bar, 1883; K.C., 1894; elec. to H. of C., Canada, for St. John City and County, 1891; defeated, 1896; elec. to New Brunswick legis. for Sunbury, 1899, 1903, 1908; leader of the opposition in the New Brunswick legis. during that period; prem. and atty.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1908-1911; has been pres. of St. John law soc. and barristers soc. of New Brunswick; mem. of the P.C. for Canada, and min. of marine and fisheries in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; chief just. of New Brunswick, Nov., 1917.

HAZLERIGG, GREG, O.B.E. (1920).—Ed. St. John's Coll., Camb., B.A., LL.B. (class tripes, 1900, law tripes, 1905); dist. commr., G. Coast, Dec., 1908; regiar., sup. ct., Ceylon, Oct., 1910; called to Ceylon bar, 1914; sec., Ceylon Coun. of legal educon., 1914; served in Belgium and France with Rifle Brig., 1915; served in C.O. from May, 1916; sec. to gov.-gen., Union of S. Africa, 1919-21; employed in C.O. since 1924; temp. ag. prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

HAZLERIGG, THOMAS MAYNARD, M.C.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1906; lieut., R.A.S.C., 1914-1915; capt., R.A.S.C., 1915 to 1920; asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, 1920; ag. asst. land offr. in addn., 1921 to 1922 and from 1922 to 1923; ag. crown solr., Mar. to Nov., 1924; treasy. solr. in addn., 1926-30; ag. dep. regiar. in addn., Apr., 1928 to Feb., 1929; pol. mag. in addn., July to Sept., 1929; regiar., sup. ct., offl. admin. and regiar., companies, Oct., 1929; ag. land offr., and regiar., marriages, Nov., 1930 to Dec., 1931; ag. crown solr., Mar. to Nov., 1932.

HEAD, ERNEST WILFRID.—B. 1876; loco. foreman Ceylon rly., Dec., 1902; dist. loco. supt., Sept., 1905; asst. loco., carriage and wagon supt., Nov., 1912; asst. do., Dec., 1915; dep. gen. man., Oct., 1924; ag. gen. man. in 1930 and 1932; gen. man., Aug., 1932.

HEAD, W. G.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on mil. serv. from July, 1917, to Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher cler. offr., 19th May, 1925; seconded for service with Br. economic mission to Australia, 16th May, 1928 to 3rd Mar., 1929.

HEARNE, HORACE HECTOR.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, Jan., 1916; 1st cls. mag., Aug., 1918; dist. mag., Jan., 1921; senr. mag., Nov., 1924; ag. puisne judge on various occasions, 1925-26 and 1928-30; ag. chan. of diocese, Nov., 1928; mem., H.M.'s ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1930; chan. of diocese, 1932; puisne judge, Tanganyika Territory, 1933.

HEARST, SIR WILLIAM HOWARD, K.C.M.G. (1917).—B. 1864; ed. pub. schls., Collingwood Coll. Inst., and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1886; K.C., 1908; prominent legal practitioner at Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario; now head of firm Hearst, Rowland and Atkin; has been counsel in all more important civil and criminal cases in Algoma district in recent years; unsuccessful cand. for legis. assembly, Ontario, 1894; elec. at g.e. 1910, 1911, and 1914; min. of lands, forests and mines in Whitney administration, 1911; premier of Ontario, 1914; also min. of agric.; defeated at g.e., Oct., 1919; resig. with his gov't., Nov., 1919; mem., internat. joint commn. (Can. section), Zanzibar Feb., 1920.

HEATH, A. P.—B. 1897; ed. Uppingham; on mil. serv., Aug., 1915; 2nd lieut., Feb., 1917 to Aug., 1917; temp. clk., Min. of Nat. S-rv., Nov., 1917; transf'd., O.S.O., Mar., 1919; apprd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., Nov., 1921.
HEATH, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1890; asst. gov't, printer, Gold Coast, 1915; ag. gov't, privy, 1920, 1921, 1923 and 1932.

HEATH, W. G.—M.B., Ch.B., Edin. Univ.; qualified 1897; med. offr., Windward dist., Dominica, 1898; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, 1901; ag. med. offr., dist. "D," Dominica, 1903; ditto, dist. "A" 1904; ag. sen. med. offr., Montserrat, 1905, 1912, and since 1916; pres., defence reserve, 1909; ag. mem. legis. coun., 1911; ag. med. offr., Antigua, 1911; mem. legis. coun., May 1915; med. offr.-in-chg. of Montserrat regt. for the B.W.I. regt., Mar., 1916; surg.-lieut., defence force, 1916; provisionally mem. exec. coun., 1916; ag. commr., Montserrat, on several occasions since Feb., 1918.

HEATON, GEORGE HERBERT.—B. 1896; ed. privately and Oxford Univ.; lieut., 1st Kent Regt., and R.F.C., 1915; H.M. Hospital instn., 1924; supt., prisons, Uganda, 1927; commr., prisons, Tanganyika Territory, 1931.

HERDEN, GEORGE BRENTNALL, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1886; ed. Bedford Schl.; tel. constr. and engr., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, May, 1929; asptg.tel.engnr., Jan., 1941; asst. engrnr.-in-charge, Jan., 1921; P.M.G., July, 1928; served with Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914-16; dir., post and tels. to C.E.F., 1915; engrnr.-in-chg., 1921; P.M.G., 1928.

HEDDON, JOHN DONALD, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1879; clk., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1913; prs. clk., East Rand, 1915; survr., Durban, 1916; ch. clk., head office, Pretoria, 1918; dep. commr., cust. and excise, 1926; commr., cust. and excise, 1930; del. for Union gov't, at Int. Cust. Conf., 1921; cust. adviser to deleg. to Australian gov't. (four shipments), 1922; mem., deleg. to Kenya to rep. on trade prospects, 1922; rep. of Union gov't at economic conference, Antananarivo, Madagascar, 1922; advisor mem., Imp. Conf., 1926; del. for Uk. gov't., Imp. Conf., 1930; adviser, Union gov't, Ottawa Econ. Conf., 1932.

HEDLEY, HAROLD THOMAS, Incorporated Acct.—B. 1887; audit dept., S. S., Dec., 1921; i/c external audit dept., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1922; audr., grade A, Jan., 1923; ag. sr. audr., Nov., 1928-Jly., 1929; audr., Penang, Aug., 1930.

HELLINGS, GEOFFREY STUART.—B. 1888; ed. Christ's Hosp. (foundation school.) and St. John's Coll., Camb., Stewart of Rannoch Univ. schol., Camb., 1909; B.A., 1910 (cl. trip ii, I); cadet, F.M.S. Nov., 1911; on mil. serv., 1915-19; contr., lab. and prot. of Chinese, Johore, Sept., 1923; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1923; offr., cls. II., prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang Nov., 1928; collr., estate duty, F.M.S., June, 1929; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat in addn., Oct. to Nov., 1929; ag. offr., assignee and reglar., companies, F.M.S. in addn., Feb., 1930; mag., Ipoh, May, 1932.

HELPS, EDMUND ARTHUR PLUNKETT.—B.1888; ed. Marlborough and Clare Coll., Cambridge; history tripos., 2nd cls. (1909); B.A. (1909); cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1912; passed cadet, July, 1914; offr., cls. V., Mar., 1915; lent for service under the admstr. of German E. Africa, July, 1917; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Apr., 1920; dist. offr., May, 1921; attd. to soc., rubber restriction comttee., Kulim, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. III., ast. adviser, Kedah, Dec., 1925; cls. II., Jan., 1929; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Nov., 1931; ag. comsnr., lands and mines, Johore, May, 1933.

HELPS, ERNEST HERRBERT.—B.1888; joined Lord Milner's staff in Transvaal, 1905, and served with Inter-Col. coun. of the Transvaal and O.F.S. until July, 1908; clk., gov.'s office, Transvaal, July 1908, and gov-gen.'s office, May, 1910; admstr.'s office, Cape Prov., Mar., 1913; senr. clk., 1920; prin. clk., 1928; munic. sec., Dar-es-Salaam, Dec., 1930.

HELPS, GEORGE SYDNEY.—B. 1886; bldg. inspr., P.W.D., S.S. Mar., 1914; war serv., Oct., 1914-Nov., 1917; ast. engnr., F.M.S. Rlys., Feb., 1918; asupt. way and wks. depot, K.Lumpur, Dec., 1921; dist. engnr., grade II, F.M.S. Rlys., Feb., 1923; dist. engnr., H.M.S. "Britannia"; Boxer Rebellion, 1900; med. and clasps; Gen. Serv. Med., with Persian Gulf bar; war serv., 1914-18; ment. in despr.; placed on ret. list, 1922; pvtc. sec. to Sir Gordon Guggisberg, 1923; harmbr., Takoradi, Sept., 1927.

HENDERSON, FREDERICK LOUIS.—Ed. at Camb. Univ.; B.A., 1897; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., (Lon.); med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prota., Oct., 1904; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1920; dist. surg., May, 1923.

HENDERSON, GUY WILMOT McLINTOCK, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1897; ed. at Blundells, Wanganui Collegiate Schl., New Zealand and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; served in Great War with R.F.A., for 3½ years; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1923; prof. clk., prosecuting staff, G.F.O., London, 1929; stip. and circuit mak., Bahamas, 1932; ag. atty-gen., in 1933.

HENDERSON, JAMES ABBEY, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), B.Sa. (Pub. Health), D.T.M. and H. (Edin.), Offr., Order St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1889; lieut., R.A.M.C., T.F., 1914; senr. offr., E.E.F., D.A.D.M.S., Jerusalem, 1919; ret. with rank of major, 1921; senr. med. offr., Jerusalem, 1920; do., Palestine Rlys. and Haifa, 1928; senr. sany. med. offr., Jamaica, 1930; ag. supt. med. offr. and mem., leg. coun., on two occasions; surg. gen., reglar. gen. and immigr. agt. gen., Br. Guiana, 1933; mem., exec. and leg. couns.

HENDERSON, JOHN, M.A., D.Sc. (N.Z.), B.Sc. (Eng.), A.O.S.M., F.N.Z.Inst.—B. 1880; ed. Otago Boys' High Schl. and Otago Univ.; instr., Reefton schl. of mines, 1903; mining geologist, geolog. survey, 1911; dir., 1928.

HENDERSON, RICHARD MCNEIL, M.I.C.E., M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1886; ed. Allan Glen's Inst. and R. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; senr. ast. engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1912; exec. engnr., 1920; ast., D.P.W., Hong Kong, Jan., 1928; J.P., 1929; mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1932; D.P.W., 1932.
HENDERSON, W. W., M.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M. (Vict.), capt., R.A.V.C. (R. of O.).—Vety. offr., E. Africa Prot., 1913; vety. offr., Nigeria, 1924; ch. vety. offr., Nigeria, 1927.

HENDRIE, CAPT. HARRY GEORGE, M.C.—Foreman of Works, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 26th June, 1921; head mast., jun. trade schl., educn. dept., Dec., 1921; on mil. serv., Sept., 1914; 2nd lieut., Sept., 1915; coy. comdr. and ag. capt., Sept., 1916; demob. with rank of capt., Feb., 1920; M.C. and Bar; seconded to Gambia as prin., new vocational schl. to organise tech. educn., 1929.

HENDRY, WILLIAM, M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1888; ed. DanielStewart's Coll., Edinburgh (dux and gold medallist) and Edinburgh Univ.; Egyptian educn. serv., 1911-15 and 1919-20; served Royal Highlanders and Royal Irish Regt., 1915-19; ment. in desp., 1919; dir. of educn., Zanzibar, 1920; M.L.C., 1926; 3rd cls., Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1928; M.E.C., 1932.

HENKEL, JOHN SURGEON.—Joined forest dept., Eastern Conservancy, Cape, July, 1888; ast. to conservator, Mar., 1890; dist. forest offr., King William's Town, June, 1893; Stutterheim, Aug., 1898; ast. conserv. forests, E. Conservancy, Oct., 1905; ast. conserv., W. Conservancy, July, 1907; prin. lecturer, forestry, S. African schl. of forestry, Oct., 1906; resig. lecturership, Feb., 1908; chmn., bd. of management, S. African schl. of forestry, May, 1907; ast. conserv., forests, Midland Conservancy, Feb., 1909; conserv. forests, ditto, 2nd grade, Apr., 1912; conserv., forests, Natal Conservancy, Dec., 1914; served as captain with dist. mounted troops during S. African war, 1901-2; Queen's med.; transf., Rhodesian forest serv., Jan., 1930.
HENLEN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 29th May, 1930.

HENNIKER-HEATON, HERBERT.—B.A. (Oxon.), C.M.G. (1928).—Ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford (hons. hist., 1901); cadet, Fiji, 1902; attached to col. sec.'s offr., to 1905; European offr., armed constab., June, 1903; in regir.-gen.'s off., Mar., May, 1904; ag. adjut., armed constab., musk. instr., and inspr. of pol., Feb., 1905, to Feb., 1906; stip. mag., Feb., 1906, and ag. stip. mag., Lau, and inspr. of taxes, Feb. to May, 1906; ch. clk., native dept., and ag. ast. native comanr., July, 1906; apptd. ast. native comanr., Mar., 1907; ag. chief pol. mag. and offr. recvr. in bankruptcy, Mar., 1910, to Feb., 1911; mem. of mining bd., Apr., 1910; comanr. of customs, Jan., 1911; mem. bd. of examiners, Feb., 1911; nom. mem. Suva schl. bd., Oct., 1911; ag. chief pol. mag., Jan., 1912; mem. native regulation bd., May, 1912; ast. col. sec., Mauritius, Oct., 1913; ag. col. sec., Mar. to Sept., 1914; ag. prot. of immigrts. and poor law comanr., Apr., 1915, to Jan., 1916; ag. col. sec., Jan. to May and from July, 1916, to May, 1917; in comd., cov., Mauritius Vol. Forces, 1916-17; col. sec., Gambia, 1917; ag. gov., June to Dec., 1918, Oct. to Dec., 1919 and July, 1920 to Jan., 1921; col. sec., Falkland Is., 1921; ag. gov., June, 1923 to May, 1924; col. sec., Bermuda, Oct., 1925; col. sec., Cyprus, Sept., 1929; ag. gov., for various periods, 1929-32.

HENRY, HON. GEORGE STEWART, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1871; ed. pub. schs., Upper Can. Coll., Univ. of Toronto, and Ont. Agric. Coll.; farmer; sl. to Ont. legis. at by-el., 1913; re-el., 1914, 1919 and 1923; min. of pub. wks. and highways in Ferguson admstrn., 1923; premier, Ontario, 1930.

HENRY, ROBERT NOEL, M.A., higher dipl. educn., T.C.D.—B. 1898; ed. Coleraine and Trinity Coll., Dublin; on staff of St. Columba's Coll., Dublin, 1924-28; war serv., 1916-19; ast. dir., educn., Cyprus, 1930; ag. dir., educn. for various periods, 1929-33.

HERBERT, PERCY FREDERICK.—B. 1887; ed. Leymer, Liverpool Inst. High Schl. and Jesus Coll., Oxford, B.A.; supt., schls., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1915; inspr. and schlmast., 1919; ag. ast. dir., S. Provs., 1927 and 1928; ag. dep. dir., S. Provs., 1929; ch. inspr., S. Provs., 1929.

HERBST, JOHN FREDERICK, C.B.E. (1919).—Ck., Cape census off., May, 1891; Carnarvon, Dec., 1891; Willowmore, May, 1895; atty.-gen.'s off., Cape Town, July, 1895; held several ag. appntns.; ast. mag., Gordonia, Apr., 1897; relvg. off., Apr., 1899; ag. ast. mag., Rietfontein, Nov., 1905; confirmed Jan., 1908; ag. civ. comsnr. and mag., Somerset, E., May, 1908; mag., St. Marks, July, 1908; detached for special service in B.B. Prot. in connection with inroads of Hottentots ex German S.W. Africa, under Simon Kooper; received thanks of the Cape gov't. and Imp. Germ. gov't. upon successful accomplishments of mission; inspecting mag., S.W. Africa Prot. (with rank of Major in Union Defence Forces), Sept., 1915; dep. secy., S.W. Africa Protectorate, Mar., 1916; secy. for Protectorate, Dec., 1916; acntg. offr. in addition, Apr., 1920; ag. admstr., S.W. Africa, Oct., 1920; sec. for S.W. Africa, civ. admstrn., 1921; sec. for native affrs., Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1923.

HERDMAN, HON. SIR ALEXANDER LAWRENCE, KT. BACH. (1929).—B. 1869; ed. at Otago and Oamaru high schls.; barrister-at-law, 1894; entd. New Zealand parl., 1902; atty.-gen., min. of justice, 1912; atty.-gen. in National ministry, 1915; resig. 1918, and apptd. judge of N.Z. sup. ct.

HERMON-HODGE, HON. H. B.—B. 1885; ed. Winchester coll. (scholar) and Magdalen coll., Oxford; B.A., hona. in mods. and hist.; ast. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1908; dist. offr., Sept., 1917; res., Mar., 1925; senr. res., Aug., 1930.

HEROLD, THOMAS BARRY.—Cape civ. ser., 1882-1901; ch. ck. to master of sup. ct., Transvaal, Oct., 1901; ast. master, June, 1902; comsnr. of protocols, Dec., 1904; master of the sup. ct., Oct., 1906; mem. of prisons advisory bd., Feb., 1907; dir. (chmn., 1907-8 and 1908-9), land and agric. bank, Oct., 1907; chmn. and gen.-man., land and agric. ank of South Africa, 1912; J.P., Union of S. Africa, 1912; man. dir., land and agric. bank, Oct., 1921-30; mem., land bank bd., 1930.

HERON, GEORGE, A.M.I.N.A., A.M.I.Mech. E.—B. 1882; temp. surv., ships, Singapore, Nov., 1915; ast. surv., Jan., 1918; ag. sr. surv., ships, May, 1921; surv., ships, Singapore, Jan., 1922 and Aug., 1924; sr. surv., ships, Dec., 1923; ag. surv., gen., ships, S.S. Apr., 1927 and Sept., 1931; dep. surv.-gen., ships, May, 1928; surv.-gen., ships, S.S., Nov., 1932.
HERRING, HERBERT RALPH.—B. 1905; ed. Ipswich and Oriel Coll., Oxford, B.A. (chem.), hona., B.A. (forestry); ast. conservator, forests, Tanganyika Territory, July, 1926.

HERTZOG, HON. J. B. M., B.A., LL.D., M.P.—B. 1866; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and Amsterdam Univ.; judge of S.W. div., 1895 to 1900; comd. Boer forces of S.W. div., 1899 to 1902; one of the signatories of the treaty of Vereeniging; atty.-gen., O.R.C.; min. of just., Union of S. Africa, 1910-1912; leader of opposition in Union parl., 1913-24; prime min. nat. party gov't., June, 1924 to Mar., 1933; do. coalition gov't., Mar., 1933.

HEWLETT, JOHN BERTRAM.—B. 1890; ast. auditor, G. Coast, Feb., 1914; ag. ast. asst. auditor, Jan. to Mar., 1919; ag. ast. asst. sec., Aug. to Sept., 1919; jun. clk., Coll. Audit. Dept., July, 1920; 2nd cls. clk., Apr., 1921; audtr., Gibraltar, Nov., 1922; audtr., Trinidad, Sept., 1926; audtr. N. Rhodesia, 1930.

HEYWOOD, GRAHAM SCOTTAMORE PERCY.—B. 1903; ed. Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; B.A. and B.Sc., 1925; meteorological research ast., war dept., Leafld, 1925; professional ast., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1932.

HEYWOOD-WADDINGTON, ALFRED.—B. 1898; ed. Devonport High Schl. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1920; licut., 3rd Somerset L.I., July, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; offr. cls. V., Mar., 1924; fedl. examr. in Tamil, Sept., 1924 and July 1926; offr. cls. IV, Mar., 1925; ast. emigr. comsnr. for Malaya; ast. dir., co-op. pernr., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; ast. contr. lab., Penang, Jan., 1931; offr., cls. III, Mar., 1933.

HIBBERT, JOHN GROFFERT, M.C.—B. 1860; ed. Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. hon. schl. mod. languages (French) 1922; pte., 28th County of London Regt., Aug., 1914; lieut., R.A.O.C., Nov., 1915; capt., May, 1916; major, May, 1918; D.A.D.O.S. 15th Sect. Divn.; ment. in desps.; attd. mission to Lithuania, Oct., 1919; junr. examr., clearing office (enemy debts) B. of T., Sept., 1920; senr. examr., Jan., 1921; ast. claims clk., Jan., 1922; ast. d. B. gov't. agt., Mixed Arbitral Tribunal, July, 1927; temp. admstve. offr., D.O., Nov., 1929; ast. Feb., 1930; ast. pte. acc. to S. of S. for the Cols., Oct., 1930.

HICKSON-MAHONY, R.S.B.M.—Civ. ser., Cape Colony and Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1903 to Mar., 1917; war ser., Boer War, 1899-1900, Great War, 9th S. African Inf., 1915-1917; ast. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1917; 1st grade admstve. offr., Apr., 1921; founded as dep. lab. comsnr., 1930-31.

HIGGINS, EVELYN SCOTT.—Ed. at Blundells Schl., Tiverton, and Chatham House, Ramsgate; served on Uganda rly., 1898-1900; supt. of pts., Somaliland Prot., May, 1905; ast. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1911; supt., pts., Kenya, Oct., 1925.

HIGGINS, MAJOR ROBERT HENRY CONSTANT, M.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and R. Vetv. Coll., Dublin; war ser., France, Mesopotamia and Archangel, 1914-1918, ment. in desps.; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1923.

HIGGINSON, ROBERT CYRIL GERARD DEAN.—B. 1884; ed. Brighton Coll.; cadet, Feb., 1907; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1908; admstved barrister and solr., sup. ct., Fiji, 1909; stip. tug., 1910; passed cadet, 1911; 2nd grade dist. commr., 1914; dist. comsnr. and prov. comsnr., Cey East, Apr., 1914; prov. comsnr., Mamoa and Serua and dist. comsnr., Navua, 1915; ag. res. comsnr., Rotuma, Nov., 1919 to Jan., 1921; prov. comsnr., and dist. comsnr., Lomaviti, Apr., to Dec., 1921; ag. ch. pol. mag., ag. regisr., sup. ct., ag. regisr. of titles, ag. curator, intest. and vacant estates, ag. pub. trustee, ag. off. recvr. in bankruptcy, Dec., 1921 to Mar., 1922; ch. pol. mag., regisr.-gen. and regisr., titles, July, 1922; nom. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1922; comsnr., cust., Sept., 1925; mem. of comte. to rept. on constitution and laws of Rotuma, Nov., 1925; seconded for ap. serv. with the W. Pacific high comsnr. and apptd. a judl. comsnr., to preside over Malaita murder trials, Solomon Is., Mar. to June, 1928, resumed duty as ch. pol. mag. etc., Oct., 1928; res. mag., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1929.

HILL, ALEXANDER GEORGE GLENONDON, B.A., Agr. B., A.R.C.Sci.-B.; 1900; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; supt., agr., Nigeria, 1923; botanist, Nigeria, 1926; senr. geneticist, sugarcane research stn., Mauritius, 1929; sent to Java to study cane breeding methods, 1930; ag. dir., agr., Mauritius, Oct., 1930 to Mar., 1932.

HILL, GILBERT.—B. 1889; war serv., 1914-19; offr. in ch., wireless stations, Scapa Flow, 1919-20; wireless engnr. operator, Gambia, Feb., 1923; tel. asst. dir., Somaliland, Oct., 1926; ag. dir., posts and tels. in 1931, 1932 and 1933.

HILL, JAMES REGINALD.—B. 1877; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; 8th clk., admtr. gen.'s off., B. Guiana, May, 1896; 5th cls. clk., police dep., Feb., 1897; sub-inspr., pol., Dec., 1900; dist. inspr., pol., Apl., 1903; ag. adjt. and muskty. instr., July to Nov., 1906; ag. county inspr. of pol., Nov., 1909 to Apl., 1910 and from June to Aug., 1911; ag. pay and qtrmr. from Sept., 1911; pay and qtrmr. Jan., 1912; commd. paymr. and staff offr. for admstr. duties with local forces, 1914-18; 1st cls. clk., audit dept., Apr., 1923; ag. dir., co-op. socs., July to Dec., 1923; ch. clk., audit dept., Jan., 1925; ag. audr. on various occasions, 1926-27.

HILL, HON. LIONEL LAUGHTON, M.P. (S. Australia)—B. 1881; comsnr. of pub. wks., min. of educn. and min. of industry, S. Australia, 1924-26; premier, treas. and min. of educn., 1926-27 and since Apr., 1930.

HILL, REGINALD DYKERS RICHARDSON.—B. 1902; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1924; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1926; ag. prin. clk. and clk. to the ct. of policy and combined ct., Sept. to Apr., 1928; ag. prin. clk. and 2nd A.C.S. on various occasions, 1928-29; mag., 1933.

HILLIER, SIDNEY CHARLES.—B. 1888; London Schl. of Economics (Brunel med.); G.W.R., 1902; mly. serv., 1915-1921; ment. in desig. (twice); G.W.R., 1921; asst. acct., Tanganyika rly., 1922; ag. dep. ch. acct., Tanganyika rly., on various occasions, 1925-29; ag. ch. acct., 1928; dep. ch. acct., July, 1930; ch. acct., 1930.

HIMSWORTH, ERIC.—B. 1905; ed. Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A.; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Feb., 1929; passed cadet, Feb., 1931; dep. regisr., Feb., 1931; attld. treasy., 1932; asst. head sanv. dep., 1932.

HINCKS, MAURICE THORP.—B. 1885; ed. Stoneygate Schl., Leicester, and Blairlodge Schl., Polmont, N.B.; sup., ct., 1908; comsnr. for oaths, 1913; on mly. serv., 1915 to 1920; junr. administrative offr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; pol. mag., Accra, Apr., 1920; ag. ch. regisr., Gold Coast, on various occasions; ag. solr.-gen., Jan. to May, 1922; ch. regisr., sup. ct., Nigeria, Aug., 1927.
HINGLEY, ANTHONY CAPPER MOORE, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1908; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1931; attld. finan. sec's. office, Jan., 1932; do., Kandy kach., Aug., 1932; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, June, 1933.

HISLOP, CAPT. FRANCIS DANIEL, M.A. (hons. hist.), Edin. Univ.—B. 1891; served Territorial Force, 1909-21; on active serv., 1914-19; served Gallipoli, Egypt, France; seconded to Machine Gun Corps, 1917; instr. in machine gunnery, 1918-19; cadet, S. Stithmta, Oct., 1919; attld. treas., Singapore, Dec., 1919; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, Singapore, June, 1920; resig. on med. grounds, June, 1921; cadet, Kenya, Dec., 1923.
HITCHENS, ALFRED EDEN.—B. 1880; asst. engnr., water wks., Trinidad, Dec., 1900; 1st grade dist. offr., P.W.D., Trinidad, Sept., 1906; divisional engnr., Apr., 1925.

HOAL, WILLIAM GARNER.—B. 1886; ed. Normal Coll. Schl. and S. African Coll., Cape Town; B.A., 1904; LL.B., 1908; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1909; clk. to mag., Woodstock, 1906; sup. ct., Cape Town, 1908; asst. regisr., prize ct., 1914; asst. regisr. and taxing offr., sup. ct., Kimberley, 1917; prof. asst. to atty.-gen., Pretoria, 1920; govt. atty., 1924; atty.-gen., O.F.S., 1929.
HOARE, ALBERT RAWLINSON.—B. 1884; govt. schlmr., Falkland Is., Aug., 1915; local audr., Apr., 1928; supt., educn. and headmnr., govt. schl., Stanley, Sept., 1928.

HOARE, HENRY SAMUEL MALORIE, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1928; attld., Kurunegala kach., Feb., 1928; attld., Anuradhapura kach., April, 1928; office asst., Jaffna kach., Feb., 1929; pvtc. sec. to gov., Feb., 1931; landing survr., cust., Nov., 1932.

HOARE, COL. FRANCIS RICHARD GURNET, C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1879; ed. Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 9th Middlesex Regt. and 4th Suffolk Regt. (Volunteers), 1894-1900; served S.A. War, 1899-1900; Queen's Med. and two clasps; ent. Transvaal civ. serv., 1903; 2nd lieut., Transvaal Horse Arty., 1912; seconded to Army Ordnance Dept. as temp. ord. offr., 4th cls. with rank of capt., 1914; do., 3rd cls. with rank of maj., A.O.D., 1915; depot commdr., 1915; temp. lt.-col., R.F.C., 1917; staff offr., air equipment to R.A.F. training brig., Canada and U.S.A.; maj. (temp. lt.-col.) (tech.), R.A.F., 1918; inspgr. ord. offr. (lt.-col.), Active Citizen Force, Union of S. Africa, 1919; lt.-col., staff offr., 1st grade, Permt. Force (staff), U.D.F., 1919; staff offr., air equipment, in addn. to duties of inspgr. ord. offr., 1920; lt.-col., S.A. Ordnance Corps, 1933; dir., tech. services, U.D.F., May, 1933; col., S.A. Staff Corps, Sept., 1933.

HOBBS, JOSEPH.—B. 1886; R.I.C., 1906-13; inspr. pol., F.M.S., May, 1913; supvr. cust., F.M.S. (Kuala Lumpur), Sept., 1921; ch. supt., prev. br., Oct., 1923; ag. dep. comsnr., trade and cust., F.M.S., Oct., 1923-May, 1924 and in May, 1927; dep. comsnr., trade and cust. (excise), F.M.S., Sept., 1927; ditto, prev. br., June, 1930; dep. comsnr., trade and cust., Perak, Jan., 1931.

HOBDAY, WILFRED ERNEST.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attld. col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1913; Colombo kach., April, 1913; Trincomalee kach., Nov., 1913; on mil. duty, Sept., 1914; landing survr., Colombo customs, May, 1919; 4th asst. col. sec., Jan., 1922; 3rd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1922; 2nd

HODSON, Sir Arnold Wienholt, K.C.M.G. (1932), C.M.G. (1922).—Ed., Italy, Mulgrave Castle, and Felsted; in Central Queensland, 1900-1902; joined Australian Commonwealth Horse for service in S. Africa, 1902; in Transvaal, 1902-4; sub-insp., Bech. Prot. pol., 1904; specially employed in connection with Damara war (med. and clasp); mem. of Ngami land mission, 1906; ag. asst. commnr., Mar. to May, 1910, and Mar. to Dec., 1911; dist. commnr. and lat. cls. mag., Somaliland, 1912; H.B.M. consul for Southern Abyssinia, 1914; ditto, S.W. Abyssinia, 1923; served part time in European War; present at coronation of Empress Zoditow, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia; African Gen. Ser. Med. and Clasp, Jubaland, 1917-18; attclhd., Abyssinian army in expedition against Tigre, 1919 (med. and clasp); late capt., Royal Sussex Regt. (Territorial); has passed Cape Univ. mag. law exam.; Somali language, lower standard; Galla language, higher standard; gov. and commdr-in-chief, Falkland Is. and their dependencies, 22nd Dec., 1925; ditto, Sierra Leone, May, 1931; author "Trekking the Great Thirst," "Seven Years in Southern Abyssinia," "Where Lion Reign," joint author of "A Practical Galla Grammar."

HODSON, Bernard.—B. 1876; ent. Natal civ. serv., 1894; clk., mag.'s office, Dundee, 1894; clk., min., native affrs., 1895; capt. and maj. mag., Volksrust, Transvaal, 1900; prin. clk., Durban, 1905; addnl. mag., Durban, 1910; mag., Nongoma, Zululand, 1915; mag., Mpofana, 1915; mag., New Hanover, 1921; mag., Pinetown, 1921; mag., Dundee, 1926; ch. mag., Maritzburg, 1929.

HODSON, Thomas Arthur.—B. 1882; ed. Cheltenham and Wadham Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; seconded asst. supt. of pol., Galle, Dec., 1906; asst. supt. of pol., Galle, July, 1908; ditto, Tangalla, May, 1909; ditto, Matara, Dec., 1909; office asst. to gov't. agt., S. Prov., Jan., 1910; pol. mag., Randv, Feb., 1911; asst. gov't. agt., Trincomalee, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, May, 1914; asst. settltn. offr. and spec. offr. under Waste Lands Ordo., June, 1916; on mil. duty, 1917; addtl. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1919; asst. gov't. agt., Kalutara, May, 1919; addnl. ass. dir., educa., Mar., 1922; asst. gov't. agt., Matale, Mar., 1924; gov't. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1925; ditto, N.W. Prov., Oct., 1926; do., Cent. Prov., Jan., 1931; do., Prov. of Uva, Nov., 1931; ofi. cls. I, grade I, 1932; gov't. agt., Cent. Prov., Oct., 1932.

HOFMEYR, Petrus Jacobus Herbert, F.S.S.—B. 1877; cl. asst., Diamond det. dept., Kimberley, 1895; clk., atty.-gen.'s and col. sec. offices, Cape, 1898; clk., mag.'s office, Klerkstad, 1902; asst. regtr. of statistics (Transvaal), 1905; sec., pub. serv. comm., 1912; pub. serv. insp., 1921; pub. serv. comm., 1926; seconded to stats alluvial diamond fields, Namqualand, as gen. man., Feb. to June, 1929.

HOGAN, Hon. Edmond John, M.L.A. (Victoria)—B. 1884; M.L.A., W. Australia, 1913; leader, Victorian parl. lab. party, Apr., 1929; premier and treas., Victoria, May, 1927 to Nov., 1928 and from Dec., 1929 to May, 1932.

HOGAN, Gerald George.—B. 1886; barrister, 1910 (Victoria); practised, Victoria until 1911; served Australian Field Art., Egypt and France, 1915-19; rank of major on retirement; mem. in desps.; stip. sp. mag., N. Territory, Australia, 1919; dep. judge, sup. ct., N. Territory, 1920; crown law offr., mandated Territory of New Guinea, 1922; M.E.C. and M.L.O., 1933.
HOGGEN, SIDNEY JOHN, B.A.—B. 1898; ed. Highgate Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; supt., educ. dept., N. Prov., Nigeria, Aug., 1921; senr. educn. offr., Palestine, 1933.

HOGG, WILLIAM EDWARD, A.R.C.S., A.M. Inst. C.E., Whitworth Schl.—B. 1880; ed. City of London Schl. and Imp. Coll. of Science; eng. inspr., C.A., 1910; asst. engr., 1919; dep. head, engrg. inspr. dept., 1920.

HOLDBROOK, HUBERT GEORGE.—B. 1887; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., 1908; M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., 1910; M.D. 1929; L.M., Rotunda, med. offr., S. Stltntns, 1912; med. offr., F.M.S., 1913; certif., L.S.T.M., 1921; health offr., F.M.S., 1921; ch. med. offr., Malacca, 1923; senr. med. offr., Selangor, Feb., 1927; ag. ch. med. offr., Penang, Sept., 1931.
HOLDING, RICHARD HENRY.—B. 1898; on mily serv., Apr. 1917 to Mar., 1920; temp. clk., O.S.O., Apr. 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., 6th Oct., 1921.

HOLE, COMMDR. GEORGE FRANCIS, R.N.—B. 1887; cadet, H.M.S. "Britannia," 1902; midshipman, 1904; sub-lieut., 1907; lieut., 1909; commanded H.M.S. "Petersel," 1914-15; invalided and pensioned, 1916; asst. master attendant, Colombo, Ceylon, Mar., 1916; offr. in charge, mine-sweeping Colombo, 1917-19; lieut.-commdr. 1917; priv. sec. and ag. A.D.C. to gov., Apr., 1919; master attendant, Colombo and Galle, July, 1922; harbour, Hong Kong, 1924; comdr., 1927; offr. mem., leg. coun., 1929; dir. air services, 1929; head, Hong Kong deleg. to Shipping Confce., Simla, 1931.

HOLFORD, GEORGE TILLSTONE, A.I.M.M.—B. 1885; ed. Brighton Coll. and Camborne Schl. of Mines; inspr., mines, F.M.S., Sept., 1911; 2nd lieut., R.H.A. & R.F.A. (S.R.), June, 1915; France, 1915-19; asst. warden, mines, Sept., 1920; warden, mines, Pahang, June, 1930; do., Selangor, 1933.

HOLGATE, MAXWELL RUSSELL, M.A.(N.Z.).—B. 1896; asst. mast., Raffles Schl., July., 1920; sr. mast., Sultan Idris Training Coll., Perak, Nov., 1922; ag. prin., do., Sept., 1926 and Apr., 1930; asst. inspr. schls., Singapore and Labuan, Nov., 1930; headmast., King Edward VII Schl., Taiping, Dec., 1930.

HOLLIDAY, ARTHUR ROBERT.—B. 1884; atty. and notary, sup. ct. of S. Africa, 1906; asst. dist. comsr., Gold Coast Colony, Jan., 1915; seconded for service in Togoland; dist. polit. offr., Misahohe, Oct., 1917; dist. polit. offr., Lome, 1918; ag. dist. comsr., Boke, N. Territories, Gold Coast, Oct., 1918; asst. admstr.-gen., Zanzibar, Jan., 1925; ag. admstr.-gen., pub. trustee. regir. of doc. and land offr. on various occasions, 1925-29; admstr.-gen. and offr. reor., Uganda, 1930.

HOLLINGSWORTH, LAWRENCE WILLIAM, M.R.S.T., B.A. (Lond.), F.R. Hist. S.—B. 1896; ed. Sir Walter St. John's Schl., London Univ., and London Day Training Coll.; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-19; headmr., teacher's training schl., Zanzibar educn. dept., 1922; author "Milango ya History" (Vols. I, II and III); "Jiografia ya Unguja na Pemba", "A Short History of the East Coast of Africa," part author "A School History of Zanzibar."

HOLLINS, NOEL CLINTON.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Cantab.), 2nd cls. hist., tripods; qualified in exam. for gen. consular service; asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Mar., 1910; ag. senr. ast. col. sec., 1911 and 1912; asst. dist. comsr., Jan., 1913; dist. comsr., Jan., 1915; senr. dist. comsr., Jan., 1931; ag. prov. comsr., cent. prov., 1922, 1928, 1929 and 1930.

HOLLIS, SIR ALFRED CLAUD, K.C.M.G. (1927), C.M.G. (1911), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1874; asst. collr., E.A.P., Mar., 1897; employed on transport duty during Uganda Mutiny, 1898; collr., June, 1900; ag. Brit. vice-consul for German East Africa, Apr., 1900 to Feb., 1901; ag. sec. to admtn. and priv. sec. to H.M. comsr., Feb., 1901 to Apr., 1903; sec. to the admtn., 1st Apr., 1903; Jubaland expdn., 1900-1, and Nandi expdn., 1905-6 (medal and two clasps); sec. for native affairs and M.L.C., June, 1907; on sp. serv. to Uganda, Oct., Nov., 1909; ag. ch. sec. to govt., Apr. to Dec., 1911, and Feb. to Oct., 1912; Swahili examiner, 1901 to 1912; col. sec., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1913; ag. gov., Oct., 1913 to Feb., 1914, June to Oct., 1915 and Jan. to Mar., 1916; sec. to provisional admtn., German E.
