Arabic exam., 1917; 1st cls. mag., June, 1917; pub. cust., enemy property in Palestine, under occupied enemy territory adminstr., Dec., 1919; 1st asst. sec., Palestine govt., 11th Oct., 1920; lent to C.O. by Palestine govt. and ag. prin., Feb., 1924 to Feb., 1925; asst. dist. comntr., N. dist., 1st Oct., 1925; dep. dist. comntr., Jerusalem divn., 12th Nov., 1926; publications, "Darfur, a Province of the Anglo-Egyptian Sudan," "The Pageant of Jerusalem," joint editor, "Handbook of Palestine, 1922"; edr., "Gen. Regs. of the Govt. of Palestine."

KELLAGHER, GEORGE BANNERMAN.—B. 1889; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1919; cadet, S.S., 1913; attd. res. councillor's office, Penang, Dec., 1913; attd. G.P.O., Singapore, May, 1914; passed cadet, Feb., 1917; supernmy. offr., cls. V., Feb., 1917; on mil. serv., Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.E., Dec., 1917; B.E.F., France, Apr., 1918; wounded Oct., 1918; lieut., June, 1919; demob., Feb., 1920; supernmy. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; offr., cls. III., 1925; currency comntr., Mar., 1927; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; European agency, Kedah, Mar., 1933.

KELLIE, GEORGE GORDON.—Seconded from London postal serv. and apptd. 2nd asst. dir., posts and tels., Somaliland, Jan., 1924; postal asst. dir., Somaliland, Apr., 1926; ag. D.P.T., June-Oct., 1927; survr., posts and tels., Tanganyika Territory, 1931.

KELLY, SIR HENRY GREENE, KT. BACH. (1906).—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dub.; honorman and prizeman in hist. and political science; B.A. and LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inn, Dub., Mich. Term, 1894; apptd. a puisne judge in the Niger Territories, 1891; acted on several occasions as chief justice; chief justice, 1899; chief justice of Southern Nigeria, 1900; ret., 10th Feb., 1908.

KELLY, HON. HUGH THOMAS.—B. 1858; ed. St. Michael's Coll., Toronto, and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1886; K.C., 1908; pres., York Co. Law Assoc., 1910 and 1911; chmn., Toronto pub. lib. bd. for 3 years; a gov. of Toronto Univ., 1906-1912; jud. of high ct. of Ontario, 1911.

KELLY, ROBERT ALSOFT, A.S.A.A., J.P.—B. 1881; ed. George Heriot's Schl., Edinburgh; clk., inland rev., 1898; S.A. war, 1900-02 (Queen's med., 5 clasps); asst. acct., N. Rhodesia, 1911; assist. audr., 1913; ag. audr., 1914-15 and in 1919; lieut., N.R. Rifles, 1916; ag. adjt., 1917-18; dep. treas., N. Rhodesia, 1921; ag. treas., 1922; ag. treas. and mem., exec. coun., 1925, 1927 and 1928; ag. comntr., taxes, 1928; treas. and currency offr., mem., exec. and leg. couns., Gold Coast, 1929.

KELLY, ROBERT WILLIAM CREIGHTON, M.R.C.S.(Eng.), L.R.C.P.(Lond.)—B. 1888; med. offr. i/c schls. and venereal diseases, S.S., Apr., 1923; med. offr., social hygiene, May, 1926; health offr., May, 1927; chief med. offr., social hygiene, S.S. Feb., 1928.
KELSICK, OSCAR RICHARDSON.—Ag. cashier, savings bank, Antigua, Aug., 1906; apptd. as ditto, Jan., 1908; acctnt., savings bank, Sept., 1908; treas., May to Aug., 1909; junr. audit

KENNIFIE, VICTOR, B.E. (SYD.), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E. (AUST.), M.I.M. AND C.T. R.—B.1856; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ.; nat. eng., P.W.D., N. S. Wales, 1916; dist. eng., P.W.D., Fiji, 1921; ag. amt. commr. of whs., 1927; ag. commr. of wks., chmn., cent. road bd., Apl. to Dec., 1927; mem., leg. coun., Fiji, Nov. to Dec., 1927; asst. D.P.W., Cyprus, Mar., 1936; ag. D.P.W. on various occasions, 1930-33.

KENT, HENRY TUFFIELD MONEY, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1886; ed. Elstow schl. Cit. and Guilds Coll.; instr., tech. schl., F.M.S., Sex., 1914; ag. timber suptd., F.M.S., rly., Dec., 1914; contr., timber supplies, F.M.S., rly., Jan., 1919; ditto, forest dept., S. Stltmtn. and F.M.S., Jan., 1923; sent to report on timber utilization problems in India and Burma, May, 1924; on spl. duty, Madison, U.S.A., Nov. to Dec., 1925; prin., tech. schl., F.M.S., Dec., 1930.
KENT, HON. JAS. M.—K.C., R.A.; M.H.A., Newfoundland, 1904; mem. of exec. com. and min. of just., 1907; judge, supreme ct., 1914.

KERR, DAVID IRVINE—B. 1878; clk. to pol. mag., Grenada, 1899; rev. offr. (various grades), 1902-15; 2nd clk., treas., 1918; ch. rev. offr., 1919; ag. ch. of pol., 1920 and 1924; ch. cl. P.W.D., 1921; sup't., of prisons and recorder's meteorological obsns., 1922; seconded for war service (Egypt and E. Africa), 1915-19; J.P., 1919.

KERR, ROBERT ASHFORD—B. 1870; ed. p.m. sch., Swellendam; served Matabele war as rebellion and seriously wounded in latter; force mem., dep. ch., and ch. of com. prov. coun., Transvaal and pres. senate; mem., Union senate 1921.

KERR, ROBERT TAYLOR, A.M.I.Mech.I., M.I., Loco.E.—B. 1888; ed. Dumfries Acad., British Rly., 1905; wks. man., Gold Coast, Apr., 1915; title changed to asst. loco. eng., Jan., 1917; seconded to Togoland mly. for 18 periods, 1917-20; dist. loco. supt., Gold Coast, Apr., 1923; wks. man., July, 1925; ch. mech. engrn., Apr., 1926.

KERR-PEARSE, MAJOR B.A.T., C.M.G., (formerly Rifle Brigade).—B. 1871; A.D.C. to gen. of Australia (Lord Northcote), 1903-07; priv. sec. to gov. of West Australia (Sir G. Strickland), 1904-12; priv. sec. to gov. of Tasmania (Rt. Hon. Sir W. Ellison-Macartney), 1913-15; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Australia (Rt. Hon. Sir R. Munro Ferguson), 1915-16; mil. sec., 1916.

KERSHAW, ARTHUR EDWIN POMEROL, M.C., A.I.M.M.—B. 1890; inspr., mines, F.M.S., 1912; sec. for mly. serv., Sept., 1917; 2nd loc. R.F.A. (S.R.), Feb., 1918; demob., Feb., 1919; inspr., mines, F.M.S., 1919; asst. warden, mines, Sept., 1921; senr. inspr., mines, B. Gajah, Feb., 1931; asst. warden, mines, Perak, Apr., 1931.
KEY, WALTER JOHN SMITH, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1896; ed. Dundee Tech. Schl., Dundee Tech. Coll. and St. Andrews Univ.; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

KIBBLE, EDMUND DAVID, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1881; ed. Highgate Schl. and City & Guilds (Engnr.) Coll., S. Kensington; asst. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Dec., 1907; ex. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1914; senr. ex. engr., 1915; att'd, govt. town planning dept., F.M.S., Aug., 1924; state engrn., Pahang, June, 1927; hyd. engnr., Aug., 1930; state engrn., Pahang, Jan., 1932.

KIDD, MAJOR GEORGE MONTGOMERY, R.A., Dublin, M.C.—B. 1889; cadet, F.M.S., 1912; seconded for mly. serv. 1915 to 1919; to advr., Kedah, Jan., 1920; ast. agt., food contr., S. Kedah, Feb., 1920; agt., food contr., Kedah, Sept., 1920; dep. supt., census, Kedah, Oct., 1920; sec., sany. bd., Alor Star, Sept., 1922; sec., rubber restn. comte., Kedah, Oct., 1922; attd., rubber restn. off., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1925; chmn., assess. comte., F.M.S., Mar., 1925; dist. offr., May, 1927; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Dec., 1929; ag. ast. advr., Muar, Sept., 1932.
KIDD, CAPT. W. R., M.C., B.A. (Oxon).—Aast. dist. comsnr., Kenya, Feb., 1920.

KIDGER, CECIL EDWARD.—B. 1875; ed. Cranbrook Pub. Sch.; ent. Cape civ. serv., 1894; Cape Garrison Art.; S.A. War, 1899-1902; ast. mag., Queenstown, Middledrift, Capetown, 1908; ditto, Taung v. Rhynsdorp, Bredasdorp, Pilgrims Rest, Witbank, Pietersburg, 1915-32; ditto, Bloemfontein, 1932.

KIDSON, ERNEST, O.B.E. (1919), M.A., D.Sc., F.Inst.P.—B. 1882; ed. Nelson Coll. and Canterbury Coll., N.Z.; on staff, Australian Commonwealth meteorological bureau, 1921-27; ast. dir., 1923-27; dir., meteorological services, N.Z., 1927.

KIDSON, NORMAN S. B.—B. 1885; served in Connaught Rangers, 1914-19; 2nd ast. mast., Boys' Gram. Schl., Fiji, Mar., 1920; clk. of peace, Rewa, Sept., 1920; ag. dist. comsnr., Rewa and ag. prov. comsnr., Naitasiri, Mar., 1921; ag. dist. comsnr. and prov. comsnr., Colo East, Apr., 1921; govt. sec., Br. Solomon Is., Prot., Nov., 1922; on sp. duty as judl. comsnr. for W. Pacific, Apr., 1924; has acted as offr.-in-charge at headqrs. on several occasions; ag.-res. comsnr. on various occasions, 1925-1932; offl. mem., advisory coun., 1927; judl. comsnr. (temp. and provisional), Nov., 1928.

KILPIN, RALPH.—B. 1887; ed. Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch, S. Africa; clk. of papers, Cape H. of A., 1905; clk. of papers, Union H. of A., 1910; 2nd clk.-ast., 1917; clk.-ast. and acctnt., 1920; admitted sworn translator (Eng. and Dutch), sup. ct., of S. Africa, 1912; assessor apptd. by admstr., Cape exec. comte., elections and elec. of senators since 1910; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-17; recd. vote of thanks of leg. assembly, S.W. Africa, for preparing standing rules and orders and assisting in inauguration of assembly, 1926; author of "The Old Cape House" and "The Romance of a Colonial Parliament."
KIMBELL, ALFRED HENRY.—B. 1875; ed. at Otago Boys' High Schl., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. pub. serv., 1895; held various positions in P.W.D., includg. land purch. offr.; und.-sec. of mines, 1920.

KIMBER, GURTH.—B. 1906; ed. Perse Schl. and Cambridge; nat. sci. schl., Clare Coll., 1924-27; 1st cls. nat. sci. tripos, pt. I, 1926; 2nd cls., pt. II, 1927; B.A., 1927; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. prin., Dominions Office, 19th Oct., 1928; temp. agt. prin., 18th Mar., 1932.

KING, CLEMENT HAMPDEN.—B. 1876; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; ag. 5th cls. clk., police dept., B. Guiana, June, 1938; gradual prom. to prin. clk.; dist. inspr. of police, 1901; county inspr., Dec., 1908; ag. adjt., July, 1907 to Mar., 1908, Sept., 1908 to Feb., 1909, and from Oct., 1911 to Apr., 1912; adjt. and staff offr., local forces, Oct., 1914 to Aug., 1919; capt., B. Guiana Militia, Oct., 1914; major, Aug., 1919; ag. deputy inspr.-gen. and supt. of Georgetown fire brig., July to Oct., 1914; ag. deputy inspr.-gen. on various occasions; dep. inspr.-gen. pol. and supt. fire brigade, Aug., 1919; comsnr. pol., Sierra Leone, June, 1921.

KING, SIR FREDERICK TRUBY, Kt. Bach. (1925), C.M.G. (1917), M.B., B.Sc.—Founder of New Zealand Plunket Soc'y, and famous for his work in connection with infant-life protection; dir. of divn. of child-welfare, N.Z. health dept., 1921; inspr.-gen., mental hosps., 1926-27.

KING, GEORGE HOWARD, I.S.O. (1932).—Ag. clk., treas., St. Kitts, 1894; ag. rev. offr., Nevis, 1895; clk., P.O., St. Kitts, 1896; ag. clk., regir.'s office, Nevis, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, Jan., 1898; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1899; rev. offr., Nevis, June, 1905; 1st clk., P.O., and sub-inspr. of schls., St. Kitts, 1906; ag. postmtr., St. Kitts, 1914; postmtr., May, 1915.

KING, JOHN EDMUND.—B. 1900; entered C.O., Sept., 1916; served with R.N.V.R. from 30th Sept. to 19th Dec., 1918; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Apr., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 4th July, 1928.
KING, JOHN GEORGE MAYDON.—B. 1908; ed. Oundle Schl. and S. Eastern Agril. Coll., Wye, Kent; dipl. agr.; C.O. Agril. schol., Cambridge, 1929-30; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1931.
KING, LOUIS HERBERT, M.I.E.E.—B. 1880; chf. engrnr. and mannr., elec. wks., Dar-es-Salaam, 1919; chf. elec. engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

KING, PERCY WILLIAM.—B. 1891; ed. Seminary, Quebec and Mungret Coll., Ireland; passed final exam., Law Soc.; ag. offr., recur., pub. trustee and crown solr., Br. Guiana, in 1920, 1921 and 1922; offr. recur., pub. trustee and crown solr., 1923; ag. ast. col. sec. in 1932.

KING, SYDNEY NOEL.—B. 1897; ed. Dover Coll. and Bedford Schl.; served in R.F.A., 1916-1920 (coman., Aug., 1916); cadet, S.S., Mar., 1920; passed cadet, Mar., 1922; offr., cls. V., Mar., 1923; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1927; ast. advr., Kukub, July, 1928; ag. 1st mag., Johore Bahru, Sept., 1928; dep. pres., town bd., in addn., Oct., 1928; dep. pub. pros., Johore, Nov., 1929; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1932; 2nd astt. sec. (A) S.S., Aug., 1933.

KING, STUART EDGAR.—B. 1898; ed. Merchant Taylor's Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S., July, 1921; astd. Chinese prot., S'pore, Aug., 1921; offr., cls. V., July, 1924; astt. prot., Chinese and astt. contribr., lab., S'pore, Apr., 1926; offr., cls. IV., astt. prot., Chinese, Perak, July, 1928; ditto, Malacca, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. III., July, 1933; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, Sept., 1933.

KING, THOMAS HENRY.—B. 1883; ed. at Exeter Gram. Schl.; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1904; passed in Cantonese, 1907; attched. to Punjab pol., 1907; passed in Hindustani, 1907; ag. astt. supt. of pol., 1908; J.P., 1908; ag.-supt., impts. and expts., Dec., 1909; astt. emigrn. offr. in addition to pol. duties, 1910; passed in Punjabi, 1910; ag. astt.-supt., pol., 1911; mem., bd. of exams., 1912; astt. supt., pol., 1912; ag. dep. supt., pol. and fire brig., 1913; dep. supt., pol., 1921; ag. capt. supt., pol., 1921 and June-July, 1925; dir., crim. intellig., 1923; ag. capt. supt., pol., Mar. to Nov., 1929; dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Sept., 1930; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., Mar.-Oct., 1932.

KING, WILLIAM CYRIL CAMPBELL.—B. 1891; ed. Cranbrook schl.; probr., Ceylon pol., 1912; astt. supt., pol., 1914; on active serv., 1917-19; supt., pol., 1921; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., pol., 1931-32; ch. commndt., pol. and inspr., prisons, Cyprus, Aug., 1933.

KING, Rt. Hon. WILLIAM LYON MACKENZIE, P.C. (1922), C.M.G. (1906), M.A., Toronto Univ., LL.B., Toronto Univ., Ph.D., Harvard Univ.—B. 1874; apptd. dep. min. of labour and editor of "Labour Gazette," 1902; regisr. of boards of conciliation and investigation, 1907; sworn of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of labour, 1909; defeated at g.e., 1911; special investigator, Rockefeller Foundation, 1914; chosen leader of the opposition, H. of C., Aug., 1919; elec. by acclamation for Prince Co., P.E.I., 20th Oct., 1919; re-elec. for N. York, Ont., g.e., Dec., 1921; prime minister, Dominion of Canada, 29th Dec., 1921, also pres. of the coun. and sec. of state for external affairs; represented Canada at Imp. Confce., 1923; prime min., 1926-30; leader of opposition, 1930.

KINGDON, SIR DONALD, Kt. Bach. (1931), K.C., M.A., LL.B.—Ed. at Eastbourne Coll. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, In. Tem., July, 1905; legal ass't. and inspr. of schls., Gambia, Jan., 1907; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; as col. sec., Mar. to July, 1912; atty.-gen., Uganda, Oct., 1912; ag. chief just., Apr., 1917, to July, 1918; atty.-gen., G. Coast, Sept., 1918; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1919; compiler of 1920 revd. edn., G. Coast laws, 1920; atty. gen., Nigeria, Apr., 1921; mem., exec. and legis. couns.; compiler, revd. edn., Nigeria laws, 1923; ag. ch. sec. to govt., Oct., 1928 to Feb., 1929; ch. just., Sept., 1929.

KING-HARMAN, SIR CHARLES ANTHONY, K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1851; ed. Chelt. Coll.; B.A. of Trin. Coll., Camb., 1872; M.A. 1879; priv. sec. to the gov. of the Bahamas, Nov., 1874, to Nov., 1879; priv. sec. to high comsnr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. comsnr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. to the ch. sec., Cyprus, Mar., 1881; ag. ch. sec. and mem. of exec. and legis. couns., June to Sept., 1882; auditor-gen., Barbados, 1883; elected mem. of house of assem., 1884-93; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. coun., 1884, 1885, and 1886; col. sec., Mauritius, 1893; ag. gov., Jan. to Aug., 1894, and Mar. to Sept., 1896; adminstr., St. Lucia, 1897; gov. S. Leone, Oct., 1900; high comsnr., Cyprus, 15th Apr., 1904; represented Mediterranean Colonies at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

KINGHORN, ALLAN.—B. 1879; ed. Toronto Grammar Schl., W. Toronto Collegiate Inst. and Univ. of Toronto, M.B. hons., 1904; M.C.P. & S., Ont., 1905; M.D., Toronto, 1929; trypanosomiasis investig., N. Rhodesia, 1907-09; apl. serv. med. offr., Gold Coast, 1909-11; in ch., Luangwa sleeping sickness comsnr., N. Rhodesia, 1911-12; med. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1912; war serv., 1914-17 (ment. in desp.); seconded, plague investig., Nyasaland, 1917; anti-plague measures, Luangwa valley, N. Rhodesia, 1917; senr. med. offr., 1931; dep. dir., med. services, 1932; dir., med. services, 1933; publications, "Bionomics Glomina Morsitans, 1911"; "Report of Luangwa S.S. Commission (collected papers), 1912"; "Human Trypanosomiasis in Luangwa Valley," 1925.

KINGSBURY, ALLAN NEAVE, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—Asst. pathologist, Middlesex Hosp. and bacteriologist, Middlesex Hosp., ven. diseases clinic; pathologist, City of Lond. Mat. Hosp.; lieut., R.F.A.; ag. govt. pathologist, Penang, 1923; pathologist, Inst. for Med. Research, F.M.S., 1924; on sp. duty in India and Ceylon, 1924; ag. bacteriologist, Inst. for Med. Research and ag. dir., govt. laboratories, F.M.S., 1925; professor of bacteriology, King Ed. VII. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, 1926; bacteriologist, Inst. Med. Research, F.M.S., Apr., 1927; ag. dir., ditto, Aug., 1927; dir., ditto, Oct., 1927; awarded N. Persian Forces memorial med. for research, Oct., 1927; del. L. of N. health organization laboratory confce., Copenhagen, May 1928; rep. C.O. at Internat. Cancer Confce., London, July, 1928; rep. F.M.S. at Leonard Wood memorial leprosy confce., Manila, Dec., 1930; author of many medical and scientific publications.
KINLOCH, JOHN BAXTER.—B. 1906; ed. Kirkcaldy High Schl. and Edin. and Oxford Univs.; B.Sc., forestry, Edin., 1927; asst. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1927.

KINNEAR, GEORGE, M.D. (Univ. of Manitoba), M.C.P.S. (Sask.), Certif. L.S.T.M. and H.—B.1894; ed. Regina Collegiate Inst., 1st certif., Regina Normal Schol., 1912; priv. Ear Grey and Govan. Pub. Schls. (Sask.), 1912-17; Leeward Is. med. serv., 1926; D.M.O., dist. B., Dominica; M.O., Bahamas gen. hosp., 1929; M.O. and bacteriologist, Bahamas gen. hosp., 1931; ag. ch. med. offr., 1932.
KIPPING, RUPERT HARRY, M.B., Ch.B. (Leeds).—B. 1904; late jun. house surg., Dartington Genl. Hosp.; locum house surg., Shepshed Roy. Hosp.; med. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1929.
KIRBY, A. C.—Asst. dist. comsnr., F.A.P., Dec., 1913; 2nd grade res., Nyasaland, Jan., 1925; 1st grade res., Mar., 1927; ag. p.r. comsnr., June, 1933.

KIRK, JAMES BALFOUR.—B. 1893; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edin. Univ., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Oxford), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), F.Z.S. (Lond. and Scot.), Vans Dunlop scholarship bot. sci., Edin. Univ., 1911, certif. (with distinc.) L.S.T.M., 1922; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914-16; lieut., R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1917; capt., 1918; med. offr., Jul., 1918-19; civ. surg., Arabia, 1919; asst. M.O., Baghdad, 1919-20; bacteriologist, R. Inst. Pub. Health, 1920-21; demobilized, L.S.T.M., 1921; M.O.H., Mauritius, 1922; med. and health dept., 1927; author of "Public Health Practice in the Tropics" (1931), "Health on Equipment and Health for intending residents in the Tropics," and several articles on med. and hygienic subjects.

KIRKHAM, G. H.—B. 1881; S. Afr. Army constab., 1901; served S. African War, 1899-1902 (wounded); Transvaal pol., 1908; adjt., 4th S. African Mounted Riflemen, 1914; S. African Rebellion, 1914; capt., 1st S. African Inf. (Overseas Brig.) 1916-18; German W. Africa campaign, 1914-16; ment. in desp., Frere, 1916-17 (twice wounded); M.C., Apr., 1917; King's pol. med.; dep. comsnr., pol. med., Tanganyika Territory, 1918; comsnr., 1919.

KIRKPATRICK, THOMAS WINIFRED, M.A. (Cantab.), Dipl. Agr. (Cantab.), F.E.S.—B. 1876; ed. Marlborough and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; mil. serv., 1915-19; entomologist, min. of agr., Egypt, 1921; do., Kenya Col., 1925; entomologist, Welcome Trop. Resear. Labs., Sudan, 1928; entomologist, E. Amani agr. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1931.

KIRSOOPP, GEORGE DOUGAL.—Asst. agr. cust., E.A.Prof., Jan., 1914; collr., cust., Keru Col., Jan., 1920; comprtr., cust., Zanzibar, May, 1923; ag. treas. for various periods, 1923-31; treas. and comprtr., cust., Jan., 1932.
KIRWAN, ARTHUR CONINGSBY—Jun. col. sec.'s off., Falkland Is., Jan., 1907.
KIRWAN, ST. JOHN WATERA, Kt. (1930).—B.1869; M.L.C., S. Prov., W. Indies. since 1908; chmn. of comtees., 1923-26; pres., leg. coun., 1926.

KITCHING, A. E.—Ed. Oundle Schi. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1919; dist. offr., June, 1926; ag. asst. sec., native affrs., Apr., 1929; asst. sec., native affrs., 1930; ag. sec., native affrs., Apr., to Dec., 1931; dep. prov. commr., Apr., 1932; prov. commr., 1934.

KITCHING, GEOFFREY CHARLES, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1892; ed. Oundle Schi. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; on mily. serv., 1914-19; asst. polit. offr., Iraq, 1920; miny. of int., Iraq, 1920-30; dep. dir., development, Palestine, 1931; gov't. sec'y., St. Helena, 1932.

KITCHING, THOMAS, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1890; ed. Lancaster and Peterhouse, Camb.; survey prob., F.M.S., May, 1913; asst. supt., rev. surveys, N. Sembilan, 1919; Perak, 1921; Selangor, 1921; supt., survey dept., F.M.S. and S.S., 1930; seconded to Trengganu as supt., surveys, 1928-31; officiating asst. survr.gen., F.M.S. and S.S., 1932, Dec., 1931 and Sept., 1932; ag. dep. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., June and Dec., 1932.

KITTERM MASTER, SIR HAROLD BAXTER, K.B.E. (1928), C.M.G. (1926), O.B.E. (1918).—Ed. Shrewsbury and Christ Ch., Oxford; Transvaal educn. dept., 1902-07; asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; dist. commr., 1915; offr. in charge, N.F.D., 1916; sec'y., to admstr., Somaliland, Aug., 1921; gov't. and commdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., 1926; gov't. and commdr.-in-ch., Br. Honduras, 1931.

KNAPMAN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—S.A. War; Transvaal civ. serv., 1900; ch. clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1909; office supt., secretariat, E.A.P., 1911; lieut., K.A.R., 1917-19; estab. offr., secretariat, Kenya, 1923; Queen's S.A. med., Br. War and Victory meds.

KNIBBS, STANLEY GEORGE CURTHOYS, F.R.G.S.—B. 1886; ed. Sydney Gram. Schi., New South Wales; dipl. in all subjects, Hawkesbury Agric. Coll., 1908; land survr., Fiji, 1911; land survr., Victoria and N.S.W., 1912; crown survr., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1913; ag. supt., pub. wks. in addn., 1918; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands during 1919; regis. of land titles in addn., 1919; dep. commr. for W. Pacific, 1919; dep. for natives in lands comm., 1920 and 1921; ag. res. commr. during 1920; commr. of currency (temp.), 1920-24; qualified as civ. engr. to shires and municipalities of N.S.W., 1923; commr. of lands and crown survr., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1924; chmn., mining bd., 1927; attended Imp. Survey Conf., and Internat. Geog. Conf., 1928.

KNIGHT, JOHN MARCUS, M.B.E. (1927).—B. 1894; ed. Grenada Grammar Schi.; 5th clk., treasy., Grenada, 1912; 2nd clk., G.P.O., 1913; served in the Great War, 1914-19; Egypt, 1916; Palestine, 1917; twice ment. in desps.; rev. and cust. offr., Grenada, 1919; 2nd clk., secretariat and clk., leg. coun., 1921; ch. clk., gov't.'s office, Windward Is. and clk., ex. coun., Grenada, 1925; sec'y. to gov't. of Windward Is., 1928.

KNIGHT, WALTER AUGUSTUS.—Copyist, G.P.O., Grenada, May, 1903; 2nd clk., G.P.O., June, 1904; clk., supt. of wks., Jan., 1906; clk., pol. mag., S. Dist., May, 1907; rev. offr. and road survr., W. Dist., Jan., 1909; addtnl. coroner, St. Mark's parish, Jan., 1913; road survr., N. Dist., Dominica, Dec., 1914.

KNIGHT-BRUCE, G. K.—B. 1891; ed. Radley Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; B.A. (2nd ols. hona., schol. of jurisprudence), 1913; asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ment. in desps., 1919; ag. dist. commr., 1920; ag. res. mag., Mombasa, 1923; res. mag., Uganda, 1925; res. mag., Zanzibar, 1925; ag. judge, Zanzibar, 1926; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1927; ag. judge, Zanzibar, for various periods, 1927-33; ag. atty.-gen., 1930 and 1933.

KNOLLYS, LANCELOT ERskINE.—B. 1885; ed. Dover Coll. and S.E. Agric. Coll., Wye, Kent (agric.); asst. supt., cotton cult., Uganda, 1909; asst. dist. commr., 1914; 1st grade admin. offr., 1925; senr. asst. sec'y., 1931; asst. ch. sec'y., Dec., 1932.

KNOWLES, CHARLES HENRY, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1878; ed. Burton-on-Trent gram. schi. and Mason Univ. Coll., Birmingham; res. master, agric. schi., St. Vincent, Jan., 1902; supt. of agric., Fiji, 1905; M.L.C., 1912; chmn., agric. experiments comm., 1916; chmn. food supply comm., 1917; ag. gov't. entomologist (conjoint), 1917 and 1919-21; J.P., 1917; M.E.C. (temp.), June, 1920; prov. supt., agric., G. Coast, May, 1921; dep. dir., agr., Aug., 1924; dir., agr., Dec., 1924.

KNOWLES, JOHN WILLIAM FREDERICK, M.B.E. (1934).—B. 1877; joined Trinidad gov't. serv., 1898; warden, counties Nariva-Mayaro, June, 1922; warden, St. Andrew and St. David, Dec., 1925; do., St. Patrick-Victoria, May, 1933

KOTZE, HON. SIR JOHN GILBERT, KT. BACH. (1917), K.C., LL.B. (LOND.).—B. 1849; ed. South African Coll. and Lond. Univ.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1874; sole judge of the high court of the Transvaal, 1877 to 1881; one of the commrs. under the convention of Pretoria to settle claims for losses and injuries suffered during the Boer War of 1890-81; chief justice of the Transvaal, 1881-1898; chmn. of bd. of examrs. in literature and science, Transvaal, 1890-1898; recd. the Portuguese honour of Knight Grand Cross of the Order of the Conception, 1896; atty.-gen. and member of exec. and legis. couns., S. Rhodesia, 1900; ag. admstr. S. Rhodesia, 1902; judge of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope, 1903, and judge pres., Eastern dista., 1904; chmn. of the coun. of Rhodes Univ. Coll.; examr. in law and jurisprudence to the Univ. of C. of G. H., 1903-1908; puisne judge, C. of G.H. Prov. Div., 1st Apr., 1913; judge, president, 14th June, 1920; judge of appeal, appellate divn., June, 1922; ret., 1932.

KOTZE, PETER CANZIUS, B.A. (Cape). Dip. For. (Oxon.).—Aast. forest offr., King William's Town, 1911; ditto, Port Elizabeth, 1912; ditto, Concordia, 1914; forest offr., Storma River, 1918; ditto, Eshowe, 1920; ditto, Kokstad, 1927; ditto, Bloemfontein, 1929; conservator, forests, Midland conservancy, 1931; forestry inspr., Midland inspectorate, 1932.

KOTZE, SIR ROBT. NEILSON, KT. (BACH.), B.A.—B. 1870; ed. at S. Africa Coll., and R. Schl. of Mines, Clausthal, Germany; gold med., S.A. Coll. and of Chem. Met. and Mining Soc. of S.A.; asst. engnr. and consulting engnr. to Transvaal Gold Fields Ltd., and subsidiary coys., 1896-1907; govt. mining engnr., Transvaal, Jan., 1908; ditto, Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chan., Univ. of Witwatersrand; ret., Nov., 1926.

KRAUSE, FREDERICK EDWARD TRAUGOTT.—B. 1868; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Amsterdam, London, Cambridge Univ.; B.A. (Hons.), Cape, 1888; LL.D., Amsterdam, 1893; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1893; LL.D., Cape, 1905; advoc., sup. ct., S. Africa and high ct., Rhodesia; state prosecutor and ag. atty.-gen., Witwatersrand, 1896-1900; sp. commdt. and mil. gov., Witwatersrand, 1900; chmn., mining regs. comsn., 1907-9; mem., Johannesburg town coun., 1905-9; M.L.A., Vrededorp, 1907-10; chmn., hosp. inquiry coman., Johannesburg; mem., prop. representation coman.; mem., Witwatersrand cent. schl. bd.; K.C., (Union of S. Afric), 1912; ag. judge, Transvaal prov. divn., 1914; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., Apr., 1923; judge-pres., O.F.S. prov. divn., 1933.

KRUGER, JOHANNES JAACOB.—B. 1888; on active serv., E. Africa and overseas, 1915-29; govt. printer, 1927; Union R. of O. with rank of major; mem., Union tender bd. and govt. rep., coun., Pretoria tech. coll.

KUIT, ALBERT.—S. Africa Repub. govt. and Z.A.S.M. rly. serv. till Br. annexation; served Jameson raid and Boer war on Boer side; finan. and estate agt., Amersfoort (T'vaal) town coun., J.P. and practically all public bodies, 1902-25; rly. comanr. since 1925; pub. debt comanr. since 1928; a comanr. of oaths for Union.

KUSCHKE, HERMANN F. G., B.A.—B. 1891; ed., Johannesburg and Pretoria; head, normal sec., Stoiberg Gedenk Schl., 1916-24; organising instr., native educn., O.F.S., 1924; ch. instr., native educn., O.F.S., 1926.

LABORDE, EDWARD DANIEL, C.B.E. (1922), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1863; 1st clk., audit office, Tobago, Jan., 1882; dep. reg., ag. confidential clk. to administrator, and clk. of couns., June, 1892; 2nd clk., adminstr.'s office, St. Lucia, Feb., 1883; ch. clk., Sept., 1883; ag. 2nd clk., gov.in-chief's office, Barbados, 1884; ch. clk. to gov., Windward Is., Grenada, 1885; priv. sec. to Gov. Sendall, 1886; ch. of pol. and exoise, St. Vincent, 1889; instr. of prisons, 1889; supervisor of cust. and port offr., 1897; in charge of relief work after hurricane of 1898; specially mentioned in despatches and thanked by S. of S. for services; chmn. town bd., 1899-1901; mem. ex. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to May, 1901; ch. of pol. and instr. of prisons, St. Lucia, 1902; has served on several comans. of enquiry in different colonies; ag. admistr., St. Vincent, June to Dec., 1904; ag. admistr., and col. sec., St. Lucia, July to Nov., 1905; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1906, to Jan., 1907; ag. administrator. and col. sec., May to Nov. 1907; treas., St. Lucia, 1912; col. treas., Grenada, 1915; ag. col. sec., Grenada, for various periods, 1915-20; admstr., govt., Sept.-Oct., 1915, and Sept. to Oct., 1920.

LACEY, MAJOR ALFRED TRAVERS.—B. 1892; ed., King Edward VI Gram. Schl., Norwich and King's Coll., Cambridge; class. tripods; hona. degree, M.A.; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; dir., educn., Nyassaland Prot., 1930.

LAFERLA, CAPT. ALBERT V., LL.D.—B. 1887; ed., Flores's Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1903; grad. in law, 1910; lieut., King's Own Malta Regt. of Militia, 1903; capt., 1911; demob., 1920; dir., elem. schls., Malta, Aug., 1920.
LA FONTAINE, SYDNEY HUBERT, O.B.E. (1930), D.S.O., M.C. (1916).—Aast. dist. comanr., E.A.P., 1910; district comanr., Kenya, Oct., 1918; 2nd grade prov. comanr., 1931; ag. ch. native comanr., 1933.

LAGDEN, SIR GODFREY YEATMAN, K.C.M.G. (1897), K.B.E. (1927), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1851; clerk, G.P.O., London, 1869-77; after compet. exam.; clk. to sec. to govt., Transvaal, 1878; priv. sec. to admistr., 1878-81; sec. to exec. coun., 1880-81; priv. sec. to Sir W. Bellairs and Sir E. Wood, while adminstr., 1881; sec. to Transvaal sub-comanr., 1881-82; engaged in compensation claims, 1881-82; engaged in respondent, Egyptian campaign, 1882-3; at all engagements, including Tel-el-Kebir capture of Cairo; on special financial coast, 1883; sec. and acctnt., Basuto land ast. comanr., 1885; established magaz., Masupha's country, 1886; acted as res. May to Nov., 1890; ag. Br. cement. sec. govt. comanr. of adminstr., Aug., 1891; comanr., Basutoland, 1893-94; comanr., native affairs, Transvaal, Aug., 1901; mem. leg. couns.; chmn. of S. Afric. nat. affs. comanr., 1903-4; retired.

LAING, GORDON DACOMBE, B.Sc., M.B. (St. And.), D.P.H. (Johannesburg)—B. 1892; Merchiston Schl., Pietermaritzburg; Macmillan Schl., St. Andrews, and St. Andrews' Univ.; in anatomy, Univ. of Witwatersrand; pathologist, S. African Inst. for Med. Res., 1927; asst. health offr., for Union of S. Africa, 1928; med. offr., health, Pretoria, 1929; published some papers on anaemic anthropological subjects in the Journa. S.African Assocn. for the Advancement of Science.

LAING, MALCOLM BUCHANAN.—B. 1894; Forest Schl., Essex; asst. commissary, Oct., 1909; seconded as ch. clk., immr. Apr., 1913; mem., govt. standing bd., in Hindi, etc., Nov., 1913; immigr. att., 1920; capt. B. Guiana Arty. (Mil.) senr. clk., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1921; exec. coun. and leg. coun., July, 1928; to gov., Apr., 1929 to June, 1930; dist. Jan., 1932.
LAKE, FREDERICK JAMES.—B. 1894; clk., G.P.O., 1912; B.E.F., France, 1915; German E. Africa, 1917; exec. offr., 1920; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika dist. offr., 1923; dist. offr., 1932.

LAKE, MORICE CHALLONER.—B. 1895; Wellington Coll. and Sandhurst; unattd. list (attd. 1st Batt. Somer. Infy.), Jan., 1904; T.A., 1905; regt. int., 15; Aden, 1913; served, Aden Field Force, 19; offr. in ch. signals, 1915-16; offr. signal schl., Lucknow, 1916-17; commanded Aden (Arab) Labour Corps raised and commanded 1st Yemen Infy. sp. duty, W.O. revising mily. rept., 1926-27; reverted, mily. duty, India adviser to C.O. and Air Mins. on raising, etc. in Aden, 1928; polit. offr., Aden, 1928; ag. 2nd asst. res. and Prot. sec., Nov., 1930.
LAMACQ, FRANK CONSTANT.—B. 1895; cler., offr., London telephone service, transf'd to C.O., July, 1923.
LAMB, F. M.—Senior staff surr., L. May, 1907; asst. dist. comanr., Uganda, Feb., 1910; asst. dist. comanr., E.A.P., 1911; dist. comanr., Jan., 1918.
LAMBERT, CHARLES DOUGLAS, M.I.L.M., M.I.Loco. E., M.I.Transport.—B. 1883; engr., Kowloon Canton rly., 1910.

LAMBERT, C. E.—B. 1900; on c. May, 1918 to Nov., 1919; temp. clk., est. 1919 to Jan., 1920; R.I.C., Feb., 1920; temp. clk., Min. of Lab., Apr., 1921; temp. clk., Min. of Lab., Apr., 1921; apptd. after compet. exec. offr., C.O., 9th July, 1923; seconded to staff of high comanr. in Canada for H.M. in Great Britain, 1923-32; returned to C.O., Sept., 1932.

LAMBERT, Sir Henry Charles Miller, K.C.M.G. (1919); C.B. (1910).—B. 1868; ed. at Eton Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; 1st class in classical mods., 1889; 1st class in final classical schls., 1891; apptd. 2nd class clk. in the C.O. after open compet. exam., Feb., 1892; mem. of comtee., emigts.' information office, 1894; chmn. of the comtee., 1897-1907; acted as priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain for the purposes of the comtee. of the H. of C. on South Africa, 1897; 1st class clk., Nov., 1898; visited Canada, 1903; visited Australia, on behalf of E. I. O., in connection with emigration questions, 1905; prin. clk., Jan., 1907; acting officer, C.O.; asst. under-sec. of state, 4th June, 1916; sec. to the Imp. Confe., senr. crown agt. for the cols., 1921-32; ag. under sec. of state for the cols., 30th July, 1924 to 4th Aug., 1925.
LATHAM, D. V., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.)—B. 1897; war serv., R.N.V.R., 1917-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925.

LATHAM, Rt.-Hon. John Gregg, P.C., C.M.G., K.C., M.A., LL.B., M.P.—B. 1877; ed. State Schls., Scotch Coll., Melbourne and Melbourne Univ.; called to bar, 1904; formerly lecturer, Melbourne Univ.; during European War was lieut.-commdr., Intelligence Branch; attended, with min. for navy, Imp. Conf., 1918; assist. sec., Br. Empire delegation, Peace Conf., Paris, 1919; Br. sec. to inter-allied comms. on Czechoslovak affs. at conf., M.H.R. (C'wealth) for Kooyong (Vic.); atty.-gen., C. of A., Dec., 1925; a rep. of C'wealth at Imp. Conf., 1926; leader of opposition H. of R., 1929; dep. leader, 1930; atty.-gen. and min. of external affs. and industry in Lyons' ministry, 1932.

LAUCHLAN, STUART, B.Sc. (FORESTRY).—B. 1891; ed. Royal High Schl., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; ast conserv., forests, Nigeria, 1914; conserv., forests, 2nd grade, 1918; senr. conserv., forests, 1927.
LAURENCE, C. G. W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compt. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

LAVILLE, LOUIS VICTOR JOSEPH.—B. 1888; ed. at Bedford Schl., and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb. (schol.), 2nd cls. class. and hist. tripes, B.A., Camb.; cadet, S. Stltms., Dec., 1912; attd. to col. secy.'s office, Jan., 1913; passed cadet, Jan., 1916; supv. officer, cls. V., Jan., 1916; lent to govt. of Kelantan, June, 1917; offg. dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, Jan., 1918; supv. offr., cls. IV, Dec., 1919; offg. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, July, 1920; offr. coll., income tax, Singapore, 3rd Apr., 1922; mag. and astt. regir., sup. ct., Seremban, Dec., 1923; dep. pub. pros., Perak, July, 1925; sheriff and dep. regir., sup. ct., Spore, Nov., 1928; 1st mag. and pres., town bd., Johore Bahru, Mar. and Oct., 1929; cls. II, Dec., 1929; offg. regir., sup. ct., Penang, Aug., 1931; ag. legal advr. and judl. comms., Kelantan, June, 1932; ag. dist. offr., P. Wellesley, Mar., 1933.

LAW, CHARLES EWAN.—B. 1884; ed. High Schl., Croydon and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; Campbell-Foster prizeman, criminal law, Middle Temple, 1903; called to bar, 1906; Burma bar, 1908; staff capt. (tempy. Brit. serv.), Burma divn., Mar., 1917; Volunteer Long Service med. (India), Feb., 1919; maj. (temp. ind. army res., of offrs.) comdg. 15th Mech. Transport Coy., July, 1919; released from serv., rank capt., I.A.R.O., Feb., 1920; crown counsel, Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. solr.-gen., Mar., 1923 to Oct., 1924; mem. legis. coun., May, 1923 to Oct., 1924; ag. atty.-gen. and mem. exec. coun., Apr., 1924; Swahili (L.S.), Sept., 1923; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1925; ag. judge, Kingston ct., on various occasions, 1925 to 1927; judge, Kingston ct., E.I., 1927; ag. puisne judge, on various occasions 2nd gt-h. 1927 to 1929; mem., standing comms., L. mag., June, 1926; mem., comms. of LAGDEN, admstr. of prisons, Sept., 1926; (1897), K.B.E Uganda, Oct., 1930; mem., ct. of clerk, G.P.O., in Africa; ag. ch. just., from May, exam.; clk. to 33; chan. of diocese, Jan., 1933; priv. sec. to adm. enquiry into admstr. of just. coun., 1880-81; pr. E. Africa, Jan., 1933; ch. LAW, Y. P., B.A. (Cantab.)—R. 1895; mast., educ. dept., Hong Kong, 1915; ag. of vernac. schls., 1915 and 1917; mem. of exam., 1919-22; inspr., vernac. schls., 1921; hon. sec., bd. of educ., in add. inspr., vernac. schls., New Territories, apptd. mem., bd. of exams., 1934; ag. of vernac. schls. for urban districts, 1925; inspr., vernac. schls., 1927.

LAWLEY, HON. Sir ARTHUR G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E. (1906); K.C.M.G. (1901); sec. to adminstr., S. Rhodesia, Matabeleland, N.W. adminstr., Matabeleland, Dec., 1906; adminstr., Mashonaland, July, 1899; W. Aust., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., Transvaal Aug., 1902; ag. gov. of Transvaal and high commsnr., 1903; chmn. of pres. of leg. coun., mem. L.C.C.; gov. of N. Rhodesia, 1906.
LAWLOR, JOHN HENRY, B.A.—R. 1895; mast., Pietermaritzburg Coll., Feb., 1896; inspr., Natal, May, 1910; inspr., Apr., 1912; ch. inspr., schls., Aug., 1917.

LAWRANCE, MAJOR SIR ARTHUR BURTON, K.B.E. (1934), C.M.G. (1931); Major, late reserve of officers; ser. I.Y. in S. Africa, 1900-1902 (Queen's medal with 3 clasps, King's medal with clasp, in desps.); served with 6th K.A.R., Somaliland, 1905-1911 (Africa Serv. medal with clasp "Somaliland service," in desps.); astt. res., N. Nigeria, Feb., 1911; dist. commsnr., Somaliland, 1914; comdt., Somaliland Camel Corps, 1913; operas. against Mullah, 1915; in desps.; A.G.S. med. with clasp; ag. Harar, 1915 and 1919; operas. against Mullah, 1920; ment. in desps., D.S.O.; adm. on various occasions, 1921, 1925; govt., 1926; commsnr. and commdr.-in-ch.

LAWRENCE, MAJOR CHARLES O.B.E. (1926)—B. 1879; ed. St. David's and at Shrewsbury Schl.; N. Nigeria Res.; A.D.C. to Gov., S. Nigeria, 1905; sec., S. Nigeria, 1910; ag. astt. prov. Cent. Prov., 1912; prte. sec. to govt. and 1915; commanded Nigeria Land Cts., 15; lat astt. sec., S. Provs., 1916; atty.-Regt., 1917-19; sec., petroleum pool bd., ag. sec., S. Provs., 1919, 1920 and 1921; astt. sec., 1921; commsnr., Br. Empire, 1923; ag. lieut.-gov., S. Provs., 1923; grade, 1928; admstr. of col., 1930.

LAWSON, Hon. H.S.W.—B. 1873; M.J. Castlemaine, Victoria, 1899; sat com. in State Parlmt. from 1899; commsnr. of survey, Dec., 1913 to Nov., 1915; astt. atty.-gen., solr.-gen. and min. of public premier and atty.-gen. 1918; premier of agr., 1920-24; now C'wealth and Victoria.
LAWSON, R. A.—Astt. traffic mnr., rly., Apr., 1919.

LEA, LESLIE.—Ed. privately and U. Schl., Gray's Inn, 1922; clk. astt., fed. E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1908; conf. clk. for lands, 1909; conveyancing clk., 1910; ag. conveyancer, 1913; astt. regis., 1916; musketry instr., K.A.R.; thanks G.O.C.L. (C.); regis. of un. dep. recorder of titles, 1919; ag. prin. crownlands, 1920; dep. regir.-gen., title controlling rev. authority, 1920-21; viser, etc., St. Helena, Aug., 1923; col. lem., bl. of commnr., crown property, 923; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, 1925; for preparation of new edn. of laws, 1925.
WALTER ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1883; Carpenters' Company's Coll. and West ech. Inst.; dist. engr., Br. Guiana, 1914; exec. engr., P.W.D., Tanganyika y., 1929.
BETTER, SIDNEY.—B. 1902; apptd. ipt. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

ITT, CLYDE.—B. 1878; ed., High Schi., Mich., Univ. of Mich. (B.A., 1901; or., 1904); ent. U.S. forest serv. as student 03; apptd. forest astt., 1904, with succ. pts. as ch. of boundaries, astt. ch., office utilization, ch., office of organization, dist. and astt. forester; ch. forester, comnr., bd. of rly. commrs., Canada, 1916; fire inspr., bd. of rly. commrs., Canada; co. of American Foresters; successively, 1 sec., Can. Soc. of Forest Engrs.

IMERE-GUPPY, GARETH EVERARD.—4; entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, May, cond. for special serv. to water authority erage bd., 1904-1905; ag. inspr. of imigrts., 1910, 1912 to 1913; statistical clk. and col. secretariat, Trinidad, July, 1918; oom. tax comte., est. comte., tenders and civ. serv. comte.; J.P. for the col. bago; dep. sub-intendant crown lands, 20; sub-intend., crown lands, Jan., 1925.

IMERE-GUPPY, P. (Junn.).—Super. clk., audit off., Trinidad, 1889; 4th b.-treasy., San Fernando, Jan., 1892; waiter, customs, May, 1894; clk., assy., Mar., 1898; acted as warden, na, in 1902, 1904 and 1905; sub-rev. ter, collr. of cust. and harbr. mast., Oct., 1917; collected fresh-water fishes dad for British Museum.

MONTE, LOUIS.—B. 1881; mag., lesser moes, Mauritius, 1909; do., Mauritius intrigues, Oct., 1913; ag. mast. and regis., 1913-14; ag. prot. of immigrts. and poor snr., 1914; war serv. in France and Mace-916-19; food contr., May, 1920; puisne uly, 1932; ag. ch. judge, sup. ct., Oct., 1930.

BORR, CLAUD MARIUS.—Served in S. Constab., 1901-7 (S.A. war med.); Bech. police, from 1907; sub-insp. Bech. Prot. 1st Apr., 1914; astt. res. mag.; passed v. serv. lower law exam. and 2nd grade na exam.; 2nd lieut., gen. list, 1919; ag., 1921; res. mag., 1921; passed Cape v. higher law exam., 1923; ag. astt. res., 1930.

LEE, H. N., O.B.E. (Mily.), 1918.—Ed. Portsmouth Gram. Schi. and King's Coll. London; articles and exams., Inst. of Chart. Acts., 1906-11; col. audit dept., 1913; astt. audr., E.A.P., Jan., 1914; E.A. Expedy. Force, 1914-19 (twice ment. in deaps.); senr. astt. audr., Kenya, 1920; ag. audr., Kenya, 1923; temp. duty Somaliland Prot., 1924-26 and in 1928; ag. audr. Kenya, 1926-27; prin. astt. audr., Jan., 1928; audr., Zanzibar Prot., Nov., 1928; audr., Tangan-yika Territory, 1933.
LEE, JOHN HENRY BURKILL.—B. 1905; ed. Univ. Coll., Leicester; cadet, Hong Kong, civ. serv., Dec., 1928; astt. treas., Apr., 1931; astt. P.M.G., 1932.
LEE, LIONEL JAMES, A.M.I.M.E.—Ed. R.N.E. Coll., Keyham; astt. engrnr., marine dept., Nigeria, 1913; prin. engrnr., 1927; supt. engr., 1933.
LEE, HON. SIR W. H., K.C.M.G. (1922), KT. BACH. (1920).—M.H.A., Tasmania; premier, chief sec., and min. for educn., 1916-22.
LEEMBRUGGEN, EDWARD LESLIE.—B. 1893; regis. clk., Western Pacific high comm., 1917; corresp. clk., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., 1920; sec. to res. comm., 1922; sec. to gov't., 1931.

LEES, AUBREY TREVOR OSWALD.—B. 1899; ed. Repton and Royal Mily. Academy, Woolwich; ent. R. Arty., Jan., 1919; served with 2nd R.W. Fos., Ireland, June, 1920; and with Arab and Kurdish Levies, Iraq and Kurdistan, June, 1922; attd. air hd. qrs., 'Iraq (air staff intell., spec. serv.) June, 1924; adminstr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1926; pvte. sec. to Br. res. and ag. astt. sec., Nov., 1926; clk. of exec. and leg. couns. and edr. of gazette, Sept., 1927; temp. attd., C.O., 1928; admstrive offr., Palestine, 1929.

LEEE, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1932).—Ast. dist. commnr., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1906; transfld. to treasy. dept., 1907; acted as supervisor of customs, 1908, and as junior astt. sec. in 1911; served as finan. offr. Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1916; dep. treas., 1921; ag. treas., May to Dec., 1921 and June to Dec., 1923; treas., G. Coast, 1924; ag. col. sec., Sept.-Nov., 1925 and from Oct., 1926 to Mar., 1927; gov't. dep., on various occasions in 1926 and 1927; treas., Nigeria, Oct., 1928.

LEEE, CAPT. ERNEST BENJAMIN, O.B.E. (1930).—3rd Batt., E. Yorks Regt., res. of offrs.; served in I.Y. in S. African War, 1900-1901 (Queen's medal with 3 clasps); subaltern in G. Coast Regt., July, 1905; capt., Jan., 1908; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov'r. of G. Coast, Nov., 1910 to Feb., 1911; trav. commnr., Gambia, Nov., 1911; served with Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1915-16; mem., exec. and leg. couns., Nov., 1929; ag. col. sec., Nov., 1930 to Mar., 1931.

LEFEVRE, PHILIP LLEWELLIN.—B. 1875; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Brighton Coll., Sussex; clk., G.P.O., Cape Town, Feb., 1895; clk., atty.-gen.'s off., Feb., 1896; clk. to res. mag., Glen Grey, Feb., 1896; clk. to res. mag., East London, Sept., 1898; clk., sol.-gen.'s off., Grahamstown, Apr., 1899; clk., atty.-gen.'s off. (treason branch), Cape Town, Sept., 1900; B.A., Cape Univ., 1900; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., O.R.C., June, 1901; sec. to law dept., Oct., 1904; ag. clk. to legis. coun., May-July, 1906; relieving mag., 1912; mag., Thaba'Nchu, May, 1917; mag., Ladybrand, Feb., 1925; mag., Grahamstown, Feb., 1929; joint editor of law-book, O.R.C., 1900-1906.

LEGGE, CHARLES ARTHUR LIONEL.—B. 1879; clerical asst., B. Guiana, May, 1897; 6th class offr., cust., June, 1899; 5th class clk., pol. dept., Dec., 1900; lieut., B.G. Artillery, 1901; attached to No. 32 Coy. R.G.A., Portsmouth, July, 1904; cert. in depression range finding, July, 1904; Hythe musk. cert., Aug., 1904; sub-inspr. of pol. and excise, Grenada, Oct., 1904; promoted capt., B. Guiana Artillery, and seconded, Dec., 1904; mem. local health authority, St. Andrew's parish, Grenada, Jan., 1905; ag. ch. of pol., Grenada, from June, 1907.

LEGGE, CYRIL HENRY ETHELWOOD.—B. 1883; joined B. Guiana civ. serv., June, 1901; clk. in surg. gens. office, cent. bd. of health, cust. and atty-gens. office; commissary, May, 1906; ag. stip. mag., Mar., 1914 to Dec., 1916; stip. mag., 2nd grade, Dec., 1916; stip. mag., 1st grade, Mar., 1921; passed final bar exam., 1912; called to bar, Lincolns Inn, July, 1922; passed govt. Hindi exam.
LEICESTER, WILLIAM SAMUEL, B.A. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B.1880; dist. surgn., Pahang, Sept., 1907; med. offr., Kuantan, Jan., 1919.

LEIGH-CLARE, HARRY JAMES LEIGH.—B. 1891; on mily. serv., Jan., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Feb., 1920; addtl. Colombo kach., Mar., 1920; attd. office of food contrlr., Apr., 1920; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Mar., 1921; office asst. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Apr. and Sept., 1922; addnl. landing survr., cust. Colombo, Oct., 1924; 2nd landing survr., Nov., 1924; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Dec., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Dec., 1927; landing survr., cust. Mar., 1928; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Matara, Dec., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Matara, Feb., 1930; admstive, secy., judl. dept., Sept., 1932.

LEMAIRE, ERNEST JOSEPH.—B. 1874; ed. Sherbrooke (Que.), Coll.; ent. privy coun. office for Canada, Jan., 1894; priv. sec., prime min., 1904-11; post office dept., 1912-23; clk. of privy coun. for Canada, Aug., 1923.

LEMBERGER, MAURICE.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., 23rd Apr., 1918; on mily. serv., 26th Aug., 1918 to 27th Feb., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; trans. to P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; trans. to C.O., 1st Sept., 1923.

LEMPRIERE, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1898; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; enlisted K.R.R.C., 1916; 2nd lieut., Cheshire Regt., 1917; ag. capt., Schi. of Instrn., E.E.F., 1919-21; demob., 1921; compt. audit dept., Oct., 1921; asst. audtr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1921; senr. asst. audtr., Kenya, 1933.

LENTON, HENRY JOHN.—Cape teleg. serv., Sept., 1898; clk., staff branch, Jan., 1902; 1st cls. clk., ditto, Apr., 1912; senr. clk., ch. clk's office, Apr., 1918; prin. clk., ditto, Dec., 1919; 2nd grade, ch. clk., Apr., 1921; asst. sec., Nov., 1923; ag. P.M.G. May to Oct., 1924 and from July, 1925; P.M.G. and chmn., civ. adv. bd., Apr., 1926.

LEONARD, HUGH GOODWIN RUSSELL, M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1880; cadet, F.M.S., 1903; passed cadet, 1905; offr. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Nov., 1921; chmn., European unemployment comtee., Apr., 1922; attd., fedl. sect. for sp. duty, Sept., 1925; contr. rubber, S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1925; finan. comanr. and audr.-gen., Johore, Aug., 1926; offr. cls. 1A, finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S., June, 1927; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Mar., 1930; Br. res., Pahang, Apr., 1930; M.F.C.

LE ROUX, JONATHAN JOHAN DU PRÉ.—B. 1900; ed. B.H.S., Paarl, Univ. Capetown, Royal Coll., Edin.and Univ., Witwatersrand; L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), D.P.H. (Wit.), 1933; med. offr., leper inst., Pretoria, Nov., 1927; med. supt., Jan., 1933.

LESLIE, ALLAN WALLACE.—Ag. clk., Estcourt magistracy, Natal, 1889; 3rd cls. clk., Newcastle, Jan., 1890; clk. of ct. and Zulu interp. (2nd cls.), Alexandra, May, 1891; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1898; transf. to Newcastle, Jan., 1899; maj.-ndwandle, July, 1900; Krantzkop, Apr., 1904; Richmond, Oct., 1906; mag. for col. of Natal, Oct., 1906; ast. under-sec., Dec., 1917; ag. prin. under-sec., Apr. to Nov., 1909; ag. under-sec. for just., Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1911; chief clk., dept. of just., Apr., 1912; mag., Dundee, Nov., 1915; mag., Durban, May, 1920; judge, native high ct., Natal, Oct., 1920.
LESSELS, RAYMOND.—B. 1897; ast. engr., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, 1924; divnl. engnr., Apr., 1933.

LESTER, A. R., M.B., B.S. (Bomb.), F.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), D.P.H., D.T.M. and H. (Edin.)—B. 1890; Indian med. serv., 1917-22; mily. serv., India and Persia, 1918-22; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1924; med. offr. in ch., sp. sociological investg., Kahama, 1927-31.

LETHAM, CHARLES KINGSLEY, B.Sc. (agr.)—B. 1901; ed. S. African Coll. Schi., Capetown; B.Sc. (agr.), Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria; Univ. of S. Africa, 1922; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924.

LETHEM, GORDON JAMES, C.M.G. (1954)—B. 1886; ed. at Mill Hill Schi., Middlesex, and Edin. and Grenoble Unive.; M.A. and LL.B. (with distinction), Edin.; mem. of Lincoln's Inn; asst. rea., N. Nigeria, Mar., 1911; p.o., Aug., 1924; ag. sec., N. Provs., Apr. to Nov., 1927; ag. sec., native affrs., Nov. 1927 to Sept., 1928; staff grade, Mar., 1928; sec., N. Provs., Apr., 1929; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Seychelles, 1933.

LEWEY, MAJOR A. W.—Ed. St. Paul's and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; M.A. (Cantab.); Middlesex Regt., European War, 1914-18; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; aide-de-camp and priv. sec., Gambia; pol. mag., Gambia, Dec., 1929; ag. legal adviser, and prov. mem., exec. and leg. couns., June-Nov., 1930; crown coun., Kenya, 1932.

LEWIN, CLAUS JOHN, M.C., B.S.—B. 1898; ed. Sexey's Sch., Univ. Coll., Reading and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; on war serv., 1915-17; supt., agr., Nigeria, Dec., 1922; junr. botanist, 1923; sent. botanist, 1925; ch. agriculturist, N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1930; ag. sec., agr., Dec., 1932; dir., agr., 1934.

LEWIS, ALFRED DALE, B.A. (Cape), M.A. (Cantab.), M.I.M.E., M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1881; P.W.D., India, 1905-07; circle engnr., irrig. dept., Cape and Union of S. Africa, 1907-15; on mily. serv. as major, R.E., in France, Egypt, Mesopotamia, 1915-19; ag. dir. of irrig., 1920; dir. of irrigation, 1921; author of "Irrigation in America."

LEWIS, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1890; linotype operator, govt. printing press, Zanzibar, Nov., 1921; ag. govt. printer, Oct. 1922 to May, 1923; asst. govt. printer, Nov., 1923; ag. govt. printer for various periods, 1925-33.
LEWIS, ERNEST HARRY, C.B.E., C.A. (S.A.L.)—Ed. King's Coll., Lond.; on war ser., S.A.L., 1899-1900; 1st grade clk., audit dept., Transvaal.

LEWIS, JOHN WILLIAMSON, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. King's schl., Canterbury; Crystal Palace schl. of practical engng.; jr. asst. engr., constr. dept., F.M.S. rly., 1909; asst. engr., F.M.S. rly., 1912; grade III, 1913; senr. dist. engr., F.M.S. rly., Nov., 1923; ditto, Johore Bahru, May, 1930; do., head office, Mar., 1931; maintenance and new wks. engr., F.M.S. rly., Aug., 1932.

LEWIS, HON. SIR NELL ELLIOTT, K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1901), B.A.—B. 1858; atty.-gen. in Dobson-Hervy ministry, Tasmania, 1902-4; premier and atty.-gen. in Lewis ministry, 1900; joined 1st Commonwealth ministry as min. without portfolio, Jan., 1901; again premier of Tasmania, 1909-1912; treas., and min. for mines, Apl., 1916.

LEWIS, WILLIAM EDWIN.—B. 1886; ed. West Monmouthshire Grammar Schl.; traffic asst., Gold Coast rly., Aug., 1911; seconded for war serv., Camerons, 1915; dist. traffic asst., Apr., 1921; traffic man., Feb., 1929.

LEWTHWAITE, RAYMOND, M.A., D.M., B.Ch. (Oxou.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1894; resch. student, Inst. Med. Resch., F.M.S., Dec., 1926; ag. pathologist I, Mar., 1928, June, 1929, and May, 1931; pathologist II, Jan., 1929; pathologist I, Dec., 1931.

LEY, MAJOR CUTHBERT HILLIAR, O.B.E. late R.E., F.R.Met.Soc.—B. 1872; coman. in R. Engrns., 1892-1907; ret. from army, 1907; army serv. in Jamaica, 1896-99; S. African war, 1899-1900 (Tugela Heights and relief of Ladysmith); A.D.C. to inspr.-gen. of fortifications and engrns., 1900-03; geodetic survey of S. Africa, 1903-05; topographical secn., gen. staff, 1906-07; survey of Fiji, 1908; ordnance survey of Gt. Britain, 1912; served during war at Southampton in depts. of fortifications, miny. of munitions, and directorate of arty.; dir., Palestine survey, Dec., 1920.

LEY, HENRY COATES.—B. 1898; clk. to gov., Leeaward Is., Nov., 1920; 1st clk. treas. and cust. and shipping master, Falkland Is., Apr., 1924; also postmgr., Apr., 1928; ag. treas. and collr. cust., man., savings bank, recr. of wrecks and regist., shipping, June 1928; ch. acctnt., treasy., Cyprus, Sept., 1930; ag. dep. treas., July-Oct., 1933.

LEYH, STANLEY GEORGE HOARE, Pay-Lieut. R.N.R., O.B.E. (Milv.)—B. 1893; ed. East Ham Tech. Coll., A.S.C. (T.F.), 1908-12; Royal Navy, 1913-20; staff of comdr.-in-chief, Grand Fleet, 1914-17; admty. war staff (allied naval coun.), 1918-19; staff, comdr.-in-chief, China station, 1919-20; "1914-15 Star," Brit. War Med., Victory Med.; librarian, col. sec.'s office, S'pore, Oct., 1920; office asst., col. sec.'s office, S'pore, Jan., 1922; offr.-in-ch., records revision, in addn., Feb., 1924; asst. supt., govt. monopolies dept., S'pore, Nov., 1929.

LIESCHING, PERCIVAL, O.M.G. (1932)—B. 1885; ed. Bedford Schl., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; H.A. 1920; pvt., 1st batt. H.A.C., 1914; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brig., Feb., 1915; lieut., June, 1915; capt., Tank Corps, Oct., 1918; served in France, 1914, 1917-18; E. Africa, 1916; ment. in desps., 1918; appdtd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 26th July, 1920; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec. for dominion affrs., 6th Aug., 1925; temp. ag. prin., 6th Jan., 1927; prin. 1st Apr., 1929; accompanied U.K. delegation to L. of N. Assembly, 1927, to signature of Pact of Paris, 1928 and to Imp. Economic Conf., Ottawa, 1932; seconded as sec. to H. Cr. for the U.K. in Canada, 1928-32 and as polit. sec. to H. Cr. for the U.K. in the Union of S. Africa, 1933; ag. high commnr. for U.K., Aug.-Dec., 1933.

LIGHT, JOSEPH.—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1921; attd., Galle kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Kegalla kach., Feb., 1923; office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Aug., 1923; pol. mag., Puttalam, Apr., 1925; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1927; ditto, Jaffna, Apr., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Jan., 1929; asst. attln. offr., Jan., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, June, 1933.

LIGHTBOURN, J. L.—B. 1889; cadet, Out Islands, Bahamas, 1910; ag. 3rd and 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, 1910-12; sec., bd. of educn., Mar., 1914; sec., pol. commr., 1921-22; ag. audr., pub. accnts., May-Nov., 1924 and from Jan. to Apr., 1926; ag. commr., Out Islands, Nov., 1926 to May, 1927; clk., grade I, audit dept., July, 1927; ag. audr., on various occasions, 1929-33.
LILLEY, CYRIL CHARLES, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; capt., Worcestershire Regt.; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1920.
LILLEY, RONALD, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Bedford and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; col. audit dept., Apr., 1930; asst. audr., Tanganyika, July, 1930.

LINDON, NORMAN LIDSTONE—B. 1892; ed. Bristol Cathedral schl., and Christ's Hosp.; pol. probt., S.S., 1912; asst. supt. pol., S'pore, Mar., 1917; dist. offr., Christmas Is., 1921; comdt., pol. depot, S'pore, 1929; dep. commr. pol., F.M.S., May, 1929; comdt., pol. depot, K. Lumpur, 1931.

LINDSAY, JAMES.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; M.B., Ch.B., 1906; late res. physician, Grampian sanatorium for consumption; late clinical asst., Royal Victoria Dispensary for Consumption and diseases of the chest, Edin.; joined W.A.M.S., Apr., 1910.
LINDSAY, K. G.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, June, 1921.
LINDSELL, HAROLD OFFLEY.—B. 1884; ed. Bradford Coll. (exhibitioner) and All Souls' Coll., Oxford, B.A. (Litt. Hum.), 1907; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Apl., 1909; dist. offr., 1917; res., Kano, 1929.

LINDSELL, ROGER EDWARD.—B. 1885; ed. at Uppingham schl. (scholar) and King's Coll., Camb. (scholar); class. tripos, part I, cls. I, div. 2; called to bar, 1930; cadet, Hong Kong, 1909; passed cadet, 1911; ag. 2nd asst. registr-gen., 1911; J.P., 1912; 3rd asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., but continued to act as 2nd ditto, 1914; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1915; ag. chief asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs. 1915 to 1916; ag. asst. P.G., 1916; ag. asst. supt., imports and exports dept., 1918; ag. dep. regiar., sup. ct., 1918; ag. P.M.G., 1923; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1919, 1921, 1922 and 1923; ag. 1st pol. mag., on various occasions, 1922-30; ag. puisne judge, 1931 and 1933; ag. asst. atty.-gen., Feb., 1932.

LINEHAN, WILLIAM.—B. 1892; ed. Christian Brother's Coll. and Univ. Coll., Cork (class. school and exhib.), 1910-13; B.A. (1st cls. class., 1913); post grad. scholarship, 1913-14; M.A. hons., class. 1916; cadet, F.M.S., 1916; 2nd asst. dist. offr. Jan.-April, 1919; asst. dist. offr., Apr., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1923; offr., cls.
III, Dec., 1928; ag. asst. Br. advr., supt., lands and regisr., titles, Kelantan, Feb., 1931.

LINNELL, CAPT. THOMAS FORESTER, R.E. (res. of offs.), A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Denstone Coll., 1899-1904; articled pupil, civ. engr., 1905-09; lieut., Royal Engineers, S.R. of O., 1910; temp. asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Br. E. Africa, 1911; transf. to Uganda rly., Apr., 1914; war serv. on Uganda rly. and in German E. Africa, 1915-18; "1914-15" Star, Victory and Gen. Serv. meds.; dist. engrnr., Kenya and Uganda rlys., June, 1924.

LINTON, REGINALD DUKES, B.Agr., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1904; ed. Mountjoy Sch., Dublin, Belfast Univ. (B. Agr.) (distinct.), 1927; Oxford Univ. and Imp. Coll., Trop. Agr. (A.I.C.T.A.), 1929; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1929.
LISSAMAN, ARTHUR EVELYN, F.S.I., M.Inst. M. & Cy. E.—B. 1897; ed. Luton Modern Schl. and Coll., Estate Management, Lond.; mil. serv., R.E.'s, 1915-19; survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921; engrnr., 1923.

LITTI-EDALE, CYRIL RICHARD EVELYN, M.A. (Dublin-T.C.D.).—B. 1893; ed. Portora Royal Schl. and Dublin Univ.; war serv., France and Gallipoli, 1914-18; Rhine Army, 1919-20; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1922.

LIVERPOOL, EARL OF, RT. HON. ARTHUR FOLJAMBE, P.C. (1917); G.C.B.; G.C.M.G. (1914); G.B.E. (1918); K.C.M.G. (1912), M.V.O., (1900); Viscount Hawkesbury of Kirkham; Baron Hawkesbury of Haselbech.—B. 1870; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brigade, 1891; lieut., 1893; capt., 1897; staff capt., Dublin district, July, 1900 to Dec., 1901; served in S. Africa, 1901-2; major, 1907; state steward and chamberlain to Earl of Aberdeen, K.T., lord lieutenant of Ireland, 1906-08; gov. of New Zealand, 9th Sept., 1912; assumed govt., 19th Dec., 1912; governor-general of New Zealand, 1917-20.

LIVINGSTON, WILLIAM, A.M.Inst.C.E., P.A.S.Inst.—B. 1890; ed. Schl. of Science & Art, Kirkcaldy and Heriot-Watt Coll., Edinburgh; war serv., Gallipoli, Egypt and France, 1914-19; asst. dist. engrnr., Trinidad, 1919; dist. engrnr., 1920; dist. engrnr., Br. Guiana, 1924; seconded for duty on reconnaissance survey to Interior, Br. Guiana, Mar.-Aug., 1927; D.P.W., Sept., 1929.

LLIWELLYN, JOHN LIONEL BRETHERTON LLWELLYN, M.A. Oxon. (hons. hist.), F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 22nd Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., N. Frontier, Sept., 1915-June, 1920; A.G.S. med., "Jubaland, 1917-18"; ag. dist. comsnr., Jubaland, Nov., 1922-May, 1924.
LLOYD, EDGAR BRINLEY.—B. 1894; ed. Aberavon; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1919; ag. land asst., 1920; ag. regisr., crown lands, documents and titles, 1921.

LLOYD, J. D.—Ed. at Blundell's schl., Tiverton, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; class. tripes, cls. II., div. I.; Blundell exhibtn., and major scholar of Sid. Sus. Coll.; B.A., 1906; cadet, Hong Kong, 1907; offr. for exam. of male assisted emigrts., 1908 to 1909; passed cadet, 1910; ag. 1st asst. regisr.-gen., 1910; J.P., 1911; asst. postmaster-gen., 1912 and 1914; 2nd asst. regisr.-gen., 1913; ag. dep. regisr., acctnt. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1913 and 1914; ag. supt., impts. and expts., ditto, 1916; seconded for mil. serv., 1917; comn. in Chinese Labour Corps, 1918; offl. recvr. and regisr. of trade marks and patents, 1919; custodian of enemy property, and contrlr. of local clearing office, 1920; admstr. of Austrian and Bulgarian property in admn., 1920; census offr. in admn., 1921; custodian of enemy property, etc., and dep. regisr., sup. ct., 1922-23; supt., impts. and expts., 1923; commended by S. of S. for repts. on opium smuggling, 1924; Hong Kong rep. to Bangkok Opium Conf., 1932.

LLOYD, T. I. K.—B. 1896; ed. Rossall Schl. and Caius Coll., Cambridge (schol.), 1914; cadet, Royal Mil. Acad., 1915; 2nd lieut., R.E., 1916; served in Egypt and Palestine, ment. in desps., 1917; apptd. under re-construction scheme, asst. prin., miny. of health, July, 1920; att. to C.O. as asst. prin., Dec., 1921; ag. prin., D.O. 4th Sept., 1929; seconded for serv. as sec. to Palest. comsnr., 1929.

LLOYD, WILLIAM BUTLER.—B. 1889; ed. Shrewsbury, and Christ Church, Oxford; M.A.; called to the bar, 1905; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1913; mag., Rodriguez, 1916; mag. for occupied territories of German East Afr., 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1922; pol. mag., S. Leone, June, 1922; puisne j.ice, Mar., 1924; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1924 to May, 1925, and from July, 1928; puisne judge, sup. ct., Nigeria, 1930; ag. ch. just. July-Oct., 1933.
LLOYD, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1889; ed. Hampden Gunner Schl.; asst. acctnt., open field Baro-Kano rly., N. Nigeria, July, 1912; trans. to admstr. ser., Apr., 1917; admstr. offr., ca. I, Dec., 1932.

LLOYD, HON. SIR WM. FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1864; ed. Owen's Coll., Manchester; and Durham Coll. of Science, Newcastle-on-Tyne; bachelor of law, London Univ., 1894; D.C.L., Victoria Univ., Toronto, 1901; vice-principal, Bishop Field Coll., St. John's, Newf., 1894-1901; editor, "Evening Telegram" 1904; M.H.A., Trinity dist., 1904; leader of the opposition, H. of A., 1916; with Sir Edward (now Lord) Morris formed a National Govt., became attorney-general, 1917; ag. prime minister, 1917; prime minister, 1918.

LLOYD-BLOOD, CAPT. L.I.N., M.C., R.A. Barrister-at-law.—B. 1896; ed. Tonbridge; Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd lieut., 6th R. Fusiliers, Jan., 1916; France, 1916-18; ment. in desps.; dep. regisr., sup. ct., Kenya, 1920; regisr., July, 1921; asst. atty.-gen., Nyanza Land, Aug., 1924; ag. atty.-gen., in 1925, 1927, 1929 and 1932; solr.-gen., Palau, 1932.

LOCK, GEORGE ROBERT.—B. 1876; ed. Westminster Coll. and Sidney-Sussex Coll., Camb., 1895 (schol.); senior optime, maths. tripes, 1st cls. mech. sciences tripes, 1899; ent. C.A., tech. ast., 1905; dep. head, wks. dept., 1915.

LOCKHART, CHARLES RAMSDALE.—B. 1874; mil. serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919 (Gallipoli, Egypt, and with K.A.R. in E. Afr.); ment. in desps.; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; ag. sen. asst. treas., May, 1920; sen. asst. treas., Apr., 1923; dep. treas., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1931; treas., Apr., 1935.
LOCKHART, F. R., M.B., Ch.B., M.A.; D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—B. 1897; mil. ser., 1916-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sep., 1924.
LOCKHART, SIR JAMES HALDANE STEPHENSON, K.C.M.G. (1908); C.M.G. (1888); F.K.S.; M.R.A.S.; LL.D. (Hong Kong Univ.).—B. 1866; Edin. Univ.; Greek medallist, etc.; cadet, Eng.

KHART, KENNETH SPENCER.—B. 1890; admstr.'s office, Dominica, 1908; 2nd regier.'s office, 1910; 2nd rev. offr. and Nevis, 1913; ag. acctnt., treasy., 1917; sec. to quarantine bd., and clk. to cent. health; govt. trade agt. to enquire into conditions re W.I. labourers at Santo Jo, Nov., 1918; rev. offr. St. Kitts, May, ag. audit. clk., Sept., 1920; 2nd clk., treas. July, 1921; capt., St. Kitts-Nevis Defence ag. 1st clk., treasy., May-Sept., 1922; lk., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1922; income assnr., 1924; prin., Oct., 1929.

KYER, SIR NICHOLAS COLSTON, KT. BACH. C.B.E., I.S.O. (1906).—B. 1855; entd. N.S. Wales, 1868; collr. of cust. and first of taxation, N.S.W., 1896; compter-gen., C. of A., 1911; mem., Inter-State July, 1913; retd.

AN, SIR EWEN REGINALD, KT. Bach.—B. 1868; ed. at Charterhouse and Exeter 3.A., 1891; M.A., 1897; student, Inner Jan., 1894; called to bar, Nov., 1899; joined N. London ses. and cent. crim. ct., serv. in Boer war as 2nd lieut., 56th squad., 1901; Queen's medal and four clasps; mines Transvaal govt., Aug., 1901, to Apr., 1902; es. mag., Transvaal, May, 1902 to May mag. E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1905; chief eyechelles, 1914; admstd. govt. from July, Mar., 1917 and from May to Oct., 1918; in conjunction with Alfred Gellé, Esq., r-at-law, code of civil procedure for Sey.; recd. thanks of sec. of state; puisne s. Coast, June, 1920, with seniority from 20; sen. puisne judge, Dec., 1922; ag. ch. Mar. to Nov., 1923 and Feb. to Mar., sh. just., Bahamas, Feb., 1925; chmn., educn., Aug., 1925; judge, high ct., N. ia, May, 1927; ret., June, 1931.

AN, WILLIAM MARSTON, O.R.E. (1933). 89; ed. Bristol and Brasenose Coll., Oxford exhibr., Bridgeman prizeman, B.A.; ast. nsnr., East Africa Prot., Mar., 1913; sec., omtce. of supplies, 1914-15; hon. lieut., hed list, 1915-17; ag. dist comsnr., 1918 to July, 1921; ag. senr. ast. sec., Sept., 1920 and July, 1921 to June, 1924; 1st instve. offr., June, 1924; sen. ast. sec., 924 to Mar., 1925; ag. ast. col. sec., Oct., 1925 and in 1926; sec. local govt., 1926; ag. ast. col. sec., 1927; prin. ast. for local govt., lands and stltnt., 1928; local govt., lands, stltnt. and mines, TGEN, W. G.—B. 1894; on mily. serv., May, 1916 to 18th Feb., 1920; temp. clk., Mar. 1920 to 27th June, 1922; apptd. optmet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., 27th 922.

LONDON, GEORGE ERNEST.—B. 1889; ed. at Warwick, and Downing Coll., Camb. (schol.); 1st nat. sol. tripes, 1910; hist. tripes, 1911; B.A., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; offr., cl. V, 1914; on mily. serv., 1916-19; lieut., Glouc. Regt.; offr., cl. IV, 1919; sec. to res., Pahang, 1920; offr., cl. III., Nov., 1923; dep. contr., rubber expts., S.S. and F.M.S. and dep. supervisor, rubber F.M.S., Nov., 1924; cl. II., Nov., 1928; ag. finan. comsmr. and audir.-gen., Johore, Jan., 1930; sec. to res., Perak, Sept., 1930; cl. IV, Apr., 1931; az. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1932; ag. Br. res., N. Sembilan, June, 1933.
LONEY, GEORGE HENRY.—B. 1871; ed. Timaru Publ. Schl.; ent. N.Z. printing and stationery dept., 1895; supt., 1922; govt. printer, 1933.

LONG, CAPT. HERBERT EDWARD.—B. 1887; ed. Kendrick Schl., Reading; B.E.F., Egypt, Gallipoli, Salonika, Mar., 1915 to Feb., 1918; India and Aden, Mar., 1918 to April, 1921; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, July, 1921; H.B.M.'s vice-consul, Jigjiga, Sept., 1927; consul, Oct., 1930.
LONGBOTTOM, JOHN LEONARD, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1886; ed. Huddersfield Coll. Schl.; dist. engnr., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1913; astt. D.P.W., Gold Coast, 1929.
LORD, GEORGE ERIC LUDWIG, B.A.—B. 1907; ed. Felstead Schl. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; col. andit dept., June, 1930; ast. audir., Gold Coast, July, 1930.

LORD, Lieut.-Col. J. E. C., C.M.G., D.S.O., V.D.—Ent. Tasmanian civ. ser., 1886; commanded 40th Batt. Tas. A.I.F. in European War from Feb., 1915 to Aug., 1919; commands 12th Inf. Brig., Australian Mil. Forces; A.D.C. to the gov.-gen.; comsmr. of pol., Tasmania.
LOUDOUN, WILSON ANDERSON.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schl., Chatham, New Brunswick; astt. audir.-gen., 1902; audir.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1907; now comptroller-general.

LOUW, ANDRIS DE ROOS ECKARD.—B. 1873; clk. oust., Cape Town, 1893; examg. offr., 1896; prin. clk., E. London, 1912; survr., Durban, 1915; ch. clk., Cape Town, 1916; colir., cust., E. London, 1928; Port Elizabeth, 1929; colir., cust. and shipping mast., Durban, 1930.

LOUW, ERIC H.—B. 1891; ed. Beaufort West High Schl. and Univ. of Stellenbosch, S. Africa; B.A. and LL.B. (Cape); called to bar, 1916 and practised at sup. ct., Grahamstown; el. M.P. for Beaufort West, 1924; first S. African comsnr. to U.S.A. and Canada, 1925; high comsnr. for Union of S. Africa, London, Mar., 1929; min. plenipotentiary, Washington, Oct., 1929; do., Rome, Nov., 1933.
LOUW, JAN DANIEL, B.A.—B.1888; ed. Victoria Coll. and Univ., Stellenbosch; educ. serv., Cape Prov., 1912-1930; govt. translator, Pretoria, 1930.

LOUWRENS, HILLIGARD MULLER.—B. 1868; ed. Normal Coll., Cape Town, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and law classes, S.A. Coll., Cape Town; admitted as atty. and notary, sup. ct., Cape Town, 1892; pract. as atty., 1892-1903; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1906; pract. at sup. ct. bar, Cape Town, 1906-23; K.C., 1922; ag. waterct. jge., 1923; jge., sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope local div., 1924.

LOWE, CAPT. HERBERT JOHN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and R. Vetv. Coll., Dublin; lieut., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1914; capt., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1915; war serv., France, 1914-18; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1918; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1923; ag. dep. ch. vety. offr., May to Dec., 1926; ag. dir., vety. serv., in 1932 and 1933; ag. dep. dir., do., in 1933.
LUCAS, HON. SIR EDWARD, KT. BACH. (1921).—M.L.C., S. Australia 1900-1918; agt. gen. for S. Australia in London, 1918-24.

LUCAS, FRANK ARCHIBALD Wm.—B. 1881; ed. St. Augustine and Marest Bros. Schls., Johannesburg, S. African Coll. (now Univ., Cape Town), Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Cape); B.A. (hons.) Oxon; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1905; advoc., sup. ct., S. Africa; K.C., 1924; mem., Transvaal provin. coun., 1914-17; mem., econ. and wage coman., 1925; mem., native econ. coman., 1930; chmn., Union wage bd. since 1926.

LUCETTE, EDWARD HENRY, B.A. (Oxon.), M.C.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Apr., 1915; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd., Anuradhapura kach., June, 1921; attd., Trincomalee kach., Mar., 1922; attd., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1922; pvte. sec. and ag. A.D.C. to the gov., July, 1924 to Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Gampola, Feb., 1926; ditto, Kandy, Dec., 1926; dist. judge, Badulla, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., Colombo, Feb., 1929; addnl. dist. judge, Kandy, July, 1931; astt. regisr., co-op. socs., Nov., 1931; astt. govt. agt., Kegalle, Oct., 1932.

LUCIE-SMITH, SIR ALFRED VAN W., KT. BACH. (1911).—Ed. at Rugby; solr., Br. Guiana, 1877; called to bar, Mid. Term., 1881; ag. solr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1887; pres. dist. ct., Famagusta, Cyprus, 1887; Limasol, 1892; ag. Queen's advoc., 1893; ag. judge, cons. ct., Constantinople, 1896; res. mag., Kingston, Jamaica, Nov., 1896; puisne judge, B.G., Jan., 1898; ag. ch. just., 1900-01; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1900; ch. just., Trinidad and Tobago, 1908; pres., W. Indian ct. of appeal, May, 1920; ret., 1924.

LUCIE-SMITH, JOHN ALFRED, O.B.E., V.D.—B. 1888; ed. Stonehurst Coll.; col. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1905-06; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; advoc. and solr., S. Sttlnts., 1910-14; on mily. serv., 1914; twice ment. in desps.; ag. pres., dist. ct., Trinidad, 1920; astt. mag., Port of Spain, 1920-23; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1924; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Dec., 1925; ag. puisne judge for various periods, 1926-27; ag. crown pros., Nov., 1926; puisne judge, Oct., 1927; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1927; puisne judge, Trinidad, 1929; chmn., oil and water bd., 1931; puisne judge, Kenya, 1931; mem., ct.of appl. for Eastern Africa.

LUCIE-SMITH, JOHN DUDLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Berkhamstead schl.; entd. civ. serv. of Jamaica, 1909; 2nd cls. elk., 1914; 1st cls. elk., 1920; sec., Westmoreland hurricane relief coman., 1912; sec. foot and mouth disease coman., 1922; ag. priv. sec. to gov. of Jamaica (Sir Sydney Olivier) 1912; priv. sec. and A.D.C to gov. of Jamaica (Sir Leslie Probyn), 1920-23; prin. elk., col. secretariat, 1929; war serv., R.F.A. (France and Flanders), 1915-18; A.D.C to C.R.A., 68th Div., 1918; W.O. staff, 1919; edr. of "The School Garden in Jamaica" and "Elementary Hygiene."

LUCKMAN, CYRIL HERBERT MORTON.—B. 1881; elk., cust., Johannesburg, 1943; 1st grade elk., Capetown, 1912; senr. elk., 1915; 2nd grade prim. elk., Pretoria, 1922; 1st grade ditto, 1926; acctnt., Pretoria, 1930; ch. elk., 1933; ch. inspr., 1933.

LUDDINGTON, NORMAN JOHN, M. Sc., Durham Univ.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 17th Nov., 1911; attd., Jaffna kach., Dec., 1911; pol. mag., Negombo, May, 1915; Panadura, June, 1915; Negombo, Aug., 1915; Galle, Apl., 1916; Colombo, July, 1921; astt. comanr., excise, July, 1923; astt. attlmt. offr., Nov., 1926; offr., cls. I, grade II, 1933; govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, July, 1933.

LUGARD, 1ST BARON (GR. 1928), RT. HON. SIR FREDERICK JOHN DEATHTY LUGARD, P.C. (1920), G.C.M.G. (1911), K.C.M.G. (1914), C.B. (1895), D.S.O. (1887).—B. 1858; ed. Rossall and Sandhurst; 1st coman., 9th foot, May, 1878; captain, Aug., 1885; major, Aug., 1896; lieut.-col., July, 1899; col., July, 1905; temp. brig.-gen., 1900-07; to India, Sept., 178; active service, Afghanistan, Sept., 1879, to Sept., 1880, medal; Sudan, Feb. to Nov., 1885; medal clasp, "Tofrek" Khedive's Star, despatches; Burmah, Oct., 1886, to Aug., 1887; mentioned in despatches, D.S.O. and medal. To Africa Feb., 1888; commanding expdn. against slave traders; very severely wounded. To East Africa Nov., 1889, to Oct., 1892; exploration of Sabaki; adminstr. Uganda; went to Borgu in conv. expdn., July, 1894, and made treaties on behalf of Royal Niger Company, returned, Apl., 1896 Feb., 1896, led expedition across Kalahari for Red Charterland Co.; apptd. comanr. and comdt., W. African frontier force, Aug., 1897; high comanr. for Northern Nigeria, Dec., 1899; resigned Sept., 1906; gov. of Hong Kong, May, 1907; gov., N. and S. Nigeria, Mar., 1912; gov.-gen., Nigeria, Dec., 1913; assumed govt., Jan., 1914; retired, 1918; Br. mem., Permanent Mandates comm. since 1922; author of "The Dual Mandate in Br. Tropical Africa" and other works.

LUGG, HARRY CAMP.—B. 1882; ed. Greytown; elk. and Zulu interp., mag.'s office, Bulwer, Dec., 1899; Msinga, Jan., 1901; Greytown, Feb., 1902; col. comm., Zululand, Aug., 1903; native affrs., Natal, Nov., 1903; Zulu interp. and astt. regisr. native high ct., July, 1906; sworn Zulu interp., sup. ct., Aug., 1909; sen. elk. and astt. mag., Durban, Dec., 1916; mag., Ndweudwe, Dec., 1918; Ingwavuma, May, 1920; Mapumalo, Aug., 1921; 2nd grade mag., Richmond, Apr., 1925; 1st grade mag., Verulam July, 1928; senr. mag., Verulam, Aug., 1930; pres., native appl. ct., Natal and Transval, Oct., 1932; ch. natrve comanr., Natal, May, 1933.

LUKE, SIR HARRY CHARLES, KT. BACH (1933), C.M.G. (1926), Knight of Justice of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1884; ed. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. med. hist., B.A., 1906; M.A., 1910; B. Lit., 1919; priv. sec. to the gov't. of Sierra Leone, 1908, and A.D.C., 1909; ag. astt. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Mar. to July, 1909; priv. sec. to the gov't. of Barbados, Feb., 1911; attached to C.O., Nov. to July, 1911 (Coronation medal); priv. sec. to the high comanr. of Cyprus (Sir H. Gool); A.M., G.C.M.G.), and elk. of exec. coun., Oct., 1914; astt. sec. to govt., Cyprus, 1912; ag. comanr., Paphos, 1917; comanr., Famagusta, 1915; sec., Cyprus defence comtee., Aug., 1914; served H.M.S. Doris on Syrian Coast, 1914-15; polit. offr. to Admirals Lord Wemyss and Campbell; govt. sec., Mudros, Feb., 1915 to June, 1916; lieut.-comdr., and ag. comdr., R.N.V.R.; recd. thanks of admv.; Italian med. for war valour; political offr. to Admiral of the Fleet Sir J. de Robeck, Constantinople and Black Sea, Aug., 1919; Br. ch. comanr. in Georgia, Armenia and Azerbaijan, Apr. to Sept., 1920; astt. govt. of Jerusalem, Oct., 1920; went as comanr. on the Orthodox Patriarchate of Jerusalem, and on the Jaffa disturbances, 1921; finan. comanr. of the Patriarchate, 1921-24; ed. sec., Sierra Leone, May, 1924; ag. gov. on occasions; ch. sec., Palestine, July, 1928; lieut. gov., Malta, Aug., 1930; passed in Turkish and modern Greek (hons.); author of "A Bibliography of Sierra Leone," 1910 and 1925; "Cyprus under the Turks," 1921; joint editor, "Handbook of Cyprus," and "Handbook of Palestine and Transjordan."

LUKE, S. E. V., B.A., 1927.—B. 1905; ed. St. George's, Harpenden and Oxford, Bracegirdle exhibr., Wadham Coll., 1924; 2nd cls. mod. hist., 1927; apptd. after compet. exam. asst. inspr., taxes (inland rev.), 1927; apptd. after further compet. exam., junr. clk., House of Commons, Feb., 1930; transf. to C.O. as asst. prin., Sept., 1930.

LUMLEY, JAMES MADDY, L.S.O. (1930).—B. 1878; ed. Royal Gram. Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne; served R.H.A., 1895-1901; S. African war, 1899-1902; wounded; Queen's medal, four clasps; King's medal, two clasps; S. African constab., 1901-08; pub. pros., and just. of the peace, Heidelberg, Transvaal, 1904-08; ast. supt. of pol., E.A.P., 1909; served in E. Africa, 1914-18; King's pol. medal, 1919; supt. pol., Kenya, 1919; ag. ast. comsnr., Kenya pol., various periods, 1920-24; ast. comsnr., pol., 1925; dep. comsnr., pol., 1926; ag. comsnr., pol., 1927.
LUNNON, GEORGE JOSEPH.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

LUSCOMBE, LAURENCE GEORGE.—B. 1880; ed. Heles schl., Exeter; served in S. African war, 1901-02; clk., Swaziland adminstr., Sept., 1902; ast. acctnt., Dec., 1909; acctnt., Apr., 1910; finan. seccy., 1918; finan. sec., Basutoland, Sept., 1927.

LYALL-GRANT, SIR ROBERT WILLIAM KT. BACH, (1934), M.A. (Aberdeen), L.I.B. (Edin.)—Vans Dunlop schol. in law, Edin. Univ.; called to Scottish bar, 1903; practised till 1909; atty.-gen., Nyasaland, Apr., 1909; ag. judge, high ct., in 1909, 1910, 1912 and 1913; judge of high ct., Aug., 1914, and mem., H.B.M. Ct. of Appeal for Eastern Africa; chmn., native rising comsnr., 1915; chmn. of claims bd. for Nyasaland, under Br. prots. defence O. in C., 1916-19; judge advco. to Nyasaland-Rhodesia Field Force, 1917-18; atty.-gen., Kenya, July, 1920; mem., exec. andleg. councils; puise just., Ceylon, Feb., 1926; ch. just., Jamaica, 1932.

LYNCH, FRANCIS CHRISTOPHER CHISHOLM.—B. 1884; ed. at pub. schls., priv. tuition, McGill Univ.; assoc. Eng. Inst., Canada; assoc. mem., Town Planning Inst. of Canada; gen. engnr., 1902-05; apptd. to interior dept., 1906; ast. supt. rly. lands br., 1911; supt., nat. resources intell. br., dept. of int. Can., Dec., 1911.
LYNDE, WALTER MIGHILLS, F.S.I. (Eng.)—B. 1883; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; quantity survr., P.W.D., E.A.P., 1910; in charge sp. wk., Kabete, 1918; contracts offr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1923; ag. D.P.W., 1926.

LYONS, Rt. Hon. JOSEPH ALOYSIUS.—B.1879; ed. state and convent schls.; M.H.A., Tasmania, 1909-29; treas., min. for educn. and min. for mines, 1914-16; premier, treas. and min. for rly., 1923-24; premier, treas. and min. for mines, 1924-27; premier and treas., 1927-28; el. to H. of R., C'wealth of Australia, 1929; P.M.G. and min. for wks. and rlys., 1929; prime min. of the C'wealth., 1932.

MAARTENSZ, LEWIS MATTHEW.—B. 1876; barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple; addtnl. crown counsel, Ceylon, Apr., 1903; crown counsel, July, 1906; addtnl. dist. judge, Kandy, Oct., 1910; ditto, Colombo, Apl., 1911; ag. dist. judge, Colombo, Apr. to Oct., 1916, Apr.-Dec., 1920, Mar. to Nov., 1923 and from June, 1924; comsnr. of assize, Apr. to June, 1925; ag. puise just., July to Sept. and from Oct., 1925; dist. judge, Colombo, Jan., 1927; ag. puise just., June, 1927.

MACB, CARL VICTOR LEOPOLD, M.C., Chevalier de la Legion d'Honneur.—B. 1889; joined "Fort Garry" Regt. 18th Canadian Reserves, Aug., 1914; served in France as comsnr. to Tank Corps, Dec., 1916; left Army, July, 1920; ment. in desps.; admstve. serv., N. Provs., Nigeria, 1920; transf. prisons dept., S. Provs. as supdt., prisons, 1924; ag. inspr., prisons, 1925; dep. dir., prisons, 1926.
