MOLLOY, PAUL AUGUSTUS, A.M.I.C.E., chartered civil engnr.—B. 1887; ed. King's Coll. Schl., Wimbledon and King's Coll., Lond.; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Madras, 1910-13; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jan., 1913; asst. engnr., irrigation, F.M.S., May, 1915; exec. engnr., antimalarial br., Jan., 1922; exec. engnr., P.W.D., Nov., 1922; ag. sr. exec. engnr., in 1927 and 1931; senr. exec. engnr., P.W.D., S.S. and F.M.S., 1934.

MOLTENO, Hon. Sir James Tennant, K.T. Bach. (1911), B.A., LL.B., M.L.A.—B. 1865; ed. at Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch, and Trin. for native aff., Aug., 1896; transf. to Rhod. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temple; entd. Cape parlmt., 1890; speaker of Cape H. of A.; speaker of H. of A., Union of South Africa, 1910.

MONK, HAROLD FRANCIS, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1890; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; attd. censor's office Dec., 1914; attd. col. sec., Apr., 1917; asst. supt., govt. monop., Penang, July, 1926; offr. cl. III, Nov., 1926; ag. legal advr., Kedah, Jan., 1931; asst. advr., Nov., 1931; ag. advr., lands, Apr., 1932.

MONTAGNAC, LOUIS IVAN DE.—B. 1880; called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1911; clk. of cts., parish of St. Andrew, Jamaica, May, 1921; ag. res. mag., St. Thomas, 1st Aug., 1922; ag. registr., sup. ct., Oct., 1924; registr., sup. ct., July, 1926.

MONTAGU, JAMES DROGO.—B. 1878; served, Cape Mounted Pol., 1903; Johannesburgh Mounted Pol., 1903-8; B.S.A. pol., 1908-9; S. Nigeria pol., 1909-18; local commt., Cyprus mily. pol., 24th Jan., 1920; ag. comnr., on various occasions in 1922-29 and 1932-33; ag. dep. ch. commdt., pol., June-Oct., 1925 and from Nov., 1926 to Feb., 1927; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., Oct. to Nov., 1926; ag. ch. commdt., pol. and inspr., prisons, July-Sept., 1928 and July to Sept., 1930; ag. Br. del. of Evcaf., July-Sept., 1930.
MONTGOMERY, H. R.—Asst. dist. comnr., E.A.P., May, 1908; dist. comnr., Apr., 1914; ag. senr. comnr., Kenya, 1926; senr. comnr., 2nd grade, 1929; 1st prov. comnr., 1932.
MOOD, H. L.—Asst. dist. comnr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; res. 2nd grade, Nyassaland, Nov., 1923; res. 1st grade, Mar., 1926; dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.

MOODY, SYDNEY, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1889; ed. King's Schl., Pontefract, Leeds Univ. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (Hist. Schol.), 2nd cls. hist. finals, 1914; pte., Pub. Schla. Batt., R. Fusiliers, 1914; 2nd lieut., E. York. Regt., France, 1915; lieut., King's Liverpool Regt., Egypt, 1916; lieut., Herefordshire Regt., Palestine, 1917-1918; held various appts. in O.E.T.A. (S.) Palestine, Dec., 1918- June, 1920; dist. offr., Palestine, July, 1920; attd. to Middle East Dept., C.O., Feb., 1923 to Feb., 1924; asst. sec., 1924; ag. asst. ch. sec. and ch. sec. on various occasions, 1925-32.

MOONESINGHE, NALIN, B.A. (Oxon.)—Cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1924; attd., Anuradhapura kach., June, 1924; do., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1925; do., Badulla kach., June, 1925; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1926; ditto, Puttalam, May, 1927; ditto, Jaffna, Aug., 1929; office asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1930; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1931; pol. mag., Jaffna, July, 1933.

MOOR, HERBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1891; obtained higher standard certif. in Forestry at Imperial Research Inst. and Coll., Dehra-Dun, India, 1914, with medals for forest engineering and sylviculture; probationer, Indian provincial forest service, 1914; passed exams. in Indian land revenue systems, forest law and accounts, and Hindustani by the higher standard, 1915; permanent extra asst. conservator, 1916; transf. to Trinidad as deputy conserv., forests, Jan., 1918; asst. conserv.. forests, Gold Coast, Jan., 1923; prin. forest offr., Apr., 1926.

MOOR, RICHARD, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1898; ed. Ernysted's Schl., Skipton and Hertford Col., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; cadet, S.S., July, 1921; asst. supt., govt. monop., S'pore., Mar., 1924; offr., cls. V, July, 1924; offr., cls. IV, July, 1928; sec. to res., Pahang, Nov., 1929; ag. solr.-gen., S.S., Sept., 1931 and Apr., 1932; dist. judge, S'pore., Penang and Malacca, Oct., 1932; ag. registr., sup. ct., Penang and offl. assignee in adnn., Nov., 1932; offr., cls. III, July, 1933.
MOORE, ERNEST STANLEY.—B. 1890; Imp. post office, 1909; war serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919; asst. survr., posts and tels., Nigeria, Sept., 1919; survr., do., 1926; divnl. survr., do., 1933.

MOORE, HENRY MONCK-MASON, C.M.G. (1930), B.A., Cantab.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1910; attd., col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1910; attd., Colombo kach., May, 1911; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avisawela, Aug., 1912; ditto, Colombo, and addtl. mun. mag., Colombo, Nov., 1912; office asst. to gov't. agt., Central Prov., Jan., 1913; office asst. to prin. collr., customs, and extra land survr., July, 1913; 4th asst. col. sec., Feb., 1914; 4th asst. col. sec., Oct., 1914; on mily. duty, May, 1916; addtl. asst. col. sec., July, 1919; 3rd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1919; 4th ditto, Jan., 1920; priv. sec. to offr. admstg. govt., Mar.-Sept., 1920, in adnn.; asst. gov't. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1920 to Dec., 1921; col. sec., Bermuda, Jan., 1922; prin. asst. sec., Nigeria, Jan., 1924; ag. dep. ch. sec. and ag. ch. sec. on various occasions, 1924-26; ag. ch. sec. to gov't., in 1926 and 1927; dep. ch. sec., Aug., 1927; gov't. dep., Sept. to Oct., 1927;

MOORE, Hon. Sir Newton James, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1870; mayor of Bunbury, W. Aust., for some years, M.L.A. for Bunbury, 1904; min. of lands and agric., W. Aust., Aug., 1905; premier Aug., 1906; resig. Sept., 1910; major comdg., 18th Aust. Light Horse; agent-gen. for W. Australia, 1911-1918; mem. of comttee. for stinting of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; on mil. ser. as G.O.C., Aust. Imperial Forces in U.K., 1915-17; M.P. (U.), St. George's, Hanover Sq., 1918; M.P. (Co.U.), N. Islington, from 1918.

MOORE, Wilfred Henry, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1925; attd., Kalutara kach., Jan., 1926; ditto, Kandy kach., July, 1926; office ast., N'Elia kach., Jan., 1927; office ast., Ratnapura kach., May, 1928; do., Kandy kach., May, 1931; ast. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Dec., 1931.

MOORE, Wm. Brownlow Asher, L.R.C.P.I., L.R.C.S.I., L.M. (Rotunda), 1912, D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), 1927.—House surg. to Meath hosp. and co. Dublin infirmary, Dublin, 1903; ast. M.O.H., sanitary dept., Hong Kong, 1905; med. offr., med. dept., 1905; surgeon-lieut. H.K.V.R., 1915; temp. capt., R.A.M.C., 1917-19; sp. plague offr., Mesopotamian Exped. Force, 1918; med. offr. in charge, civil hosp. and Asylums, H. Kong, 1920; med. offr. in charge, Kowloon and New Territories, 1922; med. offr. in charge, Victoria hosp. for women and children, 1928; dep. dir., med. and sany. serv., 1928; ag. dir., 1928, 1929 and 1931.

MOORES-WEDDON, W. C.—B. 1880; ed. Dulwich and Trin. Coll., Dublin; cadet, B.N. Borneo, 1905; ast. dist. offr., 1906; offr. in-charge Tonom-Tawau expdn., 1906; dist. offr., 1908; ag. P.M.G. and supt. of telegraphs, 1917; ag. offr.-in-charge, West Coast residency, 1917; examr. in Malay, 1917; on special service in Ceylon and F.M.S., 1918; postmr.-gen. and supt. of telegraphs, B.N. Borneo, 1918; ag. res., Interior, B.N. Borneo, 1920; ag. food contr., 1920; res., Kudat, 1921; sessions, 1922; ag. res., Sandakan and mem., leg. coun., 1922; res., East Coast, 1923.

MOORHOUSE, Lieut.-Col. Sir Harby Claude, K.T., Bach. (1925), C.M.G. (1914), D.S.O. (1906); offr., Legion of Honour.—Entd. R.A., 1891; capt., 1899; major, 1902; temp. lieut.-col., 1914; served in Uganda, 1898 (medal); W. Africa, 1900 (ment. in desps.); W. Africa, S. Nigeria, 1901-02 (ment. in desps., brevet-major, medal with clasp); W. Africa, N. Nigeria, 1903 (ment. in desps., clasp); S. Nigeria, 1904 (D.S.O.); ret. with rank of major, reserve of offrs., 1908; served in Nigeria, 1914-15 (ment. in desps.); lieut.-col., 1915; chf. ast. col. sec., S. Nigeria, 1908; sec., S. Prov., Nigeria, Jan., 1914; lieut.-gov., Nigeria, Jan., 1920; ret.

MOORMAN, Alexander William George.—B. 1887; nine yrs. Met. pol., New Scotland Yard; served in Italy, Corfu, and Salonika, 1914-18; supt., pol. in ch., C.I.D., Nigeria, 1919-21; ast. comsnr., pol. in ch., C.I.D., Nigeria, 1921-24; ast. dir., C.I.D., Gold Coast, June, 1924; ag. dir., C.I.D., 1925 and 1930; title changed to comsnr., pol., C.I.D., June, 1928.

MORE, John Rhys, M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1873; ent. serv. of Cape govt. rly. (ast. engr.), 1896; dist. engr., Mafeking, 1899; res. engr., Bulawayo, 1903; supt., Port Elizabeth, 1905; divnl. supt., Kimberley, 1910; Port Elizabeth, 1913; ast. dir. rly., S. West Africa, 1915; dir., rly., 1916; ast. gen. man., Bloemfontein, 1917; Durban, 1921; ast. gen. man., rly. and harbr. Union of South Africa, Nov., 1927; gen. man. rly. and harbrs., Mar., 1928.
MORETON, James Reginald.—B. 1866; ed. Northwich Grammar Schl. and Jesus Col., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong, Feb., 1893.

MORGAN, Hywel Glyn.—B. 1899; ed. Trin. Coll. (Wales); B.A.; LL.B. (Wales); called bar, Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1924; practised as barrister in London and on S. Wales cts., 1924-26; lieut., the Welsh Regt., Regular Res., R. of O.; war serv. with Machine Gun Corps India; crown coun., Gold Coast, Oct., 1930; ast. atty.-gen., Nyasaland Prot., Mar., 1932; ast. atty.-gen., Apr., 1932; ag. judge, Oct., 1933.

MORGAN, W., B.A. (1908).—Ed. Merioneth Taylors' Schl. (Exhibn.), and Jesus Col., Oxford; Pitt Club exhibn. in classical Div., 1908; Meyrick classical exhibn. at Jesus Col., 1904-1908; 2nd clas. class. hon. med., 1908; 3rd clas. Lit. Hum., 1908; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 1910; station mag., Sept., 1918; res., 1926; ag. sec., native affrs., Sept., 1928; ag. sec., N. Prova., Apr., 1930.

MORLEY, Arthur Harold, M.B., Ch.M. (Leeds), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (Liverpool).—B. 1900; ed. Trin. Gen. Infirmary; house surg., Leeds Gen. Infirmary, 1926; ditto, Northampton Gen. Infirmary, 1927; med. offr., Somaliland, 1928.

MORPHY, Henry, B.A. (1st clas. hon.), M.Soc., A.M.I.C.E., F.S.L.—B.1889; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin (senr. moderator), gold med. in ment. sci., etc.; 2nd grad. surve., Nigeria, Mat. surve., 1921; passed lower Hausa and Arabic; ast. surr.-gen. (supernum.), and senr. to Kano native admn., 1928.
MORRELL, Edward William.—B. 1878; Borneo serv., Sept., 1909; mag., lat. clas., Oct., 1914; dist. offr., June, 1915; res., Mar., 1916; ast. govt. sec., Mar., 1924; ag. judge, high court, Sept., 1932.

MORRIS, Baron (creat. 1918).—B. 1873; Sir Edward Patrick Morris, P.C. (Ire.), K.C.M.G. (1913), KT. Bach. (1904); LL.D., M.H.A., Newfd., 1885 to date; ast. atty.-gen., 1890-91-95; deleg. to Ottawa on federation question, 1894; deleg. to C.O. on matters, 1897; and in 1900 on the Treaty question; leader of independent party in legislature, 1898-1900; atty. gen. and minister of justice, 1903-7; resig. from Bond ministerial labour question, July, 1907; prime min., 1909; resigned premiership, 1918; mem. Impl. Confco. on naval and mil. defense; represented Newfoundland at the Coronation of King George V., and at the Imp. Confco.; editor "Newfd. Law Reports Revision," 1885-95.

MORRIS, Alfred, A.C.P., M.R.S.A., I.M.S.—1874; ed. St. Paul's, Chelsea; head master Saiyingpun, Hongkong, 1905-1926; normal master Queen's Coll., 1914; lecturer, T.I. Teach. 1914-19; English tutor, H. K. Univ., 1920-25; master of method, H.K. Univ., 1925-30; supt., St. John Amb. brigade, 1926; head master King's Coll., 1927; sec., hosp. comforts corp., 1927; J.P., 1928; ag. dir., tech. inst., Nov., Dec., 1928.

MORRIS, Clement Robertson.—B. 1873; P.O. clk., Cape, 1892; clk., mag. of cts., 1895; ast., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1903; 1st cls. clk., Port Elizabeth, 1904; Durban, 1912; senr. examr., Port Elizabeth, 1914.

MORRIS, EDWARD GILBERT, O.B.E. (1919). B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1884; ed. Bedford Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (exhibnr.); asst. dist. comsrr., Uganda, 1908; dist. comsrr., 1912; lieut., unattached E.A. Forces, 1915; capt., Uganda T.C., 1916; dist. pol. offr., Tanganyika, 1919; resigned, 1920; re-appt., 2nd grade admin. offr., Uganda, 1924; labour comsrr., 1924; insp. schls., 1925; ag. dir., educn., 1927; dep. dir., educn., 1928; dir., educn., 1929.

MORRIS, HUBERT MEREDITH, M.Sc. (Agr.), F.E.S.—B. 1886; ed. Manchester Univ.; research fellow and asst. lect., 1917-18; war serv., 1918-19; research under the Royal Soc. Grain Pests (War) Comte., 1919-20; asst. entomologist, Rothamsted experimental station, 1920-27; entomologist, Cyprus, 1927.

MORRIS, JAMES PATRICK ANDREW, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1892; ed. St. Ignatius Coll., Galway, Blackrock Coll. and Royal Vety. Coll., Dublin; capt., R.A.V.C.; vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, Dec., 1920; dep. dir., animal health, Jan., 1930; ag. dir., do., Dec., 1932; dir., do., 1933.
MORRIS, JOSEPH.—B. 1880; ch. acct., Kowloon Canton rly. constrn., Hong Kong, 1907-10; ch. acct., open line, 1910-1933.

MORRISON, HON. AULAY MACAULAY.—B. 1863; LL.B., Dalhousie Univ.; called to the bar, Nova Scotia, 1888, and B. Columbia, 1890; mem. of H. of C., Canada, 1896-1904; puisne judge of sup. ct., B. Columbia, 1905; ch. just., sup. ct., 1929.

MORRISON, CHARLES RHODWAY, I.S.O. (1925).—B. 1873; ed. Wesleyan (Boys) High Schl., Freetown; 2nd clk., dist. ct., Karene, Sierra Leone, 1897; clk., Karene Exp. Forces, 1898 (W.A. med. and clasp.); 1st clk., 1902; postal agt., 1902-05; ch. clk., 1908; finan. asst., 1911; passed higher stand., Mende and Temme and lower stand., Susu, 1912; office asst., 1921; offr. in charge, Sumbuya dist., 1921; dep. asst. mast., circuit ct. on several occasions, 1905-10.
MORT, ERIC LAIDLEY, B.A.—Ed. Tonbridge Schl., and University Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, Aug., 1914; attchd. Nigeria Regt., 1916, and 1917-18.

MORTEN, FREDERICK JOSEPH—B. 1888; ed. Lancing Coll. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1911; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1912; ag. 4th mag. and asst. dist. judge, Singapore, Mar., 1913; ag. dist. offr., Jasim, Jan., 1914; passed cadet, Jan., 1915; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Sept., 1915; attached to col. sec. Jan., 1919; ag. asst. to res., Malacca, and agent to food contrlr. in addition, Jan., 1920; asst. treas. and collr. of stams, Malacca, in addn., June, 1921; ag. sheriff and regisr., sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1924; ag. 1st asst., sec. B, and clk. of couns., Nov., 1924; ag. under sec., S.S., Feb., 1928; ag. 1st asst., sec. B, and clk. of couns., Feb., 1928; ag. 1st asst., sec. A, Dec., 1928; ag. under sec., S.S., Oct., 1929 and Dec., 1930; ag. dist. offr., P.W., Dec., 1929; ch. II., Dec., 1929; ag. dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., cls. IA, Apr., 1932.
MORTIMER, PETER.—B. 1875; cls. V. Ceylon civ. serv., June, 1929; extra office asst., Jaffna kach., June, 1929.

MORTON, JOHN, M.R.C.V.S. (1927).—B. 1901; ed. Keil Schl. and Glasgow Vety. Coll.; Fraser research schol. at Rowett Research Inst., Aberdeen, 1927-28; Scottish bd. of agr. research schol., 1928-29; vety. offr., Falkland Is., Apr., 1929; lieut., Falkland Is. Defence Force, 1929; vety. offr., Nigeria, 1933.

MOSELEY, FRANCIS ARNOLD.—B. 1883; ed. Perth, W. Australia and Brasenose Coll., Oxon.; B.A. 1904; called to bar, Middle Temp., May, 1903; judge's associate and clk. of arraigns, W. Aust., Oct., 1905; mily. serv., Australia, 1915-16; France, 1916-19; Ireland, 1920-22; M.C., 1919; asst. regisr., titles, Tanganyika Territory, 1922; ag. regisr., titles, in 1922, 1923, 1924 and 1925; ag. solr.-gen. for various periods in 1922, 1923 and 1927-32; dep. land offr., Mar., 1926; crown coun., 1926; atty.-gen., Nyasaland Prot., 1934.
MOSELEY, HENRY DOYLE.—B. 1884; junior clk., sup. ct., W. Aust., Oct., 1900; judges' assoc. and clk. of arraigns, Aug., 1903.

MOUBRAY, GEORGE ALEXANDER DE CHAZAL DE.—B. 1888; ed. Loretto Schl., Manchester Univ. (civil engng.), Zurich Polytechnikum and Univ. (pure science); cadet, F.M.S., 1912; offr. cls. V., Jan., 1916; do., cls. IV., Dec., 1919; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., F.M.S., Aug., 1920; regisr., titles and town planning admstr., Selangor, in addn., July, 1923; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1924; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1929; 1st mag., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1930; ag. dist. offr., Kinta, Mar., 1932.

MOULDER, HUGH ARTHUR WILLIAM.—B. 1879; ent. Fiji civ. serv., 1910; acctnt. and clk., Gilbert and Ellice Is., Dec., 1915; seconded for serv. in Fiji, Apr., 1921; ag. audr., Aug., 1921 to Apr., 1922; ag. treas., Apr., 1924; ag. res. comsrr., Sept. to Oct., 1925; treas. and collr., cust., 1925; col. treas., Br. Honduras, Feb., 1928; mem., exec. and legis. couns.; dir. pub. offrs. guarantee fund.

MOUNTAIN, HAROLD GRIFFITHS, A. Inst., M.M.—B. 1887; ed. at King's Schl., Worcester, and Schl. of Mines, Camborne (1st cls. dip.); served R.E., tunnelling coys. and eng. stores dept., 1916-19; inspr., mines, Cyprus, Oct., 1925; ag. water engrn., July-Oct., 1930; inspr., lab. and regisr., trade unions, Jan., 1933.

MUDDIE, NORMAN DAVID, M.A. (Edin.), Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple—B. 1884; cadet S. Stltma., Dec., 1907; passed cadet, Jan., 1911; offr., cls. V., June, 1911; dep. regisr., sup. ct., Malacca in addition, Aug., 1917; offr., assignee of debtors' estates for Malacca, Oct.-Dec., 1917; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1918; superny. offr., cls. III., Nov., 1919; stamp and probate offr., Singapore, Apr., 1922; regisr. and sheriff, sup. ct., Penang, 1924; dep. pub. pros. in addn. to dep. regisr. and asst. offr., assignee, Penang, Dec., 1924; dist. judge, Malacca, May, 1925; offr., cls. IB., 1927; dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, Nov., 1928; ag. judge, S'pore, Dec., 1930; puisne judge, F.M.S., 1932.

MULHALL, JOHN ARCHIBALD.—B. 1899; on mily. serv., 1916; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd. Kalutara kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Puttalam kach., Nov., 1922; extra office asst., Badulla kach., Oct., 1923; office asst., ditto, Jan., 1924; office asst., Galle kach., June, 1925; ditto, Anuradhapura kach., May, 1927; addnl. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1928; asst. col. sec., May, 1928; sec. retrenchment comsrr., Apr., 1932; asst. ch. sec., Oct., 1933.

MULLIN, JOHN.—B. 1878; ed. England and Wales; assayer, surveyor and mining engr.; assoc. mem. of institn. of mining and metallurgy, England; cert. sworn land surv., B. Guiana, 1906; 3rd cls. offr. and survr., B. Guiana, 1908; warden, mining dist. and J.P., 1910; 1st cls. clk., dept. of lands and mines, 1911; ag. chief clk., 1911-12, 1913-14; 1st lieut., B. Guiana local forces, 1915; seconded to govt. secretariat as conf. clk., 1916; chmn., canals polder, 1917-20; ch. clk. and ag. asst. D.P.W., 1919-20; asst. commr., lands and mines, 1922; mem. and sec., bd. of colonisation and development, 1926; ag. commr., lands and mines, chmn., local govt. bd. and mem., town council on various occasions; commr., land and mines, 1927; mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1929.

MULOCK, Rt. Hon. Sir William, P.C. (1925), K.C.M.G. (1902), K.C., LL.D., M.A.—B. 1843; graduated at Toronto Univ., 1863; gold medal in modern languages; called to the Ontario bar, 1868; vice-chanc. of Toronto Univ. in 1881; sat in parlmt. 1882 to 1905, representing N. York; postm.-gen. in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896; ch. just., exchequer div., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1905; ch. just., Ontario, 1923.

MUMFORD, WILLIAM BRYANT, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1900; ed. Manchester Gram. Schol. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; maths. tripos, 1920; midshipman R.N., 1918-19; diploma of educn., London, 1923; B. of E. teachers certif., 1923; asst. mast., educn. dept., Tanganyika Territory, 1923.
MUNRO, THOMAS AMBROSE VESPER.—B. 1883; harbmr., Bahamas, 1st Sept., 1913; ag. marshal, admv. side, sup. ct., 1919.
MURDOCH, JAMES WILSON, M.B., Ch.B. (Aberdeen).—B. 1900; 2nd asst. med. supt., ment. hosp., T. Rambutan, F.M.S., Jan., 1926; med. supt., do., July, 1931.

MURISON, SIR JAMES WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1919), B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1872; ed. privately and at Trin. Hall, Camb.; senr. class. scholr., 1891; 1st div., 2nd cls. class. tripos, 1894; 3rd cls. law tripos, Part II., 1895; Latham English prize, 1895; B.A., LL.B., 1895; called to Bar, Middle Temple, Apr., 1896; devilled and practised at the Chancery Bar, 1896-1902; registr., E.A.P., Oct., 1902; town mag., Mombasa, Jan., 1904; 2nd asst. judge, H.B.M. ct. for Zanzibar, Apr., 1904; sen. ass't. judge, Mar., 1905; ch. judge, Zanzibar, 20th Sept., 1914; mem., of ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1905-1910, and from 1914 upon transfer of Zanzibar to C.O.; judge, sup. ct. of H.H. Sultan of Zanzibar from 1908; arbitrator in dispute between Zanzibar and E.A.P. govts. as to H.H. mainland properties, 1911; recd. thanks of S. of S. for conversion of applied Indian Acts into Zanzibar Decrees, 1917; judge prize ct. Zanzibar from 1914; passed govt. exam. in Hindustani, 1903, and in Swahili, 1904; examr. in Swahili to the Zanzibar Govt.; joint edt., Zanzibar Law Repts., 1868-1918; atty.-gen., S. Sttlnts., 1919; ag. col. sec., June, 1920 to May, 1921; ch. just., S. Sttlnts., July, 1925; ret., 1933; edited the Revised Laws of the S. Sttlnts., 1926; author of "Swahili Translations," 1911.

MURPHY, HON. CHARLES, P.C. (Can.), B.A., M.P.—B. at Ottawa, 8th Dec., 1863; son of James Murphy, of Biri, King's co., Ireland, and Mary Conway, of Limerick, Ireland; ed. at the Christian Brothers schl., Ottawa, the Ottawa Collegiate Inst., and Ottawa Univ. (B.A.); barr.-at-law; sworn of the King's privy coun. for Canada on the 5th Oct., 1908, and apptd. S. of S. in the cabinet of the Rt. Hon. Sir Wilfrid Laurier on the 10th Oct. in the same year; elec. a mem. of the H. of C. of Canada at the general elec. of 1908 for the county of Russell, in the prov. of Ontario; re-elec., 1911, 1917 and 1921; resigned portfolio on defeat of Laurier govt., Oct., 1911; P.M.G. in King admtn., 29th Dec., 1921; senator, 5th Sept., 1925.
MURPHY, HON. DENIS, B.A.—B. 1870; ed. Ottawa Univ.; B.A. 1892; called to the bar, 1896; elec. to provincial legis., British Columbia, 1900; judge of sup. ct., B. Columbia, 1909.

MURPHY, JOHN HOWARD BLACKWOOD.—B. 1895; Schol. of Physic, Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1913; R.A.M.C., Jan., 1915; India Office, Apr., 1919; A.D.C. Jubaland, Dec., 1919; dist. commr., Gosha, 1920; ditto, Choreh, 1922; ditto, Kivu, Ukambani, 1923; resigned, private affairs, 1925; re-apptd., commr., Somaliland, 1927; dist. offr., Kenya, 1930.

MURPHY, HON. THOMAS GEROW, P.C. (Can.)—B. 1883; ed. at pub. and high schools of grad. of Man. Coll. Pharmacy; 1st el. to H. C., Canada, g. e., Oct., 1925; re-elec., g. e., min. of interior in Bennett adminstr., Aug., 1930; re-elec. by accl. after assuming ofte., Aug., 1930.
MURPHY, TIMOTHY.—B. 1882; joined Hong Kong pol., Dec., 1903; passed final exam. in Cantonese, 1912; inspr., Aug., 1922; King's pol. med., 1924 C.D.I., Nov., 1926; asst. supt., Feb., Mar., 1931.

MURPHY, WILLIAM LINDSAY.—B.A., Trin. Coll., Dublin; B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1910; attd., Kandy kach., Dec., 1919; addl. pol. mag., Colombo, Aug., 1911; ag. office asst., govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Jan., 1912; asst. to govt., Prov. of Uva, May, 1913; asst. and settm. offr., July, 1913; ag. asst. govt. art., Kandy, Feb., 1915; addl. pol. mag. and mun. mag., Colombo, Apr., 1915; ag. asst. gen. agt., Mullaitivu, Oct., 1915; ditto, Hambantota, Nov., 1917; ditto, Matara, Oct., 1921; addl. dist. judge, Galle, Jan., 1921; ag. asst. gen. agt., Kegalla, May, 1921; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Sept., 1922; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1923; temp. employed, C.O., 1926; addl. dist. col. sec., Jan., 1927; 2nd asst. col. sec. and ch. leg. coun., Feb., 1927; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., exec. coun., May, 1928; comptr., Jan., 1931; chmn., munic. coun. and mayor, Colombo, Mar., 1932.

MURRAY, ARTHUR EVELYN FRANCIS, B.A. (Oxon.), Barrister.—B. 1888; ed. Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxford; asst. dis. commr., Nigeria, 1912; cls. I, grade I, admtr., 1925; sec. to commr. of inquiry into disturbance in S.E. Provs., 1930; ag. sec. for native affs., 1931.
MURRAY, HON. SIR GEORGE JOHN ROSS, K.C.M.G. (1917), B.A., LL.M.—B. 1865; sup. of sup. ct., S. Australia, 1912; chief just. Jan., 1916 and lieut.-gov., Apr., 1916.

MURRAY, JOHN.—B. 1871; ed. Tokomaru dist. high schl. and Otago boys' high schl.; joined N.Z. stamp duties dept., 1899; asst. commr., stamp duties and asst. regnr., commr., Dunedin, 1909; ditto, Christchurch, 1921; asst. commr., stamp duties, 1923; commr., 1932.

MURRAY, SIR J. H. P., K.C.M.G. (G.C.M.G. (1914)).—M.A., Oxon.; ed. at Stge. gram. schl. and won Knox prize, Fairfax priz. senr. univ. prize, and John West medal; went to Brighton Coll., Germany, and Univ. Coll. London; entd. Oxford Univ. and gained foundation scholarship to Magdalen Coll., 1886; cls. class. mode., 1882; 1st ols. Lit. Hist., M.A., 1885; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1886; practised as a barrister in Australia and apptd. crown prosecutor, N.S. Wales; acted dist. ct. judge on several occasions; commr., N.S.W. Irish Rifles, 1898; served in S. Afr.

MURRAY, MENZIES.—B. 1890; ed. Stellenbosch and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (honors, Stellenbosch), 1909; B.A., LL.B. (T.C.D.), 1914; LL.B. (S.A.), 1920; war serv., 1914-19; capt., R.W. Fus., M.C.; advoc., 1921; ch. clk., patent office, Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1930; regis., patents and regis., companies, June 1932.
MURRAY, ROBERT EVERITT KING.—B. 1902; clk., grade II., Basutoland, 1924; sub.-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1930.
MURRAY, ROBERT HOWSON.—Admstrn. offr., 1st grade, Nyasaland, Feb., 1923; ag. prov. commr., July, 1925 to July, 1926; prov. commr., Jan., 1926.

MURRAY, WILLIAM ANSLEY, B.A., M.R., Ch.B. (Edinburgh), D.P.H. (Cape Town).—B. 1874; attd. to Dutch Reformed Ch. Mission in Brit. Cent. Africa Prot., 1901-12; co-operated with sleeping sickness commn. of the Royal Soc., 1911-12; capt., S. African med. corps, and was associated with Major Park-Ross in conducting the Sleeping Sickness Investigation Bureau, 1916-18; govt. pathologist, Durban, 1919; asst. health offr., Union of S. Africa, 1923; mem., statistical coun., Union of S. Africa; health research offr., Carnegie commn. of investig., poor white problem in S.A., 1929-31; senr. asst. health offr., Union, 1932.

MURRAY-JARDINE, ROBERT WILLIAM GORDON.—Ed. Marlborough Coll. and Clare Coll., Camb.; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, 1912; cov. commdr., 4th Batt. K.A.R., 1917-18; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1919; dist. mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ret. without taking up appt., 1920; dist. mag., Entebbe, Uganda, 1921; senr. mag., 1930.

MURLAND, CHARLES CAMPBELL.—B. 1884; ed. Harrogate, York and Dublin; sub.-inspr. of police, B. Guiana, 1908; dist.-inspr., 1912; ag. county inspr., 1910-11, 1914-1916; capt., B. Guiana militia, 1915; county inspr., 1920; detective inspr., 1927; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Sept., 1929 to May, 1929 and Apr.-Nov., 1930.

MUSK, HAROLD.—B. 1899; ed. Gainesboro and Knutsford; mily serv., R.N.V.R., 1917-19; Kew training, 1924; Agr. Coll., Wye, Kent, 1925; Impl. Coll., Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1926; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1927.
MYCOCK, CHARLES.—B. 1890; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Jan., 1915; asst. mast., Wantai Eng. Sch., 1930.

MYERS, HON. SIR MICHAEL, K.C.M.G., (1930), K.C. (1922), LL.B. (1897).—B. 1873; ed. Thornton schl., Wellington Coll., and Canterbury Univ. Coll. (N.Z.); in legal practice many years; ch. just., N.Z., 1929.

NAIRAC, GEORGE EDOUARD, K.C.—B. 1876; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, 1898; ag. addnl. subst. procureur and advoc.-gen., 1903-06; mem. for Port Louis in coun. of gov't., 1911-25; nominated mem., 1926; K.C., 1926; procureur and advoc.-gen., July, 1927; mem., exec. coun. and coun. of gov't.; ag. col. sec., July, 1928 to Mar., 1929; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1929 to Oct., 1930; edr., Mauritius Law Repts. since 1906 and of the Law Repts. Digest, 1902-25.

NAISH, RICHARD BRYANT.—B. 1891; B.A. (Oxon.), M.R. San. I.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attached to Galle kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Galle in addn., Apr., 1915; ag. pol. mag., Avissawella, Aug., 1916; extra office asst. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., Oct., 1916; ag. office asst. to gov't. agt., N.W. Prov., Dec., 1916; pol. mag., Gampola, Dec., 1918; asst. contr. of estate supplies, May, 1919; office asst. to gov't. agt., C. Prov., Feb., 1920; ag. asst., gort. agt., Colombo, June, 1920; ditto, Trincomalee, Aug., 1920; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avissawella, Sept., 1920; asst. finan. asst. to chmn., mun. coun., Colombo, Jan., 1921; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1921; ag. dist. judge, Tangalle, May, 1922; asst. gov't. agt., Batticaloa, Mar., 1923; ditto, Karunegala, Sept., 1925; asst. contr., rev., Mar., 1928; sec., univ. comm. in addn., July, 1928; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Sept., 1931; dep. contr., lab., Oct., 1931.

NANKIVELL, HOWARD.—B. 1893; ed. Christ's Hosp.; clk., col. secretariat, Jamaica, 1911; asst., 1912; 2nd cls. air mech., R.F.C., 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.C., 1916; lieut., tech. R.A.F., 1917-1920; 2nd cls. clk., col. secretariat, Jamaica, 1917; 1st ditto, 1920; sec., salaries regrading commn., 1922; sec., disturbances commn., 1922; sec., advisory finan. comtee., 1924; sec., civ. serv. comtee., 1925; prin. clk., secretariat, 1925; sec., prelim. W. I. confce., 1926; attd. for temp. duty, C.O., 1926; sec., salaries commn., Jamaica, 1928; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., 1928; ag. clk., privy coun., 1928; asst. col. sec., Trinidad, Apr., 1929; ag. col. sec., on various occasions, 1929-31; dep. col. sec., Jan., 1930; sec., intercol. confce. on trade relations with Canada, 1930; sec., Trinidad air bd., 1930.
NAPIER, SIR WALTER JOHN, KT. BACH. (1909), D.C.L. (Oxon.).—Barister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; atty.-gen., S. Sttlmts., 1907-1909; mem., W. African lands comtee., 1912.

NASH, GEORGE HUTCHERSON, M.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1889; ed. Queen's Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1912; passed cadet, June, 1914; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1915; supernumy. offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1919; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1924; dist. offr., K. Pilah, Apr., 1926; attd., co-op. socs. dept., Jan., 1929; offr. cls. II, Nov., 1929; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Aug., 1930; collr., land rev., K. Lumpur, July, 1932; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, May, 1933.

NASH, HERBERT FREDERICK WILLIAM.—B. 1901; entered C.O., 26th Nov., 1917; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk. and assigned to C.O., 29th Dec., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher cler. offr., 17th Sept., 1929.
NASH, WALTER.—B. 1890; ed. King's Coll., London; conf. clk. to gov.-gen., Nigeria, July, 1913; admvte. offr., 1918; res., 1932.

NATHAN, LT.-COL. SIN MATTHEW, G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1899).—B. 1862; entd. R.E., 1880; capt., 1889; maj., 1898; lt.-col., 1907; served in Nile expdh., 1885; Lushai expdh. (medal with clasp), 1889; sec. col. def. comtee., 1895; adminstr. gov't. of S. Leone in 1899; gov., Gold Coast, Oct., 1900; gov., Hong Kong, 21st Sept., 1903; gov., Natal, 1st May, 1907; sec. to G.P.O., London, 1910; chmn. of bd. of inland revenue, 1911; under sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1914; permanent sec. to ministry of pensions, 1916; gov., Queensland, 1920-25.
NATTLE, HAROLD RANDLE FITZ.—B. 1875; med. offr., Basutoland, 1904; prin. med. offr., 1928.

NATTRASS, ROLAND MARSHALL, B.Sc. (Agr.), Lond., Ph.D., Lond., D.I.C., Dip. Agr., Wye.—B.1895; ed. Nottingham High Sch.; asst. to cotton specialist, Tanganyika Territory, 1922; advisory mycologist, Western Prov.; Bristol Univ., Long Ashton Research Statn., 1924; senr. mycologist, miny. of agr., Egypt, 1928; mycologist, Cyprus, 1931.

NATRISS, ERNEST ALFRED.—B. 1882; ent. C.A. shipping agents, 1898; apptd. to C.A. office, 1912; dep. head, shipping dept., 1922; apptd. shipping offr. to the "Discovery Committee," 1927; head, shipping dept., May, 1933.

NAVARATNAM, SARAVANAMUTTU SAMUEL, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1894; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd. Matara Kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Ratnapura Kach., May, 1922; extra office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Sept., 1923; pol. mag., Matale, May, 1924; ditto, Chilaw, Feb., 1926; asst. comsnr., excise, Aug., 1927; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1930; asst. sttlmt. offr., July, 1931; sec. to min. of lab., industry and commerce, Oct., 1931; dep. contr., lab., Aug., 1933.

NEALE, HAROLD BERNARD.—Second clk. to asst. comsnr., Southern div., Bech. Prot., June, 1909; 2nd clk. to asst. comsnr., Northern Prot.; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1911; J.P., 1912; ag. asst. res. mag., 1913; sub-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1914; ag. res. mag., 1914; granted local rank of capt.; res. mag., 1921; passed 3rd grade Seewana exam., 1921.

NEAVE, JOHN RICHARD, M.C.—B. 1893; ed. King's Coll., Lond.; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; attd., res. offic., Malacca, Nov., 1919; asst. advr., Kota Tinggi, Feb., 1925; res., Labuan, Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1930; ag. comsnr., lands, Trengganu, July, 1931; 2nd asst. sec. (A), S.S., Dec., 1932; ag. asst. treas. and collr., land rev., Malacca, Aug., 1933.
NEILL, J. H.—Med. offr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919; ditto, Uganda, July, 1920.

NEIL, WILLIAM HARDLEY EDWIN, F.R.G.S.—B. 1892; ed. Cathedral Schl., Winchester, Taunton's, Southampton and Hartley Univ. Coll., Extn., Southampton; warser., Sept., 1914-Sept., 1919; survey probtr., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; asst. supt., trig. surveys, Dec., 1923; ditto, topo. br., Dec., 1925; ditto, rev. surveys, Pahang, Jan., 1931; asst. supt., surveys, N. Kedah, Aug., 1932.

NEILL, JAMES SCOTT, B.A., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1889; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; clas. schol., 1911; 1st sen. med. hist. and pol. sci., 1912; Victoria prizeman, Kings Inns., Dublin; cadet, Fiji, 1914; clk., leg. and exec. coun., Oct., 1915; dist. comsnr., 1916; regtr. sup. ct., and offr. recr., 1922; ag. atty. gen. and mem., exec. and leg. coun., July, 1922 to July, 1925; ag. prin. asst. col. sec. and agt. gen., immigr., Jan., 1924 and Sept., 1924; mem., bd. examrs., Fijian language and ag. editor, Indian newspaper "Radjut"; seconded sp. duty under Tongan govt. and apptd. mem., privy coun. of Tonga, Aug.-Sept., 1924; comsnr. of assize, circuit ct., June, 1925; ct. of review, income tax, Nov., 1925; Br. agt. and cons., gen., Tonga, Jan., 1927; is also a dep. comsnr. for W. Pacific and a judl. comsnr. for matrimonial causes; seconded for sp. duty, Ocean Is., Dec., 1930 to Mar., 1931.

NELSON, HARRY ROSS, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Med. Schl. and Aberdeen Univ.; med. offr., Uganda, 1912; temp. capt., Uganda med. staff, 1914; health offr., 1921; senr. do., 1924; ag. D.D.S.S. for various periods, 1926-32.
NELL, L.—B. 1880; ed. Blundells Schl.; asst. loco. supt., Nigerian rly., 1914; asst. supt., line, Mar., 1931.

NELSON, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. WILLIAM CHARLES SADLIER, M.A., D.D. of London; 1909; ed. Univ. of Melbourne; B.A. (Tr. Coll.) 1894; M.A. 1896; Univ. of London, B.D., London, 1909; deacon, 1891; priest, 1892; Melbourne; C.F., 1896; formerly curate of Pyramid Hill, 1891-1892; St. Paul's, Bendigo, 1892-99; incumbent of Holy Trinity, East Melbourne, 1899-1904; lecturer, St. John's Col., Melbourne, 1907-1910; canon of Melbourne Cathedral, 1901; examining chaplain to Bishop of Bendigo and Gippsland, 1902; vicar of Christ church, St. Kilda, Melbourne, 1904; lecturer Ridley College, Melbourne, 1910; consecrated Bishop of Nelson, N.Z., 21st July, 1912.

NETHERSOLE, JOHN MAPLETOFT, CBE (1933).—B. 1863; clk. to admstr. gen. and trs. in bankruptcy, Jamaica, 1880; 1st cls. clk., admstr. gen. and trustee in bankruptcy, Jamaica, 1899; custodian of enemy property, 1915; com. local clearing office, 1920; mem., income assmnt. comte., 1920; chmn., inc. tax assmnt. etc., 1922; res. mag., 1933.

NETTELTON, GERALD ENRAGHT.—Clk. to comsnr.'s office, 1914; sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1917; sp. J.P., 1917; ag. asst. res. mag., 1920; passed 1st gr. Seewana exam., 1920; passed civ. serv. lower law exam., 1922; ag. res. mag., 1922; res. mag. and hon. insp. Bech. Prot. pol., Apr., 1924.

NETTLETON, SIR SYDNEY CHARLES KNABLOW, KT. BACH. (1924).—Ed. Harrow, Trin. Coll., Oxford; B.A. class hons., 1887; M.A. 1889; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1889; went S.E circuit and Sussex sessions; pres. judge, high ct., Uganda, 1912; ag. chief just., 1913; judge of ct. of appeal for E. Africa, 1914; puisne judge, sup. ct., Gold Coast, 1915; ag. chief just., S. Leone, May to Aug., 1919, and of G. Coast, June-Oct., 1921; senr. puisne judge, G. Coast, Mar., 1920; ch. just., Pemba, Nov., 1922; pres., reformatory and indus. comsnr., 1923; ch. just., Cyprus, Dec., 1924; ag. just., Gibraltar, 1927; ret., 1931; auth. "Some Gold Coast Judgments, 1915-17"; "The Massai Case, 1915"; "The Law Affecting a Company Prospectus"; partly candidate Steeple (Attercliffe), 1909-10; S. Hackney, 1910.

NEWBOLT, CAPT. A. F.—B. 1893; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; B.A.; asst. serv., Oxfordshire Light Inf., Aug., 1914; May, 1919; served in Flanders, wounded, Apr., 1915; capt., 1915; adjt., 1918-19; asst. sec. (appts.) to S. of S., Lord Milner, May, 1919; Mr. Winston Churchill, Feb., 1921; P.U. of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; Mr. J. H. Trench, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Nov., 1924; Lord Passfield, June, 1929; prin., 1st Oct., 1929.

NEWBOULT, ALEXANDER THOMSON, M.C.—B. 1896; ed. Kingswood and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; asst. prov. sect., Dec., 1920; offr., cls V., Aug., 1923; asst. offr., Jelebu, Nov., 1925; offr., cls IV., Aug., 1927; sec. to res., N. Sembilan, Dec., 1927; cls. III, Aug., 1932.

NEWDEGATE, SIR FRANCIS ALTHUS NEWDIGATE, G.C.M.G. (1925), K.C.M.G., J.P., D.L.—B. 1862; ed. Eton and R.M.C., Sandhurst; in Coldstream Guards, 1888-89; cadet, Rugby schl.; alderman of the Warwick county coun.; M.P. for Nuneaton division of Warwickshire, 1892-1906; M.P. for Tamworth division of Warwickshire, 1909; gov., Tasman, 30th Mar., 1917; gov., W. Australia, 1920; ret., 1920.

NEWHOUSE, EDWARD, P.A.S.I.—B. 1884; ed. Friend's Schl., Lancaster; asst. engr., waterwks., Hong Kong, 1912; exec. engr., drainage, 1920; do., roads, 1927; J.P., 1931; exec. engr., waterwks., 1932; ag. A.D.P.W., 1932; asst. D.P.W., 1932.
NEWILL, COMMDR. JOSEPH BERNARD, D.S.O., R.N. (ret.).—B. 1887; harbmr., Malacca, 1922; dep. harbmr., Hong Kong, 1926.
NEWITT, CAPT. A. N.—European pilot, E. Africa Prot., May, 1918.

NEWMAN, WILLIAM AUGUSTIN, M.B.E.—B. 1873; ed. Sydney High Schl.; ent. New South Wales pub. serv., 1891; served with Australian forces, S. African war (Queen's med., 5 clasps); on staff of first privy min. of C'wealth of Aust., Jan., 1901; senr. clk., dept. external affrs., 1902; ch. clk., C'wealth electoral admstrn., 1903; C'wealth electoral offr. for Victoria, 1906; ch. clk., home affrs., 1909; offl. sec. to gov. gen., O. of A., and sec. to fedrl. exec. coun., 1913; sec. royal comm. on navy and defence admstrn., 1916; ch. clk., Australian defence dept., 1918; admstr., Island of Nauru, 1927.

NEWNHAM, HUBERT ERNEST.—B. 1886; ed. St. John's Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, Nov., 1911; pol. mag., Matale, Mar., 1913; landing survr., Colombo customs, Oct., 1913; ag. comsnr. of requests, Colombo, Feb., 1920; asst. govt. agt., Colombo, May, 1920; chmn., munic. coun., Colombo, Aug., 1924; comsnr., local govt. and pres., local govt. bd., 1930, and sec. to min. for local admstrn., July, 1931.
NEWTON, ALEXANDER STAFFORD, L.D.S. (Liv.), R.A.M.C.—B. 1896; mil'y. serv., 1914-19; dental surg., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1915.

NEWTON, SIR FRANCIS JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1892), C.V.O. (1911).—B. 1867; ed. at Rugby and Univ. Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1880; M.A., 1890; barrister, Inner Temple; lieut., 3rd batt. Hampshire regt., and extra A.D.C. to Sir H. Robinson, gov. Cape, 1881; priv. sec. to Sir Thomas Scanlen, prime minister of Cape, 1883; and to Sir H. Robinson, 1884-86; sec. to Mauritius roy. comsnr., 1886; ag. admstr. and ch. mag., Br. Bechuanaland, 1888; col. sec. and rec.gen., 1889; ag. admstr. and ch. mag., 1892; res. comsnr., Bechuanaland Protectorate, Dec., 1895-7; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1898; ditto, Barbados, 1901; treas., S. Rhodesia, 1903; ret., 1919; col. sec., 1st S. Rhodesian ministry, 1st Oct., 1923; high comsnr. for S. Rhodesia in London; ret., 1930.

NEWTON, ISAAC, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Clin. asst., Charing Cross hosp., 1924; 2nd health offr. at port, Hong Kong, 1925; visiting med. offr., Kwong Wah hosp., 1926; med. offr. in ch. Kowloon hosp., 1927; M.O.-in-ch., govt., civ. hosp., 1930; in ch., Univ. surg. clinic in adm., 1932; med. offr. in ch., Victoria hosp., 1932.
NEWTON, T. C., B.A.—B. 1882; ed. St. Peter's Schl., York and Queen's Coll., Oxford; asst. res., O. Nigeria, 1908; res., Dec., 1925.

NGATA, HON. SIR APIRANA TURUPA, KT. BACH (1927), M.A., LL.B.—B. 1874; ed. Waimatatini Native Schl., Te Aute Coll., and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; mem., house of reps., N.Z., since 1905; mem., exec. coun., repg. Maori race, 1909-12; min., native affrs., min., Cook Is., and mem., exec. coun. representing Maori race, 1928.

NICHOLAS, HARRY THEODORE, M.I. Struct. E.—B. 1882; ed. Bond's Gram. Schl., Plymouth; engr. constr., waterworks, Singapore, Jan., 1904; asst. engr., P.W.D., Perak, Aug., 1909; exec. engr., Jan., 1919; senr. exec. engr., Dec., 1926; state engr., Selangor, July, 1928; ag. dep. D.P.W. F.M.S., Jan. to July, 1930.

NICHOLLS, HON. SIR HERBERT, K.C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1868; LL.B., Tasmania; called to the bar, 1892; atty.-gen. and min. of educn., Tasmania, 1903-4; puisne judge, Tasmania, Jan., 1909; chief justice, 1914; also lieut.-gov.

NICHOLLS, LUCIUS.—B. 1884; L.S.A. Lond., M.D., B.C., B.A. Cantab.; pathologist to seamen's hosp. soc., and bacteriologist to post-graduate schl. of clinical med., 1907-8; stationed in W. Indies, 1909-12, as bacteriologist to govt. of St. Lucia and surg. to Victoria hosp.; in E. Africa, 1913-16; dir., bact. institute, Ceylon, Oct., 1915; ag. med. supt. lun. asylum, in addition to his own duties, May, 1916 to Feb., 1917.

NICHOLS, JAMES EDWARD.—B. 1882; asst. engr., Lagos rly., Feb., 1907; asst. engr., Gold Coast rlys. (open lines), May, 1911; offr. in charge, Togoland Mily. rlys., 1916-17; ch. engr., G. Coast rlys., Jan., 1920.

NICHOLS, RAYMOND, A.M.Inst.T., F.R.G.S.—B.1894; ed. Harvey Grammar Schl., Folkestone and privately; war serv., E. Kent Yeomanry (R.E.K.M.R.), 2/4th "The Buffs" (East Kent Regt.) and R.F.C., 1914-19; motor instr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, June, 1920; ag. transport offr., Feb., 1922; transf'd, motor transport dept., Aug., 1922; dep. ch. mech. transport offr., Nov., 1922; ag. ch. mech. transport offr., 1922 and 1924; ch. transport offr., Oct., 1924.

NICHOLSON, WILLIAM EWART, B.A.—B. 1890; ed., Leeds Grammar Schl., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, Sept., 1914; attached, Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; instr., arts and crafts, Jan., 1920.
NICOL, ANDREW, A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I. Struct.E.—B. 1898; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schl. and Robert Gordon's Tech. Coll., Aberdeen; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921.
NICOL, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1880; joined S.S. pol., Jan., 1906; ch. inspr., cts., May, 1914; coroner, S'pore, May, 1923; ch. inspr., cts., Mar., 1924; asst. supt., pol., S.S., Nov., 1928.

NICOLL, MAJOR FREDERICK ALAN BENSON, O.B.E., R. of O. (1921).—B. 1883; ed. St. Edward's Schl., Oxford; Imp. Yeo. S. African War, 1900-02; Natal mtd. pol., 1903-09; Natal native rebellion, 1906; dist. supt., pol., Uganda, 1910; R. Sussex Regt., 1914-20 (France, Belgium and Palestine, army of occupation, Germany, and plebiscite troops, Danzig); ment. in desp.; O.B.E. (mil. div.); Uganda pol., 1924; dep. commr., pol. and prisons, Tanganyika Territory, 1929; ag. commr., pol. and prisons, Apr.-Nov., 1930; comsnr., pol., Oct., 1933.

NIGHTINGALE, L. M.—B. 1875; ed. Wimbledon Collegiate Schl. and Soham Gram. Schl., Camb.; asst. to Mr. W. H. Thomas, M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., of Westminster, 1892 to 1901; contractors' engrn., Bermuda harbour wks., 1901-1906; 3rd cls. offr., lands and mines dept., B. Guiana, 1908; sworn land survr.; dist. engrn., West Coast, Demerara, Jan., 1915; inspr., dists., 1926; dist. commr., Essequibo, 1932.

NIHILL, J. H. B., M.A., M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Felsted and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. hist. tripods, pts. I and II; pres., Union Soc., 1914; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; 2nd lieut., Munster Fusiliers, 1914-18; investigating offr., Min. of Lab., joint industrial couns. divn., 1919; priv. sec. to Sir Wm. Edge, junr. Lord of Treas., 1920-21; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1921; ag. asst. crown solr., 1924; ag. dep. regis. and dep. offi. recvr., 1925; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1926; ag. asst. atty. gen., 1926; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1927; legal sec. to high commr., Iraq, 1927-31; ag. Br. cons., Baghdad in addn., 1928 and 1929; solr.-gen., Uganda, 1934.
NIXON, FRANK ASHLEY.—B. 1879; Transvaal civ. serv., 1902; Union civ. serv., 1910; ch. clk., dept. of mines and industries, 1918; under-secr., dept. of mines and industries, 1923.

NIXON, R., M.B., Ch.B. (L'pool), D.T.M. (L'pool), D.P.H. (L'pool).—Tempy. lieut., capt. and major, R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1921; senr. sanitation offr., 1928.

NOAD, JOHN ELLIOTT MONCHIEFF.—Ed. R. Naval Coll., Osborne and Dartmouth and Training Cruiser, H.M.S. "Cornwall"; midshipman, R.N., Aug., 1910; H.M.S. "Prince of Wales," Flagship, Atlantic Fleet; resgd., 1911; Edinburgh Univ., Oct., 1911 to Aug., 1914; sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., Aug., 1914 to Nov., 1918; interned in Holland from fall of Antwerp to armistice; while in Holland passed for A.M.I.C.E., Feb., 1917; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Kenya, Mar., 1920; asst. engrnr., Kenya and Uganda rly., Apr., 1924; senr. asst. engrnr., P.W.D., 1930; "1914" Star, War and Victory meda.

NOALL, W. E.—B.1880; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., June, 1898; promoted, with gratuity, Jan., 1911; conf. clk., 1st Mar., 1916; minor staff clk., 8th Sept., 1917; sec. to visual instr. comttee, 1st Jan., 1908.
NOBLE, G. S. P., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), Certif. Lond. S.H.T.M. (distincte).—B. 1897; mil. serv., 1916-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

NOBLE, PETER, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Elgin and Aberdeen, Scotland; asst. civ. engrnr., P.W.D., Cyprus, 1906; ag. dist. engrnr. in 1907 and 1909; dist. engrnr., Demerara, B. Guiana, Aug., 1910; ag. in addition as chief draughtsman, 1911, and as asst. col. civ. engrnr. in 1912; col. engrnr. and survr.-gen., Dominica, Aug., 1912; nom. mem., Roseau Town Board, offl. mem. legis. coun. and road board; on war service as mem. commrs. of valuation (min. of munitions); recd. thanks of sec. of state for work in connection with roads of Dominica, 1916; dist. engrnr., N. dist., Palestine, Aug., 1922.

NOOTT, P. GORDON.—B. 1880; entered army (Royal Welsh Fus.), 1898; served in Boer war, 1899-00 (Queen's medal with clasp); Tugela heights, relief of Ladysmith, Cape Colony, Orange Free State and Transvaal; seconded for serv., N. Nigeria regt., Dec., 1904; served with Dakka Kerri expdn., N. Nigeria, 1908; ret. on pension, Oct., 1913; ch. warder, Belize prison, B. Honduras, May, 1914; comm., 2nd lieut., B. Honduras Torr. Force, 1914; lieut., 1916 served on Cayo expdn., B. Honduras, 1916; instructor to 2nd B. Honduras contingent, 1916; capt., 1918; supt. of prisons, Grenada, Jan., 1920.
NORRIS, C. G.—B. 1890; ed. St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; cadet, Solomon Islands, Jan., 1915.
NORRIS, J. A.—B. 1872; ed. Ballygan and Williamstown, Ireland; acot. to treas., Victoria, Mar., 1914; audr. gen., Mar., 1919.

NORRIS, JOHN WILSON A.C.G.I., A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1890; ed. Lond. Univ. Imp. Coll. Sc. and Technology; active service B.K.F. (France) 1914-19, major R.E., mentioned in despatches; joined C.A. as engr. inspr., 1919; asst. engr., 1920; visited Gold Coast 1924; services loaned to consult. engr. reporting on Takoradi Harbour; seconded for duty as asst. engr. Gold Coast harbour staff, Takoradi 1924; rejoined C.A. 1926; dep. to ch. insp. engr., 1929.

NORTH, ROLAND AURIE CHARLES, R.A. (Oxon).—B. 1889; ed. Blundell's and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1913; seconded for serv. with Samoa govt., 1921; studied Japanese in Japan, 1922; 2nd ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1923; ch. ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1924 and 1930; sec. for Chinese affrs. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1927 and 1931; external ast. col. sec., Feb., 1931; 2nd ast. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., June, 1931; prin. ast. col. sec. and clk., couns., June, 1933.

NORTHCOTE, GEOFFREY ALEXANDER STAFFORD, C.M.G. (1931).—Ed. at Blundell's Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. (honours); and News and Huish exhibnr.; ast. collr., E. Africa Prot., May, 1904; ag. collr. and ag. dist. commr., 3. Kavirondo, 1904-06; polit. offr., Kisil Patrol, 1906; polit. offr., sleeping sickness commr., 1906-08; dist. commr., 1909; in charge, Kikuyu Prov., 1911; ag. prov. commr., May to Oct., 1919; ag. ast. col. sec., Oct., 1919 to July, 1920; ast. col. sec., Apr., 1921; ag. col. sec., July to Sept., 1921; Aug. to Sept., 1922 and Mar. to Sept., 1923; gov't. dep., July to Aug., 1923; mem., legis. coun., 1923; ag. col. sec. in 1925 and 1926; ch. sec. N. Rhodesia, Feb., 1927; mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1927; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1930; ag. gov't., 1930, 1931 and 1932.

NORTHCOTE, MAJOR LEONARD AUGUSTUS STAFFORD.—B. 1879; ed. Dulwich Coll.; 2nd lieut., Royal Fusiliers, 1900; res. of offr., Feb., 1914; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Salonika and Palestine, 1914-19; local commdt. of pol. and ast. commr., Cyprus, 1919; passed exam. in mod. Greek; ag. commr., for various periods 1924-27 and 1929-33.

NORTHCOTE, R. C.—Ed. Blundells and S. African Sch. of Mines and Technology, Johannesburg; Rhodesian admintr., R.S.A. pol., 1914-16; Rhodesian Native Regt., 1917-19; German E. Africa campaign (ment. in dep. mily. med.); ast. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1921; dist. offr., Feb., 1932.

NORTHCROFT, LESLIE ARTHUR, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. to secretariat, Jan., 1924; dist. Puttalum kach., Jan., 1925; ditto, N'Elkira kach., July, 1925; office ast., Jaffna kach., May, 1926; temp. ast., C.O. Sept., 1928 to Mar., 1929; addnl. ast. col. treas., Apr., 1929; ag. ast. govt. agt., Matara, Oct., 1929; ast. stltm. offr., Nov., 1929.

NORTHHEY, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR EDWARD, G.C.M.G. (1922), K.C.M.G. (1918), C.B.—F. 1863; served with Hazara expdn., 1891; Miranzai expdn., 1891; Isazai expdn., 1892; in S. African war, 1899-02; and in the war of 1914-18; extra A.D.C. to H.M. the King, 1915; brig. gen. (temp.) commdg. Nyassaland Rhodesian field force, 1915-18; lieut.-col. (brevet col.), 1915; King's R.C.C., 1911; gov'. R. Africa Prot. and high commr., Zanzibar Prot., July, 1918; assumed govt., Jan., 1919; ret., Aug., 1922.

NORTH-HUNT, HAROLD.—B. 1884; ed. Ipswich Sch. and St. Catharine's Coll., Cam bridge (exhibr.); war serv., 1914-20; (ment. is desps.); cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; 2nd ast. dist. offr., July, 1920; ast. dist. offr., Jan., 1921; 2nd ast. adviser, B. Pahat, Johore, Apr., 1922; offr., cls. IV, Aug., 1925; dist. offr., Limp.
NORTHMORE, HON. SIR JOHN ALFRED, K.C.M.G. (1932), LL.B.—B. 1865; judge, sup. ct. W. Australia, 1914; ch. just., 1931; lieut-gov., 1932.

NORTON, CUTHBERT HUGH.—B. 1787; ed. pte. schls. and Weymouth Coll.; ent. gov't. ser., Br. Guiana, 1913; asst. comsry., taxation, 1914; 3rd gde. do., 1917; 2nd gde. do., 1920; 1st gde. do., 1927; seconded as census comsrr., Sept., 1930 to Dec., 1931; 1st gde. offr., dist. adminstr., 1932; ag. ch. comsry. and dist. comsrr., Georgetown and East Bank, Demerara dist., 1933.

NORTON, HENRY JOHN STEPHEN, M.B.E. (1934).—B. 1896; cler. asst., Gibraltar, 1913; crane and wharf clk., 1914; treas. clk., 1914; passed compt. exam., 1917; clk.-in-charge, impta. and expts., and clk. to food contr., 1914 to 1920; dep. asst. shipping master, 1921; asst. shipping master, 1922; sec. and treas., sailors' home, 1920; clk-in-charge port dept. and ag. nat. agrt., in 1924-26; asst. sec., col. sec's. office, 1927; pvt. sec. and interpreter to gov., 1929.

NORVAL, WILLEM DIXON.—B. 1893; ed. Wepener, O.F.S.; clk., dept. of lands, 1913; clk., dept. of educn. (Union), 1919; pvt. sec. to min. of interior, educn. and pub. health, 1924; senr. clk., dept. of agr., 1926; mining comsrr., Namaqualand, 1929; do., Barberton, 1930; do., Barberton and Pilgrims Rest, 1931; under-sec. for lab., 1932.
NORWOOD, LEONARD ROBERT OSWALD.—B. 1896; ed. Lincoln Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, 2nd Nov., 1921.

NOSWORTHY, RICHARD, C.M.G. (1926).—Ed. Christ's Hosp.; ent. Jamaica civ. service, 1880; held appts. in various depts.: 1st cls. clk., col. secretariat, 1905; sec., bd. of supervision for poor relief, 1905-09; supervisor of rev. and valuation comsrr., 1907-18; ag. collr. gen. and contr., widow's and orphan's fund; chmn., marine bd. on several occasions; asst. col. sec., 1918; ag. col. sec., 1921; Island treas. and man., govt. savings bank, 1921; chmn., agrl. loan bd., chmn., currency comsrr., man., civ. service guarantee assn.; sent to America to conclude purchase of Jamaica govt. rly. bonds, 1912, and as mem. of banana comsrr., 1915; censor, 1915-19; ag. admstr., Dominica, 1922.

NOSWORTHY, HON. SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1867; ed. privately and at Boys High Sch., Christchurch, N.Z.; mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1908-28; jun. whip for reform party and later sen. whip; sen. whip for Nat. gov't.; min. of agr., 1919-26; min. of immigr., 1920-28; min. of finance and stamp duties, 1925-26; P.M.G. and min. of tels., 1926; min. of external affrs., 1926-28.

NOTT, CHARLES ROBERT HARLEY, M.A.—B. 1904; ed. Marlborough and Christ's Coll., Camb.; cadet, Fiji and W. Pacific services, Mar., 1926; col. sec.'s office, Fiji, Apr., 1926; passed cadet, Mar., 1928; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Mar., 1928; ag. A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., 1930; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1931; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., 1932.

NOVAR, VISCOUNT (cr. 1921). RIGHT HON. SIR RONALD CRAUFORD MUNRO-FERGUSON, P.C.(1910), G.C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1860; Hon. LL.D. of St. Andrew's 1910; ed. Sandhurst; late lieut., Grenadier Guards; M.P. for Ross and Cromarty, 1884-85; Leith Burghs, 1886-1914; priv. sec. to Lord Rosebery, 1886 and 1893-95; lord of the treasury, 1894; provost of Kirkcaldy, 1906-1914; vice-lieu tenant of Fife; was a referee of Bills; gov'.gen., C. of A., 1914-20; sec. for Scotland, Oct., 1922 to Jan., 1924.

NOWELL, WILLIAM, C.B.E., D.I.C., F.L.S.—B. 1880; ed. munici. tech. schlr., Halifax; R. Coll. of Sci., Lond.; on mily. serv., S. Africa, 1900; asst. supt., agr., Barbados, 1911; mycologist and agrl. lecturer, Imp. Dept. of Agr. for W. Indies, 1913; asst. dir., agr., Trinidad, 1920; mem., agrl. relief comtce.; original mem., bd. of man., govt. agrl. bank; mem., advisory bd., Emp. Cotton Growing Corp., cotton research stn.; dir., sci. and agr., Br. Guiana, 1926; dir., E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

NUNAN, SIR JOSEPH JOHN, KT. BACH. (1924), K.C.—Ed. Univ. Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; fellow of roy. univ. of Ireland, Blake hist. schlr. and 1st vice-chancellor's prizeman, Dublin Univ.; 1st mod. language schlr., roy. univ., Ireland; Bacon schlr., Gray's Inn; gold medallist in history, literature and composition; won exhibtn. and cert. of honour at honour examn. for call to the bar, King's Inn, 1898; H.M.'s vice-consul, and ch. judicial offr., B.C. Africa Prot., Oct., 1899; judge of high ct., B.C.A., Aug., 1902; also mem. of H.B.M. ct. of appeal for Eastern Africa; draughtsman, Brit. Cent. Africa Prot., 1900-5; pres., native land comsrr., Mar.-July, 1903; specially employed in connection with native disturbances, Cent. Angoniland, Jan.-Feb., 1901, and at other times specially employed; solr.-gen., B. Guiana, 1906; ag. puisne judge, Mar. to July, 1906; ag. atty.-gen., July, 1906, to Feb., 1907; ag. special puisne judge, Nov., 1906, atty.-gen., B. Guiana, 1912; comsrr. to revise B. Guiana statute law, June, 1919; in charge of B. Guiana delegation to C.O. and I.O., July-Sept., 1919, and to Indian Gov't., Nov., 1919 to Feb., 1920; asst. British del., Reparation Coman., Vienna, June, 1920 to May, 1921; ag. British rep., Nov., 1920 to Apr., 1921; senr. mem. on sp. comtce. for repartition of Austrian and Hungarian national debts, Nov., 1921; in charge of B. Guiana mission to C.O. and I.O. and to India, 1923-24; ret., 1925.
NUNAN, P. F., M.B., Bac.-Surg., M.D.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1913; senr. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1924; do., Kenya 1925.

NUNN, GEOFFREY NOEL NAPIER.—Ed. Bradfield and Magdalene Coll., Camb.; B.A. (hons.); cadet, Gold Coast, July, 1929; asst. dist. comsrr., 1930; seconded, Gambia, Dec., 1931; ag. comsrr., 1932; 2nd lieut. R.A.R.O., July, 1929.

NUNN, MAJOR REGINALD LEWIS, D.S.O., M.Inst.C.E., M.Inst.W.E.—B.1892; ed. Brighton Coll.; waterks. engnr., Trinidad and Tobago, Apr., 1921; res. engnr., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1924; asst. dir., P.W.D., Nigeria, Feb., 1927; dep. col. engnr., P.W.D., S.S., Aug., 1930; dep. D.P.W., S.S., Jan., 1932.

NUTMAN, FREDERICK JOHN, A.R.C.S., M.Sc., D.I.C.—B. 1904; ed. Newton Abbot and R. Coll. of Sci., Lond.; timber mycologist, forest products research lab., Oxford, 1926; plant physiologist, E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

OAKES, ARTHUR PODMORE, M.M., R. O. OF.—B. 1892; Europ. War, 1914-18; N. Staffordshire Regt., 1912; Northamptonshire Regt., 1917-28; K.A.R., Nyasaland and Somaliland, 1921-26; pay and qtrmr., Somaliland pol., 1926; dep. comtce., pol. and dir., prisons, 1928.

OAKLEY, PHILIP DOUGLAS, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. (Lpool).—B. 1883; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, Jan., 1911; senr med. offr., July, 1928; asst. dir., med. serv., Aug., 1928; dep. dir., Feb., 1923; dir., med. and sanry. services, Sierra Leone, 1933.

O'BRIEN, ARTHUR JOHN RUSHTON, C.M.G. (1929), M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.P.H., B.Hy. (hons.), (Durham), Certif., L.S.T.M., B.1883; joined W.A.M.S., 1911; served in Great War, 1915-19; awarded M.C. and bar, twice ment. in desp., and recvd. thanks of S. of S. for services in Cameroons; surg. specialist, Gold Coast, 1921; R.M.O., Gold Coast hospital, 1923; sec. to C.A.M.S. comtes., Dec., 1929; visited West Indies at request of S. of S., 1930; assumed duty as sec., C.A.M.S., comtes., June, 1930.

O'BRIEN, BRYAN JUSTIN.—B. 1902; ed. Uppingham and Queen's Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. hons. mods.; 2nd cls. lit. hum.; asst. sec., col. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Apr., 1927; ag. clk., exec. coun., for various periods, 1927-31; clk., exec. coun., Aug., 1931; ag. ch. asst. sec. for various periods, 1927-33; as. asst. commr., Jan. to Mar., 1928; joint edtr. Handbook of Cyprus, 1930 edn.

O'BRIEN, LT.-COL. SIR C. R. M., K.C.M.G. (1920); C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1869; ed. Felsted, on the Continent, and Royal Milly. Coll. (passed out with honos.); entrd. Army as 2nd lieut., 1878; lieut., 1880; capt., 1887; major, 1897; brev.-lt.-col., 1902; 2nd in command of 1st E. Lancs. regt., Oct., 1902; served in India, 1890-96; retrd. on pension in order to remain under C.O., Mar., 1903; served in S. African War, Jan., 1900, to end of campaign; pres. of mil. tribunal, 1900-01; S. African War med. and 3 clasps, King's med. and 2 clasps (twice ment. in desp.); senr. dep. commr. Transvaal town pol., J.F. for the Witwatersrand, and in charge of mtd. pol., May, 1901; in charge of martial law arrangements, Johannesburg and Rand, and personal asst. to commr., 1901-02; dep. commr., headqrs., Transvaal town pol., 1905-08; dep. commr., Transvaal town pol.; retrenched, 1908; col. sec., Gambia, Jan., 1910; ag. gov., Gambia, in 1910, 1911 and 1912; gov., Seychelles, Nov., 1912; gov., Barbados, June, 1918; assumed govt., Sept., 1918; ret., Sept., 1926; selected by S. of S. as sp. comm. to rept. on constitution and organisation of Freetown municipality, Sierra Leone, May to July, 1926.

O'CONNOR, ALEXANDER JAMES, Dipl.Forestry (Cape).—Clk., conserv. of forest's office, W. conservancy, 1903; forester, Uitvlugt, W. conservancy, 1905; asst. dist. forest offr., Tokai, 1908; seconded to gen. coun., Transkei, 1908; asst. dist. forest offr., Knysna, 1909; ditto, working plans, Midland conservancy, 1910; ditto, Woodbush, Transvaal conservancy, 1910; dist. forest offr., Woodbush, 1918; ditto, Pietersburg, 1924; ag. conserv., forests, Natal conservancy, 1926; 2nd grade conserv. of forests, Natal conservancy, 1927; prin. forest offr. (forestry inspr.) Transvaal and O.F.S., 1932.

O'CONNOR, CHARLES ANTHONY.—B. 1883; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; supt., Reduit gardens and plantations, forests and gardens dept., Mauritius, Sept., 1904; ch. overseer, dept. of agr., Aug., 1913; enlisted, 2nd Artists' Rifles, Aug., 1917; recl. commn., Royal Munster Fus., Sept., 1918; inspr. of plantations, Zanzibar Prot., Aug., 1919; agr. instr. and inspr., co-op. credit societies, Mauritius, Jan., 1923.

O'CONNOR, HUGH JOSEPH, F.Z.S.—B. 1887; Lieut., Gambia Artillery (V.), 1910; mem. of navigation and pilotage board, Gambia, 1914; asst. commr. of police, G. Coast, Jan., 1916; ag. staff instructor, Mar., 1916, and June, 1918; ag. commr. of police for various periods in 1917, 1919 and 1922; O.C. Togoland mil. police, and O.C. Togoland prisons, 19th Aug., 1919; capt. G. Coast Rifles, 1922; commr. of police, Jan., 1923.

O'FARRELL, EDWARD JOSEPH.—Ed. Bevedere Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; supt. ct., Ireland, 1905; dep. regiar., sup. c. Kenya, 1924; dep. offl. recr. for Coast Prov. ma ex-officio agt. at Mombasa for pub. trustee, Keny. 1930.
OGG, ROBERT CUTHBERT, M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1901; rel. med. offr., Basutoland, 1927; med. offr., 1929.
OGILVIE, J.—B. 1886; ed. Forde and Banffshire and Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; war serv., 1914-19; asst. engrnr., Nigeria 1919; divnl. engrnr., 1928.

O'GRADY, CAPT. SIB JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1929), J.P.—B. 1866; mem., town coun., Bristol, 1899; M.P., E. Leeds, 1906; S.E. Leeds, 1924; with troops in various theatres of wr. 1915-18; sent by govt. on sp. mission to India 1917; negotiated with Russian Soviet gov't exchange of Br. prisoners of war and repatriation of Br. civilians in Russia, Nov. 1918; gov't of Tasmania, 1924; gov't., and commr. in-ch. Falkland Is. and Dependencies, June, 1937.

O'GRADY, WILLIAM MICHAEL, B.L.—B. 1856; ed. Wesley Coll., Dublin, admitted sol., Ireland 1908; mily. serv., Egypt, 1916; Salonika, 1916; Palestine, 1917-18; Egypt, 1919; called to bar, Ireland, 1921; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1926; ag. puimne judge, July, 1927.

OKE, HARRIS RENDELL, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. Bishop Feild Coll., St. John's, Newfoundland; studt.-at-law, 1909-14; The Royal Newfoundland Regt., 1914-15; The Royal Scots, 1915-20; served France, 1916-18 (M.C. and ment. in despatches); served N. Russian Relief Force, 1919-20; M.C.; admative offr., Nigeria, 1920; seconded to admin. serv., the Gambia, 1932.
OLDFIELD, H. G.—Tempy. asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1919.

OLIPHANT, JOHN NINLAN, M.B.E.—B. 1876; ed. Glasgow Coll. and Christ Ch., Oxford; B.A. and dipl., forestry, 1909; imp. forest serv., India, 1909-23; dep. conservator, forests, Honduras, Dec., 1923; conservator, forests, 1924; dep. dir., forestry, F.M.S. and S.S., 1929.

OLIVER, GEO. A.—B. 1888; ent. G.P.O., Dec., 1914; apptd. asst. clk., Apr., 1916; agt. less operator, R.N.V.R., Jan., 1917 to Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf. to CO June, 1923; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Confce., Ottawa, 1932.
OLIVER, WALTER REGINALD BROCK, M.S.—B. 1883; ed. Taunanga High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. cust. dept., 1906; Dom. museum staff, 1920; dir., 1928; fmr. N. Z. Inst.

OLIVIER, 1ST BARON (created 1924). Rt Hon. Sir SYDNEY OLIVIER, P.C. (1924), K.C.M.G. (1907), C.M.G. (1898), B.A.—B. 1859; ed. Cambridge schl.; open exhibtr., Corpus Christi, Oxford; 2nd class classical mods., 1878; class in final classical schl., 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. in the office of S. of S. for cols., 17th Apr., 1882; fmr. mentioned for Cobden prize essay, Oct., 1883; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1891; ag. auditor-gen., Leeward Is., 1895, to Feb., 1896; priv. sec. to the late Selborne, Sept., 1896; sec. to the Roy... to inquire into condition of W. Indies, Dec., 1896; 1st class clk., June, 1897; sent to Washington on sp. serv. in connect. with W. Indian reciprocity negotiations, 1898; col. sec., Jamaica, 1900-1904; ag. gov., 1900, 1902, and 1904; returned to C.O. as a prin. clk., Oct., 1904; gov. of Jamaica, Apr., 1907; assumed govt., May, 1907; represented West Indian Colonies at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; perm. sec., bd. of agriculture and fisheries, 1913; mem. of comte. for settm. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; asst. compt. and audr., exchequer and audit dept., 1917; S. of S. for India, 23rd Jan. to 7th Nov., 1924.

O'MAY, LILY STEWART, M.B., Ch.B. (St. Andrews, 1909).—B. 1883; med. offr., Warren's hosp., K. Kangsar, June, 1913; grade II, May, 1914; med. offr., girls' schls., Penang, P.W. and Dindings, and i/c women and children's clinic, maternity hosp., Penang, Feb., 1926; i/c women and children's disp., Kandang Kerbau, Singapore, Oct., 1929; med. offr., Malacca, June, 1930; seconded for serv. under govt., Kedah, May, 1931.

O'MORCHOE, THE (CAPT. ARTHUR DONEL MACMURROGH).—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined Leinster Regt. (Royal Meaths), Aug., 1914; served in the great war; regtl. signalling offr., 1915; asst. adjt., 1916-17; A.D.C. to maj. gen. Hon. E. J. Montague Stuart-Wortley and camp commdt., 65th divn., 1917-18; provost marshal's dept., 1918-22; junr. pol. offr., Zanzibar, Jan., 1924; pub. proc., 1924-25; supt., pol., Feb., 1926; ag. supt., C.I.D., Nov., 1925 to May, 1926; adjt., 1928; ag. asst. commdt., 1930; A.D.C. to Br. rex., Aug., 1930; ptye. sec., May, 1931; comsnr., pol., Gold Coast, 1934.

ONRAET, RENÉ HENRY DE SOLMINIHAC.—B. 1857; pol. probationer, S. Stiltnitz, Dec., 1907; passed final exam. in Hokkien, July, 1910; passed final exam. in Malay, Aug., 1911; asst. sup't. of pol., July, 1912; ag. sup't. of pol., Singapore, Jan., 1919; asst sup't. of pol., Singapore, Mar., 1919; supt., pol. and dir. crim. intell., S.S., Mar., 1922; ch. pol. offr., S'pore, Dec., 1925; dir., crim. intell., S.S., Oct., 1926; dep. comsnr., pol., Perak, F.M.S., Oct., 1931; ag. inspr. gen., pol., S.S., Mar., 1932.
OORLOOF, CHARLES JAMES, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1930; attd., secretariat, Oct., 1930; attd., Kalutara Kach., July, 1931; office asst., Hambantota Kach., Oct., 1932.

O'REILLY, JAMES GALLUS.—B. 1883; ed. Hughes' Acad., Belfast; R.I.C., May, 1902; inspr., pol., F.M.S., Dec., 1910; inspr., wghts. and mrs., Aug., 1911-Dec., 1912; supvr., inspr., F.M.S., Sept., 1921; supt., cust. prev. br., F.M.S., Oct., 1923; supvr., ext. duties, F.M.S., Prai and S'pore, Nov., 1925 and Jan., 1927; ch. supt., prev. br., cust. dept., F.M.S., Sept., 1927; asst. sup't., govt. monops., S'pore, June, 1930; dep. comsnr., trade and cust., N. Sembilan, Feb., 1932; ditto, Perak, Nov., 1932.

ORMSBY-GORE, Rt. Hon. WILLIAM GEORGE ARTHUR, P.C. (1927).—B. 1885; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford (B.A.); late capt., Shropshire Yeomanry; intell. officer Arab bureau, 1916-17; asst. sec., War Cabinet, 1917-18; asst. pol. offr., Palestine, 1918; British mem., Permanent Mandates Coman., League of Nations, 1921-22; M.P., Stafford since Jan., 1910; accompanied C.O. mission to British West Indies, 1921-22; partly, under sec. of state for Colonies, 1st Nov., 1922 to 23rd Jan., 1924 and since Nov., 1924; chmn., partly, comen. to E. Africa, 1924; apptd. by S. of S. to visit and rept. on W. African dependencies, Jan., 1926.

ORR, SIR CHARLES WILLIAM JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1928), C.M.G. (1921).—B. 1870; ed. Bath Coll. and Woolwich Acad.; 1st comsn., Royal Artillery, Feb., 1889; capt., Sept., 1899; maj., 1904; ret., Sept., 1908, served with Chitral relief force, 1895 (medal with clasp); Indian frontier, 1897 (clasp); Tirah expdn. force, 1897-99 (clasp); China war, 1900-01 (medal); S. African war, 1900-02 (medal, 2 clasps); 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Feb., 1903; 2nd cls. res., 1904; 1st cls. res., 1908; ag. sec. to admnstr., N. Nigeria, May to Sept., 1907; ch. sec., Cyprus, Feb., 1911; adminstr. govt., July to Oct., 1911, and Apr. to Aug., 1913; ret. on pension on account of ill health, Feb., 1917; attd. W.O., Int. branch, 1918-19; col. sec., Gibraltar, June, 1919; ag. gov., on several occasions; gov. and commdr.-in-ch. of the Bahamas, 1926-1931; ret.

OSBORNE, MAJOR D. R.—B. 1879; ed. Prince Alfred Coll., Adelaide, S. Australia, Adelaide Univ. (mental and moral phil.) and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., law; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1909; practiced at bar until 1914; on mily. serv., 1914-19; ot. martial offr., Nov., 1918 to May, 1920; in Ireland as A.L.O. until Apr., 1922; stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas, June, 1923; ag. ch. just., Aug. to Sept., 1923 and from July to Nov., 1924; ag. inspr. in addn. June to July, 1924.

OSTLER, HON. HENRY HUBERT, K.C. (1925), LL.B. (1904).—B. 1876; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; assoc. to New Zealand ch. just., 1903-06; edr., N.Z. Law Repts., 1906-10; lat. ast., crown law office and crown prod., 1910; ag. solr.-gen., 1913; in private practice, 1915-25; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1925; examr. in law for N.Z. Univ. for many years; fellow N.Z. Univ. senate, 1916-20.

O'SULLIVAN, TIMOTHY ADOLPHUS, M.A., M.R.S.T.—B. 1894; ed. King's Hosp. and Trinity coll., Dublin; ast. mast., col. for blind, Worcester, St. Christophers, Eastbourne, Ranelagh sch., Athlone, 1913-15; Inns Ct. O.T.C. and R.A., 1915-19; ast. mast., Raffles inst., Singapore, 1919; examr., Malay, Penang, 1926; impnr., scha., Selangor, 1929; senr. educn. offr. (A.), S.S., 1931; inspr., scha., Penang, Nov., 1931.

OSWELL, HENRY THEODORE WARREN.—B. 1896; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; war serv., 1915-18; cadet, F.M.S., April, 1920; passed cadet, June, 1922; offr., class V., Apr., 1923; ast. dist. offr., T. Malim, Feb., 1926; ast. contrlr., lab., Seremban, Aug., 1926; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1927; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Nov., 1929; prot. lab., Kedah, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1932.

OTWAY, J. H.—Clk., treasy., Grenada, 1897; 2nd audit clk., 1899; audit clk., St. Vincent, 1901; audit clk., St. Lucia, Jan., 1916; sub coll. and dist. offr., Soufriere; J.P. for the col.; cable censor, St. Vincent, 1918; ch. clk., govt. office, and clk. of couns., St. Vincent, 1918; ag. ch. clk. and ptye. sec. to gov., Grenada, 1923; col. treas., contr., cust. and ch. of excise, St. Vincent, 1923; ag. admnstr. in 1932 and 1933.

OWEN, ALBERT EDWARD.—Asst. acot. N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1915; attd., lands dept., Oct., 1915 to May, 1922; treasy., May, 1922; ch. clk., dept., agr., Jan., 1927; treasy., Apr., 1930; ag. dep. treas., in 1928, 1929 and 1931; contr., stores and transport, Dec., 1932.

OWEN, A. H., B.A. (Camb.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. (Camb.), Cert. of S.T.M. (Lond.)—Med. offr., Uganda, Oct., 1912; sp. serv. (venereal diseases), Uganda, 1912; active serv., E.A. Campaign, and tempy. capt., U.M.S., Jan., 1915 to May, 1918; ment. in despa.; senr. med. offr., Uganda, Jan., 1921; sen. sanry. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; dep. dir., sanry. serv., 1928; dir., med. and sanry. services, 1932.

OWEN, GEORGE DOUGLAS.—B. 1887; ed. Highgate Schi. (Eng.); entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, 16th Jan., 1907; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1915; asst. sec., local defence comms., 1914-1915; ag. sec., ditto, 1915-1916; sec. to educn. comm., June, 1914 to Nov., 1916; sec., West Indian Court of Appeal Coms., 1916; priv. sec. to Sir S. W. Knaggs in 1910, 1912 and 1916; to Sir G. R. Le Hunte, 1913-1915; to Sir J. R. Chancellor, 1916, 1920 and 1921; served with H.M. Forces, 1916-19; ch. and conf. clk., col. secretariat, and clk. of legis. coun., May, 1920; priv. sec. to Sir T. A. V. Best, 1921 and to Sir S. Wilson, 1922-24; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec. for several periods since 1921; asst. col. sec., clk. to exec. coun., combined ot. and ot. of policy, Br. Guiana, Feb., 1925; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1925-30; col. sec., Barbados, Sept., 1931; ag. gov. in 1932 and 1933.

OWEN, GEORGE HUMPHREY RICHARD ST. JOHN.—Cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1920; 2nd grade admtr., offr., July, 1929; seconded to C.O. as ast. prin., Feb. to Aug., 1929; dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1931; ag. ast. admtr.-gen., 1933.

OWEN, HAROLD ALBERT.—B. 1892; entd. crown agt.'s office, Dec., 1911; seconded as 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, July, 1916; sec. to comm. of enquiry into condition of St. John's fire brigade, Nov., 1916; returned to crown agt.'s office, Sept., 1919.

OWEN, JOHN HENRY, D.S.C. (1917).—Younger brother, Trinity House, London; master mariner; ed. Univ. Coll. Schi., London and H.M.S. "Worcester"; served in war, 1914-18; lieut. naval reserve; port mine-sweeping offr., Jamaica, Oct., 1918; harbmr. and naval agt., Kingston, Jamaica, Jan., 1919; harbmr., Malacca, 1927.

PAGE, RT. HON. EARLE CHRISTMAS GRAFTON, P.C., M.B., Ch. M. (Syd.).—B. 1880; ed. Sydney High Schi. and Sydney Univ. (George Allen Maths. Scholarship and John Harris Scholarship for anatomy and physiology); served in European War with A.I.F. as surg. specialist to No. 3 Australian casualty clearing station, France, and No. 3 Australian gen. hosp., Egypt; mayor of S. Grafton, N.S.W., 1918; pres., N. Coast Development League, 1918; el. mem., H. of R., C. of A., for Cowper, Dec., 1919; treas., C. of A., 1923-29; leader of country party in Fedl. parl., 1930.

PAGE, CAPT. JOHN CAMPBELL.—B. 1893; ed. King's Schi., Canterbury, and Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.); 2nd lieut., Buffs, Aug., 1914; on active serv. in Franc., 1915-19; M.C., Oct., 1916; passed progress exam. in Mendi; ast. dist. commsnr., S. Leone, Aug., 1916; ag. dist. commsnr., Moyamba dist., 1922; dist. commsnr., May, 1928.

PAGE, SIDNEY MAYNARD—Transvaal constab., Oct., 1900; clk., mgte. off., July, 1902; ast. mgte., Mar., 1913; mgte., Mar., 1918; pub. serv. inspr., Nov., 1924; addnl. mag., Pretoria, May, 1928; mag., Pietersburg, Sept., 1928; ditto, Durban, Feb., 1930; do., Johannesburg, Mar., 1933.

PAILTHORPE, WULFRED ARTHUR BOYTON.—B. 1875; ed. King Edward VI Schi., Chelsea, and Gray's Coll., Essex; serv., S. African War, 1899-1902 (Queen's med. and 6 clasps, King's med. and 2 clasps); registr. clk., E.A.P., 1914; reg. of docs., 1910; collr. under Stamp Act, 1911; ex-officio agt. for admtr.-gen., 1911; ag. pr. reg. of docs., crown lands, etc., 1915-19; controlling rev. authority, 1918; dep. reg. of titles, Kenya, 1919; prin. reg. of docs., crown lands and land titles, 1920.

PALETHORPE, RONALD FLINT.—B. 1891; ed. Hereford Schi. and Keble Coll., Oxford; ast. mast., St. George's Schi., Windsor Castle, 1913; ast. dist. commsnr., R.A.P., 5th Apr., 1914; ag. dist. commsnr., Kenya, 1926; rep. eq., 1929.

PALMER, HAROLD.—B. 1895; appd. to compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., appd. to national health insurance audit dept., 13th Jan., 1913; on military service, 2nd May, 1918, to 26th Feb., 1920; assigned to C.O. at Mar., 1920; second ast. acctnt., 5th Nov., 1920.

PALMER, SIR HERBERT RICHMOND, K.C.M.G. (1933). C.M.G., C.B.E.—Ed. Oundle Schi. and Trinity Hall, Camb. (class. schol.), M.A. LL.B.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1904; ass. res. N. Nigeria, Oct., 1904; commsnr., native rev. N. Nigeria, 1911; rev. mission to Anglo-Egyptian Sudan, 1912; supervisor, native rev., Nigeria, 1914; ag. res., Kano Prov., Nigeria, 1915-16; res. Port Prov., Nigeria, 1917; visited Anglo-Egyptian Sudan from Bornu via Wadai and Darfur in connection with educn. proposals, 1918; ag. hist. gov., N. Provs., Nigeria, May-Dec., 1921; ag. res., Sokoto Prov., Apr. to May, 1922; lieut. gov. N. Provs., Nigeria, May, 1925; gov., Gamla, 1926; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Cyprus, 1933.

PALMER, RICHARD HENRY, B.A. (Oxon.), B. 1881; ed. St. Cuthbert's Schi., Workington, and Keble Coll., Oxford; Cape civ. serv., 1903; dist. admtr.-gen., N.W. Rhodesia, Oct., 1905; ast. nat. commsnr., May, 1908; nat. commsnr., N. Rhodesia, Sept., 1911; ag. ast. mag., Mar., 1915; ast. mag., Apr., 1916; ag. res., nat. prov. dist. in 1924 and 1925; mag., July, 1927; prov. commsnr., Apr., 1929; senr. prov. commsnr., 1932.
PAMELLONE, ARMAND JOSEPH, M.S., Ch.B. Edin.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trin., 1st Feb., 1906; dist. med. offr., 1st Aug., 1915.

PANES, JOHN NELSON.—B. 1888; ed. Den. Close Schi. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (maths.); mast., King Edward VI's Schi., Northampton, 1914-22; ast. mast., King's Coll., Lagos, Nigeria, 1922; ast., educn., S.P., Nigeria, 1925; ag. instr., educn., 1930-31; prin., King's Coll., Lagos, 1931.

PANET, LIEUT.-COL. CHARLES LOUIS.—B. 1870; ed. Ottawa Univ.; ent. civ. serv., Canada, 1889; sec., Canadian defence comms., 1890; sec. to late Sir F. W. Borden, min. of militia and defence, 1904-12; sec., dept. of militia and defence from 1907; pres., bd. of enquiry, re claims by applicants for the fenian raid volunteer batt., 1912-15; pres. of pensions and claims bd., 1917; in deep, for serv. rendered in Can., 1914-1918.
PAPAFIO, M.K.O.—Ed. Accra Grammar Schi. and in England; 6th grade teacher, 1911; 7th grade do., 1912; 2nd cls. do., 1919; 1st cls., 1920; headmistress, 1921.
PAPATETROU, ANTONI, M.R.R.—B. 1878; clk., land regist., and survey dept., Cyprus, Oct., 1903; inspr., land regist., and survey dept., Apr., 1919; ast. regt. gen., Mi.

ARHAM, ARTHUR DOUGLAS, F.R.I.B.A., I., Chartered Archt. and Quantity Surv., Town Planning Inst.—B. 1890; ed. King's College, London; temp. architectural ass't, P.W.D., 1915; 1st asst. archt., 1918; ag. ch. archt., 1919; 1923, 1926, 1927 and 1929; add., town planning divn., Miny. of Health, 1926.

ARISH, GODFREY CHARLES BRIAN.—B. 1897; County Schl., Windsor; served in R.G.A.F.; 1915-19; granted rank of capt. on reclining commn.; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1921; ag. asst. col. sec., 1920; prte. sec. and dist. commr., 1922-24; dist. commr., 1924; col. sec., 1926; col. sec., Gambia, 1931; ag. r., Gambia, 1931 and 1933.

ARK, ALEXANDER DALLAS, C.M.G. (1932), A.N.Z., A.R.A.N.Z.—B. 1882; ed. Waitaki Schl., Waimate High Schl. and privately; pub. wks., dept., 1900-18; agr. dept., 1919; pub. serv. commn.'s office, 1920; sec. of line, 1922; asst. pub. serv. commn., 1923; asst. treas., 1925; sec., 1930 also financial adviser, 1931; served with N.Z. forces in S. Africa.
ARK, CAPT. EDWARD NIGEL, O.B.E. (1933), A.N.Z.—B. 1889; ed. Aldenham Schl.; Somaliland Naval Corp., 1917-20; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, 1920; dist. commr., Somaliland, Feb., 1922.

ARK, CAPT. L. S.—Coman., 7th Batt., Hampshire Regt., 1909; Coldstream Guards, 1914; on war ser., Uganda and India; 1913-18; acted by W.O. for serv. with Yemen Inf., 1919-20; cadet, Zanzibar pol., 1923-26; finan. advr. and comdt., pol., govt. of Bahrein, Persian Gulf, 1926-32 (twice acted as advr.); 1st grade impr., Mauritius pol., 1933.

ARKER, CECIL JOSEPH, M.C., M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; asst. engnr., Peru, 1910; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jan., 1911; lieut., R.E. (old Co.), 1915-19; served in France and Italy; 1st grad. in desps., 1917; M.C., 1918; war and navy med.; ex. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jan., 1919; sen. ex. engnr., Apr., 1927; state engnr., Jan., 1930.

ARKER, JAMES T.—Cert. teacher, Leeward Dist. postmtr., Bethesda, 1901; schlr., govt. s' reformatory schl., 1903; clk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., and clk. to road bd., Antigua, 1906; 1st grad. offr., Virgin Is., 1915; inspr. of lights and measures, Virgin Is., 1917.

ARKER, LANSDOWNE—B. 1879; clk., cust., Iburg, 1902; surr., Cape Town, 1915; served S.A. in Great War; cust. investigation offr., Union in New York, 1926; collr. cust. and piping mast., Port Elizabeth, 1930.
ARKHILL, ROBERT ARCHDALE.—Ed. to H.R., C'wealth of Australia, 1927; min. for nat. affrs. and transport, Lyons' ministry, 1932.

ARKINSON, A. C. C., M.A. (1910), C.M.G.
