THOMSON, ROBERT.—B. 1892; ed. Hig. Schl., Greenock and R. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; serv. with H.M. forces, 1914-19; asst. engnr., Zanzibar, June, 1926; ag. dist. engnr. on various occasions, 1927-31.

THOMSON, ROBERT LESLIE.—B. 1880; ed. Perth Acad.; asst. commissary, Br. Guiana 1907-20; served with H.M. Forces in France, 1914-18; asst. inspr., dists., 1925; 2nd gde. offr., dist. admstr., Jan., 1932.
THOMSON, WALTER MORRIS.—B. 1906; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Dec., 1928; passed cadet, Feb., 1931; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1931.

THORNE, RICHARD ALFRED.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., sec.'s office, G.P.O., 24th Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf. to C.O., 15th Oct., 1920; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 29th Oct., 1930.

THORNE, WILLIAM HUXTABLE.—B. 1883; ed. privately; solr., 1903; commanded 3rd Vol. Batt. Lincoln Regt., 1904; called to local bar of S.S., 1907 and of F.M.S., 1908; censor and mem. S.S. Defence Force, munic. comsrr., Penang, 1914; mem., leg. coun., S.S., 1920; capt., Penang. Voir. R. of O.; puisne judge, S.S., Aug., 1925; judge, F.M.S., Dec., 1926; judge, Johore, Mar., 1930.

THORNHILL, GEORGE KRAAL, F.S.I., F.R.G.S., F.R.A.S., F.R.Met.S.—B. 1884; supernym. survr., Ceylon, July, 1901; asst. surv. surveys, Jan., 1908; supt., surveys, 2nd grade, Sept., 1911; do., 1st grade, Oct., 1914; on mil. ser., 1918-19; asst. survr.gen., Apr., 1927; dep. survr-gen., Nov., 1930; survr.-gen., Sept., 1932.

THORNHILL, WILLIAM JOHNSON, M.I.C.E., A.R.T.C.—B. 1880; head overseer, P.W.D., Ceylon, Sept., 1899; inspr., Mar., 1900; dist. engnr., 3rd grade, Jan., 1902; 2nd grade, Mar., 1903; 1st grade, Dec., 1910; on mil. ser., 1915-19; prov. engnr., grade II, Oct., 1920; asst. D.P.W., Sept., 1923; dep. dir., pub. wks., Mar., 1929; D.P.W., June, 1932.

THORNTON, LIEUT.-COL. (TEMP. COL.), ST. EDWARD NEWBURY, K.B.E. (1919), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Camb.), S.A.M.C., commdr. of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem (1924).—B. 1878; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and London Hosp.; asst. med. offr., Imp. Yeomanry Hospitals, Anglo-Boer war, 1900-01; plague serv., Punjab, 1902-03; ent. civ. serv., Cape Colony, 1903; additional M.O.H., Cap. Colony, 1906; med. inspr. of hosp. and charity insts., and ch. local govt. inspr., Cape, 1919; major, med. br., sec. I. Union Res. of Offrs. in German S.W. Africa, 1915; (ment. in despatch). Lieut.-Col., S.A.M.C. and O.C. S. African mil. hosp., Richmond, Surrey; mem., comtee. of management, and surg. to Queen Mary's aux. conval. hosp. for limbless soldiers, Roehampton, and mem., comtee. management, Queen Mary's aux. conval. hosp. for facial injuries, Sidcup; chmn., exec. comtee. on vocational training of soldiers in hosp., Lond. dist.; represented S. Africa, Inter Allied Conf. on disablement problems arising out of the war, 1916-20; senr. asst. health offr. for the Union, dir. of med. services, Union Defence Forces, and chmn., cent. housing bd. for Union, 1920; sec. for pub. health and ch. health offr. for Union, 1932.

THORNTON, GEORGE EDWIN, M.B.E. (1933). B. 1899; war serv., 1916-17 (E. Africa); clk., N. Rhodesia, 1917; ag. asst. comptnr., taxes for periods, 1926, 1927 and 1928; asst. comptnr., taxes, Apr., 1928; comptnr., income tax, Apr., 1932; ag. treas., 1933.

THORNTON, HUGH CHAMONDELEY, C.M.G. (1920), C.V.O. (1929).—B.1881; ed. Kelly Coll., Tavistock, and at Christ Church, Oxford; priv. sec. to Viscount Milner, as mem. of the War Cabinet without portfolio, Dec., 1916 to Apr., 1918; as sec. of state for war, 1918-19; as sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; apptd. crown agt. for the colonies, 1st Oct., 1920.

THORNTON, R.W.—B.1881; ed. Graaff Reiniet and Eisenburg Schl. of Agr., Cape Prov. (dipl. with hona.); served in Anglo-Boer War as Scout and intelligence offr.; joined dept. of agr., Cape Prov. field crops and experiment and research section, 1905; offr.-in-ch., agrl. research branch, agrl. dept., Cape prov., 1908; prin., Grootfontein Schl. of Agr. and experiment station, 1910; sent by govt. to England, Europe and Australia to study agrl. educn., experiment station organisation, etc., took charge of expedn. to W. Central Africa, in connection with ostrich industry of Union, 1912; active serv. in France, 1917; resumed charge of Grootfontein Schl. of Agr., 1919; dir., field and animal husbandry, Oct., 1923; dir., native agr., dept. of native affrs., Apr., 1929.

THOROGOOD, WILLIAM JESSE, M.C.—B. 1896; ed. Latymer Upper Schl. and Trinity Hall, Camb.; war serv., 1915-18; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attd., Chinese prot., Ipoh; passed cadet, Sept., 1923; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; asst. contrlr., lab., Seremban, Aug., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Gopeng and Kampar, Feb., 1926; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, June, 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; asst. contrlr., lab., Klang; asst. contrlr., lab., Penang, May, 1931; ag. emigrn. comptnr. for Malaya, May, 1932; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933.

TIBBITS, ARTHUR CHARLES KENT.—B. 1877; employed col. sec.'s off.; treas. and aud. off., Antigua, Apr. to Dec., 1894; clk. sav. bank, Jan., 1895; 1st clk., post off., Jan., 1898; ag. postmtr. on various occasions, 1899-1907; postmtr. of Antigua, and federal postmtr. of Leeward Is., Apr., 1907; liby. trustee and a J.P., Antigua, 1910; income and trade tax assessor in 1908 and in 1913-15; chmn., bd. of guardians in 1911, 1913 and 1915; dir., P.O.G. fund, May, 1912; ag. asst. censor and ch. censor during war; dep. chmn., city commsrs., Dec., 1916; ag. offr. in ch., Antigua—Montserrat flashlight serv., Aug., 1918 to Feb., 1919; ag. comptnr., Virgin Is., Aug., 1918 to Feb., 1919; warden and mag., Nevis, Oct., 1925; mem., exec. and leg. couns., St. Kitts-Nevis; ag. comptnr., Montserrat and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Leeward Is., May to Aug., 1927 and Jan. to Apr., 1928; rep. Dominics at gen. leg. coun., 1928.

TIBBITS, ELWOOD D'ABOY, O.B.E. (1929), M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1880; ag. 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, Jan., 1899 to Nov., 1900 (almost continuously); jun. audit clk., Dec., 1900; 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., July, 1901; ag. clk. to adminstr. and clk. to exec. coun., Dominica, Aug., 1908 to Apr., 1908; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, Sept., 1906; 2nd clk., clk. to exec. and legis. couns., and supt. of govt. printing off., Antigua, Oct., 1911; ag. priv. sec. to gov., in 1903, 1904, 1911 and 1912; ag. asst. col. sec., in 1907, 1908, 1911 and 1912; ag. ch. clk. on abolition of off. of asst. col. sec., June to Oct., 1912; ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Leeward Is., Oct., 1912; asst. col. sec., Oct., 1920; ag. col. sec., for various periods, 1922-32; admstr. govt. of Antigua on several occasions during that period; comptnr. for St. John, Nov., 1925; ag. comptnr., Montserrat, Jan., 1926 and Nov., 1927 to Jan., 1928.

TIBBITT, CAPT. ERIC MONTAGUE (R. of O.)—B. 1896; served in European War, 1914-18; priv. sec. to gov., Br. Honduras, Nov., 1920; ag. clk., exec. coun., Br. Hond. Nov., 1922; asst. audr., Nigeria, Aug., 1924; audr., Br. Hond., Feb., 1928; capt., Br. Hond. Defence Force, Apr., 1928.

TICKLE, ARTHUR GEORGE WARNHAM, F.R.I.B.A., F.S.I.—B. 1887; ed. Holy Trinity, Bansbury, Polytechnic and Royal Acad., London; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1913; exec. engnr., 1920; tech. sec., D.P.W., 1923-4 and 1928-32; J.P., 1931; asst. D.P.W., 1932; ag. D.P.W., 1933.

TILLEY, ALBERT.—B. 1878; Impl. serv., 1895-02; post off., exchequer and audit depts.; transferred to Transvaal serv., Mar., 1902; clk., audit off., Transvaal, 1902; senr. clk., 1909; ditto, audit off., Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., 1912; prov. audr. and local govt. inspr., Transvaal Prov., May, 1915.

TILNEY, CHARLES EDWARD, B.A. (Oxon)—B. 1909; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1932; attd., office of D.M. and S.S., Feb., 1932; attd., Galle kach., Sept., 1932; office asst., Ratnapura kach., June, 1933.

TIMMS, CHARLES GORDON, M.C. (three bars), L.R.C.P., M.R.C.S.Ed., L.R.F.P.S. (Glasgow), Certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1886; ed. Watson's Coll. and R. Coll., Surgeons, Edin.; served with R.A.M.C., Oct., 1914 to Mar., 1919; M.O., Uganda, Jan., 1922; transfd., Somaliland, Dec., 1922.

TINDALL, BENJAMIN ARTHUR.—B. 1879; ed. Victoria Coll., Cape Prov.; B.A., LL.B.; called to bar, 1903; practised, Pretoria, K.C., 1919; presided, sp. ct., Swaziland, 1914; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., 1st Dec., 1922.

TINWORTH, WILLIAM LEONARD, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1889; mil. serv., India and Mesopotamia, 1917-19; asst. acctnt., F.M.S. rly., 1921; ag. acctnt., 1926, 1929 and 1930; ag. acctnt., pub. wks. dept., 1929; dep. ch. acctnt., Tanganyika rly., 1931; ag. ch. acctnt., 1933.
TISDALE, E. G. S.—Asst. dist. comptnr., E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1919.

TISDALE, HERBERT GEORGE, B.Sc., A.R.C.Sc., A.M. Inst. C.E., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Schl. and Imp. Coll. of Science (Whitworth schol.); eng. inspr., C.A., 1907; asst. engr., engnrng. contracts dept., 1913; dep. head, engnrng. contracts dept., 1926.

TITCHENER, Henry Ian Herrick.—B. 1900; entd. C.O., Feb., 1916; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., 24th Apr., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 13th July, 1928; asst. librarian, 14th Jan., 1929.
TITCOMBE, Fred.—B. 1886; European stn. mast., F.M.S. Rlys., Dec., 1910; shorthand writer and typist, Jan., 1912; asst. sec. to gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., Mar., 1924.

TODD, Cedric Lindley.—B. 1897; ed. Sherborne Schl.; served in European War, Western fronts, 1914-18 and Rhine Army, 1919-21; asst. audr., Nyasaland, Nov., 1921; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1929; senr. asst. audr., 1933.

TODD, Ronald Ruskin.—B. 1902; ed. County High Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schol.); B.A. (hons.), 1924; M.A., 1928; cadet, Hong Kong, 1924; passed Cantonese, June, 1927; attd. secretariat for Chinese affr., 1927; attd. to impta. and expts. dept., 1930; ch. asst. to sec. for Chinese affr., May, 1933.
TOLMIE, George Mackenzie, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1904; ed. Inverness Royal Acad., Edinburgh Univ. and Imp. Forest Inst., Oxford; asst. conservator, forests, Gold Coast, 1926.

TOLMIE, Hon. Simon Fraser.—B. 1867; ed. Victoria High Schl., Ontario, and Vet. Coll., Toronto; farmer and breeder of live stock; pres., Dominion Holstein Breeders, B. Columbia Vet. Assoc., B. Columbia Holstein Breeders; chf. inspr. of health of animals branch, B. Columbia; B.C. representative on live stock comsn.; elec. of Canadian H. of C., g. e., 1917 and 1921; min. to agric. in fed. govt., Aug., 1919; resig. portfolio on defeat of admtn., in general election, Dec., 1921; premier, Br. Columbia, Aug., 1928.
TOMKINSON, C.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1915; seconded as lieut., Carrier Corps.

TOMLINSON, Sir George John Frederic, K.C.M.G. (1934), C.B.E. (1928), M.A. (Oxon), Barrister-at-law.—B. 1876; ed. Charterhouse and Univ. Coll., Oxford; Transvaal educn. dept., 1903-04; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1907; seconded to Gold Coast as dir., educn., 1909; reverted to N. Nigeria, 1911; 3rd cls. res., 1911; ag. res., 1912; 2nd cls. offr. (change of title), 1914; 1st cls. dist. offr., 1918; ag. sec., N. Provs., 1919; asst. sec., native affrs., 1921; ag. sec., do., 1921 and 1923; staff grade (supern.), 1925; ret., 1928; asst. under sec. of state, C.O., 1st Oct., 1930.

TOMLINSON, Thomas Fowler, M.C., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, and Leeds University; asst. engnr., constrn., Trinidad govt. rlys., 1911; served with R. Engns., France, 1915-19; res. engnr., constrn., Trinidad govt. rlys., 1920; temp. D.P.W., Virgin Is., 1925; dist. engnr., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1927; ch. engnr., govt. rlys., Trinidad, 1928; supt., Trinidad govt. rly., 1933.

TOMLINSON, Sir Thomas Symonds, Kt. Bach. (1925).—B.A. Trin. Coll., Camb.; B. 1877; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1901; town mag., Zanzibar, May, 1907; asst. judge of H.B.M. ct. for Zanzibar, Jan., 1911; ag. judge, Feb. to Nov., 1916; judge (now ch. just.), 11th Oct., 1919; seconded as legal adviser of the civil admtn. of the occupied territory of Ger. E. Africa, Feb., 1918; ag. atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920 to July, 1921; prepared revised edn. of the laws of Zanzibar, 1922; 2nd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1923; ret., 1928.

TONGE, Lieut. Comdr. C. G., M.B.E., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1878; ed. Fettes Coll., Edinburgh; naval transport offr., Aug. to Dec., 1914; lent for serv. in gunboats on Lake Nyasa, Jan., 1915; senr. naval offr., Lake Nyasa, and 1st offr. marine transport dept., June, 1919; Royal Naval Res. Offr.'s Decoration.

TONGUE, Eric Dauney, O.B.E. (1934), B.A., LL.B.—Ed. Taunton Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schol. and exhib.); assst. dist. comsnr., Uganda, October, 1913; dist. commr., Aug., 1923; lab. comsnr., May, 1930; dist. offr., Oct., 1931.

TOPP, Wm.—B. 1884; Gambia govt. serv., 1901-07; S. Nigeria govt., 1907-08; N. Nigeria govt., Oct., 1908 to Sept., 1910; transfd. to Gambia, Sept., 1910; 1st. clk., col. sec. in force, Nov., 1911; ch. clk., June, 1913; sp. cl. appntn., Jan., 1920; ag. clk., leg. couns. on var. occasions, 1916-22; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1924; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, July-Aug., 1924; J.P. and comsnr. ot. of requests, July, 1925.

TOHILL, John Douglas.—B. 1885; ed. Blundell's Schl.; Ontario Agr. Coll. (Toronto Univ.); B.Sc., Agr., 1910; Cornell Univ., U.S.A.; Graduate Schl., 1913, 1914; Harvard Univ., D.Sc., 1922; agt. and expert, U.S. Bureau of Entomology, 1910-11; entomologist, entomological branch, Ottawa, 1911-24; seconded as dir., Levuana campaign, Fiji, for two years Nov., 1924; ag. dir., agr., Fiji, May, 1925; dir., Levuana campaign and agr., 1926; dir. agr., Uganda, June, 1929.

TOUGHER, Robert Alexander Hunter.—B. 1891; ed. Royal Belfast Acad. Inst. and Queen's Univ. of Belfast, B.A. (1914); army, 1914-19; warserv., Dardanelles, Egypt and Suez, in desps.; capt., gen. R. of O., 1919; sol., Ireland, 1920; asst. land offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.

TOUSSAINT, James Reginald.—B. 1879; ag. off. asst., Matare Kach, Ceylon, Jan., 1917; sec., Buddhist temporalities ord. comsn. and extra off. asst. to dir., educn., Nov., 1918; cls. V. of civ. serv., July, 1919; admn. as comsnr. of stamps, Sept., 1921; cls. IV. Mar., 1926; asst. comsnr., stamps, Mar., 1930; dep. comsnr., stamps, July, 1931.

TOVEY, Duncan Lorimer Pattison.—B. 1903; ed. Clifton Coll. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; M.A.; asst. priv. sec. (appts.) to S. of S. Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Dec., 1926; to Lord Passfield, June, 1929; apptd. asst. prim., C.O., Oct., 1930.

TOWNLEY, Charles Henry.—B. 1892; served with field coy., R.E.'s (T.) in Egypt, Gallipoli, France, Belgium and Germany 1914-19; asst. govt. printer, Nigeria, May, 1920; senr. supt., Aug., 1926; ag. govt. printer, May, 1927 and Nov., 1929; govt. printer, Gold Coast, 1931.
TRAFFORD, H. H.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1913; 1st grade admstive. odr., Kenya, 1925.

TRATMAN, David Wm., C.M.G. (1934).—Ed. at Clifton Coll., schlr. of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1900; B.A., 1904; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1904; passed cadet, Jan., 1907; ag. asst. land offr. in New Territories, and regir. of land ct., 1907; mem. of land ct., 1907; 2nd asst. regir.-gen., 1909; 1st asst. regir.-gen., 1911; asst. dist. offr. N.T., (South), 1912; ch. asst. to sec. for Chinese affr., 1914; head of sanitary dept., 1917; supt.of imports and exports, 1917; local priority authority, 1918; ag. sec. treas., 1920, 1922 and 1924; dist. offr., N.
TRUELOVE, A. C.—Asts. engr., P.W.D., N. Nigeria, 1914; retrenched, 1916; asts. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1919; senr. exec. engr., 1928.

TRUMP, PHILIP, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Exeter Schl. and King's Coll., Lond., civ. engng.; asts. engr., P.W.D., Selangor, Aug., 1910; ex-engnr., grade III, 1919; ex-engnr., wks. and bldgs., K. Lumpur, May, 1923; senr. ex-engnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1927; state engr., Trangguan, Apr., 1927.

TRUPHET, JOHN SIDNEY.—B. 1883; ed. Roan Schl. and King's Coll., London; entd. Crown Agent's office, 1901; asts. head, stores dept., 1919; do., insurance and checking dept., 1920; do., shipping dept., 1922; dep. head, shipping dept., 1927; dep. head, stores dept., 1931.

TRUSTED, HARRY HERBERT, K.C.—B. 1888; ed. Ellesmere Coll. and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1913; served overseas (Duke of Cornwall's L.I. and staff, East Indies), 1914-19; puisne judge, Leeward Is., 1925; atty.-gen., 1927; commr. to revise the federal acts, Leeward Is., 1928; K.C., Leeward Is., 1929; atty.-gen., Cyprus, Dec., 1929; temp. attd., legal advisory staff, C.O., Aug., 1931; atty.-gen., Palestine, 1932.

TRUTER, SIR THEODORUS GUSTAFF, K.B.E. (1924), C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1873; ed. Boys Pub. Schl., George; Normal Coll., Capetown; Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; Cape civ. ser., Dec., 1892; chief examr., accts., 1895; lieut., Robert's Horse, 1899-1902 (med. and four clasps); asts. mag., Pretoria, Apr., 1901; mem., criminal ct., mag., Ermelo, Dec., 1904; mag., Standerton, Apl., 1908; prov. sec., Transvaal, July, 1910; commr., S. African pol., Oct., 1910; J.P., Union of S. Africa; ret., 1928.

TUCKER, D.E.A., M.C.—B. 1896; ed. at St. Francis de Sales Fathers, Givisiez (Fribourg) and Florimont Coll., Lausy (Geneva), B.-es-Letres (1re partie) Grenoble Univ., July, 1914; Wilts. Regt. and M.G.C., 1914-20; 2nd lieut., June, 1917; H.M. cust., U.K., 1923; inspr.-in-ch., preventive serv., Tanganyika cust., 1923.

TUCKER, KEITH RAVENSCROFT, C.B.E. (1932), F.I.A.A., certified acctnt.—B. 1890; articled pupil to Messrs. Yarrow & Co., Glasgow, 1906-8; entd. engineering dept., office of crown agts. for the colonies, Mar., 1908; transf. to col. audit dept., cent. office, June, 1913; asts. auditor, G. Coast, Sept., 1913; asts. audr., Nyasaland Prot., June, 1916; despatched Pretoria, on milly, audit duties, Oct., 1918 to Apr., 1919; served with Togoland Field Force, Aug.-Sept., 1914 (ment. in des.), lieut., G. Coast Volts., Apl., 1915; hon. temp. capt., Nyasaland Field Force, 1919; asts. auditor, Nyasaland Prot., June, 1916; dep. treas., Dec., 1921; ag. treas., Apr., 1923; treas., Jan., 1924; ag. ch. sec., Feb. to Mar., 1931 and Oct., 1933.

TUCKER, LESLIE.—Ed. at Liverpool Collegiate Instit. and Univ. Coll., Liverpool; prelim. and inter. B.A.; 1st cls. scholarship; 1st cls. both years certif. exam.; parchment certif., 1897; head master, govt. boys' schl., St. Helena, 1904; head master, senior schl., St. Helena, 1905; hon. sec. and treas., govt. lace schl., 1908; supervisor of govt. schls., 1910; hon. sec., bd. of educn., 1911; govt. rep. on Hussey Charity bd. of educn., 1911; J.P. and coroner and mem., bd. of health, 1912; lieut., St. Helena Rifles, Sept., 1914 to Nov., 1916; lieut., R.A.S.C., Apr., 1917 to Nov., 1919; inspr. schls., Leeward Is., Feb., 1920; ehmn., gen. purposes comtee., Antigua bd. of educn.; chmn., St. Kitts bd. of educn., 1922; ast. dir., educn., Jamaica, 1930.

TUCKER, L. M.—Ed., Mod. Schol. for Girls, Exeter, Homerton Coll., Cambridge and Univ. Coll., Exeter; mistress, Prince of Wales' Coll., Achimota, Gold Coast, Sept., 1926; organiser, female and infant educn., Apr., 1928; inspr., schls., Apr., 1933.

TUCKER, RICHARD WILLIAM ETHELBERT, M.A. (Cantab.), B.Ed., (Capetown).—B. 1892; ed. Wilson's Grammar Schol. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; lst ola., nat. sci. tripes pt. I (hons. chem.) and zoology) 1913; 1st cls. nat. sci. tripes, pt. II (zoology) 1914; astt., S. African museum, Cape-town, 1914-21; entomologist, dept. of agr., Union of S. Africa, 1921-28; entomologist, Barbados, 1928; publs. include various papers on Araneae and Acari, and economic papers on control of Acracid and insect pests.

TULL, JOHN CADMAN.—M.D. (McGill), M.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1884; capt., Canadian Army Med. Corps (mntd. in desps.), France, 1915-19; pathologist, Rangoon gen. hosp., 1919; med. offr., gen. hosp., Penang, Jan., 1921; pathologist, Penang, Jly., 1921; lect., pathology, Spore, June, 1926; govt. pathologist, Spore, Aug., 1926; deleg. from King Edward VII Med. Coll. to Intern. cong. on cancer, London, May, 1928; deleg., leprosy confco., Manila, Dec., 1930.

TURBETT, IVAN JOLIFFE TUPPER.—B. 1890; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, King's Inn, Dublin, 1913; cadet, Fiji, 1914; ag. dist. commr., 1915; ag. crown solr., 1916; ag. ch. pol. mag., 1917; pol. mag., Gambia, 1918; ag. legal adviser, 1919-22 and in 1924; ag. judge, sup. ct. on various occasions; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1926 ag. atty.-gen. in 1926, 1927 and 1928.

TURGEON, WILLIAM FERDINAND ALPHONSE, K.C., B.A.—B. 1877; ed. at New York city and Laval univ., Montreal; barrister; atty.-gen. of Saak., 23rd Sept., 1907; elec. to Sask. legis., 1907; re-elec., 1908, 1912 and 1917; atty.-gen. and prov. sec., 1915; judge, ct. of appeal, Saak., 14th Mar., 1921.

TURNBULL, DAVID OLIVER, D.S.O.—B. 1879; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; diploma, R.C.V.S., Dec., 1903; lieut., A.V.D., Sept., 1904 to July, 1908 and Nov., 1914 to Nov., 1915; capt., Nov., 1915; major, Apr., 1916 (A.D.V.S., 27th divn.); lieut.-col., 1918 (A.D.V.S., 16th Corps); desps., 1916, 1917, 1918; French Croix de Guerre, Mar., 1917; D.S.O., June, 1917; N. Rhodesia vety. dept., Sept., 1920; ag. ch. vety. offr., N. Rhodesia on two occasions in 1927; ch. vety. offr., Nyasaland, 1930.

TURNER, ERNEST EDGAR.—B. 1880; clk., post office, Sheffield, 1894-1900; served in S. African war and in S. African constab. (medal and 5 clasps); depot sergt-major, R.N.W. mounted police, Canada, 1908-1916; coronation medal, 1911; lieut., Bahamas police and defence force, 1916; capt., 1917; major and offir. com. local forces, comdt. of police, provost marshal, and inspr. of prisons, June, 1919; ch. of pol., St. Lucia, May, 1923; ch. of pol., Grenada, Jan., 1927.
TURNER, CAPT. H. W.—Port offr., E. Africa Prot., May, 1915.
TURNER, HENRY.—B. 1882; ed. Oamaru South Pub. Schol., N.Z.; N.Z. pub. trust office, 1899; astt. pub. trustee, 1923; under-sec. of defence, 1930.

TURNER, JAMES POGSON.—B. 1871; clk., pub. library, St. Kitts, 1890; clk., regiar.'s off., St. Kitts, 1897; commr. to administer oatha, St. Kitts, 1899; clk. to mag., dist. "C," St. Kitts May to Oct., 1905; 1st clk., regiar.'s off., Antigua, May, 1906; commr. to admin. oatha, Antigua, May, 1906; clk. regiar. of jt. stock companies, Nov., 1906; dep. coroner, dists. "A" and "B," Antigua, Apr., 1916; dir. of P.O.G. fund, Jan., 1917; regiar. and prov. marshal, Antigua, and chief regiar., Sept., 1917; commr. income tax, July, 1923; M.L.C., Antigua, Mar., 1926; addnl. mag., dist. A., Antigua, Jan., 1928; M.L.C., Antigua, Mar., 1929; J.P., May, 1930; commr. sup. ot., Oct., 1931.

TURTON, NIVILLE.—B. 1889; ed. at Merse Coll., Oxford; B.A. (hons.), 1911; M.A., 1915; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; astt. audr., S. Leone, 1912; ag. audr. on four occasions; volunteer, R.G.A., Sierra Leone; astt. to the atty.-gen., Uganda, 1921; 1st cls. mag. and judge in addn.; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, June, 1924; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; chrs. income tax commrs., 1924; mem., educn. bd., 1925; ag. col. sec., 1925 and 1926; ag. ch. just., 1925, 1926, 1927 and 1928; admstd. govt., Sept., 1926; solr.-gen., Uganda, Jan., 1928; temp. attd., legal advisory staff, C.O., Oct. to Dec., 1931; atty.-gen., Oct., 1933.

TUTE, RICHARD CLIFFORD.—B. 1874; ed. St. Peter's Royal Schol., York and London Univ.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; passed open compet. exam. for Indian civ. serv., 1886; dist. and sessions judge, Allahabad, Bareilly, Meerut and other dists. from 1907; served throughout European war, retiring in 1919 with rank of major. R.G.A.; dist. judge, Galilee dist., Palestine, 1919; pres., dist. ct., Samaria, 1920; pres., land ct., Samaria, 1921; pres., land ct., Jerusalem, 1925; ch. just., Bahamas, 1931; admstd. govt. in Aug., 1932; author of "A Commentary on the Ottoman Land Code," and (as joint author) a metrical translation of Omar Khayyam.

TWELLS, JOHN.—Ed. King's Schol., Canterbury and at Jesus Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A., 1907; examr., E. and A. Dept., July, 1909; astt. auditor, S. Leone, Nov., 1909; astt. auditor, E. Africa Prot., July, 1912; senr. astt. mast. educn. dept., Kenya, 1919; ag. head mast. Nairobi Schol., June to Dec., 1920; s-nr. astt. mast., Apr., 1921; ag. head mast. in 1924, 1925 and 1927.

TWEMLOW, GEORGE BROWNBILL.—B. 1897; ed. at Rossall Schol.; milry. serv., 1916-29; demob. with rank of capt.; re-enlisted, 1929-33; joined botanical and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1923; astt. supt., botanical and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1927.

TWOSE, LAWRENCE RENDLE.—B. 1891; clk. to mag., dist. A. 1915; sec. to fire brig. comm., 1916; lieut., H.M. forces, 1916-19; ag. A.D.C. to gov., Leeward Is., on occasion of visit of Prince of Wales, 1920; clk. to commr., clk., couns., dep. coroner, educn. dist. offr., Montserrat, 1924; J.P., chmn., prison justices, 1924; mag., coroner, regiar.-gen., sup. ot., 1924; M.R.C. and M.L.C., 1925.

TYLER-SMITH, H. A., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E.—B. 1887; B.Sc. (Eng.) with 1st cls. hons., civil engnr., Liverpool Univ., 1928; 2nd lieut., R.E. (ap. res.) 1910; astt. engr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1911; exec. engrnr., 1917; capt., R.E. (S.R.), 1917; D.P.W., Gambia, Dec., 1920; astt. D.P.W., Nigeria, 1930.
TYRELL, SIR FRANCIS GRAEME, K.B.E. (1934), C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1876; ed. Pembroke coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct.

TYSER, SIR C. R., KT. BACH (1909).—Ed. at Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb., M.A. (1872); called to bar, In. Tem., 1873; astt. ag. judic. comsnr. and leg. advdr., Cyprus, 1880-1; atty.-gen., Leeward, 1886-9; pres. dist. ct., Kyrena, Cyprus, 1895; puisne judge, Cyprus, Jan., 1902; ch. just., 1906; ret., 1919; author of "Marine Insurance Losses," 1894; wrote translation of Omar Hilmi Effendi's Evkaf Law, 1899; and of the Mejelle, 1901.

UNWIN, ARTHUR HAROLD, O.B.E. (1934), D.Oec., M.E.F.A., F.R.G.S., M.C.S.F.E.—Forestry branch, dept. of interior, Canada, 1903-4; astt. conservator and senr. conservator, of forests, Nigeria, 1904-17; office of woods, timber supply dept., England, 1917-19; prin. forest offr., Cyprus, 1921; rep. Cyprus and Palestine at Empire forestry confce., Australia and N.Z., Sept.-Nov., 1928; rep. Cyprus at internat. cong. of experimental forest stations, Stockholm, July-Aug., 1929; title changed to conserv., forests, Jan., 1932.
URICH, LEO GOTTFRIED WILFRED, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.C.M. & H.—B. 1899; med. supt., Chacachacare leper settlmt., Trinidad, Jan., 1933.
USHER, CAPT. C. G., M.C.—Ast. dist. comsnr., Kenya, May, 1920; ag. sec., secretariat, 1927.

USHER, WALTER WHITBREAD.—B. 1886; ed. at Marist Bros. Coll., Uitenhage; served Anglo-Boer War, 1901, S.W. Africa, 1914-15, Flanders, 1916-18; olk., Swaziland, 1910; regiar. and master, sp. st. of Swaziland, and Sheriff, 1916; dep. astt. comsnr., 1919.
UTTLEY, DENYS PLURAT, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. St. John's, Leatherhead and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; col. audit dept., May, 1930; astt. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1930.

UTTLEY, KENNETH HARRISON, M.A., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.B., B.Ch. (Camb.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1901; med. offr., Hong Kong; astt. med. offr., Victoria Hosp., 1929; med. offr. in ch., Kowloon and New Territories, Lai Chi Kok Prison, Kowloon mort.; astt. med. offr., Kowloon Hosp., in ch. offr.'families, Kowloon, and pol. surg., Kowloon, 1930; med. offr., ditto, 1933.

VAITHIANATHAN, KANTHAR, B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., June, 1923; astt., Colombo kach., July, 1923; ditto Ratnapura kach., May, 1924; ag. office astt. ditto, Sept., 1924; extra office astt., ditto, Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Sept., 1925; ditto, Avissavella, February, 1926; office astt., Galle kach., Nov., 1926; addnl. astt. gov't. agt., Hambantota, Dec., 1928; office astt., Colombo kach., Mar., 1929; addnl. astt. gov't. agt., Nov., 1929; astt. col. sec., Mar., 1930; sec. to min. for home affrs., July, 1931.

VALENTINE, DOUGLAS JAMES, M.C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.)—B. 1892; ed. Palmer's Schi. and Lond. Hosp.; temp. comsnr., K.O.S.B., 1914-16; R.A.M.C., 1917-19; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1919; in ch., civ. hosp., 1925-28; in ch., Kowloon hosp., 1929; in ch., Victoria hosp., 1930; ag. D.D.M.S.S., 1931 and 1932.

VAN DER MEULEN, SIR FREDERICK A., KT. BACH (1919); O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1875; ed. at Blundell's Schi., Tiverton, and Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A. (honors in mod. hist.), 1896; M.A., 1900; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1900; Westm. circuit; astt. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 1907; solr.-gen., 1908; ag. atty.-gen. and also ag. puisne and circuit judge on various occasions, 1908-1911; ch. mag., Gambia, 1913; puisne judge, Nigeria, Oct., 1919; ret.

VAN DER SPUY, LT.-COL. ADRIAN JOHANNES, M.B., Ch.B. (Glasgow), D.P.H. (Capetown).—B. 1889; ed. Boys' High Schi. and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Glasgow Univ. and Capetown Univ.; H.S. and H.P., Western Infmy., Glasgow, 1914-16; capt., S.A.M.C. (Overseas), 1916-20; served in France and Belgium, 1918; Br. War and Victory medals; O.C. mil. hosp., Wynberg, as capt. (temp. maj.), S.A.M.C. (A.C.F.), 1920; maj., S.A.M.C. (Perm. Force), 1923; defence headqrs. (med. services sect.), 1926; O.C. mil. hosp., Roberts' Heights and senr. med. offr., Roberts' Heights, 1926; transfd. to dept. of public health as astt. health offr. for Union, 1927; lt.-col., R. of O. (med. br.) U.D.F., 1927; dep. dir., med. services, U.D.F., attd. to Staff Corps from R. of O. (in addn. to civ. duties as astt. health offr. for Union), 1927.
VAN DER SPUY, MICHIEL LOUW.—2nd gr. compr., Natal, Feb., 1913; ch. draughtsman and compr., Apr., 1914; prof. astt., Apr., 1917; survr.-gen., Natal, Sept., 1931.
VAN DER STERR, WILLEM CORNELIS.—Dir., secondary triangulation, Union of S. Africa, Aug., 1919; dir., trigonometrical survey, Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1920.

VAN DE VELDE, MARCEL ARTHUR MAURICE.—Ed. at Mt. St. Mary's Coll., Derbyshire; olk., col. audt. branch, E. and A. dept., May, 1900; tempy., astt. audr., E. Africa Prot. and Uganda rly., Mar., 1903; astt. audr., Uganda Prot., June, 1903; astt. audr., E. Africa Prot. and Uganda rly., 1904; locnl audr., Uganda Prot., Nov., 1909; audr., Uganda Prot., May, 1910; audr., Kenya, Oct., 1923.

VAN LANGENBERG, WILLIAM JAMES ALBERT.—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1929; attd., Ratnapura kach., Jan., 1930; attd., Anuradhabura kach., May, 1930; ag. office astt., Nuwara Eliya kach., Oct., 1931.

VAN NIEKERK, CHRISTIAAN ANDRIES.—J. 1874; ed. pub. schl., Wellington and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; commtd. for Kroonstad in Anglo-Boer War (wounded); del., peace confce., Vereeniging, 1902; mem., compensation coman. till 1903; mem., leg. assem., O.F.S. 1907; leg. assem., Union, 1910-24; mem., pensions grievances coman., chmn., sp. pensions bd., 1925; mem., Union senate since 1924; pres., 1930.

VAN REKENEN, REEKEN JACOB, B.A., C.E., Assoc. M. Amer. Soc. C.E., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. at S.A. Coll., Cape Town; at graduation obtained Victoria scholarship, proceeded to Lehigh Univ., Pennsylvania, U.S.A., ast. engr., irrigtn. dept., Cape Colony, Sept., 1909; ditto, Union of S. Africa; supt. of roads and local wks., O.F.S. Prov., May, 1912; mem. of Union Drought Investigation Coman., 1920-24; chmn., Drought Investigation Coman. of S.W. Africa, 1923-24; mem., irrign. finances comsn., 1924-25; chmn., sp. irrign. comsn. 1926; mem., Angola bdy. comsn., May-July, 1926; chmn., irrign. comsn., Aug., 1926; mem., natural and historical monuments comsn., 1923 and chmn. since 1930.
VAN RENEN, R.—B. 1877; clk., treasy., 1895, and income tax office, 1914; recr., rev., Benoni, 1921; do., Krugersdorp, 1929; do., E. London, 1932; do., Bloemfontein, 1932.

VAN RYNEVELD, COL. SIR PIERRE HELPERUS ANDRIAS, K.B.E. (1920), D.S.O. (1918), M.C. (1916), B.A., B.Sc.—B. 1891; ed. Grey Coll., Schl., Grey Univ. Coll., Bloemfontein, Imp. Coll., Univ. of London; 2nd lieut., 7th Loyal N. Lancs. Regt., Aug., 1914; lieut., R.F.C., Apr., 1915; detachment C.O., Senussi campaign, Egypt, 1915-16; flight comdr., Palestine, 1916; ditto, Salonika, 1916; squad. comdr., London night defences, 1916-17; squad. comdr., No. 17 squad., R.E.F., 1917; severely wounded; wing comdr., XI. (Army) Wing, 2nd Army, 1917-19; ditto, Army wing, Rhine army of occupation, 1919; ment. in despatches six times, "1914-15" Star, Brit. War med., Victory med., Order of the Nile, 4th cls., Croix de Chevalier, Legion d'Honneur, France, Croix d'Officier, Ordre Leopold avec Croix de Guerre, Belgium; S. African liaison off., Air Miny., 1919-21; lieut.-col. (temp. col.), Permanent Force Union Defence Forces, Feb., 1920; director of air services, U.D.F., Feb., 1920; col., S.A. staff corps, Nov., 1929; O.C. troops, Roberts Heights and commdts., S.A. Milv. Coll. in addn. to duties as dir., air services, Dec., 1929; brig.-gen. and apptd., ch., gen. staff, May, 1933.

VAN SCHALKWIJK, JOHANNES ZACHARIAS, B.A.—B. 1883; ed. Bloemfontein and Stellenbosch; asst. Bethlehem; vice-prin., Ficksburg, 1906; do., Normal Practising Schl., Bloemfontein, 1910; prinn., model schl., Bloemfontein and vice-rect. Normal Coll., 1913; inspr. of schl., O.F.S., Nov., 1917.
VAN SCHALKWIJK, L. M. A. N., B.A. (Cape), Ph.D. (Amst.)—B. 1888; ed. Normal and S. African Coll., Cape Town, also in Holland, Germany and Austria; organising inspr., Union dept. of educn. since 1921.

VAN WARMELO, WILLIAM.—B. 1874; degree civ. engnr., tech. univ., Delft, Holland, 1902; asst. drainage engnr., Pretoria mun., 1906; asst. engnr., irrign. dept., Pretoria, 1908; hydrographic asst., 1909; asst. hydrographic survr., 1913; hydrographic survr., 1916.
VAN ZEYLEN, FREDERICK CORNELIUS.—B. 1885; mech. and elec. engnr., draughtsman, harbour engnr.'s office, Nassau, Bahamas, 1923; asst. civ. engnr., 1925; dep. civ. engnr., 1925; D.P.W., May, 1928.

VAN ZIJL, HENDRIK STEFANUS, LL.B. (Camb.), B.A., LL.B. (Cape)—B. 1876; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1903; M.P., Cape of Good Hope, 1906-10; partly draftsman, Union of S. Africa, 1910-15; K.C., 1919; judge, sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1920; chmn., delimitation comsn., July, 1927 and Mar., 1932.

VAN ZYL, JACOB ERNEST SIERRERT.—B. 1886; prob. clk., R.M., Hanover, 1904; clk., deeds, Vryburg, 1904; clk., R.M., Calvinia, 1905; clk., master's off., C.T., 1906; 2nd gr. clk., Union educn. dept., 1910; 1st gr. clk., do., 1914; senr. clk., do., 1917; 2nd gr. prin. clk., do., 1920; 1st gr. prin. clk., 1921; 2nd gr. ch. clk., 1925; 1st gr. ch. clk., 1928; pub. serv. inspr., 1931.

VAREY, JAMES ARTHUR, A.M.L.C.E.—B. 1879; apprentice, city engnr.'s office, Leeds, 1896; asst. engnr., ditto, 1900; ditto, waterworks engnr.'s office, Leeds, 1905; irrign. engnr., irrign. dept., Ceylon, 1907; irrign. engnr., P.W.D., B. Guiana, 1913; dist. engnr., P.W.D., Ceylon, Feb., 1920.

VASKESS, HENRY HARRISON.—B. 1873; clk., Western Pacific high comsn., Apr., 1911; on mily. serv., June, 1915; clk. in ch. sect., W. Pacific, Sept., 1919; ch. clk. and acct., Apr., 1920; ag. sec. to W. Pacific high comsn. for short periods in 1920, 1922, 1925 and 1927; sec., Western Pacific high comsn., Dec., 1929.

VASSALL, HENRY HOLLAND.—B. 1857; ed Bedford Grammar Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxford; ass't. res., Nyassaland, 1911; res., 1919; admin. offr., 1st grade, Dec., 1923; ag. prov. comm., Jan., 1929 to June, 1930 and July, 1930 to Feb., 1931.

VASSALLO, EDWIN P., L.S. & A.—B. 1876; ed. Flore's Coll. and Malta Univ.; grad. as survr. and architect, 1914; elec. for 7th div. leg. assem., Oct., 1921; re-elec., June, 1924; Aug., 1927; min. for pub. wks., 16th Aug., 1927; edr. "The Malta Times and Progress" until Aug., 1927.

VASSALLO, S., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Ed. Malta Lyceum and Malta Univ.; M.D., 1913; F.R.C.S. (Edin.), 1928; govt. exhibtn. for science, 1928-29; ditto for medicine, 1909-1913; post-graduate wrk. at Univ. Coll. Hosp. and London Hosp.; chmn. asst., St. John's Hosp., W., and Royal Ear Hosp.; supernumerary med. offr., Leeaward Isl., 1914; D.T.M. and H. (London.), certif., L.S.T.M. (Derm. med.); temp. coman., R.A.M.C. (ment. in dem. med. offr., Uganda, 1919; res. surv. offr., Zanzibar Prot., 1925; 4th cls. Order of Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1931.

VAUGHAN, JOHN HENRY, M.C.—B. 1892; ed Eastbourne Coll. and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, B.A. (hist. tripos), 1914; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-21; M.C., Arras, 1917; ass't. dist. comanr., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to pres. and clk. of the coun., July, 1924; ag. regis., high ct., 1927; called to bar, Inner Temp., 1929; res. mag., July, 1929; ag. judge in 1932 and 1933; asst. atty.-gen., 1933.

VENABLES, OSWALD ERIC, B.A. (Dublin), Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; on active ser., June, 1917; offr., cls. V., Nov., 1917; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1921; 2nd mag., Singapore, 1923; col. ser., Singapore, 1924; Br. res., Brunei, 1926; cls. III., Nov., 1926; mag., Seremban, 1927; offr., Upper Perak, 1929; collr., land rent., Kuala Lumpur, 1931; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1931; Br. advr., Perlis, 1932.

VENNING, GUT, M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1877; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., assigned to local govt. bd., May, 1896; after further compet. exam., apptd. clk. of 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 8th Jan., 1900; transf'd to C.O., 16th Apr., 1908; asst. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; staff offr., 9th Jan., 1924.

VENOUR, LOUIS STEPHEN DOUGLAS HASTON.—B.1885; ed. privately; capt., 5th batt. Marine Regt.; asst. comanr., pol., Gold Coast, 1914; ag. comanr., pol., Aug., 1915; ag. instr., in 1916 and 1919; comanr., pol., Jul., 1917; King's Pol. Med., 1923; ag. comanr., C.I.D., June to Nov., 1928; senr. comanr., 1929; ag. inspr.-gen., pol. on various occasions, 1925-33.

VERITY, JOHN.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, copyist, treasy. and cust., Br. Honduras, 19 3rd cls. clk., registr. gen.'s office, 1910; 2nd cls. clk., atty. gen's office, 1912; resig., 1913; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1918; dep. clk., cts., St. Catharine, Jamaica, 1918; clk., cts., Trelawny, 1920; ag. res. mag. for various periods, 1922-26; res. mag. for various periods, 1922-26; res. mag., Hanover, 1926; ag. judge, Kingston, c. 1927.
VERNEY, FRANK ARTHUR, C.B.E. (1932), O.B.E. (1925).—B. 1874; vet. offr., Natal, 1901-08; prin. vet. offr., Basutoland, 1908; prin. vety. and agrl. offr., 1933.

VERNON, ROLAND VENABLES, C.B. (1924).—B. 1877; ed. at Clifton and Oxford; schol. of Balliol, 1895-9; 1st cls. mods., 1897; prox. acc. Hertford schlr.; Craven schlr., 1897; Jenkyns exhib., 1899; 1st cls. lib., hum., 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. insec.'s dept., inland rev., Nov., 1899; 2nd cls. clk., C.O. 13th Aug., 1900; sec. to Trinidad riot. comm., 1903; mem., managing comte. of E.I.O.; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Elgin, Jan., 1908; priv. sec. to Col. Seely, 16th Apr., 1908; reed. S. African Union medal, 1910; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Denman, gov. gen. of Australia, 1911-12; 1st cls. clk., 9th Nov., 1912; visited and reported on British Solomon Islands, Nov.-Dec., 1911; represented H.M.G. on Anglo-French New Hebrides confce., May-Aug., 1914; transf. to treas., Oct., 1914; lent to Miny. of Munitions, June, 1916; asst. gen. sec., 1916; visited France and Belgium on sp. serv., Feb., 1917; asst. sec., Irish Convention, June, 1917 to Mar., 1918; retd. to treas., May, 1918; dep. acct.-gen., B. of E., Apr., 1920; lent to C.O., Middle East dept. as asst. sec., finan. and economic, Apr., 1921; visited Egypt, Palestine and Trans-Jordania on sp. serv., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; sp. serv. at Lausanne for Turkish Peace Treaty, Nov. to Dec., 1922 and June, 1923; transf. to permanent staff of C.O. as asst. sec., Jan., 1924; seconded for service as finan. adviser to govt. of Iraq, 1925; returned to C.O., 1st Oct., 1928; mem. Palestine Cur. Board, 1928.
VERSCHAFELT, PAUL DESIRE NESTOR, C.M.G. (1930), LL.B., F.I.A.N.Z.—B. 1887; grad. in law, Victoria Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. pub. serv., 1904; asst. pub. serv. comsnr., 1921; pub. serv. commnr., 1923.

VIADER, RENE, M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1879; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; ent. civ. serv., Mauritius, Nov. 1899; registr., col. sec's office, May, 1919; finan. asst., P.W.D., Mar., 1923; ch. clk., col. sec's office, 1923; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1926; ag. asst. col. sec., 1925-30 and Apr., 1932.

VICKERS-HAVILAND, L. A. W.—B. 1895; lieut., R. Fusiliers, 1914-18; seconded, 2nd K.A.R., 1918; asst. polit. offr., 1920; admstv. offr., 2nd grade, 1922; asst. dist. offr., 1926; attd., secretariat, Tanganyika Territory, 1930; dist. offr., 1931; seconded as asst. to dir., agr., 1932-33.

VICTORIA, RIGHT REV. GERARD HEATH LANDER, Lord Bishop of Victoria, Hong Kong—B. 1861; ed. at Trin. Coll. and Ridley Hall, Camb., B.A., 1884; M.A., 1888; D.D., 1907; consec. at Lambeth Parish Church, 29th June, 1907; curate of St. Bride's, Liverpool, 1884 to 1888; vicar of St. Benedict's, Liverpool, 1888 to 1896; of Litherland, 1896 to 1905; of St. Cyprian's, Liverpool, 1905 to 1907; chrmn., Litherland schl. bd., 1898 to 1903; chaplain to Liverpool dist. lay readers, 1892 to 1905.
VIDAL, M. R. R.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1912; 1st grade admstv. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1924.

VILJOEN, PHILIPPUS RUDOLPH, M.R.C.V.S., Dr. Med. Vet. (Berne).—B. 1889; ent. dept. of agr. as asst. vety. bacteriologist, Ondersteopoort, Pretoria, 1913; vety. research offr., Ondersteopoort and Vryburg, 1914; seconded to dept. of defence, Oct., 1914; retd. to dept. of agr., June, 1915; resigned, Jan., 1919; reassptd. dept. of agr. as senr. research offr., Ondersteopoort, 1920; sub-dir., vety. research and prof. in vety. medicine, Transvaal Univ. Coll., Apr., 1922; dep. dir., vety. services, 1927; under-sec., agr., Jan., 1931; sec., agr., May, 1933.
VINCENT, GEORGE ALVES, M.B.E. (1931), M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1872; asst. med. supt., lunatic asylum, Trinidad, Mar., 1901; supt., ditto, Apr., 1909.
VINCENT, WILLIAM HENRY, L.M.S.S.A., Lond.—B. 1886; ed. Colet Court, St. Paul's schl., St. Mary's Hosp., and London Univ.; dist. med. offr., Fiji, 1915.

VISCHER, HANNS, C.B.E.—B. 1876; ed. Switzerland and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1903; res., 1905; dir. of educn., 1908-13; lieut., Gen. List, 1915; capt., 1915; major, 1916; Etoile Noire, 1917; Legion d'Honneur, 1918; Crown of Italy, 1918; Crown of Belgium, 1918; C.B.E., 1918; demob., 1919 with rank of major in G.R.O.; mem. and sec., S. of S.'s advisory comte., on native educn. in Trop. Africa, Nov., 1923.

VISSER, DANIEL HENDRIK, J.P.—B. 1877; entered Cape pub. serv., 23rd Mar., 1894; asst. mag. and ag. civil commnr., 1902-05; clk.-asst., Cape House of Assembly, 8th Apr., 1907; clk.-asst., House of Assem., Union of S. Africa, Nov., 1910; clk., House of Assembly, 1st Oct., 1920.
VISSER, DR. THOMAS CHRISTOFFEL—Mem., Union assem., to 1929; mem., Union senate, 1930; chmn. of comteen., 1930.

VLIELAND, CHARLES ARCHIBALD.—B. 1890; ed. Exeter Schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford (schol.); 1st cls. hon., nat. sci., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1914; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, 17; 3rd asst. sec., fedl. secretariat, 1919; asst. dist. offr., 1920-21; sec. to res., N. Sembilan, 1922; pvte. sec. to O.A.G., June, 1925; addt., col. secs. office, S'pore, Nov., 1925; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1926; ag. dist. offr., B. Padang, July, 1927 to Sept., 1928; dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, Apr., 1929; sup't, 1931 census, Malaya, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1931; economy offr., S.S. and F.M.S., Aug., 1932.

VOIGT, FREDERICK CARREL MARTHINUS, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1879; clk., dept. of just., 1901; pvte. sec. to min. of nat. affrs., 1910; senr. clk., nat. affrs. dept., Pretoria, 1912; dept. of just., Feb., 1913; asst. mag., Krugersdorp, 1916; prof. asst., atty. gen's. office, Bloemfontein, 1921; ditto, Cape Town, 1926; pub. serv. inspr., 1928; asst. prov. sec., Cape adminstr., 1930; prov. sec., ch. local govt. inspr. and contr., eduonl. finance, Mar., 1933.
VOLLET, DENYS HENRI RENE.—B. 1898; served in Great War; med. offr., Basutoland, 1927.

VOLNEY, JOSEPH HENRY LOUIS.—B. 1875; entd. pub. ser., St. Lucia, Feb., 1892, as superm. clk., post off., ag. 4th clk., treasy., Apr. and May, 1893; ag. rev. offr., June to Nov., 1893; clk., immig. offr., Feb., 1894; 4th clk., treasy., Apr., 1894; ag. 3rd clk., in 1895, 1896, and 1897; 3rd clk., treasy., Jan., 1898; ag. 2nd clk. and treasy. acctnt., May to Oct., 1898; ag. 2nd clk. and cashier, treas., Aug., 1900, to Apr., 1901; 2nd clk., registr.'s off., Apr., 1901; ag. ob. clk., Aug. to Oct., 1901; ag. audit clk., Aug. to Nov., 1902; transfd. on temporary duty to pub. wks. dept.; ch. clk., registr.'s dept., Apr., 1903; ag. registr. on several occasions, 1908-1915; ag. mag., dist. III, Mar. to May; asst. mag., dist. I, Sept. to Dec., 1913, and from Aug., 1914; ag. marshal of the Royal court (Admiralty jurisdiction) from Sept., 1914.

VON BONDE, Czczik, Ph.D., M.A.—Ed. Univ. of Capetown; senr. lect. in Zoology, Univ. of Capetown, 1918; awarded "Purcell Memorial Prize" for Zoology in 1924; ag. prof., Zoology, Univ. of Capetown, 1926; dir., fisheries survey and govt. marine biologist, Union of S. Africa, 1928; seconded for marine biolog. survey to confce. of E. African govs. in Kenya and Zanzibar, 1928.

VON OPPELL, OTTO KARL ADOLF.—B. 1874; temp. clk., crown lands and P.W.D., Cape, June, 1892; 3rd grade clk., dept. lands, mines and agr., June, 1893; 2nd grade clk., agr. dept., July, 1897; 1st grade clk., July, 1902; ditto, surveys 1906; prin. clk., dept. lands, Pretoria, Apr., 1912; ch. clk., June, 1917; 1st grade ch. clk., Dec., 1928; under-secr., lands, Aug., 1932.

VOSS, GEORGE DU TOIT, K.C., B.A., LL.B., Advocate of the Sup. Ct., of S. Africa.—B. 1884; asst. registr., sup. ct. of S. Africa (Transvaal prov. divn.), 1918; registr., high ct. of S.W. Africa, 1919; atty-gen., S.W. Africa, 1925; solr.-gen., Eastern dists., Cape Prov., 1931; law adviser, dept. of just., Nov., 1931.

WADDINGTON, E. J., O.B.E. (1919).—Asst. dist. commnr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. prov. commnr., Jubaland, Nov., 1919; senr. asst. sec., secretariat, Kenya, Feb. to Sept., 1923; clk. of couns., Sept., 1923; asst. sec., govs' confce., 1926; res. commnr., Mombasa, 1928; col. sec., Bermuda, 1932.

WADE, A. DE V., O.B.E. (1931).—Ed. Lancing Coll., exhibtn. and Keble Coll., Oxford; scholar; M.A.; asst. dist. commnr., E. Africa Prot., March, 1912; ag. dep. ch. native commnr., Kenya, 1926; prin. asst., native affrs. dept., Jan., 1928; ch. native commnr., 1932; ag. col. sec., 1933.

WADE, CECIL HENRY, O.B.E. (1929), B.A. (hons.), 1909.—Ed. Lancing Coll. (exhibtn.) and Keble Coll., Oxford; 3rd grade res., Nyasaland, 5th Aug., 1911; asst. ch. sec., July, 1920; 1st asst. sec., June, 1921; examr. in Chinyanja language; ag. ch. sec., May, 1926; ag. treas., July to Dec., 1927; ag. ch. sec. May 1929 to Jan., 1930 and Apr., 1930; prin. asst. sec., Nigeria, 1931.

WADIA, SIAVAX HIRJI, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1915; attd., Jaffna Kach., June, 1915; addl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addn., Oct., 1915; finan. asst. to chrmn. munie coun., Colombo, Jan., 1918; office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1920; pol. mag., Avissawella, June, 1920; asst. contr., rev., Apr., 1922; addl. asst. contr., rev., Aug., 1925; asst. commnr., stamps, Jan., 1926; asst. contr., rev., Nov., 1926; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Sept., 1931; ag. commnr., local govt. and ag. sec. to min., local govt., Mar., 1932.

WADE, CAPT. T. G. ROCHFORT, M.A., F.R.G.S.—B. 1901; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and St. Columbas' Coll., Dublin; B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1926; M.A., 1930; inspr., schls., St. Lucia, May, 1931; C.O., St. Lucia Volunteer Force.
WADLEY, W. J. D., M.A.—B. 1903; ed. City of London Sch.; class school, Lincoln Coll., Oxford; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Sept., 1926.

WAIPU, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. HERBERT WILLIAM WILLIAMS, Litt.D.—B. 1856; ed. Christ's Coll. and Canterbury Coll., N.Z., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; Rustad schol., B.A. (2d cl. math. tripos), 1884; M.A., 1887; Lld., 1926; asst. mast., Haileybury Coll., 1885; curate, W. Wrating, Camb., 1886; vice-prin., Mas Theol. Coll., Giaborne, N.Z., 1889; prin., 1935; supt., missionary diocese of Waipu, 1903; bish. of Waipu, 1930; fellow of N.Z. Inst., 1926; pres., Polynesian Soc., 1929-31.

WAINWRIGHT, WILLIAM FREDERICK, O.R.E.—B. 1881; Indian pol., 1889; accompanied by Charles Yate on polit. mission to Mehra, Persia; transfd. to Bengal pol., 1901; on sp. famine operns., 1906-08; serv. in Gt. War, Egypt and Palestine, 1916-20; major, Territorial Reserve (Yeomanry); dep. dist. comdt. of pal. Palestine, 1920; dist. supt., pol., Palestine, 1926.

WAIT, WALTER ERNEST, C.M.G. (1930).—B. 1878; ed. Fettes and Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1902; addl. asst. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., and addl. commnr. of revenue and pol. mag., Mannar and Puttalam during fam. fishery, Feb., 1905; asst. land settm. offr., May, 1905; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Dec., 1910; asst. land settm. offr., Feb., 1911; on mil. duty, Aug., 1914; pol. mag., Colombo, May, 1915; asst. gott. agt. Puttalam and Chilaw, Sept., 1916; deputy-com. of customs, Colombo, Apr., 1919; settlement offr., June, 1920; contr., Indian immigr. lab. in Ceylon, Apr., 1923; mem., leg. coun.; prin. collr., cust. and food contr., Mar., 1927; prin. collr., cust. and food contr., July, 1927; inspr. offr., mag's. cts., Nov., 1929; inspr. offr., mag's. cts., Nov., 1931; dep. ch. sec., Nov., 1932; ag. ch. sec., Sept., 1933.

WAKEFIELD, ARTHUR JOHN, B.Sc., N.D.—N.D.D.—B. 1900; ed. Brewood Grammar Sch.; Harper Adams Agr. Coll., Reading and Edgburgh Univ.; 2nd lieut. Shropshire Light Inf., 1918; vety. dept., N. Rhodesia, 1923; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1934; senr. agrl. offr., 1930; ag. dir., agr., Jan.-Aug., 1930.
WALKER, A.—Laboratory supt., K. Afr. Prot., Oct., 1918.
WALKER, IVAN LAWRENCE.—B. 1883; ed. inspr., dept. of lab., Union of S. Africa, Nov., 1924; under-secr. for lab., Sept., 1929; sec. for lab., May, 1932.

WALKER, JOHN C.—4th batt. Yorkshire regt. Coorga Nagore rifles; served with York. 11 in S. Africa (two medals with 5 clasps); ass. polit. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1908; mentioned in despatches, 1906; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1911.
WALKER, J. W., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), 1897; mily. serv., 1916-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1929.
WALKER, REGINALD PLUMER, A.M.I.T.—B. 1891; travelling audr., F.M.S., Rly., 1914-19; asst. acct., 1918-25; dep. acct., Jan., 1925; chief acct., F.M.S., Rly., Feb., 1925-27; ditto, Apr., Nov., 1930.

WALKER, ROBERT PHILIP SEBASTIAN, K.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1898; attd., Eng. Coll., Johor, Nov., 1920; attd., govt. Eng. schl., Muar, Feb., 1921; Eng. coll., Johore Bahru, June, 1922; res. Inst., S'pore, Apr., 1924; Malay coll., K. Kangsar, May, 1924; govt. Eng. schl., Alor Star, Aug., 1925; King Edward VII Schl., Taiping, Sept., 1926; headmast., Eng. schl., Johore, Dec., 1930; prin., King Ed. VII schl., Taiping, Oct., 1931.
WALL, ERNEST, M.C., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1896; ed. Worcester Royal Grammar Schl.; R.E., 1915-19; capt.; astt. engr., P.W.D., Nyasaland, 1921; exec. engnr., 1929; ag. D.P.W., May, 1931 to Feb., 1932.
WALL, FRANCIS JOSEPH.—B. 1900; astt. prisons, Trinidad, Nov., 1932.
WALLACE, CYRIL R., L.R.C.P., L.B.O.S., L.M. (Ireland).—Ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; capt., R.A.M.C. (T.F.), 1915-18; medical officer, Tanganyika Territory, June, 1918.

WALLACE, GEORGE BRUNTON, B.Sa. (agri.), Ph.D.—B. 1899; ed. George Heriot's Schl., Edinburgh; milv. serv. R.G.A., 1898-19; B.Sa., 1922; Ph.D., 1925; sen. demonstr. in agr. and forest entomology, univ. of Edinburgh, 1922-23; astt. in plant pathology, bd. of agr. for Scotland, 1924-25; microscopeist, game prea. dept., Tanganyika territory, 1925; mycologist, dept. of agr., 1927.
WALLACE, JOSEPH EDWIN—B. 1879; astt. printer, printing dept., F.M.S., Jan. 1915; astt. printing dept., Aug., 1917.

WALLACE, JOHN HENRY, B.A.—B. 1903; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; B.A., Dublin (1926); cadet, N. Rhodesia, 1927; dist. offr., grade III, July, 1929; astt. sec., Mar., 1931; attd. C.O. as astt. prin., 1932.
WALLACE, SIR LAWRENCE AUBREY, K.B.E. (1918); C.M.G. 1910.—Acting admstr., N.E. Rhodesia, May, 1907; ag. admstr., N.W. Rhodesia, Jan., 1909; admstr., Northern Rhodesia, 1911; ret., 1921.
WALLIS, KENNETH.—B. 1901; ed. Epworth Coll., Ryland Univ. Coll., Nottingham; B.Sc. (Lond.), 1923; A.I.C., 1924; astt. govt. analyst, Trinidad and Tobago, 1929; govt. analyst, Br. Guiana, 1933.

WALSH, DUDLEY J. C.—B. 1895; ed. Merchant Taylors Schl.; served with H.A.C. in France, 1914-15; 2nd lieut., Middlesex Regt., 1915; W. African Regt., Sierra Leone, Nigeria, 1916-17; transf., 2nd K.A.R. and served in E. Africa, 1918-21; Somaliland campaign, 1920; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1922.

WALSH, GROFFERT, C.B.E. (1930).—B. 1884; Imp. cust. serv., Apr., 1906; collr. of cust., Mombasa, May, 1913; seconded to occupied territory of German K. Africa to organise cust. serv., with rank of capt., 1916-17; in charge of cust. dept. of occupied area, German E. Africa, Jan. to July, 1917; dep. commr., cust., Kenya and Uganda, Jan., 1920; commr., cust., Kenya and Uganda and mem., leg. coun., Kenya, Apr., 1923.

WALSH, PATRICK JOSEPH STANISLAUS, Ph.D., K.C.—B.1872; ed. Clermont and Paris (France), Innsbruck (Tyrol), Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to bar, King's Inns, June, 1898; K.C., Feb., 1910; Ph.D. (metaphysics and ethics, ancient languages and comparative philology, maths.); junr. and senr. crown pros., N.W. circuit and winter assizes, 1906-20; war serv., 1915-1918; ag. county ct. judge for two circuits, midland counties; dep. recorder, Galway; dep. county ct. judge for counties Kerry, Tipperary and West Riding of Cork; pres., Br. pensions appl. tribunal (Ireland), Mar., 1920-May, 1929; pres., dist. ots., Cyprus, 1929-31; ch. just., Seychelles, 1931; O.A.G., Seychelles, Dec., 1931-May, 1932.

WALTER, A., F.R.A.S.—Joined magnetic and meteorological dept., royal observatory, Greenwich, 1892; astt. director, Royal Alfred Observatory, Mauritius, Apr., 1897; statistical adviser to Mauritius Royal comsn., 1909; census commr., Mauritius, 1911 and 1921; dir., Royal Alfred Observatory, May, 1911 to 1921; ag. astt. col. sec., Mauritius, Nov. to Dec., 1918; food, trade and export contr., Mar., 1920 to Jan., 1922; ag. prot. of imigrts. and poor law commr., Jan., 1922; mem. of coun. of govt., 1922; headed deputation to India for the re-opening of emigr. to Mauritius, 21st Dec., 1922; statistician, Kenya, 1926; statistician to govt. confere., 1926; author of "Sugar Industry of Mauritius"; Insurance of crops against cyclones; editor, Mauritius almanac.

WALTERS, JOHN RADLEY.—B.A. Cantab.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 17th Nov., 1911; att. Kandy kach., 15th Dec., 1911; att. Galle kach., May, 1912; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Nov., 1912; office astt., Galle kach., Nov., 1913; pol. mag., Gampola, May, 1915; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, July, 1917; pol. mag., Karanganala, Dec., 1920; astt. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Sept., 1921; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, July, 1923; astt. govt. agt., Kegalla, Feb., 1924; dist. judge, Matara, Oct., 1926; astt. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Mar., 1927; ag. govt. agt., Badulla, Apr., 1928; astt. govt. agt., Trincomalee, June, 1929; ditto, N. W. Prov., Mar., 1931; cls. I, grade II, 1933; P.M.G., Sept., 1933.

WALTON, BRIAN STAGG.—B.A. (Oxon.); cadet, S.S. Nov., 1913; ag. astt. dist. judge and 4th mag., Singapore, Mar., 1914; astt. censor, Nov., 1914; 2nd lieut., S'pore V.R., Jan., 1916; ag. astt. supt. govt. monops., S'pore, Nov., 1916; offr., cls. V., Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.), Dec., 1917; ag. astt. supt. govt. monops., Penang, Aug., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; dist. offr., K. Selangor, May, 1924; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; astt., lands, Kelantan, Aug., 1926; 2nd mag., Penang, Dec., 1928; ag. collr., land rev. and astt. treas., Malacca, May, 1929; cls. II, Nov., 1930; 1st astt. sec. (A), S.S., Aug., 1932.

WALTON, HON. SIR EDGAR HARRIS, K.C.M.G. (1911).—M.L.A., Cape, for Port Elizabeth, since 1898, and in Union parlmt. since 1910; treas.-gen., Feb., 1904, to Feb., 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with S. Africa Bill, 1909; is proprietor and editor of Eastern Province Herald; high commr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1921-25.

WALTON, SIR GEORGE O'DONNELL, KT. B.A.C. (1925).—B.1871; matriculated London Univ., 1891; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1893; practised at the bar, Barbados, 1894 to 1902; acted on several occasions as pol. mag. and judge of astt. ct. of appeal, Barbados; apptd. pol. mag., dist. "F," Barbados, Dec., 1902; lieut. of M.I., Barbados volunteers; ag. chief just. St. Lucia, 18th May to Oct., 1908, and from March to Oct., 1911; mag. and cor., dist. "C," St. Kitts, Nov., 1911; ag. 2nd puisne judge, Leeward Is., on various occasions, 1911-15; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, May, 1915; ag. admnstr. on several occasions in 1915 and in 1916 and 1917, ag. chief just., from Oct., 1916 to Mar., 1919; atty.-gen., B. Honduras, Mar., 1919; ag. chief justice, May to Nov., 1919; ch. just., Grenada, July, 1921; 1st puisne judge, Trinidad, Apr., 1926; ret., 1931.
WALWYN, ALGERNON EDWARD VERO.—B. 1888; ed. Bath Coll. and Peterhouse, Camb.; asst. dist. offr., N. Prova., Nigeria, Apr., 1915; res., Sept., 1932.

WAMSLEY, R. W., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1880; clk., civ. commrs.'s office, Kimberley, 1903; inland rev. dept., 1911; prin. clk., 1921; recr., rev., Port Elizabeth, 1921; recr., rev., Bloemfontein, 1931; ch. rev. offr., 1932; recr., rev., Cape Town, 1933.

WARD, ESPIRE FRANCIS, M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O.—Ed. Epsom Coll. and Queen's Univ., Belfast; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, 1912; trans. Gambia, 1918; trans., G. Coast, 1920; on special service for C.O., Oct., 1915 to Feb., 1916; temp. lieut.-gen. list, Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1916; attached Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914-15; priv. secy. to ag. gov., Gambia, 1918 senr. med. offr., July, 1927; author of "Beri-Beri, Aetiology, Symptoms, and Treatment," "Favourite Prescriptions" (2nd edition), "Deaths from Lightning Stroke" (Br. Med. Jnl.)

WARD, E. R.—B. 1902; ed. King's Coll., Taunton and Coll. of Estate Management, Lincoln's Inn Fields; cadet, Nigeria, 1926; asst. dist. offr., 1929; priv. sec. to liut.-gov., S. Prova., Nov., 1930-May, 1931; ditto to gov., July, 1931-Mar., 1934.
WARD, FRANK DORRINGTON, F.R.I.B.A.—B. 1885; ed. Kent Coll., Canterbury; P.W.D., S.S., Aug., 1920; govt. architect, S.S., Nov., 1928; regiar., bd. of archts., S.S., Jan., 1932.

WARD, HOWARD HYLAND.—B. 1884; ed. pub. schls. and cent. business coll., Stratford, Ont.; ch. clk., operating dept., bd. of rly. commrs. for Canada, 1911; ch. clk. and asst., 1913; dep. min. of labour, 1923.

WARD, JOSEPH CORBETT, O.B.E.—Lieut. (afterwards capt.) I.Y., S. Africa, 1900-02 (Queen's medal and 4 clasps, King's medal and 2 clasps); Transvaal civ. ser., 1902-04; Br. E. Africa, Sotik expdn. (med. and clasp), 1905; prin. regiar. docs., land dept., E.A.P., 1905; attd. 3rd K.A.R., 1914; post. commdt., lines of commun., 1915-18; staff offr. for Br. E. Africa, 1918; temp. lieut. col. (depts. 3 times, 3 war medals); staff offr. and intell.-offr., Kenya, 1920-25; sec., office of H.M. Eastern African Dependencies, London, 1926.

WARD, LENNOX, K.C., B.A. (lit. and phil.), LL.B., 1908 (Univ. Cape of Good Hope).—Called to bar, 1909; prof. asst., atty. gen., Cape, 1910; prof. asst., solr.-gen., 1914; atty. gen., mandated territory, S.W. Africa, 1919; solr.-gen., 1925; atty.-gen., Natal, 1926; judge-pres., native high ct., 1933.

WARD, LEONARD KEITH, D.Sc. (Adelaide), B.A., B.E.—Ed. Sydney Gram. Schl. and Brisbane Gram. Schl.; entd. univ. of Sydney, 1897, as Queensland govt. exhibitioner, and graduated in arts, 1900, and in engineering, 1903; in service of Broken Hill Proprietary Mining Co., till Oct., 1903; lecturer in geology, mineralogy, petrology and mining geology, Kalgoorlie schl. of mines, W. Australia, 1903-7; asst. govt. geologist and inspr. of mines, Tasmania, 1907-11; govt. geologist, S. Australia, Jan., 1912; also dir. of dept. of mines.
WARD, LEONARD SUMNER, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1890; ed. Weymouth Coll. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, Jan., 1915; attd. N. Prova. secretariat, Nov., 1929; res., Oct., 1930.

WARD, WILFRED ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. privately and at Christ's Hosp.; served France with Civil Service Rifles, 15th London Regt., lance-corp.; 2nd lieut., 3rd Lncs. Fusiliers, May, 1915; seconded, M.G.C., France, Salonica and Palestine; M.C. and promoted capt.; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; passed cadet, Jan., 1922; asst. dist. offr., Kranj, Mar., 1922; offr., cls. V., Nov., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1923; dist. offr., Port Dickson, Apr., 1926; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1928; dist. offr., Temerloh, July, 1929; 2nd dist. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Feb., 1930; ag. asst. treas. F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Dec., 1934.

WARD, WILLIAM ERNEST FRANK, B.A., B.Litt.—B. 1900; ed. Mercer's Schil., Lonie, Dulwich Coll. and Lincoln Coll. Oxford; open hist. schol., Lincoln Coll., 1918; 2nd cls. exam. B.A., mod. hist., 1922; B.Litt., 1923; Goldsmid exhib., mod. hist., 1921; Ridley Hall, Cambridge; m.d., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

WARDLE, BRIAN.—B. 1891; ed. Kmr William's Coll., Isle of Man; Indian pol., 1911-22; King's Pol. med., 1919; seconded as intell. offr., Dublin, 1920-22; supt. in charge, C.I.D., Zanzibar, 1924; ag. asst. commdt. in 1930 and 1933.
WARDLE, D. S.—Temp. asst. treas., Kasta, June, 1920; actd. med. dept., Apr., 1921; asst. treas., July, 1923; senr. asst. treas., 1931.

WARD-PRICE, HENRY LEWIS, B.A., Barrister.—B. 1888; ed. Caterham Schil. and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1912; asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1924; asst. sec., S. Prova. secretariat, 1926; ca. L grade I, admive. serv., 1929.

WARDDROPER, PERCY REDSDALE, M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1892; B. S. A. pol., 1912; cls. N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1914; seconded to N. Rhodesia pol., 1915; lieut., N. Rhodesia pol., Jan., 1917; capt., Jan., 1927; commr., pol., Dec., 1926; as commnr., prisons, Apr., 1927.

WARE, THOMAS WALTER, M.B., Ch.B., Brist., D.P.H. (Lond.)—Med. offr., Brist. med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, in c. subord. staff and families, Kennedy Town infectious diseases hosp. and asst., Victoria hosp., 1927; in ch. civ. hosp., 1928; visited med. offr., Chinese hospa. and dispensaries, 1928; 2nd port health offr. and inspr., immigr., Sep., 1932; ag. port health offr., etc., 1933.
WARNE, OSWALD HORNBY.—B. 1891; ed. Rossall Schil. and Keble Coll., Oxford; heut. South Staffordshire Regt.; asst. dist. commr., Ashanti, G. Coast, 29th Apr., 1914.

WARNER, BRODRICK ANTON.—B. 1888; ed. Winchester Coll.; asst. dist. commr., Trans Prot., 1912; lab. commr., 1922; 1st grad. administrative offr., 1923; senr. asst. sec., 1924; prov. commr., 1929; ag. dep. ch. sec., Oct., 1931; prov. commr., June, 1933; late capt., 5th batt. City of London Regt. (T.F.); capt., Territorial Force Reserve.

WARREN, EDGAR HERBERT, M.B.E. (1914)—Served with 28th I.Y., S. African war, 1914 (medal with 2 clasps); S. African constab. (civil), 1903-1908; lieut., Transvaal H.A., 1907; as customs, Nyasaand Prot., 1908; offr. of customs, 1909; also immigr. offr., 1913; prom. immigr. offr., May, 1914; asst. contr., Apr., 1920; ag. compt., cust., July, 1922; compt., cust., Mar., 1925; ag. treas., June, 1933.
WARREN, FREDK. DRUMMOND.—B. 1881; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1914; subj. inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1922; war serv., 1913-14.

WARREN, JOHN JOSEPH.—B. 1889; R.I.C., 1910-13; inspr. pol., F.M.S., May, 1913; asst. commr., pol., Mar., 1921; dir. crim. intell., May, 1926; head, prov. serv., S'pore, Nov., 1927; asst. supt., govt. monops., S.S., Sept., 1929; senr. asst. supt., govt. monops., S.S., July, 1932.

WARRINGTON, J. C.—B. 1887; educ. Marlborough and Trin. Coll. Camb.; B.A., 1908; served in France with R.E. (Signals) 1914 to 1918; twice ment. in desps.; French Croix de Guerre; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 1919; dist. commr., Apr., 1924.

WASE-BAILEY, BASIL NOML VICTOR, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), 1924, D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), 1926, D.P.H. (Eng.), 1929.—B. 1899; med. serv., B. Guiana, 1926; G.M.O., pub. hosp.; ag. govt. hact., 1926; asst. G.M.O.H., personal asst. to surg.-gen. and dep. port health offr., 1927; govt. med. offr. of health, surg. gen.'s dep. and port health offr., 1931; ag. immigr. agt. gen., regir. gen. and chmn., poor law commrs., 1932.

WATERLAND, HAROLD.—B. 1888; ed. pvtly. and Harper-Adams Agr. Coll.; dipl. agr.; M.R.A.S.E., sci. mast., govt. gram. schl., Dominica, Aug., 1911; censor, 1914; asst. chem. Impl. dept., agr., St. Kitts-Nevia, Dec., 1914; senr. asst., agr. dept., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1916; ag. dir., agr., various periods, 1918, 1918, 1920; del., Imp. Entomol. Conf., 1920; agr. dept., Zanzibar, May, 1922; ag. asst. dir., agr., in 1926, 1928 and 1929; asst. dir., agr., July, 1929; ag. dir., agr., 1931-32.

WATERMEYER, ERNEST FREDERICK.—B. 1880; ed. Graaff Reinet Coll., Stellenbosch Boys Schl., Bath Coll. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; maths. school, Caius Coll., 1899; maths. trip., 1902; law trip., 1903; called to bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1904; pres., income tax sp., 1919; ag. puisne judge, Cape Prov., divn., sup. ct., 1920; puisne judge, C.P. divn., sup. ct., 1922.

WATERMEYER, THEODORE HEINRICH.—B. 1879; ed. Graaff Reinet Coll., Old Gymnasium, Stellenbosch, Bath Coll., Bath and Mason's Univ. Coll. (now Birmingham Univ.), M.Inst.C.E.; appkt., Cape govt. rly., 1902; asst. ch. civ. engr., 1920; rep. S. African Rlys. at Internat. Rly. Congress, Rome, 1922; asst. gen. man., Bloemfontein, 1929; asst. gen. man. for Cape suburban electrification, Capetown, and consulting engr., 1927; asst. gen. man., Johannesburg, 1928; gen. man., 1933.

WATERS, HAROLD BERTRAM B.A., Oxon.—B. 1896; ed. Royal Gram. Schl., Guildford and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; served in The Queen's (Royal West Surrey) Regt. and Signal Serv., R.E., 1914-19; aspt., agric., Nigeria, Mar., 1921; sent to F.M.S., Java and Sumatra to report on oil-palm industry, 1926; dep. asst. dir., agric., Nigeria, Apr., 1927; commr., Canadian national exhib., 1928; dep. dir., agr., Gold Coast, Dec., 1928; dir., agr., Kenya, 1933.

WATERS, HORACE FRANK, M.C., V.D., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1881; ed. Perse Gram. Schl., Camb.; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Perak, Oct., 1908; seconded for mily. serv., May, 1914; ex. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Nov., 1917; demob., Mar., 1919; Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. Med., Sept., 1921 and Decorn., Sept., 1926; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., Aug., 1922; ag. senr. ex. engrnr., Kinta, Jan., 1924; lieut., M.S.V.R., June, 1924; senr. ex. engrnr., Oct., 1926; capt., M.S.V.R., Apr., 1927; ag. state engrnr., Perak, Jan., 1928; state engrnr., Johore, Mar., 1929.
WATKINS-PITCHFORD, H. O., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H.—B. 1900; ed. St. George's Coll., Surrey; St. Thomas's Hosp. Med. Sch.; E. Africa med. serv., Zanzibar, Feb., 1925.

WATSON, ANDREW D.—B. 1882; ed. common schls., Baddeck Acad., Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1905; ent. civ. serv., Canada, dept. of insurance, Feb., 1906; actuary, 1914; assoc., Inst. of Actuaries, Lond., 1909; Assoc., Actuarial Soc. of America, 1913; Fellow, 1919; Contrib. to the jnl. of the Inst. of Actuaries, and the transactions of the Actuarial Soc.

WATSON, DONALD FRANCIS, O.B.E., (1926).—B. 1883; 2nd clk., admstr.'s office, Seychelles, 1899; dispenser and clk., med. dept., 1900; clk. to local bd. of health, quarantine and cemetery comtee, 1900; man., govt. pharmacy, 1901; ag. supt., lunatic asylum, May to Oct., 1920; ag. treas., collr. of customs, chmn., marine bd., and chmn., profiteering comtee., July, 1920 to Apr., 1921; contr., local clearing office, June, 1921; treas., postmr., collr. of cust., commr. of income tax, man. of savings bank, regir. of shipping, Apr., 1923.
WATSON, ERNEST SIBBLES, B.Sc. Tech.—B. 1902; ed. Cent. High Schl., Manchester and Coll. of Technology, Manch.; B.Sc., Tech., 1923; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

WATSON, COMMODORE FISCHER BURGES, D.S.O. (1917), R.N.—B. 1884; ed. Ashdown House pvt. schl. and H.M.S. "Britannia"; midshipman, 1900; sub-lieut., 1903; lieut., 1905; commodr., 1914; capt., 1921; naval asst. to 2nd sea lord, 1928-30; flag capt., H.M.S. "Nelson," 1930-31; commodore commdg. N.Z. station and naval adviser to N.Z. govt., 1932.

WATSON, HON. HERBERT GORDON, I.S.O. (1922).—Ck., gov. of Zululand's office, 1896; ag. sec. for Zululand at different periods, 1896; clk., gov.'s office, Natal, Dec., 1896; transf. Cape civ. serv. and apptd. clk., gov.'s office, Mar., 1901; ch. clk., July, 1904; clk., exec. coun., Apr., 1904 to Apr., 1907; ag. priv. sec. to offr. admstrg. govt., major-gen. E. S. Brook, C.B., July, 1904 to Feb., 1905; clk., exec. coun., Apr., 1907; priv. sec., major-gen. Sir Henry Scobell, K.C.V.O., Dec., 1909; asst. clk., exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, May, 1910; sec. to prime min. and clk., exec. coun., Feb., 1916; clk., exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, 1922; admstr., prov. of Natal, Feb., 1928.

WATSON, JAMES, M.A., B.Sc.—B. 1883; ed. George Heriot's Schl. and Edinburgh Univ., M.A. 1902, B.Sc. 1904; European mast., Raffles Institution, Singapore, 1906; science mast., 1910; inspr. of schls., Pahang, F.M.S., 1915; headmast., Anderson schl., Ipoh, 1920; inspr., of schls., Penang, 1921; ch. inspr. of English schls., S.S. and F.M.S., 1923; ag. dir. educn., S.S. and F.M.S., 1930-31.

WATSON, JAMES GILBERT.—B. 1889; ed. Kgl. Preuss. Forstakademie, Eberswalde, Germany; supt., govt. plantations, F.M.S., Feb., 1913; asst. conserv., forests, F.M.S., Oct., 1913; dep. conserv., forests, research, F.M.S., Nov., 1926; conserv., forests, Jan., 1930.
WATSON, P. C. M.—Treasy. asst., E.A.P., May, 1914; senr. asst. treas., Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. dep. treas., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1924; prin. asst. treas., 1930.

WATSON, HON. ROBERT.—B. 1868; M.E.C. and M.H.A., Newfoundland; ed. at St. John's and Rugby; elected for Trinity, 1897, 1902, 1908 and 1909; mem. exec. coun.; col. see., and parly. head of depts. of post office, telegraphy,

WAUCHOPE, MAJOR-GEN. SIR ARTHUR GRENFELL, G.C.M.G. (1933), K.C.B. (1931); C.B. (1923), C.M.G. (1917), C.I.E. (1919); D.S.O. (1900);—B. 1874; ed. St. Ninian's, Moffat and Repton; 2nd lieut., 4th Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders, 1893; 2nd Batt. The Black Watch, 1896; capt., 1901; served in Boer War (despa., Queen's med. with clasp, King's med. with two clasps); European War, 1914-18; major, 1914; commanded 2nd Batt. Black Watch in France and Mesopotamia, 34th Brigade and temporarily 7th divn., Indian Corps (despa.); bvt. lieut.-col., afterwards col., 1922; major-gen., 1923; mily. mem., Overseas Stilimit, delegator to Australia and N.Z., 1923; ch. of Br. sec., Int. inter-allied comm. of control, Berlin, 1924-25.

WAYLAND, EDWARD JAMES.—B. 1888; ed. Central Foundation Schol., City of London Coll.; R. Coll. of Science; nat. sci. student ship (C.L.C.), 1906; nat. schl. (R.C.S.), 1907-10; post grad. & Marshall research schol., 1910; A.R.C.S., M.I.M.M.; F.G.S., London, etc.; ass't. m.r. survr., Ceylon, 1912; war serv. in 254 Tunnelling Coy., R.E., as forward demolition reconnaissances off. (IVth Army); ag. O.C., 3rd Water Bor. Sec., R.E., apptd. to Uganda, Oct., 1918; dir. geological survey, 1928.

WAYNE, RICHARD ST. JOHN ORMOND.—B. 1904; schol. of Eton, 1917; Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1923; M.A., 1930; ass't. sec., soc. sec's. office, Cyprus, May, 1927; ag. prov. sec. to O.A.G., on various occasions, 1927-29 and 1931; ag. ch. ass't. sec. in 1930 and 1933; ag. commr., Sept.-Oct., 1931.

WEATHERHEAD, ARTHUR EVILYN.—B. 1880; served in S. African constab., 1900-1907; S. African war, Queen's med. with 6 clasps; dist. supt., Uganda police, 1908; ass't. dist. commr., Uganda Prot., Apr., 1910; ag. dist. commr., 1914; dist. commr., 1919; ag. prov. commr., 1924; lab. commr., Uganda, Jan., 1925; ag. prov. commr., Sept., 1925; prov. commr., 2nd grade, 1927; prov. commr., 1st grade, 1927; ag. ch. sec., 1932.
WEAVING, L. A.—Ass't. dist. commr., Kenya, June, 1921; ag. sec., 1930.

WEBB, AMBROSE HENRY.—B. 1882; ed. Chilton Coll., Bath Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford, 1899 (class. schol.); 1st Victoria prizeman as John Brooke schol., King's Inns, Dublin, 1900; called to bar, 1909; K.C., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Samaria, 1921; puisne judge, Kenya, 1933.

WEBB, CECIL JOHN.—R.N., 1909-11; mar. offr., S. Nigeria, 1914; war serv., Cameroons Expdy. Force; H.M.S. "Minotaur," flagship second cruiser squadron, 1916-17; commandant H.M.S. "Waterfly" and "Firefly," 1917-18; lieut., H.M.S. "Venus," flagship of E. Indies squadron, 1918-19; returned to marine dept., Nigeria, 1919; prin. marine offr., 1928.

WEBB, CECIL RICHARD, O.B.E. (1926), M.C.—Ass't. traffic manager, Uganda rly., 1914; unif. man., Tanganyika Territory rly., Oct., 1918; gen. man., govt. rly., Sierra Leone, June, 1925; gen. man., Palestine rly., 1930.

WEBB, SEYMOUR HOPPE MITCHELL, R.A.B.—B. 1906; ed. Portora Royal Schol., Enniskillen and Trinity Coll., Dublin; trop. Africa services course, Oxford, 1928-29; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1929; ass't. dist. offr., Aug., 1931; ag. res. mag., Apr., 1932.

WEBB, WILLIAM LESLIE.—B.1888; ed. Christ Coll., Blackheath and Guy's Hosp.—M.B. (hons.); B.S., Lond.; D.P.H., Lond.; M.R.C.S., (Eng.); L.R.C.P., Lond.; med. offr., Uganda, 1913; sp. serv., venereal diseases, 1913; temp. capt., Uganda med. serv., 1914-16; temp. capt., R.A.M.C., 1918-19; senr. med. offr., 1923; spec. offr., med. supt., Mulago hosp., 1926; dep. dir., med. serv., Uganda, 1928.

WEBBER, MAJOR JOHN WASHEBOURN, M.C. (R.A.R. of O.)—B.1884; ed. Merchiston; posts and tels., Transvaal, 1903; Defence Force of Union of S. Africa, 1913-19; war serv., Natal Rebellion, 1910, German S.W. Africa, Egypt, France and Flanders, 1914-18; Gold Coast posts and tels., dist. survr., Feb., 1920; ch. acct., Apr., 1929; ch. acct., posts and tels., Nigeria, Jan., 1931.

WEBLEY, H. L.—B.1884; ed. pvtely., Bloxham; E. African pol., Feb., 1912; served in European war, 1915-19; ast. comntr., pol., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1919; seconded to act as comntr., pol., inspr., prisons and sheriff, Gambia, May, 1920; ast. comntr., pol., Gambia, 1920; ag. comntr., pol., also as pvt. sec. and A.D.C. on several occasions, 1920-29; ag. crown pros. on several occasions, 1920-29; comntr., pol., inspr., prisons and sheriff, June, 1929.

WEBSTER, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B.1889; ast. polit. offr., German E. Africa provisional admntr., Oct., 1916; admintr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922; ag. senr. comntr., Dar-es-Salam dist., Jan. to Dec., 1925; dep. prov. comntr., Jan., 1926; ag. prov. comntr., E. Prov. Mar., 1926; offl. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1926; ag. sec., native affrs. and mem. exec. coun., Jan. to Apr., 1927; ag. prov. comntr., Feb., 1928; prov. comntr., junr. grade, May, 1929.
WEBSTER, H. G.—Ast. treas., Kenya, July, 1920; ast. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1923; ch. storekeeper, P.W.D., 1929.

WEBSTER, CAPT. JOHN SUTTON, M.B., Bach. Surg. (Vict. Univ. Manch.), D.P.H., D.M.R.E.—B.1884; med. offr., S.S., July, 1909; grade I, Apr., 1910; med. offr. for surg. wk., gen. hosp., Penang, July, 1910; els. IV, Jan., 1914; med. offr., P.W., Sept., 1914; W.O., Apr., 1915 (ment. in desps.); prof. med. Coll., Med., Singapore, Sept., 1922; offl. deleg., 6th Cong. Far Eastn. Assoc. of Trop. Med., Tokyo, Sept., 1925; radiologist, S.S., Apr., 1927; ag. ch. med. offr., Singapore, in addn., Apr.-June, 1930.

WEDDEBURN, MAXWELL MACLAGAN.—B.1883; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1906; pol. mag., Point Pedro and Chavakachcheri, Apr., 1908; Matara, Aug., 1909; Kurunegala, June, 1909; ast. land settm. offr., Jan., 1911; ast. land settm. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordoe," Aug., 1912; ast. censor, Aug., 1914; ast. land settm. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordoe," Oct., 1914; ast. censor, Nov., 1914; ast. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Sept., 1915; ast. settm. offr., July, 1920; rubber contr., Mar., 1925; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Sept., 1925; addnl. govt. agt., S. Prov., Nov., 1928; govt. agt., S. Prov., Apr., 1929; els. I, grade I, 1931; govt. agt., W. Prov., Mar., 1931; ag. ch. sec., Dec., 1932; ag. dep. ch. sec., Feb., 1933.

WEDGE, CAPT. CYRIL NORMAN.—B.1896; schol., mod. hist., St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge, Mar., 1915; 2nd lieut., Worcestershire Regt., Mar., 1915; served in Gallipoli (Suvla Bay), 1915; "1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory Meds.; seconded 1st K.A.R., 1918; served in Portuguese E. Africa and Nyasaland, 1918-20; capt. and adjt., 2/1st K.A.R., Tanganyika Territory, 1920-23; pay and qtrmr., pol. and prisons, Tanganyika Territory, January, 1923; supt., pol., Apr., 1931.

WEGENER, WILLIAM FREDERICK, A.M. Inst. C.E.—B.1897; ed. Crosley Schl., Halifax; Fleetwood L. & Y. & L.N.W. Joint rly., marine dept., 1914; draughtsman, C.M.E. dept., Horwich L. & Y. rly., 1919; power plant supt., loco. works, C.M.E. dept., Horwich L.M.S. rly., 1922; works man., loco. dept., F.M.S. rly., July, 1928.

WEIGALL, LIEUT.-COL. SIR WILLIAM ERNEST GEORGE ARCHIBALD, K.C.M.G. (1920).—B.1874; ed. Wellington Coll., Royal Agric. Coll. (gold medallist); major (ret.), 3rd Batt. Northants Regt.; served in S. Africa, 1902; M.P. (U.), Horncliffe Dist., Lincs., 1911-1920; capt. Lincolnshire Yeomanry, and lieut.-col. and inspr. Q.M.G. services, Eastern Comd., 1914-17; mem. of national salvage council; dep. chmn., surplus govt. property disposal bd.; mem., select comtee. on national expenditure; mem., national agric. coun.; pres. agric. organisation soc.; dep. chmn., fedn. of county agric. comtees; J.P., Kent and Lincs.; gov., S. Australia, 1920-22.

WEIR, HON. ROBERT, P.C. (Can.), B.A.—B.1882; ed. Clinton Coll. and Toronto Univ.; major, Canadian militia; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, g. e., July, 1928; min. of agr. in Bennett cabinet, Aug., 1930; re-el. by accl. after assuming office, Aug., 1930.

WEISBERG, HYMAN, B.A. Cantab.—B.1890; 1st cls. math. tripos.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, June, 1917; ast. dist. offr., Nov., 1919; dist. offr., May, 1920; mag., July, 1922; ag. ast. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, in addn., Apr. to June, 1929; addl. pub. trustee's office, Dec., 1930; ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., offr. cls. I, Nov., 1931; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., June, 1933.
WELBY, H. E.—Ast. dist. comntr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; dist. comntr., Kenya, Jan., 1920; ag. prov. comntr., 1930-31.
WELCH, T. B.—B.1898; ed. Brighton Coll.; ast. loco. supt., Nigerian rly., 1923; ast. divnl. supt. (loco.), 1927.

WELLINGTON, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. THOMAS HENRY SPROTT, M.A., D.D.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; Hebrew prize, 1877; B.A., 1878; (Respondent) 1887; Hebrew Testamur, 1879; Divinity Testimonial (1st els.) and Church Formularies Prize, 1879; M.A., 1882; B.D. and D.D. (jure dignitatis), 1911; deacon, 1879; priest, 1880; curate of Holy Trinity, Kingston-on-Hill, 1879-1882; St. John the Evangelist, Waterloo Road, 1882-1886; vicar of St. Barnabas, Auckland, 1886-1891; examining chaplain to Bp. of Auckland, 1888-1891; vicar of St. Paul's, Wellington, 1892-1911; examining chaplain to Bp. of Wellington, 1892-1911; consecrated Bp. of Wellington, 6th June, 1911.

WELLINGTON, ARTHUR ROBARTES, C.M.G. (1934), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. and D.P.H. (Camb.).—B.1877; med. offr., Sarawak, Sept., 1905; med. offr., port health office and med. offr., quarantine station, Singapore, 10th Sept., 1908; health offr., Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, May, 1909, health offr., F.M.S., Jan., 1911; senr. health offr., F.M.S., Mar., 1919; offl. del. to 4th cong., Far Eastern Asso., Trop. Med., Java, Aug., 1921; also offl. del., 5th cong., Spore, Sept., 1923; offl. del., L. of N.
